(Punjab) (b. 8th Apl., 1880).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived 28th Nov., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offd. as asst. collr. of Customs, Calcutta, from Nov., 1908, to March, 1910; on military service from March, 1915, to Dec., 1919; C.B., Jan., 1916; dep. commr., Mar., 1922; sec. to govt. of Punjab, home dept., March, 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1927; retd., Mar., 1930.

ABRAHAM, KILAKETHOLOCAL MATHAI, B.A., Indian Finance dept. (b. 23rd Nov., 1885).—Joined the service 11th Feb., 1910; asst. acct.-genl., Nov., 1915; ditto and currency officer, Burma, Aug., 1924; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, March, 1927; dep. ditto, Mar., 1928; exr. local fund accts., Bombay, Oct., 1929; asst. dir., army audit, W.C., Nov., 1930; with finl. commr., rlwys., Apl., 1931; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Calcutta, Apl., 1932; offg. acct.-genl., Bengal, June, 1933; offg. govt. exr. of accts., M. and S.M. and S.I., rlwys., Feb., 1935; dep. ch. accts. officer, E.I. rly., Aug., 1937.

ABU ABDULLAH MUHAMMAD ZAKA-ULLAH KHAN, M.A., late United Provs. C.S. (b. 1879).—Joined the service as

proby. dep. collr., 3rd June, 1902; confd. Mar., 1904; dep. registrar, co-operative societies, Aug., 1924; magte. and collr., Feb., 1929; retd., Sept., 1934.

ABU HAMID MUHAMMAD SHAMSOD-DOHA, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 1st Dec., 1901).—Joined the service 3rd Aug., 1924, apptd. supt. Bengal, Mar., 1937, awarded King's Police Medal, Feb., 1937.

ABU MUHAMMAD ARSHAD ALI, Khan Bahadur, Maulvi, B.A., Dept. of co-op. soc., Bengal (regr.) (b. 16th Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 24th Feb., 1909; asst. regr., May, 1924; regr., Oct., 1934.

ABUL FAEZ MUJIBOR RAHMAN, M.Sc. (Cal.), Indian C.S., (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 23rd Sept., 1897).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1923; arrived 10th Nov., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte. & dep. collr., Nov., 1924; offg. dist. & sess. judge, Apl., 1929; addl. ditto, Oct., 1932; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1935.

ABUL FAZL ABDUL HAMID, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 17th Jan., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 15th Jan., 1922; supt., Feby., 1929; O.B.E., June, 1933.

ABUL HASAN, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 11th Mar., 1878). Apptd. inspector of Muhammadan schools, United Provs., 1st April, 1916; retd., Mar., 1933.

ABUL MUZAFFAR AHMAD, B.L., B.C.L., Barr.; late Bengal Civil Service (b. 21st June, 1883).—Joined the service, 7th Sept., 1903; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1904; addl. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1920; confd., Jan., 1926; retd., Sept., 1938.

ABUL QASIM, KHAN BAHADUR, Indian Police (supt., United Provinces) (b. 24th Aug., 1883).—Joined the service as dep. supt. on probn., 2nd Nov., 1908; confd., Nov., 1910; offg. supt., Sept., 1911; confd., July, 1931.

ACHARIYAR, C. R. TIRUVENKATA (see Tiruvenkata Achariyar, C.R.).

ACHARYA, BEJOY KRISHNA, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st May, 1912); educated at Hare School, Calcutta, Calcutta University, University College, London, and School of Oriental Studies, London.—Joined the service 21st Sept., 1936, arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

ACHARYA, K.S.S. (see Srinivasa A. K. S.).

ACHARYA, PRASANNA KUMAR (see Prasanna Kumar A.).

ACHARYA, SIR T. VIJAYARAGHAVA (see Vijayaraghava Acharya, Sir T.).

ACHARYA, V. T. KRISHNAMA (see Krishnama Acharya, V. T.).

ACHESON, JAMES GLASGOW, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (Indian Political Service) (b. 24th Jan., 1880).—Educ. as St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived 22nd Nov., 1913, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; tempy. asst. pol. agent and asst. commr.; Quetta-Pishin, April, 1917; cantonment mag., Quetta, Nov., 1917; asst. pol. agent in Kalat, and pol. agent in charge of

Bolan Pass, Oct., 1918; on special duty in connection with Indo-Afghan Conference, March, 1920; member of British Mission to Kabul, Dec., 1920; dep. commr.; Dera Ismail Khan, Oct., 1923; pol. agent, Tochi, Oct., 1924; pol. agent, North Waziristan, Oct., 1925; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for and pol. dept., May, 1927; confd., Dec., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1929; dep. commr., Peshawar, Apl., 1932; rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1934; offg. for secty. to govt. of India for. dept., July, 1935; offg. resdt., Waziristan, Nov., 1935; pol. resdt., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1937.

ACLAND, RT. REV. CANON RICHARD DYKE, M.A. (b. 3rd Oct., 1881).—Apptd. Bishop of Bombay, 13th Sept., 1929.

ACOCK, WHITFIELD EDGAR, late Indian Police Service (b. 11th Nov., 1880).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1901, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt., July, 1906; supt., Dec., 1914; rettd., May, 1929.

ACOTT, ARTHUR SYDNEY VERNON, M.A., Indian C.S. (collector, Bombay) (b. 31st July, 1888).—Educ. at Magdalen Coll. Sch., Oxford, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 21st Nov., 1912, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. pol. agent; persl. asst. to agent to govr., Kathiawar, March, 1916, to July, 1917; actg. depy. commr., April, 1923; dep. sec. to govt., rev. dept., April, 1926; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1927; confd., Jan., 1931; member of Indian legisl. assembly; contr. of emigrant lab., Oct., 1936, to Nov., 1937.

ACTON, HAMPDEN ANTONY GREER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 30th July, 1914).—Educ. at Eton and Magdalene College, Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

ACTON, JAMES ROWLAND, F.C.S., F.I.C., Office of the High Commr. for India (dir. of inspection, store dept.) (b. 6th Dec., 1875).—Educ. at Ardingly Coll. and London Univ.; appt. viewer, India Store Depôt, 1897; asst. inspr. of stores, 1898; chief chemist, 1909; dep. supt. India Store Depôt, Aug., 1923; dep. dir. of inspection, Apr., 1927; dir. of inspection, July, 1936; on mil. service, 1914-19 (mentioned in despatches).

ACTON, THEODORE JOHN CHICHESTER, B.A., Indian C.S. (magte. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 18th Aug., 1886).—Educ. at Shrewsbury, Keble Coll., Oxford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; 2nd asst. to agent to the govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, Jan., 1916; served as asst. pol. agent, and pol. agent, Sibi, from Sept., 1916; ditto, Quetta, June, 1920; jt. mag., Nov. 1920; actg. dep. commr., March, 1921; actg. mag. and collr., April, 1925; confd., Nov., 1928.

ADAIR, ARTHUR ROBIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 10th Feb., 1913).—Educ. at Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Sch., Wimbourne, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

ADALJA, MANEKCHAND TARACHAND, L.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 18th May, 1880).—Asst. engr., March, 1906; exec. engr., March, 1914; rettd., May, 1935.

ADAM, JOHN BUCKNOLL MERCER, F.C.H., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 6th Mar., 1884).—Arrived, 29th Oct., 1906, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1911; rettd., Apl., 1930.

ADAM, JOHN HUNTER, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 1st June, 1882).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1902; supt., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1912; asst. to inspr.-gen. of police, N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1914; O.B.E., June, 1918; on special duty in charge of Intelligence Bureau, Peshawar, Dec., 1919; on depn. to England from Feb., 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Dec. 1925; pro l. ditto, Jan., 1927; offg. inspr.-genl., N.W.F.P., April, 1928; confd., July, 1931; C.I.E., June, 1933; rettd., June, 1937.

ADAM, RONALD WILLIAM LUMSDEN, Indian Police (b. 9th May, 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1938.

ADAMI, SIR LEONARD CHRISTIAN, Kt., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 15th Mar., 1874).—Educ. at Cheltenham, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1906; dist. and sess. judge, from March, 1911; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, and sec. to legisl. council, Sept., 1914; actg. judge, Patna high ct., May, 1919; confd. Nov., 1919; Kt., June, 1929; rettd., Mar., 1931.

ADAMS, ARTHUR GEORGE, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 16th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 29th Nov., 1913; dist. supt., May, 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1935.

ADAMS, FREDERICK JAMES, Office of High Commr. for India (secty., general dept.) (b. 18th Feb., 1885).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 14th April, 1905; transf. to Jud. and Public Dept., Feb., 1911; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to Jan., 1919; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1919; staff clerk, Apl., 1921; administrative officer, April, 1922; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, asst. principal, Apl., 1922; principal exec. officer, Jan., 1924; suptg. ditto, April, 1927; secty., general dept., Oct., 1938.

ADAMS, HUGH BENEDICT, Indian State Rlys. (elec. engr., N.W. rly.) (b. 27th Feb., 1893).—Apptd. asst. elec. engr., N.W. rly., 1st Dec., 1922; elec. engr., Nov., 1931.

ADAMS, JOHN EUSTACE, Indian Police (b. 9th Feb., 1915).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 16th Dec., 1934.

ADAMS, PHILIP FRANCIS, B.Com., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 18th July, 1914).—Educ. at Mitcham Secondary Sch. and London School of Economics; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

ADAMS, RICHARD PERCIVAL, O.B.E., A.M.I.M.E. (b. 4th Mar., 1876).—Joined the service as offg. asst. inspr. of factories, Bengal, 24th Feb., 1908; supt. of jail manufactures, Dec., 1909; chief inspr. of factories for Bengal, Bihar and Orissa and Assam, March, 1914; O.B.E., June, 1918; rettd. Sept., 1931.

ADAMSON, SIR HARVEY, Kt., K.C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at the Gymnasium, Old Aberdeen, and King's Coll., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 17th Dec., 1877, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. officer; dep. commr., April, 1888; commr., July, 1898; jud. commr., Upper Burma, April, 1902; C.S.I., Jan., 1903; addl. member of gov.-genl.'s legisl. council, Nov., 1903; chief judge, chief court, Lower Burma, May, 1905; Kt., Jan., 1906; member of gov.-genl.'s council, Oct., 1906; lieut. govr. of Burma, May, 1910; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1910; ret'd., Oct., 1915.

ADAVI, VISHNUTIRTH NARAYANACHARYA, B.A. (Hons.); Indian Audit. Dept. (b. 12th Sept., 1899).—Joined the service, 7th Mar., 1925; currency officer, Bombay, Feb., 1935; serv. placed temp. at disp. of Reserve Bank from Apl., 1935.

ADDAMS - WILLIAMS, CHRISTOPHER, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd April, 1877).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1897, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1907; supt. engr., Sept., 1914; C.I.E., June, 1916; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of Bengal, Sept., 1920; ret'd., May, 1932.

ADDIS, FRANK LESLIE, Office of High Commissioner for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 25th Oct., 1889).—Educ. at Strand Sch., King's Coll.; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 14th Oct., 1909, and attd. to Store Dept.; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Dec., 1917; transfed. to office of High Commissioner for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, 1st Jan., 1926.

ADDISON, SIR JAMES, Kt., M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Lahore) (b. 13th Nov., 1879).—Educ. at Banff Burgh Sch. and Aberdeen Univ., apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sec., Delhi municipality, March to Nov., 1906; spl. land acquisition officer, Delhi, Oct., 1912, to July, 1915; judge, small cause ct., Simla, Jan., 1917; offg. dist. and sessions judge, Nov., 1920; dist. and sessions judge, June, 1923; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, April, 1925; judge, ditto, Jan., 1927; knighted, June, 1935.

ADDISON, LEWIS PERCY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 16th February, 1910).—Educated at Bromsgrove and Worcester College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, November, 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; comdt., border mly. police, Dera Ghazi Khan, Nov., 1936.

ADIE, WALTER SIBBALD, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Aug., 1872).—Educ. at Trin. Coll., Camb., senr. wrangler, 1894, 1st Smith's Prize, 1895, Fellow of Trinity Coll., 1896; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to fin. dep. as asst. compt., Assam, Sept., 1899; asst. acctnt.-gen., Burma, April, 1900; dep. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Dec., 1901; re-transfed. to Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., Sept., 1903; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1905; offg. acctnt.-genl., Bengal, April, 1907; dep. acctnt.-genl., Bengal, Oct., 1907; ditto, Punjab, Nov., 1908; offg.

acctnt. gen., Burma, and commr. of paper currency, Nov., 1909; mag. and collr., Bengal, Aug., 1912; attached to Br. min. of muns., March to Sept., 1917; ret'd., Aug., 1929.

ADLARD, LEONARD STANLEY, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 3rd Dec., 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Mar., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1926; confd., Oct., 1926; services placed at disposal of the Govt. of India, Nov., 1934; principl. Govt. sch. of engrg., Rasul, Aug., 1937.

ADVANI, KESSAMAL CHATTAMAL, L.C.E., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engineers (b. 20th Sept., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. in P.W.D. from Aug., 1905, to Jan., 1920; asst. engr., 1st Feb., 1920; I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., 1st Jan., 1931; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1932; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

ADVANI, PRITAMDAS BHOJRAJ, M.Sc., Dept. of Industries, Bombay (dir.) (b. 21st May, 1891).—Appointed Director of Industries, 1st August, 1928.

ADVANI, RADHAKRISHNA TAHILRAM, M.B., B.S., Maj., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bombay (supt.) (b. 26th Jan., 1896).—Date of first commn., 1st Mar., 1922; in mil. employ until 2nd Sept., 1928; supt., Jail Dept., Bombay, July, 1929; supt., Yeravda Cent. Prison, Mar., 1938.

AFZAL, LESLIE ZIA-UD-DIN (see Zia-ud-din Afzal, L.).

AFZAL-UR-RAHMAN, B.A., Khan Bahadur, late Bengal Civil Service (b. Jan., 1874).—Joined the service, 4th June, 1894; sub. dep. collr., July, 1899; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1904; dep. mag., Oct., 1905; ret'd., Feb., 1929.

AGA, HILALY, Indian C.S. (asst. magte. and collr., Bengal) (b. 20th May, 1911).—Educ. at Madras and King's Coll., Cambridge, arrived in India, 18th Feb., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. magte. and collr.

AGARWAL, GOVERDHANDAS, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 11th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service 18th Sept., 1915; asst. engr., Jan., 1918; asst. exec. engr., June, 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1934.

AGARWAL, KEDAR NATH (see Kedar Nath Agarwal).

AGARWALA, CLIFFORD MONMOHAN, Barr. (judge, high ct., Patna).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, 11th July, 1933.

AGARWALLA, ANANDA CHANDRA, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Police Service (b. 30th Sept., 1874).—Joined the service, 24th May, 1894; asst. supt., Jan., 1920; supt., April, 1923; ret'd., Apl., 1930.

AGGARWAL, KUNDAN LAL (see Kundan Lal, A.).

AGGARWAL, WAZIR SINGH, B.A., T.C.E. Coll., Roorkee. Indian State Ry's. (Exec. Engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 1st Apl., 1893).—Joined the service 9th Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1921; exec. engr., Mar., 1938.

AGGARWALA, RAMRAKHA, M.A. (Punj.), M.A. (Delhi), Indian Audit Dept. (b. 29th Oct., 1899).—Joined the service 21st Mar., 1925; asst. acct. genl., Punjab, Apl., 1936.

AGHA HAIDER, SYED, Barr.-at-Law, late Judl. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the service as addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, 4th Oct., 1926; judge, ditto Oct., 1931; ret'd., July, 1936.

AGHA, MUHAMMAD AKRAM (see Muhammad Akram, A.).

AGRAWAL, PITAM CHAND (see Pitam Chand, A.).

AHAD, ABDUL (see Abdul Ahad).

AHMAD, A. M. (see Abul Muzaffar Ahmad).

AHMAD, ALI (see Ali Ahmad).

AHMAD, GHULAM (see Ghulam Ahmad).

AHMAD, IQBAL (see Iqbal Ahmad).

AHMAD, ISLAM (see Islam Ahmad).

AHMAD, KAMALUDDIN (see Kamaluddin Ahmad).

AHMAD, MOHAMMAD BASHIR, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 23rd Oct., 1899).—Joined the service 1st Mar., 1925; dep. comptr., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1933; dep. acct. genl., Cent. Revs., Sept., 1936; ditto, Punjab, May, 1938.

AHMAD, MIRZA MUZAFFAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 28th Feb., 1913).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937, confd., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India Nov., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

AHMAD, MUHAMMAD BASHEER, M.A., Indian Civil Service (sess. and sub-judge, United Provinces) (b. 11th Mar., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad, Aligarh Muslim Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 29th Sept., 1928; arrived, Oct., 1928, and served in the United Provinces as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. commr., Mar., 1930; offg. sess. and sub-judge, May, 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1934; author of 'Problem of Rural Uplift in India.'

AHMAD, MUZUDDIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st Oct., 1909).—Educ. at Aminabad High School, Lucknow, Government High School, Sitapur, Government Jubilee Collegiate School, Lucknow, University of Lucknow and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1936.

AHMAD, MUSHTAQ (see Mushtaq Ahmad).

AHMAD, NASIR, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 14th June, 1905).—Educ. at Punjab University and Wadham College, Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1935.

AHMAD, NAZIR, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 10th Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; apptd. on probn. 29th Sept., 1937, confd., 28th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

AHMAD, RAHIMUDDIN (see Rahimuddin Ahmad).

AHMAD, RIAZ (see Riaz Ahmad).

AHMAD SAID KHAN, MUHAMMAD, SIR, NAWAB OF CHHITARI, K.C.I.E., M.B.E.—M.B.E., June, 1918; C.I.E. June, 1921; apptd. member of govr.'sexec. council, United Provs., 3rd Jan., 1926; K.C.I.E., June, 1928, delegate to Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd. Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; temp. member govr.-genl's council, Jan., 1932; govr., United Provs., Apl. to Nov., 1933; minister of home affairs, U.P., Apl. to July, 1937.

AHMAD, SAIYED AMIN, B.A., Indian Civil Service (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 1st Mar., 1904).—Educ. at University of Patna and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. coll. and mag.; jt. mag., Dec., 1928.

AHMAD, SHAMSUDDIN, see SHAMSUDDIN AHMAD.

AHMAD, SIRAJUDDIN (see Sirajuddin Ahmad).

AHMAD SYED, KHAN SAHIB (see Syed Ahmad).

AHMAD, ZIA-UD-DIN, M.A., LL.B., late Indian Police (b. 5th Apl., 1881).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 31st July, 1904, to 17th Nov., 1906; offg. dep. supt., Nov., 1906; confd., Dec., 1907; joined the I.P.S. as supt. of police, May, 1921; commr. of police, Bhawalpur State, July, 1929; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1933; retd., Apl., 1936.

AHMED, AMIN (see Amin Ahmed).

AHMED, AZIZ, B.Sc., Indian Civil Service (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 24th June, 1906).—Educ. at Punjab University and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. coll. Dec., 1931; offg. dep. regr., co-op. soc., Jan., 1936.

AHMED, QUTUB-UD-DIN (see Qutub-ud-din Ahmed).

AHMED, SHEIKH FATEH, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr. E.I. rly.) (b. 18th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 7th Jan., 1914; resdt. engr., E.I. rly., Jan., 1922; exec. engr., ditto, Sept., 1927.

AHMED, SIR SYED SULTAN, Kt., D.L., Bar.-at-Law (b. 24th Dec., 1880).—Apptd. Dep. Legal Remembrancer to the Govt. of Bihar and Orissa, 1913; Govt. Advocate, 1916-37; acted as judge Patna high court, 1919-20; vice-chancellor, Patna Univ., 1923-30; member Hartog Educ. Committee, 1928-29; delegate, Indian Round Table Conferences, 1930-31; acting member of the Exec. Council of the Govr. of Bihar and Orissa, 1932; acting member of the Exec. Council of the Governor-General in charge of railways and commerce, 1937.

AHMED, ZAFFRUDDIN ZIAUDDIN, Indian Police (b. 28th Nov., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, 7th Feb., 1933.

AHSAN, ZAFARUL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 15th April, 1911).—Educated at St. John's College, Oxford; joined the service 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

AHSAN-UD-DIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 7th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at Govt. High Sch., Ludhiana, Ismailia High Sch., Amritsar, Punjab Univ., and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offr., Nov., 1936 to May, 1938.

AHSAN-UL-HAQ, MIAN, BARR., late Punjab Civil Service (b. 16th July, 1878).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1921; confd., May, 1926; retd., July, 1933.

AHSANULLAH, MAULVI KHAN BAHADUR, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Bengal) (b. Feb., 1875).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1896; inspr. of schools, May, 1919; ret'd., Feb., 1930.

AIKMAN, EDWARD HENRY, Indian State Rlys. (dep. traffic Manager Burma Rlys.) (b. 23rd Sept., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 19th Nov., 1909; dist. traffic supt., Mar., 1928; offg. dep. traffic manager, transportn., Aug., 1932; ditto, comml., Aug., 1932; dep. traffic manager, Burma rlys., Dec., 1932; offg. traffic manager, Feb., 1933.

AINSLEY, J. E., L.R.C.P. & S., L.D.S. (Edin.).—Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Central Provs. (b. 7th Sept., 1893).—First commn., 15th Mar., 1920; civil surgeon, Aug., 1933; reverted to mil. duty July, 1938.

AITCHISON, PATRICK EDWARD, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 17th Mar., 1881).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1903, as asst. consvr. of forests; dep. consvr., Dec., 1908; on mil. service, 1917-18; consvr. (acting), May, 1922; confd., Aug., 1927; offg. ch. consvr., Mar., 1932; confd., Aug., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1934; ret'd., May, 1935.

AITCHISON, WILLIAM, M.C., M.A., B.Sc., M.B., Ch.B., Major, I.M.S., Medical Dept., United Provs. (civil surg.) (b. 4th June, 1894).—Date of first commn., 28th Jan., 1925; civil surg., United Provs., Feb., 1932.

AITIZAZ-UD-DIN AHMAD KHAN, SAHIBZADA MIRZA, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 6th Sept., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 10th April, 1920; offg. supt., Mar., 1926; confd., Oct., 1931.

AITKEN, CECIL EDWARD, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bombay) (b. 8th Oct., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1935; (provl.) ditto, May, 1938.

AIYAR (see also Ayyar).

AIYAR, N. C. K. (see Krishna Aiyar, N. C.).

AIYAR, S. K. (see Krishna Aiyar, S.).

AIYAR, SIR P. S. A., Sivaswami (see Sivaswami Aiyar, Sir P. S. A.).

AIYAR, T. SADASIVA (see Sadasiva Aiyar, T.).

AIYAR, V. (see Venkatarama Aiyar).

AJIT CHAND, MALHOTRA, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 7th Oct., 1902).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 30th Oct., 1925; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

AJIT SINGH, KALHA, SARDAR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 27th May, 1904).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 29th Oct., 1926; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1927; exec. engr., Oct., 1936.

AJREKAR, SHRIPAD LAKSHMAN, B.A., late Indian Educational Service (b. 29th Mar., 1883).—Joined the service as prof. of botany, Royal Institute of Science, Bombay, 16th May, 1924; ditto, Gujarat coll., Ahmedabad, June, 1928; retired, Apr., 1938.

AJUDHIANATH, KHOSLA, RAI SAHIB, LALA, B.A., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 11th Dec., 1892).—Joined the service, 28th Sept., 1916; asst. engr., Sept., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1926.

AKBAR HUSAIN, CHAUDHRI, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 7th Oct., 1896).—Educ. at Canning Coll., Lucknow, and Peterhouse, Cambridge; joined the service 12th Dec., 1921; served in United Provs. as asst. commr. from 14th Jan., 1922; asst. mag. and collr., July, 1924; offg. sess. and sub-judge, Oct., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1928; confd., Sept., 1930.

AKHIL CHANDRA MITRA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 16th Jan., 1900).—Joined the service, 30th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

AKHTER HAMID, KHAN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 15th July 1914).—Educ. at Agra Univ.; joined the service, 8th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

AKHUND, ABDULLAH SHAPI MUHAMMAD, B.A., LL.B., Bombay Civil Service (dist. and sess. judge, Sind) (b. 26th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service as sub. judge, 23rd Nov., 1911; offg. asst. judge, Feb., 1925; confd., Nov., 1928; offg. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1930; confd. Oct., 1931.

AKRAM, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Akram).

AL HASAN MUHAMMAD, B.A., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (dep. P.M.G.) (b. 16th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 1st June, 1909; confd., May, 1911; asst. dep. dir.-genl., Mar., 1932; on depts. to Univ. Postal Congr., Cairo, from Jan. to May, 1934; dep. P.M.G., Nov., 1935.

ALAM, SHAH NAZIR (see Nazir Alam, S.).

ALAY RAZA, SAIFYD, Barr., late Judl. Dept., Central Provs. (b. Oct., 1879).—Joined the service as munisf. March, 1903; sub-judge, May, 1910; addl. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1931; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

ALBAN, REGINALD GEORGE EVELIN WILLIAM, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 21st Aug., 1899).—First commn., 1st Oct., 1918; in mil. employ. to 20th May, 1925; under-secy. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Dec., 1930; pol. agent and Brit. consul at Muscat, May, 1931; under-secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Oct., 1934; asst. to pol. agent, Kalat, Oct., 1935; pol. agent, Sibi, Dec., 1936.

ALCOCK, ALFRED SYDNEY, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th Oct., 1881).—Joined the service, 24th Feb., 1898, and was employed in loco. dept. to May, 1903, and in Agent's Office from June, 1903, to May, 1920; 3rd asst. secty., June, 1920; 2nd ditto, May, 1921; offg. secty., Apr., 1922; Jan., 1925 and Nov., 1927; asst. dep. agent, July, 1925; secty., G.I.P. Rly., Feb., 1930; ret'd., July, 1938.

ALCOCK, WILLIAM GEORGE, Indian State Rlys. (secty. to G.M., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 25th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service 6th Mar., 1906. Empld. as sub. in loco. dept. till Sept., 1920; pers. asst. to loco. Supt. and ch. Mechl. Engr., Sept., 1920; Secty. to agent (now termed genl. mag.), Jan., 1937.

ALDRED, LIONEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 5th May, 1915).—Educ. at Sloane Sch., London, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept.

1937; confd., 8th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

ALEXANDER, GEORGE PATTERSON, M.Inst.C.E. (Port engr., Madras Port Trust) (b. 25th Jan., 1895).—Apptd. exec. engr., Madras Port Trust, 13th May, 1930; Port engr., 25th Jan., 1933; actd. as chairman, Madras Port Trust, variously from Mar., 1935.

ALEXANDER, HAROLD GEORGE, F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., ret'd., late I.M.S., Bihar Medical Dept. (b. 7th Feb., 1888).—First commn., 16th July, 1915; offg. prof'r. of surgery, medical coll., Patna, July, 1929; confd., Oct., 1932; ret'd., Aug., 1938.

ALEXANDER, HEBER MAITLAND, D.S.O., O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Indian Stores dept. (b. 1st Feb., 1881).—First commn., dated 11th Aug., 1900; O.B.E., June, 1919; in milty. employ to 31st Dec., 1921; dir. of purchase and intelligence, Jan., 1922; ret'd., Oct., 1931.

ALEXANDER, HENRY RAYMOND, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 6th Aug., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 15th Nov., 1909; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1932, and again, Sept., 1936.

ALEXANDER, IAN URQUHART, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 13th June, 1915).—Educ. at High School of Glasgow, and Glasgow Univ.; joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

ALEXANDER, ROBERT DONALD THAIN' D.S.O., O.B.E., T.D., A.K.C., M.I.C.E., M.I.E.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th Sept., 1878).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and King's Coll., London Univ.; apptd. asst. engr., public wks. dept., ry branch, 1st Oct., 1904; served on the survey and construction of the Nagda-Muttra State ry. to Nov., 1906; engaged on preparation of projects for proposed electric rys., Burma, and frontier, Nov., 1906, to Oct., 1907; persl. asst. to engr.-in-chief, Nagda-Muttra State ry., Oct., 1907, to Feb., 1909; also signal engr., Nagda-Muttra State ry., Sept., 1908, to Feb., 1909; offg. exec. engr., Sept., 1908; services lent to Burma Ry. Co. from Feb. to June, 1909; exec. engr., Lower Ganges Bridge. Sept., 1900, to Oct., 1915 (three times deputed to England in connection with the project); on active service, Mesopotamia, Oct., 1915, to Aug., 1919 (temp. Lt.-Col., London Scottish, April, 1917; subst. Lt.-Col., T.F. Res., Nov., 1919; D.S.O., 1917; O.B.E., 1919; T.D., 1919; four times mentioned in despatches); offg. asst. sec., rly. board, April, 1920; services lent to Bengal Nagpur Rly. Co. as chief engr., Oct., 1921; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

ALEXANDER, ROBERT VEITCH, Indian Forest Engineering Service (b. 22nd Oct., 1891).—Joined the service, as forest engineer, Bombay, 24th Nov., 1921.

ALEXANDER, WILLIAM GORDON, M.A., Indian C.S. (under secty. defence dept. Govt. of India) (b. 9th July, 1908).—Educated at George Watson's College, Edinburgh, Edinburgh Univ. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after

exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; under-secty., defence dept., Govt. of India, Mar., 1937.

ALI AHMAD, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (sup'tg. engr., Assam) (b. 30th July, 1889).—Joined the service, 26th Sept., 1912; asst. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr. Sept., 1922; asst. ch. engr., and under secty., p.w.d., Assam, Nov., 1935; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1936; confd.

ALI, ASA MUHAMMAD, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt.) (b. 16th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service, 10th Nov., 1915; asst. supt., E.I. Rly., July, 1925; offg. secty., rly. bd., Apl., 1931; transpn. supt. (traffic comm.) E.I. Rly., Feb., 1935.

ALI, A. (see Asghar Ali).

ALI, A. F. M. A. (see Abdul Ali, A. F. M.).

ALI, ALBAN, Indian Police (supl., Assam) (b. 23rd Sept., 1904).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1924; in training to Sept., 1926; proby. asst. supt., Sept., 1926; confd., April, 1928; offg. supt., April, 1934; confd., June, 1935.

ALI AUSAT, MUHAMMAD MUNSHI, B.A., I.L.B., late United Provs. C.S. (b. Mar., 1875).—Joined the service, 22nd June, 1905; sub-judge, 22nd Feb., 1918; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1926; ret'd., Mar., 1930.

ALI, BAKHT YAWAR (see Bakht Yawar, A.).

ALI, HAMID MOIZUDIN ABDUL (see Abdul Ali, H. M.).

ALI HASAN, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Police. (b. 16th Dec., 1879).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st July, 1909, to 25th Feb., 1912; dep. supt., Feb., 1912; promoted to I.P.S. as supt., 8th Dec., 1929; awarded Indian police medal, Nov., 1932; ret'd., Dec., 1934.

ALI KHAN, G. (see Ghazanfur Ali Khan).

ALI KHAN, S. (see Shamshad Ali Khan).

ALI, MAULVI SAIYID IJAZ (see Ijaz Ali, M.S.).

ALI, MUHAMMED BARKAT (see Barkat Ali, M.).

ALI, MUHAMMAD HAMID (see Hamid Ali, M.).

ALI, MUHSIN (see Muhsin Ali).

ALI, NASIM (see Nasim Ali, Maulvi).

ALI, RAMZAN (see Ramzan Ali).

ALI, SAIYID FAZL (see Fazl Ali, S.).

ALI, SHUJAT OSMAN (see Osman Ali, S.).

ALI, SYED RAZA (see Raza Ali S.).

ALI, TORICK AMEER (see Ameer Ali T.).

ALI, WARIS AMEER (see Ameer Ali, W.).

ALI, ZAFAR (see Zafar Ali).

ALINGTON, NOEL STANLEY, M.C., Major I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 4th Jan., 1890).—First commn., dated 27th Oct., 1917; in milty. employ prior to 29th Mar., 1925; offg. pers. asst. to administr., Nabha, Mar., 1925; probnr. in pol. dept., Sept., 1925; pers. asst. to ch. commr. and agent to govr. genl, N.W.F.P., Jan., 1927; asst. commr., Bannu, Oct., 1928; under sec. to ch. commr., N.W.F.P., May, 1930; asst. pol. agent, Zhob, Apl., 1931; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Quetia-Pishin, Apl., 1932; pol. agent, Zhob, Mar., 1933; offg. under secty. to the Govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1935; confd., July, 1936; secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Aug., 1936.

ALLAH BAKSH, KHAN SAHIB, B.A. (Oxon.), Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, on 29th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.

ALLAH YAR KHAN, KHAN SAHIB, MALIK, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 28th June, 1887).—Apptd. asst. consvr., Punjab, from 19th July, 1922; dep. consvr., April, 1928.

ALLAN, ROBERT GEORGE, C.I.E., M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 7th Nov., 1879).—Apptd. principl., agricl. coll., Nagpur, 23rd Nov., 1907; offg. dir. of agric., Central Provs., March, 1926; employed as dir. of agric., United Provs., Dec., 1931; confd., Oct., 1933; ret'd., Nov., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1936.

ALLANSON, HARRY LLEWELYN LYONS, C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon.), late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 16th April, 1876).—Educ. at Bedford Grammar School and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 13th Dec., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and settlmnt. offr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1910; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1916; ditto, and regr., high ct., Patna, Feb., 1919; sec. to govt., judl. dept., June, 1921, to April, 1924; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1926; C.I.E., July, 1926; acting judge, high ct., Patna, Jan., 1927; addl. judge, ditto, May, 1927; actg. judge, ditto, April, 1928; ret'd., April, 1932.

ALLARDICE, EVERARD COCHRAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 14th Dec., 1914).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., Bristol, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 19th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

ALLEN, FRANCIS RICHARD WILLIAM KINKEAD, M.A., M.D., B.Ch., Major, I.M.S., Medical Dept., Central Provs. (civil surg.) (b. 1st May, 1899).—Date of first commn., 1st Aug., 1928; in Milty. employ. to May, 1933; offg. civil surg., Central Provs., 17th May, 1933; civil surg. and medl. officer, dist. jail, May, 1935.

ALLEN, GUY OLDFIELD, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th Nov., 1883).—Educ. at Rugby, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supdt., Dehra Dun, April, 1914; jt. mag., Dec., 1916; on mil. service, July, 1918, to April, 1919; sessions and sub-judge, Nov., 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1923; confd. July, 1924; offg. judge, Allahabad high ct., May-Aug., 1931; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

ALLEN, HARRY S., Indian State Rlys. (colliery manager, E.I. Rly.), (b. 14th Apl., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. colliery manager, 22nd Feb., 1914; colliery manager, Oct., 1920.

ALLINSON, HENRY JOHN, A.M.I.E.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr. (elec.)) (b. 13th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service as dist. elec. engr., E.I. Rly., 8th Apl., 1927; offg. ch. elec. engr., variously from Mar., 1930; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr. (elec.), E. B. rly., Oct., 1935; confd., Nov., 1936.

ALLSOP, FRED, M.Sc., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 26th Sept., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, on 22nd Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., Apl., 1932; prof. of forestry, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, July, 1933.

ALLSOP, JAMES JOSEPH WHITTLESEA, Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Allahabad), (b. 11th Feb., 1887).—Educ. at St. George's Coll., Weybridge, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1916; on mil. duty, July, 1918, to Jan., 1919; sess. and sub-judge, July, 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1925; offg. judl. secty. to govt., Apl., 1931; confd., Mar., 1933; offg. judge, ch. ct. of Oudh, May, 1933; addl. judge, high ct., Allahabad, Oct., 1934; judge, ditto, July, 1935.

ALLUM, FREDERICK WARNER, C.B.E., late Railway Dept.—Trained at T.C.E. Coll.; joined the service 4th June, 1890, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., June, 1902; offg. dep. agent, O and R. rly., Sept., 1914; engr.-in-chief, Nushki Extn. rly., Nov., 1916; suptg. engr., and ditto, May, 1919; C.B.E., Jan. 1919; chief engr., March, 1924; dir., civil engineering, rly. board, April, 1924; ret'd.

ALMOND, JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Sept., 1891).—Educ. at Bolton Grammar Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived 3rd Dec., 1915, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; supy. asst. commr., Peshawar, Feb., 1919; ditto, and persl. asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., April, 1919; confd. in pol. dept. of govt. of India, July, 1923; asst. commr., Mardan, Oct., 1923; dep. commr., Hazara, Dec., 1924; dist. judge, Peshawar, June, 1927; divl. and sess. judge, ditto, Jan., 1928; presdt., enquiry comee., D. I. Khan, Sept., 1931; dist. and sess. judge, Peshawar, Oct., 1931; offg. resdt. and judl. commr., N.W.F.P., Mar., 1934; addl. judl. commr., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1935; judl. commr., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1937.

AMAR NATH, RAI SAHIB, LALA, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab), (b. 26th Mar., 1890).—Joined the service, 7th June, 1915; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1931.

AMAR NATH, NANDA, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, B.A., late Public Works Dep., Punjab (b. 4th Dec., 1877).—Joined the service 19th Nov., 1899; exec. sanitary engr., Oct., 1923; sanitary engr. to govt. Nov., 1925; suptg. engr. Dec., 1928; ret'd. Dec., 1932.

AMAR NATH, SAVARA, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab), (b. 20th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service, 9th Sept., 1910; asst. engr., Sept., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1922.

AMBARNATH BHATTACHARYA, L. C. E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 1st Feb., 1884).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., Bombay, 20th Feb., 1909; asst. engr., Feb., 1910; exec. engr., Feb., 1918; ret'd., Mar., 1938.

AMBEGAONKAR, KRISHNANATH GANESH, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.), (b. 12th Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and Univ. Coll., London. —Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, 28th Nov., 1926, and served in the Central Provs as asst.

commr.; under secty. to govt., Dec., 1928; offg. dep. commr., April, 1930; and again, Mar., 1933; on deptn. under res. bank of India, Apl., 1935.

AMBIKA PRASAD, RAI BAHADUR LALA, late Indian Service of Engrs.—(b. 12th Nov., 1877).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1901; asst. engr., Nov., 1902; exec. engr., Dec., 1911; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1923; confd., Aug., 1927; offg. ch. engr., and secty. to govt., Punjab, irrigation brch., July, 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1932.

AMBLER, FRANCIS BARLOW, M.B., B.S., M.R.C.S., late Indian State Railways (b. 15th Feb., 1877).—Joined the service as dist. surg., 8th Mar., 1907; in milty. employ from 28th Mar., 1916, to 8th Mar., 1920; dist. medl. officer, 1st Apl., 1929; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

AMBRAVANESWAR, SIVARAMA KRISHNA, M.A., B.L., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 6th Feb., 1894).—Joined the service, 9th Mar., 1918; dep. acct. gen., central revs. (works), Feb., 1929; ditto (civil), Mar., 1929; dep. acct. genl., Punjab, July, 1929; asst. acct. genl., posts and tels., Feb., 1931; dep. acct. genl., ditto, Mar., 1931; offg. acct. genl., ditto, June, 1933; offg. ch. auditor, E.B. rly., June, 1934; dep. acct. genl., posts and tels., Sept., 1934; offg. dep. acct. genl. (works), Punjab, Apl., 1935.

AMEER ALI, TORICK, Barr., Judicial dept., Bengal.—Appdt. judge, high court, Calcutta, Nov., 1931.

AMEER ALI, WARIS, late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Oct., 1886).—Educ. at Wellington and Balliol Coll., Oxford; appdt. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; sub-judge, Oct., 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1924; confd. as sess. and sub-judge, Nov., 1925; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; ret'd., June, 1929.

AMIN AHMED, M.B.E., Barr., Bengal Judl. Dept. (judge, small cause court).—Offg. Judge, small cause court, Calcutta, variously from 15th Apl., 1932; confd., Jan., 1935; M.B.E., Jan., 1938.

AMIN-UD-DIN, SHAIKH, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 30th June, 1897).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1923, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1928; dep. commr. (sub. provl.), Dec., 1931; confd., Dec., 1936.

AMINUDDIN, SAIYID (see Saiyid Aminuddin).

AMIR CHAND, M.B., Lt.-COL., I.M.S. (princl., medl. sch., Amritsar) (b. 9th Sept., 1889).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1914; in milty. employ to 15th Jan., 1921; prof'r., King Edward medl. school, Lahore, Jan., 1921; princpl., medl. sch., Amritsar, April, 1926; offg. prof'r. of medicine, King Edward Medical Coll., Lahore, Mar., 1936.

AMIR HUSAIN, KHAN BAHADUR SAIYID, B.A., late United Provs., C.S. (b. 30th Sept., 1881).—Joined the service as dep. collr. on probn., 2nd Oct., 1905; confd., Oct., 1907; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1903; confd., Apl., 1932; dep. commr., Apl., 1933; ret'd., Mar., 1938.

AMIR, SAIYID ALI (see Saiyid Ali, Amir).

AMIR-UD-DIN, MIR (see Mir Amir-ud-din).

AMOLAK RAM, KHANNA, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 13th Oct., 1903).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 19th Nov., 1927; proby. asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1928; confd., Nov., 1929.

AMOOBE, HENRY JAMES, Indian Service of Engrs. (princl. Thomason engrg. coll., Roorkee) (b. 2nd Sept., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. dep. ch. engr., June, 1928; appdt. princpl. Thomason coll., Roorkee, Oct., 1932; confd., Oct., 1934.

ANAND, DHARM BIR, Ind. Service of Engrs. (b. 15th Nov., 1909).—Appdt. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 29th Apl., 1933.

ANAND, HIRA SINGH, M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H., Lt.-COL., I.M.S. (Profr. King Edward Medical Coll., Lahore) (b. 17th Sept., 1889).—First commn., 9th Nov., 1917; Profr. of physiology, King Edward Medl. Coll., Lahore, Feb., 1931.

ANAND PRAKASH WATAL, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.), (b. 21st Aug., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 3rd Oct., 1917; in milty. employ from 22nd June, 1918, to 7th Aug., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1921; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1925; confd., Oct., 1926.

ANANDA RAO, DHANVADA, RAO BAHADUR, B.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 20th Sept., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric., 5th Nov., 1909; dep. dir. of agric., Jan., 1922; princpl., agricl. coll., Coimbatore, Aug., 1933; offg. dir. of agric., Madras, Jan., 1934; confd., June, 1935; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

ANANDA RAO, KRISHNASWAMI, RAO BAHADUR, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (b. 21st Sept., 1893).—Joined the service as prof'r., Presdy. Coll., Madras, July, 1919.

ANANDA RAO, UDIPI, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras), (b. 20th Feb., 1903).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 1st July, 1926; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1927; exec. engr., Oct., 1936.

ANANTA ACHARYA, THATTAI RAO BAHADUR, B.A., B.L., Judl. Dept. Madras (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 4th Aug., 1885).—Joined the service, 13th Nov., 1913; sub-judge, Sept., 1932; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1936.

ANANTA RAMAN, KALPATHY NARAYANA, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 28th July, 1909).—Educated at Madras University and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th October, 1932; arrived in India, November, 1932, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., July, 1935.

ANANTAKRISHNA AYYAR, SIR CHITTOOR VAIDYALINGA, Kt., late Judl. dept., Madras (b. 3rd Mar., 1874).—Joined the service as govt. pleader, 16th Feb., 1923; advocate-genl., Mar., 1928; judge, high ct., Dec., 1928; Knighted, Jan., 1934; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

ANANTANARAYANA SARMA, A. N., M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras), (b. 22nd July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 26th Oct., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

ANANTANARAYANAN, MADHAVIAH, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 1st May, 1907).—Educ. at University of Madras and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 15th October, 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag.; sub-collr., Apl., 1931.

ANANTAPADMANABHA RAO, M.C.S., RAO BAHADUR, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service, (b. 5th Sept., 1874).—Joined the service, 22nd Aug., 1899; offg. prinpl., govt. coll., Kumbakonam, Jan., 1927; confd., June, 1927; ret'd., Sept., 1929.

ANANTHANARAYANAN, AYYAR HARIHARAYYAR, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 24th Nov., 1907).—Educated at Madras University and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Sept., 1934.

ANDERSON, ALEXANDER CHARLES, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Jail Dept., Madras (b. 12th Mar., 1883).—First commn. dated 31st July, 1909; in milty. employ to 25th Aug., 1920; offg. supt., central jail, Vallore, Jan., 1921; confd., July, 1921; offg. inspr. genl. of prisons, Madras, May, 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1931.

ANDERSON, EDMUND ROBERT, late Punjab Civil Service (b. 26th Sept., 1877).—Joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1897; sub. judge, Jan., 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1923; provl. ditto, Jan., 1927; confd., Oct., 1927; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

ANDERSON, FRANCIS GEOFFREY HARTNELL, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay), (b. 23rd Dec., 1869).—Educ. at Bath Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. pol. agent, Savanur, June, 1896; transfld. to fin. dept. as asst. comptr., Central Prov., Nov., 1899; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1900; on depn. as acctnt.-gen., Jammu and Kashmir States, Feb., 1903; reverted to the Bombay service as 1st asst., March, 1905; offg. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, March to Nov., 1905; junr. collr. and collr. and dist. mag., Sept., 1909; junr. collr. and pol. agent, Surat, Nov., 1909; on special duty for revising the rules under the Bombay, Land Revenue Code, March, 1914, to March, 1915; senr. collr., Oct., 1914; settlt. commr. and dir. of land records and inspr.-gen. of registrn., &c., Oct., 1916; ret'd., June, 1929.

ANDERSON, FREDERICK, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 19th June, 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1924; confd., Oct., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; ch. engr. and jt. secty. to govt. (P.W.D. Irrign.), United Provs., Nov., 1931; C.S.I., Feb., 1937; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

ANDERSON, FREDERICK JASPER, M.C., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (prof. of surgery, Medl. Coll., Calcutta) (b. 10th Mar., 1886).—First commn. dated 27th Jan., 1912; in milty. employ to 1st May, 1923; dist. medl. offr., Feb., 1925; offg. prinpl. and prof. of surgery, Vizagapatam, Apl., 1928; confd., June, 1929; offg. prof. of surgery, Mdl. Coll., Calcutta, June, 1934.

ANDERSON, SIR GEORGE, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 15th May, 1876).—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1909, as prof. of English and history, Elphinstone Coll., Bombay; junr. asst. sec. to govt. of India, educn. dept., June, 1915; spl. duty as secy., Calcutta univ. commn., from Oct., 1917; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; dir. of public instrn., Punjab, Dec., 1920; Kt., Jan., 1924; ret'd., July, 1931; C.S.I., Jan., 1932; educn. commr. with the Govt. of India, Oct., 1933, to May, 1936.

ANDERSON, GEORGE HENRY GARSTIN, D.S.O., M.C., late Indian C.S., India Office (principal, services and genl. dept.) (b. 22nd April, 1896).—Educ. at Perse Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; on mil. service, April, 1915, to Oct., 1921; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1921; arrived 4th Jan., 1922, and served as personal asst. to chief commr. of Delhi and in the Punjab as asst. commr.; apptd. asst. principal, India Office, 1st Jan., 1925; actg. principal, Aug., 1930; confd., July, 1931.

ANDERSON, GERALD ALFRED, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 26th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1913; supt., April, 1925; on depn. as dep. dir.-genl., Hyderabad State, Nov., 1934.

ANDERSON, JAMES DRUMMOND, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr. Punjab) (b. 10th Aug., 1886).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 5th Dec. 1910, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and asst. settlt. offr.; on mil. duty from Oct., 1917 to Dec., 1918; under sec. to Punjab Govt., s.p.t., Jan., 1920; dep. commr. (provl.), Sept., 1924; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1926; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1927; offg. secty. to govt., legislative dept., Punjab, April, 1931; confd., May, 1933; addl. jt. secty., legisl. dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1936; offg. jt. secty., ditto, Nov., 1936; secty., ditto, Nov., 1936; on spec. duty, Mar., 1938.

ANDERSON, Rt. Hon. Sir JOHN, P.C., G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E. (b. 8th July, 1882).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, Edinburgh and Leipzig Univs. (M.A., B.Sc.); entered Colonial Office, 1905; secty., Northern Nigeria lands comee., 1909; secty., W. African currency comee., 1911; prinpl. clerk, Office of Insurance Comms., 1913; secty., Ministry of Shipping, 1917-19; addl. secty. to Local Govt. Bd., Apl., 1919; 2nd secty., Ministry of Health, 1919; chairman of Bd. of Inland Rev., 1919-22; jt. under-secty. to Lord Lieut. of Ireland, 1920; perm. under-secty. of state, Home Office, 1922; G.C.I.E., Mar., 1932; govr. of Bengal, Mar., 1932, to Nov., 1937; G.C.S.I., Dec., 1937.

ANDERSON, JOHN, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 17th Dec., 1876).—First commn. dated 30th Jan., 1904; civil surg., Hazara, Apl., 1920; ditto, Sibi, Oct., 1922; ret'd., July, 1931.

ANDERSON, JOHN JAMES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 29th April, 1880).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to fin. commr., June, 1909; dep. commr., Apl., 1917; offg. commr., July, 1924; confd., Dec., 1929; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

ANDERSON, KENNETH, B.A., India Office (principal, finl. dept.) (b. 5th June, 1906).—Educ. at Swindon Secondary Sch. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. principal, 22nd Oct., 1928; resdt. clerk, Jan., 1929, to Sept., 1932; secty., Indian delegation to London Naval conf., Jan.-Apr., 1930; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd Session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; jt. secty. Indian Federal Finance Expert Comee., Jan.-Apr., 1932; on duty with Imp. Econ. Conf., July-Aug., 1932; pte. secty. to perm. under-secty. of state, May, 1933; actg. princpl., Dec., 1933; jt. secty., Niemeyer finl. enquiry, Oct., 1935; princpl., Oct., 1936.

ANDERSON, LAURENCE ALLFROY PELHAM, M.A., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., (dir., Pasteur Inst., Shillong) (b. 18th Apr., 1886).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1912; in milty. employ to 15th Mar., 1922; asst. dir., central research institute, Apr., 1928; offg. dir., Haffkine institute, Bombay, Apr., 1929; dir., Pasteur inst., Rangoon, Nov., 1931; ditto, Shillong, July, 1934.

ANDERSON, VEN. NICOL KEITH, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 10th June, 1882).—Joined the service as jun. chaplain on 10th Oct., 1914; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1924; offg. archdeacon of Rangoon, June, 1930; confd., Dec., 1930; ret'd., Jan., 1936.

ANDERSON, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 29th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Dec., 1923; offg. divl. supt. (junr.), Aug., 1932.

ANDERSON, WILLIAM VIVIAN, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 21st July, 1883).—Educ. at Devonport High School; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India Store Deptt, 9th Apr., 1901; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922.

ANDERTON, COAL CUTHBERT MURRELL, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., N.W.F.P.) (b. 22nd Dec., 1888).—Joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1915; asst. engr., April, 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., April, 1927.

ANDREW, THOMAS ADAMS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 26th Nov., 1885).—Joined as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; under-sec. to govt., P.W.D. (Irrgn. and Rys.), May, 1927; dep. ditto, June, 1928; suptg. engr., Nov., 1934; confd., ch. engr., Feb., 1936.

ANDREWS, FREDERICK CHARLES, India Office (higher executive officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.), (b. 12th Feb., 1892).—Second div. clerk at General Register Office, April, 1911, to April, 1913; appointed second class clerk, India Office, 10th April, 1913, and attd. to record and registry dept.; transf. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept., Sept., 1913; on mil. service from August, 1914, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher ditto, May, 1927.

ANDREWS, ROBERT STUART, M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 25th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Dec., 1928; confd., Oct., 1930.

ANGELO, ALFRED, O.B.E., Major, late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 10th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service 1st Jan., 1900; supt., May, 1904; deputed to the I.E.F., Mesopotamia, from 16th Aug., 1917; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; asst. dir.-genl., Nov., 1921; offg. variously as dep. dir. genl., post office, from Dec., 1927; P.M.G., B. & O., Mar., 1929; dep. dir. genl., staff, Mar., 1930; offg. P.M.G., Punjab and N.W.F.P., April, 1931; confd., May, 1932; P.M.G., Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1935; ditto, Bombay, Mar., 1936; ditto, United Provs., Oct., 1936; ret'd. Sept., 1938.

ANGWIN, JAMES BADEN PENROSE, Capt., R.E. Survey of India (supt.) (b. 25th Sept., 1900).—First commn., 18th Dec., 1919; asst. supt., Oct., 1926; supt., May, 1931.

ANIL CHANDRA BANERJI, M.B., B.S. (All.), D.P.H., Public Health Dept., United Provs. (asst. dir. of public health) (b. 5th Feb., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of malariology, 1st July, 1922; asst. dir. of public health, 19th Nov., 1929; confd., Apr., 1930.

ANKLESARIA, NASSERVANJI NAVROJI, C.I.E., Barr.-at-Law; member, Indian Tariff Board, May, 1938.

ANNAN, WILLIAM THOMAS, India Office (asst. princpl., public and judl. dept.) (b. 11th Jan., 1912).—Educ. at Kelvinside Academy, Glasgow Univ. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. inspr. of taxes, Inland Rev., 19th Nov., 1935; asst. princpl., India Office, 3rd Feb., 1936; resdt. clk., Sept., 1936.

ANNETT, HAROLD EDWARD, D.Sc. (Lond.), F.I.C., late Indian Agricultural Service (b. 5th Sept., 1884).—Joined the service 6th May, 1907, as supy. agric. chemist; agricultural chemist, May, 1912; opium research chemist from Dec., 1919, and actg. principal, agric. coll., Cawnpore, in addition from Aug., 1920; agricl. chemist, Central Provs., March, 1924; ret'd., une, 1929.

ANSARI, MUHAMMAD ABDUL GHANI (see Muhammad, A. G. A.).

ANSORGE, ERIC CECIL, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (rev. commr., Orissa) (b. 6th Mar., 1887).—Educ. at St. Paul's, St. John's Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 5th Dec., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; asst. to dir.-gen. of commercial intelligence, govt. of India, July, 1918; under-sec. to govt. of India, comm. and ind. dept., May, 1919, s.p.t.; dep. sec. to ditto, Aug., 1920; addl. dep. sec., govt. of India, fin. dept. (temp.), April, 1923, to July, 1924; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1925; confd., Decr., 1925; secy. to govt., edn. and dev. dept., March, 1926; to co-operative societies, Mar., 1930; offg. commr., Apr., 1935; C.I.E., May, 1937; rev. commr., Orissa, May, 1938.

ANSTEAD, RUDOLPH DAVID, C.I.E., M.A., late Dept. of Agric., Madras (b. 2nd June, 1876).—Apptd. to dept., 16th April, 1909; dep. dir. of agric., planting districts, Colymbatore, Oct., 1914; offg. dir. of agric., Madras, Aug., 1922; confd., Dec., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; ret'd., June, 1931.

ANTHONY, ALEXANDER CYRIL, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 22nd Oct., 1882).—Joined the service as probnr., fin. dept., 13th Nov., 1902; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, May, 1905; asst. compr., Post Office, Jan., 1906; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Oct., 1908; ditto, United Provs., Feb., 1909; asst. compr.-gen., Calcutta, Nov., 1913; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1914; dep. acctnt.-gen., Burma, Dec., 1916; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1918; ditto, posts and tels., April, 1922, and again Oct., 1926; dep. contr. of currency, Northern India, March, 1925; offg. acct.-gen., posts and tels., Feb., 1931; dep. ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1931; retd., Aug., 1934.

ANTHONY, RICHARD WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.B., C.M., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S. (retd.), (b. 18th May, 1874).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1898; apptd. civil surgn., Bombay, June, 1900; on mil. duty from Nov., 1914, to Nov., 1919; offg. surgeon-gen. with the govt. of Bombay, July, 1925, and again Apl., 1927; confd., Jan., 1928; C.I.E., June, 1929; retd., Nov., 1929.

ANTIA, MERVANJI JAMSHEDJI, M.A. (Bom.), M.A., LL.B. (Cantab.), Barr., Indian Educl. Service (profr., Sydenham coll. of commerce Bombay) (b. 15th June, 1888).—Apptd. lecturer in English, coll. of commerce, 21st June, 1915; profr., ditto, Feb., 1919; I.E.S. from 5th Feb., 1921; offg. princpl., June, 1928; ditto and secty., Accountancy Diploma bd., Mar., 1932.

ANVERY, ASAD ALI, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 23rd May, 1907).—Appointed asst. consvr., United Provs., Nov., 1932; dep. ditto, Nov., 1937.

APEDAILE, ERNEST GORDON STACKHOUSE, Indian C.S. (under secty., defence dept., Burma) (b. 17th Dec., 1908).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and Exeter College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty., home and pol. dept., Burma, May, 1936; ditto, defence dept., Dec., 1937.

APPA RAO D., Judcl. Dept., Madras (regtr., high ct., Madras) (b. 3rd Jan., 1889).—Joined the service, 22nd Aug., 1923; official referee, high ct., June, 1928; master high ct., July, 1934; registrar, Mar., 1938.

APPAJI RAO, KABAD, B.A., B.L., late Judl. dept., Madras (b. 9th Nov., 1877).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 13th Oct., 1900, to 9th Oct., 1906; offg. dist. munsif., Oct., 1906; confd., Mar., 1907; sub. judge, Nov., 1925; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1930; retd., Nov., 1932.

APPANDA RAJ, SRIDHAR, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 10th Jan., 1912).—Joined the service, as asst. supt., Madras, on probn., 29th Jan., 1934; confd., Jan., 1936.

APPLEBY, KINLOCH ARTHUR, O.B.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 13th Feb., 1880).—Joined the service, 17th Mar., 1898; supt., opril, 1906; dep. postmaster-genl., March, 1919; Afg. postmaster-genl., Punjab, etc., April, 1928; ditto, United Provs., Feb., 1932; retd., Feb., 1935.

ARBERRY, ARTHUR JOHN, M.A., Litt.D., (Cantab.), India Office (asst. librarian) (b. 12th May, 1905).—Educ. at Portsmouth G.S. and Pembroke Coll., Camb., 1st class in Classical Tripos Parts I and II. Oriental languages

Tripos, parts I and II, Fellow of Pembroke Coll., 1931; profr. of classics in the Egyptian Univ., 1932; asst. librarian, India Office, 1st Aug., 1934; author of various publications and translations including "Catalogue of Arabic MSS. in the India Office," Vol. II, part II, "Catalogue of the India Office Library," Vol. II, Part VI, Persian Books, and "The India Office Library: a historical sketch.

ARBUTHNOT, CLIFFORD WILLIAM ERNEST, C.I.E., B.E. and B.A. (R.U.I.), Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bombay) (b. 13th Feb., 1885).—Joined as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1908; in milty. employ from 23rd Sept., 1914, to 20th Nov., 1918; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; C.I.E., June, 1930; member, legisl. council, Bombay, Nov., 1932; member, public serv. commn., Bombay-Sind, Apl., 1937; suptg. engr. (provl.), Feb., 1938; ditto, perm., May, 1938.

ARCHBOLD, WILLIAM HUGH, Indian Police (supt. United Provs.) (b. 5th Jan., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 20th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., Jan., 1927; and again, Nov., 1932; confd., Oct., 1936; inspr.-genl., Kashmir, Aug., 1935.

ARCHER, WILLIAM GEORGE, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bihar) (b. 11th Feb., 1907).—Educ. at Strand School, Brixton, Emmanuel College, Cambridge, and School of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931; and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and coll.

ARDAGH, FREDERICK DAY, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 7th Dec., 1893).—Joined the service, 22nd Mar., 1920, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., March 1921; on depn. to Forest Research Institute, Dehra Dun, Apl., 1926; offg. consvr., May, 1936.

ARDAGH, LESLIE VERNON, M.C., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th Jan., 1896).—Educ. at Epsom Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived 6th Dec., 1920, and posted to United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1928; confd., Oct., 1929; elections petition commr., Ajmer, July, 1936; retd.

ARJAN DAS, AGGARWAL, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 1st Jan., 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1930.

ARJUN DAS, VASUDEV, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.A., LL.B., late Punjab Civil Service (b. May, 1881).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1903; extra asst. commr., April, 1906; asst. commr., inc. tax, Sept., 1921; offg. dir. of land records, Punjab, April, 1927; dep. commr., July, 1929; retd., May, 1936.

ARMOUR, JOHN STUART, M.A. (Glas.), B.A. (Oxon.), Indian Educl. Service (princpl. Patna Coll.) (b. 25th Jan., 1885).—Educ. Glasgow Univ. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service as profr., Patna, 29th Oct., 1914; offg. princ. Patna training coll., July, 1915; on milty. service from 11th Oct., 1916 to 14th April, 1919; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Dec., 1920; profr. of English, Patna

coll., Dec., 1921; offg. princ. Patna coll., Sept., 1929; offg. prinpl., Greer Bhumihar Brahman coll., July, 1931; confd., Aug., 1933; princpl., Patna Coll., July, 1935.

ARMSTRONG, ANDREW ROBERT BARKY, B.A.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 8th Sept., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905, asst. engr., Punjab, Dec., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; transferred to Burma, P.W.D., from Mar., 1927; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1927; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

ARMSTRONG, ARTHUR CAMPBELL, C.B.E., C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 26th Nov., 1878).—Joined the service, Aug., 1895; dep. supt., Oct., 1907; awarded King's police medal, Nov., 1909; dist. supt., April, 1918; O.B.E., June, 1919; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., July, 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; dep. inspr. genl., Jan., 1929; C.B.E., Jan., 1933; ret'd., Nov., 1933.

ARMSTRONG, GODFREY GEORGE, O.B.E., M.C., V.D., M.Inst.T. (chairman, Madras Port Trust) (b. 3rd Oct., 1882), O.B.E., 1919; apptd. chairman, Madras Port Trust, 11th Oct., 1928.

ARMSTRONG, JOHN ELLIOTT, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Police Service (Bengal), (b. 21st Sept., 1875).—Joined the service, Jan., 1897, and served as asst. supt.; dist. supt., Dec., 1905; offg. dep. inspr.-gen. C.I.D., May, 1919; confd., June, 1920; O.B.E., Jan., 1923; awarded King's Police Medal, 1926; C.I.E., June, 1927; ret'd., May, 1929.

ARMSTRONG, WILLIAM HERBERT FLETCHER, M.A. (Cantab.), F.C.S., Indian Educl. Service, (asst. dir. of public instn.) (b. 25th Apl., 1892).—Joined the service, 20th Sept., 1917; profrr., Khalsa coll., Amritsar, Mar., 1920; lecturer, Univ. coll., Rangoon, Feb., 1922; prinpl., govt. intermediate coll., Lyallpur, Sept., 1924; vice-prinpl., C.T. coll., Lahore, Dec., 1927; offg. prinpl. ditto, Mar., 1931; and again, July, 1933; inspr. of European schools, Oct., 1935; asst. dir. of public instn., Oct., 1935; offg. dir., Nov., 1936.

ARNOLD, FREDERICK BLACKMORE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty., fin. dept., Burma) (b. 26th June, 1906).—Educ. at the College, Bishop's Stortford, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., fin. dept., Sept., 1933; dep. secty., fin. dept., Dec., 1937.

ARORA, ALAKH BIHARI, B.A., Public health dept., Punjab (asst. dir.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1885).—Joined the service 12th Nov., 1914; asst. dir. of public health, May, 1927.

ARTE, VINAYAK BHASKAR, B.Sc., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 23rd Aug., 1895).—Joined the service, 20th June, 1916; asst. auditor, N.W. rly., May, 1925; asst. acct.-genl., Bombay, June, 1928, exr., local fund audit, Bengal, Dec., 1931; asst. dir., army audit, Southern Cmd., Nov., 1933; examr. outside audit dept., Bengal, Nov., 1937.

ARTHUR, ALLAN JAMES VINCENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 16th Sept., 1915); educ. at Rugby and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd. 12th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

ARUNACHALAM, NELICHERI, SWAMINATHA, Indian Civil Service (sub coll. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 15th Feb., 1903).—Educ. at University of Madras and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, December, 1928, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag; sub-collr. and jt. magte., Nov., 1930.

ASADULLAH, KHALIFA MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A. (librarian, Impl. library) (b. 16th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 16th Nov., 1913; offg. librarian, Impl. library, Feb., 1929; confd., Dec., 1930.

ASANAND KUMAR, M.A., Income-tax Dept., Punjab (asst. commr. (b. 17th Apl., 1891).—Joined the service, Jan., 1917; held non-gaz. appts., Jan., 1917, to Aug., 1921; collr. of income-tax, Sept., 1921; income-tax officer, Oct., 1929; confd. as asst. commr. of income-tax, Mar., 1936.

ASERAPPA, RICHARD HUMBERT, F.C.H., late Indian State Railways (b. 27th Aug., 1874).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1898, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1908; on foreign service to Gondal State from Feb., 1924; ret'd., June, 1929.

ASGHAR ABBAS, KHAN BAHADUR, M.B.E., late Indian Police Service (United Provs.) (b. 1876).—Joined the service, 2nd Apl., 1896, and held non-gazetted appts. to 27th Apl., 1909; dep. supt., Apl., 1909; apptd. to I.P.S. as supt., 24th May, 1928; ret'd., July, 1931.

ASGHAR, ALI, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 15th July, 1914).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; joined the service 14th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

ASGHAR ALI, SHAIKH, KANUNGO, C.B.E., B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th April, 1872).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Camb., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; called to the Bar, Middle Temple, 1896; arrived, 16th Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and dist. judge; dep. commr., Dec., 1909; C.B.E., June, 1918; rendered valuable services in India in connection with the war (Gaz., 3rd June, 1918); addl. sec. to govt., Punjab, April, 1919; sec., home dept., Punjab, Jan., 1921; offg. commr., Feb., 1922; confd., Oct., 1927; offg. finl. commr. (dev.), July, 1931; ret'd. Dec., 1931.

ASGHAR HAMID (see Hamid Asghar).

ASGHAR HASAN, SAIVID, B.A., LL.B., late United Provs. Civil Service (b. 9th Nov., 1877).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1905; munsif, Mar., 1907; sub-judge, June, 1916; sess. and sub-judge, Feb., 1928; dist. & sess. judge, Feb., 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1932.

ASH, FELIX, Indian Posts and Tels. (Contr. tels. stores) (b. 15th Nov., 1893).—Joined the service, 11th Mar., 1924; confd. as asst. contr. tel. stores, Apl., 1928; contr. ditto (provl.), Feb., 1932.

ASHA RAM SHARMA (see Sharma, A. R.).

ASHE, REGINALD WILLIAM ALAN, Income Tax dept., Bengal (asst. commr.) (b. 25th July, 1891).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1913; inc. tax officer, Oct., 1924; offg. asst. commr., Dec., 1926; confd., June, 1928.

ASHLEY-BROWN, REV. WILLIAM, L.Th., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 22nd Feb., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 9th Dec., 1917; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1919; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; offg. archdeacon, Bombay, Dec., 1933; confd., Oct., 1934.

ASHTON, GEORGE, O.B.E., late India Office (b. 6th Aug., 1866).—Clerk, 2nd class, 7th April, 1884; staff clerk, April, 1905; clerk for examn. of army non-effective accts., June, 1915; administrative officer for non-effective questions, April, 1921; O.B.E., June, 1929; ret'd., Aug., 1931.

ASKWITH, ARTHUR VIVIAN, Indian C.S. (secty. to Govt., home dept., Punjab) (b. 16th Nov., 1893).—Educ. Bedford Sch., apptd. asst. supt. of police in I.P.S., Nov., 1913; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Jan., 1919; joined the I.C.S., 4th March, 1921; asst. commr., Punjab, April, 1921; dep. commr. (provl.), Feb., 1927; ditto, (perm.), Nov., 1931; offg. secty. to govt., Punjab, transf. depts., May, 1934; secty. to govt., Punjab, home dept., May, 1935.

ASLI, HEHRAM DINSHAW, M.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India (b. 24th Nov., 1882).—Apptd. trade publicity officer, May, 1926; M.B.E., Jan., 1932.

ASPINALL, ROBERT STIVALA, C.I.E., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (C.M.O., W. India agency) (b. 5th Feb., 1895).—First commn., 5th Oct., 1922; agency surg., Kurram, Dec., 1929; civil surg., New Delhi, Mar., 1930, and Oct., 1934; ditto, Simla East, July, 1932; civil surg., Aymer, and C.M.O., Rajputana, Apl., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; civil surg., Sibi, Oct., 1936; C.M.O., W. India Agency, Apl., 1937.

ASRANI, MOTOOMAL ASSUDAMAI, B.A., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Ry's. (exec. engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 31st Dec., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1915; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; resdt. engr., Aug., 1924; exec. engr. (junr.), E.I. rly., Nov., 1927; ditto (senr.), July, 1934.

ASSHETON-SMITH, GUY COCHRAM, Indian State Ry's. (divl. mechl. engr., N.W. rly.) (b. 28th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1920, as asst. loco. supt.; asst. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1924; asst. works manager, Oct., 1927; divl. mechl. engr., N.W. rly., Aug., 1934.

ASTBURY, ARTHUR RALPH, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 5th June, 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1900; asst. engr., Dec., 1900; exec. engr., Oct., 1908; under-sec. to govt. buildin & roads branch, P.W. Dept., Punjab, Nov., 1915; suptg. engr. (subs. provl.), Oct., 1919; confd., Jan., 1920; offg. chief engr. and sec. to govt., Nov., 1924; confd., Nov., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; ret'd., June, 1935.

ASTLE, GEORGE HARVEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 4th Jan., 1912).—Educated at Alleyne's Grammar School, Uttoxeter, King's College, London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Jan., 1936, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

ASTON, ARTHUR HENRY SOUTHCOTE, M.A., Barr., late Judicial Dept., Bombay (b. 4th July, 1874).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1908, and served in Bombay as

govt. pleader and public prosecutor; chief presy. mag. and rev. judge, April, 1907; offg. addl. judl. commr., Sind, June, 1920; confd., July, 1923; ret'd., July, 1934.

ASTON, HERBERT REID, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 22nd Mar., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., May, 1917; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1927; confd., Sept., 1930; ret'd., May, 1935.

ASUNDI, ANANT KRISHNA, B.A., LL.B., Bombay Civil Service, late Judicial Branch. (b. 14th May, 1880).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 23rd Sept., 1909, to 11th July, 1912; sub-judge, July, 1912; asst. judge, Oct., 1927; offg. judge and sess. judge, May, 1928; confd., May, 1930; ret'd., May, 1935.

ATA MUHAMMAD, SARDAR KHAN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 8th Jan., 1914).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; joined the service 14th Sept., 1938; arrived in India Oct., 1938, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

ATAL, JAI KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., C.P.) (b. 19th Jan., 1914).—Educ. at Claymore School, Winchester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India Nov., 1937, and served in the C.P. as asst. commr.

ATAUR RAHMAN, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A. (b. May, 1879).—Apptd. asst. commr. of income tax, Bengal, 4th April, 1923; offg. commr., Nov., 1933; ret'd., May, 1934.

ATKINS, L. B. W. (see Walsh Atkins, L. B.). ATKINSON, CHARLES MCCARTHY, late Indian Police (b. 2nd Nov., 1879).—Joined the service, 2nd Feb., 1903, as probir.; asst. supt., Sept., 1904; supt., Aug., 1911; on mil. duty, July, 1918, to Jan., 1919; ret'd., Feb., 1933.

ATKINSON, DENIS JACKSON, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma), (b. 5th June, 1897).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1920; asst. consvr., Dec., 1921; divl. forest entomologist, Nov., 1925; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926.

ATKINSON, SIR EDWIN HENRY DE VERE, K.C.B., K.B.E., C.M.G., C.I.E., Lieut.-Gen., R.E.—First commn., 16th Sept., 1885; instr., R. M. Acad., Jan., 1896, to July, 1899; offg. principal, Thomason Coll., Nov., 1901; confd., Dec., 1902; C.I.E., Jan., 1913; temply. to mil. dept., Sept. 1915; mentd. in despatch, June, 1916; C.M.G., Jan., 1917; C.B., Jan., 1918; K.B.E., Feb., 1921; dir. of mil. works, India, 1921; master general of the ordnance, India, April, 1924; K.C.B., March, 1929.

ATKINSON, JOHN DOMVILLE, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 7th Aug., 1906).—Joined the service as probir. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1929; confd., Feb., 1934.

ATKINSON, MONTAGUE PARKER, M.B., B.Ch., Major, I.M.S. (agency surg., Gilgit) (b. 23rd Nov., 1894).—Date of first commn., 29th Nov., 1923; in milty. employ. to Mar., 1931; agency surg., Gilgit, Oct., 1935.

ATKINSON, THOMAS SINTON, B.A., Indian Civil Service (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 21st March, 1905).—Educ. at Methodist Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. warden, oilfields, Jan., 1933; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1934.

ATMA RAM, RAI BAHADUR, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 26th Dec., 1886).—Apptd. inspr. of schools, Punjab, 14th May, 1919; ret'd., Aug., 1931.

AUCOTT, ELLIOTT, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 1st Apl., 1867).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Office, 27th Feb., 1886, and attached to the funds dept., transf'd. to acct.-gen.'s dept., Dec., 1909; staff clerk, Oct., 1913; junr. clerk, March, 1919; services lent to high commr. for India from Oct., 1920; suptg. exec. officer, 1st April, 1922; asst. chief accounting officer, 21st April, 1922; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

AUGIER, ARTHUR REGINALD, Capt., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (Dep. P.M.G.) (b. 2nd Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1908; supt. of post offices, Oct., 1910; on depn. to Field Service and Iraq, July, 1918 to Oct., 1930; dep. P.M.G., Aug., 1936.

AUGIER, DOUGLAS ERIC, O.B.E., Opium dept., United Provs. (jt. opium officer, Malwa) (b. 24th July, 1885).—Joined the service as proby. asst. opium agent, 16th April, 1906; confd., Feb., 1908; dist. opium agent, Dec., 1926; jt. opium officer, Malwa, Jan., 1927; O.B.E., Jan., 1933.

AUNG, U. SAN, late Burma Civil Service (b. 14th Jan., 1878).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 24th Mar., 1899; extra asst. commr., Sept., 1916; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1928; confd., Aug., 1931; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

AUSTIN, THOMAS, Barr., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 20th July, 1887).—Educ. at Plymouth Coll., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1911, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag.; sub. coll. and jt. mag., July, 1917; on foreign service as supt., Pudukkottai estate, May, 1919, to Aug., 1920; collr., civil and mily. station, Bangalore, Feb. 1922; chairman, Assam labour board, Oct., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. magte., July, 1929; confd., Apl., 1930; regr. co-op. soc., Madras, June, 1934.

AVADH BEHARI GUPTA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1893).—Joined the service, 28th Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., April, 1924; exe. engr., Oct., 1926.

AVERY, GEORGE WILLIAM PERCY, Indian State Rlys. (Divl. Transpn. Officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. Mar., 1895).—Asst. traffic supt., Nov., 1919; divl. transpn. officer, May, 1935.

AVRIL, SYDNEY, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 31st May, 1880).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 11th Sept., 1915; asst. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1925; divl. ditto, Jan., 1927; senr. asst. (fuel), Nov., 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

AWATI, PRALHAD RAOJI, B.A. (Bom. and Cantab.), Natural Science Tripos, Pts. I & II, class II (Cantab.), D.I.C. (Lond.), Indian Educl. Service, (b. 18th Dec., 1887).—Appointed prof. of zoology, Royal Institute of Science, Bombay, 1st June, 1922.

AYLING, SIR WILLIAM BOCK, KT., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Weymouth Coll., and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 30th Jan., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head assist. Oct., 1895; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1900; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1904; judge, high

ct., Madras, June, 1912; knighted, June, 1915; offg. chief judge, Madras high ct., July, 1921; ret'd., June, 1924.

AYLMER, EDMUND GEORGE, Printing and Stationery Dept. (manager, Govt. of India press) (b. 16th Mar., 1890).—Held non-gazetted appts., from 15th May, 1914; dep. supt., Govt. central press, Oct., 1920; manager ditto, Mar., 1923; offg. manager, Govt. of India press, Oct., 1930; confd., Oct., 1930.

AYUB BAKSH AWAN, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 25th May, 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., 1st Feb., 1935; confd., Feb., 1937.

AYUB, MUHAMMAD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 15th June, 1914).—Educ. at American High School, Baghdad, Col. Brown's School, Dehra Dun, University College, London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1936; arrived in India, Aug., 1936, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

AYYADURAI AYYAR, VENKATAKRISHNA AYYAR, RAO BAHADUR, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr.) (b. 5th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 20th Feb., 1909; asst. engr., Feb., 1910; exec. engr., Feb., 1919; dep. ch. engr., Madras, May, 1933; suptg. engr., June, 1938.

AYYANGAR, GIYARPURAM N. R. (see Rangaswami Ayyangar, G. N.).

AYYANGAR, KUMBAKONAM SRINIVASA AYYANGAR KRISHNASWAMY, B.A., B.L. (b. 27th July, 1884) (judge, high ct., Madras).—Govt. pleader, July, 1935; apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1938.

AYYANGAR, KUPPUSWAMI RAMASWAMI (see Kuppuswami Ramaswami Ayyangar).

AYYANGAR, KOMBUR RAMASWAMI KRISHNASWAMI (see Krishnaswami Ayyangar, K. R.).

AYYANGAR, K. S. RAMASWAMI (see Ramaswami Ayyangar K. S.).

AYYANGAR, NUGGEHALLI RAMANUJA (see Ramanuja, A. N.).

AYYANGAR, NUGGIHALLI NARASIM, B.A., L.C.E., M.I.E. (Ind) late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 12th Sept., 1881).—Joined 13th March, 1906; asst. engr. (provl. service), April, 1907; exec. engr., April, 1915; under sec. to govt., P. W. D. (rds. and bldgs.), May, 1927; offg. dep. ch. engr., June, 1928; dep. sec. to govt., P. W. D.
