(b. 10th May, 1887).—Joined the service, 7th Nov., 1914 as junr. engr.; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; asst. exec. engr., E.I. Rly., Nov., 1927; exec. engr. (junr.), Mar., 1928; ditto (senr.), Oct., 1933.

BOSE, HEMENDRA KUMAR, M.A., Finance dept., govt. of India (b. 10th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service as probnr., 11th Feb., 1916; asst. acct.-genl., Feb., 1918; dep. acct.-genl., Burma, May, 1929; ditto, Bengal, Sept., 1929; examr., outside audit dept., Bengal, Aug., 1930; dep. acct.-genl., June, 1932; offg. dep. ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Feb., 1933; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Apl., 1934; dep. acct.-genl., United Provs., Jan., 1935; offg. dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Jan., 1935.

BOSE, PROBHA CHUNDER, Rai Bahadur, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 12th Dec., 1890).—Asst. traffic supt., Apl., 1922; asst. dist. traff. supt., junior scale, May, 1927; dist. traff. supt. (prov.), June, 1937.

BOSE, SUDHANU KUMAR, B.Sc., A.R.S.M., Indian School of Mines (b. 7th Oct., 1900).—Apptd. prof. of mining and surveying, 7th Dec., 1927; offg. vice-princl., Indian sch. of mines, May, 1935.

BOSE, SULHANU MOHAN, M.A., LL.B. (Cantab). Bar.-at.-Law, apptd. Member, Public Service Commission, Bengal, 13th Apl., 1937.

BOSE, VIVIAN, Barr. Judl. Dept., Central Provs. (Judge high ct., Nagpur).—Apptd. Judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936.

BOSTOCK, THOMAS MAURICE, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Aug., 1877).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll., apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1900; attached to Punjab, Dec., 1900; exec. engr., Oct., 1908; offg. supt. engr., Feb., 1921; confd., Feb., 1922; offg. ch. engr., and secty. to govt., Punjab irrigation bch., July, 1931; confd., June, 1932; retd., Aug., 1932.

BOTHAM, ARTHUR WILLIAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Dec., 1874).—Educ. at Newcastle-under-Lyme and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 21st Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1906; mag. and collr., April, 1911; dep. commr., commr. of excise, &c., Assam, April, 1912; second sec. to chief commr., Assam, Nov., 1913; dep. commr., and ditto, March, 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; chief sec. to govt., Assam, Dec., 1919; member of govr.'s exec. council, Assam, Dec., 1925; C.S.I., July, 1926; retd., Apl., 1933.

BOTTOMLEY, JOHN EDWARD, Indian State Rlys. (works manager, G.I.P. rly.) (b. 13th July, 1894).—Joined the service, 13th Jan., 1920; asst. works manager, G.I.P. rly., Jan., 1925; works manager, Oct., 1932.

BOTTOMLEY, JOHN MELLOR, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.), Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instrn., Bengal) (b. 21st Mar., 1888).—Educ. at Merchant Taylor's sch., Crosby and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service as prof., Dacca Coll., 12th Feb., 1911; inspr. of schools, Nov., 1925; asst. dir. of public instrn., Aug., 1926; prinpl., Chittagong Coll., Dec., 1926; asst. dir. of public instrn. for Muhammadan educn., Bengal, June, 1929; princl., presy. coll., Calcutta, Aug., 1931; dir. of public instrn., Bengal, May, 1933; offg. secty. to govt., Bengal, educn. dept., July, 1934; C.I.E., May, 1937.

BOTTOMS, ALVAN DOUGLAS, India Audit Office (higher executive officer) (b. 22nd Sept., 1899).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 8th Aug., 1919, after previous service from 25th April, 1917; second class clerk, 29th April, 1920; transfed. to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; transfed. to India Audit Office, March, 1922; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; higher exec. officer, Oct., 1934.

BOUCHIER, EDWARD WALLACE, Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 1st Jan., 1906).—Educ. at St. Olave's Grammar School, and University College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1929; arrived Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag; sub-collr., Sept., 1932; under secty. to govt.,

rev. dept., Madras, June, 1934; ditto, home dept., Apl., 1936; ditto public dept., Sept., 1937 to July, 1938.

BOUCHIEB, OWEN PLATT, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, India Office, 14th April, 1920; transfed. to Office of High Commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1925.

BOULD, REGINALD PERRY, Indian State Rlys. (b. 6th Jan., 1884).—Joined the service on N.W. Rly., on 16th Nov., 1905; services transferred to G.I.P. Rly. from 6th Apl., 1912; asst. tel. supt., Apl., 1912; dep. transptn. supt. (tels.), Oct., 1926.

BOULTON, Rev. WALTER, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 11th July, 1901).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Bengal, Jan., 1927.

BOURDILLON, Sir BERNARD HENRY, K.C.M.G., K.B.E., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1883).—Educ. at Tonbridge, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag., under-sec. to govt., March, 1913; regr., high court, Allahabad, May, 1915; apptd. to I.A.R.O. Sept., 1917; jt. mag. (seconded), March, 1918; judl. asst., Persian Gulf, June, 1918; land acquisition offr., Baghdad, March, 1919; dep. sec., Iraq High Comm., Dec., 1921; sec., ditto, Jan., 1922; mag. and collr., April, 1923, and sec. to high commr., Iraq; councillor to ditto, Nov., 1924; C.M.G., June, 1924; transferred to Colonial Service as Colonial Secty., Ceylon, 27th Nov., 1929; K.B.E., June, 1931; govr. of Uganda, Nov., 1932; K.C.M.G., Jan., 1934.

BOURNE, FREDERICK CHALMERS, B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Aug., 1891).—Educ. at Rugby and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 10th Aug., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1924; (dep. commr. (provl.) Nov., 1926; ditto perm.), Oct., 1929; senr. secty. to finl. commrs. and dy. secty. to govt., Punjab, development dept., Feb., 1932; secty. to govt., Punjab, elec. and industries dept., July, 1934 to Mar., 1937; dep. commr., Mar., 1937.

BOURNE, GERALD COOKE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 23rd Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Gresham's School, Holt, Norfolk, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. registrar, high ct., Rangoon, Aug., 1931; dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1934; regr., high ct., Rangoon, Mar., 1936.

BOWEN, JOHN CHARLES EDWARDS, Lieut. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 8th Oct., 1909).—First commn., 28th Aug., 1930; pers. asst. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Nov., 1934; under trng. in U.P., June, 1935; again, Oct., 1935; asst. to claims commr., Quetta, July, 1935; under secty. to resdt., Punjab States, Sept., 1937; confd., Sept., 1938.

BOWEN, ROWLAND, Indian Police (b. 27th Feb., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1936.

BOWER, EDWARD HENRY MITCHELL, I.S.O., B.A., LL.B. (Lon.), late Registration Dept., Madras (b. 17th Jan., 1876).—Appd. inspr.-gen. of registration, Madras, July, 1927; I.S.O., June, 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1931.

BOWER, GEORGE NOEL, B.A., Imperial Customs Service (collr.) (b. 25th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1907; asst. collr. of customs, Dec., 1908; ditto, Rangoon, and dep. foodstuffs commr. in addn.; rice commr., Rangoon, Jan., 1920, to March, 1921; asst. collr., Calcutta, Dec., 1922; ditto, Bombay, Nov., 1924; ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1927; offg. collr., Karachi, Mar., 1930; confd., Nov., 1930; collr., Calcutta, Mar., 1933; ditto, Madras, Apl., 1934; ditto, Calcutta, Apl., 1936.

BOWERS, EDWIN GUTHRIE, Major, M.C., M.I.E.E. (mech. and elect. engr., Madras Port Trust) (b. 21st June, 1888).—Appd. mech. and elect. engr., 1st Jan., 1928.

BOWERS, PERCY LLOYD, C.I.E., M.C., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 19th Mar., 1879).—Appd. temp. engr., Bombay, 3rd Jan., 1903; asst. engr., Jan., 1903; exec. engr., Feb., 1912; on mil. duty from July, 1916 to Oct., 1919; C.I.E., March, 1919; army rank, Col. I.A.R.O.; offg. supt. engr., April, 1926; offg. ch. engr. and jt. sec. to govt., P.W.D., Mar., 1930; confd., Mar., 1931; member, legis. council, Bombay, 1932; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

BOWLER, VERNON ROBERT, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 8th Aug., 1889).—Appd. 2nd class clerk, 23rd Jan., 1911, and att'd. to record and registry dept.; on mil. service from Dec., 1915, to March, 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, 1st Jan., 1926.

BOWMAN, ARCHIBALD IAN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 11th May, 1915).—Educ. at Glasgow High School and Glasgow Univ.; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

BOWN, ARTHUR MERVYN, B.A., M.C., Indian C.S. (commr. of inc.-tax, Burma) (b. 12th Aug., 1894).—Educ. at Gainsborough Gr. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Aug., 1914; appdtd., 3rd Feb., 1920; arrived, 25th March, 1920, and served in Burma; supt. Southern Shan States, Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., July, 1924; offg. commr. of inc.-tax, Bengal, Mar., 1933; ditto, Burma, Nov., 1933; ditto, N.W.F.P., Mar., 1934; ditto, N.W.F.P., Jan., 1935; confd., Jan., 1935; ditto, Burma, Apl., 1937.

BOWN, JOSEPH EDGAR, late Indian Police (b. 16th Feb., 1883).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1902, as asst. supt. of police; supt., Apl., 1912; on mil. service, Oct., 1918, to Apl., 1919; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Bihar, Nov., 1925, Mar., 1928, and again, Feb., 1932; confd., Oct., 1932; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

BOWSTEAD, JOHN, M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govr., Orissa) (b. 16th Feb., 1897).—Educ. at King's Sch., Worcester, and Corpus Christi, Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Nov., 1921; arrived, 13th Jan., 1922, and served in Bihar and Orissa as

asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under-sec., pol. and appt. dept., April, 1925; confd., July, 1926; offg. asst. pol. agent, Orissa Feudatory States, April, 1928; confd., Oct., 1931; offg. agent, ditto, Feb., 1932; offg. secty. to A.G.G. Eastern States and pol. agent, Sambalpur, Apl., 1933; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1935; confd., Mar., 1936; secty. to govr., Orissa, Mar., 1938.

BOYCE, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 25th July, 1885).—Educ. at Lurgan Coll., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; appdtd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 5th Dec., 1908, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1913; ditto and jt. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1914; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1921; ret'd. Sept., 1929.

BOYD, SIR DONALD JAMES, K.C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd Dec., 1877).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; appdtd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., under-sec. to Punjab govt., Nov., 1907, to May, 1910; settl. offr., Oct., 1911; dir. of land records, &c., May, 1916, to Oct., 1917; dep. commr., Oct., 1917; sec. to govt. of Punjab, home dept., Dec., 1922; elections commr., and jt. sec. to govt., transferred depts., Dec., 1925; offg. commr., April, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Punjab, Apl., 1930; confd., Oct., 1930; commr., Dec., 1930; offg. finl. commr. (dev.), and sec. to govt., Punjab (dev. dept.), May, 1932; ty. member, govr.'s exec. council, Punjab, July, 1932; offg. finl. commr. (rev.), Nov., 1932; ditto (development), Mar., 1933; member, govr.'s exec. council, Punjab, May, 1934; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1936; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

BOYD, THOMAS CRAWFORD, Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (prin. medl. coll., Calcutta) (b. 26th August, 1886).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1908; in mil. employ up to 7th Aug., 1921; chemical examr. and prof. of chemistry, Aug., 1921; prin. medl. coll., Calcutta, Apl., 1932; offg. surg.-genl., Bengal, Dec., 1934; and again, Oct., 1936.

BOYS, GUY PONSONBY, Barr., late Judicial Dept., United Provs. (b. 10th Aug., 1871).—Joined the service on 7th April, 1924, as offg. judge, high ct., Allahabad; judge, ditto, Mar., 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

BOZMAN, GEOFFREY STEPHEN, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (addl. dep.-sec. to the Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands) (b. 26th Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Whitgift Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spl. asst. settl. offr., Feb., 1925; under secty. to govt., law dept., Madras, May, 1927; offg. secty., ditto, May, 1928; secty. tariff bd., June, 1930; secty. to agent of govt. or India in South Africa, Oct., 1932; commr. of Coorg, Mar., 1935; addl. dep. secty. to the Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Mar., 1936; on spec. duty from Nov., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1938.

BRABOURNE, Baron (Sir MICHAEL HERBERT RUDOLPH KNATCHBULL), G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., M.C. (Governor of Bengal) (b. 8th May, 1895).—

Educated at Wellington and Royal Military Academy. Served in Great War, 1915-18 (despatches thrice, M.C.); Staff officer, R.A.F., 1919-20; retired with rank of captain, 1920; J.P., Kent, 1927; M.P. for Ashford Division of Kent, 1931-33; Parliamentary Private Secretary to Secretary of State, 1932-33; succeeded to barony, Feb., 1933; G.C.I.E., Nov., 1933; appointed Governor of Bombay, 9th Dec., 1933; govr. of Bengal, Nov., 1937; G.C.S.I., Nov., 1937; actg. viceroy and govr.-genl. of India, June to Oct., 1938.

BRACKEN, Sir GEOFFREY THOMAS HIRST, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Apl., 1879).—Educ. at Malvern Sch. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; supt. for Pudukkottai, March, 1909; sub.-collr. and jt. mag., Aug., 1914; sec. to commrs. of land rev., Dec., 1915; on mil. duty from June, 1916, to Aug., 1919; sub.-collr. and joint mag., Sept., 1919; collr. and dist. mag. and sec. to govt. of Madras, development dept., Feb., 1925; C.I.E., June, 1930; offg. 3rd member, bd. of rev., May, 1930; confd., Sept., 1932; ch. secty. to govt., Madras (provl. subs.), Sept., 1931; confd., June, 1933; C.S.I., June, 1934; offg. member, govr.'s exec. council, Madras, May, 1934; confd., Mar., 1935; K.C.I.E., June, 1936; presdt., tariff bd., Apl., 1937; retd., Nov., 1938.

BRACKENBURY, Sir CECIL FABIAN, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt., Madras) (b. 13th Mar., 1881).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 11th Dec., 1905, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and spl. settlt. offr.; sub.-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1916; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Dec., 1918; ditto, Dec., 1919; ditto, Nov., 1921; ditto, Nov., 1924; confd., April, 1925; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Madras, July, 1933; 3rd member, bd. of rev., Madras (provl. subs.), Aug., 1933; offg. 2nd member, ditto, and commr. of excise, Nov., 1933; 3rd member, bd. of rev. and 2nd member, ditto (provl.), Jan., 1935; offg. ch. secty. to Govt., Madras, Jan., 1935; confd., Mar., 1935; C.S.I., Jan., 1936; K.C.I.E., June, 1938.

BRADBURY, Cecil Howard, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1914; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; retd., Mar., 1935.

BRADBURY, Rev. JOHN HENRY, A.K.C., Indian Eccles. Estabt. sewr. chaplain, Punjab (b. 8th Oct., 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 23rd Oct., 1925; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1927. sewr. ditto, Apl., 1937.

BRADFIELD, Ernest William Charles, C.I.E., O.B.E., Major-Genl., I.M.S. (dir.-genl., I.M.S.) (b. 28th May, 1880).—Joined the service, 31st Aug., 1903; profir. of surg., medl. coll., Jan., 1924; offg. supt., gen. hosp., Madras, May, 1926; C.I.E., June, 1928; reverted to milty. employ, Apl., 1931; offg. surg.-genl. with govt. of Bombay, June, 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; hon. surg. to the King, Dec., 1935; presdt. medl. council of India, Feb., 1937; dir.-genl., I.M.S., Mar., 1937; member, Council of State, Feb., 1937.

BRADFORD, Walter Glynne, Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., medl. and local govt. depts., Punjab) (b. 15th Oct., 1890).—Educ. at Sherborne and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 25th May, 1916, and posted to the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. duty, May, 1915, to Aug., 1919; offg. dep. commr., May, 1923; provl. ditto, Oct., 1926; perm. ditto, Aug., 1928; secty. to govt., transf. depts., Punjab, Oct., 1935; ditto medl. and local govt. depts., Apl., 1937.

BRADLEY, Charles Alfred Kemble, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 19th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 2nd Nov., 1913; dist. loco. supt., Nov., 1922; offg. dep. loco. supt., Mar., 1930; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. rly., May, 1934; confd., Nov., 1936; offg. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1934; and again, Apl., 1937.

BRADLEY, John Bertram Granville B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 12th March, 1890).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 26th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; sub.-divinl. officer, June, 1923; offg. under-sec., to govt., fin. and rev. dept., June, 1926; under-secy. to govt., home and pol. dept., April, 1927; dep. registrar, high ct., Rangoon, Sept., 1930; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1932; confd., Nov., 1933.

BRADLEY, John William, late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 5th May, 1887).—Arrived, 28th Dec., 1910, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1915; retd., June, 1929.

BRADSHAW, Eric Jean, B.A., B.A.I., M.Sc., Geological Survey Dept. (geologist) (b. 30th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on probn., 8th Jan., 1923; confd., Jan., 1925; offg. supt., May, 1934; on for. service Burma, Apl., 1937.

BRADSHAW, John Richard Landon, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 8th Nov., 1896).—First commn., dated 16th Dec., 1914; joined the pol. dept., 23rd Oct., 1922; asst. commr., Bannu, March, 1925; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, May, 1926; dep. commr., Bannu, Nov., 1928; asst. commr., Charsadda, Jan., 1929; 3rd asst. resdt., Aden, Feb., 1930; secty. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Dec., 1930; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Jan., 1930; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., transf. depts., Jan., 1936; ditto, devel. depts., Apl., 1937; secty. to resdt. for Rajputana, Apl., 1938.

BRADSHAW-SMITH, Robert Alfred, B.Sc., (exec. engr.) Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 5th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1913; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; profir., civil engrg. coll., Roorkee, Jan., 1925; to Oct., 1928; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1934 and again, Apl., 1936.

BRADY, H. W., late Factory Inspection Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 3rd July, 1879).—Joined the service as inspr. of factories, 7th Feb., 1921; ch. ditto, June, 1923; retd., July, 1935.

BRAGANZA, LEOPOLD SCHOLASTICUS, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 10th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 19th April, 1913; temp. engr., G.I.P. Rly., Dec., 1921; resdt. engr., Apl., 1924; exec. engr., May, 1935.

BRAGANZA, PAUL, L.C.E., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 1st July, 1879), Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st April, 1905, to 19th Dec., 1919; sub-engr., Dec., 1919; asst. engr., Feb., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; offg. under-sec. to govt., P.W.D. (rds. and bldgs.), June, 1927; exec. engr., Jan., 1928; retd., July, 1934.

BRAHAM, HARRY VINCENT, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 6th April, 1886).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch. and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1910, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; temp. dep. sec. to govt., rev. and fin. depts., July, 1920; actg. collr. and dist. mag. and pol. agent, July, 1922, April, 1925, and Jan., 1926; confd., April, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; retd., Mar., 1936.

BRAHAMACHARI, BIPIN BIHARI, L.M.S., D.P.H., Dept. of Public Health, Bengal (dir. of public health laboratory) (b. 10th Sept., 1876).—Joined the service as offg. dep. sany. commr., 15th March, 1916; asst. dir. of public health, May, 1918; offg. dir. of public health laboratory, June, 1929.

BRAHAWAR, RAJ NARAYAN (see Raj, N. B.)

BRAHMAWAR, RAJ NARAYAN (see Raj Narayan, B.).

BRAIDWOOD, HAROLD LITHGOW, C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 12th Aug., 1872).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Oct., 1906; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; collr. and dist. mag., April, 1915; resdt. in Travancore and Cochin, Feb., 1917, to Dec., 1920; offg. 2nd mber. of rev., Apl., 1928; C.S.I., June 1929; retd., Feb., 1930.

BRAILSFORD, REV. CYRIL, A.K.C., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 1st Feb., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 1st Feb., 1925; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1927; senr. chaplain, July, 1935.

BRAJ BHUSAN LAL, B.A., late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 3rd June, 1880).—Joined the service as proxy. supt., 1st Apl., 1903; confd., July, 1907; dep. P.M.G., Bihar and Orissa, July, 1930; ditto, United Provs., May, 1932; retd., June, 1935.

BRAJ BHUSHAN, SINGH, Indian C.S. (asst. jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 21st August, 1904).—Educ. at University of Allahabad and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1929; arrived Nov., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. coll. and mag.; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1936.

BRAMWELL, ERNEST CHARLES, Office of the High Commr. for India (asst. engr., mechanical transport) (b. 16th Jan., 1889).—Apptd. asst. inspr. of stores, March, 1926; asst. engr., mechanical transport, Aug., 1926.

BRANDER, GEORGE MACONACHIE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Oct., 1906).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy and Exeter

College, Oxford; joined the service 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; colonization officer., Dec., 1936.

BRANDER, JAMES PATERSON, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th July, 1876).—Educ. at Kelvinside Acad., Glasgow, Glasgow Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 16th Nov., 1900, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., and mag. and asst. settlt. officer; junr. collr., July, 1918; offg. dir. of civ. supplies, May, 1919; collr. and mag., Dec., 1919; supdt. of stamps Jan., 1923; offg. land acquisition offr. for city of Bombay, May, 1923; member of legis. council, 1925 and 1932; pol. agent, Phaltan and Aundh, Jan., 1927; commr. of excise, Jan., 1928; retd., Nov., 1935.

BRANDER, WILLIAM BROWNE, C.I.E., C.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st Aug., 1880).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll. and the Univ., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., April, 1917; C.B.E., June, 1918; offg. commr. of excise, Jan., 1920; confd., May, 1920; addl. sec., home and pol. dept., Jan., 1925; chief sec. to govt. (actg.), April, 1925; confd., Feb., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; chairman, development trust, Rangoon, April, 1928; retd., Jan., 1931.

BRANDOM, HAROLD FRANK, Office of High Commissioner for India (exec. officer, accis. dept.) (b. 28th July, 1898).—Apptd. clerical officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India Office, 13th Oct., 1919; transfd. to Office of High Commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; actg. hr. exec. offcr. variously from Nov., 1936.

BRANDON, EDWIN HAROLD, M.B.E. (admin. officer, Central P.W.D.) (b. 6th Sept., 1885).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 13th May, 1903; supt., leg. dept., s.p.t., 2nd Nov., 1915; confd., June, 1916; offg. asst. sec., govt. of India, home dept., April, 1922; confd., June, 1923; M.B.E., June, 1928; transferred temp. to dept. of industries and labour, P.W. branch, from June, 1930; admin. officer, central p.w.d., Mar., 1932.

BRANFORD, RICHARD, M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Vety. Service (Punjab) (b. 6th May, 1882).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1907; supt., govt. cattle farm, Hissar; offg. chief supt., civil vety. dept., Punjab, April, 1924; retd., Nov., 1930.

BRAUND, HENRY BENEDICT LINTHWAITE, Burr., Judl. Dept., Burma (Judge, high ct., Rangoon).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Rangoon, June, 1934.

BRAY, SIR DENYS DE SAUMAREZ, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Nov., 1875). Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 21st Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; transfd. to N.W. Frontier Prov., Jan., 1902; acting asst. to agent to the govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, Aug., 1903, to July, 1906; pol. asst., July, 1906; ditto and asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1906; census supt., Baluchistan, Jan., 1910; K.-I.-H. medal, 1st class, Dec., 1911; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, April, 1912; actg. dep. sec. (foreign); for. and pol. dept., govt. of India, April, 1916,

confirmed, May, 1916; C.I.E., June, 1917; offg. sec., for. and pol. dept., Sept. to Oct., 1917; offg. pte. sec. to the Viceroy, July to Oct., 1918; offg. sec., for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1918; temp. jt. foreign sec., ditto, June, 1919; C.B.E., June, 1919; offg. foreign sec., ditto, Dec., 1920; C.S.I., Jan., 1922; confd. as foreign sec. to govt. of India, May, 1923; K.C.I.E., June, 1925; retd. from I.C.S., May, 1930; member of Council of India, May, 1930; vice-presdt., ditto, Aug., 1934; adviser to sec. of state for India, Apl. to July, 1937; K.C.S.I., June, 1930; delegate of India at Assembly of League of Nations, Sept., 1932, 1933, 1934, 1935, and 1937; author of "The Brahui Language," "Baluchistan Census Report, 1909," "The Life History of a Brahui," &c.

BRAYBROOKE, AUBREY TREVOR, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th Nov., 1878).—Joined the service, 21st Oct., 1901, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., Sept., 1922; confd., Oct., 1925; retd. Nov., 1933.

BRAYDEN, DESMOND ANTHONY, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 28th Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 29th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., April, 1926; confd., Mar., 1932.

BRAYE, PHILLIP GEORGE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 1st Dec., 1894).—Educ. at Victoria College, Jersey, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service 13th Sept., 1923; arrived, 13th Nov., 1923, and served in Central Provs., as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., June, 1926; confd., Nov., 1932; excise and opium commr. in C.I., Nov., 1934 to Nov., 1936.

BRAYNE, ALBERT FREDERIC LUCAS, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st April, 1884).—Educ. at the Royal Academy, Irvine, Glasgow Univ., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 20th Nov., 1908, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; compiled genl. admtn. report, 1909-10; supt., land records and regn., June, 1913; under-sect. to govt., rev. and finl. depts., Bombay, Aug., 1916; dep. sec. to govt., ditto, July, 1919; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Oct., 1920; offg. finl. adviser, posts and tels., finl. dept., govt. of India, April, 1923; C.I.E., June, 1923; on spl. duty in the finance dept., govt. of India, May, 1922, and again, May, 1931; financial adviser, posts and tels. (temp.), April, 1923; offg. financial adviser, mily. finance, April, 1924; confd., Jan., 1925; offg. sec. to govt. of India, finance dept., Sept., 1926; offg. sec. to govt. of India, army dept., May, 1928; ditto, finance dept., Dec., 1931; secty. to Indian delegation, monetary and economic conf., London, June, 1933; retd., May, 1935.

BRAYNE, FRANK LUGARD, C.I.E., M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. rural reconstrn., Punjab) (b. 6th Jan., 1882).—Educ. at Monkton Combe Sch. and Pembroke Coll. Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sec. to Delhi munpl. cttee., June, 1910, to Sept., 1912; on military service from Dec., 1915 (M.C., March, 1919), to Nov., 1919; dep. commr., Jan., 1922; offg. commr., April, 1931; offg. commr., Oct., 1932; confd., July, 1935; commr. rural reconstruction, Oct., 1934; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

BRAYNE, WILLIAM FREDERIC, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Burma medl. dept. (b. 4th Mar., 1876).—First commn. dated 30th Jan., 1904; in mil. employ to 7th April, 1909; medl. offr., genl. plague duty, July, 1910; offg. dep. sany. commr., Burma, Aug., 1913; offg. civil surg., June, 1914; on mil. serv. from 26th Aug., 1914, to 28th April, 1919; civil surg., March, 1921; retd., Mar., 1931.

BRAYSHAY, SIR MAURICE WILLIAM, Kt., M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Railways (agent, B.B. and C.I. rly.) (b. 7th March, 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; dep. contr., timber supplies, Sept., 1917, to July, 1918; offg. asst. sec., rly. board, Oct., 1919; confd., Jan., 1920; services lent to B.B. & C.I. Rly. Co. from 30th March, 1924; offg. member, rly. bd., Mar., 1929; retd., Oct., 1932; offg. ch. commr. of rlys., May, 1933; member, council of state, Aug., 1933; knighted, June, 1934; offg. ch. commr. of rlys., July, 1935.

BREAKEY, ARTHUR WILLIAM HUMPHREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 23rd March, 1909).—Educated at the High School, Dublin, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, December, 1933, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

BREBNER, SIR ALEXANDER, C.I.E., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 19th Aug., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; under-sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., June, 1920; suptg. engr., Feb., 1926; with govt. of India, P.W.D., from Dec., 1919, and employed as suptg. engr., Simla impl. circle; offg. ch. engr., ditto, Apl., 1931; confd., Aug., 1933; knighted, Jan., 1938; retd., Aug., 1938.

BREE, PHILIP GEORGE, name changed to BRAYE (q.v.)

BREMNER, CLAUDE EDWARD URQUHART, M.C., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service) (b. 30th Aug., 1891).—First commn. dated 4th Mar., 1911; in milty. employ to July, 1919; asst. pol. agent, July, 1919; asst. commr., Dec., 1924; on foreign service in Baroda State from 17th May, 1927; pol. agent, South Waziristan, April, 1928; vice-consul, Ahwaz, May, 1932; pol. agent and consul, Muscat, Nov., 1932; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, Apl., 1936; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, Feb., 1937; offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, June, 1937; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Mar., 1938; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, June, 1938.

BRENDON, PATRICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 17th Mar., 1913).—Educ. at Tonbridge School, and Exeter College, Oxford; joined the service, 25th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BRERETON, CHARLES CECIL TRELAWNY, M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., N.W. Rly.) (b. 14th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1909; M.B.E., June, 1919; offg. dep. traffic manager, June, 1920; dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1923; dist. comml. officer, Oct., 1926; offg. divl. supt., June, 1927; confd., Nov., 1929.

BRETT, CYRIL TEMPLETON, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep., inspr.-gen., Bihar) (b. 23rd July, 1885).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1906; asst. supt., May, 1909; supt., Oct., 1915; prinl. police training coll., Hazaribagh, March, 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1930; confd., Mar., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1931; offg. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1936.

BRETT, JOHN ALOVSIOUS, C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 20th June, 1879).—First commn. dated 26th May, 1900; in mil. employ until Aug., 1906, when apptd. asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Baluchistan; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Sibi, Feb., 1907; asst. commr., Peshawar, Jan., 1908; asst. pol. agent, Dir and Swat, Nov., 1908; asst. commr., Thal, Feb., 1909; ditto, Kohat, March, 1909; ditto, Thal, Aug., 1909; ditto, Bannu, and commdt. B.M. police, Dec., 1910; asst. commr., Thal, July, 1911; ditto, Nowshera, Jan., 1913; ditto, Hazara, March, 1913; city mag., Peshawar, Nov., 1914; dist. judge, ditto, Jan., 1915; asst. commr., Mardan, May, 1915; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, May, 1917; asst. commr., Mardan, Aug., 1917; dep. commr., Hazara, Sept., 1918; asst. commr., Mardan, Oct., 1918; pol. offr., Dir, Swat and Chitral, Jan., 1919; jt. dep. commr., Peshawar, May, 1919; dep. commr., Kohat, June, 1919; pol. agent, Khyber, March, 1922; C.I.E., June, 1925; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1926; pol. agent and dep. commr. in Quetta Pishin, Jan., 1927; pol. agent in Kalat and pol. agent in charge of Bolan Pass, March, 1928; offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Feb., 1929, and again, Mar., 1931; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1930; offg. resdt., and A.G.G. and ch. commr., Baluchistan, May, 1932; ret'd., June, 1934.

BRETT, WILLIAM BAILIE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt., Bihar) (b. 27th Nov., 1889).—Educ. at Liverpool Coll., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1913, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., pol., appt., and educ. depts., Oct., 1917; ditto and pte. sec. to govr. of Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1920; sec., Indian taxation enquiry cttee., Oct., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1926; nag. and collr., Nov., 1928; secty., fin. dept., Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1933; relief commr., Feb., 1934; ch. secty. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1935; C.S.I., May, 1937.

BREWITT, CHARLES PATRICK, M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 17th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 2nd Jan., 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Rly., Mar., 1931; offg. dep. agent, Burma rlys., Apl., 1935.

BRIDGE, MAURICE FREDERICK, M.B.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 14th Mar., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1922; forest economist, Oct., 1925; M.B.E., May, 1937.

BRIDGES, Rev. FRANCIS LLEWELLYN, M.A., late Eccles. Dept., Central Provs. (b. 27th Mar., 1879).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 21st Oct., 1910; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1913; senr. chaplain, April, 1925; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

BRIDGMAN, FRANCIS HARRY GEORGE, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 14th Nov., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; offg. supt., June, 1935.

BRIDLE, HENRY LEWIS, India Office (higher clerical officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 1st May, 1892).—Apptd. clerical offr., 21st Oct., 1921; higher clerical officer, April, 1926.

BRIERLEY, Sir CHARLES ISHERWOOD, Kt., C.I.E., Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 24th Mar., 1879).—First commn. dated 31st Aug., 1903; in milty. employ to 18th April, 1907; offg. agency surg., Wana, April, 1907; ditto, Khyber agency, May, 1908; civil surg., Peshawar, May, 1914; chief medl. officer, N.W.F.P., Oct., 1927; C.I.E., Mar., 1929; inspr.-genl. of civilhosps., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1933; ret'd., Mar., 1936; Kt., Jan., 1936.

BRIGGS, HERBERT SAMUEL, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 22nd Dec., 1881).—Apptd. clerk, second class, 25th June, 1900; minor staff clerk, May, 1920; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; actg. higher exec. officer, Dec., 1921; confd., Apl., 1922.

BRIGGS, NORMAN, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail dept., Sind (dir.) (b. 18th Nov., 1891).—First commn. dated 8th Aug., 1917; in milty. employ to 24th Mar., 1925; supt., borstal institution, Lahore, April, 1925; offg. dep. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Punjab, Mar., 1932; dir. of health and prisons, Sind, Apl., 1936.

BRIGGS, ROBERT CHAPMAN, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 4th Sept., 1882).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service 1st Oct., 1905; asst. engr., Nov., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1926; offg. divl. supt., E.I. rly., July, 1928; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

BRIGSTOCKE, FRANK LEWIS, late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 4th Sept., 1885).—Educ. at Llandovery Coll., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, Nov., 1910, and served in Madras as asst. collr., and mag.; under-sec. to govt., March, 1915; 1st asst. commr. and dist. mag., Coorg, Nov., 1915, to Nov., 1920; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1921; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1924; confd., Feb., 1930; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

BRIJ BHUSAN SARAN JETLY, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 10th July, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 21st July, 1924; offg. supt., Nov., 1932; confd., Jan., 1934.

BRIJ BHUSHAN SARAN VARMA, B.A., LL.B., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 10th June, 1908).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 25th Jan., 1932; confd., Jan., 1934.

BRIJ LAL, RAI BAHADUR, Babu, B.A., late United Provs. Civil Service (b. 25th Nov., 1875).—Joined the service, 17th July, 1899, as dep. collr.; mag. and collr., May, 1927; offg. dep. dir. of land records and inspr.-gen. of regist., United Provs.; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

BRIJ MOHAN LAL, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 13th Sept., 1898).—Joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1922; offg. exec. engr., May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1931.

BRIJ NARAYAN, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 10th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. exec. engr., April, 1925; confd., Oct., 1926.

BRIJ NARAYAN, M.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 18th Aug., 1895).—Held non-gaz. appts., May, 1918 to Apl., 1923; apptd. officr. cl. II, Apl., 1923.

BRISCOE, HUGH KYNASTON, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th Sept., 1879).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 27th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfld. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; settld. officer, Oct., 1906; under-sec. to govt., jud. and gen. dep., Feb., 1909; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1912; transfld. to Bihar, April, 1912; on duty as asst. to dir. of temp. works, Delhi, Feb., 1912; spl. offr., temp. capital, Delhi, and sec., notified area cttee., April to May, 1913; sec., board of rev., Bihar and Orissa, March, 1914; mag. and collr., and ditto. March, 1916; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1919; C.I.E., June, 1921; sec. to govt. of Bihar and Orissa, fin. dept., April, 1924; offg. chief sec. to govt., April, 1926; confd., Nov., 1927; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, June, 1930; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

BRISLEE, FRANK RICHARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 3rd Dec., 1895).—Educ. at King's Coll. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1921; arrived, 12th Dec., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1923; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1928; spl. rev. offr., Cauvery-Mettur project, May, 1930; offg. collr., Bangalore, Apl., 1935; secty., bd. of rev., Madras, Mar., 1936.

BRISTOW, CHARLES HOLDITCH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (Commr. Bombay) (b. 28th Dec., 1887).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1911, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., and supt., land records, &c.; on mil. duty, May, 1915, to April, 1919; actg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agt., May, 1920; settlement officer, Dec., 1923; dep. sec. to govt., genl., edl. and marine depts. (temp.), Nov., 1925; offg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, Dec., 1926, and again, April, 1928; on special duty, Deccan canals, June, 1927; collr., Sept., 1929; offg. secty. to govt., Bombay, home and eccl. depts., Jan., 1935; secty. to govt. of Bombay, Apl., 1935 to Aug., 1938; C.I.E., May, 1937; commr. (confd.), Dec., 1937.

BROACH, FRANK ERNEST, Indian Police (b. 2nd Feb., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on probn., Nov., 1938.

BROADBENT, ARTHUR HENRY, B.Sc., A.I.C., India Audit Office (exec. offcr.) (b. 30th May, 1913).—Educ. at Whitgift Sch. and

Birkbeck Coll., London Univ.; apptd. exec. officer, 21st Aug., 1933, after previous service at Home Office.

BROADBENT, ROBERT EVELYN CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Dec., 1909).—Educated at Charterhouse and Magdalen College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

BROADFOOT, ROBERT CAMPBELL, N.D.A., Indian Agric. Service (princl., agric. coll., Coimbatore) (b. 5th Feb., 1887).—Apptd. dep. dir. of agric., June, 1919; offg. prinpl., Agricl. College, Coimbatore, Jan., 1934; confd., June, 1935.

BROADWAY, SIR ALAN BRICE, Kt., Barr., late Judl. Dept., Punjab (b. 2nd Nov., 1873).—Apptd. asst. legal remembrancer, Punjab, 13th June, 1905; public prosecutor, ditto, Dec., 1905; asst. legal remembr., ditto, April, 1909; addl. govt. advocate, ditto, March, 1915; again asst. legal remembr., Aug., 1915; addl. govt. advocate and offl. trustee, Punjab, Oct., 1915, to March, 1916; temp. addl. judge, chief ct., Punjab, May, 1916; administr.-gen. and official trustee, Punjab, Aug., 1916; temp. addl. judge, chief ct., Punjab, from Oct., 1916, to Aug., 1917, and from Oct., 1917; judge, high ct., Punjab, April, 1919; acting chief justice, June, 1927, and Oct., 1932; Kt., June, 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

BROCK, HENRY NORMAN, Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., G.I.P., Rly.) (b. 17th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 13th Mar., 1914; asst. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; offg. divl. ditto, variously from Mar., 1927; divl. supt. confd., Jan., 1937.

BROCK, LESLIE EVELYN, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 13th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1908; asst. loco. supt., Nov., 1908; dist. ditto, April, 1915; dist. loco. officer, Dec., 1926; divl. operating officer, Dec., 1926; offg. divl. supt., April, 1928; confd., Feb., 1931; ditto (senr.), Apl., 1934; dep. ch. operating supt., N.W. Rly. (provl.), May, 1934; dep. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1936.

BROCKLEHURST, WALTER HARRISON, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 7th Sept., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 21st April, 1909; resdt. engr., Sept., 1912; divl. engr., Nov., 1926; offg. dep. ch. engr., Aug., 1933; ret'd., Apl., 1936.

BRODIE, NEAL, M.Sc., F.C.S., A.I.C. (b. 11th July, 1893).—Joined the service, 27th Dec., 1913, as asst. chemist, Govt. Test House, Alipore; chemist, Dec., 1918; supt., Govt. Test House, Aug., 1925; dir., Industrial Research Bureau (temp.), Nov., 1934; confd., Mar., 1938.

BROKENSHAW, ARTHUR, India Posts and Telegraphs (chief engr., p. and t.) (b. 8th Nov., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1906; supt., Apl., 1920; contr., telegraph office, Mar., 1927; supt., Calcutta tel. office, May, 1929; ch. supt., Bombay, tele. office, May, 1929; divl. engr., and offg. dir. of engrg., Mar., 1931; confd., Sept., 1932; offg. p.m.g., Madras, Apl., 1934;

dir. tel. engrg., Bombay, Sept., 1934; offg. p.m.g., Bombay, Mar., 1935; p.m.g., Madras, June, 1935; dep. dir-gen., tels., Apl., 1936. chief engr., posts and tels., May, 1938.

BROMAGE, JOHN ALDHELM RAIKES, Public Works Dept., Punjab (exec. sany. engr.) (b. 7th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. sany. engr., 25th Dec., 1920; exec. sany. engr., June, 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1933; suptg. engr., health (temp.), Aug., 1935.

BROMAGE, JAMES VINCENT BENNETT RAIKES, late Indian Police Service (b. 22nd Jan., 1883).—Joined the service, 17th Dec., 1903, as asst. supt.; supt., July, 1912; on mil. service, Sept., 1916, to April, 1919; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1924, and again, April, 1926; confd., July, 1927; ret'd., Jan., 1931.

BROMLEY, RALPH EDWARD, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 12th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Nov., 1912; dist. supt., Sept., 1924.

BROMMAGE, JOSEPH CHARLES, M.B.E., Indian Finance Dept.—Dep. fin. advr., mil. finance, May, 1933; controller of mil. accounts, Burma, Apl., 1937.

BROOKES, ALBERT, M.A. (Cantab.), Indian Educl. Service (lecturer, Univ. Coll., Rangoon) (b. 22nd Aug., 1885).—Joined the service as a lecturer in history, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, on 6th Sept., 1922; prinpl., inter. coll., Mandalay, Nov., 1925; lecturer, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, April, 1927.

BROOKS, JOHN BIRTWHISTLE TYRRELL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Bombay) (b. 13th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1912, as asst. consvr.; on milty. duty from 8th Nov., 1917, to 24th Nov., 1918; dep. consvr., Dec., 1917; offg. consvr., Feb., 1934; and again, Nov., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937.

BROOME, HAROLD HOLKAR, C.I.E., M.B., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 6th March, 1875).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1903; in mil. employ until Oct., 1907, when he was apptd. asst. plague med. officer, Rawalpindi; prof. of anatomy, Lahore Med. Coll., July, 1908; on mil. duty from Aug., 1915, to Nov., 1918; medl. supt., Mayo Hospital, Lahore, June, 1922; prinpl., ditto, April, 1923; principal, King Edward Medical Coll., Lahore, June, 1926; C.I.E., Mar., 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1930.

BROOME, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 18th March, 1910).—Educated at Latymer Upper School, Hammersmith, and Caius College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, November, 1932, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Nov., 1936.

BROOMFIELD, ROBERT STONEHOUSE, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct. Bombay) (b. 1st Dec., 1882).—Educ. at City of London Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 25th Nov., 1905, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and chief inspr. of factories; asst. judge and sessions judge, Nov., 1909; jt. judge and addl. sess. judge, June, 1913; offg. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1919; confirmed, March, 1925; judge, high ct., Bombay, Nov., 1929.

BROUGHTON, Rev. ARTHUR HARDWICKE, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 18th Oct., 1881).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 16th April, 1917; junr. chaplain, April, 1919; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1929; ret'd., Oct., 1936.

BROWN, ALAN SUNLEY, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 15th Feb., 1893).—Apptd. to store dept., Dec., 1921; clerical officer, Feb., 1924; exec. officer, Jan., 1926.

BROWN, ARTHUR EDWARD, M.A., LL.B., Barr., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 5th Apl., 1884).—Apptd. prof. Cotton Coll., Gauhati, Assam, 13th March, 1914; on spl. duty in legisl. dept. of govt. of India from 25th Feb., 1920, to 30th March, 1923; ret'd., Apl., 1935.

BROWN, ARTHUR FREDERICK ROBERT, M.C., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 9th March, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 18th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Aug., 1925.

BROWN, CAMPBELL OSBORNE, Indian State Rlys. (works mang., E.I. Rly.) (b. 11th Feb., 1895).—Production Engr., Jan., 1928; works manager, Senr. scale, Jan., 1937.

BROWN, CHARLES HILTON, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st March, 1890).—Educ. at St. Andrew's Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1913, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Dec., 1917; under-sec. to govt., home dept., Oct., 1918; first asst. commr. and dist. mag., Coorg, May, 1920; offg. commr. of Coorg, May, 1921, to Jan., 1922; offg. spl. offr., chief secretariat, May, 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Oct., 1924; ditto, June, 1925; offg. collr., June, 1926; offg. secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Dec., 1928; collr., June, 1933; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

BROWN, GILBERT ALEXANDER MURRAY, O.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 24th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service 1st Oct., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; asst. exec. engr., May, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; on spl. duty in Waziristan survey circle, May, 1929.

BROWN, HAROLD ARROWSMITH, B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th June, 1879).—Educ. at Plymouth and Mannamed Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 28th Nov., 1903, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt., Dec., 1906; under-sec., Oct., 1908, to Feb., 1910; dep. commr., Aug., 1916; dist. judge, Aug., 1916; offg. divl. judge, Oct., 1917; addl. judge, chief court of Upper Burma, July, 1920; actg. judge, high ct., Rangoon, April, 1924, and again, March, 1926; confd., Jan., 1927; ret'd., Nov., 1933.

BROWN, HUBERT ARNOLD, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th May, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 14th March, 1910; dist. ditto, Sept., 1915; divl. rolling stock officer, Nov., 1923; ret'd., June, 1934.

BROWN, JAMES BIRCH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 18th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 25th Nov., 1913, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mily. duty, Jan., 1918, to March, 1919; sub-collr. and jt.

mag. (seconded), Feb., 1918; temp. under-sec. to govt., June, 1919; offg. sec., board of rev. (separate rev.), July, 1922; dep. sec. to govt., finance dept., Aug., 1923; offg. sec. to ditto, April, 1925; dep. sec. to ditto, June, 1925; offg. collr. of salt rev., March, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. collr. and dist. mag., June, 1932; confd., July, 1933; offg. commr. of lab., Madras, May, 1934 to Aug., 1934.

BROWN, JOHN, late Indian State Ry's. (b. 4th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. colliery manager, 7th Feb., 1912; colliery manager, April, 1915; asst. supt., April, 1923; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

BROWN, JOHN COGGIN, O.B.E., D.Sc., F.G.S., M.I.M.E., late Indian Geological Survey (b. 24th Sept., 1884).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1905, as asst. supt.; O.B.E., June, 1918; supt., June, 1921; ret'd., July, 1934.

BROWN, LESLIE NANSCAWEN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th Nov., 1886).—Educ. at Plymouth Coll., and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1911, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; persl. asst. to agent to govr., Kathiawar, Dec., 1914; on military duty from May, 1915; att'd. to civil admin., Mesopotamia, from May, 1917; pol. offr., Qurnah divn., April to Sept., 1919; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1923; ditto and pol. agent, March, 1925; collr., Aug., 1929; pol. agent, Surgana, Oct., 1931; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

BROWN, RICHARD HAROLD, India Office (higher clerical officer, milty. dept.) (b. 27th Apr., 1882).—Appointed clerical officer, 10th Mar., 1922; higher clerical officer, Sept., 1932.

BROWN, RALPH ROBERTS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 8th July, 1881).—Educ. at Plymouth Coll., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 9th Dec., 1905, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. registrar, chf. court, Lower Burma, from March, 1914, to Jan., 1917; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; offg. commr., July, 1926; confd., Aug., 1930; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

BROWN, ROBERT SIDNEY, India Office (principal, finl. dept.) (b. 10th Feb., 1889).—Apptd. second-class clerk, India Office, 1st March, 1911, and att'd. to the registry and record dept.; transf'd. to mil. dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service from Dec., 1915, to Jan., 1919; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; asst. principal, April, 1921; temp. reading clerk to council, June, 1926—Aug., 1927; jt. editor, India Office List, Aug., 1927 to Oct., 1932; actg. princpl., Oct., 1932; confd., Dec., 1934.

BROWN, STUART KELSON, C.B., C.V.O., India Office (asst. under-secty. of state) (b. 24th Dec., 1885).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge (Porson Prize, 1906); apptd. junr. clerk, store dept., India Office, 11th Oct., 1909; transf'd. to judl. and public dept., March, 1910; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, Oct., 1911; pte. sec. to parl. under-sec. of state, July, 1913; senr. clerk, March, 1918; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Feb., 1919 (Italian Silver Medal for military valour, March, 1917; mentioned in despatches July, 1917); asst. sec. to Lord Crewe's commee. on the India Office, March, 1919; actg. pte. sec. to sec. of state, June,

1919; pte. sec. to sec. of state, June, 1919, to Sept., 1924; principal, April, 1921; joint sec., mil. dept., Sept., 1924; C.V.O., Jan., 1925; personally attached to Prime Minister for Indian Round Table Conf., London, Nov., 1930 to Jan., 1931; sec.-genl. to Indian del. to Disarmament conf., Jan.-July, 1932; and again Jan., 1933; C.B., June, 1932; actg. asst. under-secty. of state, Sept., 1934; confd., Dec., 1934.

BROWN, WILLIAM SCOTT, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and magt., Madras (b. 30th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and University, Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 12th Dec., 1914; and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., home dept., April, 1920; offg. sec., board of rev., Madras, March, 1924; offg. collr. of Madras, etc., Aug., 1927; offg. collr. and dist. magt., Nov., 1928; collr. and magt. (provl. subs.), Sept., 1932; collr., Dec., 1933; secty. to govt., p.w. and lab. dept., Madras, Jan., 1935, to Oct. 1937; C.I.E., June, 1936.

BROWNE, CECIL-ARTHUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 17th Dec., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 1st Nov., 1927; offg. exec. engr., Aug., 1934; confd., Oct., 1936.

BROWNE, DENIS ROBERT HOWE, O.B.E., A.K.C. (Lon.), M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 19th Dec., 1879).—Apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1903; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; under-sec. to govt., p.w.d. (rds. and bds.), Bombay, Feb., 1915; on mil. duty from Aug., 1916, to July, 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; Army rank, Lt.-Col. I.A.R.O.; offg. suptg. engr., Jan., 1926; offg. ch. engr. and jt. sec. to govt., P.W.D., Bombay, Dec., 1926; ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Nov., 1928; confd., July, 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

BROWNE, ERNEST GARDINER, Opium Dept., United Provs. (dist. opium officer) (b. 8th May, 1884).—Joined the service as proby. asst. opium agent, 23rd April, 1906; confd., Oct., 1907; dist. opium officer, April, 1925.

BROWNE, HORACE FAIRFAX MORESBY, Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Burma Commn. (b. 14th Dec., 1876).—Arrived in India, 17th March, 1897; served in mil. dept. to July, 1908; asst. commr., Jan., 1911; on mil. service, Dec., 1914, to March, 1919; offg. dep. commr., March, 1920; dep. commr. (provl. sub.), March, 1926; offg. commr., July, 1928; ret'd., Feb., 1930.

BROWNE, ROBERT STEEL, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 31st July, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926; pers. asst. to chief consvr., June, 1938.

BROWNE, WALTER FITZGERALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs. (b. 3rd Mar., 1897).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1921; arrived 3rd Dec., 1921; asst. mag. and collr., United Provs., 3rd Dec., 1921; asst. commr. from Oct., 1923, to May, 1924; jt. mag., Nov., 1927; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1930; offg. magt. and collr., Feb., 1935; confd., Apr., 1935.

BROWNLOW, ANDREW L'ESTRANGE, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 22nd Jan., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 19th Feb., 1921; dist. supt., Jan., 1924.

BRUCE, CHARLES EDWARD, C.S.I., C.I.E., C.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 23rd Mar., 1876).—First commn. dated 5th Sept., 1896; in mil. employ to Nov., 1901, when he was attached to for. dept.; pol. asst., Oct., 1902; city mag., Peshawar, Sept., 1911; dist. judge, Peshawar, April to Oct., 1912; asst. commr., Oct., 1913; asst. pol. agent, Sibi, Oct., 1914; ditto, Loralai, Oct., 1915; pol. agent, Zhob, Dec., 1916; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Oct., 1917; pol. agent, ditto, Jan., 1918; spl. duty as pol. offir. with Marri Punitive Force, March to June, 1918; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; dep. commr., Kohat, May, 1921; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Oct., 1924; C.B.E., June, 1925; offg. resident in Waziristan, Sept., 1926; C.S.I., March, 1929; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Sept., 1929; offg. ch. commr., Baluchistan, Oct., 1930 to March, 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1931.

BRUCE, ERNEST HENRY, late Indian Police Service (United Provs.) (b. 17th Oct., 1881).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1901, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt. of police, Oct., 1906; supt., May, 1914; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1925; confid., Sept., 1930; offg., inspr.-genl. of rly. police, etc., Rajputana, Mar., 1930; offg. inspr.-genl. Mar., 1931; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

BRUCE, HAROLD EASTON, M.C., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 18th Sept., 1897). Joined the service as asst. supt. on 20th Mar., 1921; offg. supt., Oct., 1923; confid., Mar., 1931; supt., police training coll., Feb., 1931; cent. intell. offcr., B. and O., Mar., 1938.

BRUCE-JOY, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (transportation supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 23rd Mar., 1891).—Joined the service as dist. loco. supt., 22nd May, 1920; asst. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; supt., May, 1926; offg. divl. ditto, variously from March, 1927.

BRUCE-KERR, Rev. GEORGE HENRY, B.A., late Eccles. Estabt. (b. 1st Mar., 1880).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 9th Oct., 1910; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1913; resigned on appt. to I.A.R.O. on 18th Sept., 1915; reappointed and arrived at Rangoon on 8th Dec., 1919; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1924; ret'd., Sept., 1934.

BRUEN, EDWARD JOHN, Indian Agric. Service (live stock expert to govt. of Bombay) (b. 16th July, 1886).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 14th July, 1914, to 15th Sept., 1919; dep. dir. of agric. for animal breeding, Sept., 1919; live stock expert, Aug., 1924.

BRUNYATE, Sir JAMES BENNETT, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (ret'd.).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 9th Dec., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settl. officer, and asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., June, 1896; under sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., Nov., 1897; asst. commr., March, 1901; joint mag., Nov., 1901; dep. commr., March, 1904; dept. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., July, 1905; temp. joint sec., mil. fin. branch, April, 1907; specl. duty with International Opium Cttee., Oct., 1908, to Feb., 1909; reverted to employment of govt. of United Prov.,

Jan., 1909; joint sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., mil. fin. branch, Nov., 1909; C.I.E., Jan., 1910; mag. and collr., Nov., 1912; offg. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1913; addl. member, govr.-gen. 's council, May, 1913; sec. to govt. of India fin. dept. April, 1914; C.S.I., June, 1915; ret'd. from Indian C.S., May, 1917; member of the Council of India, from May, 1917, to Sept., 1924; K.C.S.I., July, 1918; member, Indian constitutional relations commee., 1919; member, commee. on Indian exchange and currency, 1919.

BRYAN, ALAN FRANCIS, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 26th Apr., 1882).—Joined the service, 10th Apr., 1902; dist. opium officer, Aprl., 1925; ret'd., July, 1934.

BRYAN, DENZIL ARNOLD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 15th Oct., 1909).—Educated at the Lawrence College, Ghora Gali, University of the Punjab and Selwyn College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

BRYANT, STANLEY CLARE, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 13th Mar., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 11th May, 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Rlys., Aprl., 1931.

BRYANT, WALTER TELHAM, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 12th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1929; arrived Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag.; sub-collr., Aprl., 1931; pte. secty. to govr., Madras, June, 1938.

BRYSON, CHRISTOPHER LENWOOD, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 9th Nov., 1908).—Educated at Dulwich College and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.

BU, Sir MYA, Barr., Judl. Dept., Burma (ch. justice, high ch., Rangoon) (b. 10th Mar., 1885).—Apptd. asst. gov. advocate, March, 1924; addl. judge, high ct., June, 1926; judge, ditto, Dec., 1926; ch. justice, Nov., 1935; knighted, Feb., 1937.

BU, U. KLA (A.1), late Burma Civil Service (b. 21st June, 1876).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 18th Feb., 1903; extra asst. commr., Feb., 1917; offg. dep. commr., June, 1929; confid., July, 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

BUCH, NILKANTHRAI MOHANLAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 9th July, 1908).—Educ. at School of Economics and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 12th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1935.

BUCHANAN, Rev. GEORGE, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 11th July, 1906).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Church of Scotland, July, 1934; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1936.

BUCKLAND, Sir PHILIP LINDSAY, Kt., Barr. (b. 12th Sept., 1874).—Apptd. judge of the High Court, Calcutta, 10th Nov., 1919; Kt., July, 1926; actg. ch. justice Feb., 1934; ret'd., Sept., 1934.

BUCKLEY, HUGH CLIVE, M.D., F.R.C.S.E., Major-Genl., I.M.S. (surg.-genl. with the Govt. of Bombay) (b. 31st Oct., 1880).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1905; in mil. employ till Nov., 1907, and from April, 1909, to Aug., 1911; plague medl. officer, Punjab, Sept., 1911; on mil. service, Aug., 1914 to Apl., 1921; civil surgeon, United Prov., April, 1921; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hosps., United Provs., Mar., 1933; confd., Dec., 1933, ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1933; ditto, United Provs., Apl., 1935; surg.-genl. with the Govt. of Bombay, Mar., 1937.

BUCKNER, CEDRIC STANLEY, late Indian Police Service (b. 26th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 7th Sept., 1921; addl. supt., Feb., 1930; ret'd., June, 1931.

BUCKNER, JOHNSTONE STANLEY, M.B.E., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 1st June, 1873).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1893; on deputation with I.E.F., Mesopotamia, from 11th Sept., 1916; presdy. postmaster, Bombay, Aug., 1922; ret'd., Aug., 1929.

BUCKNEY, FRANK, B.A., late Imperial Customs Service (b. 1st Jan., 1883).—Joined the service as prob., 17th Feb., 1908; asst. collr., Dec., 1911; offg. collr., Madras, Feb., 1930; ditto, Karachi, Apr., 1931; ditto, Rangoon, Oct., 1932; ditto, Karachi, Mar., 1933; ret'd., Jan., 1938.

BUCKWELL, Rev. Canon FREDERICK CHARLES, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt.—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 11th Jan., 1904; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1907; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1917; ret'd., Jan., 1929.

BUDD, BERNARD WILFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 18th Dec., 1912).—Educated at West Leeds High School and Pembroke College, Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Bombay as asst. commr.

BUDDEN, FRANCIS HENRY, M.C., Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 4th Mar., 1887).—First commn. dated 24th July, 1907; joined State Rlys. on 15th April, 1912; on milty. duty from 22nd Oct., 1914; asst. traffic supt., April, 1915; asst. agent, N.W. Rly., Sept., 1919; dep. dir., statistics, April, 1924; supt., E.I. Rly., Nov., 1926; on spl. duty with Rly. bd., April, 1928; offg. ch. publicity officer, Sept., 1928; confd., Nov., 1930; divl. supt., July, 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1933.

BUDH SINGH, SARDAR, Indian Police (b. 20th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Punjab, 27th Jan., 1929; confd., Jan., 1931.

BUDHI SINGH MIAN, RAI SAHIB, late Indian Forest Service (Punjab) (b. 20th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service 12th June, 1904, and held non-gazetted appts. to 12th May, 1913; extra asst. consvr., Nov., 1914; apptd. to I.F.S. as asst. consvr., from 23rd May, 1921; dep. consvr., May, 1922; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

BULKELEY, JOHN PIERSON, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service. (b. 17th Jan., 1879).—Arrived in India, Feb., 1907, and apptd. prin. Rangoon Collegiate and Normal Schools; inspr. of schools, Oct., 1913; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Burma, Feb., 1923; offg. dir., ditto, April, 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

BULKLEY, ALFRED FRANCIS, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 9th Mar., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1908; asst. supt., Jan., 1911; supt., Nov., 1921; awarded King's police medal, Feb., 1917; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1935; confd., July, 1936; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1936.

BULL, Rev. FRANCIS FAULKENER, late Indian Eccl. Estab. (Madras) (b. 9th Mar., 1878).—Joined the service 11th Oct., 1909; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1912; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1922; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

BULL, HERBERT GEORGE, India Office (suptg. executive officer, mil. dept.) (b. 13th Feb., 1882).—Educ. at Owen's Gr. Sch., Islington; served as sec. divn. clerk, War Office, from April, 1900; apptd. 2nd class clerk, acct.-genl.'s dept., 17th April, 1902; transfld. to registry and record dept., July, 1906; temply. attached to milty. dept., Jan., 1917; minor staff clerk, April, 1918; staff clerk (temp.), Oct., 1919; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; actg. suptg. exec. officer, Mar., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; jt. editor, India Office List, May to Nov., 1934.

BULL, LOUIS ALBERT, late Punjab Civil Service. (b. 21st Oct., 1875).—Joined the service 1st Dec., 1896; extra asst. commr., Dec., 1908; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1926; dep. commr., Oct., 1927; ret'd. July, 1931.

BULL, WALTER, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 9th Feb., 1882).—Apptd. asst. elec. engr., N.W. rly., 1st May, 1921; elec. engr., Mar., 1931; offg. ch. elec. engr., N.W. rly., Nov., 1933; ret'd., Feb., 1937.

BUNN, ARTHUR GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 29th Jan., 1911).—Educated at Rutlish School, Merton, and St. Catherine's College, Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1935 and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

BUNYAD HUSSAIN, KHAN BAHADUR SAYAD, B.A., Punjab C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 2nd Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 13th May, 1912; extra asst. commr., Nov., 1913; dep. commr., confd., July, 1934.

BURBIDGE, HERBERT FREDERICK GEORGE, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 25th July, 1904).—Joined the service, Dec., 1923; and deputed to police training coll., Sardah, Bengal; proby. asst. supt., Oct., 1925; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. supt., Dec., 1932; confd., Apl., 1935.

BURDON, Sir ERNEST, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (auditor-genl. in India) (b. 27th Jan., 1881).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, July, 1912; acctnt.-gen., ditto, Oct., 1913; under sec., fin. dept., govt. of India, July, 1914; addl. dep. finl. adviser, mil. fin. branch, June, 1916; finl. adviser, Mesopotamia expedy. force, Dec., 1918, to May, 1919; temp. finl. adviser, mil. fin. branch, and member, Indian munitions bd., July, 1919; C.I.E., June, 1921; sec. to govt. of India, army dept., Jan., 1923; C.S.I., Jan., 1926; sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Aug., 1927;

auditor-genl. in India, Nov., 1929; Kt., June, 1931; K.C.I.E., June, 1934; Kt. of Grace of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, June, 1936.

BURDWAN, Sir BIJAY CHAND MAHTUB, Maharajadhiraja Bahadur of, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., I.O.M.—Member of Imperial legisl. council, 1909–1912; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1911; Bengal legisl. council, 1907–1918; temp. member of Bengal exec. council, May, 1918; confd., Jan., 1919; resigned, April, 1924; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1924. Literary works:—‘Vijaya Gitika,’ and other Bengal poetical works and dramas; ‘Impressions’ (the diary of a European tour); ‘Meditations,’ &c.

BURFORD, WALTER BECKWITH, Indian State Railways (exec. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 8th Oct., 1888).—Joined the service, 29th Dec., 1911; resdt. engr., G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1920; offg. supt. of statistics, July, 1929; exec. engr., May, 1932; offg. dep. ch. engr., bridges, G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1935.

BURGESS, FREDERICK GEORGE, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 1st Oct., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 13th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

BURGESS, GEOFFREY, Indian C.S. (sub-judge, Central Provs.) (b. 29th April, 1906).—Educ. at Hanley High Sch., Stoke-on-Trent, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, 12th Nov., 1928, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. sub-judge, Dec., 1931; regr., judl. commr.’s ct., Apl., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935; confd. as sub-judge, Jan., 1935.

BURKE, EDMUND PLUNKETT, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th June, 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. supt. engr., May, 1928; confd., Oct., 1932; offg. secty., P.W. dept., Assam; offg. ch. engr., Nov., 1931; confd., Dec., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; retd., July, 1938.

BURKE, GERALD TYLER, C.I.E., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 31st May, 1882).—First commn. dated 31st July, 1909; in mil. employ to 15th Feb., 1924; profir., King George’s medl. coll., Lucknow, Feb., 1924; civil surg., Nov., 1925; employed under Lucknow Univ., Apl., 1929; secty., India Medl. Council, May, 1935; retd., May, 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

BURKE, Sir RICHARD JOHN CHARLES, Kt., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd. (b. 5th May, 1878).—First commn., dated 22nd Jan., 1898; in mil. employ to Oct., 1900; asst. pol. agent, Bombay, Oct., 1902; admr. Sangli State, Feb., 1905–June, 1910; special duty in connection with visit of Prince of Wales, Oct. to Nov., 1905, and with visit of the Amir, Jan. to Feb., 1907; adviser to H.H. the Raja of Devas (junior branch) from Oct., 1911; on mil. duty from March, 1915; temp. asst. sec. to govt. of India, Army dept., April to Aug., 1915; vice-pres. council of administration, Bhavnagar, from 1st Nov., 1920, to 4th March, 1927; offg. resident, Baroda, March, 1927; ditto, Mysore and ch. commr., Coorg, June, 1930; confd., Aug., 1931; Kt., Jan., 1933; retd., Sept., 1933.

BURKE, SAMUEL MARTIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 3rd July, 1906).—Educ. at Punjab University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service

12th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1936.

BURKITT, FRANCIS HOLY, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 5th July, 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1903; asst. engr., Punjab, Jan., 1904; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; O.B.E., June, 1918; offg. supt. engr., Dec., 1923; confd., Sept., 1926; secty., irrigation bch., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. ch. engr., April, 1931; retd., July, 1935.

BURLINGHAM, GEORGE, late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 9th Sept., 1879).—Joined the service as asst. supt., telegraphs, 1st Oct., 1903; deputed to the N.-W. Rly. from Sept., 1914; divl. engr., Shillong, Dec., 1920; ditto, Delhi, July, 1922; divl. engr., tels., and dep. postmr.-gen., Sind and Baluchistan circle, June, 1925; retd., Jan., 1930.

BURMAN, HAROLD PERCY, Indian State Rlys. (dist. elec. engr., E.B. rly.) (b. 25th June, 1896).—Joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1923; offg. dist. elec. engr., E.B. rly., Jan., 1929; confd., Mar., 1933.

BURMAN, RAM PRASAD (see Ram Prasad B.).

BURN, DUGALD STUART, C.I.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 21st July, 1877).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 14th Nov., 1901; dist. ditto, Jan., 1907; dep. traffic manager, May, 1918; genl. traffic manager, Oct., 1921; ch. traffic manager, Nov., 1922; offg. agent, Aug., 1928; confd., Dec., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; retd., Sept., 1933.

BURN, Rev. JOHN HUMPHREY, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 6th May, 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 29th Sept., 1924; junr. chaplain, Sept., 1926; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1933.

BURN, Sir RICHARD, Kt., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at the Liverpool Inst., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1891; and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., June, 1896; under-sec. to govt., Nov., 1897; supt. of census operations, April, 1900; supt. of revision of Imperial Gaz., Oct., 1902; dep. commr., May, 1904, and ditto; editor of Imperial Gaz. for India, Feb., 1905; mag. and coll., July, 1907; K-i-H. medal, 1st class, Jan., 1909; sec. to govt., United Prov., Oct., 1910; chief sec., April, 1912, to Feb., 1918; C.S.I., Jan., 1917; temp. employed in finl. dept., India Office, from March to May, 1918; commr., Nov., 1918; member board of revenue, May, 1923; temp. member govt.’s exec. council, United Prov., 5th Dec., 1925; ditto, Aug., 1926; Kt., Jan., 1927; retd., Jan., 1927; author of ‘United Prov. Census Report, 1901,’ ‘United Prov. Gazetteer,’ and part editor of 3rd edn., ‘Imperial Gazetteer.’

BURN, SIDNEY, Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. 19th June, 1881).—Educ. at Wakefield Gr. Sch., and Queen’s Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived 11th Dec., 1905, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., March, 1913; supt., Pudukkottai State, from Oct., 1915, to Oct., 1922; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1925; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1932; Oct., 1932; Jan., 1933, and again July, 1933; confd., June, 1934.

BURN, WILLIAM GEORGE, O.B.E., V.D., B.Sc., late Indian State Ryls. (b. 8th May, 1878).—Apptd. 2nd asst. contr. of stores, 11th Jan., 1908; storekeeper, govt. of India, marine dept., Aug., 1916; dep. asst. dir., inland water transport (stores), Jan., 1918; 1st asst. contr. of stores, Sept., 1919; spl. stores officer, rly. bd., Feb., 1925; dist. contr. of stores, E.I. Rly., July, 1925; offg. dep. ditto, N.W. Rly., Nov., 1928; and again, Apl., 1932; ret'd., June, 1934.

BURNAND, WILLIAM HENRY, V.D., late Indian State Ryls. (b. 19th Aug., 1880).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 19th Sept., 1897 to 14th Sept., 1910; and again from 19th Nov., 1910, to 3rd March, 1912; asst. supt., March, 1912; dist. supt., April, 1919; coal manager, Feb., 1923; dep. ch. operating supt., Feb., 1925; offg. divl. supt., March 1926; confd., Oct., 1927; offg. ch. operatg. supt., Jan., 1931; confd., June, 1933; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

BURNE, LINDSAY ELIOTT LUMLEY, C.I.E., C.B.E., Lieut.-Col. I.A. (ret'd.) late Burma commn. (b. 1st July, 1877).—Arrived in India, Dec., 1897, and rendered services in the Mily. Dept.; asst. commdt., Burma Mounted Police, July, 1902; asst. commr., June, 1905; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; commdt., Burma mily. police, May, 1918, to May, 1919; C.B.E., June, 1919; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1920; confd., July, 1921; C.I.E., Mar., 1929; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

BURNETT, ROBERT RICHARDSON, O.B.E., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 22nd Oct., 1897).—First commn. dated 3rd July, 1915; in milty. employ prior to 16th Mar., 1922; adjutant, Zhow levy. Corps, March, 1922; probnr. in pol. dept., Feb., 1925; asst. pol. agent, Mekran, May, 1926; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1927; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat, Sept., 1929; pol. agent, Zhow, Oct., 1929; secty. to agent to G.G. and C.C., Baluchistan, Oct., 1930; O.B.E., June, 1931; offg. dep. secty., for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1932; and again, May, 1933; offg. under secty., ditto, Feb., 1933; princpl. officer and adviser, Tonk state, Apl., 1934; pol. agent, Punjab Hill States, Oct., 1936.

BURNS, FRANCIS GILBERT, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 9th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 25th July, 1912; asst. exec. engr. and offg. exec. engr., July, 1923; confd., Oct., 1928; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

BURNS, HENRY GEORGE, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 29th Apl., 1903).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 6th Nov., 1924; asst. exec. engr., 5th Sept., 1926; offg. exec. engr., Mar., 1931; confd., Oct., 1934; p.a. to ch. engr., Mar., 1938.

BURNS, HERBERT PERCY, Lieut., I.M.D. Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 9th Mar., 1887)—Civil surg., Nov., 1920.

BURNS, WILLIAM, D.Sc., Indian Agricul. service (dir. of agric., Bombay) (b. 6th July, 1884).—Apptd. economic botanist, Poona, 11th Oct., 1908; deputed to mil. dept., Nov., 1917; princpl., Agric. Coll., Poona, May, 1922; jt. dir. of agric. (temp.), April, 1926; offg. dir. of agric., Oct., 1932; confd., July, 1933; offg. agricul. expert, Aug., 1936.

BURRARD, SIR SIDNEY GERALD, Bart., K.C.S.I., F.R.S., Col., R.E., ret'd., late Survey of India.—First commn., 6th April, 1879; asst. sec. ord. committee for gunnery exports, April, 1881; apptd. to Indian establt., and attached to B. Sappers and Miners, Jan., 1883; attached to mil. works dept., Baluchistan, March, 1883; asst. supt., trigon. survey, Sept., 1884; dep. supt., June, 1889; spl. duty in connection with longitude operations between Greenwich and India, 1895; supt., Oct., 1899; offg. surveyor-gen. of India, Feb., 1908; confd., Oct., 1911; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; awarded Victoria medal of R.G.S., 1913; K.C.S.I., June, 1914; ret'd., Sept., 1919; author of 'An Account of the Scientific Work of the Survey of India' and 'Levelling of Precision in India'.

BURRELL, OSCAR LAWRENCE, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 13th Mar., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 14th Nov., 1927; confd., Aug., 1934.

BURRELL, ROY HERBERT ADAMS, Indian Police (b. 14th June, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 16th Nov., 1925; offg. dist. supt., March, 1928.

BURROWS, LIONEL BURTON, C.B.E., B.A., late Bengal Civil Service (b. 12th Mar., 1883).—Joined the service, 7th May, 1903; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1907; mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; C.B.E., Jan., 1931; offg. commr., Oct., 1932; confd., Apl., 1936; ret'd., Mar., 1938.

BURT, SIR BRYCE CHUDLEIGH, Kt., C.I.E., M.B.E., B.Sc., F.C.S., Indian Agricultural Service (agric. expert, imp. council of agric. research) (b. 29th Apl., 1881).—Joined the service, 14th Jan., 1908; apptd. dep. dir. of agri., United Provs., Feb., 1908; K-i-H. medal, 2nd class, Dec., 1911; M.B.E., Jan., 1919; sec., central cotton cttee., April, 1921; dir. of agric., Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; agric. expert, imp. council of agric. research, July, 1929; offg. vice-chairman, ditto, Oct., 1935; knighted, Jan., 1936; offl. adviser to Central Govt's delegate to Indo-British Trade negotiations, May, 1937.

BURTON, BERTRAM HAND, B.A., late Indian Police Service (b. 5th Sept., 1875).—Joined the service 2nd April, 1897, and posted to United Prov.; supt. of police, Sept., 1907; dep. inspr.-gen., Nov., 1925; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

BURTON, EDWARD, late Indian State Railways (b. 28th Jan., 1877).—Joined the service, 7th June, 1900, as asst. loco. supt.; dist. loco. supt., April, 1907; dep. ch. mechl. engr., May, 1922; ret'd. May, 1931.

BURTON, EDWARD STANLEY VINER, B.Sc. (Edin.) Indian Forest Service (b. 25th Nov., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 20th Oct., 1930.

BURTON, FRANK, India Office (hr. exec. officer, acctnt.-gen's. dept.) (b. 4th Aug., 1889).—Apptd. second divn. clerk, Board of Trade, Sept., 1908; transfed. to Board of Educn., Sept., 1909, and to Census Office, April, 1910; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 8th May, 1913, and att'd. to registry and record dept.; temporarily transfed. to mil. dept., Sept., 1914; higher clerical officer, July, 1922; exec. officer, Sept., 1932; actg. hr. exec. officer, Apl., 1936; confd., Nov., 1937.

BURTON, Sir GEOFFREY POWNALL, K.C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (fin. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 19th Nov., 1884).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1909, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under sec. to chief commr., Sept., 1913; spl. duty in sectt., Nov., 1916, to Jan., 1918; dep. commr. (prov.), Nov., 1920; confd. as dep. commr., Feb., 1921; excise commr., March, 1924; rev. secty. to govt., C.P., Jan., 1929; offg. eommr., Feb., 1932, and again, Mar., 1933; offg. commr. of settlements and dir. land records, etc., Sept., 1932; commr., May, 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; member, govr's exec. council, Central Provs., Apl., 1936; fin. commr., Mar., 1937; K.C.I.E., May, 1937.
