Dept., United Provs.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. prof. of English literature and logic, Queen's Coll., Benares, 18th Feb., 1896; inspr. of schools, Jan., 1897; asst. dir. of public instn., Nov., 1902; dir., Nov., 1906; member of lieut.-govr.'s legisl. council; C.I.E., June, 1918; Vice-Chancellor of Allahabad University, April, 1922; knighted, June, 1922; retd., 1923.

DELAHAY, VEN. WILLIAM, Indian Eccles. Estab. (archdeacon of Rangoon) (b. 25th Jan., 1884).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 13th Dec., 1919; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1921; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1929; archdeacon of Rangoon, Mar., 1937.

DE LA HEY, MISS DOROTHY CISLEY, late Indian Educl. Service (b. 6th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service as prinpl., coll. for women, Madras, 1st July, 1914; prinpl. Queen Mary's coll., Madras, May, 1923; retd., Feb., 1936; K.i.H. medal, 1st class, May, 1937.

DE LA NOUGEREDÉ, FREDERICK ADAIR, Indian State Rlys. (dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly.) (b. 15th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 16th July, 1915; asst. contr. of stores, E.B. rly., July, 1918; offg. dist. contr. of stores variously from Jan., 1919; confd., Mar., 1931; offg. contr. of stores, E.B. rly., Feb., 1935.

DELL, PERCY BERTRAM, M.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. offcr., store dept.) (b. 15th Nov., 1888).—Second class clerk, bd. of trade, Sept., 1908, to Sept., 1909; ditto, bd. of edn., Sept., 1909, to June, 1911; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 9th June, 1911, and attd. to registry and record dept.; transf. to store dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to July, 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, Jan., 1926; higher exec. offcr., May, 1938; M.B.E., June, 1938.

DE MONTE, FRANK THOMAS, C.I.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 1st Dec., 1879).—Trained at R.I.E. coll.; joined the telegraph service, 1st Oct., 1899 as asst. supt.; supt., Nov., 1910; dep. postmr.-gen. (traffic), April, 1914; asst. dir.-gen., telegraph traffic, April, 1919; offg. dep. dir.-gen.; postmr.-gen. and dir., telegraph traffic, Burma, June, 1923; dep. dir.-gen., telegraph traffic, March, 1925; offg. ch. engr., tels., April, 1928; P.M.G., Bengal and Assam, Dec., 1928; offg. ch. engr., posts and tels., April, 1930; confd., Oct., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; retd., Dec., 1934.

DE MONTMORENCY, SIR GEOFFREY FITZHERVEY, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., K.C.V.O., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd Aug., 1876).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 6th Dec., 1900, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. colonization offr., Chenab Canal Colony, Oct., 1903; special duty for delimitating the River Ravi, Nov., 1903, to July, 1904; junr. sec. to finl. commr., April, 1911; on special duty in connection with transfer of capital to Delhi, Feb., 1912; transf. to Delhi as personal asst. to chief commr., Oct., 1912; C.I.E., June, 1915; acting dep. commr., Delhi, April, 1917; offg. chief commr., Delhi, Oct., 1917; dep. commr., Punjab, April, 1918; C.B.E., Dec., 1919; addl. dep. sec. to govt. of India, Foreign and Pol. Dept., Feb., 1920; pte. sec. to the Viceroy, March, 1922; K.C.I.E., July, 1926; member of govr.'s exec. council, Punjab, July, 1926; govr. of Punjab, Aug., 1928, to Apl., 1933; K.C.S.I., Oct., 1928; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1933; retd., Aug., 1935.

DENCH, WILLIAM GEORGE, B.A., B.A.I., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 1st Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921;

under-secty. to govt., irrigation branch, Punjab, April, 1931; under-secty. (North), Punjab irrigation sectt., Jan., 1933; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1933, and again, Feb., 1936.

DENNEHY, HAROLD GEORGE, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., educ. and local self govt. depts., Assam) (b. 18th Dec., 1890).—Educ. at Clifton and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 6th Dec., 1914, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Aug. 1915, to Mar., 1919; under sec. to govt. of Assam, April, 1923; offg. chief sec., ditto, Apl., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1925; confd., June, 1933; offg. secty. to govt. in transferred depts., Assam, June, 1929; confd., June, 1933; offg. ch. secty., Assam, Sept., 1936; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; secty., educ. and local self govt. depts., Feb., 1938.

DENNING, Sir HOWARD, Kt., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 20th May, 1885).—Educ. at Clifton, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1909, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; municipal commr., Surat, May, 1915; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1916; ditto, Punjab, June, 1917; ditto, Bombay, Jan., 1918; under-sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Nov., 1918; on deputn. to England from April, 1919; sec. to commee. on Indian exchange and currency, June to Dec., 1919; offg. dep. contr. of currency, Bombay, May, 1920; confd., July, 1920; offg. contr. of currency, Calcutta, March, 1923; confd., March, 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; addl. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Oct., 1929; Kt., June, 1932; adviser to Indian delegation to monetary and economic conf., London, June, 1933; member, Ind. res. bank ctee., July, 1933; ret'd., Dec., 1934.

DENNIS, REGINALD BURTON, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.C.G.I., A.M.I.E.E., F.R.A.I., F.R.G.S.—Indian p. and t. (divl. engr.) (b. 25th Apl., 1900).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1923, as asst. divl. engr.; divl. engr., Jan., 1931.

DENT, THOMAS VALENTINE, Indian Forest service. (b. 4th Apl., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bengal, 11th Oct., 1929.

DEO, Sir RAJENDRA NARAYAN BHANJA, Raja, Kt., O.B.E.—Member of govr.'s exec. council, Bihar and Orissa, Jan., 1929, to Jan., 1934; vice-presdt., ditto, Dec., 1931; O.B.E., June, 1918; knighted, June, 1933.

DERBYSHIRE, Sir HAROLD, Kt., M.C., Bengal Judl. Dept. (ch. justice, high ct., Calcutta).—Apptd. ch. justice, high ct., Calcutta, 12th Nov., 1934.

DE ROSARIO, Miss AGNES, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 14th Aug., 1881).—Apptd. offg. inspectress of schools in I.E.S., 5th Feb., 1919; confd., Aug., 1922; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

DE ROSARIO, LEONARD GRAVES, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 8th Sept., 1875).—Joined the service as dist. medl. officer, E. I. Rly., 20th Mar., 1917; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

DERRY, ROBERT LAWRIE, Indian Forest Service (b. 17th Dec., 1888) (dep. consvr., Bihar).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 17th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930.

DESAI, BECHARBHAI PURSOTAMDAS, B.A. (Ind.), B.Sc. (Bom.), D.P.H. (Lond.), A.M.I.E.E. (Eng.), Indian P. and T. (divl. engr.) (b. 23rd Nov., 1898).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1923, as asst. divl. engr.; divl. engr., June, 1932.

DESAI, CHANDULAL CHUNILAL, B.A., Cantab), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 27th April, 1900).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Cambridge Univ. (non-collegiate); joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923; arrived, 26th Nov., 1923, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr., offg. dep. commr., June, 1927; and again, Feb., 1931; regr. co-op. society and div. of indus, Mar., 1937; dep. commr., Nov., 1937.

DESAI, DAYABHAI PRANBHAI, Rao Bahadur late Income Tax Deptt., Bombay (b. 12th Dec., 1876).—Held temp. and permanent non-gazetted appts. from 15th Nov., 1909, to 30th April 1918; income tax collr. for Sind, May, 1918; dep. collr., May, 1919; income tax officer Ahmedabad City, April, 1922; asst. commr. of income tax, Aug., 1923; ret'd. Dec., 1931.

DESAI, MANILAL JAGDISHBHAI, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (dep. secty., rev. dept., Bombay) (b. 4th Jan., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. coll.; offg. dir. of land records, etc., Bombay, Oct., 1935; collr., June, 1937; dep. secty., rev. dept., Bombay, May, 1938.

DESAI, MANULAL CHUNILAL, Indian C.S. (jt. magt. United Provs.) (b. 25th Feb., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and Cambridge (non-collegiate).—Joined the service, 18th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., March, 1928; jt. magt., Nov., 1930; offg. magt. and collr., May, 1933; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1934.

DESAI, NAGINLAL VENILAL, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Civil Service, Judicial Branch (b. 13th Nov. 1872).—Joined the dept., 10th May, 1902; asst. judge and addl. sess. judge, March, 1923; offg. judge and sess. judge, May, 1925, and again, May, 1926; confd., Oct., 1927; ret'd., May, 1929.

DESAI, SHANKAR PANDURANG, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 17th Nov., 1890).—Educ. at Bombay and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 7th Dec., 1915, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt. of Assam, July, 1921, to July, 1923; asst. commr. and settl. officer, Oct., 1923; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1929, and again, Dec., 1932; confd., Dec., 1933; offg. secty. fin. and rev. depts., June, 1931; and again, Mar., 1934; offg. secty., transfd. depts., Assam, April, 1933; offg. dir. of land records, etc., Assam, Jan., 1934; and again, Nov., 1934; secty., fin. dept., Assam, Apl., 1936, to May, 1938.

DE SALIS, Rev. ANDREW AUGUSTINE FANE, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 17th Jan., 1901).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 5th Oct., 1928; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1930; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

DESENNE, OSCAR HENRI, M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (Assam) (b. 2nd Nov., 1875).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1898, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1908; under sec. to govt., E.B. and Assam, Jan., 1912; ditto, Assam, April, 1912; suptg. engr., Dec., 1917; offg. chief engr. and sec. to govt., April, 1919; confd. as chief engr. and sec. to govt., Nov., 1924; retd., Nov., 1929.

DESHMUKH, CHINTAMAN DWARKANATH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (fin. secty. and secty. p.w.d.) (b. 14th Jan., 1896).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1919; arrived, 20th Apl., 1920, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1925; confd., July, 1929; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; offg. rev. sec. to govt., Cent. Provs., Feb., 1932; fin. secty., ditto, June, 1933; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; fin. secty. and secty. p.w.d., Cent. Provs., Nov., 1937.

DE SMIDT, GERALD EWALD OVERBECK, D.S.O., India Posts and Tels. (dir.-genl. posts and tels., Burma) (b. 1st June, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1906; on field service with Mesopotamia Expedy. Force from 15th Dec., 1915; dir. of tels. with M.E.F., April, 1919; on foreign service with Irak Admn., Nov., 1919; on deputation at India Office from 7th Aug., 1923; ditto, with N.W. Rly., from 23rd March, 1925; ditto, with Indo-European Tel. Dept., from 28th Mar., 1928; offg. dir., tel. engrg., Aug., 1933; confd., Sept., 1933; dep. dir.-genl., tels., May, 1934; p.m.g., Burma, Mar., 1936; dir.-genl. posts and tels., Burma, Apl., 1937.

DE SOUZA, FRANCIS XAVIER, M.A., LL.D., Barr. (1899), late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 20th Jan., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1899; acting joint judge, March, 1900; 2nd asst., July, 1900; 1st asst., July, 1904; judge and sess. judge, Dec., 1904; on depn. under govt. of India as member, civil justice cttee., from Feb., 1924; 1st grade judge, Aug., 1925; offg. addl. commr. of Sind, Oct., 1926; confd., Dec., 1926; retd., Jan., 1929.

DE SOUZA, FRANK AUGUSTUS, late Land Records Dept., Burma (b. 6th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service, 22nd Aug., 1900; supt., June, 1904; asst. dir., Oct., 1925; retd., Aug., 1934.

DE'SOUZA, F. J., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Railways (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 29th Sept., 1892).—Asst. exec. engr., 24th Oct., 1916; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

DEV DATT, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 3rd Sept., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1915; exec. engr., Oct., 1924.

DEVADOSS, SIR MUTHIAH DAVID, Kt. Barr., late Madras Judl. Dept.—Apptd. judge, Madras high ct., 17th Nov., 1921; retd., Dec., 1928; Kt., Jan., 1932.

DEVASAHAYAM, JACOB, B.A., Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 9th June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, on 7th June, 1923; offg. dist. supt., June, 1929; confd., July, 1936.

DEVENISH, Rev. ROBERT CECIL SYLVESTER, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (archdeacon, Lahore) (b. 22nd Nov., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 27th Oct., 1919; junior chaplain, Oct., 1921; on mil. duty from 24th Nov., 1921, to 30th Nov., 1922; senr. chaplain, March, 1928; offg. archdeacon, Apl., 1933; confd., May, 1934.

DEVI DAYAL, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Assam) (b. Oct., 1890).—Joined the service, 15th Oct., 1914; asst. engr. Feb., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; asst. ch. engr. and under-secty. to govt., p.w.d., Assam, July, 1932, to Apl., 1936.

DEVI DAYAL DHAWAN, RAI BAHADUR LALA, M.Sc., LL.B., Punjab Civil Service (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 15th Oct., 1888).—Extra asst. commr., Jan., 1934; sub judge, Nov., 1920; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935.

DEVI DAYAL SAIGAL, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 10th Dec., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 8th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Novr., 1928.

DEVI SINGH, M.A., Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 12th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 27th Oct., 1908; asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1923; apptd. to the I.S.E., as asst. exec. engr., 1st Jan., 1928; exec. engr., Jan., 1933.

DEVIN, NINA GRACE, late India Office (b. 29th Oct., 1874).—A ptid. clerical officer, 22nd Nov., 1921; higher clerical officer (acting), April, 1922; confd., Dec., 1924; retd., Jan., 1935.

DE VINE, JAMES CHANCELLOR, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 9th Aug., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 21st Oct., 1921; offg. dist. supt., March, 1927; confd., Aug., 1932.

DEVLIN, Rev. THOMAS STAFFORD, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 27th May, 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, Jan., 1923; junior chaplain, 5th Jan., 1925; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1933.

DEW, Sir ARMINE BRERETON, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (retd.).—First commn., 9th Nov., 1888; attid. to Gilgit agency, Nov., 1894; joined pol. dept., Dec., 1897; pol. asst., April, 1898; pol. agent, Sept., 1905; pol. agent, Gilgit, May, 1908; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; pol. agent, Kalat, and in charge of the Bolan Pass, Aug., 1912; C.S.I., June, 1915; resdt., and rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Dec., 1917; agent to the govr.-gen. and chief commr., Baluchistan, Sept., 1919; K.C.I.E., June, 1921; retd., Sept., 1922; war service, Black Mountain expdn., 1891.

DEY, BIMAN BEHARI, M.Sc.(Cal.), D.Sc.(Lon.), Indian Educl. Service (prof., presdy. coll., Madras) (b. 1st Nov., 1889).—Joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1916, and served in Bengal to 9th April, 1920; addl. prof. of chemistry, presdy. coll., Madras, Jan., 1924; prof. of chem., Feb., 1937.

DEV, GEORGE GOODAIR, C.I.E., M.I.E. Ind., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bengal) (b. 13th Sept., 1876).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1899, and employed as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1908; and under-sec. to govt from March, 1909, to June, 1910; suptg. engr., July, 1915; offg. chief engr. and sec. to govt., April, 1921; confd., Dec., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1928; retd., Feb., 1931.

DEV, PROMODE KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (plant pathologist, United Provs.) (b. 25th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as plant pathologist on 1st March, 1920.

DEV, RATI NATH, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 16th Jan., 1898).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923; arrived, 28th Nov., 1923, and served in United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1926; offg. dep. commr., April, 1928; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1930; official repres. of Up. govt. in central legis. assembly, Aug., 1936.

DEV, SUSHIL KUMAR, B.A. Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 16th Feb., 1907).—Educ. at Calcutta University, London School of Economics and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal, as asst. collr. and mag; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1931.

DHAND, MILKHI RAM, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 13th March, 1892).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 1st Oct., 1914; asst. engr., Oct., 1915; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1925; exec. engr., Aug., 1927.

DHANI RAM VERMA, RAI SAHIB, late Survey of India (b. 4th Aug. 1875).—Joined the service as sub-asst. supt. on 20th Nov., 1897; extra asst. supt., Dec., 1905; dep. supt., July, 1925; supt., Jan., 1926; retd. Aug., 1930.

DHANPAT RAI, RAI SAHIB LALA (asst. secty., dept. of educ., health and lands) (b. 25th July, 1882).—Joined the service, 18th Apr., 1901; supt., dept. of E. H. and L., July, 1928; asst. secty., Mar., 1937.

DHANVADA ANANDA RAO (see Ananda Rao, D.).

DHAR, NIL RATAN (see Nil Ratan Dhar).

DHAR, SATYENDRA MOHAN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (secty., educ. dept., Bihar) (b. 25th Mar., 1897).—Educ. at Mayurbhanj Sch., Orissa, Univ. Coll. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1921; arrived 18th Jan., 1922, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. commr., Apr., 1928; offg. dist. magte. and collr., Dec., 1931; dir. of industries, Bihar and Orissa, Apr., 1935; mag. and collr., Jan., 1936; secty., educ. dept., Feb., 1938.

DHARM DAS, JAINI, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 16th Mar., 1897).—Joined the service, 26th Nov., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1930.

DHAVLE, SANKARA BALAJI, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Patna) (b. 3rd Oct., 1882).—Educ. at Rajaram High School, Kolapur, Bombay Univ., and St. John's Coll.,

Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 28th Dec., 1906, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; under sec. to govt. fin. and munpl. depts., Nov., 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1914; dist. and sess. judge. also supt. and rembr. of legal affairs, July, 1919; ditto and sec. to Bihar and Orissa legis. council, April, 1920; offg. judge, high ct., Patna, Apr., 1929, and again, Nov., 1930; addl. judge, ditto, March, 1931, and again, Jan., 1932; offg. judge, ditto, March, 1932, and Mar., 1933; confd., July, 1933.

DHAWAN, PURUSHOTTAM LAL, RAI BAHADUR, M.A., Indian State Rlys. (member, fed. public services commn.) (b. 1st Oct., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 4th Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; profr., C.E. coll., Roorki, Oct., 1922; asst. dep. agent, Nov., 1923; offg. dep. chief engr., Dec., 1926; confd., Jan., 1934; offg. divl. supt., Oct., 1931; confd., May, 1934; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of rlys., Bombay, July, 1934; offg. ch. engr., Apr., 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; member, fed. public services commn., May, 1935.

DHIR, RAM DAS, Ind. Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr.) (b. 29th Dec., 1906).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 3rd Aug., 1932.

DHIRAJLAL, DAYABHAI NANAVATI (see Nanavati, D. D.).

DHODY, MANGAL SEN (see Mangal Sen D.).

DHUNJIBHOY, JAL EDALJI, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar (supt., Indian mental hosp., Ranchi) (b. 17th July, 1891).—First commn. dated 1st Nov., 1920; supt., Indian mental hosp., Ranchi, April, 1927.

DHURANDHAR, JAGANNATH RAVJI, O.B.E., B.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., Bombay (dep. secty. to Govt., legal dept.) (b. 8th Mar., 1884).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 23rd Feb., 1918; sub-judge, June, 1919; offg. asst. secty. to Govt., Bombay, legal dept., June, 1919; offg. dep. ditto, Apr., 1928; addl. dep. ditto, June, 1932; asst. judge, Oct., 1933; judge and sess. judge, May, 1935; dep. secty. to govt., legal dept., Bombay, May, 1935; O.B.E., May, 1937.

DIAS, ANTHONY LANCELOT, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 13th March, 1910).—Educ. at St. Vincent's High Sch., Poona, Univ. of Bombay, the Sch. of Economics, London, and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

DIBAKAR MAHANTY, RAO BAHADUR, Madras C.S. (sub-collr.) (b. 10th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service, 19th Mar., 1917; dep. collr., Jan., 1924; offg. collr., Aug., 1931; sub-collr. (provl. subs.), Aug., 1932; confd., May, 1934; Diwan of Jeypure, Jan., 1936.

DIBDIN, AUBREY, M.A., India Office (asst. secty., public and judl dept.) (b. 4th Mar., 1892).—Educ. at Tonbridge and Christ Ch. sch., Oxford; apptd. junr. clerk, 26th Jan., 1920; war service—served in India with 14th Bn. The Queen's (R.W.S.R.), Oct., 1914, to Nov., 1919; asst. principal and pte. sec. to permnt. under-sec. of state, Apr., 1921; principal (actg.), Jan., 1924; confd., Apr., 1924; Inter-

natl. labour conf., Geneva, 12th, 15th, 16th, 17th, 18th, 19th and 20th sessions; adviser to Indian Govt. delegation; 21st and 22nd sessions, delegate; jt. secty., R.C. on Indian labour, Aprl., 1929, to May, 1931; dep. clerk of the council, Sept., 1934, to July, 1936; asst. secty., reforms dept. (temp.), July, 1936; asst. secty., public and judl. dept., Aprl., 1937.

DIBLE, WILLIAM CUTHBERT, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 18th June, 1886).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch., Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1910, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; dep. commr., Nov., 1918; mag. and collr., Nov., 1925; offg. commr., Aprl., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1938.

DI CADILHAC, ARTHUR MARIO AGRICOLA COLLIER GALLETTI, late Indian C.S. (b. 25th March, 1877).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after the exam. of 1899; arrived, 10th Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. sec. to govt., Nov., 1904; under-sec., rev. dept., May, 1906; ditto, dept. under chief sec., March, 1909; sub-collr. and jt. mag., May, 1910; collr. and dist. mag., March, 1920; ret'd., Dec., 1935; author of lunacy and eccles. manuals and transln. of "Vinodya Tarangiri."

DI CADILHAC, ROBERT GALLETTI, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and sub-collr., Madras) (b. 27th July, 1908).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and sub-collr., May, 1933.

DICK, ALAN MACDONALD, C.B.E., M.B., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (prof. King Edward medical coll., Lahore) (b. 30th July, 1884).—First commission, 31st July, 1909; O.B.E., Feb., 1921; apptd. principal, medical sch., Amritsar, Sept., 1922; prof. of ophthalmology, King Edward medl. coll., Lahore, Oct., 1923; C.B.E., Jan., 1936.

DICKENS, DOUGLAS GLEN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 8th July, 1880).—Joined the service, 14th Aug., 1896; asst. supt., E.I. rly., July, 1920; supt., June, 1926; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

DICKESON, CHARLES FREDERICK, India Audit Office (higher exec. officer) (b. 23rd Feb., 1893).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 24th Sept., 1914, and attached to store dept.; transfed. to India audit office, Jan., 1915; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, Aug., 1926; higher exec. officer, Aug., 1930.

DICKINS, CYRIL EDWARD, Indian State Railways (dist. signal engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 4th Dec., 1889).—Asst. signal engr., 28th July, 1922; dist. ditto, 29th Nov., 1934.

DICKINS, KENNETH FREDERICK ROY, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 15th May, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 23rd Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

DICKINSON, E.C., Punjab Educ. Service (prof. of English, govt. coll.) (b. 12th May, 1892).—Joined the service, 31st Dec., 1927; apptd. prof. of English, govt. coll., from 31st Dec., 1927.

DICKINSON, FRITZ GEORGE, M.C., B.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 11th June, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr. on 1st Oct., 1919; exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1926.

DICKINSON, JULIAN FINNIMORE, late Indian Police (b. 12th May, 1881).—Joined the service as asst. supt. of police, 21st Dec., 1903; supt., Bihar, Dec., 1909; ret'd., May, 1936.

DICKSON, ARTHUR NORMAN, M.C., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 27th Nov., 1880).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1907; in milty. employ to 3rd June, 1914; offg. agency surg., Maskat, June, 1914; in milty. employ from 21st Oct., 1914; offg. M.O., N.W. Militia, Dec., 1919; offg. civil surg., July, 1920; ditto, Kurram, Oct., 1920; ditto, D.I. Khan, March, 1921; M.O., Sistan, Sept., 1924; agency surg., Bundelkhand, Jan., 1926; resdy. surg., Hyderabad, April, 1927; ditto, Bushire, Sept., 1924; civil surg., Hazara, July, 1930; resdy. surg. and C.M.O., Baluchistan, Mar., 1931; ret'd., Feb., 1934.

DIGBY, KENELM GEORGE, B.A., arr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Central Provs.) (b. 23rd March, 1890).—Educ. at Haileybury and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1914, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; serving under govt. of India, army dept., from July, 1916; sub-judge, June, 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1924; confid., Feb., 1928.

DIKSHIT, KASHINATH NARAYAN, Rao Bahadur, M.A., Archaeological Survey Dept. (dir.-genl.) (b. 21st Oct., 1889).—Joined the service, 29th Mar., 1917; supt., Sept., 1922; dep. dir.-genl., Feb., 1935; dir.-genl., Sept., 1937.

DIKSHIT, MADHAV JAGANNATH, M.A., B.Sc., Bombay C.S. (dep. collr. and magte.) (b. 31st Oct., 1884).—Joined the service, 9th Dec., 1909; dep. collr., July, 1911; offg. senr. income tax officer, Aprl., 1922; offg. collr., Mar., 1931.

DILAWAR HUSAIN KHAN, M.A., Indian Forest Service (asst. consvr., Bihar) (b. Aprl., 1896).—Joined the service as proby. extra-asst. consvr., 5th Aprl., 1922; confid., Aprl., 1923; asst. consvr., Jan., 1929.

DILLON, HENRY VERE SHIRLEY, B.A.I., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., United Provs.) (b. 9th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; off. dep. ch. eng., Mar., 1927; offg. suptg. engr. and secty., P.W.D. Rajputana, Mar., 1931; suptg. engr., Feb., 1937.

DILLON, WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd June, 1892).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 6th Feb., 1920; arrived, 7th May, 1920, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; offg. collr. and dist. magte. and pol. agent, Jan., 1925; offg. dir. of information and labour intell., April, 1927; offg. collr. of Bombay and supt. of stamps, April, 1928; member legisl. council, March, 1930; offg. collr. and pol. agent, Rewa Kantha, Mar., 1932; collr. and mag., May, 1935; ret'd., June, 1937.

DIMOND, WILLIAM ELLIOT RANDAL, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (asst. dir. p.h.) (b. 29th Sept., 1893).—First commn., dated 27th Sept., 1919; in mil. employ to July, 1925; asst. dir. of public health, N.W.F.P. (civil), July, 1925.

DIN MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, Shaikh M.A., Punjab C.S., Judl. Bch. (judge high ct., Lahore) (b. Mar., 1884).—Joined the service 1st July, 1907; sub-judge, Oct., 1915; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1924; confd., June, 1933; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, Mar., 1934; and again, July, 1936; member, Ind. delimitn. ctee., Aug., 1935; judge, high ct., Lahore, Mar., 1937.

DIPPIE, HERBERT, C.I.E., D.S.O., M.A., Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instn., Orissa) (b. 10th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1920; inspr. of schools, Aug., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; dir. of public instn., Orissa, Apl., 1936.

DIVATIA, HARSIDHBHAI VAJUBHAI, M.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., Bombay (judge, high ct.).—Apptd. addl. judge, high ct., Bombay, 19th June, 1933; judge, ditto, June, 1934.

DIVATIA, SHRINIVAS SATYENDRA, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 21st Dec., 1898).—Joined the service, as apprentice engr., 1st Nov., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1923; offg. exec. engr., June, 1927; confd., Nov., 1932.

DIVATIA, VIRMITRA BHIMRAV, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 24th Apl., 1882).—Apptd. profr. of science, Gujarat Coll., Ahmedabad, 16th July, 1912; I.E.S., from 5th Feb., 1921; retd., Apl., 1937.

DIX, ALFRED GILBERT, B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 18th Mar., 1884).—Joined the service, 18th Oct., 1914; offg. inspr. of schools, Jan., 1915; prinpl. training coll., Jubbulpore, July, 1920; dep. dir. of public instn., Nov., 1922; inspr. of schools, Nov., 1928; secty., public service commn., Oct., 1929 to Aug., 1931; tutor to Maharaja of Nabha, Sept., 1931; retd., Aug., 1937.

DIXON, ALGERNON DREW, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 17th Oct., 1907).—Educated at Bedford School, Corpus Christi College, Oxford, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Feb., 1934; exec. offer., Cawnpore improvement trust, Apl., to Oct., 1937.

DIXON, ARTHUR FREDERIC WILLIAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 11th May, 1892).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Aug., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1922; temp. dep. secty. to govt., law and educn. dept., Sept., 1929; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aug., 1933; confd., Mar., 1935; officer in ch., reforms (Orissa) dept., Mar., 1936; secty., educ. dept., Orissa, Apl., 1936, and again Dec., 1936; chief secty., Orissa, Mar. to Nov., 1937.

DIXON, GEORGE GRAHAM, B.A., Burma Office (acting asst. secty.) (b. 9th Dec., 1895).—Educ. at Merchant Taylor's Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; on mil. service (9th

Essex Regt.), Aug., 1914, to March, 1916; temp. junr. clerk, H.M. Customs and Excise, 1916-18; history master, Sherborne Sch., 1918-20; apptd. junr. clerk, rev. and stat. dept., India Office, 12th Apl., 1920; sec. to Indian delegation to Assembly of League of Nations, 1921 and 1931; pte. sec. to permanent under-sec. of state, Nov., 1924; actg. principal, June, 1926; confd., Aug., 1927; delegate of India to International Copyright Conf., 1928; adviser to Indian del. to International Labour Conf., 1930; adviser to del. of India to International Conf. on drugs, 1931; secty. to British del. to Burma Round Table Conf., 1931-32; dep. clerk of the Council, July, 1936, to Mar., 1937; acting asst. secty., Apl., 1937.

DIXON, MARTIN JOSEPH, B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th Feb., 1890).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 17th Dec., 1914, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; temp. to mil. dept. (A.D.C. to pol. resdt., Aden), Jan., 1917, to April, 1919; jt. mag., Nov., 1922; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1924; confd., Nov., 1929; retd., Feb., 1933.

DIXON, Rev. THOMAS HAROLD, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 30th Sept., 1873).—Joined the service, 21st March, 1900; chaplain on probation, Aug., 1908; junr. chaplain, March, 1910; senr. chaplain, March, 1920; retd., Aug., 1929.

DIXON, THOMAS INGHAM, B.A., B.A.I., M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 17th Oct., 1879).—Joined the service, 6th Jan., 1904; asst. engr., Oct., 1915; exec. engr., Nov., 1916; retd., Oct., 1934.

DOBBS, ARTHUR CONWAY, B.A., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 1st Nov., 1874).—Apptd. prinpl., Agric. coll., Punjab, 17th Oct., 1906; ditto, Bengal, Aug., 1908; asst. inspr. gen. of agric. in India, June, 1911; asst. to the agricl. adviser to govt. of India, April, 1912; dep. dir. of agric., Bihar and Orissa, May, 1915; dir. of agric. (s.p.t.), Jan., 1920; confd. as dir. of agric., Nov., 1921; retd., Nov., 1929.

DOBBS, WILLIAM EVELYN JOSEPH, B.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 3rd July, 1880).—Educ. at Shrewsbury Sch. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Provs. as asst. magte and collr.; jt. magte., Oct., 1913; on mil. duty, Aug., 1916, to April, 1919; magte. and collr., May, 1921; dep. commr., April, 1927; retd., May, 1931.

DOBSON, BERNARD HENRY, C.I.E., C.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (finl. commr. (rev.), Punjab) (b. 1st Jan., 1881).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. offr.; employed in directorate of mil. intell., War Office, from Dec., 1915, to July, 1919; dep. commr., Nov., 1919; C.B.E., Jan., 1920; offg. home sec. to govt., Dec., 1925; confd., Dec., 1926; offg. commr., April, 1929; confd., Dec., 1930; offg. finl. commr. (dev.) Punjab, Jan., 1935; offg. finl. commr. (rev.) and secty. rev. dept., Jan., 1936; confd., Oct., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

DODD, REV. GEORGE EDWARD, M.A., B.D., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 21st Mar., 1884). Apptd. chaplain on probation, 11th Nov., 1912; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1914; on milty. duty from Sept., 1914, to April, 1925; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1924; offg. presdy. senr. chaplain, Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1933; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

DODD, SIR ROBERT JOHN SHERWOOD, Kt., C.S.I., late Indian Police (b. 26th July, 1878).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1899, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt., April, 1906; supt., May, 1911; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1919; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., April, 1923; confd., Sept., 1924; offg. inspr.-gen., Nov., 1925; confd., Aug., 1927; C.S.I., Jan., 1928; Kt., June, 1931; ret'd., July, 1933.

DODSWORTH, OWEN ALARIC, I.S.O., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Orissa) (b. 13th Sept., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 14th Sept., 1904; proby. extra asst. consvr., July, 1908; confd., July, 1913; dep. consvr., Dec., 1928; I.S.O., May, 1937.

DODWELL, DAVID WILLIAM, M.A., Ph.D. (Columbia), Indian C.S. (sub-collr., and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 13th Dec., 1898).—Educ. at Banbury County Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr., and mag., Jan., 1924; tutor to minor Maharaja of Travancore, June, 1925, to Apl., 1928; offg. collr. and mag., Sept., 1931; addl. dep. secty., fin. dept., Madras (temp.), Nov., 1935; and again, Aug., 1936.

DOGRA, HANS RAJ, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Madras) (b. 23rd Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1919; suptg. engr., Jan., 1937; chief engr., May, 1938.

DOIG, EDWARD WILLIAM, late Indian C.S. (b. 4th April, 1896).—Educ. at Jarrow Secondary School and Durham University; joined the service, 7th Sept., 1923; arrived, 1st Nov., 1923, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. magte., Nov., 1928; ret'd. Mar., 1932.

DONALD, ARTHUR ALEXANDER, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 1st April, 1899).—Joined the service on 5th June, 1920; offg. dist. supt., April, 1928; confd., Dec., 1931.

DONALD, CHARLES HILLIARD (b. 21st Sept., 1873).—Apptd. warden of fisheries, Punjab, 25th Nov., 1915; ret'd., Nov., 1932.

DONALD, SIR JAMES, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., LL.M., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th Dec., 1873).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam as asst. commr., May, 1898; sub-divl. offr., April, 1899; under-sec. to chief commr., April, 1901, to April, 1904; joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal, Jan., 1905; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905, as offg. mag. and collr.; mag. and collr., March, 1908; provl. supt. of census operations, April, 1910; commr. of excise and salt, and inspr.-genl. of registration, E. Bengal and Assam, Sept., 1911; transfed. to Bengal, April, 1912, and apptd. commr. of excise and salt; offg. sec. to govt., finl., appt. and munpl. depts.,

from Nov., 1912; spl. duty in connection with the Public Service Commission, Oct. to Dec., 1913; mag. and collr., April, 1914; offg. sec. to govt. of Bengal, finl. dept., April, 1915; confd., Oct., 1915; member of the Bengal legisl. council, June, 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; chairman, corp'n. of Calcutta, April, 1919; offg. chief sec. to govt., Bengal, April, 1921; member of exec. council, Bengal, Nov., 1922; C.S.I., Jan., 1924; Kt., June, 1927; ret'd., Feb., 1929.

DONALD, JOHN OMMANNEY STEWART, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 10th Oct., 1905).—First commn., 29th Jan., 1925; in milty. employ until 19th Jan., 1931; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Sept., 1932; ditto, Sth. Waziristan, June, 1933; asst. commr., Mansehra, Mar., 1936; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Apl., 1937; asst. commr., Thal, Mar., 1938.

DONALD, SIR JOHN STUART, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian Pol. Dept.—Joined the service, April, 1882; extra asst. commr., May, 1884; asst. commr., Nov., 1890; accompanied mission to Kabul under Sir H. M. Durand, 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; on special duty on the N.W. Frontier, 1894; was British commr. for demarcation of Kurram-Afghan boundary, May-Dec., 1894; dep. commr., Aug., 1900; on special duty with British-Afghan commn., from March, 1903, to Feb., 1904; pol. agent, Feb., 1906; resld., Waziristan, July, 1908; special duty with Anglo-Afghan Border Offences commn., May to Nov., 1910; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; offg. chief commr. and agent to govr.-gen., N.W. Frontier Prov., Aug., 1913, to Jan., 1915; K.C.I.E., June, 1915; an addl. member of govr.-genl.'s legisl. council from Aug., 1916; chief pol. offr. with the Waziristan field force, in addn. to his own duties, from May, 1917; ret'd., April, 1920.

DONALDSON, EION PELLY, Burma Office (principal) (b. 15th Apl., 1896).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford (classical scholar); apptd. asst. principal, secretary's office, Inland Revenue, 8th Sept., 1920; transfed. to India Office, 1st Oct., 1924; pte. secty. to parly. under-secty. of state, Dec., 1927; pte. secty. to perm. ditto, Apl., 1930; principal, July, 1930; on depn. for the course of instrn. at Impl. Defence Coll., Jan. to Dec., 1936; War service—served in France with Machine Gun Corps, Mar., 1916, to Mar., 1919; Major Reserve of Officers, June, 1919.

DONALDSON, JAMES DUFF, Indian Railway Acccts. Service (dep. chief accts. offcr.) (b. 9th Jan., 1884).—Held non-gaz. appts. from 5th Dec., 1905; confd. as asst. auditor, E.I. Rly., June, 1921; confd. as accts. officer, gr. 1., Nov., 1929; dep. chief accts. offcr., June, 1938.

DONALDSON, JOHN COOTE, M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 24th May, 1895).—Educ. at Felsted and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1920; arrived, 5th Dec., 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Feb., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1925; confd., July, 1932; offg. dep. secty. to govt., industries and revenue branches, Oct., 1927; dir. of ind. U.P., Oct., 1930; ditto Malerkotla State, Sept., 1935; secty. to govr. U.P., Apl., 1937.

DONNISON, FRANK SIEGFRIED VERNON, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to fin. commrs., Burma) (b. 3rd July, 1898).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Corpus Christi Co., Oxford; joined the service 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 25th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; warden, oil-fields, March, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1929; confd., Jan., 1936; secty. to fin. commrs. Mar., 1937.

DONOVAN, JOHN THOMAS, C.I.E., B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Dec., 1885).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood Coll., Royal Univ., Ireland (Queen's Coll., Galway), and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settl. officer, Sept., 1914; jt. mag. and dep. collr., April, 1916 regr., co-op. soc., Bengal, June, 1917; offg. dy. commr., Nov., 1921; offg. sec., agri. and indus. dept., govt. of Bengal, Nov., 1922; mag. and collr., March, 1925; C.I.E., June, 1931; retd., Nov., 1933.

DOOG, JOHN MYLES GREGORY, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., Burma Rlys.) (b. 22nd Aug., 1884).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 5th Feb., 1910; asst. engr. (temp.), Aug., 1910; ditto (perm.), Aug., 1911; on milty. duty from 18th July, 1917, to 3rd April, 1919; dist. engr., Jan., 1927.

DORAB N. D. KHANDALAVALA (see Khandalavala, D. N. D.).

DORAN, ALFRED JOHN, Indian State Rys. (transpn. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 21st Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 23rd Mar., 1908; asst. supt., Jan., 1922; transpn. supt. (traffic comm.) E.I. rly., May, 1934.

DORMAN, WILLIAM STEWART, B.A., B.E., M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 5th Feb., 1876).—Joined the service, 5th Jan., 1901, as temp. engr., Punjab; asst. engr., July, 1906; exec. engr., Jan., 1910; under sec. to govt., Punjab, lldgs. and roads branch, April, 1911, to July, 1912, from April to July, 1917, and from Nov. to Dec., 1918; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1922; confd., July, 1925; offg. ch. engr., March, 1927; retd., Feb., 1931.

DOSSETOR, Rev. FRANCIS ERNEST, M.A., Indian Eccles Estabt. (b. 16th Dec., 1885).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 18th June, 1921; junr. chaplain, June, 1923; senr. chaplain, June, 1929.

DOUGLAS, Rev. PERCY SHOLTO DOUGLAS, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (senr. chaplain) (b. 13th Mar., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 15th Feb., 1921; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1923; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1930.

DOUGLAS, WILLIAM CHALMERS, M.A. (Edin.), B.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 27th July, 1884).—Joined the service, 12th Jan., 1914; offg. prin. govt. arts coll., Rajahmundry, Jan., 1926; offg. prof. presdy. coll., Madras, Dec., 1926; confd., Dec., 1931; offg. princpl., ditto, Dec., 1932; retd., Jan., 1935.

DOW, HUGH, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., govt. of India, dept. of commerce) (b. 8th May, 1886).—Educ. at Aske's Sch., Hatcham, S.E., and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1910, and served in Bombay as asst.

collr.; munpl. commr., Surat, from Oct., 1916; asst. commr. in Sind for civil supplies and recruiting, July, 1918; dep. contr. of prices, Sind, Sept., 1919, to April, 1920; dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., May, 1921; in addn., passport officer from Jan. to July, 1923; actg. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Oct., 1923; dep. sec., ditto, Nov., 1923 (temp.); fin. advr. to P.W.D. in Sind (temp.), Dec., 1925; offg. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., May, 1926; rev. offr., Lloyd barrage project (temp.), Jan., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1932; jt. secty., to govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Oct., 1934; addl. secty., ditto (temp.), June, 1936; offg. secty. ditto, Oct., 1936; confd., Mar., 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937.

DOW, THOMAS MILLER, M.A., Indian C.S. (secty. educ. dept. Govt. of Bengal) (b. 30th Dec., 1890).—Educ. at Kilmarnock Academy, George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to March, 1919; offg. mag. and collr., July, 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Feb., 1923; magte. and collr., Sept., 1928; offg. secty. educ. dept., Bengal, Feb., 1937; confd., May, 1937.

DOWLEY, FRANCIS MICHAEL, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Madras) (b. 29th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1907, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; on mil. duty from June, 1915, to July, 1920; offg. engr.-in-ch., Cauvery (Mettur) project, July, 1929; supt. engr., Nov., 1934; chief engr., Sept., 1936.

DOWLING, LIONEL EDWARD, Indian State Railways (exec. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 30th Aug., 1885).—Joined the service, 4th June, 1915; resdt. engr., G.I.P. rly., Sept., 1919; exec. engr., May, 1932.

DOWNING, FRANCIS WILLIAM HALL, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 26th Mar., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., N.W.F.P., on 30th Sept., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1932; confd., Sept., 1933.

DRACUP, ATHELSTONE HAMLEIGH, B.A. (Cantab.), Bombay C.S. (collr. and dist. magte.) (b. 26th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, Dec., 1913; dep. collr., Dec., 1915; offg. under-secty. to Govt., Bombay, genl., educ., marine and eccl. depts., Mar., 1925; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Mar., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934.

DRAKE, ANTONY ELLIOT, Indian C.S. (b. 15th June, 1907).—Educ. at Aldenham School and New College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. magte. and collr.; under-secty. to resdt. Rajputana, Mar., 1937.

DRAKE, DONALD HENRY CHARLES, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd Aug., 1887).—Educ. at Blundell's School, Tiverton, and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1912, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; on deputn. as addl. asst. master, Mavo Coll., Ajmer, from Feb., 1915; guardian to the Ticca Sahib of Poona, Dec., 1918, to Nov., 1919; dep. commr. (prov.), Nov., 1924; confd., 1925; pol. agent, C.P. Feudatory States, June, 1928; on special duty under A.G.G., Eastern States, Apl., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1933; offg. commr., Feb., 1934; retd., Aug., 1937.

DRAKE, JOHN COLLARD BERNARD, C.S.I., C.I.E., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th Mar., 1884).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 24th Nov., 1908, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1912; ditto, pol., appt. and educn. depts., April, 1915; pte. sec. t. lieut.-govr., Nov., 1915; jt. mag. and dep. collr., and ditto, March, 1916; on mil. duty from Sept., 1918 (2nd lt., 20 Deccan Horse); spl. duty, for. and pol. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1918, to Jan., 1919; under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agri. dept., 1919; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; dep. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of industries, Feb., 1921; sec. to high commr. for India, 8th Sept., 1924 to 7th Nov., 1929; actg. high commr. for India, 26th Sept. to 5th Nov., 1924, and 11th Dec., 1924, to Jan., 1925; C.I.E., July, 1926; jt. secty., govt. of India, dept. of commerce; C.B.E., July, 1930; secty. govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Mar., 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; retd., July, 1935.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, Sir DIGBY LIVINGSTONE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (chairman, United Provs. public serv. commr.) (b. 21st Dec., 1877).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 5th Dec., 1901, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlmt. offr.; jt. mag., Dec., 1911; settlt. offr. Oct., 1912; jt. regr., co-operative credit societies, Nov., 1916; dep. commr. (tempy.), Sept., 1917; ditto, and settlt. offr., Oct., 1917; employed in Jodhpur State, from Nov., 1920; revenue member, Nov., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1927; offg. commr., Nov., 1929; commr. (provl. sub.), Aug., 1930; C.S.I. Jan., 1933; offg. member, bd. of rev., Mar., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; presdt., bd. of health, United Provs., Oct., 1935; retd., Dec., 1936; knighted, Feb., 1937; chairman, United Provs. public service commn., Apl., 1937.

DRAVID, NILKANTH KRISHNA, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 24th Sept., 1908).—Educ. at London School of Economics and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the Service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. judge and addl. sess. judge, Sept., 1938.

DRAYSON, GEORGE FREDERICK, Office of High Commr. for India (higher executive officer, store dept.) (b. 11th Dec., 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 8th Aug., 1904; on mily. service from Dec., 1915, to Sept., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; pers. asst. to high commr., July, 1927; actg. higher exec. officer, Oct., 1928; confd., June, 1929.

DREDGE, ALLAN LANCELOT AUSTIN, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 14th Nov., 1907).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1927; in mil. employ up to Oct., 1933; under-secty. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, Oct., 1933; officer on special duty; Peshawar, Dec., 1935; under-secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1936; officer on special duty, civil secretariat, Peshawar, Feb., 1936; asst. pol. agent N. Waziristan, Mar., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; vice-consul, Zahidan, Nov., 1937.

DREWE, GEOFFREY GRABHAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 3rd May, 1904).—Educ. at Cheltenham, Pembroke Coll., Oxford, and School of Oriental Studies, London. Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr., Mar., 1932; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1935.

DREYFUS, BERTRAM EDWARD, late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 8th July, 1890).—Educ. at City of London School, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1914, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Sept., 1916, to Jan., 1919; jt. mag., May, 1918; offg. dep. commr., April, 1923; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1927; offg. dep. commr. Dec., 1927; retd., Oct., 1931.

DRING, ARTHUR JOHN, Capt., I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 4th Nov., 1902).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1923; in mily. employ to June, 1927; asst. pte. secty. to govr.-genl., 16th July, 1931; asst. commr., Tank, Feb., 1934; ditto, Dira Ismail Khan, Feb., 1935; dep. secty. to the govr.-genl's. exec. council (tempy., May, 1936; pte. secty. to govr., N.W.F.P., Mar., 1937; secty., ditto, Apl., 1937.

D'ROZARIO, WILFRED ALBERT VICTOR, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 7th Feb., 1875).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1893; asst. supt., Oct., 1917; supt., Calcutta central tel. office, April, 1925; retd., Sept., 1929.

DRUCQUER, SETH, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 31st May, 1911).—Educ. at Winchester Coll., Trinity Hall, Cambridge, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte. and dep. collr., Nov., 1936.

DRUMMOND, JOHN GRAHAM, late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Oct., 1884).—Educ. at Loretto and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 5th Dec., 1908, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on special duty in connection with Arms Act Manual, May to June, 1914; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1914; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916 to Feb. 1919; mag. and collr., Jan., 1922; offg. sec., govt. of Bengal, local self-govt. dept., Feb., 1926; confd., June, 1926; offg. commr., Mar., 1929; retd., Feb., 1936.

DRUMMOND, ROBERT OLIVE, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., United Provs., 11th Oct., 1929; dep. consvr., Nov., 1934.

D'SOUZA, FRANK, Indian State Rlys. (dir., rly. bd.) (b. 6th Dec., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 28th Nov., 1902 to 15th Nov., 1918; offg. asst. traffic supt., Nov., 1918; confd., Jan., 1922; offg. dep. dir. of traffic and statistics, rly. bd., Mar., 1929; confd., May, 1932; offg. dir., ditto, Apl., 1931; May, 1932; confd., June, 1934.

D'SOUZA, MICHAEL, India Posts and Tels. (chief supt.) (b. 8th Jan., 1887).—Held non-gazetted posts from 3rd Aug., 1903; supt. tel. traffic, July, 1925; ch. supt., Jan., 1936.

DUBE, ANANT PARSHAD, Rai Bahadur, I.S.O. (b. 24th Mar., 1884).—Asst. Secty., govt. of India, def. dept.; confd., Mar., 1937; I.S.O., June, 1938.

DUBE, BHAGWATI CHARAN, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Jan., 1881).—Trained at T.C.E. Coll.; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1903, and posted to Central Provs.; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; under sec. to the chief commr., public wks. dept., June, 1916, to July, 1918; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1919; confd. as suptg. engr., Feb., 1923; retd., Nov., 1934.

DUBE, KAMLAKAR (see Kamlakar Dube).

DUBE, SHAMBHU NATH (see Shambhu Nath Dube).

DUBOIS, BASIL ALLEN, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 5th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1908; dep. supt., June, 1910; promoted to I.P.S. as supt., April, 1928.

DU BOULAY, Sir JAMES HOUSSEMAINE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Winchester and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bonibay as asst. collr., and mag., and forest settl. officer; 2nd asst., May, 1896; 1st asst., April, 1899; pte. sec. to the governor, Dec., 1903; C.I.E., June, 1906; junr. collr., March, 1907; sec. to govt., pol., spl. and judl. depts., April, 1909; priv. sec. to H.E. the Viceroy, Nov., 1910; K.C.I.E., Dec., 1911; commr., and pte. sec. to Viceroy, Dec., 1915; C.S.I., June, 1916; sec. to govt. of India, home dept., and addl. member, govr.-genl.'s legisl. council, from Oct., 1916; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Oct., 1917, to Feb., 1918; temp. member of exec. council, Bombay, March, 1918, to Sept., 1918; on depn. as member of jail cttee. from April, 1919; retd., July, 1922.

DUCKFIELD, WILLIAM EDMUND, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 28th June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 2nd Oct., 1920; addl. supt., March, 1924; offg. supt., April, 1928; confd., Aug., 1928; dep. commr., Calcutta, Oct., 1935.

DUGGAN, Sir JAMSHEDJI NASARVANJI, Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., L.M. & S., D.O. (Oxon.), F.C.P.S. (prof. of ophthalmic medicine and surgery, G.M. Coll., and supt., C.J. Ophthalmic Hosp., Bombay) (b. 8th April, 1884).—Apptd. prof. of ophthalmic medicine and surgery, C.J. ophthalmic hosp., 1st Jan., 1925; O.B.E., June, 1929; C.I.E., June, 1933; knighted, Jan., 1935.

DU HEAUME, FRANCIS HERBERT, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 27th May, 1897).—Joined the service, 27th Jan., 1920; asst. dist. officer, frontier constabulary, April, 1922; asst. supt., Dec., 1925; officer in charge, intell. branch, N.W.F.P., May, 1926; supt., Feb., 1927; dist. officer, frontier constabulary, May, 1929; O.B.E., Jan., 1932; princpl. trng. school Phillaur, Apl. 1934.

DUKE CHARLES BERESFORD, Indian C.S. Indian Political Service (b. 19th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived November, 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Feb., 1934; asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy, Nov., 1934, to Jan., 1938.

DUKE, JAMES ALEXANDER, Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., Central Provs.) (b. 26th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service, Nov., 1907; asst. dist. supt., June, 1909; dist. supt., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1930; confd., June, 1933.

DUKE, JOSEPH ALGERNON HARE. (b. 27th Mar., 1881).—Apptd. oil expert, technological institute, Cawnpore, 30th Oct., 1922; offg. princpl., ditto, Apl., 1926; offg. dir. of industries, U.P., July, 1932; retd., Mar., 1936.

DUKE, WILLIAM VICTOR, M.A., late Indian Educt. Service (b. 14th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 19th August, 1912, as prof., Ravenshaw Coll., Cuttack; inspr. of schools, June, 1915; asst. dir. of public instrn., Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1921; princpl., Greer Bhumihar Brahman Coll., Muzaffarpur, Oct., 1924; princpl., Ravenshaw Coll., July, 1931; retd., Aug., 1936.

DULAT, SHAMSHER SINGH, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 3rd April, 1904).—Educ. at University of the Punjab and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., April, 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1934; addl. ditto, July, 1934.

DUMBELL, PERCY HAROLD, M.A., late India Office.—Educ. at Wolverhampton Gr. Sch. and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. junr. clerk, rev. and stat. dept., 4th Feb., 1903; transf. to mil. dept., May, 1903; transf. to pol. dept., Oct., 1903; reading clerk to Council, Oct., 1903; private sec. to 1st under-sec. of State, Mar., 1906; asst. private sec. to sec. of state, and précis writer, March, 1907; senior clerk, March, 1910; sec. to cttee. on state technical scholarships, April to Sept., 1912; on mily. service from Jan., 1917, to Dec., 1918; asst. sec., Nov., 1919; ditto, judl. and public dept., Dec., 1919; dep. clerk of council, April, 1921; sec., services and general dept. and establishment officer, April, 1924; retd., June, 1929.

DUN, U. BA, Barr., Burma Judl Dept. (b. 21st June, 1884).—Apptd. dep. govt. advocate and secty. to Burma legisl. council, 28th Jan., 1926.

DUNBAR, ARCHIBALD, M.A., late Indian Police (b. 3rd Oct., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 4th Dec., 1906; dist. supt., Aug., 1917; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Dec., 1926; confd., June, 1933; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; retd., Feb., 1937.

DUNCAN, ARCHIBALD WALLACE, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar (civil surg.) (b. 11th July, 1884).—First commn. dated 29th July, 1911; in mil. employ up to 1st April, 1923; offg. civil surg., April, 1923; confd., Nov., 1924.

DUNCAN, JOHN ELDER, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 30th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1930.

DUNCAN, JOHN SAMUEL, B.Sc., F.G.S., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 20th April, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 25th Feb., 1921; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1928; confd., Feb., 1929; retd., Sept., 1932.

DUNCAN, PERCY RICHARD, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. convr., United Provs.) (b. 15th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service as forest engr., United Provs., on 1st Dec., 1921; dep. convr., Aug., 1930.

DUNCAN, ROBERT SYMON, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 27th Sept., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, on 25th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1934.

DUNDAS, AMBROSE DUNDAS FLUX, C.I.E., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 14th Apl., 1899).—Educ. at Harrow and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 14th Dec., 1922, and served in United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., March, 1924; jt. mag., Oct., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1925; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Sept., 1925; pers. asst. to C.-C. and A.G.G., N.W.F. Prov., Dec., 1925; asst. commr., Charsadda, Nov., 1927; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1928; ditto, Dir, Swat and Chitral, Apl., 1932; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P. (transfd. depts.), Nov. 1932; ch. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1933; dep. commr., Peshawar, Apl., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1936; employed on spl. duty under For. and Pol. dept., Nov., 1936; secty. to resdt. for Punjab states, Mar., 1937; chief secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1937.

DUNDON, GEORGE HEWITT, M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 11th May, 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; under-secty., public wks. dept. (irrign. branch), Mar., 1929; under-secty., Western admin., irrign. bch., June, 1937, to Oct., 1937.

DUNKLEY, HERBERT FRANCIS, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Rangoon) (b. 2nd July, 1886).—Educ. at Wellingborough Grammar Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1910, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; regr., chief court, Lower Burma, Jan., 1919, to Nov., 1921; dist. and sessions judge, Dec., 1922; offg. regr., high ct., Rangoon, Sept., 1926; acting judge, high ct., Rangoon, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1933.

DUNLOP, Rev. Canon DOUGLAS LYALL CHANDLIE, late Indian Eccles' Estab. (b. 5th Aug., 1881).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 22nd Oct., 1914; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1917; in milty. employ from 2nd Oct., 1918, to 16th June, 1919; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; ret'd., July, 1934.

DUNLOP, Sir R. W. L., Kt., C.I.E., D.S.O., late Indian Legisl. Dept.—Apptd. solicitor to govt. of India, Jan., 1920; Kt., June, 1925; ret'd., July, 1927.

DUNLOP, SIDNEY WILLIAM CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 9th May, 1914).—Educ. at Epsom Coll. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

DUNLOP, WALTER NIGEL USHER, B.A. (Oxon.), late Indian C.S. (Bomb y) (b. 5th Dec., 1892).—E.I. c. at Fettes Coll., and Christ Ch., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1919; arrived, 29th Dec., 1919, and served in Bombay

as asst. collr.; asst. judge, April, 1923; offg. judge and sess. judge March, 1925; offg. under-secty. to govt., home and eccl. depts., March, 1928; ret'd., March, 1929.

DUNN, ARTHUR GEORGE, Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt., Burma rlys.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1898).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1922, as asst. loco. supt.; dist. ditto, Burma rlys., May, 1929; offg. transptn. officer (power), Jan., 1931; offg. dep. carr. and wagon supt., Burma rlys., Mar., 1934.

DUNN, CHARLES WILLIAM, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th March, 1877).—Educ. at Bath Coll. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settlt. offr.; under sec. to govt., Dec., 1904, to Nov., 1906; dep. dir. of land records, Nov., 1909; offg. registrar under the Co-operative Societies Act, from May, 1914; dir. of agric., and asst. to regr., co-operative societies dept., July, 1915; dep. commr. and ditto, Sept., 1914; regr. co-op. societies, Oct., 1920, to Nov., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; offg. development commr., June, 1927; offg. finl. commr., July, 1927; confd., Dec., 1927; finl. commr. (transfd. subjects) and offg. ditto (reserved subjects), Oct., 1931; ret'd., Apl., 1935.

DUNN, CUTHBERT LINDSAY, C.I.E., D.P.H., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 15th May, 1875).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1902; in mil. employ to Dec., 1904; asst. plague medl. officer, United Prov., Dec., 1904; dep. sanitary commr., May, 1912; on mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to April, 1919; sanitary commr., July, 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; ret'd., May, 1930; re-employed after retirement as dir. of public health, United Provs., for two years from 15th Nov. 1930.

DUNN, JOHN ALEXANDER, D.Sc., D.I.C., F.G.S., Geological Survey Dept. (geologist) (b. 27th June, 1899).—Apptd. asst. supt., 17th Dec., 1921, curator, geol. museum, Calcutta, May, 1929; offg. supt., Apl., 1931.

DUNN, STANLEY GERALD, M.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Educl. Service (United Provs.) (b. 7th Oct., 1879).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1908, and apptd. profr., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad; empld. under govt. of India, army dept., from Jly to Sept., 1918, and from Oct., 1918, to March, 1919; profr., Allahabad univ., Oct., 1923; ret'd., Sept., 1931; author of 'The Treasure of the Sea and other Poems,' 'In England,' 'The Indian Treasury of English Verse,' &c.

DUNN, SYDNEY ERNEST, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 26th Nov., 1899).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 22nd Oct., 1919, after previous service from 25th April, 1917; second class clerk, 29th April, 1920; transfd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922.

DUNNETT, Sir JAMES MACDONALD, K.C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th April, 1877).—Educ. at Kilmarnock Acad., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and asst. settlmnt. offr.; junior sec. to fin. commrs., Nov., 1913, to Dec., 1916; dep. commr., Oct., 1917; C.I.E., June, 1922; sec. to govt. of Punjab,

home dept., Oct., 1924; offg. chief sec., Dec., 1925; offg. jt. sec., home dept., govt. of India, from Oct. to Dec., 1926; confd., Dec., 1926; on spl. duty, home dept., July, 1927; addl. secty., ditto, Nov., 1929; reforms commr., May, 1930; Kt., June, 1932; on depn. in connection with Indian round table conf., Nov.-Dec., 1932; K.C.I.E., June, 1934; retd., Nov., 1936.

DUNNICLIFF, HORACE BARRATT, M.A., D.Sc., F.I.C., Indian Educl. Service (princpl. govt. coll., Lahore) (b. 23rd Sept., 1885).—Joined the service as prof., Khalsa Coll., Amritsar, on 7th March, 1914; prof., govt. coll., Lahore, Aug., 1917; chemist, cordite factory, Aruvankadu, Nov., 1918; prof., govt. coll., Lahore, April, 1921; spl. chemical adviser for customs, Calcutta, Oct., 1928; princpl., govt. coll., Lahore, May, 1936.

DUNSDON, ARTHUR CULHAM, Indian State Railways (act. genl. manager, E.I. Rly.) (b. 28th June, 1887).—Joined the service, 9th Oct., 1913; resdt. engr., E.I. rly., Oct., 1921; exec. engr., ditto, Aug., 1927; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1934; dy. agent (styled dy. gl. mngr.), E.I. Rly., Mar., 1938.

DURAI, KANDAPPA, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (track supply offer, E.I. Rly.) (b. 12th May, 1894).—Joined the service, 26th Mar., 1920; asst. engr., Jan., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1928; exec. engr., Dec., 1937.

DURAIRAJA AYYAR, NARAYANASWAMI AYYAR, M.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 17th Mar., 1891).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 13th Oct., 1915; asst. engr., Oct., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

DURGA PRASAD ROHATGI, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 15th Feb., 1899).—Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1922; apprentice engr., June, 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1932.

DURGA PRASHAD, M.A., Rai Bahadur, late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 16th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service, 28th July, 1902; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1903; mag. and collr., Feb., 1926; offg. registrar, co-operative societies, July, 1926; confd., Nov., 1926; retd., Aug., 1933.

DURNO, LESLIE GEORGE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Bengal) (b. 27th July, 1892).—Educ. at Rugby and Univ. Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1921; arrived, 15th Nov., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. mag. and collr., Oct., 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1927; confd., June, 1928; offg. dep. sec. finance, commerce and marine depts., Bengal, May, 1932; confd., July, 1933; dep. commr., Mar., 1937.

DUROISELLE, CHARLES, M.A., late Archaeological Survey dept. (b. 25th June, 1871).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1904; apptd. supt., archaeological survey, Burma circle, 7th Dec., 1919; retd., June, 1931; offg. as supt. archae. survey, Mar., 1936.

DURRANT, GEORGE, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 9th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1914; in mil. employ from 12th Oct., 1914, to 5th Oct., 1922; asst. supt., Oct., 1922; offg. supt., Nov., 1925; supt. (prov.

subs.), Jan., 1935; confd., Feb., 1936; awarded King's police medal, Feb., 1937.

DURRELL, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian State Rlys. (elec. engr., Burma rlys.) (b. 6th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1914; offg. asst. elec. engr., Sept., 1919; confd., Jan., 1920; dist. elec. engr., Dec., 1927; offg. dep. loco. supt. (elec.), Oct., 1930.

DURST, THOMAS ORAM, India Office (higher executive officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 25th Nov., 1889).—Educ. at Emanuel Sch., Wandsworth Comm.; second div. clerk, bd. of edn., April, 1909, to Nov., 1911; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 27th Nov., 1911, and attd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; on mil. service from May, 1915, to Jan., 1919; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; higher executive officer, Sept., 1926; assessor of inc. tax, Jan., 1928.

DUTCH, ROBERT AUSTEN, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dy. collr., Bengal) (b. 22nd Oct., 1905).—Educ. at St. Olave's Grammar School and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. coll. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1936.

DUTT (see also Datta).

DUTT, BIRENDRA CHANDRA, M.A., Finance dept., govt. of India (b. 21st Nov., 1895).—Joined the service as probnr., 28th Feb., 1920; asst. acct. genl., Bengal, Nov., 1922; ditto, Madras, Feb., 1924; ditto central revs., Mar., 1926; currency officer, Lahore, Apl., 1927; ditto, Calcutta, Jan., 1931; exr., local fund acct., Bengal, Oct., 1933; exr., outside audit, Bengal, Apl., 1935.

DUTT, DEBPAI (b. 1st Apl., 1884).—Joined the service, 1905. Asst. secty., Legis. Assembly Dept., 10th Jan., 1929.

DUTT, DIVAKAR, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st June, 1915; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1921; exec. engr., June, 1924; offg. dep. agent, N.W. rly., May, 1935.

DUTT, GURU SADAY, Indian C.S., Barr. (secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Bengal) (b. 10th May, 1882).—Educ. at Govt. High Sch., Sylhet, and Calcutta Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 11th Dec., 1905; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1911; mag. and collr., Oct., 1917; sec. to govt. of Bengal, agric. and industry depts., June, 1924; offg. commr., Aug., 1927; offg. dir. of industries, Bengal, June, 1933; secty. to govt., Bengal, local self-govt., dept., Feb., 1934; K-i-H. Medal, 1st class, Jan., 1938.

DUTT, NARESH CHANDRA, B.A., Indian Posts and Tels. (dep. P.M.G.) (b. 7th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1905; supt. of post offices, Jan., 1909; dep. P.M.G., Dec., 1935.

DUTT, PAVITRA KUMAR, Office of High Commr. for India (educal. officer, supt. exec. grade, educn. dept.) (b. 25th Mar., 1888).—Apptd. educal. officer, 8th Feb., 1929; jt. social secty. to Indian delegation to round table conf., London, Oct., 1930; and again, Sept., 1931.

DUTT, PRATAP CHANDRA, late Indian C.S. (b. 4th Feb., 1876).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 25th Dec., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub.-collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; collr. and dist. mag., Aug., 1919; on foreign service under Travancore durbar, Mar., 1930; retd., Dec., 1934.

DUTT, RABINDRA CHANDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 5th Nov., 1912).—Educ. at Hare Sch., Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DUTT, SUBIMAL, B.Sc.(Cal.), Indian C.S. (under secty., dept. of e.h. and l. (b. 13th Mar., 1905).—Educ. at University of Calcutta and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Oct., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1929; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1935; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1936; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1936; addtl. under secty., e.h. and l dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1938.

DUTTON, HUGH REGINALD, C.I.E., F.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.), late Medical Dept., Bihar and Orissa (member, medl. bd., India Office) (b. 21st Nov., 1875).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1902; in mil. employ till March, 1920; supt., central jail, Bhagalpur, May, 1920; civil surgeon, Patna, March, 1922; prinpl., Prince of Wales medical coll., Patna, March 1925; C.I.E., June, 1930; retd., Nov., 1930; addl. member, medl. bd., India Office, Jan., 1931; examr. of surgical instruments, India store dept., Apl., 1932, to May, 1934; member, medl. bd., India Office, May, 1934.

DYER, REV. BASIL SAUNDERS, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 28th Feb., 1880).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1909; junr. chaplain, Aug., 1910; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1922; retd., July, 1933.

DYER, JAMES FERGUSON, C.I.E., M.A. late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd July, 1880).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ., Glasgow; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the Central Provs., as asst. commr., and as regr., judl. commr.'s court; settlt. offr., Feb., 1915; 3rd sec. to the chief commr., Dec., 1916; dep. commr., and ditto, July, 1917; ditto and commr. of settlt. and dir. of land records, &c., Nov., 1922; offg. commr., Jan., 1926; C.I.E., March, 1929; commr., July, 1929; retd., Nov., 1932.

DYSON, WILLIAM GEOFFREY, M.C., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 7th Sept., 1892).—Joined the service, 6th Dec., 1915, as asst. consvr.; on mil. duty from 6th Dec., 1915, to 3rd Feb., 1919; dep. consvr., Dec., 1920; instr., Madras forest coll., May, 1923; prinpl., ditto, Aug., 1923; dist. forest officer, July, 1924; actg. consvr., variously since Sept., 1929; provl. sylviculturist, July, 1931.

E, U BA, B.A., late Burma Civil Service (b. 15th May, 1876).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., 1st July, 1916; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1928; confd., Feb., 1931; retd., May, 1932; apptd. member, public serv. commn., Burma, Apl., 1937.

EADES, GERALD JOHN, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., signals, N.W. Rly.) (b. 20th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. signal engr., 18th Dec., 1912; offg. dist. signal engr., Jan., 1925; confd., Mar., 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr., signals, N.W. rly., Nov., 1933; confd., Aug., 1934.

EADES, JOHN HENRY, P.W. Deptt., Burma (elec. inspr.) (b. 21st Sept., 1893).—Joined the service as elec. engr., Nov., 1926; elec. insp., Dec., 1930.

EAGEN, THOMAS GEORGE ROYAL, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (asst. exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 12th June, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st. Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1921; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1926.

EASSON, GEORGE ALEXANDER, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd Dec., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1904; served as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; and under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, P.W.D., March, 1914; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1921; confd. as suptg. engr., Dec., 1923; offg. ch. engr., June, 1929; confd. Sept., 1929; retd., Aug., 1933.

EASTMAN NAGLE, JAMES NICHOLAS, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) E.B. Rly.) (b. 7th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 25th March, 1913; dist. traffic supt., July, 1926; publicity officer, Nov., 1928.

EASTMOND, ARCHIBALD, M.C., M.Inst.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 11th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1913; in mil. employ from 16th May, 1915, to 16th Feb., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1922.

EASTON, THOMAS WILLIAM ROBERTSON, Indian Service of Engrs. (under secty. to govt. (constrn.), Punjab) (b. 29th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr. 20th Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Dec., 1926; confd., Oct., 1929; under secty. to govt. (constrn.), Burma, Feb., 1936.

EASTWELL, WILFRED HAROLD, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer) (b. 1st Mar., 1898).—Apptd. to India Office, store dept., as clerical officer, 1st Dec., 1919, after previous service in General Post Office from 22nd Oct., 1915; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Oct., 1919; transfid. to office of high commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; exec. officer, June, 1922; actg. hr. exec. offcr., Sept.-Nov., 1936.

EASTWICK, REV. ROWLAND, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (junr. chaplain) (b. 16th Aug., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 6th Oct., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931.

EATES, KENNETH RAYE, Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 30th July, 1894).—Joined the service, 10th Feb., 1920, with prev. mil. service; Indian police medal, Jan., 1934; prom. to I.P. as supt., Apl., 1937.

EAYRES, VERNON JOHN GEORGE, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. offcr., store dept.) (b. 17th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Strand Sch., King's Coll.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, 4th Sept., 1908; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to April, 1917; transfid. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; personal asst. to high commr., Oct., 1928, to Nov., 1936; hr. exec. officer, Nov., 1936.

ERDEN, JAMES WYLDE, B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 6th Feb., 1882).—Joined the service, 9th Feb., 1906, as prob. ; asst. acctnt. gen., April, 1909 ; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), from April, 1915, to May, 1920 ; auditor of govt. of India sanctions (civil), Aug., 1921 ; dep. acctnt. gen., United Prov., Nov., 1923 ; offg. dep. auditor-gen., Nov., 1925 ; offg. dep. dir. of army audit, E.C., Nov., 1926 ; off. dir. of army audit, June, 1927 ; dep. dir., army audit, Nov., 1927 ; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Apl., 1929 ; offg. acct. genl., B. & O., Apl., 1930 ; acct. genl., Burma, Nov., 1932 ; dir. of audit, defence services, Oct., 1936 ; retd., Apl., 1937.

EBDEN, JOHN ALFRED WYLDE, M.D., M.S., F.R.C.S., Major, I.M.S., Medical Dept., Madras (dist. medl. officer) (b. 20th Feb., 1891).—First commn., 6th Dec., 1929 ; dist. medl. officer, May, 1934.

EDGAR, SAMUEL GARTHWAITE, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 8th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., United Provs., on 1st Oct., 1911 ; exec. engr., Oct., 1919 ; on foreign service in Jodhpur, Nov., 1929.

EDGCOMBE, GEORGE AUBREY VALLETORT, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 9th Feb., 1931.

EDGECOMBE, ARTHUR RALPH BROOKS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 14th Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., United Provs., 1st Oct., 1924 ; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1930 ; confd., Oct., 1933.

EDGLEY, NORMAN GEORGE ARMSTRONG, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 19th June, 1888).—Educ. at schools in Switzerland, and New Coll., Oxford ; apptd. after exam. of 1910 ; arrived in India, 5th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr. ; transfld. to Bengal, April, 1912 ; under-sec., judl., poll. and appt. depts., Bengal, June, 1916 ; regr., high court, Calcutta, appellate side, April, 1918 ; dist. and sessions judge, Jan., 1924 ; services placed at disposal of govt. of Assam, Oct., 1927 ; Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 1st class, Jan., 1930 ; offg. secty. to govt., judl. dept., Bengal, and supt., and remembrancer, legal affairs, Mar., 1933 ; confd., July, 1933 ; offg. judge high ct., Calcutta, variously from June, 1934.

EDMONDS, ALBERT CHARLES, B.A., Indian Agric. Service (b. 2nd Nov., 1895).—Apptd. dep. dir. of agric., Madras, 5th Dec., 1920.

EDMONDS, Ven. Canon HERBERT JAMES, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Archdeacon of Madras) (b. 20th Mar., 1886).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., July, 1917 ; junr. chaplain, July, 1918 ; senr. chaplain, July, 1928 ; canon, 1932 ; archdeacon, 1937.

EDMUNDS, PERCY JAMES, M.A., B.Sc., India Posts and Tels. (dep. chief engr.) (b. 1st Feb., 1890).—Joined the service as physician and research officer, wireless branch, 5th Dec., 1919 ; divl. engr., Oct., 1921 ; offg. dir. of wireless, Apl., 1916 ; confd., Apl., 1927 ; contr. of broadcasting in addn., Mar., 1935 ; dep. dir. genl., wireless, Apl., 1936 ; del. to internl. conf., Cairo, 1938 ; dep. chief engr., June, 1938.

EDWARDS, ARTHUR JOHN CHARLES, F.I.A., Office of High Commr. for India (chief accounting officer) (b. 24th July, 1883).—Educ. at Aske's Hatcham Sch. ; entered Natl. Debt. Office, May, 1903 ; pte. sec. to contr., Oct., 1911, to July, 1913 ; apptd. junior clerk, India Office, acctnt. gen. s. dept., 5th July, 1913 ; senr. clerk, Feb., 1920 ; services lent to high commr. for India from April, 1921 ; clerk to commrs. of income tax, April, 1921, to April, 1922, and from Oct., 1922 ; asst. chief accounting officer, 1st April, 1922 ; deputy ditto, 21st April, 1922 ; apptd. Indian income tax officer, June, 1924 ; chief accountg. officer, and commr. of inc. tax., Nov., 1934.

EDWARDS, C. A. H., late Indian State Railways (b. 10th June, 1879).—Trained from the R.I.E. Coll. ; joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1903 ; exec. engr., Jan., 1913 ; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1925 ; offg. engr. in chief, E.B. Rly., Feb., 1927 ; dep. ch. engr. (provl.), Sept., 1927 ; offg. ch. engr., Oct., 1927 ; retd., Mar., 1934.

EDWARDS, CHARLES CYRIL ROGERS, D.S.C., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 15th July, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 6th Jan., 1921 ; exec. engr., Burma rlys., Mar., 1934.

EDWARDS, COSMO GRANT NIVEN, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 13th Aug., 1896).—First commn., dated 19th June, 1915 ; joined the pol. dept., 8th March, 1921 ; asst. commr., Kohat, April, 1922 ; ditto, Mardan, April, 1926 ; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Nov., 1926 ; dist. judge, Peshawar, Dec., 1928 ; dep. commr., D.I.
