Khan, Apl., 1929 ; offg. under-secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., April, 1930 ; and again, Mar., 1931 ; offg. dep. sec. for. and pol. dept., June, 1930 ; and again, Sept., 1932 ; secty. to resdt. in Mysore, Feb., 1934 ; pol. agent, Kalat, Apl., 1938.

EDWARDS, EDWIN NORMAN, India Police (b. 1st Oct., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., 10th Nov., 1935.

EDWARDS, JOHN ASHWORTH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 17th Feb., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Burma, 23rd Dec., 1931 ; confd., Jan., 1934.

EDWARDS, OSBORNE GEORGE, V.D. Indian State Rlys. (contr. of stores, G.I.P., Rly.) (b. 17th June, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 24th Nov., 1905 ; resdt. engr., Dec., 1911 ; in milty employ from 6th Nov., 1916, to 24th Nov., 1920 ; dist. engr., Aug., 1924 ; offg. contr. of stores, May, 1932 ; confd., Aug., 1932.

EDWARDS, PERCY ASHWORTH, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 31st July, 1877).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 21st March, 1902 ; dist. ditto, Jan., 1914 ; divl. transportation supt., Nov., 1922 ; offg. contr. of stores Apl., 1930 ; confd., Nov., 1931 ; retd., May, 1934.

EDWARDS, WILLIAM CECIL, late Indian Police (b. 19th Sept., 1888).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1909, as asst. supt., Bombay ; on milty. duty from Dec., 1914, to Apl., 1917 ; supt. of police, Sept., 1922 ; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930.

EDYE, ERNEST HENRY HUIISH, M.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd June, 1884).—Educ. at Harrow, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrd., 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; jt. mag., July, 1917; M.B.E., Jan., 1919, supt., census operations, United Provs., Apl., 1920; dep. commr., Apl., 1922; jt. mag., Aug., 1922; settl. officer, Nov., 1923; mag. and collr., Sept., 1924; offg. commr., Apl., 1935; retd., Apl., 1938.

EGAN, ERIC MARTIN, Indian State Rlys. (divl. Transpn. officr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 17th Oct., 1894).—Asst. Traffic supt., Oct., 1919; divl. transpn. officr., May, 1935.

EGAN, THOMAS JARLETTE, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Vety. Service (dir. of vety. services), United Provs. (b. 5th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., March, 1922; supt., April, 1925; profrr., Punjab vety. coll., Nov., 1933; dir. of vety. services, United Provs., July, 1934.

EGERTON, WILLIAM LE BELWARD, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 1st Dec., 1901).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1924, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy, March, 1927; asst. pol. agent, Kalat, Dec., 1929; under-secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in C.I., Nov., 1930; secty., ditto, Feb., 1931; offg. pol. agent, Bhopal, in addn. Aug., 1935; officr. on spec. duty, pol. dept., Apl., 1937; dep. secty., pol. dept., May, 1937; pol. agent, W. Kathiawar Agency, Oct., 1937; ditto, E. agency, May, 1938; on spec. duty in pol. dept., Nov., 1938.

EGGAR, SIR ARTHUR, M.A., Barr., Burma Judl. Dept. (advocate genl., Burma) (b. 22nd May, 1877).—Apptd. acting asst. govt. advocate, 7th May, 1911; law officer to R.A.F., Middle-East, 1918-19; apptd. dep. govt. advocate and sec. to Burma legisl. council, Dec., 1922; offg. govt. advocate, Mar., 1924; confd., Dec., 1925; adv.-genl., Apl., 1937; knighted, Jan., 1938.

EKAMBARAM, KOLIPAKKAM VENKATISA, Indian Service of Engineers (asst. exec. engr., Madras) (b. 2nd June, 1907).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Madras, 13th Aug., 1932.

EKANATHA RAJARAM, RAO, B.A. (Hons.), Imperial Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1901).—Joined the service, Mar., 1926; asst. coll. of customs, July, 1927.

ELATI VALIAGATH AMU SAHIB (see Amu Sahib, E. V.).

ELCOCK, ROWLAND EDWARD, V.C., M.M., Indian Posts and Tels. (divl. engr.) (b. 5th June, 1899).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1923, as asst. div. engr.; divl. engr., Oct., 1932.

ELLIOT, HENRY HAWES, M.B.E., M.C., M.B., F.R.C.S.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (surg. to Viceroy) (b. 22nd May, 1891).—First commn., 3rd Dec., 1921; offg. civil surg., New Delhi, Apl., 1929; agency surg., Apl., 1932; legn. surg., Kabul, Aug., 1933; surg. to Viceroy, Apl., 1936.

ELLIOT, WALTER, late Indian Police Service (United Provs.) (b. 21st Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 28th Jan., 1911, as asst. supt.; in mil. employ from 28th May, 1918 to 23rd Aug., 1919; supt., R.M. Rly. Police, Ajmer, Oct., 1925, to April, 1927; offg. supt., April, 1927; retd., Sept., 1930.

ELLIOTT, SIR IVO D'OYLY, Bart., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 7th March, 1882).—Educ. at Harrow, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Aug., 1912; jt. mag., Nov., 1914; on spl. duty in govt. sectt., April to May, 1915; under-sec. to govt. of India, com. and industry dept., Jan., 1916; temp. dep. sec., ditto, Nov., 1917, to Jan., 1919; dep. commr., Nov., 1919; mag. and collr., Sept., 1922; offg. rev. and judl. sec. to govt., United Provs., April, 1925; pub. health sec. to govt., April, 1926; secty. to govt., local self-govt. and public health dept., Oct., 1927; retd., Mar., 1932.

ELLIOTT, JACK, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 5th Jan., 1891).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1910; asst. supt., July, 1912; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1916; awarded bar to ditto, Jan., 1923; supt. (prov.), April, 1925; supt., govt. rlwy. police, Oct., 1926; pers. asst. to dep. inspr.-genl., C.I.D., Sept., 1927; asst. inspr. genl., May, 1929; and again, Apl., 1933; dist. supt., Sept., 1930; offg. commr. of police; Madras, Mar., 1936; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1936; commr. for town of Madras, Oct., 1937; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1938.

ELLIOTT, JAMES STURGESS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 2nd Mar., 1914).—Educ. at Alderman Newton's Sch., Leicester and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938 and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

ELLIOTT, REV. THOMAS REGINALD HATCHARD, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Bombay) (b. 5th Aug., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, Bombay, 27th Oct., 1927; junr. chaplain, Oct. 1927; senr. ditto, Oct., 1937.

ELLIS, EDWARD EARDLEY, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 26th July, 1880).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1897; asst. traffic manager, July, 1910; offg. asst. supt., March, 1913; confd., March, 1914; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1919; confd., Jan., 1922; asst. comml. manager, Oct., 1926; offg. dep. ch. comml. manager, April, 1927, and again, Feb., 1934; confd., June, 1934; retd., Aug., 1936.

ELLIS, SAMUEL FRANCIS, late Indian Police (b. 8th Jan., 1882).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1902, and posted to Bombay; supt. of police, Feb., 1912; retd., Feb., 1934.

ELLIS, THOMAS HOBART, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 11th Oct., 1894).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 31st Jan., 1919; arrived, 31st March, 1919, and posted to Bengal; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1924; offg. ditto, Feb., 1930; confd., July, 1930.

ELMES, ARCHIBALD JAMES, India Audit Office (higher exec. officer) (b. 8th Jan., 1893).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 6th Aug., 1914; on milly. service, Jan., 1917, to March, 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; transf. to India Audit Office, Feb., 1922; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1933.

ELMES, JULIUS PAUL JONATHAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 1st Nov., 1887).—Educ. at St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; temply. to mil. duty, Oct., 1915, to Feb., 1920; offg. mag. and collr. and dep. commr., March, 1920; offg. dep. commr., April, 1924; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1925; confd., July, 1927; manager, Bettiah wards estate, Jan., 1933.

ELMORE, ALBERT EDWARD, late Indian Forest Service (b. 24th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1912; dep. consvr., Burma, Nov., 1923; ret'd., Jan., 1938.

ELMSLIE, CYRIL STEWART, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 10th Apl., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt. Dec., 1913; supt., Aug., 1925.

ELWIN, DONALD HAMILTON, Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 15th April, 1904).—Educ. at Bradfield and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-secty. public dept., Madras, May, 1932; offg. under-secty. to govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1934; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Sept., 1934; pte. secty. to the Gov. of Madras, Sept., 1935; comp. to nephew of Maharaja of Mysore, June, 1938.

ELWIN, REGINALD BARNES, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th June, 1908).—Educ. at Bradfield and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov. 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to govt., Punjab, pol. and home dept., May, 1933; offg. judge, small cause ct., Simla, Mar., 1934; att'd., home dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1937.

EMERSON, DONALD BOMFORD, B.A., B.A.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 5th Feb., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1907; in mil. employ from 16th May, 1915, to 29th Feb., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1926; confd., Apl., 1930; ret'd., July, 1936.

EMERSON, GERALD HERBERT, Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Rugby and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1935; offg. asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1935; secty. to A.G.G., Eastern states, Mar., 1936; asst. commr., Nowshera sub-div., Nov., 1937.

EMERSON, SIR HERBERT WILLIAM, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., C.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 1st June, 1881).—Educ. at Calday Grange Gr. Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., manager, Bashahr State, June, 1911, to March, 1914; supt. and settlt. offr., Mandi State, March, 1915; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agri. dept., Oct., 1915; again supt. and settlement offr., Mandi State, Jan., 1916; asst. commr. and settlt. offr., Punjab, Oct., 1917; dep. commr., Jan., 1922;

C.B.E., Jan., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; sec. to govt., fin. dept., April, 1926; ch. sec., Nov., 1927; secty. to govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1930; C.S.I., June, 1931; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1933; govr. of Punjab, Apl., 1933 to Apl., 1938; G.C.I.E., May, 1937.

EMINSON, BASIL FRANKLIN, M.B., B.S. (Lon.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Sind) (b. 24th Sept., 1887).—First commn., dated 27th July, 1912; in milty. employ prior to 27th Nov., 1923; offg. civil surg., May, 1925; civil surg., Hyderabad, May, 1933.

EMMER, ANDREW FRANK, I.S.O., late For. and Pol. dept. (b. 10th May, 1880).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st May, 1896; treasury officer, Hyderabad resdy., Oct., 1918; asst. secty., ditto, Jan., 1927; asst. secty., for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1929; offg. under secty., ditto, Sept., 1932, and May, 1934; ret'd., Apl., 1935.

ENGLAND, FREDERICK SAMUEL EDWARD, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 2nd July, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 24th Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

ENGLAND, REV. CANON HERBERT GEORGE, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 7th Jan., 1880).—Joined the service as junior chaplain, 4th Nov., 1912; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1922; ret'd., May, 1934.

ENGLAND, WALTER BRASSINGTON, M.A., Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, public dept.) (b. 4th April, 1893).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. to India Office, 4th April, 1921; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, April, 1922; exec. officer, Jan., 1924; higher clerical officer, June, 1927; actg. higher exec. officer, Sept., 1929, to Aug., 1930; hr. exec. offcr. (temp.), Apl., 1938.

ENGLEFIELD, ARTHUR GUY, Indian State Rlys. (divl. mechl. engr., N.W. rly.) (b. 13th July, 1893).—Joined the service, asst. loco. supt., Dec., 1919; asst. rolling stock officer, July, 1926; asst. works manager, N.W. rly., Dec., 1932; divl. mechl. engr., N.W. rly. (prov.), Aug., 1934; confd., Sept., 1936.

ENGLISH, ERIC ERLE, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 24th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 6th Sept., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1925; confd., Feb., 1930.

ENGLISH, WALTER, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 1st Aug., 1877).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1898; dist. opium officer, July, 1924; ret'd., July 1932.

ENSOR, ERIC LOVELL, Indian State Rlys. (asst. telegraph supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 25th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. telg. supt., 4th Oct., 1921; asst. transpn. supt. (tels.), 1st Nov., 1922; offg. dep. ditto, Apl., 1932, and again, Sept., 1935.

ERACH MANEKSHAH NANAVATI (see Nanavati, E. M.).

ERSKINE, CLAUDE ERNEST TORIN, C.I.E., D.S.O., M.C., Col. I.A., ret'd. (pers. asst. to secty., mil. dept., India Office) (b. 1st May, 1887).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1906; in mil. employ to Apl., 1938; pers. asst. to secty., mil. dept., India Office, Sept., 1938.

ERSKINE, LORD, JOHN FRANCIS ASHLEY, G.C.I.E. (Govr. of Madras) (b. 26th Apl., 1895).—Educ. at Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; asst. pte. secty. to First Lord of Admy., 1920-1921; parly. pte. secty. to P.M.G., 1923; princpl. pte. secty. to Home Secretary, 1924; M.P. for Weston-super-Mare Divn. of Somerset, 1922-1923 and again 1924; asst. govt. whip in Natl. Govt., 1932; G.C.I.E., Aug., 1934; Govt. of Madras, 15th Nov., 1934.

EUSTACE, EDWARD ARTHUR RAWLINS, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 18th Nov., 1899).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and R.M.Coll., Sandhurst; joined the service, 4th Sept., 1923; arrived, 14th Nov., 1923, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., March, 1925; addl. under-sec. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., &c., Dec., 1927; offg. regr. high ct., Lahore, Jan., 1930; offg. dep. commr., Feby., 1931; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Nov., 1931; confd., July, 1935.

EUSTACE, FRED ARTHUR, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 15th Feby., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 8th Oct., 1920; exec. engr., April, 1928; retd., Nov., 1932.

EUSTACE, FREDERICK ROBERT HARRINGTON, D.S.O., Col., R.E. (retd.), late Indian State Railways (b. 14th Aug., 1875).—First commn. dated 8th Aug., 1895; joined the Public Works Dept. as asst. engr., Febr., 1898; exec. engr., Oct., 1908; on milty. duty from Sept., 1914, to Sept., 1919; supt. engr., Dec., 1922; offg. dir., civil engrg., May, 1925; ch. engr., March, 1925; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of rlys., May, 1927; retd., Oct., 1929.

EUSTACE, JOHN CURTIS WEINHER, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 22nd Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Wellington; Brentwood Coll.; Victoria, British Columbia and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service 9th Oct., 1929; arrived Nov., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1933; dep. regr., co-op. societies, Punjab, Apl., 1935; offg. regr., ditto, Mar., 1936, and again Oct., 1936.

EVANS, AIDAN OSWALD, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 26th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 30th Oct., 1909; offg. dist. ditto, Febr., 1915; offg. dep. traffic manager, Aug., 1921; with Indian Rly. conference ascen., April, 1922; supt., July, 1925; ditto, operating dept., Oct., 1926; offg. secty. to agent, E.I. Rly., March, 1928; offg. divl. supt., March, 1929; offg. dep. agent, E.I. Rly., Dec., 1929; offg. divi. supt., Oct., 1930; and again, Nov., 1932; confd., Jan., 1936.

EVANS, ARTHUR GARBETT, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 6th May, 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 1st Nov., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933.

EVANS, ARTHUR LAUREN FANE, late Indian Police (b. 17th Jan., 1886).—Joined the service 31st May, 1907; asst. supt., Nov., 1910; supt., July, 1920; asst. inspr.-genl., govt. rly. police, April, 1927; retd., Nov., 1933.

EVANS, ELWYN RICHARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 5th Nov., 1914).—Educ. at Brynmawr County Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938 and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

EVANS, ERNEST SYDNEY, late India Audit Office (b. 16th Febr., 1876).—Clerk, 2nd class, 11th March, 1895; staff clerk, Febr., 1915; junior clerk, Dec., 1921; suptg. exec. officer, April, 1922; retd., Mar., 1936.

EVANS, FREDERICK BUISSON, C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trin Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 5th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spcl. asst. settlt. officer March, 1901; asst. sec. to govt., Jan., 1904; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1904; ditto, in depts. under chief sec., May, 1906; head asst. collr. and mag., March, 1909; sub-collr. and jt. mag., May, 1910; collr. and dist. mag., Dec., 1916; C.S.I., Jan., 1922; on spl. duty in chief secretariat, March, 1925; tempy. sec. to govt., pub. wks. dept., May, 1925; sec. to govt., pub. wks. dept., Oct., 1925; offg. 2nd sec. to govt., Dec., 1925; confd., Aug., 1926; retd., Oct., 1928.

EVANS, SIR GEOFFREY, Kt., C.I.E., M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Agricl. Service.—Apptd. principal, agric. coll., Nagpur, 24th Oct., 1906; second dep. dir. of agric., Central Prov., Nov., 1907; sec., Central Prov. war board, Aug., 1917; on duty under govt. of India, army dept., from May, 1918, to May, 1919 (dir. of agric., Mesopotamia); C.I.E., June, 1919; on deputn. to Burma, June, 1919; dir. of agric., Bengal, July, 1920; retd., Febr., 1925; knighted, Jan., 1934.

EVANS, GEORGE EMLYN THOMAS HULSE, M.C., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 18th June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., March, 1925; confd. Jan., 1932.

EVANS, HUBERT JOHN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 21st Nov., 1904).—Educ. at City of London School, Jesus Coll., Cambridge, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 14th Oct., 1929; arrived 27th Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag., Mar., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1936.

EVANS, LEONARD GLYDE LAVINGTON, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S., and Indian Political Service (b. 9th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Harrow, New Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 26th Nov., 1912, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on temp. service under govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., as addl. asst. master, Mayc Coll., March, 1915; on spl. duty under pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, April, 1918; offr. of poi. dept., govt. of India, from Jan., 1919; under-sec. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Aug., 1922; on foreign service in Jodhpur State from April, 1923; sec. to resdt. in Mysore and sec. to ch. commr. of Coorg, Febr., 1925; sec. to agent to govr.-genl. in Rajputana and ch. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, April, 1927; pol. agent, Baghelkhand, Nov., 1928; pol. agent, Bhopal, March, 1929; ditto, Southern States of C.I. and Malwa, Oct., 1931; Secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in Rajputana and ch. commr. in Ajmer-Merwara, Mar., 1932; pol. agent, E. Rajputana states and presdt., council of state, Bharatpur, June, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; A.G.G., E. states, Febr., 1935; judl. commr., Coorg, Nov., 1935; offg. resdt., Mysore, Nov., 1935; retd., Mar., 1938.

EVANS, Sir T. J. CAREY (see Carey-Evans, Sir T. J.).

EVANS, VICTOR DONALD CHAPPELL, Indian Police (b. 8th Jan., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 11th Nov., 1935.

EVANS, WILLIAM HUGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 24th June, 1892).—Educ. at County Sch., Pwllheli, and Univ. Coll., Bangor; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1921; arrived, 30th Nov., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; confd., Sept., 1927.

EVANS-GORDON, KENMURE ALICK GARTH, Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Aug., 1885).—First commn. dated 24th Jan., 1906; in mil. employ to 11th May, 1913, and again from 13th Oct., 1914; services lent to Rewa Durbar from 1st Jan., 1919; asst. commr Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1924; sec. to resdt. at Mysore and sec. to ch. commr., Coorg, Feb., 1925; pol. agent, Baghelkhand, March, 1929; ditto, E. Kathiawar agency, Nov., 1930; vice-presdt., council of regency, Cooch Behar State, Mar., 1931; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar Agency, Nov., 1934; offg. resdt., Baroda, May, 1935; resdt., Jaipur, Nov., 1935; offg. A.G.G., Deccan states, June, 1936; resdt. for Kolhapur and Deccan States, Apl., 1937; ditto, June, 1938.

EVERALL, WILLIAM TEAGUE, O.B.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th Aug., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. bridge engr., 7th Dec., 1908; O.B.E., June, 1924; bridge engr. (s.p.t.), Oct., 1924; confd., Nov., 1926; dep. ch. engr. (bridges), Nov., 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

EVERETT, CECIL HOWARD, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 9th Sept., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 6th Jan., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1930; supt. (subs. provl.), Mar., 1936; confd., Oct., 1936.

EVERETT, GEORGE ELIOT, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 10th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th Sept., 1904; resdt. engr., June, 1910; senr. resdt. engr., July, 1919; dist. engr., Oct., 1921; offg. supt. engr., Feb., 1928; ret'd., June, 1934.

EVERS, Rev. MERVYN SAXELBVE, M.A., M.C., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 8th Sept., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 18th May, 1924; junr. chaplain, May, 1926; senr. ditto, May, 1932.

EVERSHED, WALLIS ALBURY, B.Sc., (Lond.), A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd July, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1913; asst. engr., Nov., 1913; in milty. employ from 9th Aug., 1916, to 5th Aug., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. supt. engr., Feb., 1929; ret'd., July, 1937.

EWART, Sir JOHN MURRAY, Kt., C.I.E., Indian Police (dir. intel. bureau, home dept.) (b. 23rd Sept., 1884).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1905; supt. of police, March, 1913; dist. offr., frontier constabulary, Hazara, May, 1919; granted King's police medal, Jan., 1919; C.I.E., June, 1924; on foreign service with Sudan govt. for 1 yr. from 12th Nov., 1924; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., April, 1926; dep. inspr.-genl. (provl.), Dec., 1928; confd., Aug., 1930; offg. inspr.-genl. of police and under-sec., home (police) dept., Punjab, Apl., 1932; confd., Aug., 1932; knighted, Jan., 1936; dir., intel. bureau, home dept., Mar., 1936.

EWBANK, ROBERT BENSON, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 22nd Oct., 1883).—Educ. at Carlisle Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd., after exam. of 1906; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1907, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; regr., co-op. creditsoc., Bombay Presy., from May, 1913; on deputn. as sec., commee. on co-operation in India, Oct., 1914, to June, 1915; offg. dep. sec., commerce dept., govt. of India, 1910; confd., April, 1921; dep. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agri., March, 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; on depn. as sec., Colonies Cttee., March to July, 1924; on depn. in connection with Southborough Cttee., Jan., 1925; dep. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., &c., Aug., 1925; offg. sec. to ditto, Sept., 1925, and again, Dec., 1926; sec., Back Bay enquiry commee., July, 1926; collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, Feb., 1927; member of East African deputation, Oct., 1927, to April, 1928; offg. secty. to govt., genl., edl. and marine depts., Mar., 1929; confd., June, 1929; member legis. council, 1929 and 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; ret'd., July, 1936; author of "Manual for Co-operative Societies in the Bombay Presidency."

EWING, ROBERT ROSS, Lt.-Col., I.A. (dep. commr., Burma).—Arrived in India 3rd Oct., 1912; served in mil. dept. until June, 1916, when he was apptd. asst. commdt.; asst. adjt., July, 1917; asst. commr., Nov., 1920; and sub-divnl. officer Aug., 1921; warden of oilfields, Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., March, 1924, Oct., 1926, and again Feb., 1931; confd., May, 1932; chairman, Rangoon devel. trust, June, 1937.

EYERS, CYRIL, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 27th Sept., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. dist. loco. supt., 4th Nov., 1905; dist. ditto, May, 1915; divl. supt. (provl.), Feb., 1925; supt., March, 1926; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., April, 1927; confd., Apl., 1931; dep. ch. mechl. engr. (running), July, 1933; ret'd., July, 1935.

EYRE, MONTAGUE HENRY, late Indian Police Service (United Provs.) (b. 11th Sept., 1882).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1902, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt. of police, Feb., 1908; supt., June, 1914; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), May, 1918, to April, 1919; ret'd., July, 1930.

EZECHIEL, CHARLES ERNEST, late Indian Police Service (Bengal) (b. 5th Nov., 1874).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1893, and served as asst. supt.; dist. supt., Nov., 1903; (s.p.t.) supt., Feb., 1914; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., April, 1924; confd., July, 1935; ret'd., Nov., 1929.

FABRE-TONNERRE, JOSEPH, late Indian Police (Bihar) (b. 20th Apl., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 26th July, 1909; supt., Nov., 1919; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

FAGAN, Sir PATRICK JAMES, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 26th Nov., 1886, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; dep. commr., Nov., 1896; commr., Dec., 1910; offg. finl. commr., 1916; confirmed, Nov., 1916; C.S.I., Jan., 1917; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1922; ret'd., 1923.

FAIRLEY, FRANCIS NOEL, Office of High Commr. for India (temp. sup. exec. officer, accounts dept.) (b. 11th Dec., 1883).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store depôt, 17th March, 1903; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1923; temp. suptg. exec. officer, Apl., 1938.

FAIRLEY, JOHN, B.Sc., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 7th June, 1880).—Joined the telegraph service, 1st Oct., 1902, as asst. supt.; supt. and asst. to chief engr., March, 1914; suptg. engr., April, 1917; divl. engr., tels., Nov., 1921; offg. dir. tel. engrg., Punjab and N.W.F. circle, Feb., 1930; confd., Mar., 1931; ditto, Burma, June, 1932; offg. P.M.G., Burma, June, 1932, and again, Mar., 1934; dir. tel. engrg., Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1932; retd., June, 1935.

FAIRWEATHER, CHARLES EDWARD STUART, C.I.E., M.A., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 16th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 26th Nov., 1909; supt., April, 1923; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, June, 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1932; confd., Mar., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1934.

FAIRWEATHER, JAMES COURTENAY THORPE, late I.P.S. (b. 5th July, 1876).—Joined the service, 26th Nov., 1896, and posted to Central Provs.; dist. supt., July, 1899; retd., Jan., 1930.

FAIZ MUHAMMAD KHAN, KHAN, Punjab Civil Service (b. 16th Dec., 1899).—Extra asst. commr., Punjab, Jan., 1914.

FAKHAR - UD - DIN, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad F.).

FALCONER, GEORGE ARTHUR, Major I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 3rd June, 1894).—First commn. dated 28th Aug., 1917; joined pol. dept., 18th June, 1923; under-sec. to pol. resident in Persian Gulf, May, 1924; asst. resident, Aden, Aug., 1927; 2nd asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Mar., 1929; secty. to A.G.G., Deccan States, Apl., 1933; ditto, Gujerat States, Oct., 1933; offg. A.G.G., ditto, Apl., 1935; consul for Kerman, Nov., 1937.

FALSHAW, DONALD, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 22nd Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Lancaster Royal Grammar Sch., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th October, 1928; arrived. Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1932; offg. ditto, Nov., 1933; sub. perm., June, 1937.

FALVEY, CORNELIUS VINCENT, L.R.C.P. and S., Major, I.M.S. Medical Dept., United Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 8th Apl., 1898).—Date of first commn., 17th Oct., 1922; civil surg., United Provs., Jan., 1932.

FANE, ARTHUR GEORGE CECIL, M.C., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Dec., 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1902, as asst. engr., Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; on mily. service from Feb., 1915, to April, 1910; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1922; confd., July, 1926; retd., Mar., 1933.

FANN, JOHN MONTAGU GROSSE, B.A., Indian Civil Service (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 31st Dec., 1904).—Educ. at Latymer Upper School, Hammersmith, and Pembroke Coll., Cam-

bridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. warden, oilfields, Apl., 1930; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1932.

FANSHAW, JOCELYN HERBERT, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 21st Nov., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th Oct., 1903; resdt. engr., Oct., 1906; in mily. employ from 26th Feb., 1917, to 31st Oct., 1919; dist. engr., Apl., 1921; offg. dep. ch. engr., G.I.P. fly., Jan., 1935; retd., Nov., 1937.

FARBROTHER, ERNEST SAMUEL, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Veterinary Service (dir. vety. services, Bombay) (b. 16th July, 1890).—Joined the service, Oct., 1913; on mily. duty from July, 1915, to April, 1919; offg. supt., Sind, July, 1919; ditto, Bombay Presdy., Feb., 1922; confd., Nov., 1923; dir. vety. services, June, 1932.

FARGUE, DUDLEY GORDON HERIOT DE LA, Major I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Sept., 1897).—First commn. dated 21st Se t., 1916; joined pol. dept., 11th April, 1923; under-sec. to agent to G.G. in C.I., April, 1925; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1926; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat, Jan., 1928; offg. under-sec. for and pol. dept., July, 1930; on foreign service in Gwalior State, Mar., 1931; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Mar., 1932; secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, and ch. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Jan., 1933; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1934; pol. agent, Zhob, Nov. 1934; home secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., July, 1938.

FARIDUNJI, R. (see Rustomyi Faridunji).

FARMBROUGH, ALFRED, A.I.C., Office of High Commr. for India (asst. chemist, store dept.) (b. 30th Sept., 1889).—Apptd. junior asst., chemist, India Store depôt, 1909; asst. chemist, 1920.

FARMER, JOHN COTTON, C.I.E., Indian Police (inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 18th May, 1886).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1906; asst. supt., Oct., 1908; supt., Nov., 1915; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1928, and Nov., 1929; confd., Jany., 1931; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; offg., inspr.-genl., Apl., 1932; and again, Apl., 1935; confd., Dec., 1936; Indian Police Medal, 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

FARMER, PATRICK SCOTT LETHBRIDGE, Indian Police (b. 18th July, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, Nov., 1932; asst. commr., Port Blair, Feb., 1938.

FARNON, JOSEPH ASHLEY ST. JOHN, M.A., late Opium dept., United Provs. (b. 23rd July, 1882).—Joined the service as proby. asst. opium agent, 5th Apl., 1904; confd., Mar., 1906; dist. opium agent (s.p.t.), Aug., 1924; confd., Oct., 1924; retd., Jan., 1938.

FARQUHAR, ARTHUR GRAHAM FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 21st Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Glenalmond and Sch. of Economics, London; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1927, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., Oct., 1929; offg. dep. commr. variously from Mar., 1931.

FARQUHARSON, FRANCIS ARCHIBALD, M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., and secty. to govt., Punjab) (b. 20th Jan., 1886).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as

asst. engr. on 1st Jan., 1907; on milty. duty from 17th March, 1915, to 26th May, 1919; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. e. gr., April, 1928; under secty., pulic wks. dept., irrig. branch (rev.), May, 1929; suptg.-engr., Apl., 1933; ch. engr. and secty. to govt. Punjab, I.B., May, 1938.

FARRAN, ARTHUR COURTNEY, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (prinpl., Karnatak Coll., Dharwar) (b. 15th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 5th Jan., 1915; prof. of history and pol. economy, Elph. Coll., Bombay, Jan., 1917; prof. of history and economy, Deccan Coll., Poona, July, 1926; offg. prinpl. and prof., Elph. Coll., Bombay, June, 1929 and again June, 1932; prinpl., Karnatak Coll., Dharwar, Nov., 1930.

FARRANT, GEORGE YORKE SILVERTHORNE, M.C., Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 23rd June, 1894).—Joined the service, 28th Dec., 1919, as asst. supt., Bombay, offg. supt., Feb., 1924; confd., Sept., 1927.

FARRINGTON, SIR HENRY ANTHONY, Bart., late Indian Forest Service.—Joined the service, 29th Nov., 1894, and served as asst. consvr. of forests; dep. consvr., June, 1901; apptd. to Port Blair in Feb., 1908, with charge of Andamans Forest Dept.; dir. of forest school, Nov., 1911; dep. consvr., Bengal, March, 1912; transf. to Central Prov. as offg. consvr., April, 1915; offg. consvr. of forests, Bengal, Jan., 1916; consvr., Sept., 1919; offg. chief consvr., Central Prov., May, 1922; confd., July, 1923; retd., Oct., 1926.

FARUQI, GHUFAN AHMAD, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 1st Aug., 1909).—Educ. at Government High School, Sitapur, Allahabad University and King's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Apl., 1935.

FARUQI, MUHAMMAD ZAHURUDDIN AHMAD (see Zahuruddin Ahmad, F. M.).

FARUQUI, NASIR AHMAD, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 15th Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. under-secty., rev. dept., Bombay, Aug., 1935; confd., Apl., 1936; under-secty., home and pol. depts., and passport offcr., Nov., 1937; attd. home dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1937.

FATEH, BAHADUR VARMA, BABU, B.A., LL.B. (b. 24th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 2nd July, 1903; sub. judge, Dec., 1915; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1925; confd., Oct., 1926; retd., Nov., 1931.

FATEH-UD-DIN, KHAN BAHADUR MAULVI, M.B.E., B.A., M.R.A.S., A.R.H.S., Indian Agricl. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Punjab) (b. 13th Apl., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric. on 10th Aug., 1909; dep. dir. of agric., Dec., 1921; M.B.E., June, 1935.

FAWCETT, SIR CHARLES GORDON HILL, Kt., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 28th June, 1869).—Educ. at Harrow and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 9th Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst. and asst. judge, Feb., 1897; under-

sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., June, 1898; acting remembrancer of legal affairs, March, 1899, and April, 1901; 1st asst. and ditto, Nov., 1901; judge and ditto, Sept., 1903; remembrancer of legal affairs, Dec., 1904, to March, 1905; agt. for Sardars in Deccan, April, 1912; addl. judl. commr., Sind, May, 1918; judl. commr. of Sind, Nov., 1918; judge, high ct., Bombay, April, 1920; Kt., June, 1927; offg. chief justice, June, 1928; retd., June, 1929, author of "The First Century of British Justice in India."

FAWCETT, EDWARD PINDER, C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 17th March, 1874).—Educ. at Dean Close Sch., Cheltenham, Ley's Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1897; arrived, 7th Dec., 1898; and erved in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1907; dep. commr., Nov., 1914; C.B.E., June, 1918; mag. and collr., July, 1918; dep. commr., March, 1921; addl. commr., Dec., 1924; offg. commr., April, 1925; commr. (prov. sub); Aug., 1926; confd., July, 1927; retd., Aug., 1930.

FAWCUS, GEORGE ERNEST, C.I.E., O.B.E., V.D., M.A. (Oxon.), Indian Educl. Service (b. 12th Mar., 1885).—Joined the service, 4th Oct., 1909; inspr. of schools, Dec., 1909; asst. dir. of public instrn., Bihar and Orissa, April, 1915; inspr. of schools, Chota Nagpur div., July, 1915; temp. dir. of public instrn., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1917; confd., June, 1921; O.B.E., Jan., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; chairman, jt. public serv. commn. for Bihar, C.P., and Orissa, Apl., 1937.

FAWCUS, LOUIS REGINALD, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 7th Nov., 1887).—Educ. at Uppingham, Trinity Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlt. offr.; transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1917; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to March, 1919; mag. and collr., Sept., 1925; offg. secty., bd. of rev., Bengal, Oct., 1927; offg. dir., land records, Bengal, Mar., 1929; offg. secy. to govt., Bengal agric. and ind. depts., Jan., 1931; confd., July, 1931; offg. secty., rev. dept., Bengal, June, 1935; offg. commr., Feb., 1936.

FAY, ARCHIBALD THOMAS, late Indian Police (b. 22nd Aug., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1901; dep. supt., Mar., 1922; apptd. to I.P. as dist. supt., Burma, 20th Mar., 1935; retd., Aug., 1938.

FAZL, A. H. A. (see Abul Fazl, A. H.).
FAZL ALI, SAIVID, Barr. Judl. dept., Bihar (b. 19th Sept., 1886).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, Feb., 1929; performed duties of Chief Justice, Patna High Ct. May to July, 1938.

FAZL MUHAMMAD KHAN, KHAN, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. Jan., 1888).—Apptd. asst. consvr., from 19th July, 1922; dep. consvr., July, 1926.

FAZLUR RAHMAN KHONDKAR, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 1st Feb., 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 22nd April, 1929; confd., Oct., 1931.

FEARNLEY-WHITTINGSTALL, FRANCIS HERBERT, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 8th June, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 15th Nov., 1914; dist. supt., May, 1918; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1921; offg. asst. commr., Rangoon, Sept., 1925; pte. sec. to govt., Burma, March, 1926; C.I.E., March, 1929; dep. commr. of police, Rangoon, Apl., 1929; asst. inspr.-genl. of police, Burma, May, 1931; ret'd., June, 1936.

FEARON, LEONARD KEARY, Salt Dept., Madras (dep. commr.) (b. 10th Nov., 1881).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 11th Dec., 1901; asst. inspr., April, 1905; inspr., April, 1910; asst. commr., Oct., 1920; dep. commr., Mar., 1932.

FELL, DOUGLAS YATES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 18th Sept., 1910).—Educ. at Charterhouse and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

FELL, SIR GODFREY BUTLER HUNTER, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Eton, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 7th Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; personal asst. to chief commr., April, 1897; private sec. to lieut.-govr., May, 1897; acting asst. sec. to govt. of Burma, March, 1899; confd., Oct., 1899; under sec. to govt., Oct., 1900; private sec. to lieut.-govr., April, 1903, to Dec., 1904; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., April, 1906; confd., July, 1906; dep. sec., home dept., Oct., 1906, to July, 1909; dep. commr., Burma, March, 1910; C.I.E., June, 1909; offg. sec. to govt., July, 1912; addl. member, gov.-genl.'s council, July, 1913; dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Nov., 1914; offg. finl. advr., mil. fin. dept., govt. of India, April, 1915; confirmed, July, 1915; member, Indian Munitions bd., in addition, from Feb., 1917; C.S.I. Jan., 1917; K.C.I.E., June, 1918; member of commee. to enquire into Indian Army administration and organization, 1919; commr., Jan. 1921; ret'd., Sept., 1924.

FENTON, SIR MICHAEL WILLIAM, K.C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Armagh, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 18th Nov., 1883, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. under sec. to govt., rev. dept., Oct., 1887, to Nov., 1891; senr. sec. to finl. commrs., July, 1892; rev. sec. to govt., Oct., 1896; dep. commr., Oct., 1899; commr., Dec., 1909; chief sec. to govt., Oct., 1910; C.S.I., Jan., 1911; second finl. commr., Jan., 1912; K.C.S.I., June, 1914; ret'd., July, 1916; author of 'Punjab Municipal Manual.'

FENWICK, EDGAR NORMAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 30th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

FERARD, JOHN EDWARD, C.B.E., late India Office.—Educ. at Eton, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; upper divn. clerk, local govt. board, and Feb., 1892; junr. clerk, India Office, 7th April, 1893; priv. te. sec. to parly. under sec. of state, Feb., 1894; private sec. to permanent under sec. of state, July, 1898, to Feb.,

1901; senior clerk, public works dept., March, 1901; transf. to mil. dept., Oct., 1903, to judl. and public dept., April, 1904; asst. sec. pol. dept., April, 1907; ditto, judl. and public dept., Oct., 1912; acting sec., judl. and public dept., Oct., 1917, to May, 1918, and from Dec., 1918; C.B.E., Jan., 1928; sec. judl. and public dept., Dec., 1919; designation of dept. changed to public and judl., April, 1924; ret'd., July, 1929.

FERGUSON, DONALD HARRY, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 8th Feb., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. transpn. supt., G.I.P. Rly., 16th June, 1924; supt., July, 1930.

FERGUSON, JAMES, M.C., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 22nd Mar., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 24th June, 1920; offg. supt., Sept., 1923, and again, April, 1925; supt. (provl. sup.), Sept., 1930; confd., Aug., 1932.

FERGUSON, ROLAND EDWARD STUART, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 22nd Aug., 1899).—Joined the service on 16th Dec., 1921; offg. supt., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1932, princpl., training coll., Hazaribagh, Mar., 1936.

FERGUSON, JOHN CARLYLE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th June, 1872).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 8th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., May, 1901; settl. offr., July, 1902; under sec. to govt., June, 1903; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Jan., 1904; confd., Dec., 1904; re-transf. to United Provs., March, 1908; asst. commr., May, 1908; dep. commr., March, 1913; mag. and collr., Sept., 1914; commr., N.I. salt rev., March, 1920; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

FERMIE, OTTO WILLIAM PATRICK, B.Sc., B.E., India Posts and Tels. (dir. tels.) (b. 16th July, 1891).—Joined the service, 21st Oct., 1918; asst. divl. engr., July, 1920; divl. engr., tels., July, 1928; supt. of tels., N.W. rly., Jan., 1932; offg. dir., tels., United Provs., Mar., 1936; confd., Aug., 1936.

FERMOR, SIR LEWIS LEIGH, Kt., O.B.E., D.Sc., A.R.S.M., F.R.S., F.G.S., F.A.S.B. M.Inst.M.M., late Geological Survey of India (b. 18th Sept., 1880).—Entered the service, 18th July, 1902; supt., Dec., 1910; transf. to service under ry. bd., Jan., 1916; on deputn. to Indian munitions bd., April, 1917, to Aug., 1918; O.B.E., June, 1919; offg. dir., Jan., 1930; confd., June, 1932; knighted, June, 1935; ret'd., Nov., 1935.

FERRAR, MICHAEL LLOYD, C.S.I., C.I.E., O.B.E., Lt.-Col. I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 16th Apl., 1876).—First commn. 22nd Jan., 1896; in mil. employ to Sept., 1901, when he was apptd. asst. commr., Punjab; asst. censor, Bombay, Dec., 1914; postal censor, Bombay, Oct., 1916; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; dep. commr., Nov., 1919; C.I.E., June, 1922; chief commr., Andaman and Nicobar Islands, April, 1923; C.S.I., June, 1927; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

FERRAR, WALTER INGLIS, V.D., M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th Feb., 1875).—Joined the service as proby. asst. engr., 6th Dec., 1895; confd., March, 1899; junr. engr., July, 1903; resdt. engr., Oct., 1906; dist. engr., June, 1917; dep. ch. engr., May, 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

FERRERS, VIVYAN MACLEOD, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 20th Jan., 1877).—Educ. at Bath Sch. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 10th Dec., 1900, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1918; offg. judl. commr. of Sind, Dec., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933; retd., Dec., 1935.

FERRIE, ALLAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 12th Apr., 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., May, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; under secty., P.W.D., irrgn. br., Punjab, Oct., 1929.

FFORDE, SIR CECIL ROBERT, KT., K.C., late Judicial Dept., Punjab (b. 24th June, 1876).—Addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, 1922; apptd. judge, high ct., Lahore, Oct., 1925; Kt., June, 1930; retd., Mar., 1931.

FFORDE, THEOBALD, late Indian Police (b. 29th Mar., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 17th Oct., 1902; dep. supt. (prov. service), May, 1914; apptd. to the I.P.S. as dist. supt., 11th Feb., 1927; retd., June, 1937.

FFORDE, THEOBALD PHILIP FEATHERSTONEHAUGH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 25th Jan., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Burma, 27th Jan., 1932; confd., Jan., 1934.

FFOULKES, GEORGE ROBERT GWYNNE, Indian Police (b. 9th June, 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar, on probn., 11th Nov., 1935.

FIELD, SIR DONALD MOYLE, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd. (b. 19th Nov., 1881).—First commn. dated 28th July, 1900; in milly. employ until 13th Nov., 1906; on spl. duty in connection with Amir's visit to India, Nov., 1906, to March, 1907, apptd. supy. asst. commr., Punjab, Mar., 1907; asst. commr., N.W.F. Prov., May, 1908; pol. asst. and asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Aug., 1908; asst. pol. agent, Kalat, Mar., 1914; temply. reverted to milly. duty, Oct., 1914; asst. resdt., Baroda, Jan., 1917; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir for Leh and Ladakh, May, 1917; under-sec. to the govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1917; pol. agent, S. Rajputana States, Dec., 1918; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1920; pol. agent, S. Rajputana States, Dec., 1920; on foreign service in Chamba State, Oct., 1923 to Feb., 1925; resdt. at Jaipur, Feb., 1925; pol. agent in Southern States of C.I. and in Malwa, Nov., 1925; resident in Mewar, March, 1928; offg. agent to govr. genl. in Rajputana and ch. commr. of Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1930; resdt. in Mewar and pol. agent, S. Rajputana, States, Feb., 1931; agent to govr.-genl., Madras States, Nov., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; retd., July, 1935; knighted, Feb., 1937.

FIELD, GEOFFREY GLENNIE, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 3rd June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 22nd Nov., 1915; I.A.R.O. from 8th July, 1918, to 8th Aug., 1919; offg. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., Dec., 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932.

FIELD, JOHN BERTRAM FINCH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 15th July, 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, 24th Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932; offg. supt., Nov., 1933.

FIELD, ROBERT EDWIN, O.B.E. India Office (principal, services and genl. dept.) (b. 27th Dec., 1888).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 22nd April, 1910, and attached to the pol. dept.; transfed. to acct.-genl. dept., Dec., 1911; asst. sec. to cttee. on State technical scholarships, April to Sept., 1912; transfed. to dept. of sec. for Indian students, Sept., 1912; sub-warden, 21, Cromwell Road, S.W., April to Oct., 1913; actg. warden, ditto, Nov., 1914, to Feb., 1919; sec. to London advisory cttee. for Indian students, Aug., 1913; minor staff clerk, Feb., 1916; asst. to educ. adviser for Indian students, Feb., 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; asst. to jt. sec. for Indian students, Nov., 1920; re-transfed. to India Office as asst. principal, April, 1921; jt. sec. to cttee. on Indian students, 1921-2; reading clerk to council, Nov., 1924; pte. sec. to perm. under sec. of state, June, 1926; actg. principal, 1st Dec., 1928; confd., Apr., 1930; O.B.E., June, 1936.

FIELDEN, LIONEL, All-India Radio (Controller of Broadcasting) (b. 15th May, 1896).—Joined the service, as Controller of Broadcasting on 30th Aug., 1935.

FIELDING, CHARLES HENRY, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.), late Jail Dept., Burma (b. 8th Nov., 1880).—First commn. dated 1st Feb., 1908; arrived, 8th Oct., 1908; offg. supt., central jail, Insein, June, 1912; reverted to milty. service from 21st Sept., 1914, to 20th March, 1921; supt., March, 1921; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, May, 1927; retd., May, 1930.

FIELDING, STUART SKENE McA'FEE, late Survey of India (b. 21st Jan., 1877).—Joined the service as sub-assst. supt. (proby.), 21st Nov., 1898; confd., Nov., 1899; extra asst. supt., Oct., 1906; supt., June, 1928; retd., Jan., 1932.

FIELDING-HALL, JOHN GRAHAM, Indian C.S. (under secty., fin. dept., Burma) (b. 1st May, 1908).—Educ. at Rugby and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty., fin. dept., Burma, Aug., 1936.

FIELDS-CLARKE, VICTOR HERBERT TOM, late Indian Forest Servic. (b. 11th Feb., 1888).—Arrived, 18th Dec., 1909, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1914; offg. consvr., Nov., 1926; confd., Sept., 1930; retd., Feb., 1935.

FIFE, JOHN EDWARD, late Indian Police Service (b. 25th Apr., 1880).—Joined the service, 7th Jan., 1901, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt. of police, April, 1906; supt., Oct., 1913; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1922; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1927; retd., July, 1932.

FILSON, JAMES THOMAS WARNOCK, late Indian Police Service (Madras) (b. 23rd Apr., 1880).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1900, as probr.; asst. supt., July, 1903; supt., June, 1910; asst. inspr.-gen., Madras, Sept., 1915; offg. commr. of police, Madras, April to Nov., 1923; offg. dep. inspr. of police, April, 1925; confd., Jan., 1927; retd., Mar., 1932.

FINDLAY, SIR CHARLES STEWART, KT., M.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 28th Aug., 1874).—Educ. at Aberdeen and Edinburgh Univs. and at Univ. Coll., London;

apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 5th Dec., 1898, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; employed in legislative dept., govt. of India, from June to Dec., 1912; and in sectt., Central Provs., from Dec., 1912, to March, 1913; legal rembr., sec. to legisl. council, and sec. to chief commr., Central Provs., April, 1913; dep. commr., and ditto, Sept., 1914; dist. and sess. judge, and ditto, May, 1917, to March, 1919; addl. judl. commr., March, 1919; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1921, to March, 1925; offg. judl. commr., March, 1925; confd., March, 1926; Kt., June, 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1932.

FINDLAY, JAMES, M.A., M.B., Ch.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. Jail Dept., Burma (inspr.-genl.) (b. 19th Aug., 1886).—Date of first commn., 26th July, 1913; supt., jail dept., Burma (temp.), Mar., 1924; ditto (permn.) Feb., 1925; inspr.-genl. of prisons, Mar., 1934.

FINLAY, WILLIAM WATERS, M.A. (Edin.), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Provs.) (b. 8th May, 1899).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1931, and again, Sept., 1933; offg. secty., bd. of rev., United Provs., Jan., 1934; dep. commr., Nov., 1934.

FINLAYSON, ANDREW, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 11th Aug., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, on 17th Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932.

FINLOW, ROBERT STEEL, C.I.E., B.Sc., F.I.C., late Indian Agricul. Service (b. 17th Aug., 1877).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1905 (prevy. nominally employed in service of Bihar Indigo Planters' Assocn., but mainly in research work for govt. of Bengal); jute expert, April, 1905; fibre expert, E. Bengal and Assam, July, 1906; employed as 2nd dep. dir. of agric., July, 1910, to July, 1911; fibre expert, Bengal, April, 1912; offg. dir. of agric., Bengal and dir. of fisheries, Oct., 1922; dir. of agric., Bengal, Feb., 1925; C.I.E., March, 1929; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

FINNEY, PHILIP EDMUND STANLEY, O.B.E., Indian Police Service (dep. commr., of police, Calcutta) (b. 11th Dec., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 5th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., temp. employed as supt., Deoli jail, Dec., 1931; confd. as supt. July, 1937; O.B.E., June, 1934; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Feb., 1938.

FIRTH, JOHN RUPERT, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service.—Joined the service as master-in-charge, training class for European schools, Sanawar, 4th March, 1915; on mil. duty from 23rd April, 1916, to 17th Nov., 1919; prof'r. govt. coll., Lahore, Nov., 1919; ret'd., Feb., 1929.

FIRTH, LESLIE GEORGE BECKWITH, late Revenue survey dept., Madras (b. 24th Sept., 1879).—Joined the service as asst. dir., 25th March, 1911; offg. dir., April, 1927; confd., May, 1928; ret'd., Sept., 1934.

FISH, Rev. FREDERIC JOHN, B.A., M.C., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 13th April, 1894).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, Punjab, 5th Feb., 1930; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1932.

FISHER, D., M.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 2nd July, 1882).—Joined the service, 30th July, 1908; offg. supt., watch and ward, E.B. rly., July, 1929; confd., July, 1931; ret'd., July, 1937.

FISHER, FRANK, B.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 6th March, 1886).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., Clare Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; apptd. temp. to pol. dept. as asst. commr., N.-W. Frontier Prov., Dec., 1916; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1922; dep. commr. (prov. sub.), Feb., 1926; ret'd., Feb., 1931.

FISHER, GERALD THOMAS, C.I.E., B.A., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 27th Aug., 1887).—First commn., 9th Sept., 1908; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, as asst. commr., Kohat, 13th May, 1919; under-sec. to agent to gov.-gen. in Central India, March, 1921; confd. as pol. officer, June, 1922; pol. agent, Chagai, March, 1924; H. M. Consul for Sistan and Kain, Jan., 1925; asst. pol. resdt., Aden, Dec., 1927; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Nov., 1928; secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Mar., 1932; ch. minister, Kapurthala state, Jan., 1935; resdt. at Gwalior and pol. agent for States of Rampur and Benares, Apl., 1937, again, Oct., 1938; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

FISHER, WILLIAM HUMPHREY WALTER, Indian Police (b. 30th Mar., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on probn., Nov., 1938.

FISHWICK, DAVID, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 29th April, 1906).—Educ. at Bradford Grammar School and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., local govt. dept., Burma, Mar., 1933; asst. collr., Impl. customs service, Rangoon, Mar., 1934; asst. commr., Oct., 1934.

FISK, SYDNEY PATRICK, Indian Police (b. 2nd June, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab on probn., Nov., 1938.

FITT, WATKIN, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 22nd June, 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 10th Oct., 1919, after previous service from 22nd Oct., 1915; on mil. service from Feb., 1916 to Aug., 1919; second class clerk, 29th April, 1920; services lent to high commr. for India from April, 1921; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, Nov., 1934.

FITZE, KENNETH SAMUEL, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 6th Jan., 1887).—Educ. at Marlborough, Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; apptd. temp. to pol. dept. as asst. to resdt., Kashmir, April, 1915; first asst. to resdt., Baroda, May, 1917; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1918; apptd. substantively to pol. dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1919; census supt., Central India, Oct., 1921, to Jan., 1922; sec. to agent to gov.-gen. in Central India, Jan., 1922; dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1924; pol. agent, Baghalkhand, Nov., 1926; jt. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept.

(temp.), July, 1930; jt. secty. to British delegation, Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; jt. secty., Indian States enquiry comee., Jan., 1932; pol. agent. southern states of C.I., Nov., 1932; offg. and opium commr. in C.I., Apl., 1933; offg. resdt. A.G.G., in C.I., Jan., 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; resdt. for Central India, Apl., 1937; ditto, July, 1938.

FITZGERALD, DESMOND, late Indian Police (b. 20th June, 1879).—Joined the service, 14th Aug., 1903; supt. of police, May, 1912; ret'd., June, 1934.

FITZGERALD, SEYMOUR GONNE VESEY, M.A., LL.D., Barr., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Charterhouse and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. afterexam. of 1906; arrived, 5th Dec., 1907 and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; asst. commr. and sub-judge, Feb., 1910; ditto and dist. judge, Sept., 1910; regr., judl. commr.'s ct., Feb., 1913; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1913; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1917; legal remembr., sec. to legisl. council and sec. to chief commr., in legal dept., Feb., 1918; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1918; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), June, 1918, to March, 1919; ret'd., Nov., 1923; lecturer to I.C.S. probationers under the Indian Civil Service delegation from Oct., 1925, to June, 1932, and a lecturer in Muhammadan Law under the Colonial Office scheme for African Civil Service probationers from Oct., 1926, to June, 1933; lecturer, Inns of Ct. since 1927; lecturer, sch. of Oriental Studies, Oct., 1932; supervisor of I.C.S. probationers, ditto, Oct., 1937.

FITZHERBERT, RICHARD ANTHONY, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bombay) (b. 4th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; in milty. employ. from 12th Nov., 1917, to 16th Jan., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; suptg. engr. (prov.), Feb., 1938.

FITZMAURICE, DESMOND FITZ-JOHN, B.A., Major, R.E. (master, security printing) (b. 17th Aug., 1893).—Apptd. dep. mint master, on probn., Nov., 1929; ditto (temp.), Dec., 1932; offg. master, security printing, Sept., 1934; confd., Oct., 1934.

FLACK, ALAN WILLIAM, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 1st Nov., 1913).—Educ. at City of London School and St. John's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

FLATT, LESLIE NEEVE, B.Sc., V.D., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (ch. mechl. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 26th March, 1889).—Joined the service, 4th Jan., 1913; asst. loco. supt., E.I. Rly., June, 1913; in milty. employ. from 22nd Feb., 1916, to 24th Feb., 1919; works manager, G.I.P. Rly., Aug., 1920; dep. ch. mech. engr., March, 1923; dep. carr. and wagon supt., Oct., 1923; dep. ch. mech. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. dep. loco. supt., April, 1927; offg. loco. and carr. supt., Dec., 1930; offg. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. rly., Feb., 1934; confd., July, 1935; ditto, N.W. Rly., Jan., 1938.

FLEETON, ERIC RALPH, Indian State Rlys. (supt.) (b. 24th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. carr. and wagon supt., 1st Sept., 1920; transptn. (power) supt., E.I. rly., Sept., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. rly., Mar., 1936.

FLETCHER, ALAN HENRY SAUL, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 12th Oct., 1903).—Educ. at Sherborne, and Trinity College, Dublin; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1934.

FLETCHER, ANTONY LEOCADIA, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Dec., 1909).—Educated at St. Joseph's High School, Trivandrum, the Universities of Madras and Nagpur, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th October, 1933; arrived in India, November, 1933, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. officer, Nov., 1936 to May, 1938.

FLETCHER, EDWARD WALTER, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 23rd July, 1899).—First commn., 23rd Jan., 1919; in milty. employ. to Jan., 1928; British trade agent, Gyantse, and asst. to pol. officer in Sikkim, Nov., 1929; secty., Brit. legn., Kabul, Dec., 1932; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, Mar., 1935; on depn. to Baltic States for 13 mths., from Dec., 1936; secty. to resdt. for W.I., May, 1938.

FLETCHER, JOHN HINTON, M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (under secty. to govt. (northern admin.), Punjab) (b. 23rd June, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; Oct., engr., Oct., 1925; under secty. to govt., irrigation branch, Punjab, Jan., 1931; ditto (construction), Oct., 1934; offg. ditto (northern admin.), Feb., 1936.

FLETCHER, LEONARD RAWCLIFFE, late Indian Forest Engrg. Service (b. 9th March, 1891).—Joined the service as forest engr., Burma, on 26th Dec., 1921; ret'd., Mar., 1933.

FLETCHER, THOMAS BAINBRIDGE, F.L.S., F.E.S., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 25th Mar., 1878).—Joined the service as supt. entomologist, 2nd March, 1910; confd., March, 1912; govt. entomologist, Coimbatore, April, 1912; impl. entomologist, Dec., 1913; ret'd., Mar., 1933.

FLEURY, WALFRID RONALD, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Orissa) (b. 7th Apl., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 25th Aug., 1930.

FLEWETT, WILLIAM EDWARD, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 19th Aug., 1894).—Apptd. dep. consvr., Punjab, 16th Dec., 1921; div. forest officer, Andamans, Sept., 1931; offg. ch. forest officer, Andamans, Aug., 1931 to Aug., 1936.

FLINT, HARRY EWART, O.B.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 22nd Aug., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 18th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., May, 1925; O.B.E., June, 1932.

FLOWERDEW, RICHARD EDWARD, C.I.E., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept. (inspr. genl. of prisons, Bengal) (b. 26th Sept., 1886).—First commn. dated 30th Jan., 1909; services placed at disposal of govt. of Burma from 28th May,

1922; supt., central jail, Insein, May, 1922; ditto, Rangoon, Feb., 1925; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Burma, June, 1930; inspr.-genl. of prisons, Bengal, Mar., 1931; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

FLOWERDEW, SPENCER PELHAM, V.D., late Indian State Railways (b. 6th Oct., 1881).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., state rys., 1st Oct., 1902; exec. engr., Nov., 1910; asst. agent, N.W. Ry., Oct., 1917; junior dep. agent, ditto, Oct., 1919; offg. divl. supt., March, 1925; supt. engr., July, 1926; offg. ch. engr., E.I. Rly., March, 1927; confd., June, 1928; offg. dir. of civil engrg., rly. bd., Dec., 1929; ret'd., July, 1932.

FLUX, DUNDAS A. D. (see Dundas, A. D. F.).

FLUX, RONALD CECIL, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 2nd June, 1901).—Joined the service on 1st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932.

FOGARTY, PHILIP CHRISTOPHER, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 20th Dec., 1884).—Educ. at St. Joseph's Seminary, Galway, and Queen's Coll., Galway, Royal University of Ireland; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived 13th Dec., 1909, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to finl. commr., Burma, April, 1914; on mily. duty from Jan., 1917, to April, 1919; dep. commr. (provl. sub.), Oct., 1924; confd., April, 1926; jt. regr., co-op. societies, Dec., 1927; regr., ditto, Dec., 1928; secty., forest dept., Burma, Mar., 1932, to Feb., 1935.

FOOKS, RAYMOND HATHERELL, B.A., late Indian Police (b. 23rd June, 1888).—Joined the service, 10th Dec., 1908; asst. supt., Oct., 1909; supt., March, 1919; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1931; dep. inspr.-genl. (provl. subs.), Aug., 1932; ret'd., June, 1935.

FORBES, ALISTER ESME BUCHAN, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys., G.I.P. Rly. (b. 4th Apl., 1881).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 30th Dec., 1904; dist. ditto, June, 1915; in mily. employ. from 3rd Dec., 1917, to 27th March, 1918; O.B.E., June, 1919; divl. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; contr. of stores, Aug., 1926; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

FORBES, GEORGE FREEMAN MURRAY, O.B.E., M.C., Indian Police (supt., Orissa) (b. 5th June, 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1922; asst. supt. (proby.), Sept., 1922; confd., Jan., 1927; offg. supt. govt. rly. police, May, 1927; offg. dist. supt., Oct., 1927; confd., July, 1930; O.B.E., Jan., 1932.

FORBES, SIR GEORGE STUART, K.C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Aberdeen Gr. Sch. and Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 16th Oct., 1872, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; from Sept., 1880, was employed as acting under sec. in the rev. dept., asst. sec., jud. and legisl. dept., and under sec. in the depts. under the chief sec.; from Feb., 1883, served under the govt. of India in the home dept. as offg. under sec., also in the foreign dept., on special duty, Dec., 1884, and as junr. under sec., Jan., 1885; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Dec., 1886; acting 1st asst. to the rest. at Hyderabad, and sec. for Berar, Dec., 1887, to April, 1889; sub-collr. and joint mag., Sept., 1888; district and sessions judge, April, 1890; collr. and mag., Jan., 1891;

acted as rev. sec. in 1895, 1896, 1897 and 1898; sec. in local and municipal depts., Oct., 1897; addl. member of legisl. council, Dec., 1897, June, 1898, June, 1899, June, 1900, June, 1902, and Feb., 1904; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Sept., 1898; offg. chief sec. to govt., Nov., 1901, May, 1902, and March, 1903; confd., Nov., 1903; temp. member of council, Madras, May, 1904, and May, 1906; C.S.I., June, 1904; ordinary member of council, Madras, June, 1906; K.C.S.I., June, 1909; ret'd., July, 1909.

FORD, WILLIAM ALAN WILBRAHAM, ate Indian Finance Dept. (b. 25th Oct., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. examr. on 1st Oct., 1906; asst. acct.-gen., Sept., 1910; dep. acct.-gen., Central revenues, Dec., 1923; dep. acct.-gen., United Provs., April, 1925; dep. dir. of audit, ditto, April, 1926; audit and accts. officer, Bombay development scheme, Nov., 1926; offg. dep. dir. army audit, E.C., Oct., 1928; offg. dep. ch. auditor, N.W. Rly., April, 1929; with finl. commr. (rlys.), Oct., 1929; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

FORDHAM, JAMES ALFRED, Indian Civil Service (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 27th Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Sloane School, Chelsea, and Downing Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived 19th Dec., 1926, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; temp. addl. sess. and sub-judge, Nov., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1933; settlement offer., Sept., 1935.

FORREST, GEORGE HENRY, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 10th Aug., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1908, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., June, 1918; offg. supt. engr. (s.p.l.), Sept., 1918; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

FORREST, JOHN, M.B., Lt-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 22nd Oct., 1876).—Joined the service, 26th July, 1902; D.M.O. and medl. officer, central jail, Coimbatore, Dec., 1923; offg. D.M.O. and medl. officer, central jail, Bellary, April, 1926; ret'd., 4th Nov., 1929.

FORRESTER, CHARLES, A.H.W.C., F.I.C., Ph. D. (Edin.), F.R.S.E., A.M.I.Chem.E., Indian School of Mines (princpl., Indian Sch. of Mines) (b. 6th Mar., 1895).—Apptd. prof. of chemistry and assaying on probn., 3rd Nov., 1926; confd., 1929; offg. principal, Indian School of Mines, May, 1927, Nov., 1929, and June, 1934; principal, ditto, Nov., 1936.

FORSTER, WILLIAM HUGHAN CHARLES, C.I.E., M.B., C.M., D.Ph., Maj.-Genl., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 15th June, 1874).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1899; in mil. employ until April, 1904, when he was appointed dep. sanitary commr., Punjab; prof. of pathology and hygiene, Lahore med. coll., April, 1909; dep. sany. commr., Punjab, March, 1912; sany. commr., Aug., 1918; dir. of public health, Punjab, Nov., 1921; inspr.-genl. of civil hosps., Burma, Nov., 1927; surg. genl. with govt. of Bombay, Jan., 1930; member of legisl. council, Sept., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; ret'd., July, 1933; author of various works on medical subjects.

FORSYTH, JAMES PETERS, B.A., Office of High Commr. for India (dir. of purchase, store dept.) (b. 22nd Apl., 1883).—Educ. at R. Grimmer Sch., Lancaster, and Caius Coll., Cambridge (8th wrangler); apptd. junr. clerk, store dept.,

19th Oct., 1906; senr. clerk, July, 1912; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; dir. of purchase (temp.), April, 1927; confd., Nov., 1932.

FORTESQUE, Rev. CECIL FRANCIS, L.Th. Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Bombay) (b. 14th Oct., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 18th Jan., 1922; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1924; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1930.

FORTY, FREDERICK CHARLES, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 17th Sept., 1894).—Apptd. to acct.-gen.'s dept. India Office, 15th Dec., 1919; transf. to office of High Commr. for India, 8th April, 1921; clerical officer, June, 1921; exec. officer, April, 1922.

FOSTER, ESMOND LEWIS PEARCE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 28th Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924; divl. forest officer, 5th. Andamans, Aug., 1935; ch. forest officer, ditto, Oct., 1935.

FOSTER, RODNEY, Lt.-Col. I.A., ret'd., late Survey of India (b. 27th May, 1882).—First commn. dated 8th May, 1901; apptd. asst. supt., trigl. survey, 12th Dec., 1906; temp. reverted to mil. duty, Nov., 1914, to April, 1919; dep., June, 1921; supt., Jan., 1926; offg. dir., Oct., 1931; ret'd., Sept., 1934.

FOSTER, Sir WILLIAM, Kt., C.I.E., B.A., late India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd Sept., 1882; staff clerk, Jan., 1901; asst. Registrar, Oct., 1901; Registrar and Supt. of Records, Oct., 1907; editor, India Office List, 1891-95; C.I.E., Jan., 1913; ret'd., Nov., 1923; apptd. to temporary post of Historiographer, Nov., 1923; resigned, 31st Dec., 1927; Kt., Jan., 1925; has published 'The Embassy of Sir T. Roe to India, 1615-19,' 'Letters received by the E. India Co., 1615-17' (4 vols.), 13 vols. on 'The English Factories in India,' 'The Journal of John Jourdain, 1608-17,' 'The Founding of Fort St. George,' 'Early Travels in India, 1583-1619,' 'A Guide to the India Office Records, 1600-1858,' 'Catalogue of the India Office Pictures' (five editions), 'The History of the Indian Wars,' 'The East India House,' 'John Company,' Supplementary Calendar of India Office Records, 1600-40, 'The Travels of Thomas Herbert in Persia,' 'Hamilton's New Account of the East Indies,' 'The Travels of John Sanderson 1584-1602,' 'British Artists in India, 1760-1820,' 'England's Quest of Eastern Trade,' and 'The Voyage of Captain Best to the East Indies, 1612-14.'

FOWLE, FULWAR WILLIAM CRAVEN, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) b. 4th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. eng., 27th Nov., 1908; resdt. engr., Sept., 1912; in milty. employ from 27th Sept., 1917, to 26th May, 1920; divl. engr., Feb., 1926.

FOWLE, Sir TRENCHARD CRAVEN WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., C.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Nov., 1884).—First commn., 29th Nov., 1905; in mil. employ till April, 1914; pol. officer on probation, June, 1914; H.M. Consul for Sistan and Kain, Mar., 1921; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Dec., 1921; ditto Zhob, Sept., 1922; pol. agent, Chagai, Dec., 1923; H. M. Consul for Kerman and Persian Baluchistan, March, 1924; first asst. resident, Aden, Sept., 1925; offg. pol.

resdt., Aden, Sept., 1927, and again, April, 1928; C.B.E., Mar., 1929; pol. agent and H.M. Consul, Muscat, June, 1930; offg. pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, May to Nov., 1931; again pol. agent, Muscat, Nov., 1931, to July, 1932; pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, July, 1932; K.C.I.E., May, 1937.

FOWLER, GEORGE, L.R.C.P., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 29th Apl., 1874).—First commn., 27th June, 1901; in mil. employ till May, 1914, when he was apptd. civil surgeon (Central Provs.); on mil. duty, Jan., 1915, to Oct., 1919; ret'd., Sept., 1929.

FOWLER, ROBERT WILLIAM DOUGHTY, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I.) (asst. commr.) (b. 6th Mar., 1914).—Educ. at Queen Elizabeth's Grammar School, Mansfield, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1937, and served as asst. commr.

FOX, CYRIL SANKEY, D.Sc., Geological Survey of India (supgt. geologist) (b. 24th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 2nd Mar., 1911; supt., Dec., 1930; offg. dir., July, 1936.

FOY, THOMAS ARTHUR WYNES, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 20th Feb., 1925).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1927; under secty. to govt., irrigation branch, Punjab, May, 1931; ditto (constn.), Punjab irrig. sect., May, 1933; chief engr., irrign., Bikaner State, from Oct., 1935.

FRAMJI, KAVSI KAIKHOSRU, B.A., B.Sc., Ind. Service of Engrs. (b. 21st Mar., 1908).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 30th July, 1932.

FRAMJI, K. S. (see Kaikhosru Sorabji Framji).

FRAMPTON, HENRY JAMES, M.C., B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (dep. secty., home dept., govt. of India) (b. 14th Aug., 1897).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and St. John's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1921; asst. mag. and collr., United Provs., 2nd Dec., 1921; jt. mag., Dec., 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1926; dep. secty. to govt., United Provs., genl. bch., Oct., 1932; mag. and collr., Nov., 1936; dep. commr., Mar., 1937; dep. secty., home dept., govt. of India, May, 1938.

FRANCE, Rev. ALEXANDER, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 24th Oct., 1886).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, United Provs., 16th Mar., 1925; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1927; senr. chaplain, Madras, 1937.

FRANCE, ROBERT EDWARD, India Office (hr. exec. officer) (b. 16th Aug., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk 27th Oct., 1919, after previous service from 27th Apl., 1916; on mil. service, Aug., 1916 to Oct., 1919; clerical service, Jan., 1920; exec. offr., May, 1926; actg. hr. exec. officer, Oct., 1936; confd., Sept., 1938.

FRANKAU, GEORGE NEUBERG (b. 15th Feb., 1881).—Apptd. chief inspr. of boilers, Central Provs., Dec., 1925; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

FRANKLIN, ERIC ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 3rd July, 1910).—Educated at Government English High School, Maymyo, Rangoon University and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; joined the service, 7th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

FRANKLIN, GEORGE DENNE, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., M.B., B.C., Bt.-Col., I.M.S. ret'd., (b. 28th July, 1877).—First commn., 26th July, 1902; agency surg., Gilgit, Sept., 1913; ditto, Southern States of C.I., Sept., 1914; in milty. employ from Nov., 1914, to Jan., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; civil surg., and C.M.O., Rajputana, March, 1923; C.M.O., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1923; C.M.O., Delhi, April, 1924; hony. surg. to King., Jan., 1928; civil surg., Ajmer Merwara and C.M.O., Rajputana, April, 1928; C.I.E., June, 1928; resdy. surg., Hyderabad, Dec., 1928; ret'd., July, 1934.

FRANKLIN, MURIEL CURRAN, LL.A., Indian Educl. Service (inspectress of schools, Burma) (b. 18th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service as headmistress, govt. normal school, Rangoon, on 3rd Jan., 1924; inspectress of schools, Feb., 1929.

FRANKLIN, ROGER WARWICK, late Indian Police Service (b. 2nd Sept., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., on 26th Jan., 1920; offg. dist. supt., May, 1923; ret'd., June, 1932.

FRASER, DENHOLME DE MONTALTE STUART, C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 5th Oct., 1889).—First commn. dated 20th Jan., 1909; first asst. to pol. resident, Persian Gulf, Nov., 1919; under-sec. to agent to gov.-gen. in Central India, Nov., 1922; asst. mag. and collr., Moradabad, Dec., 1924; asst. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1925; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, May, 1926; pol. agent, Eastern Kathiawar States, Dec., 1926; pol. agent in Southern States of C.I. and Malwa, Oct., 1928; on foreign service in Tonk State, Oct., 1930; pol. agent, Malwa, Apl., 1935; offg. secty. to A.G.G. in C.I. in addn., May, 1936; pol. agent, Malwa, Apl., 1937; offg. resdt. for C.I., Mar., to July, 1938; redt. in Kashmir, Oct., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1938.

FRASER, DONALD MACDONALD, Major I.M.S., Civil Surgeon, United Provs.—Date of first commn., 18th Jan., 1925; civil surgeon, March, 1934.

FRASER, FREDERICK CHARLES, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 15th Feb., 1880).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1906; in mil. employ to 29th May, 1911; asst. supt., govt. maternity hosp., Madras, June, 1911; reverted to mil. duty from 12th Aug., 1914, to 16th March, 1921; civil surg., Coorg, April, 1923; prof., King George hosp., Vizagapatam, Sept., 1926; prinpl. and prof. of surgery, medl. coll., Vizagapatam, April, 1927; dist. medl. officer, April, 1928; supt., central jail, Coimbatore, Jan., 1933; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

FRASER, IAN MARTIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 13th Sept., 1894).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 7th March, 1921; arrived, 29th April, 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Oct., 1927; and again, Sept., 1932; offg. registrar, high ct., Madras, July, 1929; confd., Oct., 1929; collr. (prov.) and commr. of Coorg, Mar., 1936.

FRASER, JAMES DUNCAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 11th Jan., 1915).—Educ. at Haileybury and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. on prob., 29th Sept. 1937; confd., 18th Oct., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

FRASER, SIR JOHN HUGH RONALD, Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th March, 1878).—Educ. at Haileybury and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 1st Dec., 1902, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr.; pol. asst., Feb., 1906; asst. commr., Thal, Nov., 1909; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Jan., 1910; dist. judge, Peshawar, Sept., 1911, to April, 1912; addl. divnl. and sess. judge, Oct., 1913; asst. commr., Peshawar dist., April, 1914; dep. commr., April, 1914; O.B.E., June, 1919; judl. commr., N.W.F. Prov., Aug., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1927; Kt., June, 1932; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

FRASER, SIR STUART MITFORD, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S., Political Dept.—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 25th Nov., 1884, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; tutor and guardian to the Raja of Kholapur, the Chief of Kagal, and Kunvar Sahib of Bhaunagar, May, 1889, to April, 1894; 2nd asst., April, 1894; 1st asst., Sept., 1895; tutor and govr. to H.H. the Maharaja of Mysore, May, 1896, and asst. to resdt., July, 1898; C.I.E., June, 1902; junr. collr., Aug., 1902; dep. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Nov., 1903, and April, 1905; resdt., Dec., 1903; pol. agent, Jan., 1904; special duty in foreign dept., from Dec., 1903; offg. sec., foreign dept., Oct., 1904, to Oct., 1905; dep. sec., ditto, April, 1905, to Oct., 1905; resdt., Mysore, and chief and jud. commr. of Coorg, Nov., 1905; resdt., Kashmir, Dec., 1911; C.S.I., Jan., 1913; offg. resdt., Hyderabad, March to Oct., 1914; resdt., Hyderabad, July, 1916; K.C.S.I. Jan., 1918; ret'd., June, 1920.

FRASER, THOMAS, A.M.I.C.E., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 30th Oct., 1874).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 16th Dec., 1904; resdt. engr., Jan., 1908; in milty. employ from 19th Oct., 1918, to 12th April, 1920; dist. engr., April, 1920; offg. dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1931.

FRASER, WILLIAM GORDON, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 14th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service as prof., 5th June, 1915; on mil. service from 8th Aug., 1918, to 8th Aug., 1919; prinpl., govt., high school, Moulmein, Aug., 1919; lecturer in English, univ. coll., Rangoon, Jan., 1924; ret'd., Dec., 1935.
