FRASER-TYTLER, WILLIAM KERR, C.M.G., M.C., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 26th Dec., 1886).—First commn. dated 24th Aug., 1910; asst. sec., for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1921; sec., Kabul legation, April, 1923; asst. to agent, gov.-gen., Madras States, Dec., 1925; asst. commr., Mansehra, March, 1927; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, Dec., 1927; and again, Oct., 1930; dep. commr., D.I. Khan, June, 1928; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, Oct., 1930; charge d'affaires, ditto, Apl., 1932; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Feb., 1933; confd., Sept., 1933; C.M.G., June, 1933; offg. foreign secty. to Govt. of India, May, 1933; offg. pte. secty. to Viceroy, May, 1934; H.B.M.'s minister, Kabul, Mar., 1935.

FREAK, LESLIE ASHFIELD, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 28th Dec., 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

FREDERICK, AMRULLUR EZEKIEL DAVID, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 7th May, 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. 6th Sept., 1928; confd., Sept., 1930; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; offg. dist. supt., Jan., 1936.

FREDERICK, JESUBATHAM MOSKS, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 19th Nov., 1901).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 8th July, 1925; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1933; confd., Oct., 1935.

FREELAND, SIR HENRY FRANCIS EDWARD, K.C.I.E., C.B., M.V.O., D.S.O., Major-Gen., R.E., late Railway Drpt.—First commn. dated 13th Feb., 1891; apptd. asst. engr., mil. works services, Oct., 1896; transfed. to railway dept., June, 1898; dep. traffic manager, N.W. Ry. Jan., 1911; M.V.O., Dec., 1911; traffic manager, April, 1916; addl. member, rly. board, 1918-19; agent, Bombay, Baroda and Central India Ry. Co., Feb., 1920; war service, Chitral, 1895, medal and clasp; European War 1914-18 (asst. dir. of rly. transport under W.O., Sept., 1914; dep. dir. of rly. transport, May, 1915; dir. of transportation (graded as brig.-gen.), Oct., 1916; dep. dir.-gen. of transportation, Feb., 1917 (temp. maj.-gen., Oct., 1917); mentd. in despatches; D.S.O.; C.B.; officer of Legion of Honour, K.C.I.E., Dec., 1919; ret'd., July, 1920.

FREEMAN, WILLIAM PITT, late Indian State Railways (b. 27th June, 1874).—Joined the service, Aug., 1905; asst. traffic supt., N.W. Rly., Aug., 1906; dist. traffic supt., April, 1909; offg. dep. traffic manager, May, 1919; dep. ch. operating supt. (provl.), Oct., 1924; offg. asst. accts. officer, E.I. Rly., Jan., 1928; ret'd., June, 1929.

FREKE, CECIL GEORGE, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 8th Oct., 1887).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors', London, St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 21st Nov., 1912, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; spl. duty as dep. contr. of munitions, Bombay circle, Sept., 1918; temp. under sec., comm. and ind. dept., govt. of India, April, 1919; asst. to dir.-gen. of comml. intellig., govt. of India, Oct., 1919; offg. dir.-gen. of comml. intellig., Sept., 1921; confd., Dec., 1922; on depn. as Indian trade commr., Aug., 1924; dy. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Feb., 1926; secty., ditto, Apl., 1932, to May, 1937; member legisl. council, 1927 to 1932; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

FREMANTLE, SIR SELWYN HOWE, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., V.D., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 27th Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settlt. officer, and asst. commr.; joint mag., Jan., 1896; dep. commr., Oct., 1903; mag. and collr., May, 1905; regr., co-operative credit societies, June, 1907, to Jan., 1912; spl. duty in England under commerce

fin. and industry dept., Jan. to Nov., 1909; C.I.E., June, 1915; commr., April, 1918; controller of passages, govt. of India, Dec., 1918; commr. and offr. in charge of ex-Amir of Kabul, July, 1919; C.S.I., Jan., 1920; member of exec. council, United Provs., May, 1922; member, board of rev., Nov., 1922; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, United Provs., April, 1925; Kt., June, 1925; ret'd., March 1926; author of reports on Rae Bareli and Bareilly settlt., and labour supply in the United Provs. and Bengal. and 'A Policy on Rural Education.'

FRENCH, CHARLES EDWIN CLUGE, late Survey of India (b. 16th Mar., 1875).—Joined the service as proby. sub.-asst. supt., 14th Nov., 1896; confd., Nov., 1897; extra asst. supt., Sept., 1904; dep. supt., Nov., 1924; supt., Nov., 1926; ret'd., Mar., 1930.

FRENCH, JOSEPH CHARLES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th Jan., 1883).—Educ. at Wimbledon Coll., Lincoln Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1912; I.A.R.O., Jan. to March, 1918; mag. and collr., Oct., 1918; ret'd., Apl., 1932.

FRENCH, LESLIE HAROLD VINCENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 11th Nov., 1912).—Educ. at Copthorne School, Sussex, Winchester Coll., and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1937; arrived in India, Jan., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

FRESTON, THOMAS ANTHONY, late Indian C.S. (b. 20th May, 1898).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Dec., 1922; served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr. from 16th Dec., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1929; offg. magte. and collr., Apl., 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

FRIEL, RALPH, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 25th June, 1884).—Educ. at Waterford and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 10th Dec., 1907; and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1912; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), July, 1918, to March, 1919; dep. commr., Sept., 1921; offg. sec. to govt. of Assam, transferred depts., Jan., 1927; confd., Sept., 1927; ret'd., July, 1934.

FROME, NORMAN FREDERIC, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.E.E., D.F.C., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (dir. of telegraphs) (b. 23rd Sept., 1899).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1923, as asst. div. engr.; div. engr., Oct., 1931; dir., Sind and Baluchistan Circle, Nov., 1934; senr. elec. engr., Mar., 1935; dir. of tels., Apl., 1938.

FRY, ARTHUR BROWNFIELD, C.B., C.I.E., D.S.O., M.D., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) late Bengal Medical Dept.—Joined the service, 27th July, 1899; in mil. employ prior to 31st Aug., 1910; addl. dep. sanitary commr., malaria research, Dec., 1910; and offg. sanitary commr., April, 1914; on mil. service from Oct., 1914; D.S.O., Aug., 1917; C.I.E., June, 1919; profro. of hygiene, sch. of tropical medicine, etc., Calcutta, Feb., 1922; reverted to mil. employ, April, 1926; C.B., Jan., 1930; ret'd., Apl., 1930.

FRY, AUGASTINE SARGOOD, M.B., Ch.M., F.R.C.S.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Punjab) (b. 4th Nov., 1890).—First commn., 13th Mar., 1916; civil surg., Punjab, Aprl., 1933.

FRY, REV. ERNEST HENRY, Indian Eccles. Estab. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Madras, 16th Oct., 1931.

FRY, JOHN TIMOTHY, late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 10th Feb. 1873).—Joined the service, 6th Jan., 1888; asst. storekeeper, March, 1903; 1st asst. storekeeper, June, 1906; services lent to Munitions Board from 5th July, 1917, to 30th Sept., 1919; asst. stores supt., Oct., 1917; dep. ditto, June, 1918; dep. contr. of stores, Aug., 1926; offg. contr. of stores, May, 1927; ret'd. July, 1929.

FRY, LESLIE ALFRED CHARLES, Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 17th Aprl., 1908).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1928; in mil. employ till Nov., 1933; asst. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, Nov., 1933; asst. commr., Lucknow, June, 1934; under training in the Govt. of India Secretariat, May, 1935; asst. commr., Lucknow, July, 1935; under-secty. to the resdt. at Hyderabad, Oct., 1935; vice-consul, Zabul, Dec., 1936; confd., Aprl., 1937.

FRYER, ARTHUR CLARE, Indian Police (asst. supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 2nd Oct., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 18th Nov., 1922; comdt., Baluch. Levy, April, 1926; asst. supt., govt. rly. police, March, 1927; services placed at disposal of N.W.F.P., Dec., 1932; supt. (subs. provl.), Feb., 1936.

FULLER, EDWIN GERALD, Burma Commn. (dep. commr.) (b. 16th Jan., 1893).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 20th Oct., 1914; extra asst. commr., Mar., 1921; addl. dist. magte., July, 1931; dep. commr., Aug., 1934.

FURLEY, HUGH DENYS, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 7th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service, 16th Mar., 1906, as asst. loco. supt.; dist. loco. supt., N.W. Rly., April, 1912; divl. supt. (provl.), Oct., 1924; offg. dep. chief operating supt., Oct., 1926; offg. divl. supt., Oct., 1927; confd., Sept., 1928; offg. ch. operating supt., N.W. rly., Aprl., 1934; confd., Sept., 1934; ret'd., Jan., 1938.

FURNESS, CECIL ARUNDEL, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accs. dept.) (b. 5th Mar., 1885).—Apptd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India Office, 4th Sept., 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, 1st Aprl., 1921; clerical officer, Oct., 1921; exec. officer, April, 1922.

FURNESS, HORACE GEOFFREY, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 11th Mar., 1890).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1909; asst. supt., July, 1912; I.A.R.O. from 6th Aug., 1916, to 28th Feb., 1919; offg. supt., Jan., 1920, and again, Jan., 1923; confd., Oct., 1926; princpl., police training sch., Vellore, June, 1936.

FYFE, WILLIAM, Dept. of Industries, Madras (b. 25th Jan., 1885).—Apptd. inspr. of industrial schools, 9th June, 1920.

FYSON, HUGH, O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 16th May, 1880).—Educ. at Ressall Sch. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 1st Dec. 1904; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; K.i-H. medal, 2nd class, Jan., 1915; supt.,

Mandi State, Sept., 1915, to April, 1917; O.B.E., Jan., 1921; dep. commr., Dec., 1919; ret'd., Oct., 1929.

FYSON, PHILIP FURLEY, M.A., F.L.S., late Indian Educ. Service (b. 17th Aprl., 1877).—Apptd. prof. of biology, presdy. coll., Madras, 11th July, 1904; prof. of botany, ditto, ditto, Dec., 1914; dist. educ. officer, Feb., 1923; offg. princpl., presdy. coll., July, 1925; ret'd., Aprl., 1932.

GABRIEL, CECIL HAMILTON, C.V.O., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 16th Feb., 1879).—First commn. dated 7th Dec., 1901; was in mil. employ until 18th Sept., 1904, when he was employed on special duty under govt. of India, for. dept.; spl. asst. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, March, 1905; pol. asst., and supy. asst. commr., Jan., 1906; asst. commr., Delhi, March, 1906; H.B.M.'s consul, and asst. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, July, 1906; second asst. to resdt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1908; asst. pol. agent, Chilas, Oct., 1909; first asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Jan., 1913; ditto to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Jan., 1919; dist. judge, Kohat, Nov., 1920; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Feb., 1921; confd., Aug., 1921; C.V.O., Feb., 1922; pol. agent, Bhopal, April, 1925; resident, Mewar, Feb., 1928; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1928; resident, Jaipur, May-Sept., 1928; ditto, Rajputana states, May, 1928; offg. resdt., Gwalior, Aprl., 1929; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

GABRIEL, SIR EDMUND VIVIAN, C.S.I., C.M.G., C.V.O., C.B.E., V.D., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 7th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offd. as commr., Ajmer-Merwara, June-July, 1901; offd. as asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen. in Central India, Nov., 1901; asst. to pol. A.D.C. to S. of S., India Office, for H.M.'s coronation, June to July, 1902; on deputation as attache, govt. of India, Nov., 1902, to Jan., 1903; asst. sec. in the foreign dept. Feb., 1903; offg. under sec., March, 1905; confd., Nov., 1905; C.V.O., Jan., 1906; pol. offr. with the Prime Minister of Nepal, Sept., 1908; resd. in W.R. States, Sept., 1908; sec. to chief commr., N.W.F. Prov., Jan., 1909; first asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Oct., 1909; pol. agent and ditto, Feb., 1910; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; sec. to central citce. for coronation Durbar, Delhi, Dec., 1910, to March, 1912; special duty, govt. of India for. dept., Jan., 1913, to March, 1914; on deputation to Bikaner State, March, 1914; on special duty under agent to the govr.-gen. in the Rajputana, Sept., 1914; service, replaced with govt. of Bengal, March, 1915; on mil. duty, from March, 1915; finl. adviser, Occupied Enemy Territory, 1918-19; Knight of Justice of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; Order of Crown of Italy (Officer); Order of St. Maurice and St. Lazarus (Officer); Italian War Cross; C.M.G., June, 1918; C.B.E., June 1919; ret'd., Mar., 1920; knighted, May, 1937.

GADRE, NARAYAN BALKRISHNA, M.A.I.E. (Ind.), B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 3rd Dec., 1899).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 8th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Nov., 1931; confd., Oct., 1933.

GADSDEN, CECIL HOLROYD, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 18th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1907; asst. supt., May, 1909; supt., Dec., 1917; asst. inspr.-genl., May, 1929; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Aprl., 1930, June, 1933, and again, Jan., 1935; confd., Dec., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

GAGE, EDWARD TREGURTHA, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (inspr. of schools, Burma) (b. 6th April, 1888).—Joined the service as prinpl., govt. high school, Rangoon, on 18th Nov., 1918; head master, Taunggyi, Nov., 1922; ditto, English high school, Maymyo, Aprl., 1933; inspr. of schools, Burma, Aprl., 1936.

GAINSFORD, DONALD, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 18th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 12th June, 1920; offg. supt., April, 1926; confd., Oct., 1931.

GAISFORD, PHILIP, Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Nov., 1891).—First commn. dated 18th Jan., 1911; in milty. employ to 24th June, 1919; asst. commr., June, 1919; sec. to agent to govt.-genl. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, April, 1921, offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and p.l. dept., March, 1927; confd., Oct., 1927; in foreign service as guardian to H.H. the Maharajah of Gwalior, Mar., 1929; secty. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Nov., 1934; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Mar., 1936; confd., Nov., 1936; dep. secty., pol. dept., Aprl., 1937, and again, Sept., 1937; offg. jt. secty., ditto, May, 1937; resdt. at Gwalior and pol. agent for states of Rampur and Benares, Aprl., 1938.

GAIT, Sir EDWARD ALBERT, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 11th Dec., 1884, and served as asst. commr. in Assam; provl. supt. of census, 1890, and wrote the provl. report; acted as sec. to the chief commr., 1891-92 and 1895-96; dep. commr., Nov., 1892; dir. of land records and agric., Nov., 1893; mag. and collr., Nov., 1897; supt. of census operations, Bengal, April, 1900; offg. census commr. of India, Jan., 1903; dep. commr., Dec., 1904; member of legisl. council, Bengal, June, 1905; chief sec. to govt. of Bengal, pol., appt. and rev. depts., Jan., 1908; C.I.E., Jan., 1907; commr., July, 1909; census commr. for India, Nov., 1909; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa as member, board of rev. April, 1912; C.S.I., June, 1912; member of exec. council, Aug., 1912; K.C.S.I., June, 1915; lieut.-govr., Bihar and Orissa, 20th Nov., 1915; retd., Dec., 1920; member of the Council of India, 20th June, 1922, to 19th June, 1927; author of 'Report on the Census of Assam, 1891, and other papers dealing with Assam govt., 'A History of Assam,' 'Bengal Census Report, 1901,' 'India Census Report, 1901,' and 'India Census Report, 1911.'

GALBRAITH, IAN WILLIAM, M.C., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 10th Dec., 1898).—First commn., dated 30th Jan., 1917; joined the pol. dept., 24th Oct., 1922; asst. commr., Kohat, Jan., 1923; supy. asst. commr., Peshawar, Mar., 1924; under-sec. to Ch. Com. N.W.F.P., May, 1925; asst. commr., Charasadda, Feb., 1927; ditto, Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1927; commr., ditto, May, 1930, and again, Sept.,

1934; pol. agent, Mahil Kantha, Feb., 1931; secty. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, June, 1935; pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1937.

GALE, BERKELEY, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Punjab) (b. 5th Feb., 1884).—First commn. dated 30th Jan., 1909; in mil. employ to 1st Jan., 1923; civil surg., Jan., 1923.

GALE, LUCY, India Office (hr. clerical officer) (b. 16th Jan., 1882).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st June, 1921; actg. hr. ditto, July, 1935; confd., Jan., 1936.

GALE, U. MAUNG (C1), Indian Police (b. 5th May, 1889).—Apptd. dist. supt., Burma, on 7th April, 1925; awarded King's Police medal, Jan., 1927; awarded bar to ditto, Jan., 1932.

GALE, U. MAUNG (A8), B.A., late Burma commission (b. 15th June, 1880).—Previous service from July, 1904; dep. commr., March, 1924; offg. dep. sec. to govt., March, 1924; sec. to govt., local govt. dept., May, 1927; ditto, judl. dept., Aug., 1929; retd., June, 1935.

GALES, Sir ROBERT RICHARD, Kt., F.C.H., M.Inst.C.E., M.A.S.C.E., late Railway Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 24th Aug., 1886, and posted to state rlys.; exec. engr., Nov., 1895; suptg. engr., March, 1910; chief engr., March, 1915; knighted, June, 1915; chief engr. with the rly. board, July, 1915; offg. agent, N.W. Ry., May, 1917; temp. agent, ditto, July, 1917; retd., Oct., 1919.

GALLAGHER, WILLIAM NORMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 12th Jan., 1913).—Educ. at Royal Academy Institution, Belfast, and Trinity College, Dublin; joined the service, 18th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay asst. as collr.

GALLANT, MAURICE NAHUM, B.A., B.Sc. Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 24th Mar., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928; for. econ., Oct., 1934.

GALLETTI DI CADILHAC, A. M. A. C. (see Di Cadilhac, A. M. A. C. Galletti).

GALLETTI DI CADILHAC, R. (see Di Cadilhac, R.)

GALLOWAY, ARNOLD CRAWSHAW, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 5th Jan., 1901).—First commn., 24th Dec., 1920; in milty. employ to June, 1928; under-secty. to A.G.G. in the states of Western India, Mar., 1932; vice-consul, Zahidan, Jan., 1933; under-secty. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, Feb., 1934; secty., ditto, June, 1934; and again, Aprl., 1937; secty., British legn., Kabul, Nov., 1934; asst. pol. agent, Bahrein, Nov., 1937; secty. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Feb., 1938.

GALVIN, JOHN SMART, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Bombay (civil surg.) (b. 4th Mar., 1892).—First commn., 15th Mar., 1920; civil surg., Mar., 1931; offg. presdy. surg., Bombay, Feb., 1935.

GALVIN, PERCY HAROLD, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 6th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 27th Sept., 1913 as asst. supt., E.I. rly.; transpn. supt. (traffic comml.) E.I. rly., May, 1934.

GANAPATI, CODANDA MADIAH, Major, I.M.S., Medical Dept., Madras (dir. of public health) (b. 1st Sept., 1886).—Date of first commn., 15th Mar., 1920; asst. dir. genl., I.M.S., June, 1924; dep. ditto, May, 1933; dir. of public health, Madras, Oct., 1934.

GANAPATIPILLAI, NATARAJA, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. Oct., 1879).—Apptd. 1st Jan., 1928; offg. exec. engr., Sept., 1928; confd., Jan., 1933; retd., Oct., 1934.

GANDASING CHEEMA, SARDAR, D.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (horticulturist to govt. of Bombay) (b. 2nd Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as horticulturist, 13th June, 1921.

GANDY, G. L., A.M.R.A.E.S., Civil Aviation Directorate (dep. dir.) (b. 30th June, 1900).—Apptd. dep. dir. of civil aviation in India, 2nd July, 1935; offg. dir., Apl., 1936.

GANGA NATH, BABU, B.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., United Provs. (judge, high ct., Allahabad) (b. 11th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 19th May, 1908; sub judge, June, 1923; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1933; addl. judge, high ct., Allahabad, variously from Oct., 1934; judge, ditto, Apl., 1937.

GANGARAM KAULA (see Kaula, G.).

GANGARAM, V. (see Vadhumal Gangaram).

GANGULI, ANIL BEHARI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1933.

GANGULI, JOGENDRA NATH, Rai Bahadur, B.A., late Indian Police Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 11th July, 1875).—Joined the service, 15th June, 1897, and held non-gazetted appt. prior to 27th Feb., 1913; inspr., Feb., 1913; dep. supt., July, 1921; apptd. to I.P.S., 1st June, 1928; supt., June, 1928; retd., Sept., 1930.

GANGULI, JYOTI PRAKASH, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Stores Dept. (b. 20th Feb., 1882).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st Apl., 1900; offg. asst. secty., Indian munitions board, Jan., 1910; confd., Feb., 1919; off. under-secty., industries and labour dept., Aug., 1924; on spl. duty in posts and tels. dept., Nov., 1925; dep. dir. of admn. and intell., June, 1930; retd., June, 1937.

GANGULI, NIBARAN CHANDRA, Rai Bahadur, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 13th Mar., 1875).—Joined the service, 7th Feb., 1901; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1924; exec. engr., Nov., 1924; retd., Mar., 1930.

GANGULI, SANAT KUMAR, B.L., Bengal Judl. Service (b. 9th Nov., 1886).—Apptd. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1928.

GANGULI, SUSIL KUMAR, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (b. 16th July, 1884), Joined the service, 20th June, 1905; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Apl., 1910; asst. regr. co-op. societies, Oct., 1922; offg. asst. secty., agric. and industries dept., Bengal, Dec., 1926; asst. secty. to Govt., Bengal, irrign. dept., Feb., 1929; regr., co-op. societies, Bengal, Jan., 1931; retd., Oct., 1934.

GANPAT RAI, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, B.A. Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 16th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service, 24th Sept., 1915; asst. engr., Sept., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; under-secty. to govt., irrigation bch., Punjab, Oct., 1931.

GANY, HAFIZ MUHAMMAD ABDUL (see Hafiz, M. A. G.).

GARBETT, COLIN CAMPBELL, C.S.I., C.M.G., C.I.E., B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (fin. commr., Punjab) (b. 22nd May, 1881).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 28th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; settlt. offr., Mandi State, 1910; dist. judge, Simla, 1913; lent to mil. dept., 1915; asst. postal censor, Karachi, March, 1915; ditto, Bombay, Sept., 1916; on active service, Mesopotamia, 1916; revenue commr., Mesopotamia; Feb., 1917, and administrator, agricl. development (mil.), June, 1917; twice mentd. in despatches. C.I.E., Aug., 1917; temply. empld. in pol; dept., India Office, July, 1919, to Aug., 1920; civil sec. to high commr., Mesopotamia, Sept., 1920, to May, 1922; dep. commr., Dec., 1921; C.M.G., June, 1922; ch. sec. to govt., Punjab, April, 1931; commr., Multan, Mar., 1935; C.S.I., June, 1935; finl. commr. (devel.), Dec., 1937.

GARDINER, REGINALD THOMAS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr.) (b. 27th Apl., 1906).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1929; confd., Nov., 1930.

GARDINER, WILLIAM NORMAN, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 1st June, 1882).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, store dept., 13th Nov., 1901; on mily. service, Nov., 1917, to Feb., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, March, 1926.

GARDNER, JAMES CLARK MOLESWORTH, Indian Forest Service (systematic entomologist, Dehra Dun) (b. 15th Mar., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 11th Dec., 1922; systematic entomologist, Aug., 1923; offg. forest entomologist, Feb., 1932.

GAREWAL, AJAB SINGH, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), Major, I.M.S. Jail Dept. (supt., Central Jail) (b. 13th Jan., 1894).—First commn., 24th Apl., 1920; in mil. employ up to 16th Feb., 1931; supt., Central Jail, Jubbulpore, Apl., 1931; ditto, Hazaribagh (B. & O.), Dec., 1931; ditto, Central Provs., Mar., 1933.

GAREWAL, SARDAR JAGDEO SINGH, M.R.C.V.S., I.V.S. (b. 18th Nov., 1894).—Joined the service as supt., C.V.D. (Punjab and N.W.F.P.), 30th Jan., 1918; confd., Jan., 1921.

GARLAND, EDWARD ANDREW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 10th May, 1898).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1922, as asst. consvr., Bombay, dep. consvr., Nov., 1925; p.a. to I.G. of forests, Dehra Dun, Aug., 1938.

GARNIER, CHARLES NEWDIGATE, O.B.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 5th Jan., 1883).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. supt., on 1st Oct., 1906; O.B.E., June, 1918; asst. divl. engr., Nov.,

1920; divl. engr., March, 1925; offg. dir., tel. engrg., Punjab and N.W.F. circle, Mar., 1931; confd. as dir. of tel. engrg., May, 1934; offg. P.M.G., Punjab, Apl., 1935; confd., Aug., 1936; retd., Apl., 1938.

GARRAD, ERNEST WILLIAM, Indian Police (b. 9th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 12th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., July, 1929; addl. asst. inspr.-genl., Simla, June, 1935.

GARRAD, HAROLD SEPTIMUS, India Office (higher clerical officer, military dept.) (b. 21st Aug., 1886).—Apptd. clerical officer, 17th Oct., 1921; higher clerical officer, Jan., 1927.

GARRATT, RICHARD, Lt.-Col., I.A. (retd.) (b. 3rd Oct., 1874).—First commn. dated 15th May, 1895; in mil. emp. to July, 1901, when he was apptd. sanitary officer; supy. asst. commr., Punjab, Dec., 1902; pol. asst., June, 1903; pol. agent, Tochi, Feb. to Sept., 1910; asst. commr., Thal sub-dn., Jan., 1913; dep. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1914; attd. offr., Kurram agency, Nov., 1914; pol. agent, Kurram, Dec., 1914; dep. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1917; pol. agent, Tochi, Dec., 1917; ditto, N. Waziristan, Sept., 1919; dist. judge, Peshawar, April, 1922; pol. agent, Khyber, March, 1925; offg. rev., commr., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1928; retd., Oct., 1929.

GARRETT, HERBERT LEONARD OFFLEY, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 16th June, 1881).—Educ. at Charterhouse and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Dec., 1912; profr., govt. coll., Lahore, 5th Jan., 1913; on milty. duty from 1st Oct., 1917, to 31st Dec., 1918; sec., publicity dept., June to Oct., 1919; keeper of the records of the govt. of Punjab since 1924; member of the Indian Records Commn. since 1926; prinpl., govt. coll., Lahore, Sept., 1927; retd., June, 1936; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; supervisor of I.C.S. probationers at Cambridge University, Oct., 1937. Publications:—'Cunningham's History of the Sikhs' (new ed.), 'Muhammadan Period of Indian History', and a number of historical textbooks; general editor of Punjab Govt. Record Office publications.

GARRETT, JOSEPH HUGH, C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 22nd June, 1880).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. settlt. offr., dep. commr. of salt and excise, northern divn., Dec., 1919; offg. collr. and dist. mag., and pol. agent, Jan., 1921; offg. collr. and Talukdari Settlement officer, June, 1923, and again, June, 1925; confd., Jan., 1926; offg. commr., March, 1925, Feb., 1926, Mar., 1929, and again, June, 1933; member legisl. council, July, 1929; again, Mar., 1931; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bombay, pol. and reforms dept., Apl., 1935, and again, Apl., 1936; commr., Ahmedabad, Nov., 1935; actg. govt. of Sind, Aug. to Oct., 1938.

GARROD, EDGAR SAMUEL, D.S.O., M.C., D.C.M., Indian Police (asst. supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 6th Mar., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 12th Oct., 1923; asst. dist. officer, frontier constabulary, Jan., 1925; offg. supt., May, 1933.

GARROD, REV. WILLIAM FRANCIS, Indian Eccl. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 23rd Mar., 1893).—Apptd. junior chaplain, 10th Feb., 1930.

GARROD, WILLIAM HENRY EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr. Bombay) (b. 10th May, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; cons. pub. health engr. to govt., Nov., 1937.

GARSON, WILLIAM AUBREY, M.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd May, 1895) Joined the service as asst. exec. engr. on 1st Oct., 1921; exec. engr., Bihar, Oct., 1927; retd., June, 1938.

GARSTIN, WILLIAM ARTHUR MACDONNELL, C.B.E., Lt.-Col. I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 14th May, 1882).—In mily. employ. from 28th July, 1900; asst. resdt. Western States, Rajputana, Aug., 1907; supy. asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Feb., 1908; asst. commr., ditto, July, 1909; presl. asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Dec., 1910, to May, 1911, and from Sept., 1911, to May, 1914; district judge, Kohat, Dec., 1916; asst. sec. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., April, 1918; dep. commr., Bannu, Nov., 1920; O.B.E., Jan., 1923; divl. and sess. judge Peshawar, May, 1925; pol. agent, Khyber, Feb., 1928; C.B.E., Jan., 1930; pol. agent, southern states of Central India and in Malwa, Mar., 1932; resdt., Gwalior, Nov., 1932; resdt., Mewar, and pol. agent, S. Rajputana states, Mar., 1933; offg. resdt., and A.G.A., Madras states, Feb., 1935; retd., May, 1937.

GARVIN, STEPHEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 2nd Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

GASH, REV. IVER JOHN, A.K.C., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 26th May, 1890).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, Bengal, 9th Oct. 1928; transferred to Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1929; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1930; transfd. to Central Provs., Oct., 1932.

GASKELL, WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 14th Jan., 1874).—Educ. at Loughborough and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 21st Nov., 1898, and served in N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1902; under sec. to govt., April, 1904; asst. settlmnt. offr., Oct., 1905; dep. commr. and jt. sec. to board of rev., April, 1912; temply. employed in finl. dept., India Office, from Jan. to Dec., 1916; mag. and collr., March, 1918; commr. of income tax, Dec., 1920; opium agent, Ghazipur, March, 1924; C.I.E. June, 1925; offg. member, central bd. of revenue, Oct., 1928; retd., July, 1932.

GASKING, REV. CLEMENT AUGUSTINE, Indian Eccl. Estabt. (junr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 6th June, 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 12th Oct., 1926; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1928.

GASSON, LIONEL BELL, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 9th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1910; asst. supt., July, 1912; supt., Jan., 1926; dep. commr., March, 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928; asst. inspr.-

genl., Nov., 1930; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1934; supt., spl. branch, C.I.D., Apl., 1935; cent. intell. offcr., Madras Prov., May, 1938.

GASTER, VIVIAN ISAAC, B.A., Office of High Commr. for India (educ. officer, principal exec. grade, education dept.) (b. 11th Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; on mil. service Nov., 1914, to April, 1917; asst. principal (temp.), Home Office, Oct., 1918, to Feb., 1920; asst. to local adviser to Indian students, London, Feb., 1920; transfed. to Office of High Commr. for India as asst. to joint secs., Indian students dept., Oct., 1920; principal exec. officer, educ. dept., April, 1925.

GASTRELL, EVERARD HUDDLESTON, O.B.E., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 30th Apl., 1898).—First commn., dated 14th Nov., 1916; joined pol. dept., 8th March, 1922; Brit. Consul for Arabistan, July, 1922; vice-consul, Ahwaz, Nov., 1922; on settlement training in Punjab, Nov., 1926; services placed at disposal of Ch. Commr., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1927; asst. resdt. Kolhapur, March, 1927; vice-consul, Duzdap, Dec., 1930; secty. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, Dec., 1931; and again, Nov., 1933; pol. agent, Bahrein, July, 1933; consul-genl. for Khorasan, Mar., 1934; claims commr., Baluchistan, July, 1935; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Mar., 1936; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Nov., 1936; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Dec., 1936; pol. agent i/c. of the Bolan Pass and Chagai dist., Feb., 1937; O.B.E., May, 1937.

GATELEY, TERRENCE PATRICK (b. 16th Dec., 1898).—Elec. engr., Central Public Works Dept., Oct., 1933.

GATES, SIR FRANK CAMPBELL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Barr. (1891), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 13th Dec., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; from Aug., 1884, served in Burma as asst. commr.; on special duty in Upper Burma, March, 1887; junr. sec. to chief commr., April, 1887; under sec., Oct., 1887; dep. commr., Aug., 1889; junr. sec., March, 1891; sec., March, 1893; rev. sec., Jan., 1895; sec. to govt., May, 1897; commr., Aug., 1901; chief sec. to govt., Sept., 1903; member of legisl. council, Burma, Sept., 1903, and Oct., 1905; finl. commr., April, 1907; C.S.I., Jan., 1906; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1913; retd., April, 1914.

GATFORD, HARRY, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 6th Apl., 1889).—Joined the service, 10th Apl., 1920; resdt. engr., E.I. rly., Apl., 1924; exec. engr. (junr.) E.I. rly., Apl., 1930; ditto (senr.), Nov., 1933.

GAUL, Rev. ARTHUR CECIL, M.A. (Oxon), late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 20th Oct., 1880).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 22nd April, 1918; junior chaplain, April, 1920; senr. chaplain, May, 1927; retd., Oct., 1935.

GAULD, WILLIAM O.B.E., late India Office (b. 5th Mar., 1875).—Clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 28th Dec., 1895; transfed. to public works dept., Jan., 1902; resdt. clerk, March, 1914 to March, 1923; staff clerk, April, 1919; administrative officer, April, 1922; apptd. asst. govt. dir. of Indian rly. cos., Jan., 1925; O.B.E., Jan., 1934; retd., Jan., 1936.

GAUNT, CLIVE HERBERT, LL.B., late Burma Judl. dept. (b. 7th June, 1874).—Apptd. offg. asst. govt. advocate, 16th April, 1914; confd., April, 1921; retd., June, 1934.

GAUNTLETT, SIR MAGER FREDERIC, K.C.I.E., K.B.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Oct., 1873).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived 17th Nov., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. stilt. officer; asst. acct.-gen., Bengal, Dec., 1897; dep. acct.-gen., N.W.P. and Oudh, March, 1898; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1901; transfed. to fin. dept., govt. of India, as dep. acctnt.-gen., United Provs., April, 1903; offg. acctnt.-gen., ditto, Nov., 1904; ditto. Burma, April, 1906, to Nov., 1907; acctnt.-gen., Burma, and commr. of paper currency, Nov., 1908; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1909; ditto. Burma, April, 1906, to Nov., 1907; acctnt.-gen., Burma, and commr. of paper currency, Nov., 1908; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1909; ditto, rys., April, 1910; offg. compt. and auditor-gen., March, 1912, to Oct., 1914; C.I.E., Jan., 1915; on deputation under the Board of Admiralty, Aug., 1915 to Nov., 1917; again offg. compt. and auditor-gen., Jan., 1918; confd. July, 1918; C.B.E., Jan., 1918; K.B.E., Jan., 1923; K.C.I.E., June, 1929; retd., May, 1930.

GAURI, NATH, P.M.S. Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 20th Sept., 1884).—Civil surg., Apl., 1927.

GAVAGHAN, EDWARD LAWRENCE, late Finance Dept., Govt. of India (b. 18th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service, Apl., 1908, as asst. examr., P.W. Accts.; on milty. service from 7th Jan., 1916, to 22nd July, 1920; asst. acct.-gen., Bombay, Jan., 1921; dep. ditto, Bengal, Feb., 1921; ditto, United Provs., Dec., 1921; ditto, Central Provs., April, 1923; dep. dir. of audit, U.P., Oct., 1926; offg. dep. acct.-genl., Punjab, March, 1927; ditto, posts and tels., Calcutta Nov., 1927; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, Nov., 1929; offg. dep. auditor-genl., Dec., 1930; offg. compt., Assam, Feb., 1931; ch. auditor, E.B. rly., and govt. exr. of accts., Assam rly., Jan., 1934; offg. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Apl., 1934; retd., Aug., 1936.

GAWAN-TAYLOR, EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and magte., Sind) (b. 29th Oct., 1889).—Educ. at Durham Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 28th Nov., 1914, and served in Bombay as asst. magte. and collr., supt. land records, Southern Div., Jan., 1921; on depn. as settl. officer from Dec., 1921, to May, 1922; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1923; ditto and Talukdari settlement offr., Feb., 1926; confd., Apl., 1931.

GAWNE, EWAN MOORE, Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. magte., Madras) (b. 26th March, 1889).—Educ. at Wellington, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 8th Dec., 1913, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr., and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1919, to Feb., 1921; temp. dep. sec. to govt. of Madras, April, 1924, to June, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., July, 1925; confd., Feb., 1932.

GAWTHORNE, ARTHUR RICHARDSON, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 12th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 15th Apl., 1920; exec. engr., Burma Rlys., July, 1933.

GEARING, ALFRED HENRY, late Indian Police (b. 23rd Mar., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on 18th Mar., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1933; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

GEBBIE, SIR FREDERICK ST. JOHN, KT., C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1893, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1902; suptg. engr., Aug., 1912; acting chief engr. in Sind, and sec., Indus River Commission, April, 1915; chief engr., and actg. sec. to govt., Bombay, Dec., 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; confd. as sec. to govt., Jan., 1920; offg. inspr. gen. of irrign., govt. of India, July, 1921; consulting engr. to govt. of India, June, 1923; Kt., Jan., 1925; ret'd., Aug., 1926.

GEMMELL, GEOFFREY WOODBURN, Major, I.A. Survey of India (supt.) (b. 26th Sept., 1897).—First commn., 15th Nov., 1915; asst. supt. geodetic bch., May, 1927; supt., Apl., 1930.

GENNINGS, JOHN FREDERICK, C.B.E., Barr. (b. 21st Sept., 1885) (dir. of information and commr. of lab., Bombay).—Joined the service as dir. of information (temp.), 26th Dec., 1920; confd., 10th Oct., 1924; member, iegisl. councl, Bombay, 1932; commr. of labour, Bombay, Apl., 1933; delegate of Govt. of India to internatl. lab. conf., Geneva, 1933; presdt., appellate ct., Dec., 1933; C.B.E., June, 1933.

GENTLE, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Barr., Judl. Dept., Madras (judge, high ct.).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, Jan., 1936.

GENTLE, JOSEPH ALFRED HECTOR ROBERTS, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 26th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 2nd Jan., 1925; dep. consvr., Jan., 1929.

GENTRY, EDGAR CHARLES (b. 10th Aug., 1893).—Architect, Central Public Works Dept., Oct., 1933.

GEORGE, FRANK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 9th Feb., 1907).—Educ. at Priory School, Shrewsbury, and Downing Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

GEORGE, FRANK SEAVAR SCOTNEY, late Indian Police Service (b. 16th Mar., 1876).—Joined the service as prob'r., Madras, 22nd Nov., 1896; asst. supt., July, 1898; supt., June, 1907; services lent to Travancore Durbar from Aug., 1910, to Aug., 1915; dep. inspr.-gen., July, 1919; awarded King's police medal; ret'd., May, 1930.

GEORGE, HUGH SHAW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provinces) (b. 29th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 6th Dec., 1915; on mil. duty from 9th Aug., 1917, to 8th March, 1919; dep. consvr., Nov., 1921; sylviculturist, C.P., Oct., 1932.

GEORGE, JAMES, B.A. (Cantab.) Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 17th May, 1905).—Educ. at Priory

County School, Shrewsbury, Queen's Coll., Cambridge, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Apl., 1931; offg. under-secty. to govt., Bengal, pol. and appt. depts., Sept., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1936.

GEORGESON, WILLIAM WARES, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and sub-collr., Madras) (b. 13th March, 1906).—Educ. at Aberdeen Grammar School, Aberdeen University, and Pembroke College, Oxford; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Jan., 1933.

GERAGHTY, MICHAEL JOSEPH, Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 29th Dec., 1929.

GERRARD, FREDERICK WERNHAM, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 25th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service, 10th Dec., 1908; serving in Army dept. as asst. censor, Oct., 1914, to April, 1915; I.A.R.O., April, 1915, to Jan., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1920; supt. of police, May, 1922; on foreign service in Shanghai, May, 1929 to Mar., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

GERRARD, MISS JESSIE MARGARET, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (prinpl., Lady Willingdon training coll., Madras) (b. 9th Jan., 1892).—Apptd. prof'r., Queen Mary's Coll. Madras, 8th Aug., 1921; prinpl., Lady Willingdon trng. coll., Dec., 1925; confd., Jan., 1927.

GHAFFUR, ABDUL (see Abdul Ghaffur).

GHANSHYAM, D.S., RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.A., LL.B., PUNJAB C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 10th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service, 2nd Aug., 1906; munisf., Mar., 1910; extra asst. commr., Apl., 1913; sub-judge, Apl., 1916; dist. and sess. judge, confd., Jan., 1934.

GHARPUREY, H. G. (see Hari Ganpat Rao Gharpurey).

GHARPUREY, KHANDU GANPAT RAO, B.A. (Cal.), L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.) L.R.F.P. & S. (Glas.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 20th July, 1880).—Joined, 1st Feb., 1906, and served in mil. dept. prior to 12th July, 1911; on milty. duty from 14th Aug., 1914; to 18th Jan., 1920; civil surg., Dec., 1922; offg. surg.-genl. with govt. of Bombay, Jan., 1934; ret'd., July, 1935.

GHATAK LABANYA MOHAN, M.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 2nd Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as an officr. Cl. II, May, 1923; dep. mil. acct. genl. (jnr.), Mar., 1935; ditto (senr.), Apl., 1936; dep. finl. adv'r., mil. fin., Apl., 1936.

GHATAK, NARENDRANATH, M.B.E., Barr., Judl. Dept., Bengal (b. Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as master and official referee, high ct., original side, Calcutta, 22nd Nov., 1922.

GHATE, BAL GANGADHAR, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian Police (b. 1st Oct., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 27th April, 1928.

GHATE, NAMDEV SITARAMPANT, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 4th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930.

GHAZANFAR ALI KHAN, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st Mar., 1875).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., Christ's Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 8th Dec., 1899, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Jan., 1915; O.B.E., June, 1918; excise commr., Nagpur, April, 1920; offg. commr., Jan., 1924, and Mar., 1925; confd., Aug., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; retd., July, 1934.

GHAZANFAR ALI NAQVI, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (b. 17th Apl., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 27th May 1926; offg. supt., Mar., 1933.

GHOLAP, LAKSHMAN TRIMBAK, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 4th Oct., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. magte. and collr.; offg. pol. agent, Jawhar, Mar., 1933; collr. and dist. magte., Hyderabad.

GHOSAL, SIR JOSNA, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 13th June, 1871).—Educ. at Metropolitan Coll., Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 25th Nov., 1895, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; junr. collr. and pol. agent, April, 1912; K.i.H. medal, 1st class, Jan., 1913; C.I.E., June, 1918; senr. collr., Jan., 1918; member of legisl. council, 1922, 1924; offg. commr. and commr. of excise, March, 1924; confd., Jan., 1926; commr., Jan., 1928; C.S.I., June, 1929; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, Bombay, Mar., 1930; retd., Jan., 1931; knighted, Jan., 1936.

GHOSE, BIRENDRA NATH, Indian State Ry.s. (Transpn. traffic-commcl. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 12th Aug., 1900).—Joined the service, 15th Apl., 1919; asst. supt., June, 1922; senr. scale (provl.), Feb., 1938.

GHOSE, SIR SARAI KUMAR, .A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 3rd July, 1879).—Educ. at the Metrop. Instn. and the Univ., Calcutta, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1910; s.p.t., dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1914; confd. as dist. and sessions judge, Jan., 1916; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, May—Sept., 1928, and again, May, 1929; confd., Sept., 1929; knighted, June, 1938.

GHOSH, AJOY KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Apl., 1908).—Educated at King's College and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Nov., 1932; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

GHOSH CHAUDHURI, HARI SADHAN, B.Sc., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 11th Mar., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931.

GHOSH CHAUDHURI, TAPENDRA KUMAR, Rai Sahib, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 16th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service as proby. dep. supt., Jan., 1911; confd., Jan., 1913; addl. supt., Dec., 1918; offg. supt., April, 1919; confd., May, 1927.

GHOSH, E. M. B., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th Feb., 1907).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 5th Sept., 1932; transfed. to Central P.W.D., May, 1935; offg. exec. engr., May, 1936.

GHOSH, J. M., Barr. Judl. Dept., Bengal (regr. in insolvency, high court, Calcutta) (b. 16th Apl., 1884).—Joined the service 14th May, 1921; asst. regr., high ct., Calcutta, original side, Nov., 1921; dep., regr., ditto, Dec., 1931; offg. regr., in insolvency, original side, Nov., 1932.

GHOSH, MAHIM CHANDRA, M.A. (Cantab.), B.A. (Calc.), Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 20th Jan., 1880).—Educ. at Santosh Jahnavi Sch., Bengal, Calcutta Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; jt. mag., and dep. collr., June, 1911; retransfed. to Bengal, April, 1912; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1916; offg. sec. to govt., judl. dept., Nov., 1927; confd., March, 1928; offg. judge, Calcutta high ct., May, 1930; and again, Nov., 1931; confd., Nov., 1932.

GHOSH, NAGENDRA NATH, Inc. tax dept. (asst. commr.) (b. 8th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1912; inc. tax officer, Oct., 1927; asst. commr., Bihar, Nov., 1933.

GHOSH, NIBARAN CHANDRA, B.A., Indian State Ry.s. (divl. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 21st Dec., 1890).—Apptd. to the service, 20th Mar., 1913; asst. supt. E.I. rly., Jan., 1916; supt., Apl., 1928; offg. divl. supt., E.I. rly., variously from Apl., 1934; confd., Mar., 1938.

GHOSH, RADHAKANTA, B.L., late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 23rd July, 1874).—Joined the service, 4th March, 1902; munsif, April, 1904; sub-judge, April, 1918; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; retd., June, 1930.

GHOSH, SACHINDRA MOHAN, B.A., Indian police (b. 1st Apl., 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 1st Mar., 1935; confd., Mar., 1937.

GHOSH, SHOTTYENDRA KUMAR, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 21st May, 1896).—Educ. at Scottish Churches Coll., Calcutta; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1927; addl. dist. mag., April, 1928; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1928; confd., Dec., 1933; contr. of emigrant labour, govt. of India, Nov., 1937.

GHOSHAL, UMESH KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 25th Aug., 1912).—Educ. at Hindu School, Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., and Lincoln College, Oxford; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1938.

GHULAM ABBAS, B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 17th May, 1902).—Joined the service, cl. II, Mar., 1925; asst. mil. acct. genl., Apl., 1933; secty., Quetta Rec. Cttee., Nov., 1935; dep. mil. acct. genl. (junr.), Oct., 1936.

GHULAM AHMAD, MIAN, M.A., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 12th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 29th Aug., 1923; offg. supt., June, 1928; and again, Oct., 1932; confd., Apl., 1934; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., variously from Feb., 1936.

GHULAM, AHMAD KHAN, B.Sc., M.B., B.S., Major, I.M.S., Jail Dept., Punjab (supdt.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1892).—First commr., 1st Nov., 1920; supdt., Central Jail, Oct., 1930.

GHULAM AHMED, MADANI, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1913).—Educ. at Faiz-I-Am High School, Meerut, and Aligarh Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

GHULAM, MUHAMMAD, M.A., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (finance officer, Communications) (b. 29th Aug., 1895).—Joined the service, 7th March, 1920; asst. audit officer, O. and R. rly., Jan., 1921; govt. exr. of accts., B. & N.W. rlys., Jan., 1925; services placed at disposal of rly. board, Nov., 1925; on foreign service under Bhopal state, June, 1932 to Feb., 1934; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Mar., 1934; offg. dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels., finance, May, 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; offg. finance officer, posts and tels., July, 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; fin. offer., communications, Oct., 1937.

GHUZNAVI, SIR ABDEL KERIM, NAWAB BAHADUR, Kt.,—Member of govt's council, Bengal, 22nd April, 1929, to Apl., 1934.

GIBBS, WALTER FELIX, Indian Police (b. 12th Apl., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supdt., Bengal, on probn., Nov., 1938.

GIBLING, MORLEY THEODORE, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 12th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 1st Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1931; secty., cent. bd. of irrig., Simla, May, 1935.

GIBSON, ALEXANDER JAMES, F.C.H., late Indian Forest Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 14th May, 1877).—Joined the service, 18th Nov., 1898, as asst. consr. of forests; instr., Imperial Forest Sch., Dehra Dun, April, 1905; dep. consr., July, 1906; Imperial forest economist, Dehra Dun, April to Oct., 1908; dep. consr. and pol. asst. to supdt., Simla Hill States, March, 1909, to April, 1910; consr., July, 1921; offg. chief consr., Punjab, March, 1922; consr., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1925; retd., Nov., 1929.

GIBSON, EDMUND CURREY, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 6th July, 1886).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., London, St. John's Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; employed temply. under govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., from June, 1915; tutor and guardian to H.H. the Maharaja of Bharatpur; asst. pol. agent, E. Rajputana States (in addn.), Sept., 1917; addl. asst. master, Mayo Coll., March, 1918; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, April, 1918; dep. commr., Oct., 1923; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Jan., 1924; pol. agent, Eastern States of Rajputana, March, 1925; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, April, 1927; offg. resdt., and agent to govt.-genl., Eastern States, Apl., 1933, C.I.E., June, 1933; offg. resdt., Gwalior, Nov., 1934; resdt., cl. 11, Aug., 1935; resdt. for States of W.I., Oct., 1937.

GIBSON, JOHN CARRINGTON, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 28th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supdt., 8th March, 1912; dist. loco.

supdt., April, 1918; supdt., E.I. Rly., July, 1925, offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Feb., 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; offg. dep. agent (organisation), E.I. rly., Jan., 1936.

GIBSON, JOHN PERCIVAL, M.A., India Office (principal, pol. dept.) (b. 2nd June, 1898).—Educ. at Royal Academ. Inst., Belfast and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. asst. principal, mil. dept., 19th Oct., 1921; transfd. to commerce and revenue dept., June, 1923, and to pol. dept., April, 1924; resident clerk, Oct., 1924; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, Oct., 1927; pte. secty. to perm. under-sec. of state, Dec., 1928; principal Apl., 1930.

GIBSON, RAYMOND EVELYN, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Oct., 1878).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1902, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; supdt. of land records and registrn., Sind, Jan. to April, 1906, and from April, 1907; colonisation offr., Jamrao Canal, Dec., 1909, to Oct., 1910; asst. commr. in Sind, and Sindhi translator to govt., Jan., 1911; pte. sec. to govt., Bombay, Oct., 1912, to March, 1914; collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1922; actg. commr. in Sind, April, 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; member of legisl. council, 1924; commr., Jan., 1932; rev. commr. for Sind, Apl., 1936; C.S.I., June, 1936; retd., Dec., 1937.

GIBSON, THOMAS LOUTTIT STAUNTON, Indian Police (b. 20th Oct., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supdt., Bombay, on probn., Dec., 1937.

GIDNEY, CLAUDE HENRY, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. Indian Political Service (b. 23rd Nov., 1887).—Educ. at Haileybury, Hertford Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; held various temp. appts. under govt of India, for. and pol. dept., from Oct., 1914; pol. offr. (on probn.), and asst. pol. agent, Quetta, Jan., 1918; asst. for Mekran to pol. agent, Kalat, Sept., 1918; pol. agent, Sibi, Nov., 1919; second asst. to agent to gov.-genl. in Baluchistan, Feb., 1920; sec. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, April, 1921; dep. commr., Bannu, Dec., 1923; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Nov., 1926; secty. to ch. commr. and agent to gov.-genl., N.W.F.P., May, 1928; dep. commr., Bannu, May, 1930; secty. to ch. commr. and agent to gov.-genl., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1930; ch. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., June, 1932; mem. exec. council of govt., N.W.F.P., Apl. and Nov., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1932; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1934; member, govt.'s exec. council, N.W.F.P., May, 1936; resident, Hyderabad, Apl., 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937; resdt. at Hyderabad, Oct., 1938.

GIDWANI, VISHINO LAKHIMAL, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 4th Feb., 1914).—Educ. at N.J. High School, Karachi and Bombay Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

GILBERT, CHARLES FREDERICK, Indian State Rlys. (dep. agent, E.I. Rly.) (b. 25th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Feb., 1923; offg. exec. engr., April, 1923; confd., Oct., 1923; offg. senr. supdt., Nov., 1932; offg. dep. agent, E.I. rly., Mar., 1935; confd., Jan., 1936.

GILBERT, LEONARD BURGES, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 20th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. ch. engr., Apl., 1933; offg. consulting engr. to the Govt. of India (roads), July, 1936.

GILBERT, LEONARD ERSKINE, C.I.E., M.D., B.S. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Cantab.), Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Burma Medical Dept.—Arrived 12th May, 1900, and served in mil. dept.; offg. civil surg. Feb., 1903; civil surg. Oct., 1905; offg. surg. to H.E. the Govr. of Madras, Jan. to Dec., 1915; on mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to Aug., 1920; C.I.E., June, 1919; offg. insp.-genl., civil hospitals, March, 1927; ret'd., Jan. 1929.

GILCHRIST, ROBERT NIVEN, C.I.E., M.A., Indian Educl. Service (reforms commr., Bengal) (b. 13th Apl., 1888).—Apptd. prof'r. Presy. Coll., Calcutta, 1911; asst. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, 1914; principal, Krishnagar Coll., 1916; centr., labour bureau, govt. of India, 1921; labour intellige. officer, Bengal, and dep. sec., commerce dept., 1922; jt. secty. to govt., commerce and marine depts., July, 1930; labour commr., Bengal, Mar., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1934; reforms commr., Oct., 1934; author of 'Indian Nationality,' 'Principles of Political Science,' 'Conciliation and Arbitration.'

GILDEA, ERIC PERCIVAL, Indian State Rlys. (divl. rolling stock officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 9th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 22nd Apl., 1916; temp. asst. loco. supt., Sept., 1916-Oct., 1919; confd., Nov., 1919; asst. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1924; offg. variously as divl. rolling stock officer from Jan., 1925; confd., Nov., 1927.

GILES, ALEXANDER GEORGE, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 13th May, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, on 21st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932.

GILL, CLIFFORD ALLCHIN, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., Col. I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 12th Aug., 1878).—First commn., 31st Aug., 1903; in mil. employ till Jan., 1906, when he was appointed asst. plague medical officer, Punjab; chief malaria medical officer, Punjab, Oct., 1913; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to Dec., 1918; asst. dir. of public health, Punjab, Nov., 1921; offg. dir. of public health, Punjab, June, 1924; confd., Nov., 1927; offg. insp.-genl. of civil hosp., May, 1930; ditto, Burma, July, 1932; confd., Feb., 1933; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

GILL, FRANK FREDERICK PERCY, Indian Police (asst. to I.G., Orissa) (b. 2nd Apl. 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 18th Nov., 1925; confd., Sept., 1929; asst. supt. India home intell., Apl., 1931 to Feb., 1933.

GILL, GURDIAL SINGH, M.B., Ch.B., Major, I.M.S., Jail Dept., Madras (supt.) (b. 21st Mar., 1896).—Date of first commn., 28th Aug., 1926; offg. supt., jail dept., Nov., 1931; confd., April, 1932.

GILL, HARRY ANDERSON CLIFFORD, Indian C.S. (dep. secty., fin. dept., Govt. of India) (b. 12th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at Bradfield and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1935; under-secty., finance dept., Govt. of India, Apl., 1937; dep. ditto, Apl., 1938.

GILLAN, GEORGE VAN BAERLE, C.I.E., Lt.-Col. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 3rd Sept., 1890).—First commn. dated 19th Jan., 1912; in milty. employ to 31st Dec., 1918; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., March, 1924; offg. dep. sec. to ditto, Nov., 1924; H.B.M.'s consulgenl., Khasgar, July, 1925; 1st asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Nov., 1928; pol. agent, Gilgit, Sept., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; secty. to A.G.G., Punjab states, Aug. 1935; resdt., W. Rajputana States, Apl., 1937; resdt., Jaipur (addtl. charge), Dec., 1937.

GILLAN, SIR ROBERT WOODBURN, K.C.S.I., B.A., LL.B., Barr. (1889), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Ayr Acad. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1893; offg. city mag. and wasika officer, Lucknow, March, 1894; asst. commr., May, 1896; settlt. offr., Oct., 1896; dep. commr., July, 1899; mag. and collr., Dec., 1901; joint sec., board of rev., April, 1902; sec., board of rev., Jan., 1903; offg. sec. to govt., Sept., 1907; compt. and auditor-gen., and head commr. of paper currency, fin. dept., govt. of India, July, 1910; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1912; member, Royal commn. on Indian finance and currency, April, 1913; member, ry. board, April, 1914; temp. member of govr.-genl.'s council, April to Oct., 1914; presdt., ry. board, April, 1915; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1916; commr., United Provs., April, 1919; ret'd., April, 1920.

GILLESPIE, JAMES ROSS, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 5th Aug., 1914).—Educ. at Dunfermline High Sch., St. Andrews Univ., and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 9th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938 and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

GILLIAT, ALGERNON EARLE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 1st Jan., 1884).—Educ. at Manchester Grammar Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxf.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 7th Dec., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec., rev. sec.'s dept., April, 1913; under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., April, 1916; offg. dep. commr., April, 1921; offg. sec. to govt., fin. and rev. dep., May, 1923; dep. commr., Sept., 1924; ditto and addl. sec., fin. and rev. dept., Dec., 1924; sec. to govt., fin. dept., May, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1928; offg. commr., May, 1932; confd., Jan., 1936.

GILLIES, JOHN ERNEST, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th April, 1889).—Educ. at Old Deer Public School, Aberdeenshire, Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and Aberdeen University; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 9th Dec., 1913, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service, July, 1918, to Feb., 1919; under-sec. to govt. of Burma, July, 1919; offg. dist. and sessions judge, April, 1924; confd., May, 1924; ret'd., Feb., 1931.

GILLIES, PETER, L.D.S. (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 22nd May, 1897).—Apptd. dental surg. and supt., Punjab dental hosp., Lahore on probn., 19th Mar., 1930; confd., Sept., 1932.

GILLIGAN, WILLIAM BOYD, Indian C.S. (backward cl. offer.) (b. 3rd Apl., 1893).—Educ. at High School, Dublin, R.M.C. Sandhurst, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 20th Feb., 1922; arrived, 10th April, 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; offg. collr. and dist. magte., May, 1926; offg. criminal tribes settlement officer, Bombay Presdy., Nov., 1927; Labour officer, Bombay, Oct., 1934; collr. and mag., July, 1935; backward class officer, Nov., 1937.

GILMORE, EDWARD FITZGERALD GELSTON, B.Sc. (Hons.), M.I.Mech.E. (b. 25th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 12th Feb., 1924, as physicist, Govt. Test House, Alipore; supt. Govt. Test House (prov.), Nov., 1934; subst. Mar., 1938.

GIMSON, CHRISTOPHER, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 24th Dec., 1886).—Educ. at Oundle, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 8th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. commr.; transfed. to Assam, April, 1912; settl. offr., July, 1914; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Feb., 1918, to Jan., 1919; presdt., Manipur State Durbar, Feb., 1919, to Nov., 1921; offg. depy. commr., March, 1923; confd., Apl., 1928; pol. agent, Manipur, Feb., 1933.

GITA RAM, GARG, Lala.—Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 30th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 8th Sept., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr. Apl., 1927; confd. Oct., 1928.

GLADDING, DONALD, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., finance, commerce and marine depts., Bengal) (b. 22nd Aug., 1888).—Educ. at St. Olave's Gr. Sch., Southwark, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 10th Dec., 1913, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. duty, May to June, 1918; under-sec., govt. of Bengal, fin. dept., Oct., 1918; ditto, pol. and appt. dept., Dec., 1918; industrial intelligence officer Bengal, July, 1920; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1923; labour intelligence officer, Bengal, Jan., 1925; offg. dep. sec., fin. dept., Nov., 1925; confd., Feb., 1927; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., June, 1928; magte. and collr., Bengal, July, 1928; dep. secty. to govt. of India, home dept., June, 1931; secty. to govt., finance, commerce and marine depts., Bengal, July, 1933; C.I.E., June, 1936.

GLAISBY, ARTHUR GEOFFREY TINDALL, Indian State Ry's. (dep. genl. manager (wks.), G.I.P. Ry.) (b. 12th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service, 12th Sept., 1914; asst. engr., G.I.P. ry., Apl., 1917; resdt. engr., Sept., 1921; liaison officer, G.I.P., ry., Jan., 1934; offg. dep. contr. of stores, Nov., 1935; offg. dist. contr. of stores, Mar., 1936; dep. agent, styled dep. gen. manager (works), May, 1937.

GLANCY, SIR BERTRAND JAMES, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 31st Dec., 1882).—Educ. at Monmouth School and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and pol. asst.; temp. under-sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., March to Nov., 1913; asst. resident, Mewar, May, 1914; 1st asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen. in Rajputana, March, 1915; 1st asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Dec.,

1918; dep. sec. to Govt. of India, pol. dept., Oct., 1921, and again, April, 1927; employed under Kashmir durbar from Nov., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; offg. pol. secty. to govt. of India, June, 1928; offg. agent to gov. genl., Punjab states, Apl., 1929; in foreign service as president, council of state, Jaipur, Oct., 1929; offg. resdt., and A.G.G., Punjab states, Apl., 1932; offg. pol. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., July, 1932, and again, July, 1933; confd., Nov., 1933; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; resdt. and A.G.G. in C.I., June, 1933; member, council of state, variously from July, 1933; secty., Chamber of Princes, Oct., 1934; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1935; secty. to H.E. the Crown Representative, Apl., 1937.

GLANCY, SIR REGINALD ISIDORE ROBERT, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (adviser to the sec. of state for India) (b. 19th Sept., 1874).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to agent to govr.-gen. and chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901, to Sept., 1903; settmt. offr., Bannu, Oct., 1903; pol. asst., Feb., 1906; pol. agent, Feb., 1907; first asst., resdt., Hyderabad, Dec., 1909; asst. minister of fin., H.H. the Nizam's govt., July, 1911; C.I.E., June, 1917; C.S.I., June, 1921; offg. agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, April, 1924; confd., Dec., 1924; K.C.I.E., June, 1928; retd. Apl., 1930; adviser to delegate for Hyderabad, Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930; Jan., 1931; member of council, of India, July, 1931; member, Indian States enquiry comee., Jan., 1932; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1936; adviser to the sec. of state for India, Apl., 1937.

GLASCOTT, JOHN RICHARD DONOVAN, C.I.E., late Indian State Ry's. (b. 10th June, 1877).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 9th Jan., 1904; ditto (perm.), June, 1905; signal engr., Nov., 1908; dist. engr., Mar., 1915; dep. ch. engr., Sept., 1917; ch. engr., Nov., 1919; agent, Mar., 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; retd., Apl., 1934.

GLASS, ERNEST LESLIE, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Oct., 1879).—Trained at T.C.E. Coll., apptd. asst. engr., Punjab, Nov., 1902; on deputn. to Gwalior State, Oct., 1908, to April, 1914; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; transfed. to Central Provs., Dec., 1914, and temply. to Bengal, Dec., 1917; suptg. engr., April, 1920; offg. ch. engr. and secty., irrgn. dept., Jan., 1928; confd., May, 1928; retd., Oct., 1934.

GLASS, FRANK CECIL, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th Oct., 1881).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; asst. engr., Oct., 1925; apptd. to I.S.E. as asst. asst. engr., Jan., 1927; exec. engr., Jan., 1932; retd., Aug., 1934.

GLASS, GEORGE WILLIAM, Indian Audit Dept. (comptroller, p. and t. accts., Burma) (b. 29th Dec., 1887).—Held non-gazetted appt. from Aug., 1906 to Dec., 1918; offg. asst. accts. officer, posts and tels., Feb., 1919; confd., Dec., 1919; dep. acct. genrl., ditto, Aug., 1927; examr., outside audit dept., Bengal, Feb., 1935; dep. acct. genl., Bengal, Apl., 1935; attd. officer, office of the dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Madras, Feb., 1937; on for. service as comp. of posts and tele. accts., Burma, Apl., 1937.

GLASS, HARRY LISTER, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 23rd Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 23rd Sept., 1909; on spl. duty, recruiting, 4th Dec., 1916, to 12th Feb., 1917; exec. engr., Feb., 1918; offg. dep. dir., projects, Rly. Bd., April, 1925; offg. dep. agent, April, 1928; offg. senr. ditto, Mar., 1930; confd., Aug., 1930; offg. divl. supt., Apl., 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1932; ret'd., Feb., 1934.

GLASS, LESLIE CHARLES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 28th May, 1911); educated at Bradfield, Trinity College, Oxford, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Dec., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. warden, Burma oilfields, Sept., 1937.

GLASSON, ALFRED KIRBY, late Indian Forest Service (b. 11th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 7th Dec., 1912; dep. consvr., Dec., 1917; employed under Oxford Imperial Forestry Institute, Dec., 1927, to July, 1929; ch. forest officer, Andamans, Aug., 1931; offg. consvr., Apl. 1933; ret'd., July, 1935.

GLEDDHILL, ALAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 26th Oct., 1895).—Educ. at Rugby School, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1920; arrived, 19th Feb., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; offg. ditto, March, 1928; confd. June, 1928.

GLEESON, HENRY JOSEPH, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 14th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1902; asst. engr. (provl. service), Jan., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; offg. under sec. to govt., P.W.D. (rds. and bldgs.), Jan., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; confd., Jan., 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

GLENN, HENRY JAMES, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 25th Sept., 1874).—Joined the service 4th July, 1897; asst. engr., Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Sept., 1914; awarded Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 1st class, Jan., 1916; ret'd., Sept., 1929.

GLENN, JOHN ARTHUR GOULD, Indian Police (b. 31st July, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provinces, on prob., Dec., 1937.

GLENNIE, EDWARD AUBREY, D.S.O., Lt.-Col., R.E., Survey of India (supt.), (b. 18th July, 1889).—First commn., 23rd July, 1910; asst. supt. (proby.), 20th Dec., 1919; confd., Dec., 1921; supt., May, 1928; offg. dir., Nov., 1936.

GLOVER, HAROLD MATTHEW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Punjab) (b. 29th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 18th Dec., 1908, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., April, 1912; offg. consvr., Jan., 1925; confd., Nov., 1929; served in United Provs., Oct., 1932; posted as consvr., Punjab, June, 1933.

GLOVER, JOHN WILLIAM THOMSON, C.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 30th July, 1887).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1906; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, 10th April, 1914; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir for Leh, July, 1919; pol. agent, Wana, March, 1923; O.B.E., June, 1924; asst. commr., Thal, April, 1926; dep. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1926; pol.

agent, Dir. Swat and Chitral, Oct., 1928; C.B.E., June, 1931; pol. agent, Khyber, Aug., 1931; consul-genl at Kashgar, Nov., 1933; resdt., Kashmir, Oct., 1937.

GOBIND NARAYAN PAREEKH, B.A., Indian Police (b. 10th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 21st Jan., 1920.

GOBIND SARUP MATHUR, BABU, B.A. (b. June, 1878).—Joined the service as offg. munsif, 23rd June, 1906; confd., May, 1908; sub-judge, Dec., 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1928; confd. Jan., 1932; ret'd., June, 1933.

GODBER, ALEXANDER RICHARD, V.D., late Burma Civil Service (b. 18th Apl., 1876).—Held various ministerial appts. from 1st April, 1902; asst. master, Rangoon coll., April, 1905; extra asst. commr., April, 1905; ret'd., Aug., 1929.

GODBOLE, YESHWANT ANANT, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 4th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and at Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 24th Nov., 1913, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., April, 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; confd., Dec., 1928; regr. of co-op. societies, Bihar and Orissa, Mar., 1934; offg. dir. of industries in addn., Mar., 1935.

GODFREY, AUGUSTUS EDWARD, Comdr., R.N.R. (ret'd.).—Marine dept., Madras. Apptd. port officer, Tuticorin, Nov., 1925; ditto, Cochin, Nov., 1927; pers. asst. to presdy. port officer and agent for govt. consignments, April, 1928; ret'd., Dec., 1929.

GODFREY, REGINALD WILLIAM, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 10th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 15th March, 1921; confd., March, 1923; offg. supt., Apl., 1930; confd., June, 1930; pol. officer, Sadiya fron. tract, Apl., 1938.

GODSON, CHARLES AUBREY, M.C., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 15th Nov., 1880).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1906; in milty. employ up to 27th Dec., 1909; offg. civil surg., Jan., 1910; on mil. duty from Aug., 1914, to Jan., 1920; civil surg., Oct., 1921; ret'd., Nov., 1935.

GODWIN, CHARLES, Office of High Commr. for India (executive officer, store dept.) (b. 3rd Sept., 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, store depot, 2nd May, 1904; transfed. to office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922.

GOFF, GEORGE HERBERT, Indian State Railways (dist. traffic supt., E.B. rly.) (b. 25th Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1910; dist. traffic supt., E.B. rly., Mar., 1931.

GOGHARI, DULLABHAJI WAGHJI, L.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 31st May, 1875).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Feb., 1905, to 3rd Aug., 1915; sub-engr., Aug., 1915; asst. engr. (provl. service), Jan., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., Jan., 1926; ret'd., May, 1930.

GOIL, DWARKA PRASAD, M.B., F.R.C.S. (Edin.), K.H.P., Maj.-Genl., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 1st Aug., 1879).—Joined the service, 31st Jan., 1903; arrived, 21st July, 1903; in mil. employ to Apl., 1907; civil surgeon, Dec., 1908; offg.

prin. medl. coll., Calcutta, Mar., 1930; inspgenl. of civil hosp., Punjab, Apl., 1932; surg.-genl. with govt. of Bengal, Dec., 1933; retd., Mar., 1938.

GOKHALE, BHALCHANDRA KRISHNA, M.A., India C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 23rd July, 1892).—Educ. at Bombay and Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived 15th Dec., 1915; and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; ditto and asst. settlmnt. officer, Nov., 1919; offg. dep. commr., April, 1927; secty. to govt., educn. and dev. dept., Mar., 1929; magte. and collr. (provl. sub.), May, 1930; confid., Dec., 1931.

GOKHALE, RAO BAHADUR GANESH HARI, B.A., LL.B., late Central Provs. C.S. (b. July, 1881).—Joined the service as munisf. and Apl., 1906; sub-judge, Nov., 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1933; confid., Apl., 1933; retd., Oct., 1936; member, Jt. public serv. commn., Bihar, C.P., and Orissa, May, 1937.

GOKHALE, VINAYAK GOPAL, L.Ag. (Bom.), late Indian Agric. Service (b. 10th Mar., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Feb., 1905, to 4th Sept., 1915; extra dep. dir. of agric., Sept., 1915; dep. dir. of agric., June, 1924; prof. of agric., agric. coll., Poona, Jan., 1929; acting principl. ditto, Oct., 1932; confid., July, 1933; retd., Mar., 1938.

GOKUL CHAND BADHWAR, B.A., LL.B., Barr. (1898), late Indian C.S. (b. 6th Mar., 1875).—Educ. at the Punjab Univ., and Christ's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 18th Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. sess. judge, Nov., 1905; Jt. mag., Feb., 1910; asst. sess. judge, April, 1912, to Feb., 1913; sess. and sub. judge, March, 1916; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1916; retd., Nov., 1934.

GOLDING, GORDON HENRY ARCHIBALD, late Indian Forest Service (b. 6th Dec., 1887).—Arrived, 28th Dec., 1910, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1915; on mil. service, Feb., 1916, to March, 1920; retd., Dec., 1933.

GOLDNEY, ALFRED PEEL, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th Jan., 1878).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1901; asst. traffic supt., April, 1902; dist. ditto, Aug., 1909; dep. traffic manager, Nov., 1922; offg. ch. traffic manager, March, 1927; confid., Dec., 1929 retd., Jan., 1935.

GOLDSMITH, ARTHUR OLIVER, India Audit Office (higher executive officer) (b. 26th Mar., 1886).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 28th Dec., 1904; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922.

GOLDSWORTHY, FRANK TRUSCOTT, India Office (exec. officer, acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 11th Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Bristol Grammar Sch.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, 25th Feb., 1913; on mil. service from May, 1915, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922.

GOLDSWORTHY, LEONARD JAMES, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 12th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service on 10th Oct., 1914; profrr., Nagpur, Dec., 1914; on mil. service from 23rd Oct., 1917, to 23rd Mar., 1920; inspr. of schools, Central Provs., Mar., 1920; lecturer in chemistry, Rangoon, June, 1923; retd., Feb., 1938.

GOLLAN, DONALD WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 16th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918.

GONSALVES, JOSEPH NICHOLAS, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 7th May, 1914).—Educ. at Montfort Boys' High Sch., Yercand, Madras Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confid., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938 and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

GOOD, JAMES FULLER, M.B., B.Ch., Col., I.M.S. (rettd.), late Burma Medical Dept.—Arrived, 22nd Sept., 1900, and served in mil. dept.; offg. civil surgn., May, 1903; civil surgn., Dec., 1908; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1919; reverted to milty. employ. July, 1928; retd., Aug., 1931.

GOODCHILD, WILLIAM BADEN, Burma Office (asst. princpl.) (b. 19th May, 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, India Office, 22nd Mar., 1920; higher clerical officer, May, 1928; resdt. clerk, Dec., 1928, to July, 1934; asst. secty., Indian delegations to Assembly of League of Nations, 1928 to 1934; asst. secty., Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931; actg. pte. secty. to perm. under-secty. of state, July to Oct., 1935; asst. princpl., Nov., 1935.

GOODERSON, RICHARD NORMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 3rd Mar., 1915).—Educ. at Northampton Town and County Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confid., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

GOODMAN, REGINALD TERENCE WYNNE, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., N.W.F.P.) (b. 26th July, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 20th Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., May, 1929; confid., Oct., 1931.

GOODWYN, HAROLD PETER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 19th July, 1913).—Educ. at Wellington College and Peterhouse, Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

GOPAL, NAND (see Nand Gopal).

GOPAL, RAM (see Ram Gopal).

GOPALASWAMI AYYANGAR, NARASIMHA, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., B.L., Diwan Bahadur, late Madras C.S. (b. 31st Mar., 1882).—Joined the service as dep. collr., 1st Apl., 1905; collr. and dist. magte., Dec., 1920; secty. to bd. of rev., land rev., April, 1923; secty. to govt., public works and lab., Madras; offg. second secty. to govt., Madras, Nov., 1932; 3rd member, bd. of rev., Madras, and offg. 2nd ditto, May, 1935; C.I.E., June, 1935; 2nd secty. to govt., Madras, Mar., 1936; offg. 1st member, bd. of rev., May, 1936; commr. of excise, Madras, Jan., 1937; retd., Mar., 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937; prime minister, Kashmir.

GOPALAKRISHNA RAO, BELTHANGADI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st June, 1909); educ. at Board High School, Puttur, University of Madras and St. Johns College, Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1935.

GOPALAKRISHNAN, PANANGATTI ANANTHANARAYANAIYER, B.A., Indian C.S.
