(jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 20th Dec., 1909).—Educated at Raja's High School, Kollengode, South Malabar, Madras University, University College, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933 and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1935.

GOPALASWAMI, RAMASWAMI AYYANGAR, Indian Civil Service (sub collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 18th Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and magte.; sub collr. and jt. mag., Mar., 1930.

GORDON, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr. and secty. to govt., p.w.d., Sind) (b. 15th June, 1880).—Joined as asst. engr., on 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; supt. engr. (subt. tempy.), Jan., 1927; ditto (prov'l. perm't.), Dec., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935; ch. engr. and secty. to govt. p.w.d., Sind, May, 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

GORDON, ARCHIBALD DOUGLAS, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 14th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 5th Dec., 1907; supt., Oct., 1915; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Nov., 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; dep. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; offg. commr. of police, Calcutta, Apl., 1935.

GORDON, EYRE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (chairman, fed. public services commn.) (b. 28th Feb., 1884).—Educ. at Rossal and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 24th Nov., 1908, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1912, to July, 1915; settl. offr., Jan., 1916; dep. commr. (prov'l.), May, 1921; ditto, and rev. sec. to govt., Cent. Provs., June, 1921; commr. of settlts., dir. of land records, I.G. of reg., regr.-genl. and sec. to govt., April, 1926; offg. commr., Jan., 1929; offg. ch. sec. to govt., Cent. Provs., Dec., 1929; confd., Aug., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, Central Provs., May, 1933; confd., Nov., 1933; C.S.I., June, 1935; chairman, fed. public services commn., Oct., 1936.

GORDON, FRANCIS WALDEN, M.C., M.A., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 10th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 8th Feb., 1921; dep. consvr., Jan., 1925; retd., Oct., 1930.

GORDON, GERALD WATSON, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Assam) (b. 31st Oct., 1902).—Joined the service, 27th Oct., 1925, as asst. exec. engr.; under secty., p.w.d., Assam, June, 1937.

GORDON, JAMES, M.B.E., India Office (exec. officer, military dept.) (b. 27th Dec., 1898).—Apptd. clerical officer, 3rd Oct., 1921; M.B.E., June, 1934; actg. exec. officer, Nov., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935.

GORDON, JOHN DE LA HAY, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 30th Mar., 1887).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1906; in mil. employ till Dec., 1911, when he was apptd. asst. pol. agent

(on probn.); asst. resdt., Aden, April, 1913; mil. covr., Basra, Sept., 1916, to Sept., 1919; O.B.E. June, 1918; pol. agent, Eastern Kathiawar States, Jan., 1925; pol. agent, Mahi Kanthar, Dec., 1927; 1st asst. resdt., Aden, Nov., 1931; pol. agent, eastern Kathiawar agency, Mar., 1932; offg. A.G.G., Deccan States, Aug., 1935; offg. A.G.G., states of W.I., June, 1936; dep. A.G.G., states of W.I., Nov., 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; offg. resdt., Mysore and chief commr., Coorg, Dec., 1937.

GORDON, NORMAN HORATIUS, B.A., late United Provs., C.S. (b. 27th Dec., 1832).—Joined the service, 10th June, 1907; dep. collr., Mar., 1911; offg. mag. and collr., June, 1925; confd., May, 1933; retd., Dec., 1937.

GORDON, ROLAND GRAHAM, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th Oct., 1880).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; compiled admn. report, 1906-07; supt. of land records and registrn., Jan., 1908, to Aug., 1912; talukdari settlt. offr., April, 1915; offg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, April, 1920, and again, April, 1924; confd., Feb., 1925; on spl. duty in connection with talukdari estates, Dec., 1925, and again, Nov., 1926; ditto in rev. dept., March, 1926; pol. agent, Surgana, March, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1932; retd., Jan., 1934; author of a land survey manual.

GORDON, RONALD EAGLESON, M.C., Lt.-Col., R.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. agent (organisation) N.W. Rly.) (b. 17th Jan., 1889).—First commn. dated 23rd Dec., 1900; asst. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., June, 1921; offg. divl. supt. (senr.), N.W. rly., Dec., 1932; and again, Mar., 1935; offg. dep. agent (organisation), Nov., 1936.

GORDON, SIDNEY VICTOR LEOPOLD DE MONTMORENCY, late Indian Police (b. 9th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service, 16th Nov., 1903, and posted to United Provs., asst. supt. of police, Nov., 1906; supt., Dec., 1915; ditto and A.D.C. to lieut.-govr., March, 1916; on mil. duty from Sept., 1917, to Feb., 1919; principal, police training sch., Moradabad, April, 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1929; confd., Apl., 1932; retd., Mar., 1934.

GORRIE, Rev. LESLIE MANIFOLD, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 21st Mar., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 18th April, 1919; junr. chaplain, April, 1921; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1929.

GORRIE, ROBERT MACLAGAN, D.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 26th June, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th Dec., 1922 dep. consvr., Sept., 1930.

GORTON, WILLIAM HUGH, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 27th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; retd., Apl., 1932.

GORWALA, ASTAD DINSHAW, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty. govt. of India, communications dept.) (b. 7th Dec., 1900).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Cambridge Univ. (Non-Coll.); joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 5th Dec., 1924, and served in Bombay as asst. magte. and collr.; offg. collr. and dist. magte., May, 1928; offg. asst. rev. officer, Lloyd barr.

and C.S., Nov., 1931; dep. secty., govt. of India, communications dept., Nov., 1937 and again, July, 1938; on spec. duty, Sind, May, 1938.

GOSCHEN, Viscount, Rt. Hon. GEORGE JOACHIM GOSCHEN, OF HAWKHURST, P.C., G.C.S.I. G.C.I.E., C.B.E.—Educ. at Rugby and Balliol Coll., Oxford; M.P. for East Grinstead, 1895 to 1906; joint parly. sec. Board of Agriculture, 1918; govr. of Madras, 14th April, 1924, to 11th Nov., 1929; actg. govr. genl. of India; G.C.S.I., June, 1929; P.C., Feb., 1930.

GOSS, EDWARD SLADE, M.C., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (b. 25th Sept., 1883).—First commn. dated 29th July, 1911; in military employ up to 31st Dec., 1920, and again from 6th Jan., 1923 to 30th Sept., 1929; offg. asst. dir. genl., I.M.S. (stores), Jan., 1921, and again, Oct., 1929, confd., Nov., 1929; offg. dep. dir. genl., I.M.S., Oct., 1932; officer in charge, medl. store depot, Lahore, Aug., 1933.

GOSS, JAMES SAMUEL, late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 18th Apr., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 25th March, 1912; dist. loco. supt. (prov.), April, 1919; ditto (perm.), March, 1922; asst. agent, N.W. Rly., Oct., 1926; divl. personnel officer; retd., Feb., 1932.

GOSSET, HUGH HENRY EYRE, M.C., Major R.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 3rd Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1912; asst. engr., July, 1913; on milty. duty from 21st Oct., 1914; exec. engr., Jan., 1921; offg. dep. ch. engr., Sept., 1932.

GOSWANI, UPENDRA LAL, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I.) (asst. commr.) (b. 5th Jan., 1912).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ.; joined the service, 24th Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.

GOULD, ALFRED JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (b. 28th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as lecturer in civil engrg., Univ. Coll., Rangoon, 29th Sept., 1924.

GOULD, Basil JOHN, C.M.G., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. Indian Political Service (b. 29th Dec., 1883).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 29th Nov., 1907, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and pol. asst.; asst. to agent to gov.-gen. in Central India, May, 1909; asst. sec. to govt. of India for dept., June, 1910; special duty in for. dept. govt. of India, Sept., 1910; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., Oct., 1910; Br. trade agent, Gyanse, May, 1912; offg. pol. offr., Sikkim, Oct., 1913; asst. commr., Nowshera, Oct., 1914; ditto and asst. commr., Charsada, May, 1915; dist. judge, Peshawar, Sept., 1916; asst. commr., Mardan, May, 1917; asst. pte. sec. to Viceroy, Sept., 1917; on spl. duty under orders of H.B.M.'s Consul, Seistan, from May, 1918; H.B.M.'s Consul for Seistan and Kain, Nov., 1918; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, April, 1920; C.I.E., Sept., 1921; first asst. to resident in Kashmir, Dec., 1925; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, June, 1926; and again, June, 1928; chargé d'affaires, Kabul, March, 1927; C.M.G., June, 1929; pol. agent, Kurram, Novr., 1929; ditto, Dir. Swat and Chitral, Jan., 1931; offg. resdt., Waziristan, Oct., 1931; rev. and judl. commr. in Baluchistan, Oct., 1933; and again, Dec., 1934; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Baluchistan, July, 1934; pol. officer, Sikkim, Dec., 1935.

GOULD, HERBERT ROSS, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th April, 1887).—Educ. at Clifton, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1911, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; on milty. duty, July, 1916, to Jan., 1919; depy. comr., U.S. Frontier, Jan., 1921; offg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, Dec., 1924; member of legisl. council, Aug., 1929; pte. secty. to govr. of Bombay, Aug., 1930; and again, Dec., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1931; retd., Dec., 1936.

GOVINDA MENON, A. V., Rao Bahadur, Barr., late Madras Civil Service (Judicial Branch) (b. 31st Jan., 1882).—Apptd. addl. sess. judge, 10th July, 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1926; retd., Jan., 1937.

GOVINDAN NAYAR, ULLATHIL, B.A., B.L., late Judl. dept., Madras (b. 9th Nov., 1877).—Joined the service as offg. dist. munsif, 6th July, 1907; confd., Feb., 1908; sub-judge, Dec., 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; retd., Nov., 1932.

GOVINDARAJA AYYANGAR, NARASIMHA, Rao Bahadur, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (dep. ch. engr., P.W.D., Madras) (b. 10th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 16th June, 1914; asst. engr., July, 1917; exec. engr., June, 1924; dep. ch. engr., bldgs. and roads, Dec., 1935.

GOVIND DINANATH MADGAONKAR (see Madgaonkar, G. D.).

GOVIND MAHADEV PANDIT (see PANDIT G. M.).

GOW, PETER FLEMING, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (prof. midwifery, Med. Coll., Calcutta) (b. 28th June, 1885).—Joined the service, 27th Jan., 1912, apptd. prof. midwifery, Med. Coll., Calcutta, Oct., 1932.

GOYDER, CECIL WILLIAM, B.Sc., A.C.G.I., All-India Radio (chief engineer) (b. 1st Apr., 1905).—Joined the service as chief engineer, ditto, 20th Aug., 1936.

GRABHAM, REGINALD GEORGE DENIS, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 11th May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 23rd Dec., 1922; offg. supt., March, 1926; asst. commr., Port Blair, Dec., 1930; supt., May, 1935; addtl. asst. to I.G., Feb., 1937.

GRACE, Oliver Gilbert, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 10th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1920; asst. supt. Feb., 1922; supt., June, 1925; dist. officer, frontier constabulary, N.W.F.P., Jan., 1929; O.B.E., Jan., 1934.

GRACIE, CLEMENT FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 29th June, 1907).—Educ. at King Edward VII School, Sheffield, and Queen's College, Oxford; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; secty. to finl. commr., Burma, July, 1936 to Dec., 1936.

GRAHAM, CYRIL GEORGE, Indian State Railways (exec. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 28th May, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1909; resdt. engr., G.I.P. rly., Nov., 1914; exec. engr., May, 1932; offg. dep. agent, G.I.P. rly., Apr., 1935; offg. dep. ch. engr., maintenance, G.I.P. rly., May, 1936; and again, Sept., 1937.

GRAHAM, DAVID LIVINGSTONE, O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 1st Sept., 1882).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1907; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; in milty. employ to 26th Nov., 1921; offg. civil surg., 27th Nov., 1921; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

GRAHAM, FREDERICK ERNEST, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 29th Apl., 1892).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 8th Sept., 1914; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Sept., 1919; transf'd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, April, 1926.

GRAHAM, GEORGE KENNETH, Capt., I.M.S. (b. 30th Apl., 1907).—First commn., 3rd Feb., 1931; in mil. employ to 4th Nov., 1934; agency surgn., N. Waziristan, Nov., 1934; medl. officer, Lady Reading Hosp., Peshawar, Mar., 1938.

GRAHAM, HAROLD, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., educn. dept., Bengal) (b. 26th April, 1889).—Educ. at Manchester Cr. Sch. and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived in Bengal, 10th Dec., 1913, and served in Bengal as an asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., June, 1915, to Jan., 1919; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1922; confd., Nov., 1926; pte. sec. to govr. of Bengal, April, 1927; offg. commr., Sept., 1931, and again, Jan., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; secty. to govt., educn. dept., Bengal, Mar., 1936.

GRAHAM, HAROLD JOHN, O.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transportation supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 1st June, 1886).—Joined the service 30th June, 1905; asst. traffic supt., April, 1907; dist. ditto, Sept., 1911; in milty. employ from 4th Oct., 1914, to 14th Feb., 1920; supt. of claims, Nov., 1922; pers. asst. to ch. transportation supt., Oct., 1923; offg. dep. agent, March, 1926; adviser to Indian delegation to the internatl. labour conference, Geneva, May-June, 1928; divl. transportation supt., Nov., 1928.

GRAHAM, HUBERT, Indian C.S. (magte. and collr., Bengal) (b. 7th Nov., 1890).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 18th Dec., 1914, and served in Bengal as an asst. mag. and collr.; offg. addl. under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, political dept., April, 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1923; confd., Mar., 1931; asst. commr., inc. tax, Bengal, Jan., 1927; commr. of inc. tax, B. & O. (temp.), Mar., 1929.

GRAHAM, SIR JAMES DRUMMOND, Kt., C.B., C.I.E., M.B., Major-Genl., I.M.S. ret'd. (b. 21st Jan., 1875).—First commn., 28th June, 1900; in mil. employ till April, 1908; offg. civil surgeon, United Provs., April, 1908; civil surgeon, June, 1911; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to May, 1920; C.I.E., March, 1919; offg. public health commr. to govt. of India, Feb., 1924; confd., Sept., 1924; C.B., June, 1931; rep. of Govt. of India on Imp. council of agricl. research, Mar., 1933; ret'd., Feb., 1934; knighted, June, 1934.

GRAHAM, JOHN FULLER, late Indian C.S. (b. 4th Nov., 1872).—Educ. at Bruton Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 6th Dec., 1896, and

served in Assam as asst. commr., and subdiv. offr.; asst. mag. and collr., Bengal, Jan., 1902; joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam, Nov., 1906; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1909; transf'd. to Assam, April, 1912; re-transferred to Bengal, Feb., 1919; member of Bengal legisl. council, April, 1919; sec. to govt. of Bengal, legisl. dept., Nov., 1919; actg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, March to Aug., 1924, and from May, 1925; addl. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Feb., 1926; actg. judge, ditto, March to Aug., 1926; confd., March, 1927; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

GRAHAM, SIR LANCELOT, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (govr. of Sind) (b. 18th April, 1880).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and pol. agent; asst. judge and sess. judge, from Jan., 1909; asst. remembr., legal affairs, and sec. for making laws and regns., March, 1911; judge and pol. agent and judl. asst. in Kathiawar from Aug., 1913; addl. jt. sec. to govt. of India, legislv. dept., Nov., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; actg. sec. to govt. of India, legislv. dept., April, 1923; confd., Dec., 1924; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1930; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1936; govr. of Sind, Apl., 1936.

GRAHAM-SMITH, CYRIL, O.B.E., A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 2nd June, 1882).—Apptd. prof. of engrg., coll. of engrg., Poona, 10th Jan., 1916; prinpl. and prof. ditto, Nov., 1927; O.B.E., June, 1919; ret'd., Apl., 1938.

GRANT, CHARLES FREDERICK, C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th Sept., 1878).—Educ. at Dundee High Sch. and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., April, 1915; on mil. duty from May, 1915, to Oct., 1919; chief sec. to govt. of Burma, Aug., 1922; commr. (s.p.t.), May, 1924; confd., April, 1926; offg. finl. commr., Jan., 1928; chairman, dev. trust, Rangoon, Jan., 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937; chairman, public services commission, Burma, April, 1937.

GRANT, CHARLES WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 6th Feb., 1879).—Educ. at Dulwich and Exeter Coll., Oxford, apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 1st Dec., 1902, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1913; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Oct., 1913, to Oct., 1915; mag. and collr., April, 1920; dep. commr. Nov., 1921; mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; offg. commr., June, 1928; confd., Dec., 1931; offg. member, bd. of rev., United Provs., May, 1935; presdt., bd. of public health, United Provs., May, 1935; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

GRANT, JOHN LESLIE, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 12th Oct., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1913; in milty. employ from 1st Aug., 1916, to 18th March, 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922.

GRANT, JOHN WILLIAM, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agricl. Service (b. 18th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, on 14th Nov., 1922.

GRANT, LACHLAN COLVILLE PATRICK MAC-ALPINE, O.B.E., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (asst. dep. dir. genl. [engrg.]) (b. 12th Aug., 1902).—Arrived in India, 27th Mar., 1925; joined the service as asst. div. engr., 30th Mar., 1925; div. engr., Sept., 1927; p.a. to chief engr., July, 1935; asst. dep. dir. genl. (engrg.), June, 1938.

GRANTHAM, HENRY DONALD, late Indian Police Service.—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 8th Dec., 1907; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1920; dep. commr. of police, Rangoon, Nov., 1925; offg. commr. of police, Rangoon, March, 1926; ret'd., Mar., 1929.

GRANTHAM, STANLEY GEORGE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th Aug., 1882).—Educ. at City of London School, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and as settl. offr.; asst. sec. to finl. commr., May, 1911, to July, 1913; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; supt., census operations, Burma, May, 1920, to June, 1921; dir. of statistics and labour commr., Burma, June, 1931; ret'd., Feb., 1937.

GRASSBY, CYRIL GEORGE, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 15th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service on 29th Aug., 1921; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., Aug., 1931; awarded King's Police Medal, 1934.

GRAY, ALFRED GORDON, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 3rd Sept., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th Sept., 1904; resdt. engr., March, 1909; dist. engr., Jan., 1921; dep. ch. engr., July, 1926; ret'd., Dec., 1934.

GRAY, WILLIAM COWAN, Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 4th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1905; civil surg., Cochin, Dec., 1923; offg. profr., medl. coll., Madras, July, 1924; offg. D.M.O., Madras, Jan., 1927; confd., June, 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

GREAVES, SIR WILLIAM EWART, Kt., Barr.—Apptd. temp. addl. judge, high court, Calcutta, 23rd Nov., 1914; judge, ditto, March, 1916; Kt., Jan., 1924; ret'd., Dec., 1927.

GREEN, SIR ALAN MICHAEL, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Apl., 1885).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 19th Nov., 1909; and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; compiled general administration report for 1912-13 and 1913-14; asst. censor, Poona and Bombay, Oct., 1914, to March, 1915; offg. asst. collr. of customs, Bombay, April, 1915; confd. as asst. collr. of customs, Oct., 1917; collr. of customs, Madras, Nov., 1921; collr. impl. customs service and collr. of customs, Bombay, Dec., 1922; actg. trade commr., London, Mar.-Nov., 1929; secty. to high commr. for India, Nov., 1929; dep. high commr. for India, Sept., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1933; knighted, June, 1935; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

GREEN, ALFRADE DALE, V.D., late Indian State Railways (N.W. Rly.) (b. 13th Nov., 1874).—Joined the service, 8th May, 1895; asst. traffic supt., June, 1907; dist. traffic supt., April, 1913; on mil. duty from May, 1917, to May, 1918; offg. dep. chief comml. manager, April, 1927; dep. ch. operating supt. (pro.l.), Oct., 1927; on spl. duty, rly. bd., Jan., 1928; ret'd., Nov., 1929.

GREEN, ARTHUR EATOUGH, O.B.E., M.C., M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 16th Dec., 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; under sec., public works dept., Bihar and Orissa, Jan., 1926; O.B.E., May, 1937.

GREEN, CHARLES WILLIAM TANDY, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 21st May, 1887).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1909, as asst. engr.; I.A.R.O. from April, 1915, to Dec., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1926; pers. asst. to ch. engr. and secty., P.W. dept., March, 1928; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1929; confd., Dec., 1931.

GREEN, EDWARD WASTIE, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 10th Nov., 1877).—Apptd. addl. profr. of hist. presy., coll., Madras, 5th Feb., 1910; inspr. of schools, Jan., 1918; princpl. govt. coll. for Muhammadans, June, 1921; ret'd., Nov., 1932.

GREEN, HARRY DALE, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th July, 1876).—Joined the service, 9th Aug., 1899, as asst. engr., N.W. Rly.; exec. engr., March, 1908; suptg. engr., March, 1924; offg. chief engr., June, 1925; confd., Jan., 1928; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of rlys., Mar., 1929; ret'd., July, 1931.

GREEN, JOHN MOWBRAY, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 23rd Apl., 1897).—Joined the Service, 31st Jan., 1921; asst. supt., Sept., 1922; offg. supt., Apl., 1925; confd., Oct., 1930; comdt. of police, Aden, May, 1934; reverted, Oct., 1934.

GREEN, LESLIE BENTON, M.B.E., M.I.E. Ind.), Dept. of Industries, Madras (dir. of industries) (b. 11th June, 1892).—Apptd. offg. asst. dir., 16th Dec., 1915; M.B.E., Dec., 1919; dep. dir., Feb., 1922; dir., June, 1935.

GREEN, THOMAS CROTHERS, late Indian Police (b. 26th June, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt. Bihar and Orissa, on 22nd Nov., 1923; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

GREEN - ARMYTAGE, VIVIAN BARTLEY, M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Bengal Civil Medical Dept. (b. 14th Aug., 1882).—Joined the service, 27th July, 1907; in mil. employ to May, 1911; officiated as civil surg., Jalpaigari, from June, 1911, and as resdt. surg., Calcutta, from Aug., 1911; profr., Medical Coll., Calcutta, March, 1921; ret'd., July, 1935.

GREENE, HUBERT EUGENE, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 10th June, 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 27th Feb., 1901; on field service with Mesopotamia expedy. force; on foreign service with Iraq admin. from 1st Nov., 1919, to 23rd Mar., 1921; asst. supt., Aug., 1924; supt., Feb., 1926; dep. P.M.G., traffic, Bengal and Assam circle, Nov., 1930; ch. supt., Madras tel. office, Jan., 1932; ditto, Bombay, May, 1934; ret'd. June, 1938.

GREENEP, DONALD PRESTWICH, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 20th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asss. consvr., Burma, on 22nd Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1933.

GREENFIELD, HARRY, C.I.E., Impl. Customs Service (collr. of salt rev., Madras) (b. 2nd Oct., 1898).—Joined the Indian Police service as assist supt. on 27th Dec., 1919; dep.

commr., Calcutta, Oct., 1925; asst. collr. impl. customs service, Bombay, 1927; ditto, Calcutta, Feb., 1930; collr. of salt rev., Madras, Nov., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

GREENFIELD, HENRY CHALLEN, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Central Provs.) (b. 8th Dec., 1885).—Educ. at Lancing Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., July to Sept., 1913; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, May, 1915; commr. (temp.), ditto, Dec., 1916, to Jan., 1917; 2nd asst. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, July, 1918; dep. commr. (provl.), Feb., 1921; confd., Aug., 1923; rev. sec. to govt., April, 1927, and again, July, 1928; secty. in settlts. and land records, etc., depts., Jan., 1929; commr. of settlements and dir., land records, Mar., 1933; offg. commr., May, 1933; confd. (provl.), July, 1934; subst., May, 1936; C.I.E., June, 1934; temp. member, gov.'s exec. council, Central Provs., during Mar., 1937.

GREENING, LAURENCE EDWIN, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 19th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., May, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; dep. secty. to govt., P.W.D. (roads and bldgs.), Bombay, Mar., 1934.

GREENWAY, CHARLES MERVYN, late Indian State Rlys (b. 19th Apr., 1886).—Joined the service as 1st asst. supt. of stores, 30th Apr. 1913; dep. supt. of stores, June, 1921; supt. of stores, Oct., 1925; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

GREENWOOD, DAVID ABERNEATHY, Jail Dept., Madras (sup't.) (b. 13th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service 14th Nov., 1919; supt. central jail, Vizagapatam, 1st Nov., 1920; awarded Kaisar-i-Hind medal (2nd class), Jan., 1924; offg. supt., Alipuram jail, Bellary, variously from Dec., 1924.

GREENWOOD, EDWARD JOSEPH BERNAL M.Sc., M.I.E.E., M.I.E. (Ind.) (dep. chief engr. elec.), Electricity Dept., (Madras) (b. 16th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as elec. inspr. to Govt., 25th Feb., 1913; elec. inspr., Jan., 1933; deputy chief engr. for electricity, Apr., 1937.

GREG, LIONEL HYDE, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 19th Jan., 1879).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1900; exec. engr., Oct., 1908; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1926; confd., June, 1927; offg. ch. engr. (irrign.), Aug., 1928; confd., July, 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

GREGORY, ALBERT EDWARD, India Office (hr. clerical officer Acct. Genl.'s dept.) (b. 12th Feb., 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, 16th Apr., 1920; actg. higher ditto, July, 1935; confd., Aug., 1936.

GREGORY, JOHN ROGER, late Indian Police Service (b. 2nd Feb., 1893).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1912; on milty. duty from May, 1915, to March, 1921; supt. of police, July, 1924; ret'd., July, 1932.

GREGORY, MARCAR SHERIDAN, O.B.E., M.C., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 23rd June, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; I.A.R.O., 14th March, 1915; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. agent, N.W. Rly., Jan., 1926; confd., Dec., 1928; on spl. duty on E.B. Rly., Oct.,

1929; ditto with rly. bd., May, 1930; ch. personnel officer, N.W. Rly., Apr., 1931; O.B.E., June, 1932; ret'd., May, 1935.

GREGORY, RALPH, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 11th Dec., 1908).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London, and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Jan., 1935.

GREGORY, THEODOR EMANUEL GUGENHEIM, D.Sc. (b. 10th Sept., 1890).—Apptd. econ. adviser to the Govt. of India, Jan., 1938.

GREGORY, WALTER GASPER, Barr., Judl. Dept., Bengal.—Acted as addl. judge, high ct., from 27th March, 1925, to 27th Aug., 1925, and from 9th Nov., 1925, to 11th March, 1926; offg. judge, high ct., March to Aug., 1926, Nov., 1926, to Aug., 1927, and again from Nov., 1927.

GREGORY MOSES, RALPH, Indian C.S.—Name changed to GREGORY, RALPH (q.v.).

GREGSON, EDWARD GELSON, C.M.G., C.I.E., Barr., late Indian Police Service (b. 10th June, 1877).—Joined the service, 14th Jan., 1899; supt. of police, N.W.F. Prov., Nov., 1906; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; spl. duty in connection with suppression of illicit arms traffic, Persian Gulf, Sept., 1914; commr. of police, Basra, April, 1916; C.M.G., June, 1917; pol. offr., Bushire Fd. Force, Oct., 1918, to April, 1919; reverted to police duties as supt., Punjab, May, 1919; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., March, 1925; confd., April, 1926; ret'd., May, 1931.

GREGSON, WILLIAM, N.D.A., Indian Agricl. Service (b. 28th July, 1892).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, on 3rd April, 1923.

GREG, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., B.A., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.).—First commn., 19th April, 1902; supt., central jail, Montgomery, Oct., 1913; ditto, Lahore, Dec., 1921; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Punjab, Feb. to April, 1924, and from Oct., 1924; ret'd., Jan., 1929.

GREWAL, BALBIR SINGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 28th Aug., 1912).—Educated at Government High School, Ludhiana, the School of Oriental Studies, London, and Pembroke College, Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Dec., 1936, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

GRIERSON, Sir GEORGE ABRAHAM, O.M., K.C.I.E., Ph.D. (Halle), Litt. D. (T.C.D.); LL.D. (Cantab.), D. Litt. (Oxon.), late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at St. Bees, Shrewsbury, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 25th Oct., 1873; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and small cause court judge; on famine duty, 1874; on special duty in the rev. dept., Dec., 1882; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1884; mag. and collr., March, 1890; opium agent, Bihar, Oct., 1896; on special duty with Linguistic Survey of India, May, 1898; ret'd., Sept., 1903; C.I.E., May, 1894; hon. Ph.D. (Halle), Aug., 1894; Litt. D., Dub. 1902; hon. member, Asiatic Soc. of Bengal, Nagari Pracharini Sabha, Benares, American Oriental Soc., Société Finno-Ougrienne, Helsingfors; for assoc. member, Société Asiatique de Paris

corres. member, Königlische Gesellschaft der Wissenschaften zu Gottingen; awarded Prix Volney (1905) by the Institute de France; was vice-presdt., R. Asiatic Soc.; R. Asiatic Soc.'s gold medal (1909); K.C.I.E., June, 1912; O.M., June, 1928; now hon. sec., R. Asiatic Soc.; has publ. various works in connection with the Behari language and its dialects, translns., collections of folk songs, &c.; also "Curiosities of Indian Literature," 1885; "Vidyapati and his Contemporaries," 1885; "Modern Vernacular Literature of Hindustan," 1890; and "Inscriptions of Piyadasi," 1891.

GRIEVE, ROBERT GEORGE, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educ. Service (b. 18th Oct., 1881).—Apptd. vice-prinpl., Teachers' Coll., Saidapet, 31st Oct., 1906; inspr. of schools, Madras, Jan., 1910; dep. dir. of public instrn., Madras, Aug., 1912; princlpl., Teachers' Coll., July, 1920; offg. dir. of pub. instrn. variously from Aug., 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

GRIEVE, WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian Educ. Service (dir. of public instrn., Bombay) (b. 21st Mar., 1885).—Joined the service, 9th Nov., 1914; educ. inspr. in Sind, July, 1923; offg. inspr. of European schools, Bombay Presyd., April, 1924; offg. dep. dir. of public instrn., April, 1926; confd., Sept., 1929; offg. dir., ditto, Feb., 1932; confd., Aug., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1938.

GRIFFE-WILLIAMS, JOHN HENRY, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 11th Aug., 1881).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined dept., 23rd Nov., 1901; asst. acct.-genl., post office and tels., Mar., 1911; dep. acct.-genl., ditto, Feb., 1913; examr. of accts., Oudh and Rohilkhand Rly., Dec., 1915; dep. acct.-gen., Rangoon, Apr., 1920; ditto, Madras, March, 1924; ditto, Bombay, April, 1926; ditto, Burma, Nov., 1928; offg. dep. acct.-genl., Punjab (works), June, 1929; ret'd., Sept., 1934.

GRIFFIN, ARTHUR CECIL, O.B.E., B.Sc. (secty. rly. bd.) (b. 30th Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1911; on mil. duty from 5th Nov., 1914; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. dep. agent, Apl., 1933; divl. supt. (sen.), Nov., 1934; dep. chief engr. (prov. perm.), Feb., 1938; secty. rly. bd., May, 1938.

GRIFFIN, FREDERICK CHARLES, M.I.C.E., Engrg. Branch, Public Health Dept., Bengal (ch. engr.) (b. 20th Feb., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. sany. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. ch. engr., Nov., 1924, and again, Jan., 1927; confd., Apl., 1927.

GRIFFIN, LANCELOT CECIL LEPEL, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 5th Jan., 1900).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923; arrived, 17th Dec., 1923, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Naini Tal, March, 1925; jt. mag., Nov., 1925; under-secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in C.I., Nov., 1927; asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy, Apl., 1929; secty. to A.G.G., Punjabstates, Mar., 1932; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Apl., 1936; addl. dep. secty., pol. dept., Apl., 1937.

GRIFFITH, ARTHUR LAWRENCE, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 24th May, 1903).—Joined the service, 17th Dec., 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930.

GRIFFITH, SIR FRANCIS CHARLES, Kt., C.S.I., O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 9th Nov., 1878).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1898; supt. of police, Bombay, June, 1906; ditto, and asst. to inspr.-genr., Bombay, Sept., 1909; ditto, and dep. commr. of police, C.I.D., Bombay, March, 1913; recd. King's police medal, Jan., 1916; O.B.E., June, 1919; offg. commr. of police, Bombay, Feb., 1919; confd., Nov., 1919; inspr.-genl., Sept., 1922; C.S.I., Jan., 1923; Kt., Jan., 1931; ret'd., July, 1933.

GRIFFITH, FRANCIS GORDON, O.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 23rd Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 1st April, 1913; on spl. duty, rly. bd., March, 1920; asst. dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; in milty. employ from July, 1924, to March, 1926; O.B.E., June, 1926; transportation officer, Aug., 1926; statistical officer, Apl., 1929.

GRIFFITH, SIR RALPH EDWIN HOTCHKIN, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., Lt.-Col. I.A., ret'd. (b. 4th Mar., 1882).—In mil. employ from 8th May, 1901, to 18th Nov., 1908, when apptd. to N.W. Frontier Prov. as supg. asst. commr.; asst. pol. agent, May, 1909; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, May to Sept., 1909; asst.-commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Sept., 1909; city mag., Peshawar, Jan., 1911; pol. asst., and ditto, April, 1911; asst. commr., Peshawar, Oct., 1911; ditto, Bannu, March, 1914; dep. commr., Bannu, June, 1914; C.I.E., Jan., 1917; asst. commr., Charsadda, March, 1917; ditto, Mardan, Jan., 1919; pol. agent, Tochi, May, 1920; dep. commr., Peshawar, March, 1924; offg. resdt. Waziristan, June, 1929; offg. C.C. and A.G.G., N.W.F.P., Sept., 1931; Kt., Jan., 1932; K.C.S.I., Apl., 1932; govr., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

GRIFFITH, WILLIAM EDWARD, M.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Educ. Service (Bengal) (b. 13th Sept., 1874).—Joined the service, 21st Jan., 1907; prin., David Hare training coll., Feb., 1911; addl. dir. of public instrn., Nov., 1925; ret'd., July, 1931.

GRIFFITH, WILLIAM MAURICE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 7th May, 1875).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 3rd Jan., 1903; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1927; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

GRIFFITHS, CHRISTOPHER HOWARD, Indian State Railways (signal engr., E. B. rly.) (b. 11th Nov., 1883).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1922 as asst. signal engr., N.W. rly.; dist. signal engr., June, 1931; offg. signal engr., E.B. rly., Aug., 1935; confd., May, 1936.

GRIFFITHS, DAVID THOMAS, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 10th Oct., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 1st Jan., 1923; offg. dep. consvr., Aug., 1924; confd., Feb., 1927.

GRIFFITHS, HARRY MELVILLE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 16th July, 1881).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1903; exec. engr., April, 1912; on mil. service, April, 1917, to Dec., 1919; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1920; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

GRIFFITHS, JOHN COLDWELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 30th Apr., 1913).—Educ. at Swansea Grammar School and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1937; arrived in India Nov., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

GRIFFITHS, PERCIVAL JOSEPH, B.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Lond.), late Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Bengal) (b. 15th Jan., 1899).—Educ. at Cambridge (Peterhouse); joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. magte. and collr., May, 1928; and again, Sept., 1933; chief manager, Dacca nawab estate, Mar., 1929; mag. and collr., Apr., 1936; offg. dep. commr., May, 1936; confd., May, 1936; retd. Aug. 1937.

GRIFFITHS, WILLIAM THOMAS, Indian State Rlys. (dep. agent (staff) G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 20th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 1st May, 1916; 3rd asst. secty., May, 1921; offg. publicity supt., Apr., 1923; asst. traffic manager, Oct., 1923; divl. ditto, Feb., 1929; offg. dep. agent, G.I.P. rly., July, 1933; confd., Dec., 1935.

GRIGG, Sir PERCY JAMES, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., (member, govr. genl.'s exec. council) (b. 16th Dec., 1890).—Educ. at Bournemouth Sch. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. to Treasury, 1913; served R.G.A., 1915-1918; prin. pte. secty. to successive chancellors of the exchequer, 1921-1930; Chairman, Bd. of Customs and Excise, Nov., 1930; member, govr. genl.'s exec. council, May, 1934; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1936.

GRIGSON, WILFRED VERNON, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 11th Oct., 1896).—Educ. at St. John's Sch., Leatherhead, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925; and again, March, 1931; confd., Apr., 1934; on foreign service as adminr., Bastar State, March, 1927.

GRILLE, Sir FREDERICK LOUIS, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Nagpur) (b. 20th Feb., 1889).—Educ. at Harrow, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1913, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; serving under govt. of India, army dept., from Oct., 1917, to Oct., 1919; sub-judge, Central Provs., Oct., 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1920; confd., Dec., 1926; offg. legal remembr. and secty. to govt., legal dept., Oct., 1927; offg. addl. judl. commr., July, 1931; confd., March, 1934; offg. judl. commr., June, 1933; confd., Jan., 1935; judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936; knighted, May, 1937.

GRIMES, Ven. CECIL JOHN, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 6th Jan., 1881).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1915; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1918; offg. archdeacon of Calcutta, Dec., 1926; confd., July, 1928; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1928; retd., Jan., 1934.

GRINDAL, ALAN DOUGLAS, late Punjab Civil Service (b. 14th Dec., 1881).—Joined the service, 16th Dec., 1899, and held non-gazetted appts. to 6th Dec., 1913; extra asst. commr., Jan., 1915; asst. regr., ch. ct., Lahore, Apr., 1918; asst. secty. to finl. commrs., Punjab, Dec., 1920; offg. under-secty. to govt., finl.

dept., May, 1922; dep. secty., ditto, Apr., 1923; offg. secty., ditto, Apr., 1934; and again, Aug., 1936; retd., Dec., 1936.

GRINDAL, ERIC BERTRAM WOOD, Indian Finance Dept. (b. 25th Oct., 1885).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 30th Mar., 1904; senior acct., Apr., 1920; accts. officer, central accts. office, July, 1929; dep. acct.-genl., central revs., Apr., 1932; offg. acct.-genl., ditto, Aug., 1934; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Mar., 1936; offg. comptr., N.W.F.P., July, 1936; audit officer, Indian Stores dept., Sept., 1936.

GRINDLEY, ARCHIBALD PERCIVAL REGINALD, Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. Sept., 1892).—Asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1914; divl. transpn. officer, May, 1935.

GRIST, FREDERIC EDWIN, C.I.E., India Office (asst. secty., finl. dept.) (b. 9th Nov., 1883).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 23rd Jan., 1902; attached to mil. dept., May to Oct., 1907; transf. to mil. dept., Nov., 1912; minor staff clerk, March, 1917; staff clerk, June, 1919; principal, Oct., 1921; actg. asst. secty., July, 1931; secty., capitation rate tribunal, Nov., 1932; asst. secty., finl. dept., Dec., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1938.

GROSE, FRANK SAMUEL, C.B.E., late Burma Frontier Service (b. 15th Oct., 1881).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 7th Dec., 1924; extra asst. commr., Sept., 1906; asst. supt. (B.F.S.), Dec., 1922; dep. commr., June, 1927; offg. commr., Mar., 1933; retd., Mar., 1938; C.B.E., June, 1938.

GROSSETT, GEORGE ANGUS, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th Feb., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1903; exec. engr., Nov., 1911; retd., Feb., 1930.

GRUER, HAROLD GEORGE, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Nagpur) (b. 6th May, 1886).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, Aberdeen Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; addl. sr. sub-judge, July to Oct., 1915; sub-judge, June, 1917; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), Nov., 1917, to March, 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1923; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. addl. judl. commr., June, 1934; and again, Sept., 1935; judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936.

GRUNDY, ERIC EDMUND LEIGH, Indian State Rlys. (b. 6th Apr., 1888).—Joined the service as princl., technical school, Jamalpur, 29th Sept., 1924.

GUBBAY, HENRY ABRAHAM, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 26th Sept., 1883).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1904; R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1904; asst. sany. engr., Bengal, Oct., 1908, to Jan., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; temp. supt. engr., April, 1922; supt. engr., Aug., 1924; offg. ch. engr. and sec. to govt., roads and bldgs. branch, April, 1926; confd., July, 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; retd., Mar., 1936.

GUBBAY, MOSES MORDECAI SIMEON, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Clifton, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 18th Dec., 1899, and served in

Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. collr. of customs; compiled the gen. admn. report, 1901-02; sec. stores committee, 1906; offg. under sec. to govt. of India dept. of commerce and industry, June, 1906; confd., 1908, to Feb., 1910; coll. of customs, Bombay, Oct., 1910; on spl. duty in England as Indian trade commr. from Sept., 1914, to March, 1915; on spl. duty under govt. of India as wheat commr., April, 1915; dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Oct., 1915; C.I.E., Jan., 1916; offg. controller of currency, July, 1916; confirmed, May, 1917; C.S.I., June, 1918; on deptn. as foodstuffs commr. with govt. of India, and jt. sec., dept. of rev. and agri., Nov., 1918, to April, 1919; member, commee. on Indian exchange and currency, 1919; sec. to govt. of India, finance dept., May, 1920; ret'd., Apl., 1921; apptd. commr. for I.C.S. Family Pension Fund, Oct., 1937.

GUGGALI, GURUSIDDAPPA HOOVAPPA, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 18th Oct., 1897).—Educ. at Fergusson Coll., Poona, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; confd., Oct., 1933.

GUHA, BRAJA KANTA, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 3rd July, 1900).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty. to govt. of Bengal, fin. commerce and marine depts., Mar., 1929; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1931; K-i-H. medal, 1st class, Feb., 1937.

GUHA, SURENDRA KUMAR, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 4th May, 1894).—Joined the service, 7th Jan., 1920; asst. exec. engr., April, 1923; exec. engr., Jan., 1930.

GUHA, SURENDRA NATH, RAI BAHADUR, M.A., B.L., late Bengal Judicial dept. (b. 11th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service as senr. govt. pleader, 1st Dec., 1927; addl. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Nov., 1929; judge, ditto, Nov., 1933; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

GUHA RAY, SAILENDRA NATH, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Nov., 1899).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1930.

GUL MUHAMMAD KHAN, B.A., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 8th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., on 30th Jan., 1923; offg. supt., Jan., 1931; confd., June, 1934.

GULAM HUSSAIN RAHIMTULLA KHAIRAZ (see Khairaz, G.H.R.).

GUMBLETON, JOHN GILLUM MAXWELL, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 13th Sept., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on 17th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; confd., Jan., 1934; dep. inspr.-genl., states of W.I., Oct., 1936.

GUMBRILL, ARTHUR, Indian State Rlys. (publicity officer, E.I. Rly.) (b. 15th June, 1887).—Joined the service as publicity officer (temp.) 15th Mar., 1927; confd., Dec., 1928; offg. central publicity officer, Indian State rlys., Apl., 1933, and again, Apl., 1937.

GUNDEVIA, YEZDEZARD DINSHAW, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 19th June, 1908).—Educ. at Bombay University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settlement officer, Oct., 1936.

GUNDIL, NARAYAN RAMKRISHNA, B.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., Bombay (asst. judge) (b. 22nd Feb., 1885).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1913; asst. judge, Dec., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge variously from Dec., 1931.

GUNDYR, ARTHUR ROBERT, late Indian State Railways (b. 15th Nov., 1881).—Served in Calcutta Mint from May, 1907, to May, 1910; joined rly. dept. as elec. engr., E. Ben. Rly., 16th May, 1910; dep. loco. supt. (electrical), Nov., 1926; ch. electrical engr., Jan., 1928; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

GUNJIKAR, KASHINATH RAMCHANDRA, M.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Bom.), Indian Educl. Service (prof., R.I. of Science, Bombay) (b. 8th Mar., 1894).—Joined the service as prof. of maths., Elph. Coll., Bombay, 7th June, 1925; ditto, R.I. of Science, Bombay, Nov., 1926.

GUNN, JOHN PATERSON, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 31st Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1913; I.A.R.O., from 7th Sept., 1916, to 11th April, 1920; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; pers. asst. to ch. engr., Nov., 1927; dir., irrign. research inst., Lahore, in addn., June, 1935, to Nov., 1936.

GUNNING, CARDWELL SINCLAIR, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 28th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; acting under-sec. to chief commr., Sept., 1916; confirmed, Dec., 1916; on mil. duty, I.A.R.O., Oct., 1918, to Jan., 1919; offg. dep. commr., May, 1923; confd., Sept., 1928.

GUPTA, AJYOJ KUMAR, B.A., Indian State Railways (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 30th Apl., 1894).—Asst. traffic supt., 17th Nov., 1915; dist. ditto, Dec., 1936.

GUPTA, ARUN CHANDRA, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 30th Oct., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bengal, 3rd Dec., 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930.

GUPTA, ASHOK CHANDRA, O.B.E., M.A., Indian Finance Dept. (acct.-genl., United Provs.) (b. 4th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1905; asst. acctnt.-gen., Jan., 1906; dep. contr. of war accts., Aug., 1917; O.B.E., June, 1917; auditor of govt. of India sanctions, April, 1921; dep. acctnt.-gen., March, 1923; offg. dep. auditor-genl., Sept., 1923; offg. acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, March, 1926; offg. dir. of audit, United Provs., Feb., 1927; offg. acct. genl. Punjab, Mar. 1929; dir. of army audit, Mar., 1930; acct.-genl., United Provs., Apl., 1933.

GUPTA, AVADH BEHARI (see Avadh Behari G.).

GUPTA, BINODE BIHARI, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 15th Apl., 1905; inspr., Dec., 1928; dep. supt., July, 1929; promoted to I.P., as addl. supt., July, 1932; supt., Aug., 1937.

GUPTA, BIRENDA CHANDRA, A.I.E.E. late Indian Educ. Service (b. 10th Dec., 1881).—Joined the service, 28th Jan., 1913; profir., civil engrg. coll., May, 1919; princpl., Dacca engng. sch., May, 1933; ret'd., July, 1938.

GUPTA, D. C., B.Sc. (Har.), Industries Dept., Bihar and Orissa.—Dir. of industries, 13th Apl., 1926 to Apl., 1933.

GUPTA, HARISH CHANDRA, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 15th July, 1910).—Educated at Ewing Christian College, Allahabad, University of Allahabad and New College, Oxford; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1936.

GUPTA, HIRENDA NATH, B.Sc., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 23rd Nov., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 16th Aug., 1924; offg. supt., Mar., 1931; confd., Apl., 1937.

GUPTA, IRESH CHANDRA, M.Sc., A.I.C., His Majesty's Mint, Bombay (b. 11th Mar., 1898).—Joined the service, 30th Aug., 1921, as dep. assay master; offg. assay master, April, 1922, March, 1923, and Sept., 1926.

GUPTA, JATINDRA CHANDRA, B.A., Barr., late Bengal Judl. Dept. (b. 23rd June, 1877).—Joined the service, 15th Sept., 1908, as regtr. and chief. mins. offr., Calcutta small cause ct.; offg. addl. judge, Feb., 1913; judge, Calcutta small cause ct., Nov., 1916; offd. as chief judge variously from Feb., 1924; ret'd., June, 1932.

GUPTA, JYOTISH CHANDRA, B.A., Income-tax Dept., Bengal (asst. commr.) (b. Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, Apl., 1910; held non-gaz. appts. prior to 8th Mar., 1916; sub-dy. collr., Burdwan, Mar., 1916; income-tax officer, Apl., 1922; confd. as asst. commr. of income-tax, May, 1934.

GUPTA, KULADA CHARAN DAS (see Das Gupta, K.C.).

GUPTA, LAKSHMAN SARUP (see Lakshman, S.G.).

GUPTA, NAGENDRA NATH, Judl. Dept., Bengal M.A., Barr. (b. 9th April, 1884).—Joined the service as municipal magie., Calcutta, 16th Sept. 1926.

GUPTA, PANNA LAL, Indian State Railways (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 16th Jan., 1896).—Asst. engr., 24th Sept., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr. (Provl.), 1937; confd., Oct., 1938.

GUPTA, RADHA KRISHANA (see Radha, K.G.).

GUPTA, RAGHUVANSH LAL, M.Sc. (Allbd.), Indian C.S. (under secty., fin. dept., govt. of India) (b. 7th Aug., 1905).—Educ. at Allahabad University and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under secty. to govt. fin. dept., Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1934; and again, Feb., 1936; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Oct., 1936; under-secty., fin. dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1937.

GUPTA, RANJIT, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 1st Feb., 1906).—Educ. at University Coll., Aberystwyth, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1935; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1935.

GUPTA, ROMESH CHANDRA, late Indian State Railways (b. 13th Dec., 1877).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1899; dist. traffic supt., Oct., 1913; offg. dep. traffic manager, March, 1924; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

GUPTA, SAMARENDRA NATH, Dept. of Industries, Punjab (princl., Mayo sch. of arts) (b. 29th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. princpl., Mayo sch. of arts, 2nd Jan., 1914; vice-princl., ditto, May, 1928; princpl., ditto, Nov., 1929.

GUPTA, SARAT KUMAR DUTTA, M.A., F.S.S. M.B.E., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 17th June, 1881).—Held ministerial appt. from 15th Jan., 1902; asst. acct.-genl., Bengal, July, 1913; asst. contr., India treasuries, Sept., 1915; addl., asst. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Nov., 1916; asst. secty., ditto, Nov., 1917; dep. contr. of war accts., munitions beh., May, 1918; contr. of army factory accts., Oct., 1928; ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Mar., 1932; offg. dep. dir. of rly. audit, Nov., 1932; ch. accts. officer, N.W. rly., Jan., 1933; ret'd., June, 1936.

GUPTA, SATISH CHANDRA, C.I.E., Barr. (b. 16th Sept., 1876).—Apptd. asst. secty., Bengal legisl. council (tem.), 22nd Dec., 1911; legal asst., legisl. dept., govt., of India, Sept., 1914; offg. addl. sec., ditto, April, 1923; addl. jt. secty. ditto, Oct., 1925; secty., legislative assembly dept. (temp.), Jan., 1929; confd., Apl., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

GUPTA, SATYENDRA NATH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. customs, Karachi) (b. 29th July, 1895).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., London, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1918; arrived, 10th Jan., 1919, and posted to Bengal; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1921; offg. asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta, 1922; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1925; confd., Aug., 1930; offg. dep. Indian trade commr. in London, July, 1928; offg. Indian trade commr., Nov., 1929; trade commr., Hamburg, Mar., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1935; collr. of customs, Karachi, Dec., 1937.

GUPTA, SEN (see Sen Gupta).

GUPTA, SHAIBAL KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 26th Feb., 1902).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1925; arrived, 7th Dec., 1925, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1932; addl. ditto, 24-Parganas, Aug., 1935.

GUPTA, SHAMLAL, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 15th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 21st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1929.

GUPTA, SHORLINDRA CHANDRA, M.A. (Cantab.), Indian Finance Dept. (b. 20th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service, 20th May, 1916; currency officer, Cawnpore, Apl., 1922; under-secty., fin. dept. Govt. of India, Dec., 1925; offg. under-

secty. for. and pol. dept., Aug., 1930; audit officer Indian stores dept., Nov., 1931; offg. dep. govt. exr., B.N. rly., Feb., 1933; exr., outside audit dept., Bengal, Dec., 1933; compt. Assam, Feb., 1935.

GUPTA, SUKUMAR, M.A., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 1st Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 7th Dec., 1922; addl. supt., Mar., 1928; offg. supt., Mar., 1931; confd., Oct., 1932.

GUPTA, SUNNY, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th Mar., 1905).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 14th Apr., 1931.

GUPTA, SURESH CHANDRA, RAI BAHADUR, M.A., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 10th Aug., 1881).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 16th Sept., 1903; supt., May, 1906; 1st asst. post-master, Calcutta, July, 1909; supt., rly. mail service, Apr., 1911; supt., Aug., 1913; supt., post offices, Oct., 1924; offg. dep., P.M.G., Bengal and Assam, Apr., 1926; confd., Dec., 1927; offg. dep. dir.-genl., postal services, Apr., 1933; p.m.g., United Provs., June, 1935; retd., Aug., 1936.

GURBACHAN SINGH, SARDAR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 15th Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 26th Oct., 1925; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

GURBAKSH SINGH, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 2nd Sept., 1877).—Joined the service as apprentice asst. supt., 5th June, 1899; asst. supt., June, 1900; supt., Sept., 1911; divl. engr., Aug., 1920; offg. dir., tel. engrg., June, 1926; confd., Oct., 1927; retd., Sept., 1932.

GURBAKSH SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR, B.A., M.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 15th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1905; asst. engr., Sept., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; M.B.E., June, 1919; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1931; confd., July, 1932.

GURCHARN DAS, LALA, MEHTA, Rai Sahib, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 11th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service, 18th Oct., 1909; asst. engr., Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Apr., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1933.

GURDIAL SINGH, Indian Police (b. 15th June, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., 3rd Feb., 1932; confd., Feb., 1934.

GURDIAL SUNDER SINGH, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 22nd July, 1899).—Joined the service, 9th Nov., 1923; asst. consvr., Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

GURJAR, NARAYAN PANDURANG, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 12th Apr., 1895).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 7th Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1928; confd., Oct., 1929; constg. p.h. engr. to govt. Sind (in addn.), Apr., 1938.

GURMUKH SINGH, late Income Tax Dept., Bombay (b. 14th July, 1879).—Held temp. and permanent non-gazetted appts. from 12th Sept., 1900, to 3rd March, 1924; pers. asst. to commr. of income tax, March, 1924; asst. commr. of income tax, June, 1927; retd., Oct., 1934.

GURNER, CYRIL WALTER, Indian C.S. (chairman, Calcutta improvement trust) (b. 18th Jan., 1888).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., London, Oriel Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; under-secty. to govt. genl. dept., Sept., 1915; dep. chairman, Calcutta Corp., July, 1918; dep. sec. to govt., of Bengal, pol. and appt. dept., Sept., 1923; mag. and collr., March, 1925; secty. govt. of Bengal, local self-govt. dept., Mar., 1929; offg. chairman, Calcutta improvement trust, Mar., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; confd. as commr. of a divn., Feb., 1938.

GURNEY, ANGUS HUGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 24th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Highgate School, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1913; and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1918; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Aug., 1918, to Jan., 1919; dep. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, May, 1922; offg. acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, April, 1923; ditto, Punjab, March, 1926; dep. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, Oct., 1926; offg. dir. of audit, United Provs., April, 1928; dep. acct.-genl., central revenues, Nov., 1928; offg. dir. audit, U.P., Nov., 1929; acct. genl., U.P., Nov., 1931; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1933; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1934.

GURNEY, WILFRED ERIC, V.D., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (divl. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 2nd Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 16th Nov., 1911; on milty. service, 16th Nov., 1914, to 1st Oct., 1919; resdt. engr., Oct., 1919; divl. engr., Oct., 1930; offg. dep. ch. engr. (bridges), July, 1936.

GUTHRIE, ALAN, M.B.E., late Dept. of Industries, Madras (b. 1st June, 1881).—Joined the service as leather expert, 1st Sept., 1911; on mil. duty from 30th June, 1915, to 29th May, 1917; on duty as dep. contr. of hides, Madras, under Indian Munitions Board from 6th June, 1917, to 31st July, 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; principl., leather trades institute, Madras, Oct., 1923; officer on spl. duty, ditto, Feb., 1930; retd., Aug., 1935.

GWILLIM, JOHN DAVID LLEWELYN, Burma Police (Class I.) (b. 30th Oct., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Dec., 1937.

GWYER, Sir MAURICE LINFORD, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., K.C. (Chief Justice of India).—Member of Indian States Enquiry Committee, 1932, late First Parliamentary Counsel to the Treasury; apptd. first Chief Justice of India, Oct., 1937.

GWYN, AYLMER HAMOND, Lt.-Col., I.A., retd., late Survey of India (b. 13th Feb., 1883).—First commn. dated 27th Aug., 1902; apptd. asst. supt. trigl. survey, 17th Feb., 1908; reverted to milty. duty from Dec., 1914, to Aug., 1918; dep. supt., June, 1922; supt. Jan., 1926; offg. dir., Oct., 1931; retd., Oct., 1934.

GWYNNE, CLEMENT WANSBROUGH, C.S.I., C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt., United Provs.) (b. 3rd July, 1883).—Educ. at the High Sch., Newcastle, Staffs., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov.,

1907, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; city mag., Lucknow, Feb., 1913; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., United Provs., and jt. mag., July, 1917; offg. asst. sec. to govt. of India, army dept., Feb., 1918-Mar., 1919; sec., Indian soldiers' bd., March, 1919; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1919; confd., Sept., 1920; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; on special duty in connection with retirement measures in the Andamans, 1922; sec., arms rules cttee., June, 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; mag. and collr., March, 1924; dep. commr., Nov., 1924; jt. secty. to govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1929; ch. secty. to govt., United Provs., Apl., 1935; reforms commr., U.P. (temp.), Oct., 1935; offg. ch. secty. to govt., U.P., Oct., 1936; confd., Dec., 1936; C.S.I., May, 1937; author of "A Monograph on Wire and Tinsel."

GWYTHER, BERTRAM GRANVILLE, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Nov., 1879).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1902; arrived 11th Nov., 1904, and apptd. asst. engr.; exec. engr., June, 1909; suptg. engr., March, 1919; ret'd., July, 1929.

GWYTHER, CYRIL EDRYK, B.A., F.S.S., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 3rd March, 1882).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Feb., 1907, as prob. fin. dept.; asst. acctnt.-genl., May, 1907; dep. contr. of paper currency, Calcutta, June, 1919; dep. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Oct., 1921; dep. contr. of currency, Calcutta, Oct., 1922; ditto, Delhi, Jan., 1924; dep. auditor-genl., May, 1929; acct.-genl., Punjab, Oct., 1931; ret'd., May, 1936.

GYAN NATH, RAI BAHADUR, Raja, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Political Service (b. Feb., 1885).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 19th July, 1909, to 22nd Aug., 1913; extra asst. commr., Jan., 1916; under secty. to agent to govr.-genl., Punjab States, Jan., 1922; on foreign service to Nabha State, 1st May, 1928; presdt., council of regency, Nabha state, Mar., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; Raja as hereditary distinction, June, 1938.

GYAN SINGH, BHAI, B.A., Income Tax Dept., Punjab (asst. commr.) (b. 28th Aug., 1884).—Joined the service 6th May, 1911; income tax collr., Jan., 1923; extra asst. commr., Oct., 1924; offg. asst. commr., June, 1926; confd., Jan., 1931.

GYANI, SUNDRA SINGH, Sardar Bahadur, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. offcr., N.W. rly.) (b. 14th May, 1893).—Asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1914; divl. transpn. offcr., May, 1935.

GYAW, U., B.A., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 6th Mar., 1891).—Temp. service as agric. engr. from 3rd Jan., 1919; agric. engr., Oct., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1936.

GYAW, U ON, O.B.E., Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 8th June, 1887).—Apptd. asst. dist. supt., April, 1920; dist. supt., Jan., 1924; O.B.E., June, 1932.

GYI, SIR JOSEPH AUGUSTUS MAUNG, Kt., Barr., Burma Judl. Dept. (b. 31st Dec., 1872).—Minister in charge of agric., excise and forest depts., Burma, Jan., 1923; apptd. judge,

high ct., Rangoon, Nov., 1924; member of govr.'s exec. council, Burma, July, 1926; Kt., Jan., 1927; actg.-govr., Burma, Aug., 1930; minister, govt. of Burma, May, 1932; minister for transf. subjects, Jan., 1933; member, Burma legisl. council, Oct., 1932.

GYI, U THA DIN, Barr., Burma Judl. Service (b. 18th Oct., 1890).—Apptd. second judge, small cause courts, Rangoon, 21st April, 1925; offg. ch. judge, ditto, Nov., 1932.

HAAS, ADOLPH LOUIS, M.I.Mech.E., Office of High Commr. for India (mechanical engr., store dept.) (b. 25th Oct., 1878).—Apptd. sub-inspr. of stores, Dec., 1903; asst. ditto, Feb., 1909; asst. mech. engr., June, 1919; mech. engr., Nov., 1920.

HABIBULLAH, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad H.).

HABIBUR, RAHMAN, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 10th May, 1894).—Joined the service, 2nd Jan., 1918, apptd. to I.P. as supt., Bengal, Apl., 1937.

HACKFORTH, PERCY, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 23rd June, 1880).—Joined the service as junr. engr., 27th March, 1903; resdt. engr., Oct., 1914; dist. engr., April, 1923; off. dep. ch. engr., Dec., 1926; confd., Feb., 1930; offg. ch. engr., Mar., 1932 and Apl., 1934; confd., July, 1934; ret'd., Feb., 1937.

HACKING, Rev. HENRY M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (Madras) (b. 8th Dec., 1877).—Joined 18th March, 1907; jun. chaplain, March, 1910; on mil. duty from 20th Dec., 1916, to 5th May, 1919; senr. chaplain, March, 1920; ret'd., Mar., 1930.

HACKNEY, NORMAN, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (chemist and metallurgist, N.W. rly.) (b. 18th Nov., 1891).—Apptd. asst. chemist and metallurgist, G.I.P. rly., 26th June, 1919; chemist and metallurgist, N.W. rly., Apl., 1927.

HADDOW, JOHN REID, B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M., Indian Vety. Service (vety. research officer) (b. 4th April, 1896).—Joined the service, 24th Aug., 1923; dep. dir., Impl. vety. serum inst., May, 1930; vety. research officer (serology), May, 1933.

HADOW, DOUGLAS SCOTT, late Indian Police Service (Punjab).—Joined the service, 15th Oct., 1897, as proby. asst. dist. supt. of police; asst. dist. supt., May, 1899; supt., Aug., 1905; senior supt. of police, Delhi, Aug., 1914; on mil. duty, Aug., 1918, to April, 1919; senr. supt., Simla, April, 1919; inspr.-gen. of police, Kashmir State, July, 1924; ret'd., May, 1929.

HADOW, SIR RAYMOND PATRICK, Kt., C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 26th Mar., 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1900, as asst. engr., Punjab; exec. engr. (temp.), Aug., 1907; confirmed, Oct., 1908; under-sec. to govt., Punjab, irrign. branch, Dec., 1914; dep. sec. to govt. of India, P.W. dept., Nov., 1920, to Nov., 1922; suptg. engr., Jan., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1925; offg. ch. engr., and sec. to govt. P.W.D., irrigation branch, April, 1926; confd., July, 1927; knighted, Jan., 1934; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

HADOW, REGINALD MICHAEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., C.P. (b. 17th Aug., 1915).—Educ. at Berkhamsed and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938 and served in the C.P. as asst. commr.

HAEGERT, GOTTFRIED ERNEST JOHN, Public works dept., Punjab (asst. secty. to govt., P.W.D. bldgs., and roads bch., (b. 8th Aug., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 7th Jan., 1901; offg. asst. secty. to govt., Punjab, P.W.D. bldgs., and roads bch., Apl., 1920; confd., May, 1921.

HAFAZAT HUSSAIN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. United Provs.) (b. 28th Feb., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Patna and Allahabad, and Caius College, Cambridge; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty., industries and labour dept., govt. of India, June, 1930; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1934; dep. secty. to govt., United Provs., industries branch (temp.), Apl., 1935.

HAFIZUDDIN, A. K. MUHAMMAD, B.A., Indian Police (b. 15th Jan., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. supt. of police, Bengal, on probn., 21st Apl., 1933; confd., May, 1935.

HAFEEZ, MUHAMMAD ABDUL (see Abdul H. M.).

HAGGAR, HENRY JAMES, Marine Dept., Bengal (b. 17th Feb., 1880).—Apptd. senr. ship surveyor, Calcutta, 8th Feb., 1929.

HAIDAR, SHAMS-UD-DIN, KHAN BAHADUR, O.B.E., late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 16th July, 1879).—Joined the service as proby. dep. magte. and dep. collr., 7th Jan., 1902; confd., Nov., 1903; magte. and collr., Nov., 1929; O.B.E., June, 1931; secty., bd. of rev., Sept., 1932; ret'd., July, 1934.

HAIDER, AGHA (see Agha Haider).

HAIDER, SAIDY WAHID-UD-DIN, Khan Bahadur, P.M.S. (civil surgeon, United Provs.) (b. Oct., 1886).—Civil surg., Apl., 1929.

HAIG, GEOFFREY ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 19th May, 1900).—Educ. at Winchester and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provinces as asst. magte. and collr., asst. settlement offcr., Oct., 1936; on spec. duty, Dec., 1937 to May, 1938.

HAIG, SIR HARRY GRAHAM, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (govr., United Provs.) (b. 13th April, 1881).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1905, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; special duty in fin. dept. of govt. of India, April, 1910; under-sec. to govt., United Provs., June, 1910, to May, 1912; commr. of municipalities, Indore State, Dec., 1912, to May, 1915; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., May, 1915; reverted from milty. duty, April, 1919; dep. sec., fin. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1919, to Nov., 1920; secty., Indian fiscal commn., 1921-1922; mag. and collr., Sept., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1923; on special

duty with royal commn. on superior civil services in India, 1923-4; offg. pte. sec. to the Viceroy, Jan., 1925; offg. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., April, 1926; confd., Dec., 1926; C.S.I., Jan., 1930; temp. member, exec. council of govr.-genl., Mar.-July, 1930; attended Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Mar., 1932; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1933; govr., United Provs., Dec., 1934.

HAIG, WOLSELEY DE HAGA, D.S.O., Lt.-Col. R.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., United Provs.) (b. 25th Aug., 1884).—First commn., dated 15th July, 1903; arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1905; asst. engr., Mar., 1909; exec. engr., Feb., 1914; dep. ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Dec., 1926; confd., Apl., 1930; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1936; confd., Feb., 1937.

HAIGH, FRANK FRASER, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 4th Feb., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1913; in military employ, 1916-19; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; under-sec., to govt. P.W.D. (irrigation), April, 1927.

HAILES, MARTIN HUNTER, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., Burma Rlys.) (b. 10th Apl., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 29th July, 1908; dist. traffic supt., Nov., 1928.

HAILEY, PHILIP COTES, Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Jan., 1903).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1923; in milty. employ to Sept., 1927; under-secty. to A.G.G. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1929; asst. to pol. agent, Kalat, Apl., 1930; asst. pol. agt., S. Waziristan, Oct., 1931; offg. under-secty., for. and pol. dept., June, 1933; Brit. trade agent, Gyantse and asst. to pol. officer in Sikkim, Dec., 1933; confd., Mar., 1937; offcr. on spec. duty, F. & P. dept., July, 1936; under-secty., f. and p. dept., Aug., 1936; under-secty., pol. dept., Apl., 1937; dep. ditto, Mar., 1938; first asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Oct., 1938.

HAILEY, Baron, WILLIAM MALCOLM HAILEY OF SHAHPUR AND NEWPORT PAGNELL, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th Feb., 1872).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 6th Dec., 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; junr. sec. to finl. commr., April, 1898, to Oct., 1899; settl. offr., Dec., 1899; colonization offr., Jhelum Canal, Nov., 1901, to Oct., 1904, and from Sept., 1905, to March, 1907; dep. commr., Oct., 1906; sec. to Punjab govt., Nov., 1907; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., July, 1908; confd., Nov., 1909; special duty in fin. dept. from March, 1911; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; 1st chief commr., Delhi, from 1st Oct., 1912; apptd. to Imperial Delhi Cttee., 1913; C.S.I., June, 1915; on deputn. with Reforms Commee. from Nov., 1918; finance member, govr.-genl.'s council, Dec., 1919; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1922; govr. of Punjab, 1st June, 1924; G.C.I.E., June, 1928; govr., United Provs., Aug., 1928, to Oct., 1930; Apl., 1931, to Apl., 1933; and again, Nov., 1933 to Dec., 1934; attended Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; G.C.S.I., June, 1932; Baron, June, 1936.

HAINES, REGINALD, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 10th Mar., 1881).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. examr. of public wks. accts., 1st Oct., 1902; asst. engr., Bombay, Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1927; confd., Dec., 1931; retd., Nov., 1932.

HAJARA, KARUNA KUMAR, M.A. (Cal.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 22nd Aug., 1903).—Educ. at University of Calcutta and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1930; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1935.

HAKEWILL, CHARLES THOMAS LANCELOT, Indian Police (b. 31st Oct. 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Central Provs., on probn., Oct., 1936.

HAKIM RAI, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 2nd Sept., 1897).—Joined the service, 17th Oct., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1931.

HAKSAR, SUNDAR NARAIN, M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr. Punjab) (b. 4th Dec., 1909).—Educated at D.N. High School, Delhi, Punjab University, Delhi University, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 6th October, 1933; arrived in India, November, 1933, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HAKUMAT RAI, DIWAN, M.C., M.D., Col., I.M.S. (insp. genl., civil hosps., Cent. Provs.) (b. 15th May, 1883).—First commn., dated 27th July, 1907; in milty. employ to 21st Aug., 1921; offg. civil surg., Aug., 1921; confd., Nov., 1921; profr., King Edward medl. coll., Oct., 1923; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hosps., Punjab, Apl., 1935; offg. dep. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Nov., 1935; inspr. genl., civil hosps., Central Provs., July, 1937.

HALDAR, BINAY RANJAN, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 27th Aug., 1883).—Joined the service, 28th Feb., 1909; asst. engr., May 1910; exec. engr., May, 1922; retd., Mar., 1934.

HALDAR, SUDHANSU KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian Civil Service (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 6th Dec., 1901).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and King's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte. and dep. collr., Dec., 1927; commr. of excise and salt, Oct., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1933.

HALDAR, SUDHINDRA KUMAR, M.A., Indian Civil Service (secty. dept. of communications and works, Bengal) (b. 4th Oct., 1891).—Educ. at Calcutta and London University; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 21st Nov., 1915, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. mag. and collr., Bengal, April, 1919; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1923; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; magte. and collr., July, 1928; dep. secty. to govt. of Bengal, fin. commerce and marine depts., July, 1929; sec. retrencht. ctee., Bengal, May, 1932; commr. of excise and salt, Bengal, Oct., June, 1935; secty. dept. of communications and works, Bengal, Apl., 1937.

HALE, HARRY WILLIAM, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 5th Feb., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., on 20th Nov., 1923; dist. officer, frontier constaby., Mar., 1930; secty., frontier regns. ctee., May, 1931; on spl. duty under dir., intel. bureau, Nov., 1936 to Sept., 1937.

HALES, THOMAS CHRISTMAS, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt. (senr.), N.W. Rly.) (b. 15th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1908, as asst. traffic supt.; dist. ditto, July 1917; offg. dep. ch. comml. manager, Jan., 1928; senr. asst., comml., Apl., 1929; and again, Apl., 1931; offg. divl. supt., Apl., 1929; offg. dep. ch. operating supt. (movements), Feb., 1932; offg. divl. supt. (senr.), N.W. rly., variously from Jan., 1933; dep. ch. comml. manager, N.W. rly., Aug., 1933; offg. ch., ditto, Apl., 1935; divl. supt. (senr.), July, 1936.

HALFHIDE, ROBERT ALFRED, M.B.E., Printing and Stationery Dept. (dep. contr., printing) (b. 5th Dec., 1886).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 8th Feb., 1904; supt., May, 1920; dep. contr., Mar., 1924; M.B.E., June, 1936.

HALFORD, DAVID REECE CHARLES, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 14th Sept., 1910).—Educ. at St. Paul's School, Emmanuel College, Cambridge, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934 and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

HALIFAX, VISCOUNT, RT. HON. EDWARD FREDERICK LINDLEY WOOD, K.G., P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.—Viceroy and governor-general of India, 3rd April, 1926, to 17th April, 1931.

HALIM, ABDUL, Munshi (see Abdul Halim).

HALL, ARTHUR GEORGE, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 7th Nov., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; confd., Jan., 1922; exec. engr., June, 1929; govt. insp. rlys., Burma, Apl., 1937 to Apl., 1938; exec. engr., N.W. rly., Apl., 1938; confd. (provl.), Mar., 1938.

HALL, CLARENCE ESME, Indian Railway Accts. Service (accts. officer) (b. 16th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. auditor and acct., G.I.P. Rly., 15th Sept., 1919; services permanently placed at the disp. of finl. commr. rlys., Oct., 1929; confd. as accts. offcr., Gr. I., Feb., 1936.

HALL, DANIEL GEORGE EDWARD, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 17th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as lecturer in history, Univ. coll., Rangoon, May, 1921; retd., Mar., 1936.

HALL, FIELDING (see Fielding-Hall).

HALL, FREDERICK JOSEPH, M.B.E., late Printing and Stationery Dept. (b. 26th Apl., 1879).—Held non-gazetted appts. up to 24th April, 1916; dep. supt., April, 1916; offg. supt., April, 1916; M.B.E., Jan., 1919; dep. contr. (temp.), Jan., 1923; confd., Oct., 1923; manager, G.I. press, Oct., 1926; retd., Apl., 1931.

HALL, GEOFFREY FOWLER, C.I.E., M.C., Indian Service of Engineers (suprg. engr., Bihar (b. 9th March, 1888).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1911; asst. engr., Nov., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. suprg. engr., May, 1931, and again, Feb., 1933; confd., Mar., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1935; ch. engr. (temp.), Jan., 1936.

HALL, Sir JOHN FREDERICK, C.S.I., C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian C.S. (member, bd. of rev., Madras (b. 14th Apl., 1882).—Educ. at Hymer's Coll., Hull, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 11th Dec., 1905, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and joint mag., Feb., 1913; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Feb., 1919; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; confd., April, 1925; sec. to govt. rev. dept., Mar., 1925; C.I.E., June, 1931; offg. 3rd member, bd. of rev., Madras, and commr. of land rev. and settlement, Feb., 1933; offg. 2nd member, ditto, July, 1933; 2nd member, ditto (provl.) and 1st member offg., May, 1935; C.S.I., Feb., 1937; knighted, June, 1938.

HALL, RIDLEY MARTIN, Indian Police (b. 25th Mar., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 22nd Oct., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Mar., 1931.

HALL, WILLIAM ÆNEAS, B.A., Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., Burma (b. 26th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1912; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1921; offg. exec. engr., Aug., 1926; confd., April, 1928; offg. suprg. engr., June, 1932.

HALL, WILLIAM STIRLING, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab (b. 26th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 20th Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1931.

HALL, WILLIAM Telford, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs. (b. 2nd June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 27th Feb., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1922; instr., Forest coll., Dehra Dun, Mar., 1935 to Apl., 1938.
