NAIFF, JOHN CALVERLEY, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 31st Oct., 1913).—Educ. at Hanley High School, Stoke-on-Trent, and St. John's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 28th September, 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.

NAIK, SHRIDHAR ATMARAM, late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 9th Mar., 1875).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 18th May, 1903, to 31st Aug., 1906; sub-judge, Sept., 1906; judge, small cause ct., Poona, Nov., 1927; retd., Mar., 1930.

NAIR (see also Nayar).

NAIR, PALAT GOVINDAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 20th May, 1916).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 27th Sept., 1938; arrived in India Oct., 1938, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

NAIR, THEKATH KUMARAN, B.A. (Mad.), B.Sc. (B'ham), Indian State Rlys. (chemist and metallurgist, E.I. Rly.) (b. 16th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. chemist and metallurgist, 2nd Aug., 1915; offg. chemist and metallurgist, July, 1916; confd., July, 1917.

NAIR, UNNI KANNAN, Local Fund Audi dept., Burma (asst. examr.) (b. 15th Apr., 1886).—Previous service in non-gazetted appts. from 15th April, 1908; divl. auditor, Feb., 1925; offg. asst. examr., May, 1926; confd., Sept., 1926; offg. examr., June, 1931.

NAJABAT HUSAIN KHAN BAHADUR, B. L., late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 24th July, 1879).—Joined the service, 24th May, 1907; offg. munisif, Nov., 1909; confd., June, 1910; sub-judge, Feb., 1922; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1931; retd., Oct., 1934.

NAMBIYAR, AMANDA KALOTH KUNHIRAMAN (see Kunhiraman Nambiyar, A. K.

NAMJOSHI, VINAYAK KRISHNA, B.A., late Bombay C.S. (b. 5th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service, 18th May, 1900; administrator, Cambay State, from 18th April, 1918, to 10th June, 1929; collr. and dist. mag., March, 1932; retd., Oct., 1930.

NANAK SINGH, Sardar Sahib Sardar. Punjab Civil Service (dep. commr.) (b. 1st Mar., 1889).—Extra asst. commr., Sept., 1913; dep. commr., Sept., 1936.

NANA RAO, TANJORE KRISHNARAO, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 15th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service, 16th Dec., 1916; dep. acct. genl., B. and O., Jan., 1934; ditto, Madras, May, 1935; dep. govt. examr. of accts., B.B. and C.I. Rly., July, 1937.

NANAVATI, ERACH MANEKSHAH, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th July, 1877).—Educ. at Surat High Sch., Bombay Univ., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 19th Dec., 1901, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., July, 1911; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1919; offg. judge, chief ct. of Oudh, March, 1928; offg. secty. to govt., legal dept.; judge, ch. ct., Oudh, Mar., 1930; retd., July, 1937.

NAND GOPAL, DIWAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., central p.w.d.).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 24th July, 1931; apptd. to I.S.E., as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, July, 1932; transfed. to central p.w.d., Apr., 1935.

NAND GOPAL, LAIA, A.M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 1st Mar., 1894).—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1917; asst. engr., Oct., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1927.

NAND KISHORE, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 14th Nov., 1883).—Joined the service, 29th Sept., 1906; asst. engr., Sept., 1907; exec. engr., Sept., 1915; offg. supt., April, 1926; retd., Jan., 1933.

NANJAPPA, VENKATASUBRAMANIER, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 26th Jan., 1911).—Educ. at Government Collegiate High School, Bangalore, Mysore University, University College, London, and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

NAPIER, ALEXANDER, Capt., I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 7th Sept., 1904).—First commn., 26th Aug., 1924; in milty. employ to Nov., 1930; under-secty. to A.G.G., in C.I., Apr., 1932; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Dec., 1933; asst. commr., Kohat, Dec., 1936; asst. commr. and city magte., Peshawar, Apr., 1937; under secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1937; v. consul, Khoramshar, Nov., 1938.

NAPIER, ALEXANDER HARPER, M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd., late Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 28th Sept., 1880).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1907; in mil. employ up to 19th May, 1922; civil surg., May, 1922; retd., Mar., 1932.

NAPIER-MUNN, Rev. WILLIAM ERNEST, Indian Eccl. Estab. (b. 25th Apr., 1886).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, United Provs., 21st Apr., 1929.

NAQVI, ABU TALIB, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 1st March, 1907).—Educ. at Allahabad University and Oriel College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; on spec. duty at govt. h.q., Nov., 1937.

NAQVI, GHAZANFAR ALI (see Ghazanfar, A. N.).

NARA SIMHAM, RAMASWAMY LAKSHMI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 27th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at Madras University and New College, Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1935.

NARAHARI RAO, VYAKARANA, C.I.E., M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 6th July, 1893).—Joined the service, 5 Mar., 1917; dep. acct. genl. posts and tels., May, 1923, under-secty., fin. dep., Govt. of India, Aug., 1924; offg. asst. secty., ditto, Nov., 1927; offg. under secty. govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Apr., 1929; addl. dep. secty., ditto, Mar., 1933; temp. jt. secty., ditto, in addn., Aug., 1936; addtl. dep. secty., and Crown fin. offcr., pol. dept., Apr., 1937; C.I.E., May, 1937.

NARASIMHA AYYANGAR, RAJAGOPALA AYYANGAR, Diwan Bahadur, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. Sept., 1881).—Joined the service, 10th Feb., 1904; asst. engr., Feb., 1908; exec. engr., Feb., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., Aug., 1928; confd., Apl., 1931; offg. ch. engr., Sept., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; ret'd., Sept., 1936.

NARASIMHAM PANTULU, MANTHRIPRA-GADA, Rao Bahadur, Madras C.S. (member, Madras public services commn.) (b. 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1894; dep. collr., Mar., 1919; offg. collr., May, 1932; member, Madras public services commn., Feb., 1935.

NARASIMHAN, CHAKRAVARTHI VIJIRAGHAVA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 21st May, 1915).—Educ. at National High School, Trichinopoly, Madras Univ., and St. Catherine's Society, Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

NARASIMHULU, NAIDU G., Rao Bahadur, B.A., B.L., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (dir. p. and t.) (b. 9th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 22nd Dec., 1910; confd., 3rd. July, 1912; asst. dep. dir. genl., Sept., 1935; dep. P.M.G., July, 1937; dir. posts and tels., S. and B. circle, June, 1938.

NARAYAN, APPADWADULA LAKSHMI, M.A., D.Sc., Meteorological Dept. (asst. director) (b. 15th July, 1890).—Joined service as meteorologist on probn., 18th Dec., 1928; confd., 18th Dec., 1930; asstt. dir., ditto, Jan., 1934; again, Jan., 1937.

NARAYAN, BRIJ (see Brij Narayan).

NARAYAN, JAGAT (see Jagat N.).

NARAYAN, PANDIT HARI (see Pandit Hari Narayan).

NARAYAN, PRAG (see Prag, N.).

NARAYAN, RAJ (see Raj Narayan).

NARAYAN, SHIV, M.A., B.Sc. (Punj.), B.E., M.Sc. (U.S.A.), Mem. A.I.E.E., A.M.I.E.E. (Lond.), A.M.I.E. (India), Indian Educl. Service (b. 12th Nov., 1883).—Appdt. prof. of physics and elec. engr., coll. of engg., Poona, March, 1921; offg. prinpl., ditto, June, 1932.

NARAYAN, S. L. (see Lokur, Narayan Swamirao).

NARAYAN, SRI (see Sri Narayan).

NARAYANA AYYAR, RAJAPPAYA, Barr., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 8th July, 1876).—Educ. at Madras University and King's Coll., Cambridge; appdt. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 25th Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., May, 1910; dist. and sessions judge, May, 1923; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

NARAYANA KUDVA, VENKATARAYA (see Kudva, V. N.).

NARAYANA RAI, AJILIDAY, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 28th Jan., 1908).—Appdt. proby. asst. supt., Madras, 7th Mar., 1932; confd., Mar., 1934.

NARAYANASWAMI AYYAR, OMBATHIVELI RAMASWAMI AYYAR, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 2nd Aug., 1904).—Appdt. asst. exec. engr., Madras, 16th Aug., 1929; exec. engr., Aug., 1938.

NARAYANASWAMI, BRAHMADESAM SUBRAMANYA, M.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 10th Dec., 1899).—Joined the service as officr., Cl. II, Oct., 1923.

NARENDRA NATH SEN, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 1st Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., April, 1929.

NARIMAN, RUSTOM KAIKHUSRO, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 25th May, 1877).—Appdt. asst. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1899; exec. engr., Dec. 1908; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1921, and again Feb., 1925; ret'd., May, 1932.

NASH, GEORGE WILLIAM, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 10th Jan., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., United Provs., 15th Oct., 1927; offg. exec. engr., June, 1930; confd. Oct., 1936.

NASH, PHILIP GEOFFREY ELWIN, Indian C.S. (asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy) (b. 20th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at St. Paul's School and Worcester College, Oxford; appdt. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., Burma, Mar., 1934, to May, 1936; on spec. duty with the govt. of Burma, May, 1936, to Apl., 1937; secty. to Burma delegate Imperial Conf., Apl.-June, 1937; addtl. asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy, Aug., 1937; asst. ditto, Jan., 1938.

NASIM ALI, SYED, M.A., B.L., Bengal Judl. Dept. (judge, high ct., Calcutta).—Appdt. addl. judge, high ct. Calcutta, 13th Nov., 1933; appdt. judge, ditto, Oct., 1936.

NATARAJAN, JAGADISH, B.A., Hons. (Bombay) (b. 17th May, 1902).—Appdt. dep. princpl. information officr., home dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1938.

NATESAN, VISHWANATHAN, M.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 1st Aug., 1898).—Joined the service as officr., Cl. II, Apl., 1923; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Apl., 1932; again, Feb., 1937.

NATH, AJUDHIA (see Ajudhia Nath).

NATH, AMAR (see Amar Nath).

NATH, BABU KAMESHAR (see Kameshar, N. B.).

NATH, GAURI (see Gauri Nath).

NATH, GYAN (see Gyan Nath).

NATH, JAGAN, Juneja (see Jagan Nath, J.).

NATH, JAGAN KUKAR (see Jagan, Nath K.).

NATH, JOGENDRA CHANDRA, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 22nd Sept., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 30th Jan., 1925; dep. consvr., Jan., 1930.

NATH, PREM (see Prem Nath).

NATH, SURATH (see Surath Nath).

NATHA SINGH, BAWA, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 5th July, 1882).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1927; confd., May, 1932; offg. ch. engr. and secty. to govt., Punjab, p.w.d. irrign. bch., May, 1936; ret'd., July, 1937.

NAUBAT RAI, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 7th Sept., 1888).—Joined the service as tempy. engr., 1st April, 1922; asst. engr. (provl. service), April, 1926; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1928; offg. exec. engr., May, 1930; confd., Jan., 1936.

NAUBAT RAI, RAI BAHADUR, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 4th Sept., 1875).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 23rd Aug., 1894; supt., Feb., 1904; dep. postmaster-genl., Punjab and N.W.F., June, 1924; retd., Oct., 1930.

NAVKLAR, Miss RUBY, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Bombay) (b. 25th Nov., 1875).—Joined the service as lady supt., training coll. for women, Poona, 19th May, 1920; awarded K.-i.-H. medal, 2nd class, Jan., 1930; retd., Nov., 1930.

NAWAB SINGH, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) b. 9th April, 1907).—Educ. at University of Nagpur and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., June, 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1935, to Apl., 1937.

NAWABZADA, AMINULLAH KHAN, A.M.Inst.T., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. offcr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 16th Aug., 1893).—Asst. traffic supt., Nov. 1915; divl. transpn. offcr., May, 1935.

NAYAK, PANGAL RAMANATHA, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 29th March, 1911).—Educated at Christian High School, Udipi, University of Madras and The School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

NAYAR, CHETTUR, Govindan, Diwan Bahadur, B.A., B.L., Barr-at-law, Judl. dept., Madras (Law secty. to the Govt. of Orissa) (b. 23rd Dec., 1881).—Apptd. District Munsif, June, 1910; offg. dist. judge, June, 1933; confd., Aug., 1933; secty. govt. of Orissa, law and comm. dept., Apl., 1936.

NAYAR, CHETTUR MADHAVAN, Judl. Dept., Madras (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. Jan., 1879).—Apptd. asst. law reporter, Feb., 1915; prof., law coll., Aug., 1916; advocate-gen., Feb., 1923; offg. high ct. judge, March, 1924; confd., March, 1927; offg. ch. justice, Madras, Sept., 1935, to Oct., 1935; again, July, 1938.

NAYAR, KRISHNAPILLAI KARUKAIL KARUNAKARAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 11th Sept., 1907).—Educ. at Madras University and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; judge, small causes ct., Dec., 1936.

NAYAR, MANNATH GOVINDAN, B.A., B.L., late Indian Police (b. Aug., 1881).—Joined the service, 27th Jan., 1907; supt., Nov., 1924; retd., Aug., 1936.

NAYAR, MANAKAMPAD KESAVANUNNI, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 25th June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

NAYAR, M. KRISHNAN (see Krishnan Nayar, M.).

NAYAR, PADUNHARANKUNNATH THAZATHAVIL RAMAN, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 14th Jan., 1910).—Educ. at St. Joseph's Boys' High School, Calicut, University of Madras and St. Catherine's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub.-collr., Jan., 1937.

NAYAR, U. GOVINDAN (see Govindan Nayar, U.).

NAYLOR, LEONARD BROWN, Burma Frontier Service (asst. supt.) (b. 19th Mar., 1887).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Mar., 1914; asst. supt. (B.F.S.), July, 1923.

NAYUDU, BANDI VENKATA SRI HARI RAO (see Sri Hari, R. N. B. V.).

NAYUDU, BHASKARA RAO, T. (see Bhaskara Rao Nayudu, T.).

NAYUDU, BUNDY SRIRAMULU (see Sriramulu Nayudu B.).

NAYUDU, RAI BAHADUR, Sir K. V. R. (see Venkata Reddi Nayudu).

NAYUDU, PAKALA VENKATARAMANA RAO, Rao Bahadur (see Venkataramana Rao Nayudu, Pakala, Rao Bahadur).

NAYUDU, VENKATANARAYANA J. (see Venkatanarayana, N. J.).

NAZIM-UD-DIN, KHWAJA, Sir, K.C.I.E. (member govr.'s exec. council, Bengal).—Apptd. minister, govt. of Bengal, 18th Dec., 1929; member, govr.'s exec. council, Bengal, May, 1934; K.C.I.E., June, 1934.

NAZIR ALAM, SHAH, Indian Police (asst. supt., United Provs.) (b. 4th Mar., 1906).—Joined the service as dep. supt. (proby.), 2nd Jan., 1927; confd., Jan., 1929; apptd. to I.P.S. as asst. supt., 31st Aug., 1930; offg. supt., Apl., 1931; and again, May, 1933.

NAZIRUDDIN, KAMALUDDIN, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Orissa) (b. 31st Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 26th June, 1922; asst. exec. engr., June, 1923; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1928; confd., Aug., 1932; transf. to Orissa, April, 1936.

NAZR-UL-BAKAR, SHEIKH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 21st Dec., 1913).—Educ. at Denny's High School, Rawalpindi, Govt. High School, Murree, and Punjab Univ.; joined the service 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

NEAL, REGINALD FRANCIS, Indian Police (b. 8th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 5th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., Apl., 1933.

NEALE, REGINALD KINGSLEY, Major, Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 19th July, 1897).—First commn., Apl., 1916; joined the service as offcr., Cl. II, July, 1935.

NEALE, WALTER GORDON, Lt.-Col., C.I.E., C.V.O., I.A. (retd.) India Office, (pol. A.D.C. to secty. of state) (b. 2nd Oct., 1880). First commn. dated 15th Feb., 1902; in mil. employ until July, 1906, when he was apptd. prob. for pol. dept., N.W.F. Prov.; personal asst. to chief commr., April, 1907; dep. commr., temp., D.I., Khan, Dec., 1908; asst. commr., ditto, Jan., 1909; asst. pol. agent, Dir Swat and Chitral, Aug., 1909; asst. commr., Hazara, March, 1910; ditto, Oghi and commdt. B.M. Police, March, 1910; asst. sec. to chief commr., N.W.F. Prov., and supt. of census, Aug., 1910; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Nov., 1911; pol. offir., Deoli, April, 1912; asst. resdt., Western States of Rajputana, Nov., 1912; asst. pol. agent, Eastern States of Rajputana, May, 1913; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, July, 1913; asst. pol. agent, Eastern Rajputana States, Nov., 1913; asst. to resdt. in Persian Gulf,

June, 1914, to April, 1915; on spl. duty under chief pol. officer, Basra, 1916; second asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Oct., 1916; asst. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Feb., 1917; pol. agent, Dir, Swat and Chitral, Dec., 1920; pol. agent, Eastern Rajputana States, April, 1924; first asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Nov., 1924; pol. agent, Bhopal, Oct., 1926; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., June, 1928; C.I.E., March, 1929; ret'd., July, 1930; pol. A.D.C. to secty. of state, Sept., 1933; C.V.O., May, 1937.

NEEDHAM, HERBERT SIDNEY, B.A. (Vic.), late Indian Police Service (Bombay) (b. 7th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1911, as asst. supt.; supt. of police, June, 1923; dep. commr. of police, Nov., 1924; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

NEEDHAM, Sir RICHARD ARTHUR, Kt., C.I.E., D.S.O., M.D., B.Sc., F.R.C.P., D.P.H., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 31st July, 1877).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1903; in mil. employ until 27th May, 1911, when apptd. offg. health officer, Simla; on special duty with Chinese troops returning from Tibet, 1912; health officer, Simla, Feb., 1914; reverted to mil. duty, Aug., 1914; asst. dir.-gen., I.M.S. (stores), Oct., 1916; asst. dir.-gen., I.M.S. (sany.), 1918; dep. dir.-gen., I.M.S., Oct., 1918; C.I.E., June, 1919; on special duty to Europe to attend Intl. Sanitary Conference, 1921; inspr. of medical education (in addition), 1922; on deputation to England to General Medical Council, 1923; officer on special duty, govt. of India, rly. dept., 1925; on special duty as inspr. of medl. educn., 1926-7; ret'd., Oct., 1930; Kt., June, 1932.

NEHPAL SINGH, B.A., M.R.A.S., Indian Educ. Service (asst. d.p.i., United Provs.) (b. 14th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as prinpl., jubilee inter. coll., Lucknow, 4th July, 1922; hd. master, govt. high school, Bareilly, April, 1924; prinpl., govt. inter. coll., Almora, Oct., 1927; offg. inspr. of schools, June, 1928; confd., July, 1932; asst. dir. of public instrn. variously from Apl., 1935; confd., Oct., 1937.

NEHRU, BRAJ KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 4th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at the Government Intermediate College, Allahabad, University of Allahabad, the School of Economics, London and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub. divl. officer, Apl., 1937.

NEHRU, BRIJLAL, M.A., Barr., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 5th May, 1884).—Joined the service, 16th Nov., 1898; asst. acctnt.-gen., Ju e, 1910; dep. acctnt.-gen., Burma, Nov., 1920; asst. ditto, Bengal, Oct., 1922; dep. ditto, United Provs., Sept., 1923; dep. ditto, Punjab, Jan., 1926; offg. asst. auditor-genl., Dec., 1926; offg. dep. ditto, Mar., 1929; offg. dep. ch. auditor, N.W. Rly., Apl., 1930; ditto, E.I. Rly., Mar., 1931; dep. govt. ext., B.N. Rly., Nov., 1931; offg. ch. accts. officer, E. B. Rly., Nov., 1932; offg. ch. auditor, N.W. rly., Apl., 1933; offg. dir., rly. audit, July, 1935; offg. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Oct., 1935; confd., June, 1936; acct.-genl., Punjab, Mar., 1938.

NEHRU, RATAN KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S., Impl. Customs Service (asst. collr. (b. 10th Apl., 1902).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1925; arrived, 5th Dec., 1925, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1929; Apl., 1932; and again, Mar., 1933; asst. collr. of customs, Bombay, Mar., 1934; ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1935; offg. collr., June, 1936; first secty. to central bd. of rev., etc., Feb., 1937; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1937; first secty., cent. bd. of rev., Mar., 1938.

NEHRU, SHRI SHRIDHAR, M.A., Ph.D., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 17th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Agra Coll. School, India, Allahabad; Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, and Heidelberg; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 1st Dec., 1913, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; jt. mag., Mar., 1918; under-sec. to govt., April to July, 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1923; confd., June, 1930; secty. to govt., industries and educn. dept., June, 1931; dir. of publicity and reforms officer, Jan., 1932; member, Impl. council of agricl. research, June, 1933; magte. and collr. again from Dec., 1933.

NEIL, JOHN FREDERICK, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 12th June, 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, store dept., India Office, Nov., 1919; clerical officer, Jan., 1920; transfed. to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, March, 1926.

NEILSON, ERIC FITZROY, late Indian Police Service (b. 5th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1908; dist. supt., April, 1920; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1931.

NEILSON, JAMES, late Indian State Railways (b. 21st Aug., 1882).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll., joined the dept., 1st Oct., 1904, as asst. engr., state rlys.; exec. engr., E. Bengal Rly., Oct., 1912; on mil. duty on Mesopotamia rlys., April to Dec., 1917; dep. ch. engr. and senr. govt. inspr., Oct., 1926; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

NELSON, Sir ARTHUR EDWARD, K.C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Apl., 1875).—Educ. at Newcastle High Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 16th Nov., 1899; served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr. and registrar, judl. commr.'s court; provl. supt., impl. gazetteer and supt. of ethnography, Aug., 1908; 3rd sec. to chief commr., July, 1909; served in fin. dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1910, to March, 1911; settlt. duty, July to Sept., 1910, and April, 1911, to June, 1912; asst. commr. and settlt. offr., June, 1913; dep. commr., Jan., 1915; commr. of excise, &c., March, 1916; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; chief sec. to chief commr., March, 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1923; temp. member of exec. council, Cent. Provs., March, 1927, and again Aug., 1927; confd., Dec., 1927; Kt., March, 1929; K.C.I.E., June, 1932; actg. govr. of Central Provs., July-Nov., 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

NELSON, JAMES STEWART, late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 11th Oct., 1877).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 20th July, 1898; asst.

postmaster, Allahabad, Apl., 1903; supt., Feb., 1904; supt., post offices, Oct., 1923; offg. asst. dir.-genl., Oct., 1927; dep., P.M.G., U.P., Dec., 1927; ditto, Bengal and Assam, Dec., 1929; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

NELSON, JOHN JOSEPH HARPER, O.B.E., M.C., M.D., Lt.-Col. late I.M.S. (b. 12th Jan., 1882).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1908; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; prof'r., King Edward medl. coll., Lahore, 24th Nov., 1919; principal and prof. of medicine, ditto, Mar., 1930; ret'd., Jan., 1937.

NELSON, JOHN WOOD, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 14th Sept., 1884).—Educ. at George Watson's College, Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. offr.; transf'd. to Bengal, April, 1912; personal asst. to dir. of central intelligence, Simla, Jan., 1914, to April, 1919; on special duty under dir., central intelligence, April to May, 1919; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1919; mag. and collr., May, 1920; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1921; offg. secty., judl. dept. and supt. and remembr. of legal affairs, Bengal, May, 1930; confd., Oct., 1930; ret'd., Feb., 1934.

NELSON, WILLIAM HENRY, C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (member, bd. of rev., Bengal) (b. 15th June, 1880).—Educ. at Watson's Coll., and the Univ., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1905, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. offr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr. and settl. offr., Sept., 1911; mag. and collr., Sept., 1917; on mil. service, Aug. to Oct., 1918; offg. dir. of 1st and records, Bengal, Feb., 1920; offg. secty. of rev. and irrigation depts., Bengal, Feb., 1928; offg. commr., Dec., 1928; confd., May, 1930; offg. member, board of rev., Bengal, Feb., 1933; and again, Feb., 1936; confd., Feb., 1938; C.S.I., Jan., 1938.

NEOGI, PANCHANAN, M.A., Ph.D., F.C.S. (Lond.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 4th Oct., 1883).—Held appts. in the provl. service from 11th Nov., 1907; prof'r. (I.E.S.), Feb., 1921; vice-princl., Dacca Coll., July, 1921; offg. prof'r., B.E. Coll., Nov., 1921; offg. prof'r., presdy. coll., Calcutta, July, 1925; confd., Nov., 1925; ret'd., Dec., 1938.

NEOGI, PROBHA CHANDRA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 31st July, 1900).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 22nd Jan., 1926; offg. exec. engr., May, 1929; confd., Jan., 1935.

NETER PARKASH MOHAN, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 12th June, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 14th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

NETHERSOLE, MICHAEL HENRY BRADDON, D.S.O., B.A., Indian Civil Service (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1891).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; arrived, 17th Dec., 1919, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1925; confd., Dec., 1931; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1927, to Nov., 1933.

NEVILL, HENRY RIVERS, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th May, 1876).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived,

21st Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Provs. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; engaged in revision of district gazetteers from Aug., 1901, to Oct., 1909; jt. mag., April, 1911; on mil. service from Nov., 1917; dep. commr. (seconded), July, 1918; O.B.E., June, 1919; C.I.E., June, 1921; magte. and collr. and record officer, Nov., 1923; offg. commr. (addl.), March, 1925; commr. (provl. sub.), Apl., 1929; confd., July, 1930; author of a number of district gazetteers; ret'd., June, 1932.

NEVILLE, COLLIN MAXWELL WRIGHT, Indian Police (suft., Bihar) (b. 19th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 1st May, 1921; supt., April, 1926.

NEWCOMB, CLIVE, C.I.E., M.D., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.); office of high commr. for India, (examiner of medl. stores) (b. 3rd Oct., 1882).—First commn. dated 30th July, 1910; in mil. employ. to 23rd June, 1914, and again from 13th Aug., 1914, to 22nd Feb., 1920; offg. chemical examiner, Lahore, Feb. to Nov., 1920; prof. of chemistry, Madras, Dec., 1920; actg. princpl., medl. coll., Madras, May, 1932; offg. surg.-genl. with the govt. of Madras, Oct., 1935; and again Apl. 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; ret'd., Oct., 1937; apptd. examr. of medl. stores, office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1937.

NEWLAND, BRODERICK EDWARD MIDDLETON, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 19th Aug., 1878).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1905; in mil. employ. to 15th July, 1913; civil surg., Nov., 1920; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

NEWMAN, CARL DAMIEN, M.B., B.S., Indian State Ryls. (b. 13th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service as dist. medl. officer, E.B. Ry., 13th Feb., 1893; offg. ch. medl. officer variously from June, 1931.

NEWMAN, FELIX LAWRENCE, M.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 12th May, 1879).—Joined the service, 21st March, 1907, and held non-gazetted appts. up to 21st Oct., 1914; dep. supt., Oct., 1914; M.B.E., June, 1927; supt., July, 1927; ret'd., June, 1934.

NEWMAN, HAROLD LANCELOT, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 5th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service, 13th Nov., 1901, as asst. consr. of forests, Bombay; dep. consr. of forests, Jan., 1907; consr., June, 1922; offg. ch. consvr., Feb., 1928; confd., Aug., 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

NEWSAM, WILLIAM OSWALD, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 13th March, 1892).—Educ. at Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 10th Dec., 1915, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty from Nov., 1917, to March, 1919; sub-collr., Oct., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1926; offg. regr., high court, July, 1932; dist. and sess. judge, Chittoor, and addtl. judge, court of Resdnt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1935; ditto, Mysore, Jan., 1936-July, 1937.

NEWTON, BERNARD ST. JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 13th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1913; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; ditto and under-sec. P.W.D., Nov., 1925; offg. suptg. engr., Apr., 1931.

NEWTON, EDWARD BERNARD, late Indo-European Tel. Dept. (b. 5th June, 1881).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st Sept., 1898, to 28th Feb., 1918; asst. supt., March, 1918; supt. April, 1923; asst. dir., Nov., 1925; offg. dir., Persian section, Dec., 1930; retd., Sept., 1931.

NIAMAT ULLAH, CHAUDHRI, late Judl. dept., United Provs.—Apptd. addl. judge, Allahabad high ct., Nov., 1928; judge, ditto, Aug., 1932; retd., Dec., 1937.

NIAZ MUHAMMAD KHAN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (jt. magte. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 12th June, 1907).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte. and dep. collr., May, 1933; under secty. fin. dept., Apl., 1937; K.I.H. Medal, 1938.

NICHOLL, REV. ESMOND MCKILLOP, M.A., M.C., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 16th Mar., 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 19th Nov., 1919; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1921; senr. chaplain, June, 1930.

NICHOLLS, WILLIAM HENRY, F.R.I.B.A., late Madras Public Wks. Dept. (b. 5th July, 1875).—Educ. at Rugby, and Hertford Coll. Oxford, and trained under Sir Thos. G. Jackson, Bart., R.A., architect; apptd. archaeological surveyor, United Prov. and Punjab circle, 1904; consulting architect to the govt. of Madras, July, 1907; on deputn. as architect member, Imperial Delhi Committee, Delhi Prov., May 1913, to April, 1917; retd., July, 1930.

NICHOLS, HAROLD LACY, late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd April, 1884).—Educ. at Manchester Grammar Sch., and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 7th Dec., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Dec., 1914; on mil. duty from Oct., 1917, to Oct., 1919; liquidator of hostile firms in Burma, May, 1921; offg. depy. commr., June, 1921; dep. commr., Jan., 1924; dep. sec., home and pol. dept., Dec., 1926; secty. to govt., judl. dept., May, 1927; secty., rev. dept., June, 1930; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Burma, May, 1932; offg. commr., June, 1933; confd., Dec., 1935; retd., Oct., 1938.

NICHOLSON, JOHN PATRICK, United Provs., C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. 14th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 22nd June, 1907; dep. collr., April, 1911; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1931; confd., Sept., 1934.

NICHOLSON, JOHN WILFRED, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Orissa) (b. 13th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 3rd March, 1916; provl. sylviculturist, Dec., 1921; on deputation to Kenya for 3 years from Sept., 1927; dep. consvr., Feb., 1931; offg. consvr. variously from Sept., 1931.

NICHOLSON, MARK ALLEYNE, M.B., I.t.-Col., I.M.S. (C.M.O., Central India) (b. 10th June, 1885).—First commn. dated 31st July, 1909; civil surg., Kurram, Oct., 1922; M.O., S.W. Scouts, Dec., 1923; offg. resdy. surg. in Persian Gulf, Sept., 1924; agency surg., Hazara dist., Oct., 1928; ditto, and C.M.O., Rajputana, Mar., 1931; C.M.O., Central India, and resdy. surgeon, Indore, Apl., 1932.

NICOL, CAMERON MACDONALD, M.A., M.D., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Dept. of Public Health, Punjab (dir.) (b. 20th July, 1891).—First commn., 9th Feb., 1923; asst. dir. of public health, Rawalpindi, 18th Feb., 1934; dir. of public health, Aug., 1936.

NICOLAS, FRANCIS CARMINOWE, Lieut.-Col., I.A. (retd.) (b. 16th Nov., 1874).—First commission 30th Aug., 1893; in mil. employ to Oct., 1902, when he was apptd. asst. commr., Punjab; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1915, and Nov., 1919; dist. and sessions judge, Jan., 1923; retd., Nov., 1929.

NICOLLS, REGINALD NOEL, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 29th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 1st July, 1908; on milty. duty from 10th May, 1916; dist. traffic supt., N.W. Rly., April, 1918; on depn. to D.H. Rly., 31st Mar., 1928; retd., Sept., 1931.

NICOLSON, JOHN FRANCIS HUNTER, M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 28th Sept., 1894).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., April, 1920; offg. exec. engr., June, 1925; confd., Oct., 1927; ch. public works officer, F.S.S., Apl., 1935.

NIGAM, BISHAN NARAIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 4th Apl., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.

NIGAM, JAGDISHWAR (see Jagdishwar Nigam)

NIGEL JONES, M. E. (see Jones, M. E. Nigel).

NIGHTINGALE, ARTHUR WINFIELD, Indian Service of Engrs. (supg. engr., Madras) (b. 18th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1908; asst. engr.; on mil. duty from April, 1915, to Dec., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; supg. engr., Sept., 1936.

NILAKANTA AYYAR, MAHADEVA, RAO BAHADUR, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th Dec., 1878).—Joined the service, 5th Feb., 1902; asst. engr., Feb., 1903; exeg. engr., Feb., 1916; offg. supg. engr., May, 1926; confd., Apl., 1930; retd., Dec., 1933.

NILAKANTA MAHADEVA AYYAR, M.A. (Mad.), Indian C.S. (dep. secty., dept. of labour, govt. of India) (b. 14th May, 1899).—Educ. at Madras University and Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived in India, 2nd Jan., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and sub. divl. officer, June, 1924; at disposal of govt. of India, fin. dep. (cent. rev.), March, 1925, as offg. asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta; confd., Dec., 1925; asst. collr. of customs, Madras, June, 1926; offg. magte. and collr., Bengal, July, 1928; and again, Mar., 1932; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Nov., 1928; offg. ditto, Bengal, Oct., 1930; mag. and collr., July, 1935; addl. dep. secty., dept. of indus. and lab., govt. of India, Apl., 1936; dep. secty., dept. of labour, Nov., 1937.

NILKANTH SHIRAM JATAR (see Jatar, N. S.).

NIL RATAN DHAR, M.Sc. (Cal.), D.Sc. (Lon.), F.I.C., F.C.S., Indian Educl. Service (b. 2nd Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as prof., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, 19th July, 1919; with Allahabad Univ. from July, 1922.

NIND, WILLIAM WALKER, C.I.E., B.A., late Impl. Customs Service (b. 26th Sept., 1882).—Arrived, 29th Jan., 1906, and served as prob. and asst. collr. in Burma; ditto and supt. preventive service, May, 1913; member of the commission for Port of Chittagong, March, 1915; British delegate to Anglo-Afghan Trade Conference, Aug., 1922, to Feb., 1923; offg. collr. of customs, Calcutta, Feb., 1923; asst. coll., do., Oct., 1924; asst. collr. of customs, Bombay, Oct., 1925; offg. collr. of customs, Bombay, Feb., 1926; ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1926; ditto, Rangoon, Nov., 1927; confd., Nov., 1929; collr., Bombay, Oct., 1932; temp. addl. member, central bd. of rev., Jan., 1934; offg. jt. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., June, 1934; offg. member, central bd. of rev., Apl., 1935; and again, June, 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

NINNIS, ROBERT DOUGLAS KIALLMARK, Indian Police (sup., Orissa) (b. 30th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th Mar., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1926; confd., Sept., 1929; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931.

NIRANJAN DAS, GULHATI, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 15th Nov., 1904).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., Oct., 1926; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1927; exec. engr., Oct., 1936.

NIRINJAN PRASAD, GOVAL, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 30th Apl., 1903).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 28th Oct., 1905; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

NIVAS, HARI KRISHNA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 14th Nov., 1928; exec. engr., Oct., 1937.

NIX-JAMES, EDWARD WILLIAM, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 22nd Mar., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 28th March, 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1927.

NIX-SEAMAN, Rev. A. J. (see Seaman).

NIXON, ARTHUR BAMFYLDE, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 6th Dec., 1885).—Arrived, 19th Dec., 1910, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1915; personal asst. to consvr., June, 1919; ret'd., June, 1933.

NIXON, Sir JOHN CARSON, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (secty. fin. dept., Govt. of India) (b. 21st Sept., 1887).—Educ. at Technical Institute, Portsmouth, Royal Coll. of Science, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 29th Nov., 1912, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; services placed at disposal of Indian munitions board, March to Dec., 1918; under-sec., govt. of Bengal, fin. dept., Dec., 1918; under-sec., comm. and ind. dept., govt. of India, July, 1919; under-sec., finance dept., govt. of India, April, 1920; dep. accent.-gen., Bengal, April, 1921; dep. contr. of currency, Bombay, Oct., 1923; offg. dep. contr. of currency, Madras, April, 1925; acct.-genl. Madras, Jan., 1926; ditto, Bombay, Nov.,

1928; on spl. duty, fin. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1930; temp. jt. secty., ditto, July, 1931; C.I.E., June, 1932; dir., rly. audit, Nov., 1932; acct.-genl., Bengal, Nov., 1933; addl.-secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept. (temp.), Mar., 1935; member, council of state, Apl., 1935; secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Oct., 1935; C.S.I., June, 1936; offg. member, govr.-gen'l's exec. council, Apl., 1937; K.C.I.E., June, 1938.

NIYOGI, MACHRAJ BHAWANI SHANKAR, C.I.E., M.A., LL.M., Judicial Dept., Central Provs. (judge, high ct., Nagpur) (b. 30th Aug., 1886).—Apptd. offg. addtl. judl. commr., June, 1930; confd., Sept., 1930; judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1936.

NIZAM-UD-DIN, KHAN BAHADUR, Indian State Railways (exec. engr., N.W.Rly.) (b. 3rd July, 1893).—Asst. engr., 17th Sept., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1922; exec. engr., Aug., 1935.

NOAD, CHARLES HUMPHREY CARDEN, B.A., Barr.-at-Law, late Judl. Dept., Punjab (b. 25th Jan., 1880).—Apptd. asst. legal membr., Mar., 1922; admr.-genl. and offl. trustee, Nov., 1922; govt. advocate (provl.), Oct., 1926; confd., Feb., 1930; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

NOBLE, Rev. REGINALD HARRY, M.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 11th Nov., 1928; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1936.

NOEL, EDWARD WILLIAM CHARLES, C.I.E., D.S.O., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 14th Apl., 1886).—First commn., 21st Dec., 1904; in mil. employ till Dec., 1912, when he was apptd. to pol. dept. on probation; H.M.'s Vice-Consul, Ahwaz, April, 1915; confd. as pol. officer, Feb., 1916; on special duty in the Caucasus, Jan. to Oct., 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; served under High Commr. for Iraq, Nov., 1918, to Jan., 1920; pol. agent, Kurram, April, 1924; consul for Kerman and Persian Baluchistan, July, 1929; dep. commr., Dehra Ismail Khan, May, 1931; dir. of agric. and allied depts., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1933; dep. commr., Mardam, Apl., 1938.

NOON, Sir FIROZ KHAN, K.C.I.E. (high commr. for India).—Educ. Chief's Coll., Lahore, and Wadham Coll., Oxford; advocate of Lahore high ct., and member Punjab legisl. council from 1921; minister for local self-govt., Punjab, 1927; educn. Minister, Punjab, 1930; high commr. for India, 1st July, 1936; K.C.I.E., May, 1937.

NORBURY, HARRY CARTER, J.P.M.Inst.T., Indian Railway Accts. Service (chief accts. offcr.) (b. 18th Oct., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. auditor and acct., G.I.P.Rly., 2nd Oct., 1913; dep. chief auditor, G.I.P.Rly., Mar., 1924; confd. as dep. agent, Oct., 1926; served under Finl. Commr. Rlys. from Oct., 1929; dep. chief accts. offcr., G.I.P.Rly., Oct., 1929; confd. as chief accts. offcr., G.I.P.Rly., Mar., 1930.

NORMAN, CHARLES ARCHIBALD KENSIT, B.A., India Office (asst. solicitor) (b. 12th Mar., 1884).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; solicitor, Supreme Court, 1909; served on the legal staff, Public Trustee's Office, and H.M. Customs and Excise, Jan., 1910, to Feb., 1914; apptd. clerk, legal adviser's dept., 2nd Feby., 1914; asst. solicitor; Aug., 1933.

NORMAN, DOUGLAS REAY, M.A., Indian C.S. (judi. commr., states of W.I.) (b. 30th Aprl., 1890).—Educ. at Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 28th Nov., 1914, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; on mil. duty from July, 1916, to Dec., 1918; asst. membr. of legal affairs and sec. to Bombay legislative council, May, 1921; offg. judge and sess. judge, March, 1923; confd., April, 1929; judi. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Dec., 1932; ditto, states of western India and Gujerat states, Aprl., 1933; and again, Oct., 1935.

NORMAN, WILLIAM JAMES, M.C., Major R.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 18th Aprl., 1894).—Joined the service, 18th July, 1913; in milty. employ to 10th Aug., 1921; asst. supt., 11th Aug., 1921; offg. dep. supt., Nov., 1924; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; confd., May, 1929.

NORMAN-WALKER, J. N. (see Walker, J. N.).

NORMAND, CHARLES WILLIAM BLYTH, C.I.E., M.A., D.Sc., Indian Meteorological Dept. (dir. genl. of observatories) (b. 10th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1913, as impl. meteorologist; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), Feb., 1916, to Aprl., 1919; offg. dir.-gen. of observatories from June, 1924; confd., Mar., 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

NORONHA, MARK STANISLAUS, B.A., LL.B., Judicial dept., Bombay (b. 19th July, 1889).—Apptd. judge, small cause ct., Bombay, 30th Dec., 1928.

NORRIS, ERIC MORTIMER, late Indo-European Telegraph Dept. (b. 11th Dec., 1875).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 15th May, 1894, to 14th Sept., 1913; offg. asst. supt., Sept., 1913; confd., May, 1915; supt., Jan., 1919; asst. dir., Oct., 1924; dir., Persian section, Oct., 1929; retd., Dec., 1930.

NOKKIS, JAMES CECIL, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 6th Aprl., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, 22nd Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., Aprl., 1931; retd., June, 1933.

NORTHEY, HENRY STEWART, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Madras) (b. 24th July, 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906, as asst. engr.; on mil. duty, Sept., 1916, to Feb., 1920; exec. engr., March, 1920; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1929; confd., Dec., 1933.

NORTON, ERNEST LINGHAM, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th Sept., 1883).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 30th Nov., 1908, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; asst. censor, Bombay, Dec., 1914; jt. mag., Aprl., 1917; serving under pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, from Sept., 1917, to March, 1919; on special duty in civil adminstrn. of Mesopotamia from March, 1920, to Jan., 1922; jt. mag., offg. first addl. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1923; confirmed, Aug., 1924; offg. legal remembr. to govt., Aprl., 1926; retd., Aug., 1933.

NOTTAGE, JOHN BOWTELL, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th Feb., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1913; on mil. service from 3rd Oct., 1917, to 7th Aug., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; offg. supt. engr., May, 1932.

NOTT-BOWER, JOHN REGINALD HORNEY, C.V.O., late Indian Police (b. 16th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1911; supt., Mar., 1924; awarded King's police medal, June, 1931; on deputation in Public and Judl. dept., India Office, Jan., 1932; retd., June, 1934; C.V.O., July, 1937.

NOYCE, SIR FRANK, K.C.S.I., C.B.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th June, 1878).—Educ. at Salisbury Sch., and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 1st Dec., 1902, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spl. asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1906, to March, 1908; asst. sec., March, 1909; under-sec., dept. of chief sec., May, 1910; sub-collr. and jt. mag., June, 1911; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agric., Dec., 1912; confirmed, March, 1913; offg. sec., dept. of rev. and agric., Oct. 1915, to Jan., 1916; temply. employed in store dept., India Office, from July to Aug., 1916; on spl. duty under govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agriculture, as sec. cotton commee, July, 1917; ditto under comm. and ind. dept. as controller of cotton cloth, Sept., 1918; ditto under rev. and agric. dept. as vice-presdt., and later presdt., Indian sugar cttee., Oct., 1919; C.B.E., Dec., 1919; ditto under govt. of Burma as member Burma Land Revenue Cttee., Dec., 1920; Indian trade commr. in England, July, 1922, to March, 1923; offg. sec. to govt. of Madras, development dept., May, 1923; C.S.I., June, 1924; presdt., Indian Coal Cttee., Oct., 1924, to March, 1925; offg. commr. of labour, Nov., 1925; offg. sec. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Jan., 1926; member, tariff board, govt. of India, commerce dept., Aprl., 1926; on spl. duty, dept. of educn., govt. of India, Dec., 1928; Kt., June, 1929; secty., ditto, July, 1929; member, govr. genl.'s exec. council, Aprl., 1932; K.C.S.I., June, 1934; retd., Nov., 1937.

NUNAN, DR. WILLIAM, B.A., M.D., B.Ch. (Dub.), T.C.D., late Medical dept., Bombay (b. 26th Jan., 1880).—Apptd. police surg., &c., and profr. of medl. jurisprudence, Bombay, 1st Aprl., 1920; retd., Jan., 1935.

NUNN, ALBERT JAMES, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 18th Mar., 1884).—Educ. at City of Westminster Sch.; served as 2nd div. clerk, bd. of educn., March, 1903, and bd. of trade, May, 1904; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Office, 20th May, 1905; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, Aprl., 1922.

NUR ILAHI, KHAN BAHADUR, Shaikh, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (princpl., Central Training Coll., Lahore) (b. 8th July, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. prof., govt. coll., Lahore, 4th Oct., 1905; offg. inspr. of schools, Feb., 1921; confd., Oct., 1925; asst. dir. of public instn., Punjab, Aug., 1931; princpl., Central Training Coll., Lahore, Oct., 1935.

NUR, KHWAJA MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Nur, Khwaja).

NUR MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR Shaikh, M.A., Punjab C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 26th Aprl., 1886).—Joined the service as extra asst. commr. on probn., 31st Mar., 1910; confd., Aprl., 1913; waterlogging asst. to finl. commrs., Punjab, Oct., 1928; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1931; confd., July, 1933.

NUTT, HAROLD ROTHERY, M.D., F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S., retd. (b. 28th Dec., 1876).—First commn. 27th June, 1901; in mil. employ till Sept., 1909, when he was apptd. dep. sanitary commr., United Provs.; civil surgeon, Dec., 1912; on mil. service from Oct., 1914, to Oct., 1915, and from Sept., 1915, to April, 1920; proftr. and supt., King George's Hosp., Lucknow, Jan., 1924; inspr.-genl. of civil hosps., Apl., 1930; offg. surg.-genl. with govt. of Bombay, Mar., 1933; confd., July, 1933; hon. surg. to the King, Feb., 1934; retd., Aug., 1935.

NWE, U NAN, B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 29th Feb., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, 22nd Dec., 1926; retd., Jan., 1934.

NYUN, MAUNG, B.A., Indian C.S. (under secty. com. and indus. dept. govt. of Burma) (b. 30th Dec., 1909).—Educ. at A.V. High School, Thaton, Rangoon University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Dec., 1933, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty. com. and indus. dept., Burma, Mar., 1937.

NYUN, MAUNG SOE, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 6th Mar., 1906).—Educ. at Calcutta University, Rangoon University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 3rd Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

OAK, VIDYADHAR GOVIND, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 18th May, 1909).—Educ. at Allahabad University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provinces as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte., Nov., 1934.

OAKDEN, SIR RALPH, Kt., C.S.I., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 13th July, 1871).—Educ. at Cranbrook Gr. Sch., and at Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 16th Dec., 1895; and served in N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., March, 1904; supt., Dehra Dun, Nov., 1910; mag. and collr., Jan., 1914; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; commr., Nov., 1923; and pol. officer in charge of ex-Amir of Kabul (in addn.), April, 1924; C.S.I., Jan., 1925; offg. member, board of revenue, April, 1927; confd., Nov., 1928; retd., Dec., 1930; Kt., Jan., 1931.

OAKLEY FRANK STUART, Indian Police (b. 8th Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 22nd Nov., 1923; offg. dist. supt., 1927.

OATFEN, EDWARD FARLEY, M.A., LL.B., late Indian Educl. Service (Bengal) (b. 24th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service, 20th Oct., 1909, as proftr., Presy. Coll., Calcutta; on mil. service, Sept., 1916, to March, 1919; asst. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, Feb., 1921; offg. dir. of public instrn., Feb., 1924; confd., June, 1924; retd., July, 1930.

OBADULLAH, ABU ZAFAR, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 28th Feb., 1911).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 1st July, 1931; confd., Sept., 1933.

O'BRIEN, CHARLES RICHARD, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., retd. (b. 4th Apl., 1879).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1906; arrived, 26th Sept., 1906; in mil. employ to Oct., 1908; civil surgn., Sept., 1910; retd., Apl., 1934.

O'BRIEN, J. A., Civil Aviation Directorate (chief inspector of aircraft) (b. 1st Feb., 1881).—Joined the service as chief inspr. of aircraft (temp.), Nov., 1936.

O'BRIEN, JOSEPH, O.B.E., B.A., late Indian Fin. Dept. (b. 7th Apl., 1879).—Joined the service as asst. examr., p.w. accts., 5th Feb., 1903; dep. examr., p.w. accts., 5th Feb., 1903; dep. examr., Jan., 1906; asst. acctnt.-gen., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1909; govt. examr. of accts., R. and K. Ry., Feb., 1911; audit offr., Delhi Prov., May, 1913; on mil. service, Sept., 1914, to April, 1920; O.B.E., June, 1919; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, April, 1921; audit officer, Lloyd barrage and canals construction, Oct., 1923; offg. dep. finl. adviser ditto, Dec., 1926; retd., Apl., 1934.

O'BRIEN, MICHAEL, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 1st May, 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1914; offg. supt. engr., May, 1930; confd., Jan., 1934; chief engr., Madras, June, 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; retd., May, 1938.

O'BRIEN-SMITH, EDWARD QUINTON, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 20th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

O'CALLAGHAN, HARRY BIRCHELL HONNER, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 9th Jan., 1874).—Joined the service, 26th May, 1896; asst. audit officer, June, 1918; asst. acctnt.-gen., Rlys., Feb., 1919; dep. ditto, Oct., 1923; auditor, J.B. Rly., April, 1924; asst. auditor, N.W. Rly., Feb., 1927; offg. dep. ch. accts. officer, E.I. Rly., March, 1927; offg. dep. auditor, N.W. Rly., May, 1928; dep. ch. accts. officer, Lahore, Apl., 1929; retd. Mar., 1930.

O CALLAGHAN, TIMOTHY PATRICK MORIARTY, C.I.E., late Indian Police, Assam (b. 25th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 8th Dec., 1906; in mil. employ from 16th Apl., 1915, to 14th Mar., 1919; supt., Apl., 1917; inspr.-genl., Sept., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1933; retd., July, 1937.

O'CONNOR, ARCHIBALD JOHN, late Indian Police (b. 19th Mar., 1881).—Joined the service, 19th March, 1908; I.A.R.O., from 5th March, 1913, to 19th Jan., 1923; supt., May, 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1927; retd., Feb., 1936.

O'CONNOR, RODRICK AUGUSTUS, late India Fin. Dept. (b. 19th July, 1878).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1899, as asst. examr., P.W. accts.; dep. examr., Jan., 1905; asst. acctnt.-gen., post office and telegraphs, March, 1914; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, April, 1914; on depn. to mil. accts. dept., May, 1915, to March, 1919; dep. auditor, E.B. Rly., March, 1919; dep. auditor, N.W. Rly., Oct., 1923; offg. chief auditor, O. & R. Rly., June, 1924; offg. dep. auditor-gen., Rlys. (inspn.), Nov., 1925; offg. ch. auditor, G.I.P. Rly., Dec., 1926; ch. auditor, N.W. Rly.; retd., Jan., 1932.

O'CONNOR, Sir WILLIAM FREDERICK TRAVERS, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., C.V.O., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (ret'd.).—1st commn., dated 14th Feb., 1890; in mil. emp. to Aug., 1899, when he was apptd. special asst. to pol. agent, Gilgit; intell. offr., Tibet Frontier Commn., June, 1903; C.I.E., Dec., 1904; Br. trade agent, Gyantse, April, 1905; asst. sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., April, 1908; H.B.M.'s consul for Seistan and Kain, Sept., 1909; H.B.M.'s consul-gen. and agent to govt. of India in Khorasan, April, 1910; H.B.M.'s consul for Seistan and Kain, Dec., 1910; H.B.M.'s consul for Kerman and Persian Baluchistan, Oct., 1912; H.B.M.'s consul at Shiraz, Nov., 1912; resdt. in Nepal, Dec., 1918; pol. officer in Sikkim, Jan. 1921; Brit. envoy at court of Nepal, Oct., 1921; C.V.O., June, 1922; C.S.I., Jan. 1924; Kt., June, 1925; ret'd., July, 1925.

ODDIN-TAYLOR, HARRY WILLOUGHBY, C.B.E., A.C.G.I., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 12th July, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; resigned the service from 26th Sept., 1920; re-apptd. as exec. engr., 7th Dec., 1926; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1931; O.B.E., Jan., 1933; jt. secty. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, in the public works dept., Jan., 1936; C.B.E., May, 1937.

ODELL, FRANCIS DAVID, M.A., Indian Agric. Service (b. 20th Feb., 1801).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, 2nd Dec., 1922.

ODGERS, SIR CHARLES EDWIN, Kt., Barr., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 17th Jan., 1870).—Apptd. junr. prof'r., Law Coll., Madras, 26th Aug., 1902; admr.-gen. and official trustee, Madras, Oct., 1911; acting judge high ct., Madras, July, 1919; judge, high ct., Madras, March, 1921; Kt., Jan., 1930; ret'd., Jan., 1930.

ODLING, RICHARD EARNEST, Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 14th June, 1886).—Joined the service as mil. acct. 2nd Cl., July, 1920; dep. finl. advr., R.A.F., May, 1933; D.M.A.G. (senr.), Oct., 1934.

O'DONNELL, Sir SAMUEL PERRY K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 16th Dec., 1874).—Educ. at Repton, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1903; joint mag., Dec., 1906; under-sec. to govt., Nov., 1907; asst. commr., Nov., 1909; regr., high court, N.W. Prov., April, 1910; dep. commr. and sec. to govt., April, 1912; on special duty in fin. dept. sectt., govt. of India, from April to June, 1914; mag. and collr., and sec. to govt., May, 1915; actg.-chief sec. to govt., Nov., 1917; confd., Feb., 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1919; sec. to govt. of India, reforms office, Jan., 1920; sec. to govt. of India, home dept., March, 1921; member of govt.'s exec. council, United Provs., 4th Jan., 1923; C.S.I., Jan., 1923; K.C.I.E., June, 1925; actg. govt., Aug., 1926; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1928; ret'd., May, 1930.

O'DOWD, GERALD RONEY, B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b 1st Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as mil. acct., 2nd Cl., Dec., 1909; confd., Cl. I offcr., June, 1930; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Oct., 1930; confd. as cmd. contr., Oct., 1935.

O'DRISCOLL, MICHAEL JAMES, late Indian Police Service (b. 6th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 25th Nov., 1906; supt., Oct., 1919; supt., watch and ward, G.I.P. Rly., Nov., 1925, to June, 1930; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

O'DWYER, Sir MICHAEL FRANCIS, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at St. Stanislaus Coll., Tullamore, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after open exam. of 1882; arrived, 19th Nov., 1885, and was apptd. asst. commr. in the Punjab; settlt. collr., 1889-94; dir. of dept. of land records and agric., Punjab, Dec., 1896; settlt. officer for Ulwar and Bhurtpore, July, 1897; special duty in connection with the scheme of the new frontier, April, 1901; dep. commr., July, 1901; rev. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901; resident and ditto, Feb., 1906; resident, Hyderabad, May, 1908; C.S.I., June, 1908; resident, Indore, Aug., 1910; lieut.-govr., Punjab, 26th May, 1913; K.C.S.I., June, 1913; G.C.I.E., June, 1917; ret'd., 1st Oct., 1920; member of commee. on Indian Army administration and organization, 1919.

O'FLYNN, ERIC MORTIMER, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 28th Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, 11th Apl., 1930; confd., Mar., 1933.

OGILVIE, CHARLES MACIVOR GRANT, C.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt. of India, defence dept.) (b. 6th May, 1891).—Educ. at Bedford Grammar Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 4th Dec., 1915, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. administrator, Nabha State, July, 1923; asst. ditto, Sept., 1923; offg. dep. commr., May, 1925; provl., ditto, June, 1926; senr. secty. to finl. commrs., April, 1928; C.B.E., June, 1928; home secty. to govt., Punjab, May, 1929; and again, June, 1933; addl. secty. to govt., Punjab, fin. dept., Sept., 1933; offg. secty., ditto, Oct., 1933; confd., Oct., 1934; on depn. for course at Impl. Defence Coll., Jan., 1936; secty. to govt. of India, defence dept., Apl., 1937.

OGILVIE, Sir GEORGE DRUMMOND, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Lt.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Service (b. 18th Feb., 1882).—First commn. dated 28th July, 1900; supy. apptd. asst. commr., Delhi, Feb., 1905; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Dec., 1905; vice-consul, Bam, June, 1906; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Jan., 1908; asst. pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Feb., 1908; first asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Dec., 1910; asst. pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Jan. to April, 1911; boundary settlt. commr. in Central India, April, 1911, to June, 1912; second asst. to resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1913; asst. sec. to govt. of India, Army Dept., J.n., 1915; dep. sec. (political) to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Jan., 1919; sec. to agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, Nov., 1921, and again in Nov., 1922; resident in Mewar, March, 1922; dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Feb., 1923; resident in Mewar, Nov., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; secty., Indian States commee., Jan., 1928; offg. resdt., Kashmir, Apl., 1929; offg. A.G.G. in C.I., Sept., 1931; C.S.I., Jan.,

1932; A.G.G. in Rajputana and ch. commr. Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1932; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1936; resdt. for Rajputana, Apr., 1937; ret'd., Oct., 1937.

OGILVIE, GEORGE HAMILTON, M.C., B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 20th Feb., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 2nd Dec., 1912; on mill. service from 1st Dec., 1914, to 7th May, 1920; dep. consvr., Dec., 1917; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

OGLE, JOHN WILFRED, Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic sup't., 25th March, 1913; asst. dist. traffic sup't., Jan., 1922; divl. transportation officer, July, 1925; publicity officer, Mar., 1931.

O'GORMAN, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Indian Police (sup't., Bombay) (b. 8th July, 1891).—Joined the service, 2nd July, 1913, as asst. sup't.; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924; sup't. of police, Jan., 1925; awarded bar to King's police medal, Jan., 1930; cent. intell. offcr., Bombay Prov., Aug., 1938.

O'GORMAN, MICHAEL THOMAS, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 15th Jan., 1881).—Joined the service, 16th March, 1899; asst. sup't., Oct., 1919; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1923; offg. sup't., July, 1923; confd., July, 1927; O.B.E., Jan., 1935; ret'd., Jan., 1936.

OHN, MAUNG, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 16th October, 1909).—Educ. at Government High Schools, Thaton and Rangoon, Rangoon University and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Jan., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

OJHA, RADHESHYAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 1st Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Patna Univ.; apptd. on probn., 4th Oct., 1937; confd., 27th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

OKA, DATTATRAYA SITARAM, LL.B., late Bombay Civil Service (Judicial Branch) (b. 20th June, 1883).—Held non-gazetted temp. appt. from 17th Sept., 1923, to 8th Jan., 1925; asst. judge and asst. sess. judge, Jan., 1925; offg. judge and sess. judge, Apr., 1928; ret'd., June, 1938.

OLIVER, HENRY ARTHUR, Indian Police (b. 14th Apr., 1916).—Apptd. asst. sup't. Punjab, on probn., Dec., 1937.

OLIVIER, OF RAMSDEN, 1st Baron, Rt. Hon. SYDNEY HALDANE OLIVIER, P.C., K.C.M.G., C.B., B.A., LL.D.—Secty. of State for India, 23rd Jan., to 6th Nov., 1924.

OLPHERTS-FORRESTER, SHIRLEY WYBRANDTS GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 2nd Sept., 1905).—Educ. at University of Allahabad; University of Nagpur; University Coll., London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, 15th Nov., 1929, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.

OLVER, Sir ARTHUR, C.B., F.R.C.V.S., Col., R.A.V.C., ret'd., Impl. Council of Agricl. Research Dept.—Apptd. animal husbandry expert, 18th Apr., 1930; knighted, May, 1937.

OLVER, STEPHEN JOHN LINLEY, Indian Police (b. 16th June, 1916).—Apptd. asst. sup't., United Provs., on probn., 1st Nov., 1935.

OMAR, ZAFAR (see Zaiar Omar).

OMMANNEY, DOUGLAS GRÆME, late Indian Police Service (Bombay) (b. 19th April, 1874).—Apptd. police prob'r., 17th Aug., 1893; asst. sup't., Oct., 1895; sup't., May, 1903; recd. King's police medal, Dec., 1911; dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1922; ret'd., April, 1929.

OMMANNEY, FREDERICK ROY, Indian Police (sup't., Sind) (b. 12th Sept., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. sup't., Bombay, 15th Dec., 1922; offg. sup't., Mar., 1926; confd., Jan., 1933.

O'NEILL, JOHN CONNELL, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., N.W. rly.) (b. 27th May, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1911; exec. engr., Jan., 1922; services lent to Burma Rlys., from 2nd Dec., 1922; offg. divl. sup't., N.W. rly., July, 1934; dep. chief engr. (prov. perm.), Feb., 1938.

O'NEILL, NIALL O'HAGAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., C.P.) (b. 14th Nov., 1914).—Educ. at Beaumont and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the C.P. as asst. commr.

O'NEILL, PATRICK LAURENCE, C.I.E., Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 28th Feb., 1876).—Joined the service, 27th June, 1901; C.I.E., June, 1919; civil surg., Dec., 1923; offg. D.M.O. and M.O., central jail, Salem, Nov., 1925; inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Burma, June, 1930; ret'd., Feb., 1933.

O'NEILL, PATRICK LAWRENCE, Major, I.M.S. (b. 5th June, 1904).—Date of first commn., 30th Apr., 1928; mill. employ till Apr., 1936; confd. as civil surg., Cuttack, Apr., 1937.

O'NEIL SHAW, JOHN CHARLES LIDDELL, Indian Police (sup't., N.W.F.P.) (b. 5th Mar., 1896).—Joined the service, 5th May, 1920; asst. sup't., Feb., 1922; offg. sup't., March, 1925; confd., Aug., 1926.

O'NEILL, Rev. WILFRED STEPHEN, M.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 23rd Nov., 1886).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Lahore, 24th March, 1927; ditto, N.W.F.P., Apr., 1927; senr. chaplain, Mar., 1937.

OPPENHEIM, ERNEST FERDINAND, C.I.E., B.A., Barr. (1899), late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd July, 1875).—Educ. at Rugby Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 5th Dec., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; jt. mag., May, 1908; dep. commr., Nov., 1914; mag. and collr., Oct., 1918; offg. commr., April, 1925; confd., Jan., 1928; member, bd. of rev., Dec., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Dec., 1934.

ORAM, ARTHUR, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (sup'tg. engr., N.W.F.P.) (b. 20th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; under secty. to govt. P.W.D. (irrigation), May, 1926; offg. sup'tg. engr., Nov., 1927; sup'tg. engr. (provl. perm.), July, 1935.

ORANGE, SIR HUGH WILLIAM, K.B.E., C.B., C.I.E.—Apptd. director-gen. of education in India, 6th Mar., 1902; retd., 30th Nov., 1910; author of 'Review of the Progress of Education in India, 1902-7'.

ORDE, PERCY LANCELOT, C.I.E., Indian Police (inspr. genl., Punjab) (b. 19th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1907; asst. supt., April, 1908; supt., July, 1916; C.I.E., June, 1927; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., May, 1928; confd. (provl. subst.), May, 1930; confd., July, 1932; offg. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1935; inspr.-genl., and jt. secty. to govt. Punjab, home dept., confd., Sept., 1936; King's Police Medal, Feb., 1937.

O'REILLY, JOSEPH JAMES, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 12th Feb., 1875).—Joined the service, 25th Jan., 1903, as temp. engr.; asst. engr., June, 1915; exec. engr., Aug., 1916; retd., Aug., 1930.

O'REILLY, MICHAEL PATRICK, late Indo-European Telegraph Dept. (b. 25th Apl., 1877).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st March, 1894, to 27th July, 1917; offg. asst. supt., July, 1917; confd., Sept., 1918; supt., June, 1924; retd., Apl., 1931.

ORGILL, TYRREL CHURTON, C.I.E., M.A., F.R.G.S., Indian Educl. Service (b. 7th June, 1884).—Joined the service as hd. master, Zilla Sch., Ranchi, 1st May, 1913; asst. dir. public instrn., Oct., 1924; dep. ditto, Dec., 1926; inspr. of schools, May, 1921; offg. dir. of public instrn., N.W.F.P., April, 1928; prinpl., Ravenshaw Coll., Oct., 1929; services placed at disposal of admin. of N.W.F. province, Nov., 1931; dir. of public instrn., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1933; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

O'RIORDAN, KEVIN MORAN, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., United Provs.) (b. 6th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 25th Nov., 1906; supt., Nov., 1921; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1931; Oct., 1932; and again, Oct., 1934; confd., June, 1935; O.B.E., June, 1935.

ORMEROD, CHARLES MAURICE, Indian C.S. dist. and sess. judge, Punjab (b. 27th Aug., 1904).—Educ. at Rugby and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to govt., Punjab, l.s.g. dept. Nov., 1930; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

ORMEROD, RICHARD CATON, B.A., India Office (asst. princl., services and genl. dept.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1915).—Educ. at Winchester Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. princl., 11th Oct., 1938.

ORMISTON, GEORGE ALFRED, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (E.I.Rly.) (b. 2nd Sept., 1875).—Joined the service as asst. dist. loco. supt., 30th Jan., 1904; dist. ditto, Feb., 1910; dep. loco. supt., April, 1922; dep. ch. operating supt., Feb., 1925; retd., Dec., 1930.

ORR, ARCHIBALD, Indian State Rlys. (supt. of collieries) (b. 27th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. mining engr., 28th Apl., 1920; asst. colliery supt., June, 1929; offg. ch. mining engr., July, 1931; offg. manager, Jerangdih Colliery, Mar., 1933; confd. as supt. collieries, June, 1935.

ORR, CECIL BRUCE, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 30th Nov., 1921; offg. dist. supt., May, 1929; confd., May, 1933.

ORR, HERBERT, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th Mar., 1895).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1920; asst. engr., Dec., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Feb., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1927; retd., July, 1934.

ORTCHESON, JOHN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 6th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Perth Academy, Edinburgh University, and Christ's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., Mar., 1936.

OSMAN ALI, SHUJAT, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 1st July, 1912).—Educated at St. Joseph's College, Coonoor, Madras, University of Madras, and Brasenose College, Oxford; joined the service, 11th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr., Feb., 1938.

OSMASTON, ARTHUR EDWARD, late Indian Forest Service (b. 4th Mar., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1907, as asst. consr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1912; offg. consvr., Sept., 1925; confd., Feb., 1929; principal and prof. of forestry (temp.), forest coll., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1930; retd., Aug., 1935.

OSMASTON, BERTRAM HUTCHINSON, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 30th Mar., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., United Provs., 9th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926; offg. asst. forest economist, Dehra Dun, May, 1928; pers. asst. to forest economist, Apl., 1931.

OSMASTON, FITZ WALTER COMPLYON, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bihar) (b. 3rd July, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Feb., 1923; instr., forest research institute, April, 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

OSMASTON, GEORGE HUTCHINSON, M.C., Capt. R.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 26th Sept., 1898).—First commn., dated 26th May, 1916; in milty. employ to 30th June, 1921; proby. asst. supt., July, 1921; confd., Oct., 1925; supt., Aug., 1929.

OSS, HENRY WILLIAM, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 16th Nov., 1879).—Joined the service, 23rd Aug., 1897; offg. asst. traffic supt., Apl., 1920; confd., Jan., 1921; asst. traffic manager, Nov., 1922; transpn. supt., Oct., 1925; retd., Nov., 1936.

O'SULLIVAN, ALFRED ERNEST, late Indian Police Service (b. 28th April, 1875).—Joined the service, 28th Nov., 1895, and served in Bengal and Assam as asst. supt.; supt., Oct., 1905; and asst. to inspr.-gen. of police, crime and rlys., Sept., 1914; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1919; offg. inspr.-gen., April, 1927; confd., Aug., 1929; retd., Apl., 1930.

O'SULLIVAN, HUGH PATRICK, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 26th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 7th June, 1923; offg. supt., Mar., 1926; confd., Dec., 1933.

OTTEWILL, WILLIAM THOMAS, O.B.E., India Office (supt. of records) (b. 21st April, 1878).—Educ. at Rochester Mathematical School; clerk, 2nd class, 12th Sept., 1898; asst. to joint editors, India Office List, Oct., 1914; temp. resdt. clerk, from March, 1917, to Aug., 1920; staff clerk, Aug., 1913; joint editor, India Office List, Nov., 1921, to April, 1926; attached to Indian delegation to League of Nations, 2nd Assembly, 1921; parliamentary clerk, April, 1922; asst. sec. to Indian delegation to League of Nations, 3rd, 4th, 5th and 6th assemblies, 1922, 1923, 1924 and 1925; M.B.E., June, 1925; supt. of records, April, 1926; O.B.E., June, 1935.

OULSNAM, SAMUEL HARRISON YARDLEY, C.I.E., M.C., B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 17th Jan., 1898).—Educ. at Newcastle-under-Lyme High Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1921; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1921, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., March, 1926; offg. dep. commr., March, 1928; Sept., 1932; and again, Apl., 1933; offg. reforms offcr., Jan., 1935; dep. commr., Oct., 1937; C.I.E., May, 1937; addtl. dep. secty., dept. of educ., health and lands, May, 1938-July, 1938.

OUNG, TUN HLA, Indian Police.—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 23rd May, 1927.

OVERBECK-WRIGHT, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, M.D., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 30th May, 1879).—First commn., dated 29th Jan., 1902; in milty. employ to 28th Jan., 1909; offg. supt., lunatic asylum, Agra, March, 1909; reverted to milty. employ from 28th Nov., 1909, to 3rd March, 1910; supy. supt., central jail, Dacca, March, 1910; ditto, Agra, Dec., 1910; supt., lunatic asylum, Agra (s.p.t.), April, 1912, and again, March, 1919; in milty. employ from 30th Dec., 1914, to 3rd March, 1919; supt., mental hosp., Agra, Oct., 1925; ret'd., May, 1934.

OWDEN, JOHN SCAMBLER, Indian Forest Service (consvr.) (b. 28th Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 18th Dec., 1909; in mil. employ from 12th Nov., 1915, to 8th March, 1919; dep. consvr., March, 1919; offg. consvr., U.P., June, 1929; ditto, Assam, April, 1930; and again, June, 1932; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1933; confd., July, 1934.

OWEN, GODFREY ELWIN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Feb., 1890).—Educ. at Harrow and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 19th Nov., 1913, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., rev., and judl. depts., March, 1920; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1922; offg. secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Nov., 1927; magte. and collr., Dec., 1928; secty. to local self-govt. dept., Dec., 1928; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

OWEN, LEONARD, M.A., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 6th Oct., 1890).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., Bangor; apptd. after exam. of 1914; on mil. duty to March, 1919; arrived, 27th March, 1919; and posted to the United Provs.; jt. mag., Nov., 1921; offg. depy. commr., Nov.,

1922; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1924; confd., Dec., 1931; offg. settlement officer, Nov., 1926; repres. of U.P. govt. in central legis. assembly, Aug. to Sept., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

OWEN, LLEWELLYN GORDON, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (prof., Univ. of Rangoon) (b. 5th Mar., 1885).—Joined the service as prof. of mathematics, 12th Feb., 1915; on mil. service from 29th Aug., 1916, to 26th Oct., 1919.

OWEN, MORRIS, M.Sc., F.Inst.P.H., Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instrn., C.P.) (b. 16th Feb., 1885).—Joined the service as prof., Victoria Coll. of Science, Nagpur, 3rd Feb., 1912, princpl., Morris Coll., Nagpur, April, 1923, and again, Jan., 1926; princpl., Victoria Coll. of Science, Nov., 1924; offg. princpl., coll. of science, Nagpur, July, 1926; confd., May, 1928; offg. dir. of public instrn., C.P., Dec., 1934; confd., Apl., 1937.

OWENS, JOSEPH HUBERT, C.I.E., M.B.E., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (dep. P.M.G.) (b. 18th Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 12th Jan., 1909; confd., Dec., 1910; on depn. to I.E.F. from Oct., 1914, to Apl., 1922; dep. p.m.g., June, 1935.

OWENS, TERENCE FRANCIS, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Burma Med. Dept. (b. 8th Oct., 1878).—Rendered service in the mil. dept. and as probationer in chemical examr.'s office; offg. chemical examr., June, 1909; chemical examr. and bacteriologist, Dec., 1911; examr. of medical stores, India store dept., May, 1932; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

OXBURY, HAROLD FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S., Impl. Customs Service (asst. commr.) (b. 11th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at King Edward VI Sch., Norwich, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secy. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1931; ditto, local govt. dept., Mar., 1932; ditto, home and pol. dept., Mar., 1933; dep. secty., fin. dept., Burma, Dec., 1934; asst. collr., Impl. Customs Service, May, 1935; asst. commr., Feb., 1938.

OXLEY, HUMPHREY LESLIE MALCOLM, India Office (clerk, legal adviser's dept.) (b. 9th Oct., 1909).—Apptd. clerk, legal adviser's dept., 23rd Sept., 1933.

OXLEY, JAMES CHARLES STEWART, F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 15th Nov., 1874).—First commn. 28th June, 1900; in mil. employ. to July, 1903, when he was apptd. civil surgn.; on mil. service from Jan., 1915, to Aug., 1920; ret'd., May, 1930.

OZANNE, ROBERT DE LA CONDAINE, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 26th June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 13th Oct., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1926; confd., Aug., 1937.

PACKMAN, KENNETH CHALMERS, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 14th May, 1899).—First commn. dated 15th April, 1919; in milty. employ to March, 1924; asst. for Mekran to pol. agent in Kalat, May, 1925; asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Sept., 1928; 2nd addl. ditto, June, 1930; pol. agent, Dir, Swat, and Chitral, Oct., 1930; ditto, North Waziristan, Apl., 1931; secty. to A.G.G., Deccan states, Mar., 1935; A.G.G., Deccan States, and resdt. at Kolhapur (in addn.), May, 1936; consul-genl., Kashgar, Oct., 1936.

PADDON, ERROL GRAHAM EVANS, B.Sc., A.C.G.I., Indian State Rlys. (dist. elec. engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 21st June, 1898).—Asst. elect. engr., Aug., 1921; dist. ditto, May, 1938.

PADDON, Sir STANLEY SOMERSET WREFFORD, Kt., C.I.E., A.M.I.Mech.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.); Office of High Commr. for India (dir.-gen., stores dept.) (b. 28th March, 1881).—First commn., 15th Aug., 1900; apptd. to I.A., March, 1905; ret'd., Feb., 1914; re-employed as temp. staff offr., directorate of mil. operations, War Office, Nov., 1914; ditto, mil. dept., India Office, July, 1917; C.I.E., Jan., 1919; supt., India store depot, March, 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; dir.-gen., store dept., 3rd Oct., 1923; Kt., Jan., 1932.

PADDONROW, LINDSAY ARTHUR, Indian Police (b. 18th Feb., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, Nov., 1932.

PADFIELD, Rev. GEORGE AUGUSTUS SELWYN, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 7th July, 1876).—Apptd. a chaplain on probation, 22nd March, 1909; junr. chaplain, Aug., 1911; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1921; ret'd., Feb., 1930.

PADMANABHA AYYAR, NARAYANA, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 10th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 11th June, 1923; asst. exec. engr., July, 1924; offg. exec. engr., May, 1928; confid., July, 1933.

PADMANABHAN, DORAI SWAMIER, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 15th March, 1909).—Educ. at Madras University and King's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Apl., 1934.

PAGE, SIR ARTHUR, Kt., K.C., Barr. (1901), late Judicial dept., Bengal (b. 9th Mar., 1876).—Addl. judge, high ct., Calcutta, from 1st Jan., 1923; apptd. judge, high ct., Calcutta, 18th Jan., 1924; ch. justice, high ct., Rangoon, Apl., 1930; Kt., June, 1930; ret'd., Apl., 1936.

PAGE, ARTHUR JOHN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Nov., 1882).—Educ. at Cheltenham Gr. Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settlt. offr.; dep. commr., May, 1918; offg. commr. of settlts. and land records, March, 1925; confid., July, 1925; offg. secty. to govt., Burma, judi. dept., Nov., 1928; dir. of statistics and lab. commr., Dec., 1929; offg. commr., Nov., 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

PAGE, JAMES ALFRED, A.R.I.B.A., late Archaeological Survey dept. (b. 25th Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 21st Mar., 1913; supt., Jan., 1921; dep. dir. genl. of archaeology, Apl., 1929; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

PAHLUMAL, KALUMAL (see Kalumal Pahlumal).
