PAI, AMMEMBAL VITTAL, Indian C.S. (agents of the govt. of India in Ceylon) (b. 11th Oct., 1901).—Educ. at University of Madras and Wadham College, Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and magte.;

sub-collr., and jt. magte., March, 1928; under-secty. to govt., P.W. and labour dept., Feb., 1931; agent of the Govt. of India in Ceylon, Apl., 1936.

PAI, AMMEMBALA UPENDRA (see Upendra Pai, A.).

PAI, K. RAMA (see Rama Pai, K.).

PAI, MANGALORE PURUSHOTHAM, Indian C.S. (sub. coll. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 1st Dec., 1906).—Educ. at University of Madras, King's Coll., London, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1919; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag.; sub.-collr. and jt. magte., Nov., 1930; diwan of Sivaganga, July, 1938.

PAINE, CRISPIN BERNARD CHITTY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 2nd Mar., 1906).—Educ. at St. Paul's School and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. under secty. to govt., Assam, Apl., 1933; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1935.

PAING, MAUNG, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (under secty. to govt., educn. dept., Burma) (b. 7th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Rangoon University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Jan., 1932, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty to govt., educn. dept., Burma, June, 1936.

PAISLEY, JOHN FREDERICK, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 18th June, 1906).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, Oct., 1928.

PAKENHAM-WALSH, ERNEST, late Indian C.S. (b. 19th June, 1875).—Educ. at Birkenhead Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 26th Dec., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub.-collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; temp. addl. sess. judge, July, 1917; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1919; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1928, Oct., 1928, Jan., 1929, July, 1930, July, 1931, and again, Feb.-May, 1932; apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1932; ret'd., June, 1935.

PAKENHAM-WALSH, WILFRED PAKENHAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 2nd June, 1898).—Educ. at Malvern Coll. and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1922; arrived, 26th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. regr., high ct., Rangoon, March, 1928; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1929; registrar, high ct., Rangoon, Sept., 1931; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1933; transf. to govt. of Punjab, 1938.

PAL, NIRANJAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 26th Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

PALIT, ANATH NATH, O.B.E., F.R.C.S.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar (suplt., cent. jails) (b. 14th Oct., 1883).—First commn., dated 29th Jan., 1910; in mil. service up to 30th Sept., 1922; civil surg., Oct., 1922; O.B.E., June, 1936; supt., cent. jails, Bihar, Sept., 1937.

PALMER, CADWALLADER EDWARDS, M.A., M.B., Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 24th Dec., 1880).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1905; in mil. employ, Sept., 1905, to Oct., 1907, and from Sept., 1908, to Apl., 1909; offg. jail supt., April, 1909; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to Nov.,

1919; supt., central prison, Allahabad, Nov., 1919; offg. inspr.-gen. of prisons, April, 1926; confd., Feb., 1930; offg. inspr.-genl., Assam, Jan., 1935; confd., Jan., 1935; retd., Dec., 1937.

PALMER, R.T. REV. EDWIN JAMES, M.A., D.D. (b. 1869).—Fellow and tutor of Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. Bishop of Bombay, 30th July, 1908; resigned, Sept., 1929.

PALMER, FRANCIS EDWARD CHETWYND, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 6th May, 1887).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1911; extra asst. consvr., June, 1914; promoted to I.F.S. as dep. consvr., United Provs., Nov., 1931.

PALMER, REGINALD WILLIAM VAUGHAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 2nd May, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 23rd Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., July, 1923.

PALMER, WILLIAM JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (assi. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 25th April, 1909).—Educated at Cheltenham Grammar School and Christ's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

PALMES, WILLIAM TEVERY, late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Aug., 1888).—Educ. at Bradford Sch., Polytechnikum, Brunswick, Trinity Coll., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 9th Dec., 1913, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1916, to May, 1919; offg. under sec. to govt. of Burma, April, 1920; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1921, Sept. 1923, and again, Dec., 1926; confd., Nov., 1930; retd., Jan., 1934.

PALSULE, KESHAV BALKRISHNA, B.A., LL.B., late Central Provs. C.S. (Judl.) (b. 6th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service as munsiif., 10th Mar., 1903; extra asst. commr., May, 1906; sub. judge, July, 1910; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1929; retd., Oct., 1933.

PAMPHILON, ALFRED HART, India Office (exec. officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1890).—Served as asst. clerk, customs house, from June, 1910, to July, 1912, and as sec. div. clerk, foreign office, from July to Nov., 1912; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Office, 11th Nov., 1912; and attd. to registry and record dept.; transf. to mil. dept., Jan., 1917; on service with Royal Navy from Nov., 1916, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922; transf. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept., Feb., 1923.

PANANDIKAR, Dr. SATTYASHRAYA GOPAL, M.A. (Bom.), D.Sc. (Lon.), Indian Educl. Service (b. 18th July, 1893).—Joined the service as prof. of history and pol. economy, Elph. Coll., Bombay, 24th Oct., 1924.

PANCHAPAGESAM, TIRUVALUR RAMASWAMI, M.A., Finance dept., govt. of India (b. 4th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. prof., presdy. coll., Madras, 31st July, 1911; probnr., finance dept., Apl., 1915; asst. acct. genl., Calcutta, July, 1916; ditto, Madras, Nov., 1916; ditto, Bombay, May, 1921; ditto, central revs., Oct., 1923; dep. auditor, N.W. Rly., Apl., 1924; divl. audit officer, N.W. Rly., Nov., 1924; asst. dir. army audit, southern comd., Mar., 1925; dep.

acct. genl., Madras, Apl., 1927; ditto, Bombay, Feb., 1928; ditto, Delhi, Nov., 1929; ditto, central revs., July, 1930; acct. genl., ditto, May, 1931; dep. acct.-genl. (works), Punjab, May, 1932; ditto (civil), Nov., 1932; offg. ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Nov., 1933; offg. acct.-genl., Burma, May, 1935; offg. ch. auditor, Burma rlys., June, 1935; comptr., Orissa., Apl., 1936; dir. rly. clg. accts., May, 1938.

PANCHAPAKESA AYYAR, AIYLAN SUBRAHMANYA AYYAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 26th Jan., 1899).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Oxford Univ., (non-collegiate); joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 5th Dec., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub.-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aug., 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1933; confd., Mar., 1935.

PANCKRIDGE, HUGH RAHERE, Barr., Judicial dept., Bengal (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 2nd Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as standing counsel, 13th Jan., 1926; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, May, 1929; addl. judge, ditto, Nov., 1929; judge, ditto, April, 1930.

PANDALAI, KRISHNAN, RAO BAHADUR, LL.D., Barr., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 6th April, 1874).—Joined the service as 3rd judge, ct. of small causes, July, 1919; 2nd judge, ditto, July, 1921; ch. presdy. magte., Feb., 1927; offg. ch. judge, court of small causes, Feb., 1928; judge, high ct., Madras, Dec., 1928; retd., Apl., 1934.

PANDALAI, KRISHNAN GOPINATH, M.B., Lt.-Col., late Indian Medical Service, (b. 20th May, 1885).—First commn. dated 28th Jan., 1911; in milty. employ till 23rd April, 1922; dist. medl. officer, Jan., 1924; 2nd surg., genl. hosp., Madras, June, 1929; retd.

PANDE, CHANDRA DUTT, RAI SAHIB, M.A., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 28th July, 1880).—Joined the service, 15th July, 1902, and held non-gazetted appts. to 27th Jan., 1907; supt., Jan., 1907; asst. dir. genl., post office, Nov., 1928; retd., May, 1934.

PANDE, GAURI DATT, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 10th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1928.

PANDE, JIVA NAND (see Jiva Nand Pandé).

PANDE, PITAMBAR DUTT, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 15th Dec., 1896).—Joined the service, 22nd Mar., 1921; services placed at the disposal of Finl. Commr. Rlys., Dec., 1935; on for. service under govt. of Burma, Oct., 1937.

PANDIT, ANAND DATTATRAYA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 1st April, 1909).—Educ. at Patwardhan High School, Nagpur, Universities of Nagpur and Allahabad and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service 5th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provinces as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte., Mar., 1936.

PANDIT, GOVIND NARAYAN, B.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engineers (b. 28th Mar., 1906).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 11th Aug., 1931.

PANDRANG ROW. VOMBATHKERE, B.A., Barr. (1904), Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. 11th June, 1881).—Educ. at Govt. Coll., Mangalore, Madras Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 19th Dec., 1904, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1912; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1924; dep. sec., local self-govt. dept., Oct., 1924; collr. and dist. mag. and sec. to govt., devl. dept., Dec., 1925; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1928; offg. judge, high ct., Jan., 1934; confd., July, 1934.

PANDYA ISHWARRAI NARBHERAM, Income-Tax Dept., Bombay (asst. commr.) (b. 21st Sept., 1883).—Joined the service, Apl., 1917; held non-gaz. appts., 1916-1924; confd. as income-tax officr., Bombay, June, 1925; confd. as asst. commr. of income-tax, July, 1936.

PANDYA, DEBI DAT (see Debi, D. P.).

PANIKAR, ITTEKOTE ACHUTA, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 11th July, 1900).—Joined the service, 21st Feb., 1923; dep. acctnt.-genl., Burma, Sept., 1935; on for service under the govt. of Burma, Apl., 1937.

PANJABI, KEWALRAM LALCHAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and magte., Bombay) (b. 29th Dec., 1898).—Educ. at St. Patrick's School, Karachi, and Bombay and London Universities; apptd. after exam. of 1923; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1923, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; offg. collr. and pol. agent, Surat, March, 1927; offg. regr., co-op. societies, Poona, Nov., 1931; collr. and magte., Oct., 1936.

PANNA LAL, B.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc., LL.B., Barr., Indian C.S. (commr., United Provs.) (b. 23rd Nov., 1883).—Educ. at Agra Collegiate Sch., Allahabad, and Calcutta Univs., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 31st Dec., 1907, and served in United Provs. as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte., Jan., 1916; under-secty. to govt., April, 1917, to Aug., 1918; forest settl. officr., Dehra Dun, Oct., 1918; magte. and collr., Oct., 1923; spl. officer in addition, July, 1924; dep. secty. to govt., industries dept., April, 1927; secty. to govt., industries and educn. dept., July, 1927, and again, Feb., 1935; offg. commr., July, 1931; and again, Feb., 1933; confd., Feb., 1938; author of reports on "Jaunsar-Bawar Forest Settlement," "Forest Settlement, Etawah District," and "Kumaun Local Customs."

PANT, PANDIT CHINTAMANI, Rai Sahib, Central Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.) (b. 11th May, 1885).—Joined the service, 17th July, 1908; asst. engr., Feb., 1920; transfed. to central p.w.d., Sept., 1922; exec. engr., Apl., 1930.

PANT, PITAMBAR, Rai Bahadur, P.M.S. Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 1st Oct., 1883).—Civil surgeon, Mar., 1925.

PAPWORTH, HAROLD CHARLES, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (princl., presdy. coll., Madras) (b. 16th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as prof. of English, presdy. coll., Madras, on 29th Jan., 1916; offg. prinpl., govt. arts coll., June, 1926; offg. prinpl., Victoria Coll., Palghat, Jan., 1928; ditto, Govt. Muhammad Coll., Madras, Jan., 1933; princl. presdy. coll., Madras, Feb., 1937.

PAPWORTH, WALTER FRANCIS, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 12th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 2nd Apl., 1915; inspr. of schools, Aug., 1921; retd., July, 1934.

PARANJP, DHUNDIRAJ VISHWANATH, B.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., Central Provs. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 31st Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 19th Feb., 1910; sub-judge, Nov., 1923; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1933.

PARANJP, GOPAL RAMCHANDRA, M.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (b. 30th Jan., 1891).—Apptd. prof. of physics, Royal Inst. of Science, Bombay, 26th Oct., 1920; offg. princl. ditto, June, 1933.

PARANJPYE, RAGHUNATH PURUSHOTTAM, M.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Bom.), D.Sc. (Cal.) (b. 16th Feb., 1876).—Educ. Maratha H.S., Bombay; Fergusson Coll., Poona; St. John's Coll., Cambridge (Fell); Indian Govt. scholar; princ. and prof. of math., Fergusson Coll., Poona, 1902-26; Fellow of the Univ. of Bombay, 1904-27; sometime vice-chancellor of the Indian Women's Univ., Poona; member, Bombay legis. council, 1913; awarded Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 1st class, 1916; minister, Bombay govt., 1921-23, 1927; member of council of India, April, 1927 to Apl., 1932; member, Indian taxation inquiry ctee., 1924-25; member, reforms inquiry ctee., 1924; delegate of the govt. of India to internatl. labour conferences, Geneva, 1927-1930; member, Int. conf. on treatment of foreigners, 1929; Int. conf. for formation of Int. Relief Union, 1927; Int. conf. on limitation of manufacture of narcotic drugs, 1931; author of lives of Gopal Krishna Gokhale and Dhondo Keshar Karve, and "The Crux of the Indian Problem."

PARASHURAM JOG, DATTATRAYA, Indian Service of Engineers (b. 9th Nov., 1906).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Madras, 1st Oct., 1931; confd., June, 1933.

PAR, JOSEPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 22nd July, 1914).—Educ. at County Sec. Sch., West Bridgeford, Nottingham Univ., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 6th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

PAREEKH, GOBIND NARAYAN. (see Gobind N.P.)

ARIJA, PRANKRISHNA, B.Sc., M.A., Indian Educl. Service (b. 1st Apl., 1891).—Apptd. prof. of botany, Ravenshaw Coll., Cuttack, 4th Aug., 1921.

PARIKH, HIMATLAL BAPALAL, M.I.E. (Ind.), L.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 24th May, 1888).—Joined the pr vl. service as asst. engr., 29th March, 1911; exec. engr., March, 1921; spl. road engr., Bombay, Nov., 1935; ditto, Sind, Apl., 1936.

PARISH, FREDERICK FRANK, Indian Railway Accts. Service (accts. offer.) (b. 5th May, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. auditor and acctnt., G.I.P. Rly., 1st May, 1919; services permanently pla ed at the disposal of finl. commr. rlys., Oct., 1929; confd. as accts. offer., gr. I., May, 1935.

PARISH, JAMES EDWARD, Office of the High Commr. for India (asst. engr., mechanical transport, store dept.) (b. 21st Mar., 1887).—Apptd. to Office of H.C. for I., July, 1921; asst. inspr. of stores, Sept., 1922; asst. engr., mechanical transport, March, 1926.

PARK, Rev. WILLIAM ROBERT, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 12th Jan., 1880).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Rangoon, on 26th Mar., 1912; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1915; C.I.E., Aug., 1917; O.B.E., June, 1919; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1925; offg. archdeacon of Rangoon, May, 1931; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

PARKER, FREDERICK ROWLAND, Office of High Commr., for India (higher clerical officer, accts. dept.) (b. 29th June, 1884).—Apptd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India Office, 12th Jan., 1920; transf'd. to office of high commr. for India, 1st April, 1921; clerical officer, Feb., 1922; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

PARKER, HARDING NEVILLE, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 20th July, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 2nd Dec., 1912; in milty. employ from 12th May, 1919, to 14th Aug., 1920; dist. traffic supt., Dec., 1921; offg. asst. agent, E.B. Rly., Jan., 1928; dep. traffic supt., Oct., 1930; offg. dep. traffic manager, comml., E. B. rly., Mar., 1933; ditto, transpn., July, 1935; offg. dep. agent personnel, E.B. rly., July, 1936.

PARKER, HERBERT, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 17th Apl., 1889).—Educ. at Nottingham High School, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 10th Dec., 1912, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept.; asst. rice commr., Rangoon, Feb. to Aug., 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1924; confd., May, 1924; offg. judge, high ct., Rangoon, Apl., 1936, to Nov., 1937.

PARKER, RICHARD HENRY, Indian C.S. (jt. secty., dept. of commerce and labour, Bengal) (b. 21st July, 1898).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1922; arrived, 15th Dec., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr.; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, for, dept., Feb., 1926; under-secty. to A. to G.G. in Rajputana, and C.C., Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1926; ditto, to resdt., Hyderabad; reverted to Bengal govt., 5th Sept., 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1930; and again, May, 1932; confd., Mar., 1934; commr. for Workmen's Compensation, Feb., 1934; jt. secty., dept. of commerce and labour, Bengal, July, 1937.

PARKER, RICHARD NEVILLE, F.C.H., Indian Forest Service (ch. consvr., Punjab) (b. 4th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 10th Nov., 1905, as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Dec., 1910; offg. consr., Dec., 192; confd., Nov., 1926; on depn. to forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Sept., 1924; offg. ch. consvr., May, 1933; confd., Aug., 1934.

PARKES, RODERICK WALLIS, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 2nd Apl., 1909).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., Magdalen Coll., Oxford, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after the exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; addl. asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Oct., 1935; asst. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1935; secty., British legation, Kabul, Aug., 1936; under-secty. to resdt. for E. States, Dec., 1938.

PARKIN, GEORGE DEVEREUX, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 30th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service 29th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1925; supt. of police, Benares State, Apl., 1931; supt., Nov., 1932; on depn. as dep. inspr.-genl., Rampur State, Dec., 1934, to Nov., 1937.

PARKINSON, JOSEPH ERNEST, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 13th Apl., 1883).—Joined the service as vice-prinpl., C.T. Coll., Lahore, 10th Mar., 1913; inspr. of schools, Sept., 1922; offg. asst., dir. of public instrn., Apl., 1925; inspr. of training institutions, Oct., 1925; prin., C.T. Coll., Lahore, Oct., 1926; offg. dir., public instrn., and under-secty. to govt., Punjab, July, 1933; confd., Oct., 1935; educl. commr., with the govt. of India, Nov., 1936; ret'd., Apl., 1938; re-empld. as educl. commr. with govt. of India, Apl. to Nov., 1938; C.I.E., June, 1938.

PARLBY, JOSHUA, O.B.E., M.Sc., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 3rd Mar., 1889).—Joined the service as mil. acct., 2nd Cl., Nov., 1913; finl. asst., I.E.F., Apl., 1917 to Dec., 1918; O.B.E., 1919; dep. secty., high commrs. sect., Iraq, Dec., 1918; advr. and secty. to Iraqi acct. gen., Jan., 1921; fin. secty. to high commr., Iraq, Apl., 1922; spec. duty for closing of "Accts. of occ. Territories," July, 1922 to May, 1923; aud.-genl. and inspr.-genl. of accts., Iraq, Mar., 1924 to May, 1925; on dep. to London and Geneva as jt. rep. of Iraq govt., arbitration proceedings, League of Nations, Jan., 1925; secty. to finl. commn., Iraq, Apl. and May, 1925; compt. and aud.-genl., Iraq, May, 1925 to May, 1935; awarded the "Order of the Two Rivers" by H.M. the King of Iraq, 1935; confd. as Cl. I offer., Apl., 1937; spec. duty in mil. acct.-genl.'s office, Nov., 1937; offg. mil. acct.-genl., Apl., 1938.

PARMANAND, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 7th Feb., 1901).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Cambridge Univ. (non-coll.); joined the service, 30th Oct., 1924; arrived, 10th Feb., 1925, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., variously from Jan., 1928; dep. commr., provl., Aug., 1937; confd., Sept., 1938.

PARNELL, RALPH, late Indian Forest Service (b. 29th Jan., 1884).—Joined the service, 10th Nov., 1905, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1910; offg. consvr., Mar., 1922; confd., Nov., 1927; offg. ch. consvr., May, 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

PARPIA, YUSUF RAHIM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. coll., Bombay) (b. 25th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at University of Bombay and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, December, 1929, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; administ., Kurla municipality., Feb., 1936.

PARR, ALFRED ERNEST, B.Sc., Ph.D., late Indian Agricultural Service (United Provs.) (b. 12th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1907, as dep. dir. of agriculture; on mil. service, Sept., 1915, to Oct., 1919; offg. dir. of agric., April, 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1931.

PARR, CLIFFORD HEATLEY, B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. of agric., United Provs.) (b. 15th July, 1892).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., 1st Jan., 1920.

Cttee. of Intl. Bureau of Public Health, Paris, 1922; administrative adviser to Indian delegation, International Sanitary Conference, Paris, 1926; sec. to Indian delegns. to 5th, 6th and 7th Assemblies of League of Nations, 1924, 1925 and 1926; actg. secty., pol. (internal) dept., Apl., 1930; jt. secty. to British delegation, Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931, 3rd session, Nov., Dec., 1932; jt. secty., Indian States Enquiry comtee., Jan.-June, 1932; asst. secty., pol. (internal) dept., Dec., 1933; C.S.I., June, 1934.

PATRO, ANNEPU VASUDEVA, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Aug., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, 11th June, 1930; confd., June, 1932.

PATTENSON, ARTHUR ERIC TYLDEN, Indian State Rlys. (member, rly. bd.) (b. 15th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1908; asst. traffic supt., May, 1910; dist. ditto, June, 1916; asst. trans. supt., Nov., 1922; asst. traffic manager, Dec., 1922; dep. ditto, Oct., 1924; offg. ch. traffic manager, Dec., 1923, Oct., 1924, and again, Nov., 1926; offg. ch. publicity officer, March, 1927; confd., Jan., 1929; offg. ch. transpn. supt., Apl., 1930; agent, July, 1932; offg. member, rly. bd., Nov., 1934; confd., Feb., 1935.

PATTERSON, BRIAN WALTER, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 24th Jan., 1899).—Asst. traffic supt., Apl., 1918; dist. ditto (prov.), Aug., 1936.

PATTERSON, DAVID CLARKE, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 5th May, 1879).—Educ. at Oundle Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 10th Dec., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., April, 1911; s.p.t. mag. and collr., Sept., 1915; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1916; on mil. service, Sept., 1918, to Feb., 1919; offg. supt. and membr. of legal affairs, Bengal, March, 1926; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, June, 1929; Apl., 1930; and again Mar.-Sept., 1931; confd., Nov., 1931.

PATTERSON, SIR STEWART BLAKELY AGNEW, K.C.V.O., C.S.I., C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Indian Office (b. 18th Mar., 1872).—First commn. dated 13th Aug., 1892; in mil. employ. to June, 1898; asst. pol. agent for Dir and Swat, Nov., 1898; commdt. B. mil. police, Sept., 1899; asst. to resdt. in Mewar, May, 1900; asst. to a.g.g. in Rajputana, Oct., 1902; asst. resdt. in Mewar, April, 1903; asst. to resdy. in Kashmir, Feb., 1904; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Nov., 1908; pol. agent, Wana, Dec., 1908; asst. commr., Ajmer, Oct., 1909; asst. sec. to govt. of India, for dept., Nov., 1910; finl. member, Marwar state council, July, 1913; attd. to adjt.gen.'s branch, hd. qrs. of army, April, 1917; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; resdt. at Jaipur, Feb., 1922; offg. sec., pol. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1924; member of council of state, Jan.-Aug., 1925; agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Aug., 1925; C.S.I., Jan., 1927; ret'd., Mar., 1927; apptd. pol. A.D.C. to secty. of state, Sept., 1928; K.C.V.O., June, 1933; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

PATTIPATE HANIMIREDDIGARI RAMA REDDI (see Rama Reddi, P. H.).

PATTLE, ERNEST GODFREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (finl. commr., Burma) (b. 9th Aug., 1883).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., London; open scholar, St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settl. offr.; regr., chf. ct., Lower Burma, Jan., 1917; warden of the Yenang-Yaung, Minbu, and Singu oilfields, Dec., 1917, to March, 1919; contr. of rents, city of Rangoon, April, 1920; dep. commr., Oct., 1923; services placed at disposal of central board of revenue as commr. of income tax, April, 1925; offg. sec. to govt., educn., local govt. and public health dept., Burma, Dec., 1925; registr., co-op. societies, Nov., 1926; senr. warden, oilfields, June, 1927; offg. commr., Sept., 1933; commr. (prov.), Jan., 1936; ditto (perm.), Jan., 1937; finl. commr., Mar., 1938.

PATTON, ARNOLD GORDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. fin. and rev. dept., Assam) (b. 2nd Dec., 1892).—Educ. at City of London Sch. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1916, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Jan., 1918, to March, 1919; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1925; and again, Feb., 1932; confd., Feb., 1934; secty., fin. and rev. dept., Apl., 1937.

PATUCK (see Patak).

PATWARDHAN, NARHAR MAHADEO, B.A., LL.B., Barr., late Bombay Civil Service—Appt. commr. for workmen's compensation, Bombay, June, 1924; offg. judge and sess. judge, April, 1928; confd., Aug., 1932; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

PATWARDHAN, RAMKRISHNA PURUSHOTAM, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (prof., Elph. coll., Bombay) (b. 8th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service, 5th Feb., 1921; prof. of Indian history and economics, Deccan Coll., Poona, Feb., 1921; prof., Elph. Coll., Bombay, July, 1926, and again, Oct., 1937; prof. of history, Deccan coll., Poona, June, 1932.

PAUL, CHAVUR JOHN, Rao Bahadur, Madras C.S. (sub-collr.) (b. 6th July, 1887).—Supt., rev. sectt., Jan., 1919; asst. secty., dev. dept., July, 1921; dep. collr., Aug., 1931; sub-collr., Apr., 1936; secty. to govt., dev. dept., Madras, Apl., 1936.

PAUL, MARCUS JAYARAJAN ROLAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 17th Oct., 1910).—Educ. at Royal College, Colombo and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1936.

PAVRY, FAREDUN CURSETJI, C.I.E., F.C.H., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 23rd June, 1877).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1900; exec. engr., Oct., 1908; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1921; suptg. engr. (prov.), April, 1924; offg. ch. engr., June, 1925; suptg. engr., Sept., 1925; chief engr., Jan., 1928; C.I.E., June, 1930; ret'd., June, 1932.

PAW, MAUNG SAN, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 6th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 6th Mar., 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Rlys., Dec. 1932.

PARRY, CLAUDE FREDERICK, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 9th Mar., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1919; dist. supt., Oct., 1925; principl. pol. training sch., Saugor, Feb., 1933.

PARRY, NEVILL EDWARD, late Indian C.S. (Assam) (b. 3rd July, 1884).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 10th Dec., 1907, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1912; under sec. to chief commr., Assam, Dec., 1913; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Aug., 1916, to April, 1919; dep. commr., June, 1923; supt., Lushai Hills, March, 1924; retd., Aug., 1932.

PARSONS, Sir ALFRED ALAN LETHBRIDGE, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (adviser to the sec. of state for India) (b. 22nd Oct., 1882).—Educ. at Bradfield Sch., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 18th Nov., 1907, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sec., fin. dept., Punjab, July, 1912; under-sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., June, 1916; addl. dep. finl. adviser, mil. fin., govt. of India, Dec., 1918; offg. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1919; confirmed, Jan., 1920; addl. finl. adviser, mil. fin. branch, Nov., 1920; offg. depy. controller of currency, Bombay, Jan., 1922; offg. fin. commr., rly. board, Jan., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; offg. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of industries and labour, March, 1925; offg. finl. commr., rly. board, April, 1926; confd., Jan., 1927; Kt., Jan., 1932; secty. to govt. of India, finl. dept., Apl., 1932; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, June, 1932; member, Ind. staty. rly. bd. ctee., June, 1933; retd. from I.C.S., Nov., 1934; member of council of India, Nov., 1934; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1935; adviser to the sec. of state for India, Apl., 1937.

PARSONS, Sir ARTHUR EDWARD BROADBENT, K.C.I.E., C.B.E., D.S.O., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 5th Aug., 1884).—First commn., 4th Apl., 1906; pol. officer on probn., May, 1919; confd., Aug., 1921; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; pol. agent, Tochi, Dec., 1923; apptd. dep. sec., govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., 23rd Oct., 1924; C.B.E., June, 1927; dep. commr., Bannu, Oct., 1927; counsellor, H.M. Legation, Kabul, March, 1930; on spl. duty in Khyber agency, Oct., 1930; pol. agent, Khyber, Jan., 1931; dep. commr., Hazara, Aug., 1932; resdt., Waziristan, June, 1933; dep. agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, June, 1935; retd., cl. II, Aug., 1935; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Baluchistan, Apl., 1936; chairman, frontier ctee., Mar., 1936; ditto, frontier watch and ward ctee., Nov., 1936; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Baluchistan, Dec., 1936; offg. secty. to govt. of India, ext. affairs dept., June, 1937; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Baluchistan, Oct.-Nov., 1937; again, Mar., 1938; confd., Aug., 1938; K.C.I.E., June, 1938.

PARSONS, CHARLES WULSTAN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. to signal engr., 21st Oct., 1905; resdt. engr., Jan., 1909; offg. dep. ch. engr., signals, Feb., 1921; confd., Nov., 1922; retd., June, 1934.

PARSONS, CYRIL GORDON, M.C., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 7th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Dec., 1919; offg. supt., March, 1923; confd., April, 1927.

PARSONS, FRANK THOMAS, Indian Police (b. 9th Sept., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on probn., Nov., 1936.

PARSONS, RUDOLPH ELLIOT REBSCH, Indian Police (asst. supt., Assam) (b. 17th Jan., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Assam, 14th Oct., 1930; confd., and asst. to pol. officer, Saduja frontier tract, May, 1933.

PARTAB SINGH, BABU, B.A., LL.B., late United Provs. Civil Service.—Joined the service, 22nd Feb., 1899; sub-judge, Feb., 1915; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1921; retd., Feb., 1929.

PARTAB SINGH, B.A., Indian C.S., (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1896).—Educ. at Khalsa Coll., Amritsar, and Punjab Univ.; arrived, 11th Jan., 1922, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr. (provl.), August, 1928; ditto (perm.), Apl., 1932.

PARTAP SINGH, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (b. 10th Jan., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, on 9th Nov., 1923; transf. N.W.F.P., Mar., 1932.

PARTAP RAM (see Ram Partap).

PARTHASARATHI AYYANGAR, SRINIVASA AYYANGAR, B.A., Indian Police (b. 1st May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 1st May, 1927; offg. supt., Apl., 1931.

PARTRIDGE, EDWARD HENRY WILLIAM, Office of the High Commr. for India (engr., mechanical transport, store dept.) (b. 22nd April, 1879).—Apptd. asst. engr., mechanical transport, Oct., 1924; engr., ditto, March, 1926.

PASCOE, Sir EDWIN HALL, Kt., M.A., D.Sc., F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 17th Feb., 1878).—Joined the service, 2nd March, 1905, as proby. asst. supt., geological survey of India; confirmed, May, 1907; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., May, 1915 to April, 1917; supt., geological survey, April, 1917; dir. geol. survey of India, June, 1921; Kt., Jan., 1928; retd., June, 1932.

PASRICHA, MUKUND LAL, C.I.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 25th July, 1879).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the telegraph service, 1st Oct., 1901, as asst. supt.; supt., Nov., 1912; offg. postmr.-gen., central circle, May, 1923; offg. dep. dir.-gen., tel. traffic, 1st March, 1926; confd., July, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. ch. engr., posts and tels., May, 1931; p.m.-g., central circle, Nov., 1931; ditto, Bengal and Assam, Dec., 1932; chairman, postal enquiry ctee., Apl., 1934; retd., May, 1935.

PATCH, CHARLES JAMES LODGE, M.C., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (medl. supt., Punjab mental hosp.) (b. 31st Oct., 1887).—First commn., dated 15th Dec., 1910; in milty. employ. to 5th Nov., 1922; supt., Punjab lunatic asylum, Nov., 1922; supt., Punjab mental hosp., July, 1924.

PATE, HENRY REGINALD, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Aug., 1880).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in

Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spl. asst. settl. offr., Nov., 1906, to Jan., 1908; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1914; sec., board of rev., June, 1916; dep. sec. to govt. of India, army dept., Aug., 1921; confd., Jan., 1922; collr. and dist. mag., Madras, Nov., 1925; secty. to govt., rev. dept., Madras, Nov., 1928; C.I.E., June, 1931; offg. 2nd secty. to govt. of Madras, Apl., 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

PATEL, BHAGVATPRASAD RAOJIBHAI, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 24th Aug., 1912).—Educ. at Proprietary High School, Ahmedabad, Bombay University, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Sind as asst. collr.

PATEL, BHAILAL KHUSHALDAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. coll., Sind) (b. 12th April, 1906).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Bombay as asst. coll.

PATEL, BHAILAL SHANKARBHAI, B.Ag., N.D.A., N.D.D., C.D.A.D., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Gujarat) (b. 29th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., June, 1921; prof. of agric., April, 1924; dep. dir. of agric., Jan., 1929; offg. dir., Feb., 1935; livestock expert up to Dec., 1937.

PATEL, BHOGILAL PRABHUDAS, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 16th Oct., 1912).—Educ. at Shree-Sayaji High Sch., Baroda, and Bombay Univ.; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PATEL, HIRALAL MULJIBHAI, Indian C.S. (Indian trade commr., Hamburg) (b. 27th Aug., 1904).—Educ. at Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; offg. collr., Jan. and Dec., 1932; att'd. Central bd. of rev., June, 1934; offg. dep. secty. to govt., Bombay, fin. dept., Mar., 1936; Indian trade commr., Hamburg, July, 1937.

PATEL, RANCHHODBHAI BHAIBABHAI, M.A., LL.B., Barr., Judl. Dept., Bombay (prothonotary and senior master, high ct.) (b. 18th Dec., 1880).—Joined the dept., 3rd Feb., 1905; asst. prothonotary, Sept., 1921; offg. prothonotary, June, 1926; confd., Oct., 1928.

PATERSON, HUGH JAMES, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Central p.w.d.) (b. 18th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as proxy. asst. exec. engr., 18th Dec., 1928; confd., June, 1930; transferred to central p.w.d., Jan., 1934; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1935.

PATERSON, NOEL KENNEDY, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Andamans) (b. 25th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh, Univ. of Edinburgh, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1929; arrived, 25th Nov., 1929, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., Central Provs., Mar., 1934; under-secty., fin. dept., govt. of India, May, 1937; dep. commr., Andamans, Apl., 1938.

PATERSON, ROBERT HAND, Indian State Rlys. (dist. contr. of stores, N.W. Rly.) (b. 17th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 21st June, 1912; asst. contr. of stores, N.W. rly., Apl., 1917; dist. contr. of stores, Mar., 1931; offg. dep. contr. of stores, Aug., 1936.

PATERSON-MORGAN, JOHN ANTHONY, Burma Police, (Cl. I) (b. 26th July, 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., on probn., Nov., 1938.

PATHAK, CHAUBE SALIG RAM (see Salig Ram P.C.).

PATHAK, VINAYANAND (see Vinayanand, P.).

PATIL, PANDURANG CHIMNAJI, Rai Bahadur, L.Ag., M.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 19th June, 1877).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Feb., 1906, to 31st March, 1909; divl. inspr. of agric., April, 1909; dep. dir. of agric., March, 1918; offg. prof. of agric. economics, July, 1927; offg. princpl. agric. coll., Poona, Mar., 1932; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

PATIL, RAMRAO KRISHNA RAO, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. commr. Central Provs.) (b. 13th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Benares University, Nagpur University and Cambridge (Non-Collegiate); joined the service, 14th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.

PATKAR, SHANTARAM KRISHNA, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Civil Service, Judicial Branch (b. 26th Nov., 1878).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 21st Feb., 1907, to 17th Dec., 1914; sub-judge, Dec., 1914; offg. asst. judge, Sept., 1927; confd., May, 1930; offg. judge and sess. judge, June, 1930; ret'd., Nov., 1933.

PATKAR, SITARAM SUNDERRAO, B.A., LL.B., late Judicial Dept., Bombay (b. 16th May, 1873).—Apptd. addl. judge, high ct., Bombay, July, 1926; judge, ditto Nov., 1926; offg. ch. justice, June, 1931; ret'd., May, 1933.

PATON, WILLIAM CALDER, M.C., M.B., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (chief medl. offcr., Delhi) (b. 27th Jan., 1886).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1912; resdt. medl. officer, gen. hosp., Madras, Jan., 1924; offg. civil surg., Coonoor, July, 1924; offg. D.M.O., Sept., 1927; confd., June, 1929; prof'r., medl. coll., Madras, May, 1932; chief medl. offcr. and civil surg., Delhi, Jan., 1934.

PATRICK, Rev. ALEXANDER, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (senr. chaplain) (b. 11th Nov., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 24th Aug., 1922; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1922; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1932.

PATRICK, PAUL JOSEPH, C.S.I., M.A., India Office (asst. secty., pol. (internal) dept.) (b. 6th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at Rugby and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. to sec.'s office, G.P.O., Jan. to Sept., 1913; apptd. junr. clerk, India Office, 1st Oct., 1913, and att'd. to mil. dept.; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., March, 1916; temp. asst. sec., army dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1917, to May, 1918; served with 4/11th Gurkha Rifles in Palestine, May, 1918, to Jan., 1919; resumed duty at India Office, Feb., 1919; private sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, July, 1920, to Sept., 1920; addl. pte. sec. to govt. of Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1920, to April, 1921; senior clerk, March, 1921; principal, April, 1921; establishment officer, Feb., 1922; delegate of govt. of India to Permt.

Cttee. of Intl. Bureau of Public Health, Paris, 1922; administrative adviser to Indian delegation, International Sanitary Conference, Paris, 1926; sec. to Indian delegs. to 5th, 6th and 7th Assemblies of League of Nations, 1924, 1925 and 1926; actg. secty., pol. (internal) dept., Apl., 1930; jt. secty. to British delegation, Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931, 3rd session, Nov., Dec., 1932; jt. secty., Indian States Enquiry comtee., Jan.-June, 1932; asst. secty., pol. (internal) dept., Dec., 1933; C.S.I., June, 1934.

PATRO, ANNEPU VASUDEVA, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Aug., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, 11th June, 1930; confd., June, 1932.

PATTENSON, ARTHUR ERIC TYLDEN, Indian State Rlys. (member, rly. bd.) (b. 15th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1908; asst. traffic supt., May, 1910; dist. ditto, June, 1916; asst. trans. supt., Nov., 1922; asst. traffic manager, Dec., 1922; dep. ditto, Oct., 1924; offg. ch. traffic manager, Dec., 1923, Oct., 1924, and again, Nov., 1926; offg. ch. publicity officer, March, 1927; confd., Jan., 1929; offg. ch. transpn. supt., Apl., 1930; agent, July, 1932; offg. member, rly. bd., Nov., 1934; confd., Feb., 1935.

PATTERSON, BRIAN WALTER, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 24th Jan., 1899).—Asst. traffic supt., Apl., 1918; dist. ditto (provl.), Aug., 1936.

PATTERSON, DAVID CLARKE, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 5th May, 1879).—Educ. at Oundle Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 10th Dec., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., April, 1911; s.p.t. mag. and collr., Sept., 1915; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1916; on mil. service, Sept., 1918, to Feb., 1919; offg. supt. and remembr. of legal affairs, Bengal, March, 1926; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, June, 1929; Apl., 1930; and again Mar.-Sept., 1931; confd., Nov., 1931.

PATTERSON, SIR STEWART BLAKELY AGNEW, K.C.V.O., C.S.I., C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late India Office (b. 18th Mar., 1872).—First commn. dated 13th Aug., 1892; in mil. employ to June, 1898; asst. pol. agent for Dir and Swat, Nov., 1898; commdt. B. mil. police, Sept., 1899; asst. to resdt. in Mewar, May, 1900; asst. to a.g.g. in Rajputana, Oct., 1902; asst. resdt. in Mewar, April, 1903; asst. to resdy. in Kashmir, Feb., 1904; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Nov., 1908; pol. agent, Wana, Dec., 1908; asst. commr., Ajmer, Oct., 1909; asst. sec. to govt. of India, for dept., Nov., 1910; finl. member, Marwar state council, July, 1913; attd. to adjt.gen.'s branch, hd. qrs. of army, April, 1917; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; resdt. at Jaipur, Feb., 1922; offg. sec., pol. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1924; member of council of state, Jan.-Aug., 1925; agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Aug., 1925; C.S.I., Jan., 1927; ret'd., Mar., 1927; apptd. pol. A.D.C. to secty. of state, Sept., 1928; K.C.V.O., June, 1933; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

PATTIPATE HANIMIREDDIGARI RAMA REDDI (see Rama Reddi, P. H.).

PATTLE, ERNEST GODFREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (finl. commr., Burma) (b. 9th Aug., 1883).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., London; open scholar, St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settlt. offr.; regr., chf. ct., Lower Burma, Jan., 1917; warden of the Yenang-Yaung, Minbu, and Singu oilfields, Dec., 1917, to March, 1919; contr. of rents, city of Rangoon, April, 1920; dep. commr., Oct., 1923; services placed at disposal of central board of revenue as commr. of income tax, April, 1925; offg. sec. to govt., educn., local govt. and public health dept., Burma, Dec., 1925; registr., co-op. societies, Nov., 1926; senr. warden, oilfields, June, 1927; offg. commr., Sept., 1933; commr. (provl.), Jan., 1936; ditto (perm.), Jan., 1937; finl. commr., Mar., 1938.

PATTON, ARNOLD GORDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. fin. and rev. dept., Assam) (b. 2nd Dec., 1892).—Educ. at City of London Sch. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1916, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Jan., 1918, to March, 1919; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1925; and again, Feb., 1932; confd., Feb., 1934; secty., fin. and rev. dept., Apl., 1937.

PATUCK (see Patak).

PATWARDHAN, NARHAR MAHADEO, B.A., LL.B., Barr., late Bombay Civil Service—Appt. commr. for workmen's compensation, Bombay, June, 1924; offg. judge and sess. judge, April, 1928; confd., Aug., 1932; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

PATWARDHAN, RAMKRISHNA PURUSHOTAM, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (prof., Elph. coll., Bombay) (b. 8th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service, 5th Feb., 1921; prof. of Indian history and economics, Deccan Coll., Poona, Feb., 1921; prof., Elph. Coll., Bombay, July, 1926, and again, Oct., 1937; prof. of history, Deccan coll., Poona, June, 1932.

PAUL, CHAVUR JOHN, Rao Bahadur, Madras C.S. (sub-collr.) (b. 6th July, 1887).—Supt., rev. sectt., Jan., 1919; asst. secty., dev. dept., July, 1921; dep. collr., Aug., 1931; sub-collr., Apr., 1936; secty. to govt., dev. dept., Madras, Apl., 1936.

PAUL, MARCUS JAYARAJAN ROLAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 17th Oct., 1910).—Educ. at Royal College, Colombo and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1936.

PAVRY, FAREDUN CURSETJI, C.I.E., F.C.H., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 23rd June, 1877).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1900; exec. engr., Oct., 1908; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1921; suptg. engr. (provl.), April, 1924; offg. ch. engr., June, 1925; suptg. engr., Sept., 1925; chief engr., Jan., 1928; C.I.E., June, 1930; ret'd., June, 1932.

PAW, MAUNG SAN, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 6th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 6th Mar., 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Rlys., Dec. 1932.

PAWSEY, CHARLES RIDLEY, M.C., B.A. (Oxon), Indian Civil Service (dep. commr. Assam) (b. 14th July, 1894).—Educ. at Berkhamsted Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1919; served in Assam as asst. commr. from 4th Dec., 1919; offg. dir. of land records, inspr. genl. of registrn., etc., Assam, Sept., 1932; and again, Mar., 1934; dep. commr. (prov. subs.), Apl., 1935; confd., Oct., 1935.

PAYMASTER, BARJOR BEHRAMJI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 10th Jan., 1912).—Educated at Parsi High School, Panchgani, University of Bombay, University College, London, and The School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PAYMASTER, BEHRAMJI BURJORJI, L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.), L.F.P.S. (Glas.), Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.).—Joined 26th July, 1902, and served in mil. dept. prior to 8th March, 1909; civil surg., April, 1911; on milty. duty from 1st Nov., 1914, to 13th Dec., 1921; ret'd., Jan., 1929.

PAYNE, Miss HELENA ELIZABETH, B.A. (Bom.), late Indian Educl. Service (Bombay) (b. 9th June, 1876).—Joined the service, 3rd April, 1922; inspectress of girls' schools, C.D. and Bom. Div., April, 1923; lady supt., training coll. for women, Ahmedabad, May, 1926; offg. inspectress of girls' schools July, 1926; ret'd., June, 1931.

PAYTON, WILFRED HUGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., home dept., Burma) (b. 7th Apl., 1892).—Educ. at King Edward's Schl., Birmingham, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; appt. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1916, to Sept., 1919; asst. supt., S. Shan States, April, 1921; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1925; confd., June, 1930; offg. secty. to govt., educn. dept., April, 1928; dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept.; addl. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Oct., 1930; secty., ditto, Mar., 1932; lab. commr., Burma, Apl., 1935; secty., home dept., Apl., 1937.

PE, MAUNG WIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 5th May, 1912).—Educ. at New College, Oxford; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

PE, U. ZAW, C.I.E., B.A., F.R.C.I., late Indian Educl. Service. (b. 11th Nov., 1879).—Apptd. to the I.E.S., as inspr. of schools, Burma, 23rd Nov., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1931; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

PEACE, GORDON, Dept. of Explosives (ch. inspr.) (b. 4th Aug., 1890).—Apptd. ch. inspr. of explosives in India, 10th Oct., 1934.

PEACOCK, DAVID HENRY, M.A., D.Sc., F.I.C., F.C.S., Indian Educl. Service (prof. of chemistry, Univ. of Rangoon) (b. 9th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as lecturer in chemistry, univ. coll., Rangoon, 24th Oct., 1922; prof. of chemistry, Rangoon univ., Nov., 1935.

PEARCE, CHARLES FREDERICK BRYDE, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., fin. dept., Burma) (b. 13th June, 1892).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Aug., 1920; arrived, 19th Feb., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under sec.

to govt., June, 1923; offg. dep. sec., ditto, Dec., 1924; asst. settl. officer, March, 1925; settl. offr., July, 1927; dep. commr., Oct., 1930; dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Burma, June, 1934; secty. ditto, Nov., 1934; and again, Feb., 1936; apptd. representative of the Govt. of Burma, applicn. ctee. for the Indo-Burma finl. award, Jan., 1936.

PEARCE, CYRIL, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt.) (b. 26th Sept., 1892).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1923, as asst. dist. loco. supt., E.I. rly.; asst. supt., Dec., 1929; transpn. supt. (power), Aug., 1934.

PEARCE, FREDERICK SPENCER, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 26th June, 1879).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 6th Jan., 1899; dist. supt., Aug., 1908; supt., Feb., 1925; ret'd., April, 1930.

PEARCE, GEORGE ALFRED, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 28th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 18th Oct., 1920; offg. supt., May, 1923; addl. supt., Dec., 1926; supt., Aug., 1928; O.B.E., June, 1938.

PEARCE, NATHANIEL, late Indian State Railways (b. 20th June, 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr. rlys., 1st Oct., 1901; exec. engr., Oct., 1909; offg. agent, E.B. Rly., Feb., 1926; confd., Sept., 1926; ret'd., June, 1934.

PEARCE, ROGER RAMSDEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 28th Oct., 1914).—Educ. at Bishop's Stortford Coll., and Claire Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 13th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PEARCE, WILLIAM ROBERT, late Income Tax Dept., Punjab (b. 18th Oct., 1879).—Joined the service, 7th Feb., 1898; extra asst. commr., Apl., 1916; asst. commr. of income tax, Punjab, Dec., 1923; awarded Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 2nd class, 2nd June, 1923; offd. variously as commr. of income tax since Oct., 1927; commr. of income tax, N.W.F.P., Oct., 1932; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

PEARMAN, JAMES EDGAR, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Orissa) (b. 18th May, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 10th Oct., 1921; offg. supt., Jan., 1924; confd., June, 1926; awarded King's police medal; O.B.E., June, 1935; asst. to I.G. and supt., C.I.D., May, 1937.

PEARSE, ARTHUR ERNEST, V.D., late Indian State Railways (E.I. Rly.) (b. 25th May, 1876).—Joined the service, 3rd Oct., 1898, as asst. loco. supt.; dist. loco. supt., April, 1906; dep. loco. and carr. supt., April, 1913; carr. and wagon supt., N.W.Rly., Oct., 1922; offg. chief mechanical engr., E.I. Rly., March, 1925; ret'd., May, 1931.

PEARSON, Rev. Canon CYRIL GREENWOOD, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 3rd Nov., 1881).—Joined the service, 13th Nov., 1922; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1925; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

PEARSON, Sir HERBERT GRAYHURST, Barr., late India Office (b. 5th Aug., 1878).—Apptd. acting addl. judge, Calcutta high ct.,

Nov., 1920; judge, ditto, Feb., 1922; Kt., Jan., 1931; legal adviser and solicitor to the sec. of state, June, 1933; retd., Dec., 1938.

PEARSON, SIR RALPH SNEYD, C.I.E., F.L.S., late Indian Forest Service.—Joined the Service 4th Nov., 1898, as asst. consvr. of forests; dep. consvr., July, 1905; imperial forest economist, forest research inst. and coll., Dehra Dun, from May, 1909; C.I.E., June, 1920; consvr., May, 1922; retd., Aug., 1927; Knighted, June, 1933.

PEART, CHARLES LUBÉ, C.I.E., Col., I.A. (retd.) (b. 2nd April, 1876).—First commn. dated 22nd Jan., 1896; in mil. emp. to April, 1912, when he was apptd. sec., board of examrs., Calcutta; temply. reverted to mil. duty; C.I.E. March, 1919; retd., Aug., 1930.

PECK, LEONARD JASPER, M.A., M.C., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 2nd May, 1884).—Joined the service, 28th Mar., 1908; asst. acctnt.-genl., Aug., 1910; on military duty from 5th Mar., 1915 to 30th Mar., 1921; dep. acctnt.-genl., June, 1921; dep. comptr., Assam, Oct., 1922; dep. acct.-gen., central rev., Nov., 1925; offg. dep., auditor-gen., March, 1927; confd., Mar., 1929; offg. acct.-genl., central revenues, April, 1930; ditto, Punjab, April, 1931; comptr., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1931; offg. acct.-genl., U.P., Nov., 1932; dir. of army audit, Apl., 1933; retd., July, 1937.

PECK, NORMAN FREDERICK, C.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Bihar) (b. 12th May, 1886).—Educ. at Latymer Upper Sch., Hammersmith, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1909, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; transfid. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1916; offg. supt., Mayurbhanj State, April, 1917; confirmed, Dec., 1918; mag. and collr. and asst. pol. agent, Orissa Feudatory States, Nov., 1922; commr. excise and salt, Mar., 1932; C.B.E., June, 1932; offg. member, bd. of rev., Apl., 1936; commr. (prov. subs.), Apl., 1936.

PEDDIE, CROFTON, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 2nd Nov., 1875).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1898, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1908; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1921; confd., April, 1926; retd., Nov., 1930.

PEDDIE, KENNETH, M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 17th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service as junr. engr., 20th July, 1907; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; resdt. engr., Sept., 1919; exec. engr., July, 1925; offg. supt., way and works, Nov., 1925; offg. dep. ch. engr., variously from Sept., 1930; confd., Aug., 1935.

PEDLEY, JOHN EDWARD, M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 4th Dec., 1891).—Educ. at Repton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 2nd Aug., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. regr., high ct., Allahabad, March, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1930; confd., Jan., 1932.

PEEBLES, ALEXANDER SPALDING MACKIE, M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.), late Medical Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 20th Nov., 1876).—

First commn. 1st Sept., 1904; in mil. employ till May, 1911; supt., lunatic asylum, Berhampur, July, 1911; civil surgeon, Jan., 1923; retd., Nov., 1931.

PEEK, THOMAS ARTHUR, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, general dept.) (b. 14th Aug., 1884).—Apptd. to India Office, 27th June, 1919; transfid. to office of High Commr. for India, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, Jan., 1924.

PEEL, EARDLEY GARFORTH BRYAN, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. insp genl., United Provs.) (b. 12th Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th Dec., 1907; supt., Sept., 1920; C.I.E., June, 1923; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Feb., 1926; inspr.-genl., Kashmir durbar, Jan., 1933; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Mar., 1936; asst. inspr.-genl., rly. police, Apl., 1936; dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1937.

PEEL, ROLAND TENNYSON M.C., B.A., India Office (asst. secty., political (ext.) dept.) (b. 9th Oct., 1892).—Educ. at Bedford School, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. junr. clerk, correspondence dept., 28th July, 1919; war service—served in France with 6th Bn. Seaforth Highlrs. (T.F.), May, 1915, to Sept., 1917 (M.C., Jan., 1918); asst. principal, April, 1921; resident clerk, April, 1921, to March, 1922; asst. pte. sec. of sec. of state, March, 1922; pte. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, Oct., 1923; principal, Aug., 1924; actg. asst. secty., public and judl. dept., Nov., 1930, to Apl., 1931; and again, Sept., 1931; confd., Oct., 1937; asst. secty., political (ext.), dept., Oct., 1937.

PEERS, ELWIN VICTOR AUSTIN, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 13th Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 17th March, 1921; offg. dist. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., June, 1928.

PEGGE, CYRIL CUTHBERT COLBECK, late Indian Police (b. 26th Dec., 1881).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Nov., 1901, to 25th Jan., 1916; dep. supt., Jan., 1916; apptd. to I.P.S. as asst. supt., Oct., 1920; offg. supt., June, 1923; confd., Sept., 1930; retd., Sept., 1933.

PEJAVAR MOHAN RAO (see Mohan Rao, Pejavar).

PELLY, CORNELIUS JAMES, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 8th April, 1908).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood College, Co. Kildare, and Trinity College, Dublin; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on for. serv. as colon. offcr., Bahawalpur Govt., Nov., 1937.

PELLY, HUTCHISON RAYMOND, Lt.-Col., I.A., (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 22nd Sept., 1887).—First commn. Aug. 1906; apptd. to I.A., Nov., 1909; seconded as asst. commr., Burma, Jan., 1914; reverted to mil. employment, Dec., 1914, to Jan., 1920; asst. commr., Namtu, Feb., 1920; offg. dep. commr., March, 1923; transfid. to supply list, Jan., 1924; offg. dep. commr., July, 1926; confd., Apl., 1930.

PEMBERTON, FREDERICK, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 7th Sept., 1890).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., 18th Dec., 1929; offg. exec. engr., Mar., 1931; confd., Feb., 1938.

PENDHARKAR, GANPAT MAHADEO, B.A., B.L., late Central Provs., C.S. (b. Feb., 1879).—Joined the service as munsiif, 30th July, 1904; sub-judge, June, 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1932; confd., Apl., 1933; retd., Feb., 1934.

PENMAN, DAVID, C.I.E., D.Sc., late Dept. of Mines in India (b. 9th Apl., 1883).—Apptd. principal, Indian School of Mines, Dhanbad, 18th March, 1921; chief inspr., Oct., 1932; member, mining board, Bengal, Nov., 1932; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; retd., Apl., 1938.

PENNELL, KENNETH EUSTACE LEE, B.A., M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr. Assam) (b. 26th July, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1912; in mil. employ from 6th Dec., 1915, to 16th July, 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1922; exec. engr., Dec., 1925; offg. under-secty., P.W.D., Assam, Jan. 1928; asst. ch. engr. and under secty. to govt. (p.w.d.), Assam, Nov., 1934; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1936; confd., Nov., 1936; chief engr. and secty. (p.w.d.), June, 1938.

PENNELLS, SIDNEY GILBERT, India Office (actg. hr. exec. officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 4th May, 1896).—Apptd. clerical officer, 20th Oct., 1921; executive officer, May, 1927; actg. hr. ditto, May, 1938.

PENNY, CYRIL CUNNINGHAM, Indian State Rlys. (asst. transpn. (power), G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 19th Mar., 1891).—Asst. transpn. supt., junr. scale, Sept., 1924; senr. scale (provl.), June, 1936.

PENNY, JAMES DOWNING, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (chief secty. to govt. of Punjab) (b. 25th May, 1886).—Educ. at Marlborough Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. registrar, co-operative credit societies, Punjab, Mar., 1914; pol. asst. and commdt., B.M. Police, July, 1916; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Punjab, Oct., 1917; on mil. duty, June, 1918, to March, 1919; supt., Simla Hill States, April to May, 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1923; confd., Oct., 1933; offg. dep. sec., govt. of India, commerce dept., June, 1926; ditto, fin. dept., Dec., 1926; confd., March, 1927; sec., fin. dept., Punjab, Nov., 1927; offg. commr., variously from June, 1932; on special duty, Punjab civil secty., Aug., 1933; chief secty. to govt., and C.I.E., May, 1937.

PENNEY, THOMAS MAXWELL MONCRIEFF, Major, R.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 28th Mar., 1893).—First commn. dated 19th July, 1912; asst. supt. (proby.), 13th Oct., 1920; confd., Oct., 1922; supt., Dec., 1928.

PERCIVAL, PHILIP EDWARD, C.I.E., B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 11th Nov., 1872).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 25th Nov., 1895, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and joint addl. sess. judge; under-sec. to govt., pol., judl. and legisl. depts., Oct., 1903, to Aug., 1905; regr., high court, appellate side, April, 1909; judge and sess. judge, Sept., 1910; remembr. of legal affairs and sec. to govt., legal dept., July, 1911, to Aug., 1915; judge and sess. judge and agent or Sardars in Deccan, Nov., 1915; C.I.E., June,

1924; addl. judl. commr. of Sind, Sept., 1924; actg. judge, Bombay high ct., 1925; member of legisl. council, 1925-26; remembrancer of legal affairs and sec. to govt., legal dept., Bombay; actg. judge, Bombay high ct., June, 1926; offg. judl. commr. of Sind, Oct., 1926; confd., Dec., 1926; chairman, Bombay riots enquiry comm., June, 1929; retd., Nov., 1930.

PEREIRA, LEO VINCENT, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 28th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service, Feb., 1920; asst. engr., Feb., 1922; exec. engr., Jan., 1936.

PERKIN, EMIL ATHOL OWEN, C.I.E., Indian Police (inspr.-genl., Orissa) (b. 25th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Nov., 1909; supt., July, 1920; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1934; deputed as inspr.-genl., Orissa, Apl., 1936; inspr.-genl., July, 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937.

PERROTT, ARTHUR FINCH, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 13th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1912; asst. supt., May, 1914; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1919; supt., Mar., 1924; awarded bar to King's police medal, Jan., 1925; dist. offr., frontier constabulary, Dec., 1925; offg. commdt., frontier constab., April, 1926; offg. dep.-inspr.-genl., Feb., 1934.

PERRY, EDWARD WILLIAM, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and magte., Bombay) (b. 12th June, 1891).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 9th Dec., 1915, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; on mil. duty, Nov., 1917, to Feb., 1919; dep. sec. to govt., genl., etc., dept. (tempy.), Oct., 1922; addl. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept. (tempy.), June, 1923; offg. collr., Bombay, sub. dist., and Salsette dev. officer (in addn.), May, 1924; offg. collr. and supt. of stamps, April, 1926; member legisl. council, Feb., 1927; pte. sec. to govr., April, 1927; asst. sec., Indian Statutory Commission, Nov., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1930; secty., Re-organisation Ctte.; collr., Jan., 1933; offg. dir. of information and commr. of labour, Bombay, May, 1933; offg. jt. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Apl., 1934.

PERRY, WILLIAM ERIC, M.C., Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Survey of India (b. 2nd Jan., 1884).—First commn. dated 16th Mar., 1905; proby. asst. supt., 19th April, 1910; confd., April, 1912; on milty. duty from 1st Sept., 1915, to 5th Aug., 1918; dep. supt., Sept., 1924; supt., Jan., 1926; asst. surveyor-genl., April, 1927; dep. master, security printing, India (on probation), Dec., 1928; confd., Oct., 1930; retd., Oct., 1935.

PERSHAD, LADLI (see Ladli, P.).

PESTONJI SORABJI PATAK (see Patak, P.S.).

PETCH, DONALD BURMAN, M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt. of Burma, com. and ind. dept.) (b. 16th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' School, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1920; arrived, 19th Feb., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., Oct., 1923; offg. dep. commr. and settlt. officer, July, 1924; under-sec. to govt., educn., local govt. and public health dept., Nov.,

1925; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; ditto, (prov. subs.), Dec., 1934; secty. to finl. commr., Burma, Nov., 1934; addl. secty., rev. dept., Burma, July, 1936; secty., com. and indus. dept., May, 1937.

PETERS, CHARLES ROBERT, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 26th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1907; asst. supt., Nov., 1907; supt., Apl., 1921; asst. I.G. rly. police, Feb., 1937.

PETERS, FELIX THEODORE, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 12th Aug., 1882).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 9th Mar., 1905; confd., Oct., 1906; offg. dep. P.M.G., Bihar and Orissa, Jan., 1929; dep. P.M.G., Madras, Nov., 1929; offg. dep. dir.-genl., postal services, Mar., 1936; retd., Aug., 1937.

PETERSON, ERIC CUTHBERT JAMES, Indian Finance Dept. (dep. comptr., Assam) (b. 2nd Oct., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. accts. officer, 26th July, 1905; asst. examr., June, 1926; examr., local fund accts., Bihar and Orissa, July, 1927; dep. acct. genl., ditto, Apl., 1931; dep. comptr., Assam, June, 1934.

PETERSON, IVAN EMILLE KERR, Central Excises and Salt Dept., Northern India (suptg. mech. and elec. engr.) (b. 13th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 28th Nov., 1923; elec. and mech. engr., Khewra Mines (temp.), Nov., 1923; suptg. ditto, Oct., 1928.

PETIGARA, KAVASJI JAMSHEDJI, KHAN BAHADUR, C.I.E., O.B.E., I.S.O., late Indian Police (b. 24th Nov., 1878).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 2nd Nov., 1903, to 31st Mar., 1919; supt., Bombay city police, C.I.D., Apl., 1919; Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 1st class, Jan., 1923; I.S.O., July, 1926; promoted to I.P.S. as supt. and dep. commr. of police, Bombay, Oct., 1928; O.B.E., Jan., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1933; retd., Apl., 1937.

PETRIE, SIR DAVID, Kt., C.I.E., C.V.O., C.B.E., M.A., late Indian Police (b. 9th Sept., 1879).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. asst. dist. supt. of police, Dec., 1900; adjutant, Samana Rifles, Oct., 1907; supt. of police and ditto, Aug., 1908; asst. to dep. inspr.-gen., C.I. Dept. and Rly., April, 1909; asst. dir., criminal intelligence, India, March, 1911; special duty, Delhi, Dec., 1912; dep. inspr.-gen. of olice, May, 1915; C.I.E., June, 1915; spl. duty under dir., central intelligence, from March, 1916; O.B.E., Jan., 1918; C.B.E., Jan., 1919; M.V.O., Feb., 1921; dep. inspr.-gen. intelligence bureau, March, 1921; C.V.O., June, 1922; member of royal commn. on the superior civil services in India, 1923-24; dir., intelligence bureau, Oct., 1924; Kt., June, 1929; member, public services commn., Oct., 1931; chairman, ditto, Aug., 1932.

PETRIE, HARRY, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 4th Apl., 1898).—Apptl. clerical officer, store dept., India Office, March, 1920; transf. to office of High Commr. for I., Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Ja., 1926.

PETTY, JOHN, M.C., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 24th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1921, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1923.

PHARASKHANEWALA, BALKRISHNA SHAMRAO, B.A., LL.B., Judl. dept., Central Provs. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 10th July, 1884).—Joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1909; sub-judge, Nov., 1923; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1935.

PHELIPS, DENIS CHARLES PRESCOT, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 14th Dec., 1899).—Educ. at Gresham's Sch., Holt, Norfolk, and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Dec., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. settl. officer, Dec., 1924; warden, oilfields, May, 1926, and again, Mar., 1932; offg. dep. commr., variously from June, 1929; dep. commr. (prov.), Nov., 1936; confd., Jan., 1937.

PHEROZE MERWAN KHAREGAT, C.I.E., B.A. Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., industries and educn., United Provs.) (b. 29th May, 1890).—Educ. at Bombay and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 9th Dec., 1914, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1922; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, June to Nov., 1922; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1923; confd., Apl., 1931; offg. registrar, co-op. societies, Dec., 1926; secty. registrar, U.P. dept. of industries and educn., Mar., 1932; and again, Sept., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1935.

PHIBBS, ALFRED, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 5th Dec., 1873).—Educ. at King Edward VI.'s Gr. Sch., Stourbridge; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, Sch. dep., India Office, 16th April, 1894; joint editor, India Office List, from Oct., 1907, to Nov., 1914; minor staff clerk, Dec., 1907, to apptd. member, standing cttee. of enquiry into working of Indian transport service, Jan., 1912; staff clerk, Nov., 1914; junior clerk, store d pl., April, 1917; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; principal exec. officer, April, 1922; retd., June, 1932.

PHILBY, RALPH MONTAGUE, Capt., R.I.N., (nautical adviser to the govt. of India) (b. 1st Apl., 1884).—Apptd. principal officer, Mercantile Marine Dept., and offg. nautical adviser to the govt. of India, May, 1935.

PHILIP, CHARLES LYALL, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Dec., 1881).—Educ. at Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlement offr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1911; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1921; Mayurbhanj State, Oct., 1912, to May, 1916; offg. pol. agent, Feudatory States of Orissa, April, 1917; mag. and collr., and ditto, May, 1917; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), Aug., 1918, to Jan., 1919; addl. pol. agent, Orissa Feudatory States, Jan., 1919; commr., May, 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; offg. member, bd. of rev., May, 1934; confd., Dec., 1935; retd., Oct., 1937.

PHILIP, GEORGE MORRISON, D.S.O., M.C., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs., M.C., Mar., 1892).—Joined the service, (b. 29th 1919; asst. exec. engr., Feb., 1920; 10th Oct., Oct., 1924; retd., Dec., 1937.

PHILIP, MODEIL MANI (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 13th Feb., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Sept., 1928; arrived,

20th Oct., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., June, 1935; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1936.

PHILIPP, CHARLES HENRY, late Indian Forest Service (b. 12th Oct., 1881).—Arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1904; dep. consvr., Dec., 1909; offg. consvr., May, 1922; confd., Apl., 1925; ret'd., Oct., 1936.

PHILLIMORE, REGINALD HENRY, Col., D.S.O., R.E., ret'd., late Survey of India (b. 19th June, 1879).—First commn. dated 23rd June, 1898; joined survey dept., June, 1903, as asst. supt.; dep. supt., Aug., 1911; temp. reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to April, 1919; supt., Jan., 1926; offg. dir. March, 1926; dir., geodetic branch, May, 1928; dir., frontier circle, Dec., 1928; dir., map publ., Apl., 1930; ret'd., June, 1934.

PHILLIPPE, WILLIAM AUGUSTINE, Income Tax Dept., Bengal (asst. commr.) (b. 17th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, 11th July, 1904; asst. commr., Aug., 1934.

PHILLIPS, ALAN ANDREW, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (divl. comml. officer, N. W. Rly.) (b. 18th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 18th Nov., 1912; dist. traffic supt., 2nd July, 1924; divl. comml. officer, N.W. Rly., Feb., 1927; offg. comml. instructor rly. staff coll., Dehra Dun, Nov., 1929.

PHILLIPS, ARTHUR GORDON, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., United Provs.) (b. 20th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1906, as asst. supt.; supt., Aug., 1918; offg. dep. inspr. genl., Sept., 1930; confd., Sept., 1933; inspr. genl., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

PHILLIPS, E. H. (addl. dep. dir. of public information, home dept., Govt. of India).—Apptd. addl. dep. dir. of public informn., Jan., 1936.

PHILLIPS, HENRY FRANCIS TRAVERS, late Indian Police Service (Madras) (b. 15th Feb., 1876).—Joined the service, 15th Feb., 1899, and posted as asst. supt., Madras; supt., March, 1908; off. dep. inspr. gen., Oct., 1920; confd., Sept., 1921; commr. of police, town of Madras, Oct., 1925; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

PHILLIPS, JAMES ROLAND, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr. Bombay) (b. 1st Nov., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., March, 1927; confd., Sept., 1933; dep. secty. to govt., Bombay, P.W.D. (irrig. and rlys.), Nov., 1933.

PHILLIPS, JOHN, India Office (exec. officer, acct. genl's dept.) (b. 8th Feb., 1896).—Apptd. clerical officer, 18th July, 1921; exec. officer, May, 1927.

PHILLIPS, JOHN ALFRED STEELE, C.I.E., M.B., C.H.B., D.P.H., Col., I.M.S. (inspr. genl. of civil hosp., United Provs.) (b. 6th Oct., 1882).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1907; in milty. employ to 15th May, 1921; spl. malaria officer, United Provs., May, 1921; asst. dir. of public health, U.P., Oct., 1924; offg. dir. of public health, Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1926; confd., Apl., 1930; offg. inspr. genl. of civil hosp., B. and O., variously from Oct., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1934; inspr. genl. of civil hosp., United Provs, Mar., 1937.

PHILLIPS, KEITH DAVID, Indian Police (b. 5th Aug., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., Dec., 1937.

PHILLIPS, PERCY PHILIP, Ph.D., F.I.C., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 22nd Mar., 1877).—Joined the service, 7th Oct., 1904, as prof. of chemistry, Thomason Coll., Roorkee; off. variously as prinpl., ditto, from Oct., 1923; principal, Thomason Coll., Roorkee, Aug., 1931; ret'd., Sept., 1932; author of various works on chemical subjects.

PHILLIPS, WILLIAM HENRY, Factory Dept., Bombay (inspr.) (b. 22nd May, 1892).—Apptd. inspr. of factories, Bombay, 15th Oct., 1923, after non-gazetted service from 12th May, 1922; offg. ch. inspr., May, 1930.

PHILPOT, HAMLET CUNNINGHAM VACHELL, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 21st Oct., 1887).—Educ. at Repton, and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and collr., April, 1918; I.A.R.O., April to June, 1918, and Sept., 1918, to Jan., 1919; offg. sec., board of rev., June, 1924; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1926; confd. Nov., 1926; offg. secty. to govt., rev. and irrigation depts., Dec., 1928; confd., Feb., 1931; ret'd., Apl., 1936.

PHIPPS, ALBERT HENRY, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 22nd Feb., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 14th Jan., 1922; offg. dist. supt., March, 1928; confd., July, 1932.

PHIPSON, EDWARD SELBY, C.I.E., D.S.O., M.D., B.S., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Col., I.M.S. (inspr. genl., civil hosp., Assam) (b. 10th Mar., 1884).—First commn., dated 1st Aug., 1908; in mil. employ prior to Mar., 1914; reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to Apl., 1917; on depn. 1st Jan., to 28th Feb., 1922, in connection with H.R.H. Prince of Wales' visit; health offcr., Simla, Oct., 1915; offg. health offcr., Port of Aden, July, 1923; confd., Nov., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; inspr. genl., civil hosp. and prisons, Assam, Nov., 1937.

PHUKAN, RADHA NATH, Rai Bahadur, M.A., late Assam Civil Service (b. Dec., 1876).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1903, as proby. extra asst. commr.; confd., April, 1905; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1927; dep. commr., March, 1927; dir. of agric. and ind., Feb., 1928; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1930; ret'd., Dec., 1931.

PIARE LAL RASTOGI, BABU, B.A., LL.B., late Judicial Dept., United Provs. (b. 22nd Feb., 1877).—Joined the service as offg. munsi, 24th April, 1906; confd., May, 1909; sub-judge (s.p.t.), Dec., 1919; confd., Jan., 1921; temp. addl. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1927; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

PICKETT, WILLIAM DAVID, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 15th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service, 13th Dec., 1902; asst. secy. to agent, N.W. Rly., Sept., 1925; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

PIGGOTT, SIR THEODORE CARO, Kt., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Kingswood Sch., Bath, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 30th Nov., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1893; dep. commr., July, 1900; dist. and

sess. judge, Aug., 1900; addl. sess. judge, March, 1909; offg. 2nd addl. judl. commr., Oudh, April, 1909; 1st addl. judl. commr., April, 1911; judl. commr., Nov., 1911; judge, Allahabad high court, May, 1914; member, criminal procedure code committee, Sept. to Nov., 1916; Kt., June, 1924; retd., Oct., 1925.

PILDITCH, DENYS, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 6th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1912; supt., July, 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933, addl. dep. dir., intell. bureau, Nov., 1936.

PILGRIM, HENRY GUY ELLCOCK, D.Sc., F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 24th Dec., 1875).—Apptd. asst. supt., geological survey of India, 20th Oct., 1902; palæontologist, ditto, June, 1912; to mil. duty, June, 1915; on depn. to Persian Gulf and Persia, Oct., 1917, to March, 1918; supt., June, 1920; palæontologist, Nov., 1920; retd., Dec., 1930.

PILLAI, ARON APPADURAI, DIWAN BAHADUR, I.S.O., late Madras Civil Service (b. 13th June, 1876).—Joined the service, 15th April, 1898; dep. collr., Oct., 1914; asst. secty., bd. of rev., land rev., Feb., 1923; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Sept., 1927; sub-collr., Nov., 1928; offg. dir. of industries, July, 1929; I.S.O., June, 1931; retd., June, 1931.

PILLAI, A. SAVARINATHA (asst. commr., inc. tax, Madras) (b. 7th May, 1882).—Apptd. to income tax dept., central board of revenue, 1st March, 1923; asst. commr., northern range, Madras, 2nd March, 1925; ditto, western range, Coimbatore, July, 1931.

PILLAI, GOBIND KRISHNA, Public Works Dept., United Provs. (b. 10th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. sany. engr., 25th Dec., 1920; exec. engr., July, 1922; on depn. to Allahabad municipal bd., July, 1925.

PILLAI, NARAYANA RAGHAVAN, C.B.E., B.A. (Mad.) M.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (collr. of customs, Karachi) (b. 24th July, 1898).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Nov., 1922; arrived, 16th Jan., 1923, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; asst. collr. customs, Madras, Dec., 1927; dep. dir. of comml. intell., Calcutta, May, 1929; dep. sec. to govt. of India, commerce dept., Mar., 1932; temp. jt. secty., ditto, June, 1934; offg. ditto, Feb., 1936; collr., Karachi, Apl., 1936; C.B.E., Feb., 1937; officr. on specl. duty, govt. of India, commerce dept., Apl., 1937; dep. commr., confd. July, 1937.

PILLAI, T. G. P. S. (see Sanjivi Pillai, T. G. P.).

PILLAI, VELLAYAMBALAM KRISHNA BALAKRISHNA (see Balakrishna Pillai, V. K.).

PILLAY, THILLAINAYAKAM SIVARAMA SETU, M.A., B.L., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 14th Apl., 1899).—Joined the service, 7th Feb., 1923; attd. offer., office of auditor-genl., Feb., 1936; asst. auditor-genl. (personnel), Mar., 1936; 2nd secty., cent. bd. of rev., May, 1938.

PIM, Sir ALAN WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 17th May, 1871).—Educ. at Leipzig Univ., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of

1894; arrived, 4th Dec., 1895, and served in the N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., May, 1898; asst. settle. offr., Nov., 1898; joint mag., Dec., 1903; settlt. offr., Dec., 1903, to Dec., 1906; dep. commr., May, 1910; joint sec., board of rev., Oct., 1910; mag. and collr., and ditto, Nov., 1911; sec. to govt., April, 1912, to Aug., 1917; C.I.E., June, 1918; commr., May, 1921; member, board of revenue, April, 1925; C.S.I., Jan., 1926; temp. member, exec. council, United Prov., Dec., 1928; K.C.I.E., June, 1929; retd., July, 1930; author of a monograph on woollen fabrics.

PIMPLE, PRABHAKAR MAHIPAT, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 15th May, 1896).—Joined the service as dep. supt. of Police, Cent. Provs., 1st Sept., 1919; confd., July, 1921; dist. supt., Apl., 1937.

PINHEY, LOUIS ALEXANDER GORDON, Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Oct., 1901).—First commn., 14th July, 1921; in milty, employ to Nov., 1925; under-secty. to A.G.G., and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Apl., 1929; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Aug., 1929; ditto, Sibi, Dec., 1929; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, July, 1930; vice-consul, Zahidan, Nov., 1931; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Feb., 1933; secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, Oct., 1934; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1934; addl. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Apl., 1936; offg. pol. agent, ditto, in addn., Nov., 1936; secty. to A.G.G., resdt. and C.C., Baluchistan, Sept., 1937; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta-Pishin, June, 1938.

PINSELL, LEONARD GEORGE, C.I.E., Indian C.S.
