(secty. to govr., Bengal) (b. 10th June, 1896).—Educ. at City of London Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; manager, Dacca Nawab Estate, Nov., 1925; addl. depy. secty., fin., commerce and marine depts., Feb., 1930; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1930; confd., July, 1933; offg. lab. commr., Bengal, Apl., 1934; pte. secty. to govr. of Bengal, Aug., 1935; secty. to the Governor, Apl., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

PINTO, LIGUORI SIMON, late Indus. Dept., Madras (b. 19th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of indus., Madras, 3rd Aug., 1920; indus. engr., May, 1922; retd., Sept., 1937.

PIPE, THOMAS SYLVANUS, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 20th May, 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1920; retd., Aug., 1937.

PIR, AHSAN-UD-DIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 7th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at Government High School, Ludhiana, Ismalia High School, Amritsar, Punjab University and Queen's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 4th October, 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., name changed, Nov., 1934, to AHSAN-UD-DIN (q.v.).

PITAMBAR PANT (see Pant Pitambar).

PITAMBERDAS LAVJI THACKER (see Thacker, P. L.).

PITAM CHAND AGRAWAL, RAI BAHADUR, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. Jan., 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1929; Rai Bahadur, Jan., 1931.

PITKEATHLY, Sir JAMES STOTT, Kt., C.M.G., C.I.E., C.V.O., C.B.E., D.S.O., Indian Stores Dept. (chief contr. of stores) (b. 10th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1909, as electrical inspr.; electrical engr., April, 1911; C.V.O., Dec., 1911; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Sept., 1911; asst. dir. of works, electrical and mechanical sections, Mesopotamia, from Aug., 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; chief contr. of stores, Indian Stores Dept., 1st Jan., 1922; on foreign service under Ceylon govt., June, 1928; C.M.G., Jan., 1930; Knighted, June, 1933; on spl. duty under Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Nov., 1936, to Jan., 1937.

PITT CHARLES HARRY, M.C., M.Sc., Northern late India Salt Revenue Dept. (b. 11th Mar., 1893).—Joined the dept., 20th Feb., 1921; offg. gen. manager, salt mines, Punjab, Sept., 1925; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

PITT, WILLIAM HENRY, late Indian Police Service (Madras) (b. 29th Apr., 1885).—Joined the service, 19th Dec., 1904, as asst. supt.; supt., Sept., 1914; on foreign service as commr. of police, Travancore, 26th March, 1921; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

PLATT, ARTHUR JAMES, Indian C.S. (sub.-collr., Madras) (b. 13th Aug., 1908).—Educated at Liverpool Collegiate School and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr., Mar., 1934.

PLATT, SYDNEY ERNEST, O.B.E., B.Sc., Public Health Dept. (engrg. bch.) Bihar (suprg. engr.).—Apptd. suptg. engr., public health dept., 3rd Feb., 1925; O.B.E., Jan., 1938.

PLATTS, MATHEW GEORGE, O.B.E., M.C., B.Sc., M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 17th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918.

PLAYNE, HAROLD FRANCIS, B.A., V.D. Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 14th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service as mil. acctnt., 2nd cl., Dec., 1909; confd. as cl. I offcr., Aug., 1929; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Apr., 1930; confd. as comd. contr., June, 1932.

PLEW, SIDNEY FERDINAND, Indian Police (b. 8th May, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Nov., 1936.

PLOWDEN, CHARLES TERENCE CHICHELE, C.I.E., Lt.-Col. I.A. (ret'd.), late Indian Political Service (b. 6th Feb., 1883).—In mil. employ from 27th Aug., 1902; apptd. probnr., pol. dept., and supy. asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., 25th Jan., 1908; pol. asst., May, 1909; asst. commr., D.I. Khan, Oct., 1909; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, June, 1910; on spl. duty, Bundelkhand, April, 1911, to Feb., 1912; on spl. duty under agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Feb. to Nov., 1912; asst. pol. agent, E. Rajputana States, May, 1914; temply. reverted to mily. duty, Oct., 1914; 1st asst. to resdt. in Mysore and sec. to chief commr.,

Coorg, April, 1919; vice-presdt., Council of Regency, Cooch Behar State, Nov., 1923; secty. to agent to govr.-genl. and ch. commr., Baluchistan, March, 1928; pol. agent in Kalat, Nov., 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; resdt., Mysore and ch. commr., Coorg, May, 1933; ret'd., Feb., 1938.

PLOWDEN, PHILIP PETER MEREDYTH CHICHELE, B.A. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.), Indian C.S. (b. 28th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at Eltham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 13th Dec., 1913, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Aug., 1919, to Nov., 1919; joint mag., April, 1921; addl. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1922; sess. and sub-judge, Feb., 1927; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1927; confd., June, 1929; offg. judl. secty. to govt., United Provs., May, 1933, to Oct., 1934.

PLOWMAN, ALBERT CHARLES, India Office (supr., tel. and mails bch.) (b. 16th June, 1883).—Educ. at Bedford Modern Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 12th June, 1901; transfed. to finl. dept., Jan., 1917; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1919; higher clerical officer, Apr., 1922; supt., tel. and mails bch., July, 1938.

PLUMB, WALTER CYRIL, Indian Police (supr., N.W.F.P.) (b. 14th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service, 26th Dec., 1915; asst. supt., June, 1919; asst. dist. officer, Oct., 1921; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924; supt., Oct., 1924; dist. officer, frontier constabulary, Sept., 1925, to Mar., 1937.

PLUMMER, JOHN COATES, late Indian Police Service (b. 26th Oct., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 6th Dec., 1927; ret'd., July, 1932.

PLUMPTRE, CYRIL MACDONALD, M.R.C.S. (Eng.) L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (profir., medl. coll., Madras) (b. 27th Dec., 1884).—First commn. dated 29th July, 1911; in milty. employ to 19th March, 1923; medl. offr., Kathiawar pol. agency, April, 1923; offg. civil surg., Nov., 1924; confd., April, 1926; offg. presy. surgeon, Bombay, Apr., 1932; profir., medl. coll., Madras, Mar., 1934.

PLUNKETT, PERCY EDWARD, late Indian Forest Service.—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1898; dep. consvr., March, 1920; ret'd., Mar., 1929.

PLYMEN, FRANCIS JOSEPH, C.I.E., A.C.G.I., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 7th Feb., 1879).—Apptd. agricl. chemist, Central Provs., 5th Apr., 1906; principal, agricl. coll., Nagpur, in addn. from April to Sept., 1914; served under govt. of India, army dept., from June, 1917; dep. dir. of agric., Central Provs., July, 1918; offg. dir. of agric., June, 1923; confd., Sept., 1924; C.I.E., June, 1929; ret'd., Feb., 1934.

POLLARD, ARMELL RICHARD, B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 20th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service 1st Oct., 1905; as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1920; confd., Nov., 1924; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

POLLARD, RICHARD CHARLES, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Sept., 1908).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 26th Novr., 1929; confd., Jan., 1933.

POLLOCK, AUBRAY KEATINGE HALLIDAY, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 8th Feb., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. officer, 16th March, 1916; dist. medl. officer, April, 1908; ch. medl. officer, Aug., 1924; hon. surg. to Viceroy, Jan., 1927; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

POLLOCK, JOHN DOUGLAS ALEXANDER, Indian Police (b. 28th June, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, United Provs., Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

POLLOCK, RONALD EVELYN, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Nagpur) (b. 17th April, 1891).—Educ. at Harrow and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 11th Dec., 1915, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; ditto, and under-sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1920; sub-judge, Nov., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1924; confd., Dec., 1928; secty., legal dept., Central Provs., March, 1930; offg. addl. judt. commr., June, 1932, and again, May, 1933; confd., Jan., 1935; judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936.

PONT, LEON VICTOR, Indian State Rlys. (dist. signal engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 7th Dec., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. signal engr., 23rd March, 1909; I.A.R.O., 20th Feb., 1916; dist. signal engr., March, 1924; offg. dep. ch. engr. (signals), N.W. rly., Mar., 1936.

POPE, ALFRED HENRY GEORGE, M.B.E., India Office (higher clerical officer, tel. and mails bch.) (b. 24th Mar., 1904).—Apptd. clerical officer, 14th Oct., 1921; actd. higher ditto, Nov., 1932; confd., Dec., 1934; M.B.E., May, 1937.

POPE, JAMES ALISTER, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd June, 1883).—Educ. at Eton and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1907, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; compiler, genl. admn. report, 1910-11; under sec. to govt., rev. and finl. depts., Bombay, Aug., 1915; on mil. duty, 1916-1918; excise commr. for Central India, June, 1923; collr. and mag., June, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

PORTEOUS, JOHN BRIAN, India Office (exec. offr., acct. genl.'s dept.) (b. 29th Aug., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 13th Oct., 1919; clerical officer, Jan., 1920; exec. offr., Dec., 1926.

PORTER, ALFRED ERNEST, Indian C.S. (jt. secty., agric. and indus. dept., Bengal) (b. 2nd Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Manchester Grammar Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1922; arrived, 14th Dec., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. dir. of comml. intell. dept., govt. of India, April, 1925; offg. under-sec., govt. of Bengal, pol. and appt. dept.; confd., March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1927; supt., census operations, Bengal, Apl., 1930; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1933; confd., June, 1933; jt. secty., agric. and indus. dept., Bengal, July, 1935.

PORTER, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Burma Frontier Service (dep. commr.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1887).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Jan., 1911; asst. supt. (B.F.S.), Dec., 1922; dep. commr., Mar., 1938.

PORTER, Rev. JOHN, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (senr. chaplain) (b. 7th Apl., 1889).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 2nd Oct., 1921; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1923; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1931.

PORTER, MALCOLM TINDAL, M.C., Lt.-Col., R.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th Nov., 1882).—First commn., dated 4th Feb., 1901; apptd. asst. engr., state rlys., 12th Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Sept., 1911; supt. of works, Lower Burma Rlys. Survey, April, 1912, to Sept., 1913; on milty. duty from Oct., 1914, to Sept., 1919; offg. suptg. engr., Nov., 1923; employed on Burma-Siam rly. survey from 12th July, 1925; dep. ch. engr., N.W. Rly., April, 1927; ret'd., July, 1934.

PORTER, NOEL TINDAL, late Indian C.S. (b. 9th Nov., 1894).—Educ. at St. Edmund's Sch., Canterbury, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Nov., 1919; served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr. from 25th March, 1920; offg. dep. commr., May, 1924; dep. commr., (provl.) July, 1929; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

PORTER, RICHARD REGINALD MAITLAND, M.C., M.B., Major, I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Bihar and Orissa Civil Medl. dept.—First commn. dated 25th Jan., 1913; in milty. employ prior to 7th Feb., 1927; profir. of surgery, Patna medl. coll., Feb., 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1931.

POTTER, ARTHUR KINGSCOTE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 7th Apl., 1905).—Educ. at Charterhouse and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928; and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty., home and pol. dept., Aug., 1931; dep. secty., fin. dept., Burma, Mar., 1934; offg. dep. commr., June, 1934; contr. of fin. (defence), Apl., 1937.

POULTON, HENRY MORTIMER, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 23rd Apl., 1898).—First commn., dated 18th June, 1917; joined the pol. dept., 16th May, 1922; supy. asst. commr., Bannu, March, 1924; asst. commr., ditto, April, 1924; asst. commr., Kohat, March, 1925; asst. pol. agent, Zhob, Dec., 1927; pol. agent, Chagai, and dep. commr., Shorarnd, Aug., 1928; pol. agent, Zhob, Nov., 1928; ditto, Kalat, June, 1929; ditto, Chagai, Aug., 1929; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Sibi, Aug., 1930; pol. agent, Kalat, Nov., 1931; ditto Quetta-Pishin, May, 1932; ditto, Haroati and Tonk, Oct., 1932; ditto E. Rajputana States, Sept., 1934; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar agency, Aug., 1935.

POUND, GRAHAM TREVOR, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 23rd Jan., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1913; on mil. service from 4th Oct., 1915, to 17th May, 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; ret'd., May, 1931.

POUNDE, HERBERT, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 27th May, 1881).—Held non-gazetted appt. from June, 1898; dep. supt., Apl., 1918; supt. of post offices, Sept., 1919; dep. P.M.G., traffic, Patna, Mar., 1926; ditto, Madras, Nov., 1927; ditto, Punjab and N.W.F., Mar., 1930; ret'd., Apl., 1932.

POUNDE, HERBERT RICHARD GILDER, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 31st Jan., 1905).—Apptd. exec. officer, 10th Dec., 1935, after previous unstead. service from 8th Aug., 1927.

POWELL, EDWIN VALENTINE MURRAY, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. officer, Burma Rlys.) (b. 14th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 23rd July, 1920; offg. variously as dist. loco. supt., from Mar., 1921; transpn. officer, Apr., 1929; offg. dep. carr. and wagon supt., Oct., 1936.

POWELL, JOHN GLYNN, Indian Service of Engineers (ch. engr., Bihar) (b. 27th Sept., 1884).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1925; confd., July, 1926; ch. engr. and secty. to govt., p.w.d., buildings and roads bch., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1933.

POWELL, Rev. LLEWELLYN MONTAGUE SAXON, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 8th Feb., 1906).—Joined the service Feb., 1936; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1938.

POWELL, RICHARD, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 4th Apr., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1908; in milty. employ from 19th Apr., 1915, to 4th June, 1916; asst. to inspr.-genl., C.I.D., Aug., 1918; supt., May, 1922; prinpl., P.T. school, Moradabad, Dec., 1925; asst. to inspr. genl., Jan., 1937.

POWELL, WILLIAM JACKSON, C.I.E., B.A., M.D., B.Ch., Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Central Provs. (b. 17th Dec., 1881).—In mil. employ to Aug., 1920; apptd. supt., central jail, Jubbulpore, 18th Aug., 1920; offg. inspr.-gen. of prisons, Cent. Provs., Jan., 1921; confd., Feb., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1930; offg. inspr.-genl., civil hosp., Central Provs., Mar., 1933; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, Army Dept., Dec., 1934; hon. surg. to the King, Feb., 1937.

POWELL-PRICE, JOHN CADWAGAN, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (dep. dir. of public instrn., United Provs.) (b. 11th June, 1888).—Joined the service, 9th July, 1920; head master, govt. high school, Allahabad, Dec., 1920; prinpl., govt. inter. coll., Almora, April, 1921; offg. inspr. of schools, April, 1926; confd., Jan., 1929; asst. dir. of public instrn., United Provs., Mar., 1934; offg. dep. dir., ditto, Mar., 1936, and again, Oct., 1936; confd., Oct., 1937.

POWER, GERVASE BUSHE, C.I.E., M.C., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 17th Aug., 1883).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to telegraph dept. as asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1905; ditto, engrg., Apr., 1914; deputed to field service, Mesopotamia, Sept., 1915 (three times mentioned in despatches), and to field service (Afghan War), May, 1919; C.I.E., Dec., 1916; on deputation with N.W. Rly., Nov., 1920; divl. engr., tels., Lahore, Jan., 1927; dir., posts and tels., Sind and Baluchistan, Aug., 1929; dir. tel. engrg., June, 1932; offg. p.m.g., Madras, Sept., 1934; p.m.g., Burma, May, 1935; ret'd., Oct., 1936.

POWER, ROBIN TURENNE, Indian State Rlys. (dep. traffic manager, Burma Rlys.) (b. 9th Oct., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 29th July, 1908; dist. traffic supt.,

July, 1924; offg. dep. traffic manager, June, 1932; confd., Aug., 1932; offg. traffic manager, Mar., 1933.

POWIS, HARRY BADAN, late Indian Police (b. 4th Nov., 1880).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1902; dep. supt., Apr., 1923; appt. to I.P. as supt., Feb., 1935; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

POWIS, LEONARD JOHN PEARSON, M.I.St.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 9th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st June, 1915; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1922; exec. engr., June, 1924.

POYAMANI NAGASWAMI NATESH AYYAR, L.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 16th May, 1874).—Joined the service, 10th June, 1896; asst. engr., June, 1897; exec. engr., June, 1908; offg. sany. engr., Nov., 1919, May, 1923, and April, 1926; offg. mechnl. engr. to govt., April, 1926; offg. sany. engr., May, 1928; ret'd., May, 1929.

PRABH SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR, Sardar late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th July, 1883).—Joined the service, 17th Sept., 1906; asst. engr., Sept., 1907; exec. engr., Apr., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., Apr., 1928, and again, Apr., 1932; confd., May, 1935; ret'd., May, 1937; member pub. serv. com., Punjab and N.W.F. Prov., May, 1937.

PRABHU DATTA SHASTRI, M.A., B.Sc., Ph.D. (Kiel), Indian Educl. Service (vice-prinpl., Rajshahi Coll.) (b. 24th Mar., 1885).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 14th Oct., 1905, to 30th Nov., 1911; profr., presy. coll., Aug., 1912; vice-prinpl., Hooghly Coll., July, 1926; offg. profr., presdy. coll., Calcutta, July, 1928; vice-prinpl., Rajshahi coll., Jan., 1929; offg. prinpl., ditto, May, 1933; posted from Bombay presdy.

PRADHAN, DWARKANATH RAMCHANDRA, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind) (b. 8th June, 1906).—Educ. at Bombay University and Fitzwilliam House, Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, December, 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PRADHAN, Sir GOVIND BALVANT, Kt., B.A., LL.B., Apptd. member, govt.'s exec. council, Bombay, Sept., 1928; Kt., June, 1931.

PRAGER, Miss MILDRED F., B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 17th July, 1882).—Apptd. offg. inspectress of girls' schools, 2nd Feb., 1920; confd., Jan., 1926; ret'd., July, 1937.

PRALL, SAMUEL REGINALD, M.D., B.C., M.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Bombay (supt., St. George's hosp.) (b. 18th Feb., 1891).—Date of first commn., 25th Feb., 1921; in milty. employ until 23rd June, 1930; civil surg., June, 1932; offg. supt., St. George's hosp., Mar., 1934; confd., July, 1934.

PRAMANIK, SUSHIL KUMAR, M.Sc., Ph.D., Meteorological Dept. (meteorologist) (b. 7th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as meteorologist on probn., 25th Feb., 1927; confd., Feb., 1929.

PRANCE, BASIL CAMDEN, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Rugby and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 17th Dec. 1907, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. offr.; transfd. to Bengal, April, 1912;

jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1913; I.A.R.O., June, 1917, to May, 1919; O.B.E., (military), Jan., 1919; mag. and collr., Jan., 1920; retd., July, 1924; apptd. actg. magte. and collr., Dacca, Aug., 1932; apptd. actg. commr., Mar., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; retd., May, 1935.

PRANCE, GEOFFREY BERNARD SANDEMAN, Indian Police (sup., N.W.F.P.) (b. 28th July, 1896).—Joined the service, 29th April, 1919; asst. supt., May, 1921; addl. supt., April, 1925; supt., Aug., 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1927; awarded bar to ditto, Jan., 1928; dist. officer, frontier constabry., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1932.

PRASAD, AMBIKA (see Ambika P.).

PRASAD, BISHNATH (see Bishnath Prasad).

PRASAD, JAGAT (see Jagat Prasad).

PRASAD, KUNWAR JAGDISH (see Jagdish Prasad, Kunwar.)

PRASAD, JWALA (see Jwala Prasad).

PRASAD, KANCHI (see Kanchi Prasad).

PRASAD, KRISHNA (see Krishna Prasad).

PRASAD, MAHABIR (see Mahabir Prasad).

PRASAD, NIRINJAN, GOYAL, LALA (see Nirinjan, P. G. L.).

PRASAD, RAGHUNATH (see Raghunath Prasad)

PRASAD, RAM (see Ram Prasad).

PRASAD, SHYAM SUNDER (see Shyam Sunder Prasad).

PRASADA, SHANKAR, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. United Provs.) (b. 11th March, 1905).—Educ. at Allahabad University and Magdalen College, Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Apl., 1935.

PRASAD, SHANTI (see Shanti Prasad).

PRASAD, SITAL (see Sital Prasad).

PRASANNA KUMAR ACHARYA, M.A., D.Litt., Ph.D., Indian Educl. Service (prof. Muir central coll., Allahabad) (b. 21st Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as prof., Muir central coll., Allahabad, 1st Dec., 1920; with Allahabad Univ. from 1st July, 1922.

PRASHAD, BAINI (see Baini Prashad).

PRASHAD, DURGA (see Durga Prashad).

PRASHAD, KAMTA (see Kamta Prashad).

PRATAP BIKRAM SHAH, KUNWAR, late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 24th Oct., 1894).—Educ. at Mayo Coll., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1914, as proby. dep. collr.; asst. commr., 10th Jan., 1922; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, March, 1923, to Oct., 1924, and from March, 1925; asst. commr., Nov., 1925; retd., Aug., 1931.

PRATAP, RAM (see Ram Pratap).

PRATT, EDWARD BROUGHTON, Indian Police (b. 22nd Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 26th Nov., 1926; offg. comdt. of police, Aden, Apl., 1933.

PRATT, Sir HENRY SHELDON, Kt., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 26th Jan., 1873).—Educ. at King's Grammar School, Warwick, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 30th Nov., 1896, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dist. judge, May, 1905; divl. judge, April, 1914; addl. judge, chief court of Lower Burma, Nov., 1917; judge, chief court of Upper Burma, May, 1919; judge, high ct., Rangoon, Dec., 1922; Kt., June, 1928; retd., July, 1930.

PRATT, WALTER PHILIP R., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 11th May, 1879).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 13th June, 1902; dist. traffic supt., May, 1914; offg. dep. traffic manager variously from Apl., 1919; retd., Feb., 1932.

PREM CHAND, Puri, Lala, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 20th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service, 24th Oct., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

PREM NATH, LALA, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 1st Jan., 1884).—Apptd. asst. consvr., Punjab, 19th July, 1922; on deputation to Chamba State from 19th April, 1926, to Jan., 1931; dep. consvr., Jan., 1931.

PREM SWARUP, Indian Police (b. 16th Aug., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs. on probn., Feb., 1938.

PRESCOTT, RICHARD GORDON BATHGATE, Indian Police (depy. commr. of police) (b. 30th May, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 21st Dec., 1915; dist. supt., Oct., 1923; awarded King's police medal, June, 1932; dep. commr., Rangoon police, May, 1937.

PRESSWELL, HENRY EDWARD, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st Jan., 1879).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1895; asst. supt., Jan., 1913; dist. supt., April, 1920; divl. traffic manager, April, 1920; asst. comml. manager, March, 1926; supt., Oct., 1926; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., March, 1927; confd., Mar., 1930; offg. dep. agent, Apl., 1932; retd., Nov., 1935.

PRESSWELL, THOMAS GEORGE, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 12th Mar., 1877).—Joined the service as dist. supt., 15th March, 1920; dep. ch. comml. manager (s.p.t.), May, 1925; offg. junr. dep. agent, May, 1925; confd., March, 1927; senr. dep. agent, May, 1928; retd., Nov., 1931.

PRIA DAS, TANDAN, Rai Sahib, Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 6th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service as qualified student, 19th Sept., 1908; temp. engr., Sept., 1909; asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1923; apptd. to the I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1927; exec. engr., Jan., 1932.

PRICE, ARCHIBALD MILNER, Dept. of indus. and labour, Govt. of India (asst. secty., dept. of labour) (b. 18th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service, 8th Dec., 1906; supt., dept. of indus. and lab., July, 1927; offg. asst. secty., ditto, public works bch., June, 1932; confd., Oct., 1934; asst. secty., dept. of lab., Nov., 1937.

PRICE, EARDLEY EDWARD CARNAC, A.C.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 11th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service, 11th June, 1919; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1920; asst. dir. of comml. audit, Aug., 1925; asst. acct.-genl., Bombay, Nov., 1928; asst. dir. comml. audit, Bombay, Nov., 1928; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, May, 1932; services lent to Jodhpur Darbar, Jan., 1933.

PRICE, ERNEST, M.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India (prinpl. exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 14th Dec., 1879).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store deptt, 9th March, 1898; supervisor, April, 1915; M.B.E., Jan., 1918; transfid. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; prinpl. exec. officer, May, 1926.

PRICE, GLADYS MAUD, India Audit Office, (hr. exec. officer) (b. 22nd May, 1898).—Apptd. clerical officer, 20th Jan., 1922; exec. officer, Oct., 1926; hr. ditto, Sept., 1938.

PRICE, MURRAY ROKEBY, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 29th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 23rd May, 1924; offg. supt., May, 1929; confd., Feb., 1934.

PRICE, WILLIAM ALAN BOURNE, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 28th April, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., April, 1925; confd., May, 1929.

PRICHARD, HENRY MONTAGUE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th May, 1887).—Educ. at Marlborough Brasenose Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1911, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. commr.; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1912; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Oct., 1918, to Jan., 1919; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1922; offg. sec., fin. and rev. depts., June, 1927; confd. and dep. commr., Oct., 1927; finl. secty. to govt., Assam, Nov., 1934; ret'd., Aug., 1938.

PRIDAY, LESLIE MORRIS, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 31st May, 1883).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., Aug., 1920; suptg. engr., Jan., 1925; ret'd., May, 1934.

PRIDEAUX, EDWIN TERREY, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 16th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Latymer Upper School, Hammersmith, and Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. collr. and mag.

PRIDMORE, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provinces) (b. 17th Aug., 1910).—Educ. at Rugby and Queen's College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Nov., 1936.

PRIESTLEY, GERALD WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (collr. and d.m., Madras) (b. 12th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at West Monmouthshire Sch., Pontypool, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 29th Nov., 1912, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; supt., Pudukottai State, Aug. to Sept., 1915; spl. asst. settlt. offr., Nov., 1916; sub-collr. and jt. mag. and ditto, Oct., 1917; special settlt. officer, July, 1919, to March, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1927; confd., Aug., 1930; commr. of Coorg, Nov., 1937; commr. of labour, Nov., 1936 to Oct., 1937.

PRING, NOEL GRATTAN, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 30th Dec., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 6th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Dec., 1926.

PRINGLE, IVAN TERENCE ST. CLAIR, M.A., Indian State Rlys. (dep. agent (personnel), E. B. Rly.) (b. 20th Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 29th Dec., 1911; dist. traffic supt., Dec., 1920; offg. asst. agent, E.B. Rly., May, 1926; offg. dep. secty., rly. bd., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. agent, E.B. Rly., Dec., 1929; offg. dep. agent (personnel), E.B. rly., Jan., 1933; confd., Apl., 1934.

PRINGLE, JOHN MACKAY, B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 27th April, 1888).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, Merton Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 29th Nov., 1912, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., April, 1918; I.A.R.O., Aug. to Oct., 1918; jt. mag. and dep. collr., April, 1919; addl. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1924; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1925; ret'd., June, 1931.

PRINGLE REGINALD JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 23rd Mar., 1910).—Educ. at Felsted School and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. pte. secty. to govr., Bengal, Feb., 1936.

PRIOR, CHARLES GEOFFREY, C.I.E., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 9th Dec., 1896).—First commn. dated 14th July, 1915; joined the pol. dept. 24th Aug., 1923; under-sec. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Oct., 1924; under-sec. to pol. resident in Persian Gulf, Aug., 1926; sec. to ditto, April, 1927; under-secty. to A.G.G., Punjab States, Mar., 1929; pol. agent, Bahrein, Apl., 1929; offg. dep. secty., for. and pol. dept., June, 1933; confd., May, 1934, offg. jt. secty., ditto, May, 1934; and again Apl., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1936; prime minister, Alwar State, Nov., 1936.

PRIOR, HENRY CARLOS, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., fin. dept., Bihar) (b. 6th Jan., 1890).—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 30th Nov., 1914; and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; temply. to army dept., Oct., 1915 (comm'dt., 3rd (Naga Hills) Bn., Assam Rifles), to Oct., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1921; confd., Jan., 1929; manager, Bettiah wards estate, June, 1926; addl. secty. to govt., rev. dept., July, 1929; secty., ditto, Oct., 1931; secty., fin. dept., B. and O., Apl., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1936.

PRISTON, W. H., F.R.C.V.S., late Indian Veterinary Service (United Provs.) (b. 26th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as third supt., 1st Dec., 1920; offg. veterinary adviser to govt., March, 1925; supt., Nov., 1925; offg. dir. vety. services, Cent. Provs., Mar., 1930; ret'd., July, 1931.

PRITAM SINGH, BRAR, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 17th Nov., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 7th Aug., 1921; offg. supt., Feb., 1928; confd., Nov., 1933.

PRITCHARD, HUGH ROBERT NORMAN, C.I.E., O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. ret'd. (b. 11th Apl., 1879).—First commn. dated 20th May, 1899; in mil. emp. to June, 1904, when he was apptd. sup. asst. commr.; pol. asst., Feb., 1906; asst. to agent. govr.-gen., Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmer-Merwara, April, 1913; pol. agent, Bhopawar, July, 1913; pol. offr., Bundi, Oct., 1913; second asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Nov., 1913; reverted temply. to mil. duty, March, 1915; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Dec., 1917; 1st asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, May, 1919; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; pol. agent, Bhopal, April, 1921; res'dt. in Mewar, April, 1924; pol. agent, Haraoti and

Tonk, Oct., 1926; pol. agent, Eastern Rajputana States, March, 1927; to gov.-agent genl. in C.I., Mar., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1929; agent to gov. genl., Madras States, Oct., 1930; ret'd., Apl., 1933.

PRITCHARD, JOSEPH WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (sub.-collr., and jt. magc., Madras) (b. 5th Aug., 1903).—Educ. at Newport (Salop) Grammar Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927; and served in Madras as asst. magte. and collr.; sub.-collr., July, 1932; offg. mag. and collr., variously from May, 1936; sub. collr. and jt. magte., Oct., 1937.

PROCTOR, ALFRED HENRY, D.S.O., M.D., Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 21st Jan., 1879).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1904; arrived, 9th June, 1905; in mil. employ. to Feb., 1911; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, home dept., from Aug., 1914; offg. resdt. surgn., presidency gen. hosp., Calcutta, June, 1920; offg. surg. supt., ditto, Mar., 1925; confd., July, 1927; offg. surg.-genl. with govt. of Bengal, Mar., 1933; inspr.-genl. of civil hospss., United Provs., Dec., 1933; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

PROCTOR, ERNEST, M.C., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., Burma Rlys.) (b. 3rd July, 1894).—Joined the service, 15th Apl., 1920, as asst. engr.; exec., engr., Burma rlys., July, 1933.

PROMODE KUMAR DEY (see Dey, P. K.).

PROPHULLA CHANDRA KANJILAL, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1886).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1911; dep. consvr., Oct., 1923; with govt. of Assam from Sept., 1929-Oct., 1931.

PROTHERO, EDWIN LYNDON, Indian Service of Engrs. (under-secty. to govt. (estab.), Punjab) (b. 20th June, 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Dec., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; addl. secty. to govt., rev. dept., July, 1929; under-secty. to govt. (estab.), Punjab, Feb., 1935.

PROUDLOCK, ROBIN, Indian State Rlys. (divl. comml. officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 12th June, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 8th Nov., 1914; asst. dist. do., Jan., 1922; offg. dist. do., Oct., 1923 (lent to Q.M.Q.'s branch, Jan., 1927); divl. transpn. officer, July, 1927; divl. comml. officer, Oct., 1930.

PRU, U SAW HLA, B.A., B.L., Burma Civil Service (excise commr., Burma) (b. 24th Nov., 1886).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., 22nd Feb., 1915; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1930; secty. to govt., judl. dept., Burma, Oct., 1933; excise commr., Burma, Mar., 1936.

PRYDE, ARTHUR WALTER, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 3rd Nov., 1896).—Joined the service, 26th Aug., 1923; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; confd., Oct., 1932; lab. officr., Bombay, Nov., 1935; awarded Indian Police medal, Feb., 1937.

PUCKLE, FREDERICK HALE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt., Punjab) (b. 8th June, 1889).—Educ. at Uppingham, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1913, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from

May, 1915, to April, 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), Oct., 1925; C.I.E., June, 1930; fin. secty. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1930; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1933; confd., Feb., 1935; employed on spl. duty in the Home Dept., Govt. of India, May, 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938.

PUDDEN, HEDLEY HERBERT CRAWFORD, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 1st Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 24th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Oct., 1928.

PUGH, MAURICE DOUGLAS, Indian Police (asst. supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 9th Nov., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 12th Dec., 1923; asst. dist. officer, frontier constab., Nov., 1929; offg. supt., Sept., 1933.

PULLA REDDI, OBULREDDIGARI, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., excise, Madras) (b. 11th July, 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and magte.; sub. collr., July, 1930; under-secty. to govt., dev. dept., Mar., 1931; Diwan of Jeypore Estate, Jan., 1932, to Dec., 1935, dep. commr., excise, Aug., 1937.

PULLAN, AYRTON GEORGE POPPLEWELL, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 9th June, 1879).—Educ. at Malvern Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, May, 1908, to July, 1909; jt. mag., April, 1913; sess. and sub-judge, Dec., 1916; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1918; legal remembr. to govt. of United Provs., Oct., 1923; addl. judl. commr. of Oudh, April, 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1924; actg. judge, Allahabad high ct., May to Oct., 1926, from Oct., 1926, again and from Mar., 1929; Jan., 1931; confd., Jan., 1931; offg. judge, chief ct., Oudh, May, 1927; confd., Oct., 1929; ret'd., July, 1933.

PULLEYNE, GEOFFREY BERNARD, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 12th May, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1910; dist. supt., March 1924; supt. rly. police, Hyderabad State, Oct., 1929.

PURDY, JOSEPH WILLIAM, Indian Police (b. 29th Nov., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Bengal, Nov., 1933.

PURI, AMAR NATH, Rai Sahib, B.A., LL.B., Commerce Dept., Govt. of India (asst. secty.) (b. 3rd May, 1897).—Joined the service, 10th June, 1918; supt., dept. of comm., Jan., 1929; asst. secty., Govt. of India, dept. of comm., Govt. of India, Apl., 1935.

PURI, BALDEO DAS, M.A. (b. 26th Mar., 1876).—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1922, as prof. of maths., Thomson Civ. Engrg. Coll., Roorkee; confd., May, 1926.

PURI, B. D., RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Railway Accts. Service (b. 23rd June, 1883).—Held non-gza. appts. from 1st May, 1906; asst dir. of fin., Oct., 1924; Rai Bahadur, June, 1926; on for service to Baroda state, May, 1926, and to Porbunder Durbar, Aug., 1927; officr. on spec. duty, Rly. Bd., July, 1927; accts. officr., gr. I., N.W. rly., Apl., 1929; confd. as dep. chief accts. officr., N.W. rly., Apl., 1930; ret'd., June, 1938.

PURI, BALWANT SINGH, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 16th July, 1894).—Joined the service, 7th Oct., 1918; asst. engr., Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Apr., 1928; confd., Oct., 1929; services placed at disposal of central p.w.d. (temp.), Mar., 1935.

PURI, INDRA SINGH, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 17th May, 1895).—Joined the service, 1st Mar., 1919; dep. dir. rly. audit, Apr., 1929; offg. contr., rly. accts., Mar., 1931; accts. officer, rly. clearing accts., Feb., 1933; offg. dep. dir., ditto, Mar., 1933; accts. officer, N.W. rly., Nov., 1933; offg. dep. ch. accts. offcr., N.W. rly., July, 1934, and again, June, 1937; offg. dep. dir., rly. clearing accounts office, Oct., 1935.

PURI, MADAN LAL (see Madan Lal, Puri).

PURI, NEHCHAL DAS, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Punjab (dep. inspr. genl. of prisons) (b. 5th Dec., 1888).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1914; supt., central jail, Jan., 1924; dep. inspr. genl. of prisons, Feb., 1927; offg. inspr. genl. of prisons, Mar., 1932; June, 1933; and again, Feb., 1935.

PURI, PREM CHAND (see Prem Chand Puri).

PURI, TARA CHAND, M.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 1st Apr., 1914).—Educ. at D.A.V. High Sch., Lahore, Punjab and Nagpur Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

PURKIS, FREDERIC CHARLES, late Indian Forest Service (b. 27th Mar., 1877).—Joined the service, 4th Feb., 1903; dep. consvr., March, 1926; ret'd., Apr., 1929.

PURSELL, RICHARD STANLEY, C.I.E., O.B.E., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 5th July, 1882).—Joined the telegraph service, 1st Oct., 1904, as asst. supt.; ditto, and persl. asst. to the dir.-gen. of posts and telegraphs, April, 1910; O.B.E., June, 1918; divl. engr., Feb., 1921; asst. to chief engr., tels., May, 1923; divl. engr., tels., Dec., 1923; offg. dir., tele. engrg., U.P. circle, Nov., 1929; ditto, Bengal and Assam circle, Apr., 1930; dep. dir. genl., tels., May, 1931; C.I.E., June, 1933; offg. ch. engr., posts and tels., May, 1934; confd., Dec., 1934; ret'd., May, 1938.

PUTNAM, JAMES, O.B.E., late India Office (b. 27th Mar., 1873).—Clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., India Office, 26th Feb., 1892; transf. to pol. dept., Dec., 1895; went to India as confidential clerk on the staff of the Viceroy, Nov., 1897; returned to India Office, July, 1905; proceeded to India in the suite of the Prince of Wales, Oct., 1905; actg. asst. pte. sec. to Viceroy, 1904-5; personal clk. to Sir W. Lawrence during Prince of Wales' tour in India, Oct., 1905, to April, 1906; resumed duty in pol. dept., April, 1906; apptd. clerk to the Royal Commn. on Indian Decentralisation, Nov., 1907; clerk to educatl. adviser to Indian students, April, 1909, to Nov., 1912; asst. sec. to Royal Commn. on Public Services in India, Dec., 1912; services lent to War Office, Dec., 1914; temply. attached to mil. dep., Jan., 1916; staff clerk, Jan., 1917; suptg. exec. officer, April, 1922; ret'd., Mar., 1933; O.B.E., June, 1933.

PUTTICK, HAROLD WALTER, M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (dist. elect. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 17th June, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. elect. engr., 4th Oct., 1921; offg. dist. elect. engr., Oct., 1928; confd., Nov., 1936.

PYNE, WILLIAM HENRY JOHN, Indian State Rlys. (transptn. supt.) (b. 30th June, 1882).—Joined the service, 3rd Jan., 1913; asst. transptn. supt., Oct., 1923; transptn. (traffic comml.) supt., G.I.P. Rly., Dec., 1933.

PYPER, JOHN CLARK, O.B.E., M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (civil surg., Quetta) (b. 18th Mar. 1889).—First commn. dated 30th July, 1921; in milty. employ. to Oct., 1928; agency surg., Gilgit, 19th Oct., 1928; resdy. surg., Kashmir, May, 1933; medl. officer, Zabul, Nov., 1934; civil surg., Quetta, Nov., 1937.

PYU, MAUNG KHIN MAUNG, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 19th March, 1905).—Educ. at University of Rangoon and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived Dec., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

QADIR, ABDUL (see Abdul Qadir).

QAIYUM, A. (see Abdul Qaiyum).

QUARRY, PHILLIPPE STOWELL, late Indian Police (b. 19th Mar., 1883).—Joined the service as proby. asst. opium agent, 10th April, 1905; confd., March, 1907; asst. supt. Sept., 1909; supt., Sept., 1916; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

QUAYLE, THOMAS, C.I.E., D.Litt., M.A., Office of High Commr. for India (sec., education dept.) (b. 13th Sept., 1884).—Apptd. jt. sec. for Indian students, July, 1921; joint sec., education dept., April, 1925; asst. ditto, Aug., 1926; designation change to secty., ditto, Oct., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1936.

QUILTER, CHARLES FREDERICK GRANT, India Posts and Tels. (dep. dir. genl., Burma) (b. 13th Dec., 1883).—Held non-gaz. appt. from 1st March, 1906; clerk, Dec., 1906; supt., Sept., 1907; I.A.R.O. from 21st Nov., 1915, to 9th Aug., 1920; offg. asst. dir. genl., May, 1928; confd., Oct., 1930; dep. P.M.G., Burma, Oct., 1930; dep. dir.-genl., Apr., 1937.

QUIN, THOMAS MICHAEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 21st Sept., 1911).—Educated at Stonyhurst, New College, Oxford, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

QUINLAN, DAVID, M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Civil Vety. Dept. (b. 6th July, 1880).—Joined the service, 28th Jan., 1906; supt. Bengal, April, 1908; vety. adviser to govt. of Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1920; ret'd., June, 1930.

QUINLAN, PATRICK BAGOT, B.A. Indian Educ. Service (dir. of public instrn., Burma) (b. 12th Oct., 1884).—Join d the service as prinpl. of govt. high and normal school, Moumein, 16th Feb., 1915; on mil. service from 1st Sept., 1916, to 2nd March, 1919; inspr. of schools, Aug., 1919; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; dir., ditto, Feb., 1935.

QUINN-YOUNG, FREDERICK CHARLES STEPHEN (dist. supt.), Indian Police (b. 22nd Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Central Provs., 27th Nov., 1922; offg. dist. supt., June, 1925, Feb., 1926, and again, Oct., 1932; confd., Aug., 1937.

QUINTON, HAROLD, late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Nov., 1890).—Educ. at Berkhamstead Sch., and Brasenose College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 18th Dec., 1914, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on special duty, govt. of Bengal, gen. dept., Dec., 1919; ditto, finl. dept., Jan., 1920; jt. mag. and dy. collr., Feb., 1922; offg. mag. and collr., June, 1922; confd., Nov., 1927; retd., July 1935.

QURAISHI, ABUL HASAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st July, 1908).—Educ. at Government High School, Ballia, Queen's Intermediate College, Benares, Allahabad University and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 5th Oct., in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and sub-divl. offcr., July, 1935; spec. offcr., fin. dept., Nov., 1937.

QURBAN ALI KHAN, MIR, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 11th May, 1898).—Joined the service, 8th Nov., 1921; asst. supt., Nov., 1923; supt., July, 1932; dep. insp.-genl., in addn., May, 1935.

QUTB, SYED NIAZ, KHAN BAHADUR, M.A., LL.B., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 1st July, 1877).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 6th Jan., 1900; supt., March, 1902; dep. postmaster-genl., April, 1920; offg. P.M.G., United Provs., April, 1928; dep. dir.-genl., postal services and offg. P.M.G., central circle. Mar., 1930; confd., Feb., 1931; offg. P.M.G., Bengal and Assam, Mar., 1931; retd., July, 1932.

QUTUB-UD-DIN AHMED, C.I.E., KHAN BAHADUR.—Apptd. member of govr.'s exec. council, Assam, 1st March, 1924; C.I.E., June, 1929; retd., Mar., 1929.

RAB, SAIYID MUJIBUR (see Saliyid Mujibur, R.).

RACHHPAL SINGH, Rai Bahadur THAKUR, Barr., United Provs. Civil Service (judge, high ct., Allahabad) (b. 20th Sept., 1880).—Joined the service as offg. munisf., 1st Dec., 1905; confd., Nov., 1907; sub-judge, Jan., 1917; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1930; confd., Apl., 1931; acting judge high ct., Allahabad, Nov., 1932; actg. addl. ditto, Feb., 1933; and again, July, 1933; judge. ch. ct., Oudh, Jan., 1934; judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1934.

RACHPAL SINGH, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 9th June, 1887).—Apptd. asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 26th July, 1922; offg. dist. supt., May, 1924; confd., Jan., 1936.

RADHA KISHAN, BABU, Rai Bahadur, late United Provs. C.S. (b. 8th Mar., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1909; sub-judge, Jan., 1925; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1934; Rai Bahadur, May, 1937; retd., Mar., 1938.

RADHA KISHEN, RAI BAHADUR LALA, M.A., late Punjab Civil Service (b. July, 1878).—Joined the service as proby. extra asst. commr., 7th Feb., 1904; confd., Nov., 1908; dep. commr., Jan., 1930; retd., July, 1933.

RADHA KRISHNA AIYAR, TARURAI SESHUIYAR, Rao Bahadur, B.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 15th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 19th Oct., 1907; asst. act.-genl., posts and tels., Aug., 1925; ditto, United Provs., Oct., 1925; currency officer, Cawnpore, June, 1926; dep.

acct.-genl., Madras, Feb., 1930; ditto, posts and tels., Madras, May, 1930; govt. exr. of accts. M. and S.M. and S.I. Rlys., May, 1936; dep. ch. auditor, E.I. rly., Jan., 1937; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Calcutta, Jan., 1937.

RADHA KRISHANA GUPTA, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 18th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 30th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

RADHAKRISHNAN, KOMARALINGAM, B.A. Indian C.S. (asst. commr. Central Provinces) (b. 15th Oct., 1909).—Educ. at the P.S. High School, Mylapore, Madras, the Madras University, the School of Economics, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt. C.P., Jan., 1937.

RADHAKRISHNAN, SIR SARVEPALLI, Kt., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 5th Sept., 1888).—Apptd. princpl., govt. Victoria Coll., Polghat, 21st June, 1927; dist. educl. offcr., Apl., 1929; profir. of philosophy, Univ. of Calcutta, until 28th Feb., 1931; vice-chancellor, Andhra Univ., May, 1931; knighted, June, 1931; retd., Aug., 1937.

RADICE, PETER WOLFE, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 18th June, 1908).—Educ. at Blundell's School, Tiverton, and Exeter College, Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Oct., 1933.

RAE, BERTRAM LANGFORD DENIS, Indian Police (b. 28th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 12th Dec., 1924; offg. dist. supt., May, 1933.

RAE, CECIL DOUGLAS, C.I.E., O.B.E., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 29th June, 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1899; dep. postmaster, Calcutta, Aug., 1910; postmaster, Simla, July, 1915; ditto, Lahore, Feb., 1918; presdy. postmaster, Madras, Jan., 1919; ditto, Calcutta, May, 1921; O.B.E., June, 1927; offg. dep. dir.-genl., April, 1928; confd., Aug., 1928; P.M.G., United Provs., Apl., 1930; ditto, Madras, Feb., 1932; ditto, Bombay, Apl., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; senr. dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels., Mar., 1936; retd., Aug., 1938.

RAE, CECIL JAMES, M. Eng., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., cent. p.w.d.) (b. 23rd June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1923; offg. exec. engr., Bombay, Feb., 1928; confd., Oct., 1931.

RAE, MAITLAND EASTON, Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd. (b. 16th Mar., 1880).—First commn. dated 17th Jan., 1900; in mil. employ until 9th March, 1907, when apptd. supy. asst. commr., D.I. Khan; asst. to resdt., Persian Gulf, May, 1908; asst. resdt. and H.B.M.'s consul at Bunder Abbas, May, 1909; pol. asst., Ogbi, and commdt., B.M. Police, Hazara, Aug., 1911; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, May, 1912; city mag., Peshawar, July, 1913; dist. judge, ditto, July, 1914; city mag., ditto, Sept., 1914; temply. reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914; 1st asst. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Aug., 1915, to April, 1919; dep. commr., Kohat, April, 1920; sec. to chief commr., N.W.F. Prov., Mar., 1924; dep. commr., Hazara, Nov., 1927; rev. commr.,

N.W.F.P., March, 1929; dep. commr., Hazara, May, 1930; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1931; offg. residt., and rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, May, 1932; retd., Sept., 1933.

RAFI, MIAN MUHAMMAD, B.A. (Oxon.), Barr. (secty., legislative assembly dept.) (b. 1st Sept., 1889).—Apptd. dep. secty., legisl. assembly dept., 1st Aug., 1931; secty., ditto, Sept., 1933.

RAFIQUE, SYED AHMAD, M.A. Hons. (Cantab.), Barr., (b. 2nd June, 1904).—Apptd. information officer, home dept., Govt. of India, May, 1938.

RAGHAVA AYYANGAR, SRINIVASA, B.A., B.L., late Madras Civil Service (Judicial Branch) (b. 1st June, 1874).—Joined the service as dist. munsif., 1st Feb., 1902; sub-judge, March, 1919; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1928; retd., June, 1929.

RAGHAVACHARI, NEMAM ECHAMBADI SRINIVASA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 10th June, 1912).—Educated at Town High School, Guntur, Andhra University, Madras University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 17th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, October, 1936, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

RAGHAVAN, NARASINHA VIJAYA, B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 27th Mar., 1879).—Apptd. probir., fin. dept., 13th May, 1901; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, May, 1902; asst. compt.-India treasuries, Sept., 1903; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Dec., 1903; asst. compt.-gen., outside audits, Calcutta, June, 1904; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Jan., 1905; dep. compt.-P.O., Calcutta, March, 1908; offg. compt.-Central Prov., Sept., 1908; supt., check office, Indian tel. dept., Sept., 1909; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1909; dep. compt.-P.O., Dec., 1909; dep. acctnt.-gen., ditto, April, 1910; asst. acctnt.-gen., posts and telegraphs, Sept., 1910; dep. acctnt.-gen., ditto, Aug., 1911; addl. asst. sec., fin. dept., Nov., 1913; dep. acctnt.-gen., Burma, April, 1914; addl. asst. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Nov., 1914; dep. acctnt.-gen., United Provs., May, 1915; dep. acctnt.-gen., posts and telegraphs, Delhi, Sept., 1917; ditto, Nagpur, Nov., 1918; ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1922; offg. acctnt.-gen., central revs., April, 1924; offg. finl. adviser, posts and tels., Oct., 1924; acctnt.-gen., Bombay, May, 1925; ditto, Madras, March, 1928; retd., Jan., 1932.

RAGHAVAN, TANDALAM NARASIMHACHARI SRINIVASA, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty. to govt. fin. dept., Madras) (b. 1st Oct., 1901).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th November, 1925, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Aug., 1927; under-secty. to govt., rev. dept., Madras, July, 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1936; secty. to commr. of excise, Madras, July, 1936.

RAGHAVENDRA RAO, E., Barr., Apptd. member, govr's exec. council, Central Provs., 23rd Jan., 1931, after officiating from 8th Oct., 1930; actg. govr., Central Provs., May to Sept., 1936.

RAGHAVENDRA RAO, PATTADAKAL VENKANNA, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 19th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at Maharaja's High School, Mysore, Universities of Mysore and Calcutta, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

RAGHAVENDRA RAU, Sir PANAMBUR, M.A., Indian Finance dept. (b. 24th May, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1912; asst. acctnt.-gen., Nov., 1912; asst. contr. of currency, March, 1919; dep. ditto, April, 1920; asst. sec. to govt. of India, finance dept., April, 1921; under-sec. to ditto, Jan., 1922; dep. dir. of finance, rly. board, Aug., 1925; offg. dir. of finance, rly. board, March, 1926; confd., Oct., 1928; offg. finl. commr., March, 1929; confd., Apl., 1932; chairman, standing fin. cttee. for rlys., May, 1936; knighted, Feb., 1937; acct.-genl., Bombay, Aug., 1937.

RAGHUBAR DAYAL SHUKUL, PANDIT, B.A., LL.B., late United Provs. Civil Service (b. 15th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service as offg. munsif, 3rd Feb., 1903; confd., Feb., 1904; sub. judge, Oct., 1914; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1927; confd., Oct., 1927; retd., Oct., 1933.

RAGHUNATH PRASAD, RAI BAHADUR BABU, B.A., LL.B. (b. Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 23rd May, 1905; sub. judge, Oct., 1917; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1926; confd., March, 1929; retd., Nov., 1931.

RAGHUNATH RAI, HANDA, Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 9th Nov., 1899).—Joined the service, 11th Oct., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1923; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1932; confd., Oct., 1932; offg. under secty. local self govt. dept., Punjab, Jan., 1933.

RAGHUNATHA RAO, S., B.A., Barr., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 10th May, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 14th Feb., 1924; dep. consvr., Feb., 1928.

RAHA, SHARAT KUMAR, RAI BAHADUR, late Excise and Salt Dept., Bengal (b. 3rd Jan., 1877).—Apptd. supt. of excise and salt, June, 1915; dep. commr., ditto, Feb., 1921; offg. commr., ditto, April, 1926; confd., Apl., 1929; retd., Jan., 1932.

RAHIM BAKHSH, KHAN BAHADUR, SHAIKH, O.B.E., M.A., late Punjab Civil Service (b. 21st Sept., 1874).—Joined the service, 27th Feb., 1899, as extra asst. commr.; sub-judge, Oct., 1909; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1922; confd., Oct., 1926; retd. Sept., 1929; O.B.E., June, 1930.

RAHIM, JALLALUDDIN ABDUR, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. of Customs, Calcutta) (b. 27th July, 1906).—Educ. at Trinity College, Cambridge and Munich University; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Mar., 1935; asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta, Nov., 1936.

RAHIM, KHWAJA ABDUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 14th Sept., 1908).—Educated at Punjab University and Peterhouse, Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; name changed to Abdur Rahim (q.v.)

RAHIM, MUHAMMAD INAMUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 1st Jan., 1908).—Educ. at Morris Coll., Nagpur, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. by nomination in India; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., variously from March, 1927; under-sec., govt. of India, dept. of educn., &c., June, 1927, to Oct., 1930; dep. commr. (confd.) Mar., 1937.

RAHIMTOOLA, FAZAL IBRAHIM, Commerce dept., Govt. of India (presdt., tariff board).—Apptd. member, tariff board, 2nd Nov., 1930; and again, Oct., 1934; offg. presdt., ditto, June, 1935; member, spl. tariff bd., Oct., 1935; presdt., tariff bd., May, 1938.

RAHIMTULLA, Sir IBRAHIM, G.B.E., K.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Member of govr.'s exec. council, Bombay, from 1st March, 1918, to 17th March, 1923; K.C.S.I., June, 1923; presdt., Bombay legislative council, 1923; member of R.C. on Labour in India, July, 1929; presdt., legisl. assembly, Jan., 1931; G.B.E., June, 1935.

RAHIMUDDIN AHMAD, Khan Bahadur, B.A., Indian Police (suplt., Assam) (b. 22nd Oct., 1884).—Joined the service, 16th Sept., 1909; suppt., July, 1925.

RAHMAN, A.F., B.A. (Oxon.), LL.D., (member, federal pub. services commission).—Prof. of history, M.A.O. Coll., Aligarh, and later Muslim Univ., Aligarh, 1914-1921; Provost, Muslim Hall, Univ. of Dacca, 1921-27; secty., Aligarh Univ. Enquiry Ctte., 1927; member, Punjab Univ. Enquiry Ctte., 1932-1933; vice-chancellor, Univ. of Dacca, and chairman, Bd. of Inter. and Secondary Educ., Dacca, 1934-1936; member, Federal Public Service, Commn., 1937.

RAHMAN, ABDUR (see Abdur Rahman).

RAHMAN, ABUL FAEZ MUJIBOR (see Abul Faez Mujibor Rahman).

RAHMAN, ATAUR, Khan Bahadur (see Atauro Rahman).

RAHMAN, AZIZUR (see Azizur Rahman).

RAHMAN, MOWDUDUR (see Mowdudur Rahman).

RAHMAN, MUHAMMAD ABDUR, Lt.-Col. I.M.S. ret'd. (b. 22nd Oct., 1881).—First commn. dated 31st July, 1909; in mil. employ. to 5th Aug., 1921; civil surg. and offg. prinpl., medl. school, Agra, Aug., 1921; ret'd., Oct., 1936.

RAHMAN, MUHAMMAD EHSANUR (see Muhammad E. R.)

RAHMAN, SHAIKH ABDUR, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 4th June, 1903).—Educ. at University of the Punjab and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 29th Sept., 1928; arrived, Oct., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., Apl., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1935.

RAHMAT-ULLAH, MUHAMMAD, Shaikh, Khan Bahadur, late Indian Forest Service (b. 6th May, 1877).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1900; I.F.S., Mar., 1924; dep. consvr., March, 1925; ret'd., May, 1932.

RAHMATULLAH, SHAHABUDDIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal). (b. 9th June, 1913).—Educ. at Arrah Zillah Sch., Arrah, and Patna Univ.; joined the Service 1st Dec., 1937; arrived in India, Dec., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

RAI, AJILIDAY NARAYANA (see Narayana Rai, A.).

RAI, DIWAN GANPAT, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 11th Nov., 1875).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1902; civil surg., Madras, Dec., 1923; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

RAI, GANPAT, LALA (see Ganpat Rai, L.).

RAI, HAKIM (see Hakim Rai).

RAI, HAKUMAT (see Hakumat R.).

RAI, INDARJIT (see Indarjit Rai).

RAI, LAKHPAT (see Lakhpat Rai).

RAI, MANGAT (see Mangat Rai).

RAI, NAUBAT (see Naubat Rai).

RAIKES, BERTIE, late Public Works Dept., Burma.—Apptd. electrical inspr. to govt., 17th July, 1905; services placed at disposal of govt. of India for employment under Indian munitions bd., Feb. to May, 1918; dep. controller of munitions, Burma, May, 1918, to April, 1919; on special duty in connection with Rangoon water supply scheme, from 25th Oct., 1926; ret'd., Feb., 1929.

RAINY, Sir GEORGE, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Feb., 1875).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., finl. and muncl. depts., July, 1904; under-sec. to Govt. of India dept. of commerce and industry, March, 1906; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal, August, 1909; transfid. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; mag. and collr., April, 1912; offg. sec. to govt., fin. and muncl. depts., March, 1914; finl. member, Imperial Delhi Committee, April, 1914; on spl. duty in Bihar and Orissa sectt., May, 1916; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., July, 1916; confirmed, May, 1917; C.I.E., June, 1918; spl. duty as representative of govt. of India with reforms ctee., Nov., 1918; offg. chief sec. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, April, 1919; confd., Oct., 1919; C.S.I., Jan. 1921 on depts. to govt. of India, dept. of commerce, June, 1923; president, tariff board, 5th July, 1923; offg. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of commerce; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1925; member, gov.-genl.'s council, April, 1927; K.C.S.I., June, 1930; delegate of India to monetary and economic conf., London, June, 1933; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

RAISMAN, ABRAHAM JEREMY, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (member, central bd. of rev.) (b. 19th Mar., 1892).—Educ. at Leeds Central High Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1915; arrived, 5th Dec., 1916, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., finl. dept., Oct., 1920; asst. collr. of customs, Bombay, Oct., 1922; ditto, Calcutta, June, 1926; commr. of inc. tax, Punjab and N.W.F. Provs., Feb., 1928, and again, Sept., 1929; on spl. duty, central bd. of rev., Aug., 1929; temp. dep. secty., govt. of India, commerce dept., Feb., 1931; offg. jt. secty., ditto, May, 1931; confd., Mar., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; offg.

member, central bd. of rev., June, 1934; confd., Jan., 1935, addl. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept. (temp.), June, 1936; offg. secty., ditto, Apl., 1937; Director, Reserve Bank of India, Oct., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938.

RAJ BAHADUR HUKKOO, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 27th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 23rd March, 1914; asst. engr. (prov. service), June, 1919; asst. exec. engr., April, 1921; offg. exec. engr., May, 1926; confd., June, 1928; retd., June, 1934.

RAJ NARAYAN, Rai Bahadur, M.B.E., B.A., M.I.E.(Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th Nov., 1879).—Trained at T.C.E. Coll., and apptd. in India; asst. engr., Sept., 1903; exec. engr., Sept., 1911; M.B.E., June, 1919; suptg. engr., June, 1921; retd., Nov., 1934.

RAJ NARAYAN BRAHMAWAR, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr. United Provs.) (b. 2nd Aug., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; divl. forest officer, April, 1926; dep. consvr., Oct., 1931.

RAJ NARAYAN SINGH, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr. United Provs.) (b. 15th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., May, 1926.

RAJA AYYAR, ABHIRAMAPURAM KRISHNAMURTI, B.A., M.B.E., Diwan Bahadur, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. Dec. 1881).—Joined the service, 17th Feb., 1908; dep. supt., July, 1910; awarded King's police medal; M.B.E., June, 1925; supt., Sept., 1926; dist. supt., govt. rly. police, Madras, June, 1930; hon. A.D.C. to govt., Sept., 1932.

RAJA RAM, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E. (b. 28th June, 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1919; temp. asst. engr., P.H. dept., 1st Apl., 1919 to Oct., 1922; apptd. prof. of civ. engrg.; Thomason C.E. Coll., Roorkee, Oct., 1922; confd., May, 1926; on for serv. under Ind. Research Fund Asscn., as malaria engr., from July, 1934, to July, 1937.

RAJADHYAKSHA, GANPAT SAKHARAM, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 9th Sept., 1896).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1920; arrived, 18th Jan., 1921, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. magte. and collr.; transfed. to Bombay, Aug., 1921; doing duty as asst. judge, April, 1924; offg. judge and sess. judge, June, 1926; offg. dep. secty. to govt., legal dept., Jan., 1928; confd., May, 1929; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; offg. secty. to govt., legal dept., Bombay, July, 1935 and again Apl., 1936.

RAJAGOPAL, POORAEETI NADAMUNI, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 19th Apl., 1896).—Joined the service, 2nd Mar., 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Madras, Jan., 1922; currency officer, Madras, May, 1924; exr., local fund accts., Punjab, June, 1928; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Apl., 1931; currency officer, Bombay, Sept., 1931; asst. dir., army audit, northern cmd., Jan., 1932; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Nagpur, Apl., 1935, and again, Mar., 1937; offg. dep. dir.-genl., fin., posts and tels., June, 1936; attd. offcr., office of comp., Orissa, Nov., 1937; comptr., Orissa, Jan., 1938.

RAJAGOPALA AYYAR, R., Income-tax dept., Madras (asst. commr. of inc. tax) (b. 1st July, 1888).—Joined the service as income tax officer, 5th Apl., 1922; offg. asst. commr., Mar., 1925; confd., Nov., 1929; asst. commr., northern range, Bezwada, Aug., 1931; ditto, southern range, Madras, Dec., 1933.

RAJAGOPALA AIYAR, CHIDAMBARA AIYAR, M.B.E., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 11th July, 1888).—Held non-gaz. and sub. gaz. appts., Aug., 1908, to July, 1920; apptd. as mil. acctt., 2nd cl., July, 1920; A.M.A.G., July, 1922; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Apl., 1925; M.B.E., 1919.

RAJAGOPALA RAO, KARADIBAVI LAKSHMAN, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 12th Oct., 1897).—Joined the service 12th Dec., 1921; curr. offcr., Madras, Aug., 1933; serv. placed at disposal of Res. Bank of India, Apl., 1935; examr., loc. fund accts., Cent. Provs., Apl., 1938; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, July, 1938.

RAJAGOPALA THAMBAN, VENGANAD KOVILAGAM, Indian Police (b. 10th Oct., 1891) (dist. supt., Madras).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 10th Oct., 1918; offg. supt., Feb., 1924; confd., Aug., 1930; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1936.

RAJAGOPALAN, KUMMAYA MANAKADAN B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 26th Sept., 1912).—Educ. at Christian College Sch., Georgetown, Madras, and Madras Univ.; joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

RAJAGOPALAN, PUNDI, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 29th April, 1901).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 15th Nov., 1924, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Feb., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1933; confd., July, 1936.

RAJAGOPALAM, TANJORE KRISHNA, B.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 10th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 10th Feb., 1906, as probr., finance dept.; asst. compt.-gen., Delhi, Oct., 1916; dep. acct.-gen., Madras, April, 1920; ditto, Bombay, March, 1922; on special duty in finl. dept., govt. of India, from Nov., 1923; offg. ch. auditor, G.I.P. Rly., May, 1930; ditto, and govt. examr. of accts., B.B. and C.I. Rly., May, 1931; ditto, G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1932; offg. ch. accts. officer, G.I.P. rly., Sept., 1933; dir. of rly. audit, Apl., 1934; acct.-genl., Madras, Oct., 1934; acct.-genl., Central Revs., Apl., 1936; dir. of rly. audit, Apl., 1938.

RAJAN, ARUNACHALA TYAGA, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 6th Oct., 1883).—Educ. at Amalapuram High Sch., Madras Univ., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge, apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 1st Jan., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dist. judge, July, 1921; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922.

RAJAN, SRINIVASA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 10th Jan., 1911).—Educated at American College, Madura, University of Madras and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 10th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.

RAJAN, VISWANATHA NATA, M.Sc., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 22nd Oct., 1907).—Educ. at the School of Economics and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1932.

RAJESHWAR DAYAL, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provinces) (b. 12th Aug., 1909).—Educ. at Kayastha Pathahala Intermediate College, Allahabad, Allahabad University and New College, Oxford; joined the service, 7th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Oct., 1936.

RAJU, MANTHENA TIMMA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 5th Oct., 1911).—Educ. at P.S. High Sch., Madras, Govt. Model Secondary Sch., Benares Hindu Univ., University Coll., London, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr., Apl., 1938.

RAJU, VADREVV BHADIR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 28th Oct., 1907).—Educated at Madras University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. judge (under trng.), Nov., 1937.

RAJWADE, LAKSHMANRAO GANPATRAO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 14th Sept., 1912).—Educ. at V.C. High Sch., Gwalior, and Bombay Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

RAKE, BEAVEN CRAWFORD, late Indian Police (b. 18th April, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1905; dist. supt., Dec., 1914; dep. inspr.-gen., Feb., 1924; retd., June, 1934.

RAKHÁ, RAM (see Ram Rakha).

RAKSHIT, JITENDRA NATH, F.I.C., F.C.S. (opium chemist) (b. 15th June, 1887).—Joined the service, May, 1912; held non-gaz. appts., May, 1912 to Apl., 1924; opium chemist, govt. of India, Oct., 1929.

RAM, AMOLAK (see Amolak Ram).

RAM, ATMA (see Atma Ram).

RAM, BASHI (see Bashi Ram).

RAM BATALIA (see Batalia, R.).

RAM CHAND SAKSENA, BABU, B.A., LL.B., late United Provs. Civil Service (b. 9th March, 1875).—Joined the service, 29th May, 1902; sub. judge, March, 1915; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1926; retd., Mar., 1930.

RAM CHANDRA, B.A., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th June, 1890).—Joined the service, Sept., 1913; asst. engr., Sept., 1914; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; retd., Apl., 1935.

RAM CHANDRA, C.I.E., M.B.E., M.A. Indian C.S. (secty. to govt. fin. dept., Punjab) (b. 1st Mar., 1889).—Educ. at Central Model School, Lahore, Punjab, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 17th Nov., 1913, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; coin. officer, Upper Chenab Canal, Nov., 1915,

to Jan., 1917; M.B.E., June, 1919; temp. under-sec. to govt., Punjab, June, 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), Dec., 1925; sec. to govt., transferred depts., Punjab, Jan., 1926; dep. secty. to govt. of India, educn. dept., May, 1928; offg. jt. secty., ditto, Aug., 1930; confd., Oct., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; offg. secty. to the Govt. of India, educn. dept., Sept., 1935; secty., fin. dept., Punjab, Jan., 1936.

RAMCHANDRA KESHAV BAL (see Bal, R. K.).

RAM CHANDRA SAHAI, KUNWAR, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 19th March, 1900).—Joined the service, 15th Oct., 1923; apprentice engr., May, 1924; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., July, 1927; confd., Oct., 1933.

RAM DAS, RAI SAHIB LALA, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 7th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 9th Aug., 1899; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; on deputation to Bahawalpur State from 9th Dec., 1921; exec. engr., Sept., 1926; retd., Nov., 1931.

RAM, GITA (see Gita, R.).

RAM GOPAL, M.Sc., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (b. Dec., 1894).—Joined the service, 21st Mar., 1921; dep. acct.-genl., U.P., Oct., 1936; ch. auditor, E.B. Rly., June, 1938.

RAM, JAI (see Jai Ram).

RAM KISHAN, RAI SAHIB, Indian State Ryis. (dep. ch. engr.) (b. 1st May, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; divl. engr., O. and R. Rly., April, 1925; on loan to Baroda durbar; dep. ch. engr., July, 1934.

RAM LABHU (see Labhu, R.).

RAM LAL, BATRA, Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 20th July, 1884).—Joined the service, 11th Sept., 1907; asst. engr., Sept., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. supt. engr., Apl., 1933.

RAM LAL, DIWAN, B.A., Barr., Judl. Dept., Punjab (high ct. judge).—Apptd. offg. asst. legal remembrancer 17th May, 1925; asst. legal remembrancer (conveyancing), Feb., 1930; admr. genl. and offi. trustee, June, 1931; govt. advocate, Oct., 1933; offg. secty. to govt., Punjab, legisl. dept., Oct., 1936; advocate-genl., Apl., 1937; judge, high ct., Lahore, Feb., 1938.

RAM LAL, KHANNA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 11th Dec., 1905).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 16th Jan., 1929; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1930.

RAM LAL, LALA RAI BAHADUR, M.B.E., Dept. of Industries, Punjab (dir. of industries) (b. 18th Aug., 1888).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 2nd May, 1912; extra asst. commr., Nov., 1918; M.B.E., Jan., 1921; under-secty. to govt., rev. dept., Punjab, Jan., 1927; dir. of industries, Aug., 1930.

RAM LAL SETHI (see Sethi, R. L.).

RAM LAL, SONDHI, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. to consulting engr., p.w. bch., Govt. of India) (b. 17th Oct., 1895).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Burma, 10th Feb., 1921; transferred to Punjab, July, 1929; exec. engr., Feb., 1930; asst. to consulting engr., dept. of indus. and lab., Govt. of India (p.w. bch.), Jan., 1935.

RAM, MOTI (see Moti Ram).

RAM NATH, RAI BAHADUR PANDIT, B.A., (second secty., central bd. of rev.) (b. 14th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service, 20th Oct., 1904; secty., central bd. of rev., June, 1931; second secty., ditto, Nov., 1934.

RAM PRASAD, BURMAN, LALA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 18th Jan., 1900).—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., May, 1925; exec. engr., Apl., 1935.

RAM PRATAP, VASISHTH, Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 21st Oct., 1899).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 25th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

RAM, RALA (see Rala Ram).

RAM RAKHA, RAI BAHADUR LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (supvg. engr., Punjab) (b. 10th June, 1883).—Joined the service, 17th Sept., 1906; asst. engr., Sept., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., July, 1930; confd., July, 1935.

RAM, SADHU (see Sadhu Ram).

RAM, SALIG (see Salig Ram).

RAM, SANT (see Sant Ram).

RAM, SHEO (see Sheo Ram).

RAM SAHGAL, BASHI (see Bashri Ram, S.).

RAM SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 17th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service, 16th Sept., 1910; asst., engr., Sept., 1911; exec. engr., April, 1921; offg. dep. ch. engr., variously from Sept., 1929.

RAM SINGH, THAKUR, B.Sc., Income Tax Dept., United Provs. (asst. commr. of inc. tax) (b. 4th Feb., 1889).—Joined the service, 16th Mar., 1920; asst. commr. of inc. tax, Benares, Oct., 1929.

RAMA AYYAR, KOLLINGODE, RANGA IYER, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 15th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service, 28th Feb., 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Nov., 1921; asst. dir. of audit, United Provs., Apl., 1926; dep. dir., ditto, Sept., 1926; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, June, 1929; ditto, Madras, Nov., 1932; ditto, posts and tels., Madras, May, 1935; ditto, Bombay, Apl., 1937; dep. ch. accts. offcr., N.W. Rly., June, 1938.

RAMA PAI, KRISHNA, M.A., Patents and Designs (contr.) (b. 15th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as proby. asst. examr., 3rd Sept., 1920; confd., Sept., 1922; contr., Feb., 1926.

RAMA RAO, UDYAVAR, Rai Bahadur, late Madras Civil Service (b. 9th July, 1877).—Joined the service as dep. collr., 11th April, 1900; collr. and dist. magte., Sept., 1928; secty. to govt., law and educn. dept., July, 1930; retd., Aug., 1932.

RAMA RAU, BENEGAL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (agent-genl., S. Africa) (b. 10th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 17th Nov., 1913, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Nov., 1917; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Dec., 1918; offg. under-sec. to govt., development dept., July, 1920; ditto, local self-govt. dept., Sept., 1920; confd., July, 1921; tempy. dep. sec. to govt., local self-govt., Oct., 1922; offg. dep. sec. to

govt., finl. dept., April, 1926; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, finance dept., June, 1926; confd., March, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1930; on depn. to Indian Statutory Comm., 1929-30; offg. jt. secty., dept. of indus. and labour, govt. of India, Oct., 1930; jt. secty., British Indian delegation to Indian round table conf., 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; collr., Sept., 1932; secty. to British Indian delegation to jt. ctee. on Indian Constitutional Reform, May-November, 1933; dep. high commr. for India, Dec., 1934 to Mar., 1935; agent-genl. for India, S. Africa, May, 1938.

RAMA REDDI, PATTIPATE HANIMIREDDI-GARI, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (b. 15th Feb., 1888) (dep. dir. agric., Madras).—Apptd. dep. dir. of agric., Madras, 17th Jan., 1919; vice-princl. agricl. coll., Coimbatore, June, 1928; offg. princpl. ditto, Sept., 1929; secty., Indian Central cotton ctee., Dec., 1932; dep. dir. agric., Madras, Sept., 1937.

RAMABHADRAN, TIRUVALYANGUDI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 25th Aug., 1910).—Educated at Hindu High School, Triplicane, Delhi University and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Oct., 1936.

RAMACHANDRA AYYAR, VENKATARAMA, Rao Bahadur, M.A., late Indian Police (b. 13th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service, 4th Mar., 1907; dep. supt., Apl., 1909; supt., Madras, Apl., 1925; Rao Bahadur, June, 1932; retd., Jan., 1938.

RAMACHANDRAN, THIAGARAJA SADASIVA, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 12th Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras, London School of Economics, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and magte.; sub. collr. and jt. magte., July, 1928; offg. collr. and dist. magte., June, 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1936.

RAMADHYANI, RANGASWAMY, KALKUNTE, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 20th Feb., 1908).—Educ. at Mysore University, University College, London, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930; and served in the Central Provs. as asst., commr.; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1934.

RAMAKRISHNA AYYAR, PERUMALAYYAR, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 5th Feb., 1908).—Educated at Madras Univ. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Madras, as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. magte., Feb., 1936.

RAMAKRISHNAN, CHITTUR ANANTA-KRISHNA, M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 9th Nov., 1910).—Educ. at P.S. High School, Mylapore, University of Madras, University College, London and Trinity College, Dublin; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1937.

RAMAKRISHNA, VELGAPUDI, M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 4th April, 1896).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived 10th Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1923; offg. under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag. and sec. to govt., rev. dept., June, 1925; dewan to Maharaja of Jeypore, Nov., 1925; dir. of industries, Madras, Nov., 1931; offg. dep. secty., dept. of indus. and lab., Govt. of India, June, 1935.

RAMALINGAM, ALAMPADI SUBBARAYA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 26th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 6th Feb., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1924.

RAMALINGAM, PONNAMBALAM, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 1st July, 1897).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Colombo, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1921; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1931, and again, Mar., 1933; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1935.

RAMAMURTI, SONTI VENKATA, B.A., Indian C.S.
