(collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 1st Aug., 1888).—Educ. at London Mission High Sch., Vizagapatam, Madras Univ. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; offg. collr. and dist. mag., April, 1924; confd., Aug., 1930; offg. secty. to govt. dev. dept., July, 1929; dir. of agriculture, Madras, Feb., 1932 to June, 1935; commr. of lab., July, 1938.

RAMAN, A. SUNDARA, Local Fund Audit Dept., Burma (asst. exr.) (b. 14th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 26th Nov., 1913; asst. exr., Dec., 1933.

RAMAN, AGNESWARA VENKATA, Public Works dept., Madras (dep. sany. engr.) (b. 12th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service as offg. asst. sany. engr., 22nd Jan., 1921; confd., Mar., 1922; dep. sany. engr., Apl., 1927.

RAMAN, KODAGANALLUR SRINIVASA VENKATA, India C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 15th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Madras University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 7th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty., govt. of Orissa, home and rev. dept., Mar., 1937.

RAMAN, KRISHNASWAMI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 28th Oct., 1911).—Educated at Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.

RAMANA RAO, KOTY VENKATA, Major, I.M.S. (D.M.O., Madras) (b. 14th Jan., 1888).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1914; in milty. employ until 10th July, 1925; offg. variously in civil appts., from July, 1925 to June, 1929; D.M.O., June, 1929.

RAMANAN, COIMBATORE SUBRAHMANYA VENKATA, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Madras (supt.).—First commn., 11th Jan., 1921; in milty. employ to 22nd June, 1931; jail supt., Madras, July, 1933.

RAMANATHAN, KALPATHI RAMA KRISHNA, M.A., D.Sc., Meteorological Dept. (meteorologist) (b. 28th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service as temp. meteorologist, 2nd Oct., 1925; meteorologist on probn., Apl., 1926; confd., Apl., 1927; offg. dir., Kodaiikanil observatory, Mar., 1932.

RAMANATHAN, VENKATARAMA, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., B. har.) (b. 4th Sept., 1911).—Educated at Board High School, Namakal, University of Madras and The School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in B. and O., as asst. mag. and collr.

RAMANUJA AYYANGAR, NUGGEHALLI, Diwan Bahadur, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 9th Jan., 1884).—Joined the service, 2nd Jan., 1907; dep. supt., Apl., 1909; asst. supt., June, 1920; supt., Nov., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1925; commr. of police, Travancore, Nov., 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1935; Diwan Bahadur, May, 1937.

RAMAPPA, UCHIL, B.A., B.L. Judl. Dept., Madras (judge, city civil ct., Madras) (b. 6th July, 1882).—Joined the service, 24th July, 1912; sub. judge, Apl., 1929; offg. judge, city civil ct., Madras, July, 1933; confd., Oct., 1933.

RAMASUNDARAM, UNJALUR SUBRAHMANYA AYYAR, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 28th Dec., 1901).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 2nd July, 1924; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1925; offg. exec. engr., May, 1931, and again, Jan., 1933; confd., Oct., 1934.

RAMASWAMI AYYAR, RANGASWAMI IYER, B.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 12th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 24th Aug., 1920; dep. govt. ex. of accts., S.I. rly., Apl., 1936; dep. acctnt.-genl., P. and T., Nagpur, Oct., 1937.

RAMASWAMI AYYANGAR, GANDHARVAKOTA RANGASWAMI, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 1880).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 16th Feb., 1903; asst. engr., Nov., 1903; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Sept., 1920; confd., March, 1925; ret'd., June, 1933.

RAMASWAMI AYYANGAR, K. R., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Madras) (b. June, 1876).—Apptd. profr., coll of engrg., 23rd Feb., 1921; ret'd., June, 1931.

RAMASWAMI AYYAR, BATLAGUNDU SITARAMA, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 18th April, 1889).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 26th June, 1911; asst. engr., June, 1912; exec. engr., June, 1921.

RAMASWAMI AYYAR, SIR CHETPAT PATTABHERAMA, K.C.I.E.—Apptd. advocate-general, Madras, Jan., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1923; member of govr.'s exec. council, Madras, Feb., 1923, to April, 1928; K.C.I.E., June, 1925; on deputation to League of Nations from 8th Aug. to 9th Oct., 1926; delegate to Indian round table

conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931, 2nd session Sept.-Dec., 1931; temp. member of govr.-genl.'s council, Aug., 1931; and again, June, 1932; delegate of India to monetary and economic conf., London, June, 1933; member, govt. of India Cttee. on secretariat procedure, 1935-1936.

RAMASWAMI AYYAR, GOPALA AYYAR, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd Feb., 1881).—Joined the service, 31st Jan., 1905; asst. engr., March, 1908; exec. engr., March, 1917; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1929; confd., Oct., 1933; ret'd., Feb., 1936.

RAMASWAMI, PALAMANERI NARAYANASWAMI, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 17th May, 1900).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Feb., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935; confd., Sept., 1937.

RAMASWAMI MUDALIAR, ARCOT (see Mudaliar, Sir Ramaswami).

RAMASWAMI SASTRI, KADAMBI SUNDARARAMA, B.A., B.L. (b. Aug., 1878).—Joined the service as offg. dist. munisf, 5th July, 1907; confd., Mar., 1908; sub-judge, Dec., 1925; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1931; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

RAMASWAMI, VAIDYANATHIER, M.Sc. (Allbd.) Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bihar) (b. 30th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at University of Madras, University of Allahabad; Imperial Coll., and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, 29th Nov., 1929 and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and coll.; actg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1936.

RAMCHANDANI, RANJIT SINGH V., Indian Rlys. Accts. Service (depy. chief accts. offcr.) (b. 19th June, 1888).—Held non-gaz. appts. from June, 1911; confd. as asst. contr. of stores, E.I. rly., June, 1926; confd. as asst. audit offcr., May, 1927; asst. ditto, A.G. rlys., Aug., 1927; transf. to Dehra Dun Coll., Nov., 1929; accts. offcr., gr. I (prov.), July, 1933; on special duty, rly. bd., Nov., 1937; depy. contr., rly accts., Mar., 1938; depy. chief accts. offcr., E.I. Rly., July, 1938.

RAMESAM, SIR VEPA, Kt., B.A., B.L. (b. 27th July, 1875).—Joined the service, 13th July, 1916, as govt. pleader; judge, high ct., Madras, Nov., 1920; Kt., March, 1929; offg. ch. justice, high ct., Madras, July, 1931, and again, July, 1933; ret'd., July, 1935.

RAMESHWAR, SAHAI, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Orissa) (b. 13th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Madras, 11th Nov., 1929; offg. dist. forest offcr., Aug., 1933; dep. consvr., Nov., 1934; transf. to Orissa prov., Apl., 1936.

RAMPLING, HAROLD, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 18th April, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 26th Jan., 1920; dist. supt., June, 1927.

RAMRAO ANNAPI HEMMADI (see Hemmadi, R. A.)

RAMSAY, Sir JOHN, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (ret'd.), late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 9th Sept., 1882;

from July, 1886, served at Ajmir and Indore as acting asst. agent; confd. in latter post, Jan., 1889; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, Dec., 1891; 1st asst., Indore, May, 1892; in addn., asst. to gen. supt., Thagi and Dakaiti, Nov., 1892; pol. agent in Bundelkhand, April, 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1898; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1898; offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, from Nov., 1902, to April, 1905; pol. resdt. in Turkish Arabia, March, 1906; and H.B.M.'s consul gen. at Baghdad, Oct., 1907; resdt. in Kashmir, May, 1910; agent to govr.-gen., and chief commr., Baluchistan, April, 1911; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; K.C.I.E., June, 1915; ret'd., Dec., 1917.

RAMSAY, NORMAN, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 11th Nov., 1907).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1927; in milty. employ prior to 20th Nov., 1933; 2nd-in-command and adjt., Kolhapur Inf., June, 1933; P.A. to Resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1933; under training in the Punjab, July, 1934; under-secty. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Apl., 1935; again under training in the Punjab, Jan., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Punjab Hill States, Oct., 1936; under-secty. to the resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; asst. pol. agent, Punjab Hill States, Nov., 1938.

RAMSBOTHAM, RICHARD BURY, M.B.E., M.A. (Oxon.), B.Litt. (Oxon.), F.R.Hist.S., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 27th Apl., 1880).—Joined the service, 3rd June, 1908, as inspr. of schools; profr., Dacca col., July, 1908; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to April, 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; principal, Hooghly coll., July, 1920; offg. prinpl. presy. coll., Calcutta, June, 1928; ditto, Chittagong coll., July, 1929; confd., July, 1930; on dep. to Muslim univ., from Feb., 1931; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

RAMSDEN, GEOFFREY CHARLES FRESCHVILLE, M.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 21st April, 1893).—Educ. at Haileybury and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 1st Sept., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Nov., 1923; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; confd., July, 1927; excise commr. and supt. of stamps, Jan., 1929; offg. commr., Feb., 1936.

RAZMAN, ALI, Khan Bahadur, late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 14th Aug., 1883).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 7th June, 1905; confd., Oct., 1906; offg. dep. P.M.G., Bombay, Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1928; dep. P.M.G., Punjab and N.W.F.P., Feb., 1930; offg. dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels., Sept., 1934; P.M.G., Bihar and Orissa, Mar., 1936; P.M.G., Dec., 1936; ret'd., Aug., 1938.

RANA LOKE SHAMSHER JANG, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 4th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 11th July, 1910; offg. dist. supt., Sept., 1920; confd., Jan., 1930.

RANA, UDAYASINHJI HARMIRSINHJI, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 3rd Nov., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 20th Aug., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1932; confd., July, 1936.

RANADE, DATTATRAYA DAMODAR, Rai Bahadur, late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 1st Jan., 1884).—Joined the service, 4th Jan., 1906; asst. acct.-genl., United Provs., Nov., 1930; dep. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Nov., 1931, and again, Feb., 1935; ditto, Bombay, Jan., 1933; ditto, U.P., Oct., 1936; ret'd., Mar., 1938.

RANDHAWA, MOHINDAR SINGH, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (ft. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Feb., 1909).—Educated at Khalsa High School, Huktsar, University of the Punjab and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provinces as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte., Oct., 1936.

RANDLE, HERBERT NIEL, M.A. (Oxon.), D.Phil., India Office (librarian), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 19th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service, 31st July, 1905, and officiated as prof. of philosophy, Queen's Coll., Benares; inspr. of schools, April, 1906; profsr., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, Nov., 1906; ditto, Queen's Coll., Benares, April, 1909; ditto, Muir Central Coll., Sept., 1918; actg. prinpl., Queen's Coll., April, 1919; Allahabad Univ. from July, 1925; asst. librarian, India Office, Sept., 1927; librarian, Sept., 1933.

RANDOLPH, Rev. THOMAS BERKELEY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (junr. chaplain) (b. 15th Mar., 1904).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bengal; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1934.

RANG BEHARI LAL, M.A., Finance dept., govt. of India (b. 19th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service, 5th Mar., 1914; probnr., fin. dept., Feb., 1915; asst. acct. genl., U.P., May, 1916; ditto, posts and tels., Aug., 1916; ditto, Punjab, May, 1917; asst. acct. genl., posts and tels. (tel. branch), Apl., 1919; asst. auditor, E.B. Rly., Sept., 1920; ditto, O. and R. Rly., Nov., 1921; and again, Jan., 1924; asst. acct. genl., posts and tels. (postal branch), Sept., 1923; asst. auditor, N.W. Rly., Oct., 1924; divl. audit officer, N.W. Rly., Nov., 1924; examr. of accts., C.I.S. coalfields rly. construction, Feb., 1926; audit officer, Indian Stores dept., Aug., 1926; dep. acct. genl., posts and tels., Nov., 1926; govt. examr., M. and S.M. Rly., Mar., 1928; dep. auditor, G.I.P. Rly., Oct., 1929; offg. ch. pay and accts. officer, U.P., Feb., 1930; offg. dep. ch. acctg. officer, U.P., Apl., 1931; dep. ch. auditor, Burma, rlys., Apl., 1932; dep. acct.-genl., Burma, Nov., 1932; offg. dep. ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Jan., 1933; offg. audit officer, Lloyd Barr. and canals constrn., Feb., 1933; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Nov., 1933; offg. dep. contr. of rly. accts., Jan., 1935, and again, Dec., 1935.

RANGANATHAN, CONDARAMANICKAM RAGHUNATHA, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 15th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 30th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927; dir., forest coll., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1938.

RANGANATHAN, MAYAVARAM KRISHNASWAMI, B.A., B.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 16th Sep., 1892).—Joined the service as ppr. engr., on 10th June, 1916; asst. engr., June, 1917; asst. exec. engr., July, 1922; exec. engr., June, 1926.

RANGANATHAN, SAMUEL E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 30th Dec., 1877).—

Apptd. to I.E.S., 23rd Feb., 1921; addl. profsr., presdy. coll., Madras, July, 1922; on foreign service as vice-chancellor, Annamalai Univ., May, 1929; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

RANGANATHAN, SAURIRAJAN, Indian C.S. (ft. mag., United Provs.) (b. 12th Nov., 1908).—Educated at Madras University, School of Oriental Studies, London, and Oriel College, Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provs. as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte., Feb., 1935; asst. settlement officer, Nov., 1936.

RANGANATHAN, SUBRAHMANYA, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 2nd Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1916; arrived, 1st Jan., 1918, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Dec., 1919; sub. collr., Dec., 1922; emigration agent, Ceylon, Sept., 1923; offg. dep. secty. to govt., local self govt. dept., April, 1928; O.B.E., Jan., 1930; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Aug., 1930; ditto (provl. subs.), Dec., 1934; confid., Sept., 1935; inspr. mun. councils, etc., Madras, Oct., 1938.

RANGASWAMI AYYANGAR, GIVARPURAM NADATHUR, Indian Agricultural Service (millett specialist, Madras) (b. 19th May, 1887).—Apptd. asst. economic botanist, June, 1920; millet specialist, Oct., 1924.

RANGASWAMI AYYANGAR, S., B.A., B.L., Judl. dept., Madras (b. 21st July, 1889).—Apptd. administrator-genl. and offil. trustee, 16th July, 1929.

RANGASWAMI IYER, ALAGIAMANAVALAM, RAO BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 4th Mar., 1881).—Joined the service, 7th Nov., 1900; asst. dir. of comml. audit, Apl., 1926; dep. acct. genl., Madras, Apl., 1932; offg. dep. govt. exr. of accts., Bengal Nagpur rly., July, 1934; dep. acct.-genl., Madras, Nov., 1934; ret'd., Mar., 1936.

RANGI LAL, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.A., late Punjab Civil Service (b. 1st Mar., 1882).—Joined the service as proby. extra asst. commr., 15th Jan., 1906; confid., Sept., 1910; sub. judge, Aug., 1912; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1929; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, Oct., 1933; and again, Oct., 1934; offg. judl. commr., Quetta, Apl., 1936; ret'd., Mar., 1937.

RANGNEKAR, SIR SAJBA SHANKAR, B.A., LL.B., Barr., late Bombay Judicial Dept. (b. 20th Dec., 1878).—Joined the dept., 29th Sept., 1924; chief presdy. mag., Sept., 1924; actg. judge, high ct., Bombay, Aug., 1926, June, 1927, and again, June, 1928; confid. April, 1929; offg. ch. justice, high ct., Bombay, June, 1936; knighted, June, 1938; ret'd., Dec., 1938.

RANKIN, SIR GEORGE CLAUS, P.C., Kt., Barr., late Bengal Judicial Dept. (b. 12th Aug., 1877).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Calcutta, 14th Nov., 1918; Kt., Jan., 1925; chief justice, high ct., Nov., 1926; ret'd., Nov., 1934; P.C., 1935; apptd. member of judcl. cttee of P.C., Oct., 1935.

RANSFORD, ALISTER JOHN, Lt.-Col., C.I.E., R.E. (mint master, Bombay) (b. 5th Jan., 1895).—employ up to Jan., 1924; dep. mint m. ster, Feb., 1926; offg. mint master, May, 1927; again, May, 1929; confid., Sept., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1936.

RAANSFORD, CHARLES DE ST. CLEATHER, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 16th Sept., 1877).—Joined the service, 18th Jan., 1910, as head master, govt. high school; inspr. of schools, Aug., 1912; dist. educ. offr., Bellary, Feb., 1923; inspr. of European schools, Madras, Feb., 1930; retd., Sept., 1932.

RAO, BELTHANGADI GOPALAKRISHNA (see Gopalakrishna, R. B.).

RAO, CHIKBALLAPUR VENKATA SRINIVASA (see Srinivasa Rao, C.V.).

RAO, CHIRRAVOORI BALKRISHNA (see Chirravoori B. Rao).

RAO, DHANVADA ANANDA (see Ananda Rao, D.).

RAO, EKANATHA RAJARAM (see Ekanatha Rajaram Rao).

RAO, GHANTA VENKATA (see Venkata Rao, G.

RAO, KABAD APPAJI (see Appaji Rao, K.)

RAO, KASARAGOD PATANASHETTI LAKSHMANA (see Lakshmana Rao, K.P.).

RAO, KAIWAR SETU (see Setu Rao, K.)

RAO, KOTY VENKATA RAMANA (see Ramana Rao, K.V.).

RAO, KRISHNASWAMI ANANDA (see Ananda, R.K.).

RAO, KRISHNA KARNAD (see Krishna Rao).

RAO, MUTTA VENKATASUBBA (see Venkata-subba Rao).

RAO, MYSORE SUBBA (see Mysore Subba Rao).

RAO, NANDOLIKE DEVARAO KRISHNA (see Krishna Rao, N.D.).

RAO, NEELAMANGALA KESHAVA (see Keshava Rao, N.).

RAO, PEJAVAR MOHAN (see Mohan Rao, P.)

RAO, R. (see Ramachandra Rao).

RAO, BENEGAL SANJIVA (see Sanjiva Rao, B.)

RAO, P. H. SESHAGIRI (see Seshagiri Rao).

RAO, S. RAGHUNATHA (see Raghunatha Rao, S.).

RAO, UDYAVAR RAMA (see Rama Rao, U.).

RAO, VALLURI KAMESWARA (see Valluri K. Rao.)

RAO, VYAKARANA NARAHARI (see Narahari Rao, V.)

RAO, V. P. (see Pandurang Rao, V.).

RAO, V. SURYAPRAKASA (see Suryaprakasa Rao, V.).

RAOOOF, ABDUL (see Abdul Raoof).

RAPER, JOHN HUGH FRANCIS, Indian State Rlys. (ch. traffic manager, G.I.P. Rly.). (b. 23rd Sept., 1889).—Joined the service, 28th June, 1912; asst. traffic supt., June, 1915; dist. ditto, March, 1920; asst. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; asst. traffic manager, Nov., 1926; offg. dep. ditto, Nov., 1926; confd., July, 1929; offg. ch. traffic manager, June, 1931; and again, Jan., 1933; confd., Mar., 1933; offg. agent, G.I.P. Rly., May, 1936.

RASHEED KHAN, Abdul, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 28th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Lucknow University and Exeter College, Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

RASHID, ABDUL (see Abdul R.).

RASHID, MIAN ABDUL, Khan Bahadur, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. traffic commcl. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 3rd May, 1894).—Asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1919; provl. in senr. scale, Aug., 1937; on depn. to Gaekwar, Baroda State Rly., from Apl., 1935.

RASHID-UZ-ZAMAN, SAIVID, B.A., Indian C.S. (magt. and collr., Bihar) (b. 28th Mar., 1895).—Educ. at Patna Coll., Presidency Coll., Calcutta, and London Univ.; joined the service, 14th Dec., 1921; arrived, 19th Jan., 1922, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag., and collr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., Aug., 1925; confd., Jan., 1926; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1929; offg. addl. dep. secty. to the govt. of India, dept. of health, educn. and lands, Apl., 1933; secty., public dept., office of High Commr. for India, Mar., 1934; actg. dep. trade commr., June, 1934; adviser to Govt. of India delegation to Internatl. labour conf., Geneva, June, 1935; reverted to Bihar as magt. and collr., Nov., 1937.

RASTOGI, PIARE LAL, Babu (see Piare Lal Rastogi).

RATCLIFF, GEORGE MASSEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 8th Aug., 1906).—Educ. at King Edward VII School, Lytham, Lancs., and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1933.

RATNAGAR, RATAN DHANJIBHOY, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 14th Oct., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1914; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; offg. supt. engr., Oct., 1932.

RATNAGAR, SHAPURJI NASARVANJI JAMSHEDJI, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 28th Sept., 1882).—Joined the service, 11th July, 1911, as proby. extra asst. consvr.; extra asst. consvr., Oct., 1913; in foreign service, Junagadh State, from 1st June, 1915; dep. consvr., Dec., 1922; offg. consvr., Aug., 1934; retd. Sept., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

RATNASWAMI, M., C.I.E., Barr. (member, Madras services commn.) (b. 15th Aug., 1885).—Apptd. offg. princpl., law coll., Madras, 3rd Jan., 1928; confd., June, 1928; member, Madras services commn., Jan., 1930; offg. chairman, ditto, June, 1935.

RAU, BANTWAL GOPALAKRISHNA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 10th Nov., 1912).—Educated at Canara High School, Mangalore, Madras University, City and Guilds (Engineering) College, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.

RAU, SIR BENEGAL NARSINGA, C.I.E., B.A. (Camb.), Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Bengal) (b. 26th Feb., 1887).—Educ. at Canara High Sch., Mangalore, Madras and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1916; ditto, Assam, May, 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1922; offg. sec., legisl. dept., supt. and remember., legal affairs, March, 1925; confd., Dec., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; addl. jt. secty., legisl. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1934; offg. jt. secty., ditto, Jan., 1935; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Mar. 1935; on spec. duty, reforms office, govt. of India, Sept., 1935; on depn. to India Office, Oct.-Dec., 1938; knighted, June, 1938; judge, high ct., Bengal, Dec., 1938.

RAU, B. RAMA (see Rama Rau, Benegal).

RAU, HANUMANTA BHIMASENA (see Bhimasena Rau, Hanumanta).

RAU, HATTIANGADI SHANKAR, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Finance Dept (b. 29th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service, 22nd Aug., 1907, and held non-gazetted appts.; offg. supt., fin. dept., Nov., 1922; pers. asst. to contr. of currency, May, 1923; supt., fin. dept., Aug., 1923; asst. secty., fin. dept. (temp.), March, 1924; confd., Aug., 1924; offg. under-secty., fin. dept., July, 1925; confd., Nov., 1926, and again, April, 1928; budget officer, Dec., 1926, and again Nov., 1928; offg. dep. contr. of the currency, Bombay, Apr., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1931; member and secty., Indian federal finance expert commee., Jan., 1932; offg. contr., of the currency, Apr., 1935; retd., Sept., 1937.

RAU, KALLE RAMA RAU SADASHIVA (see Sadashiva Rau, K. R. R.).

RAU, LAJMI LAKSHMAN, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 18th Sept., 1880).—Joined the service as apprentice asst. supt., 15th Oct., 1904; asst. supt., June, 1906; supt., July, 1916; dep. postmaster-genl., traffic, Bombay Circle, Oct., 1923; offg. dir., tele. engrg., Lucknow, April, 1930; ditto, Madras, Mar., 1931; retd., June, 1932.

RAU, P. RAGHAVENDRA (see Raghavendra, R. P.).

RAU, PENDYALA SATIANARAINA, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 15th Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1918; served in Central Provs. as asst. commr. from 27th Jan., 1919; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1924; confd., Dec., 1927; offg. commr. of settlements and dir. of land records, etc., C.P., Feb., 1936; offg. finl. secty. to govt., C.P., Mar., 1936, to Mar., 1937.

RAWLINS, ERIC FORD, late Indian Police Service (b. 2nd July, 1887).—Joined the service 3rd Dec., 1907; asst. supt., Feb., 1911; supt., Dec., 1917; retd., Aug., 1932.

RAWLINSON, HUGH GEORGE, C.I.E., M.A., (Cantab.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 12th May, 1880).—Joined the service, 7th Jan., 1908, as profro. of English literature, Deccan Coll., Poona; princpl. and profro. of English and history, Karnataka Coll., Dharwar, Sept., 1919; offg. principal and profro., Deccan Coll., Poona, June, 1923; confd., Dec., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; retd., May, 1935.

RAWLEY, RATAN CHAND, M.A., M.Sc., D.Sc. (Lon.), late Dept. of Industries, Punjab.—Apptd. dir. of industries, 7th Nov., 1927; adviser and secty. to the Indian delegation to the Internatl. labour conference, Geneva, May-June, 1928.

RAY (see also Roy).

RAY, ANNADA SANKAR, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 14th March, 1904).—Educ. at University of Patna and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived Nov., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1936.

RAY, SIR ASOKE KUMAR, Bengal Judl. Dept., (advocate genl., Bengal) (b. 9th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as standing council, Bengal

(offg.), 20th Dec., 1929; confd., Apr., 1930; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Apr., 1932, and again, Nov., 1933; advocate genl., Bengal, Mar., 1934; knighted, Feb., 1937.

RAY, BIMALENDU, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 11th Oct., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 15th April, 1926; offg. supt., Oct., 1936.

RAY, CHUNI LAL, Rai Bahadur, B.A., late Excise and Salt Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 3rd May, 1881).—Joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1902; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1903; supt. of excise and salt, Oct., 1916; dep. commr., Sept., 1923; offg. commr., March, 1925; retd., Oct., 1932.

RAY, GERALD GEORGE, Indian Police (supt., Sind) (b. 14th June, 1895).—Joined the service, 6th Dec., 1915, as asst. supt.; on mil. duty from 20th June, 1917, to 17th Feb., 1919; offg. supt., Apr., 1922, Bombay; confd., June, 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932.

RAY, GORDON MEREDITH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 2nd Aug., 1912).—Educ. at Caterham Sch. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Dec., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

RAY, JITENDRA NATH, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 23rd August, 1892).—Joined the service, 2nd January, 1917; dep. supt., April, 1928; apptd. to I.P. as supt., June, 1934.

RAY, JNANENDRA NATH, I.S.O., M.A., B.L., Rai Bahadur, late Registration dept., Bengal (b. 17th May, 1876).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., 24th July, 1899; dep. magte. and dep. coll., July, 1901; inspr.-genl. of registration, Oct., 1926; I.S.O., June, 1929; retd., June, 1931.

RAY, KARUNAMAY, B.A., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 10th Nov., 1885).—Joined the service, 21st Oct., 1910; apptd. to I.P. as supt., Bengal, Dec., 1935.

RAY, KIRAN CHANDRA, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 27th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst., consvr., 2nd Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov. 1927.

RAY, KUMUD BHUSON, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 30th Jan., 1891).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 15th Jan., 1915; asst. engr., Jan., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1925; exec. engr., July, 1937.

RAY, LALIT KUMAR, L.M.S. (Punj.) D.P.H. (Cantab.), Public Health Dept., United Provs. (Asst. Dir. of Public Health) (b. Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as health officer, 1st Mar., 1914; asst. dir. of Public Health Nov., 15th, 1934.

RAY, NAGENDRA NATH, Bihar C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. 1st July, 1885).—Joined the service, 16th Mar., 1905; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1907; mag. and collr., May, 1937.

RAY, PRABHAT CHANDRA, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 23rd Feb., 1888).—Joined the service, 15th Feb., 1913; asst. engr., Feb., 1914; exec. eng., June, 1926.

RAY, PRAN KRISHTO, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., late Judl. dept., United Provs. (b. 9th Aug., 1875).—Joined the service, 10th July, 1905; sub. judge, Dec., 1917; offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1926; confd., Nov., 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

RAY, RAJENDRA CHANDRA, L.M.S., D.P.H., Public Health dept., Bengal (b. 9th Dec., 1877). Apptd. asst. dir. of public health, Mar., 1921.

RAY, RAJESHVAR PRASHAD, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 15th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service, 20th June, 1920; transferred to I.P., as supt., May, 1934.

RAY, RANAJIT KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Oct., 1906).—Educ. at Calcutta University and King's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1933.

RAY, REGINALD EDWARD ANTHONY, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 15th May, 1891).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 16th Dec., 1910; confd., June, 1912; addl. supt., Nov. 1924; spl. supt. Aug. 1925; awarded Indian police medal, 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1934.

RAY, RICHARD LIONEL. V.D., M.I.M.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Feb., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 4th Nov., 1905; dist. loco. supt., 1st July, 1912; offg. dep. loco. supt., Dec., 1922; confd., June, 1923; dep. ch. mech. engr., 1st April, 1925; offg. ch. mech. engr., June, 1929; confd., May, 1931; ret'd., Jan., 1937.

RAY, SAILENDRA NATH GUHA (see Guha Ray, S.N.).

RAY, SANAT KUMAR, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr. Bihar) (b. 6th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service, 24th Jan., 1910; asst. engr., Feb., 1911; exec. engr., Jan., 1921; suptg. engr., Mar., 1934.

RAY, SATISH CHANDRA, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (b. 24th May, 1888).—Apptd. inspr. of schools, Assam, 12th Nov., 1923; offg. princpl. Cotton Coll., Ganhati, variously from July, 1933.

RAY, SATYENDRA NATH, Indian C.S. (dep. secty., fin. dept., Bengal) (b. 25th June, 1902).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 2nd April, 1925; arrived, 4th May, 1925, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1934; and again, Feb., 1936; confd., Mar., 1937; dep. secty., fin. dept., Bengal, Oct., 1937.

RAY, TARAK CHANDRA, late Bengal Civil Service (b. 1880).—Joined the service, as proby. dep. magte. and dep. collr., 4th Aug., 1902; confd., May, 1903; magte. and collr., Sept., 1929; ret'd., July, 1935.

RAY CHAUDHURI, NIRMALKANTI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dy. collr., Bengal) (b. 16th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Calcutta University, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1931.

RAYNEAU, CYRIL ALEXANDER, Indian State Rlys. (coal supt.). (b. 8th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. coal supt., 1st May, 1916; offg. coal supt., April, 1928; confd., Apl., 1930.

RAYNER, ADRIEN BOOKER, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 15th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service, Oct., 1919, as asst. engr.; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

RAYNER, WILLIAM RUFFELL, India Office (admin. officer for statistical questions, economic and overseas dept.). (b. 16th Mar., 1890).—Educ. at King Charles' School, Tunbridge Wells; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 25th Jan., 1909, and attached to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; transf. to pol. dept., March, 1912; services lent to Colonial Office from March, 1921, to Jan., 1922; transf. to judl. and public dept., Jan., 1922; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; deputy reading clerk to Council, Feb., 1923; asst. princpl., Aug., 1924; jt. editor, India Office list, Oct., 1932, to May, 1934; admin. officer for statistical questions, economic and overseas dept., May, 1934.

RAYNES, CHARLES HENRY, M.C., Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 10th Aug., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 19th Feb., 1921; offg. dist. supt., May, 1927; confd., May, 1933; crimes asst. to D.I.G. Rlys., and C.I.D., June, 1937.

RAYNOR, ERIC WALTER, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 20th Sept., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 12th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Sept., 1925.

RAYNOR, FRANK HENRY CHARLES, Indian Police (supt., Orissa) (b. 18th Jan., 1902).—Joined the service, 6th Dec., 1921; offg. dist. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., Oct., 1932.

RAYNOR, VERE OWEN, Indian State Rlys. (dep. chief comml. mgr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 20th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 29th June, 1908; dist. ditto, April, 1918; offg. dep. ch. operating supt. (movement), N.W. rly., Jan., 1933; confd., June, 1936; dep. ch. comml. mgr., confd. (provl.), June, 1936.

RAZA ALI, SYED, Sir, Kr., C.B.E., B.A. LL.B. (agent-genl. for Govt. of India in Sth. Africa) (b. 29th Apl., 1882).—Educ. at high sch., Moradabad, and Muhammad Coll., Aligarh; member, U.P. legl. council, 1912-20; member, council of state, 1921-26; member, frontier enquiry comee., 1922; on deputation to S. Africa in connection with Indian question, 1925; C.B.E., July, 1926; member, public services commn., Oct., 1926, to Nov., 1931; agent-genl. for govt. of India in Sth. Africa, Jan., 1935; knighted, June, 1935.

RAZA, KAZIM (see Kazim Raza).

RAZA, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Raza).

RAZA, SAIYID ALAY (see Alay Raza Saiyid).

RAZA, SAIYID HASHIM (see Hashim Raza, S.).

READ, JAMES, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 11th Jan., 1898).—Educ. at Winchester and Wellington Mill. Coll., India; on mil. service, May, 1916, to April, 1921; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1923; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1923, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., Simla, March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1930; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Oct., 1931; confd., Oct., 1936.

REAKS, FRANK HUBERT, late Indian State Railways (E.I. Rly.) (b. 8th Sept., 1876).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1897, as traffic candidate; asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1899; dist. supt., April, 1907; dep. traffic manager, April, 1918; offg. traffic manager, Feb., 1924; dep. chief operating supt., July, 1925; offg. ch. comml. manager, Nov., 1927; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

REAKS, REGINALD RUPERT, M.B.E., late Indian Stores Dept. (b. 10th May, 1875).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Nov., 1893; supt., June, 1912; with Indian Munitions Board from 30th April, 1917; second asst. ch. contr., Nov., 1920; asst. ditto, April, 1921; offg. contr. of purchase, Aug., 1925; ret'd., May, 1930.

REAY, ROBERT WILLIAM, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 14th June, 1875).—Clerk, 2nd class, 23rd Oct., 1894; staff clerk, June, 1917; services lent to high commr. for India, from Oct., 1920; suptg. exec. officer, April, 1922; asst. ch. acctg. officer, Oct., 1930; dep. ditto, and clerk to commrs. of inc. tax, Nov., 1934; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

REBELLO, ALBIN RICHARD, O.B.E., F.S.S., F.R.E.S., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 3rd Apr., 1886).—Joined the service, 25th Mar., 1908; supt., fin. dept., Mar., 1922; asst. secty., taxation enquiry ctee., Nov., 1925; asst. acct. genl., Punjab, Mar., 1927; asst. dir., army audit, May, 1927; dep. acct. genl., Bengal, Mar., 1933; ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Nov., 1935.

REDCLIFT, S. W. (electrical adviser and ch. electric inspr., Bengal) (b. 9th Oct., 1887).—Apptd. electric adviser and electric inspector, Bengal and Assam (on probn.), 1st July, 1926; confd., July, 1928; electrical adviser and ch. electrical inspr., Bengal, Dec., 1928.

REDDI, OBULREDDIGARI PULLA (see Pulla Reddi, O.).

REDDI, P. H. RAMA (see Rama Reddi, P. H.).

REDHA, KANTA SARAN, Income-tax Dept., Bihar and Orissa (asst. commr.) (b. 29th June, 1886).—Asst. commr., Sept., 1921.

REDMOND, LIONEL, M.I.C.E., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 11th Dec., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (tempy.), 14th Jan., 1908; confd., April, 1912; resdt. engr., Aug., 1915; in milty. employ from 8th Jan., 1918, to 16th Sept., 1919; exec. engr., July, 1925; ret'd., Dec., 1935.

REDPATH, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 4th Sept., 1909).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1929; pers. asst. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Nov., 1934; under trng. in Punjab, July, 1935; under-secty. to the resdt. for C. India and dep. opium agent, Malwa, Apr., 1937; confd., Mar., 1938.

REES, OWEN MEREDITH, M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Bolton Grammar Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 4th Dec., 1909, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from April, 1916, to March, 1919; dep. commr. (prov. sub.), Sept., 1924; offg. commr. of settl. land records from July, 1923; ret'd. July, 1927; apptd. second addl. magte., 24 Parganas, Jan., 1933 to Mar., 1935; K-i-H. medal, 1st cl., June, 1935.

REES, STANLEY VERNON LLOYD, Indian Police (asst. supt., Assam) (b. 1st May, 1907).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1926; in training to May, 1928; proby. asst. supt., May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1928; offg. supt., rly. police, Mar., 1935.

REEVE, GILBERT AMOS, late Indian Police (b. 7th Dec., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 2nd Dec., 1920; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; asst. to A.G.G. in Central India, Oct., 1927; supt. (prov. sub.), Dec., 1928; confd., Feb., 1929; ret'd., July, 1934.

REGE, DATTATRAYA VAMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 18th Sept., 1897).—Educ. at Bombay Univ., and Cambridge (non-Collegiate); joined the service, 14th Nov., 1921; arrived, 7th Jan., 1922, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., April, 1925; and again, May, 1931; confd. (prov.), July, 1934; confd. May, 1936.

REGE, SHANTARAM BALKRISHNA, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 24th Oct., 1890).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., 30th March 1915; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., March, 1925.

REHMAN, MUHAMMAD BAZLUR, M.A., Ph.D., Bombay Educl. Service (prinpl., Ismail Coll.) (b. 11th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service as prinpl. Ismail Coll., Andhari, on probn., 7th Apr., 1930; confd., Apr., 1932.

REID, ARTHUR BEATSON, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., local self govt. and public health depts., United Provs.) (b. 16th March, 1888).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr., under-sec. to govt., Oct., 1919; dep. sec. to govt., March, 1921; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1925; addl. dep. secty. to govt. of India, educn., etc., dept., April, 1927; jt. secty., ditto, Dec., 1927; dep. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., etc., Apr., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; offg. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., etc., Apr., 1932; secty. to govt., United Provs., local self-govt. and public health depts., Mar., 1935.

REID, DOUGLAS FERNIE PATON, Indian Police (comdt., Aden) (b. 4th March, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 15th Oct., 1923; offg. supt., May, 1930; offg. dep. insp.-genl., W.I. States Agency, Mar., 1932; supt., Sept., 1935; comdt., Aden, July, 1936; under Col. govt., Aden, Apr., 1937.

REID, FREDERICK DAVID, late Northern India Salt Revenue Dept., (b. 27th Sept., 1876).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 27th Sept., 1894; offg. asst. supt., June, 1897; confd. Oct., 1898; supt., Nov., 1902; Kaisar-i-Hind medal (1st class), Jan., 1905; asst. commr., Feb., 1914; genl. manager of the salt mines (tempy.), Feb., 1917; confd., Jan., 1924; dep. commr., Sept., 1925; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

REID, JAMES EDWARD, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 5th Oct., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 27th Dec., 1919; offg. supt., Nov., 1927; confd., June, 1928; personal asst. to inspr.-genl., Sept., 1929.

REID, Rev. JAMES POTTER, M.A., K.I.H., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 8th July, 1885).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, 1st Jan., 1923; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1935; K.I.H. Medal.

REID, JOHN STUART, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 30th Aug., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1927; offg. exec. engr., May, 1931; confd., Oct., 1936.

REID, Sir ROBERT NEIL, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., Indian C.S. (govr. of Assam) (b. 15th July, 1883).—Educ. at Malvern and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst. magte. and collr.; under-sect. to govt., rev. and gen. depts., Jan., 1912; ditto, and jt. mag. and dep. collr., and on special duty as sec., Calcutta port facilities cttee., Nov., 1913; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; mag. and collr., Dec., 1919; K.-I.H. medal, Jan., 1924; offg. secty. govt. of Bengal, agric., &c., dept., April, 1927; confd., Aug., 1927; offg. commr., Mar., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1930; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bengal, Sept., 1930; confd., June, 1932; tempy. member, exec. council, Bengal, June, 1932; and again, Dec., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1936; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1937; govr. of Assam, Mar., 1937; actg. govr. of Bengal, June, to Oct., 1938.

KEID, Sir WILLIAM JAMES, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Glasgow High Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 25th Nov., 1891, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and sub. divl. officer; personal asst. to chief commr., Nov., 1898, to March, 1899; dep. commr., Nov., 1900; mag. and collr., Jan., 1906; offg. sec., govt. of E. Bengal and Assam, fin. and municipal depts., April, 1907, to Feb., 1908; confd., March, 1911; chief sec. to chief commr., Assam, April, 1912; offg. commr., Surma Valley and Hill Dists., May, 1914; C.S.I., June, 1914; commr., Surma Valley and Hill Dists., Sept., 1914; addl. member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council from Aug., 1916; commr., Assam Valley Dists., May, 1919; member of govr.'s exec. council, Assam, Jan., 1921, to Dec. 1925; K.C.I.E., June, 1924; offg. govr. of Assam, 11th April to 5th Aug., 1925, and from 11th Aug. to 8th Dec., 1925; ret'd., Nov., 1926.

REILLY, Sir BERNARD RAWDON, K.C.M.G., C.I.E., O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Service (b. 25th Mar., 1882).—Served in mil. dep. until 17th April, 1908, when apptd. asst. pol. agent, Kolhapur; on deputn. as tutor and guardian to minor chief of Sangli, June, 1909; spl. duty, pol. dept., Bombay sectt., June, 1910; tutor and companion to Mir. of Khairpur, Jan. to July, 1911; asst. to resdt., Aden, April, 1912; O.B.E., June, 1918; actg. pol. resdt., Aden, May, 1925, and again June, 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; pol. agent, Banas Kantha, Feb., 1928; 1st asst. resdt., Aden, Nov., 1928; offg. resdt. and C.-in-C., Aden, May, 1930; confd., Mar., 1931; ch. commr., Aden, Apl., 1932; K.C.M.G., June, 1934; ret'd., Mar., 1937.

REILLY, CALLCOTT, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 1st June, 1875).—Joined the service, 21st March, 1900; asst. acctnt.-genl., April,

1915; examr. of customs accts., Bombay, Dec., 1920; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Feb., 1927; ditto, Madras, Dec., 1927; ditto, posts and tels., Jan., 1929; ret'd., June, 1930.

REILLY, Sir HENRY D'ARCY CORNELIUS, late Indian C.S. (b. 15th Jan., 1876).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Nov., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. ditto, Aug., 1909; regr., Madras High Court, July, 1910, to July, 1913; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1916; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1924; ditto, July and August, 1925; ditto, June, 1926; actg. addl. judge, ditto, May, 1927; judge, ditto, Dec., 1928; ret'd., July, 1934; chief justice in Mysore State; knighted, Jan., 1938.

REINHOLD, CARL HENRY, M.C., F.R.C.S.B., Col., late I.M.S. (b. 4th Dec., 1880).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1905; in milty. employ till Sept., 1908, and from Apl., 1909, to Apl., 1913; on milty. service, Aug., 1914, to Feb., 1921; civil surgeon, United Provs., Oct., 1920; ditto and jail supt., Feb., 1921; ch. medl. officr., Delhi, Apl., 1928; inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Punjab, Dec., 1933; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

REMFY, CHARLES OLIVER, B.A., LL.B., Barr. (chief judge, Calcutta small cause ct.) (b. 19th Sept., 1877).—Apptd. offg. chief judge, small cause court, Calcutta, 1st Mar., 1925; confd., March, 1926; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, variously from June, 1928.

REMFY, MAURICE, late Judicial Dept., Bengal (b. 15th Nov., 1873).—Joined the service as dep. regr., 11th Dec., 1902; offg. regr. of insolvency, high ct., origl. side, June, 1915; confd., Feb., 1916; offg. regr., high ct., origl. side, May, 1921; confd., Nov., 1921; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

REMINGTON, JAME BOILEAU, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transportation supt.) (b. 12th Feb., 1891).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1910; asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1913; dist. ditto, June, 1917; asst. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; offg. divl. trans. supt., Jan., 1925; confd., April, 1926; offg. ch. trans. supt., May, 1933.

RENDELE, Hugh BAINBRIDGE, I.S.O., late Excise Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 27th Aug., 1895; asst. commr., Sept., 1914; I.S.O., June, 1925; ret'd., July, 1928.

RENNICK, DENIS KINGSTON, M.B.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 25th Apl., 1884).—Joined the service, 4th Jan., 1904; extra asst. supt., Feb., 1912; supt., Oct., 1932.

RENNIE, Rev. JOHN YULE, M.A., B.D., B.Litt., Indian Eccles. Estab. (presdy. senr. chaplain, Bombay) (b. 10th Dec., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 1st Dec., 1920; junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, Dec., 1922; senr. ditto, Dec., 1932; presdy. senr. ditto, Oct., 1937.

RENNISON, Rev. ERIC DAVID ROBERT, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 7th Oct., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 14th Oct., 1922; junior chaplain, March, 1923; senr. ditto, Mar., 1929.

RENWICK, Henry Percival, Indian State Rlys. (dep. trans. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 6th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., Nizam's G.S. Rly., 3rd Aug., 1912; dist. loco. and carr. supt., June, 1918; dist. loco. supt.,

G.I.P. Rly., Aug., 1920; asst. trans. supt., Dec., 1922; supt., Feb., 1929; offg. divl. transportn. supt., Mar., 1933; offg. dep. transportn. supt., Oct., 1933; confd., Oct., 1936.

REUBEN, DAVID EZRA, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bihar) (b. 3rd Sept., 1893),—Educ. at Bombay and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1918; arrived in India, 23rd Jan., 1919, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., July, 1925; offg. settlement officer, Feb., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1928; confd., Oct., 1932.

REYNELL, CHARLES ARTHUR, late Indian Police (b. 30th May, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Nov., 1905; dist. supt., May, 1915; King's police medal, Jan., 1918; dep. inspr.-gen., June, 1924; retd., Sept., 1933.

REYNELL, DOUGLAS, C.I.E., M.A., B.C.L. (Oxon.) late Indian Educl. Service (b. 23rd Nov., 1877).—Joined the service as inspr. of schools, 22nd April, 1919; asst. dir. of public instrn., Punjab, March, 1922; secty., public services commn., Aug., 1931, to Aug., 1936; offg. member, ditto, Aug.-Oct., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1936; retd., May, 1937.

REYNOLDS, CECIL HOWARD, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 22nd May, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on 28th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., April, 1927; confd., Feb., 1935; on spec. duty, indus. dept., Mar., 1937, to Dec., 1937.

REYNOLDS, FRANK, late Indian Police Service (United Provs.) (b. 10th May, 1877).—Joined the service, 21st April, 1899, and posted to United Prov.; asst. supt. of police, April, 1906; supt., Nov., 1912; retd., June, 1930.

REYNOLDS, FRANK ARMSTRONG ABBOTT, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 30th May, 1913).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Burma, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; supt., Jan. 1936.

REYNOLDS, HENRY FORSYTH, Indian Police (commr. of police) (b. 11th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 4th Dec., 1909; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; offg. commr. of police, Rangoon city, Mar., 1932, and again, Dec., 1935; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Dec., 1932; commr. of police, Rangoon, Dec., 1936.

REYNOLDS, HENRY OSBORNE, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 11th Oct., 1883).—Educ. at Sedbergh Gr. Sch., Owen's Coll., Manchester, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt., Nov., 1911; asst. sec. to govt., rev. dept., No., 1911, to Nov., 1912; under sec., chief sec.'s dept., Feb., 1913, to Nov., 1914; dep. commr., March, 1918; offg. secy. to Govt., reforms office, Jan., 1922; secy. to govt. and dep. commr., Jan., 1923; confd., Aug., 1923; offg. sec. to govt., agric., excise and forest dept., Nov., 1925; co. fd., March, 1926; commr. of settlements and land records, Nov., 1926; offg. addl. secty. home and pol. dept., Oct., 1931; offg. commr., Apl., 1932; confd., Aug., 1935; finl. commr., Burma, Apl., 1935.

REYNOLDS, SIR LEONARD WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.C., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th Feb., 1874).—Educ. at Bradford and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of

1897; arrived, 6th Dec., 1898, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent in Central India, May, 1902; offg. 1st asst. to ditto, April, 1904; confd., Feb., 1906; K-i-H. medal, 1st class, Jan., 1905; temp. asst. sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., June, 1908; confirmed, Nov., 1909; dep. sec., ditto, Nov., 1910, to Feb., 1914; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; press censor, War Office, Oct., 1914, to June, 1916; on spl. duty under resdnt., Kashmir, Oct., 1916; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Jan., 1917; spl. duty, Rajputana, Nov., 1918; resdnt., W. Rajputana States, March, 1919; services lent to Jaipur Durbar from April, 1924; agent to gov.-gen. in Rajputana and ch. commr., Ajmer Merwara, March, 1927; C.S.I., June, 1928; attended Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; K.C.I.E., June, 1931; retd., Dec., 1933.

RHIND, DONALD, B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (economic botanist, Burma) (b. 26th Sept., 1899).—Joined the service as mycologist, Burma, 21st March, 1923; economic botanist, Dec., 1927.

RHODES, SIR CAMPBELL WARD, K.C.I.E., C.B.E. (b. 17th Jan., 1874).—C.B.E., Dec., 1919; Presdnt., Bengal Chamber of Commerce and Presdnt., Associated Chambers of Commerce of India and Ceylon, 1922; member of Indian fiscal commission; Kt., Jan., 1923; member of Council of India, 12th May, 1925 to May, 1935; vice-chairman, Ind. staty. rly. bd. ctee., June, 1933; member, Ind. res. bank ctee., July, 1933; K.C.I.E., June, 1935.

RHODES, CHARLES KENNETH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (chief secty., govt. of Assam) (b. 5th May, 1889).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after the exam. of 1912; arrived, 10th Dec., 1913, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), May, 1915, to July, 1919; under sec. to govt., Assam, Nov., 1920; asst. commr. and settlt. officer, Nov., 1923; offg. secty. to govt. of Assam, fin. and rev. dept., Apl., 1929; confd., Dec., 1930; dep. commr., Dec., 1930; member of ctee. on public accts., Assam, 1933-34; offg., dep. secty., fin. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1934; jt. secty., reforms office, govt. of India, Oct., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; offg. chief secty., govt. of Assam, May, 1937.

RIAZ AHMAD, MIAN, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (b. 24th Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. conser., Punjab, 22nd Jan., 1929.

RIAZUDDIN AHMAD KHAN, MUNSHI, B.A., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 16th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as dep. supt., 8th July, 1913; apptd. to I.P. as supt., Dec., 1934.

RICE, PERCIVAL RYAN, late Imperial Customs Service (b. 29th July, 1877).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 24th June, 1897; curator, comml. museum, comml. intell. dept. (temp.), July, 1915; confd., June, 1918; apptd. to office of Indian Trade commr. from Sept., 1919, to March, 1923; officer in charge, comml. intell. sec., British Empire exhib., April-Oct., 1924; offg. asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta, Feb., 1925, and again, July, 1926; offg. collr. ditto, Chittagong, June, 1925; asst. collr., Calcutta, Jan., 1927; ditto, Bombay, Oct., 1930; retd., July, 1932.

RICH, WILLIAM HAROLD ADRIAN, Indian Police.—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 20th Nov., 1928.

RICHARDS, CECIL JOHN, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 8th March, 1894).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Aug., 1920; arrived, 5th Jan., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. settl. officer, Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1927; confd., Apl., 1931.

RICHARDS, REV. GEORGE HENRY, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 24th Sept., 1892).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 26th Mar., 1930.

RICHARDS, PHILIP BERNARD, A.R.C.S., F.E.S., Indian Agric. Service (entomologist to govt., United Provs.) (b. 31st May, 1886).—Joined the service as entomologist to govt. on 15th March, 1921; offg. dir. of agric., United Provs., May, 1934, and again May, 1936.

RICHARDS, ROBERT.—Educ. at the University Coll. of Wales, Aberystwyth and St. John's Coll. Cambridge; Lecturer at Glasgow Univ., and Head of Dept. of Economics and Extension Lecturer at Bangor Univ., M.P. for Wrexham division, Nov., 1922, to Oct., 1924; parly. under sec. of state for India, Jan. to Nov., 1924.

RICHARDSON, ALAN, Indian State Ry's. (ch. mech. engr., G.I.P. Ry.) (b. 9th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1906; asst. carr. and wagon supt., Sept., 1910; dist. ditto, July, 1914; dep. transportation supt., April, 1925; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr. (C. & W.), March, 1927; dep. ditto (prov.), Dec., 1927; offg. ch. mech. engr., Mar., 1933; confd., Apl., 1933.

RICHARDSON, ALEXANDER ARMITAGE, late Indian State Ry's. (b. 8th Dec., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., Jan., 1907; exec. engr., N.W. Ry., Jan., 1918; ditto, E.B. Ry., April, 1921; retd., April, 1933.

RICHARDSON, CHARLES RULAND SUGDEN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1913; exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1922.

RICHARDSON, EDWARD ALBERT, M.A. (Oxon), late Indian Educl. Service (United Provs.) (b. 19th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 21st July, 1907, and apptd. inspr. of schs., United Provs.; asst. dir. of public instrn., ditto, July, 1912; offg. dir. of public instrn., ditto, May, 1913, to May, 1920; retd., Nov., 1931.

RICHARDSON, FRANCIS ALBERT ILFORD, Impl. Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 26th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service, 17th Mar., 1921; asst. collr., Mar., 1923.

RICHARDSON, HARRY MILLARD, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 1st Feb., 1878).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 7th Dec., 1896; offg. asst. supt., Aug., 1899; confd., Jan., 1900; with Govt. of Bombay on famine work, Apl., 1900; offg. supt., Sept., 1901; confd., Feb., 1904; postmaster, Oct., 1907; presy. postmaster, May, 1921; offg. P.M.G., Madras, Mar., 1931; presy. postmr., Bombay, July, 1931; retd., Mar., 1933.

RICHARDSON, HUGH EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 22nd Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Glenalmond and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1930;

arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1932; offg. asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1934; British trade agent, Gyantse, July, 1936; pol. officer, Sikkim, in addn., from May, 1937, to Nov., 1937.

RICHARDSON, MAURICE ROBERT, C.I.E., B.A., B.A.I., Indian Service of Engrs. (secty. to govt., P.W.D., irrign. bch., United Provs.) (b. 12th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., Aug., 1926; confd., Oct., 1931; offg. ch. engr., and jt. secty. to govt. p.w.d. irrign. bch., Nov., 1933; and again, Nov., 1934; confd., Aug., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1935.

RICHARDSON, SIR THOMAS WILLIAM, Kt., late Indian C.S., Barr. (1905).—Educ. at Brighton Coll., Cheltenham Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 8th Dec., 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec., gen. rev., and statistical depts., March, 1893; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1895; registrar, high court, appellate side, Dec., 1895; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1899; sec. to govt. of Bengal, judl. and gen. depts., Dec., 1906; offg. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., March to Dec., 1907; acting judge, Calcutta High Court, Jan., 1909; dist. and sessions judge and supdt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, Sept., 1909; addl. judge, Calcutta high court, March, 1912; confirmed, Jan., 1913; Kt., Jan., 1922; retd., Nov., 1924.

RICHARDSON, WILLIAM NORMAN, O.B.E., late Bombay C.S.—Held non-gazetted appts. from 14th Feb., 1895, to 7th May, 1907; dep. coll., May, 1907; controller, hostile trading firms in Sind, from Dec., 1914; also custodian of enemy property; city mag., Karachi, Dec., 1919; retd., April, 1928; M.B.E., June, 1928; O.B.E., June, 1934.

RICHMOND, SIR ROBERT DANIEL, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 29th Oct., 1878).—Apptd. asst. consvr. of forests, 16th Nov., 1901, and posted to Madras; dist. forest officer, March, 1907; principal, Madras forest coll., Coimbatore, Aug., 1913, to May, 1914; asst. inspr.-gen. of forests, India, Jan., 1919; consr., March, 1923; offg. ch. consvr., April, 1927; confd., June, 1928; C.I.E., June, 1932; retd., Nov., 1932; member, Madras public services commission, Nov., 1932; chairman, Jan., 1935; knighted, June, 1936.

RICKERBY, LEONARD WILLIAM ROBERT, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accis. dept.) (b. 1st Mar., 1901).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st Mar., 1920, after previous service from 23rd Apl., 1919; exec. officer, Nov., 1934.

RICKETTS, ALFRED ARTHUR HICKMAN, M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian Service of Engrs. (subg. engr., Burma) (b. 5th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service, 6th Oct., 1906; asst. engr., Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; offg. supt. engr., May, 1930; confd., Sept., 1934.

RICKETTS, THOMAS CHARLES DONALD, M.C. (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 3rd July, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 17th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Dec., 1934.

RICKIE, JAMES HEPBURN, Indian State Ry's. (b. 25th July, 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 1st Nov., 1906; ditto (perm.), July, 1908; dist. engr., Sept., 1920; offg. dep. ch. engr., variously from May, 1926; confd., Mar., 1935; offg. dep. agent, Apl., 1932; ch. engr., Oct., 1935.

RIDLEY, SIDNEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to Agent Genl., for India in Sth. Africa) (b. 26th March, 1902).—Educ. at Royal Grammar School, Lancaster, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aug., 1929; offg. dep. commr., U.S. frontier, Oct., 1931; offg. dep. secty. to govt., Bombay, fin. dept., Jan., 1935; secty. to govt., fin. dept., Sind, Apl., 1936; secty. to Agent Genl. for India in South Africa, Oct., 1936.

RIDSDALE, Rev. ARTHUR CYRIL, D.D., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Bengal) (b. 24th March, 1877).—Joined the service, 11th Sept., 1905; junr. chaplain, Sept., 1908; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1918; ret'd., Feb., 1930.

RIEU, Sir JEAN LOUIS, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 23rd Nov., 1872).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 20th Nov., 1893, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., June, 1900; ditto, and manager, encumbered estates in Sind, Feb., 1903, to May, 1906; 1st asst., Dec., 1904; ditto, Bombay, March, 1905; junr. collr., Nov., 1909; sec. to govt., gen., educatl., &c., depts., Nov., 1911; senr. collr., and ditto, Dec., 1915, to Feb., 1916; senr. collr. and supt. of stamps, Feb., 1917; actg. sec. to govt., rev. and finl. depts., Feb., 1918; temp. addl. sec. to govt., ditto, July, 1918; C.S.I., June, 1920; commr., Feb., 1923; member. govt.'s exec. council, Bombay, Jan., 1926; K.C.S.I., June, 1929; ret'd., Oct., 1929.

RIGG, Rev. ARTHUR CECIL PIETRONI, M.A., Eccles. Dept., United Provs. (senr. chaplain) (b. 27th Feb., 1889).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 16th April, 1917; junr. chaplain, April, 1919; senr. chaplain, April, 1929.

RIGG-STANSFIELD, Rev. HUBERT, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 22nd Sept., 1897).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 25th Dec., 1931.

RIJHWANI, JETHANAND MANDHANDAS, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 4th June, 1903).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 21st Apl., 1928; offg. exec. engr., Mar., 1933; confd., Apl., 1937.

RIKH, LIONEL JAIPAL, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 21st Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Feb., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1924; confd., Nov., 1929.

RILEY, Percy BROWN, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Veterinary Service (b. 13th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as 2nd supt., civil vety. dept., Bihar and Orissa, 27th Mar., 1920; dep. dir., Dec., 1925; offg. dir., April, 1926, and again, Feb., 1928; confd., Nov., 1930; dir. vety. services, Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1932.

RIPDAMAN, SINGH, Ind. Service of Engrs., (b. 6th Oct., 1908).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st May, 1933.

RIPPON, STEWART ROSS, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Vety. Service (dep. dir., Burma) (b. 29th July, 1895).—Joined the service as supt., Burma, 18th Feb., 1924; dep. dir., Dec., 1929; offg. dir., July, 1932.

RISHWORTH, HENRY RICHARD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Ry's. (dist. medl. officer, G.I.P. Ry.) (b. 8th Feb., 1891).—Joined the service as dist. surg., G.I.P. Ry., 22nd June, 1923; dist. medl. officer, Apl., 1929.

RITCHIE, ADAM ALEXANDER, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 5th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. princpl., Aitchison Coll., Lahore, 21st Mar., 1915; asst. master, Mayo coll., Dec., 1927; vice-princpl., ditto, Jan., 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

RITCHIE, CHARLES STEWART, late Indian State Ry's (E.B. Ry.) (b. 31st May, 1880).—Joined the service, 6th Dec., 1900; asst. traffic supt., May, 1907; dist. ditto, April, 1915; offg. dep. traffic manager, Nov., 1927; offg. dep. agent, Aug., 1929; ret'd., May, 1931.

RITCHIE, JOHN HALL, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 22nd Jan., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric., Oct., 1913; dep. ditto, Jan., 1915; offg. secty., Indian central cotton commee., Bombay, Feb., 1928; offg. princpl., agricl. coll., Nagpur, Apl., 1933; dir. of agric., Central Provs., Feb., 1934; ditto, United Provs., Nov., 1935.

RITCHIE, WILLIAM DUNCAN, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 4th April, 1877).—First commn., 27th June, 1901; in mil. employ to July, 1904; civil surgn., Assam, Sept., 1904; on mil. service from Oct., 1914, to Oct., 1920; offg. inspr.-genl., April, 1929; ret'd., Apr., 1932.

RIVAZ, CECIL ARTHUR GLENCAIRN, B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 5th Nov., 1876).—Joined the Indian fin. dept., 22nd Feb., 1902; asst. acct.-gen., Punjab, May, 1902; ditto, United Provs., Sept., 1904; ditto, Punjab, Nov., 1905; asst. compr., Central Provs., April, 1907; asst. acctnt.-gen., E. Bengal and Assam, May, 1908; ditto, paper currency, Bombay, Oct., 1912; examr. of govt. press accts., Calcutta, March, 1915; offg. dep. compr.-gen., May, 1918, to Feb., 1919; dep. contr. of currency, Bombay, Aug., 1920; ditto, Calcutta, Jan., 1921; contr. of currency, Northern India, April, 1922; compr., Assam, May, 1923; acctnt.-gen., Central Provs., Nov., 1925; ditto, Burma, April, 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1929.

RIVETT-CARNAC, HERBERT GORDON, Lt.-Col. (ret'd.), late Indian Pol. Service (b. 13th Feb., 1892).—First commn. dated 6th Sept., 1911; in milty. employ prior to 3rd Dec., 1923; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, Dec., 1923; asst. to agent to Madras States, Nov., 1927; British trade agent, Gyantse, and asst. to pol. offr. in Sikkim, May, 1928; asst. pol. agent in Southern states of C.I. and in Malwa, Nov., 1929; under-secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Sept., 1930; secty. ditto, Aug., 1931; civil secty. to ch. commr. at Aden, Nov., 1932; vice-consul, Zahidan, Sept., 1934; ret'd., Feb., 1938.

RIZAWI, SAIYID MUHAMMAD SHUAIB, Khan Bahadur (see Saiyid, M.S.R.).

ROBBINS, CHARLES ROBINSON, M.C., D.F.C., M.A., late Indian Forest Service (Burma).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., April, 1925; retd., March, 1929.

ROBERT, ARTHUR WILLIAM, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th March, 1880).—Apptd. asst. engr., Madras, 12th Sept., 1903; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1928; confd., Nov., 1930; retd., Mar., 1935.

ROBERTS, ALLAN ARBUTHNOT LANE, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Oct., 1884).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 29th Nov., 1909; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sect. to govt., Punjab, Sept., 1915, to Nov., 1917; sec., municipal commee., Delhi, March, 1918; dep. commr. (prov.), Nov., 1923; ditto (perm.), Feb., 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; retd., Mar., 1934.

ROBERTS, The Ven. ARTHUR BETTON, late Eccles. Dept., Central Provs. (b. 27th Dec., 1880).—Joined the service, 13th May, 1913, as junr. chaplain; senr. chaplain, May, 1923; offg. archdeacon, April, 1928; retd., Jan., 1934.

ROBERTS, DAVID EDWARD, M.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 14th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Feb., 1914; I.A.R.O. from 7th Sept., 1918, to 15th Feb., 1919; prin., Murarichand Coll., Sylhet, Dec., 1926; offg. dir. of public instrn., Assam, June, 1933; prin., Cotton Coll., Gauhati, Mar., 1934; retd., May, 1939.

ROBERTS, Sir ERNEST HANDFORTH GOODMAN, Kt., Barr., Burma, Judl. Dept. (ch. justice, high ct., Rangoon).—Apptd. ch. justice high ct., Rangoon, 28th Apl., 1936; knighted, June, 1936.

ROBERTS, FRANCIS LANE, Major, I.A. (retd.) late Burma Commn. (b. 24th April, 1886).—Arrived in India, 16th Nov., 1905; asst. commdt., July, 1913; reverted to military duty from April, 1917, to Oct., 1919; asst. commr., July, 1920; sub. divl. officer (in addn.), Nov., 1923; offg. dep. commr., April, 1924; retd., Sept., 1930.

ROBERTSON, ALAN CRAIGIE, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 11th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 13th Nov., 1907; dist. ditto, June, 1914; spl. fuel officer, E.I. Rly., Oct., 1926; supt., May, 1929; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., E.I. rly., Jan., 1933; confd., July, 1933; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr. (running), E.I. rly., Nov., 1933; retd., July, 1936.

ROBERTSON, ALLAN NELSON MCINNES, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 25th Nov., 1881).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905; asst. engr., Dec., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1926; confd., April, 1931; retd., Mar., 1934.

ROBERTSON, ANTHONY WILLIAM, D.F.C., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 1st Dec., 1896).—Joined the service, 17th April, 1913; offg. supt., Sept., 1923; confd., Nov., 1932; asst. commr. in the Andamans from 16th Oct., 1923, to 7th Feb., 1927; Diwan, Bundi State, Sept., 1936.

ROBERTSON, ARTHUR WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 24th Nov., 1905).—Educ. at High School, Dublin and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1929; arrived Dec., 1929; and served in Burma as asst. commr.

ROBERTSON, Sir BENJAMIN, K.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., C.I.E., LL.D., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at the Inst., Elgin, the Gr. Sch. and Univ. of Aberdeen, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 28th Nov., 1885, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; transf. to Central Prov. as provl. supt. of census operations, April, 1890; asst. commr., Oct., 1892; commr. of excise, Oct., 1894; dep. commr., Nov., 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1898; chief sec. to chief commr., May, 1902, to March, 1903, and Dec., 1903, to Jan., 1906; commr., Berar, July, 1907; offg. sec. to Govt. of India, dept. of commerce and industry, Feb. to Nov., 1907, and March to July, 1908; sec., ditto, July, 1908, to March, 1912; C.S.I., Jan., 1910; addl. member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, March, 1910; temp. member of govr.-gen.'s council, March to Nov., 1910; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1911; chief commr., Central Provs., 3rd August, 1912; special duty in S. Africa, Jan. to April, 1914; K.C.M.G., June, 1914; retd., Feb., 1920; member of the Council of India, Nov., 1922, to Nov., 1927.

ROBERTSON, CHARLES ROBERTSON OVENS, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 10th Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 22nd Nov., 1922; offg. supt., April, 1920; confd., June, 1934.

ROBERTSON, DONALD MURRAY SLADEN, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (ch. opertg. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 19th Oct., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Sept., 1903; dist. supt., April, 1917; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., March, 1926; offg. divl. supt., March, 1927; offg. ch. operating supt., Mar., 1933; ch. comml. mangr., E.I. rly., Mar., 1934; ch. opertg. supt., E.I. rly., Oct., 1935.

ROBERTSON, EDMUND GEORGE, late salt rev. dept., Burma (b. 22nd Sept., 1885).—Apptd. collr. of salt revenue, May, 1928; offg. commr., Apl., 1933; retd., Nov., 1934.

ROBERTSON, EDWARD GEORGE DONALD, Indian Police (b. 22nd Dec., 1916).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Burma, on probn. Dec., 1936.

ROBERTSON, FRANCIS CALDER FORD, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 19th March, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 21st Feb., 1924; dep. consvr., April, 1929.

ROBERTSON, FREDERICK EWART, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th March, 1881).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 13th Nov., 1901; dist. supt., Jan., 1912; on milty. service from 5th Dec., 1914, to 1st Jan., 1920; chief supt., April, 1920; jt. dep. agent, June, 1923; divl. supt., Feb., 1925; offg. ch. operating supt., Jan., 1926; offg. agent, E.I. Rly., April, 1929; offg. ch. operating supt., Apl., 1929; confd., Apl., 1929; retd., June, 1933.

ROBERTSON, FREDERICK WYNNE, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Feb., 1885).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch.; Real Gymnasium, Hildersheim (Hanover), and

Trin. Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 13th Dec., 1909, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; ditto, and asst. settl. offr., Nov., 1913; jt. mag. and dep. collr., April, 1915; on mil. duty, Aug. to Oct., 1918; mag. and collr., April, 1922; sec., board of rev., Bengal, Feb., 1923; offg. commr., Mar., 1930; confd., June, 1933; C.I.E., June, 1935; ret'd., Mar., 1937; chairman pub. serv. comm., Bengal, Aprl., 1937.

ROBERTSON, GEORGE STRAFFORD, B.A.I., B.A., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian State Ryis. (b. 29th May, 1886).—Joined the service as junr. engr., 14th Oct., 1909; resdt. engr., Sept., 1919; exec. engr., May, 1925; pers. asst. to ch. engr., Oct., 1929; ret'd., Oct., 1937.

ROBERTSON, HENRY FERGUSON, B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Burma) (b. 31st May, 1895).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, 16th Dec., 1921; prof'r. of agric. and mycologist, July, 1927; offg. princpl., agric. coll., Mandalay, Aprl., 1932; dep. dir. of agric., Burma, Mar., 1933.

ROBERTSON, JAMES PERCY WYNNE, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 17th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 18th Aprl., 1920; dist. supt., Aprl., 1924; awarded Indian police medal, Feb., 1937.

ROBERTSON JOHN, C.B.E., V.D., B.A., B.A.I., late Indian State Ryis. (b. 3rd June, 1878).—Joined the service as junr. engr., 15th Aug., 1902; resdt. engr., May, 1913; in milty. employ from 9th May, 1916, to 2nd Aug., 1919; C.B.E., June, 1919; dist. engr., Oct., 1920; supt. of works, surveys and construction, Oct., 1927; offg. dep. ch. engr., Aprl., 1930; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

ROBERTSON, MURRAY, Indian State Ryis. (junr. dep. agent, E.I. Rly.) (b. 15th June, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. secty. to agent, 20th Aug., 1920; jt. secty. to agent, Aug., 1924; secty. to agent, Jan., 1925; offg. junr. dep. agent, Oct., 1930; confd., Nov., 1931.

ROBERTSON, THOMAS HENRY, Office of High Commr. for India (executive officer, store dept.) (b. 8th June, 1895).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 4th Feb., 1915, and attached to store dept.; on mil. service from May, 1915, to Aug., 1919; transf'd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922.

ROBERTSON, WHEATLEY ALEXANDER, F.R.G.S., late Indian Forest Service (b. 4th Aug., 1885).—Arrived, 29th Oct., 1906, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1911; instructor, forest coll., Nov., 1918; offg. consvr., July, 1925; confd., Sept., 1926; ret'd., June, 1933.

ROBEY, EDWARD BEAUMONT, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 30th Dec., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 29th Sept., 1903; exec. engr., Sept., 1911; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1925; suptg. engr., Sept., 1927; offg. ch. engr., Feb., 1928; Aprl., 1930, and again, Aprl., 1933; ditto, E.B. rly., Oct., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of ryis., April, 1929; confd., Oct., 1934; ret'd., Dec., 1935.

ROBINS, LESLIE CYRIL FREDERICK, Indian Police (b. 4th Dec., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 24th Nov., 1928; confd., Nov., 1930; offg. supt., June, 1933; police asst. to A.G.G. in C.I., Mar., 1937.

ROBINSON, EDWARD CHARLES, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 5th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1912; supt., July, 1925.

ROBINSON, GILBERT CRESCENS, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Madras) (b. 12th March, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 23rd Nov., 1914; dep. consvr., Nov., 1919; chief forest officer, Coorg, May, 1925; offg. consvr., Oct., 1933; confd., June, 1937.

ROBINSON, HAROLD WOOD, Indian State Ryis. (dep. ch. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 7th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. exec. bridge engr., 2nd Feb., 1923; bridge engr., N.W. Rly., Mar., 1931; services loaned to M. and S.M. Rly. from Feb., 1929; offg. dep. ch. engr. (bridges), N.W. rly., Mar., 1935; confd., Aug., 1935.

ROBINSON, STEPHEN ERIC, Indian Police (asst. supt.).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., 24th Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

ROBINSON, SYDNEY HERBERT, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 31st May, 1876).—Apptd. med. offr., India store depot, 1st Jan., 1909; ret'd., Oct., 1938.

ROBINSON, SIR SYDNEY MADDOCK, Kt., Barr. (1888).—Apptd. offg. junior govt. advocate, chief court, Punjab, Aug., 1889; govt. advocate, April, 1899; has offd. as legal rememberancer on several occasions since 1902; offg. judge, chief court, Lower Burma, April, 1909; confd., Dec., 1911; Kt., Jan., 1922; chief justice, high ct., Rangoon, Dec., 1922; ret'd., Dec., 1925.

ROBINSON, TOM, late Indian Police (b. 29th Sept., 1883).—Held non-gaz. appts. from 8th Oct., 1909, to 22nd July, 1922; apptd. to I.P.S., as asst. supt., July, 1922; offg. supt., Nov., 1925; confd., Feb., 1932; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

ROBINSON, THOMAS MIDDLETON, Indian State Ryis. (divl. mech. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 4th Feb., 1894).—Asst. carriage and wagon supt., junr. scale, 28th Feb., 1922; divl. mech. engr., senr. scale, Nov., 1936.

ROBINSON, WILLIAM DOUGLAS, Indian Police (b. 5th Nov., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 16th Nov., 1927.

ROBSON, HENRY WILLIAM CUMINE, C.I.E., O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 8th Dec., 1886).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1906; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, as asst. pol. agent, June, 1919; pol. agent, Chagai, April, 1921; second asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, April, 1923; sec. to resdt. at Hyderabad, March, 1925; O.B.E., June, 1929; administrator Bharatpur State, March, 1930; pol. agent, Bhopal, Nov., 1933, and again, Jan., 1936; offg. A.G.G. in C.I., Aug., 1935; ditto, Punjab States, Aprl., 1936; resdt. for E. States, Mar., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938.

ROBSON, HUBERT ALAN HIRST, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. late Burma Medl. Dept. (b. 17th April, 1885).—First commn., dated 29th Jan., 1910; services placed at disposal of govt. of Burma from 13th Dec., 1920; supt., mental hosp., Rangoon, Dec., 1920; ret'd., May, 1933.

ROBSON, WILLIAM RAYMOND, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 4th Oct., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1930; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

ROCH, ERNEST TWINING, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 24th May, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; offg. exec. engr., Dec., 1923; confd., Oct., 1924.

ROCHE, WILLIAM, C.I.E., B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 20th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service, 6th Sept., 1905, as temp. engr.; asst. engr., Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. supt. engr., Oct., 1926; confd. Jan., 1927; C.I.E. Jan., 1927; offg. ch. engr. and jt. secty. to govt., U.P., Apl., 1931; ch. engr., Bengal, Oct., 1931; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

RODGER, Sir ALEXANDER, Kt., O.B.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 11th Aug., 1875).—Joined the service, 29th Nov., 1898, and served in Burma as asst. consvr. of forests; dep. consvr., Oct., 1905; impl. forest economist, impl. research inst., March, 1911; instr., forest coll., Nov., 1911; services placed at disposal of Indian munitions board, in addition to own duties, from March, 1917, to Jan., 1920; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; consvr., July, 1921; pres., forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Jan., 1925; inspr. genl. of forests, Sept., 1926; Kt., Jan., 1930; ret'd., Feb., 1931; apptd. member, Forestry Commn., May, 1932.

RODGER, JOHN, O.B.E., M.C., M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (b. 19th Dec., 1891).—First commn. dated 30th July, 1920; in milty. employ to Jan., 1929; resdy. surg., Mewar, 7th Jan., 1929; civil surg., Sibi, Oct., 1932; offg. resdy. surg. and C.M.O., Baluchistan, Feb., 1933; and again, Sept., 1935; civil surgeon, Sibi and Loralai, in addn., Apl., 1934, and again, Oct., 1936; O.B.E., May, 1937; legation surg. and H.M. Charge d'Affairs, Nepal, June, 1938.

RODGER, ROBERT CRUDEN, M.C., D.C.M., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 7th June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 11th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., May, 1928; confd., Apl., 1935; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Poona, Apl., 1937, to Apl., 1938.

RODGERS, E. W. F., late Dept. of Industries and Labour, Civil Aviation Dir. (b. 21st Nov., 1880).—Apptd. senior supt., 1st Mar., 1931; asst. secty. to govt., Mar., 1932; adm'n. officer, Apl., 1936; ret'd., Oct., 1938.

RODWELL, ERNEST GRAVENOR, O.B.E., F.C.H., late Indian State Railways (b. 11th April, 1881).—Joined the public wks. dep. as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1903; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; offg. engr.-in-chief, Nushki exten. rly., April to June, 1919; offg. dep. agent, O. & R. Ry., April, 1921; O.B.E., April, 1921; offg. dep. chief engr., O. & R. Ry., April, 1924; confd., Jan., 1929; offg. ch. engr., E.B. rly., June, 1932; ret'd., Apl., 1936.

ROE, HAROLD RILEY, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 15th Oct., 1883).—Joined the service, 16th Feb., 1904, and posted to United Prov.; asst. supt. of police, Sept., 1907; service under govt. of India, foreign dept., Oct., 1909, to Feb., 1911; asst. supt., Benares, July, 1912; supt., ditto, June, 1916; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1930; confd., Dec., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; ret'd., June, 1935.

ROGERS, ERIC MAXFIELD, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 2nd Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 25th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., April, 1926; confd., Jan., 1934; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Oct., 1936.

ROGERS, Rev. ERIC WITHAM, Indian Eccles. Estabt., United Provs. (junr. chaplain) (b. 17th Apl., 1898).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 31st Mar., 1935; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1937.

ROGERS, FREDERICK COLIN, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 1st Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1902; D.M.O., and medl. officer, cent. jail, Trichinopoly, Dec., 1923; ret'd., Mar., 1931.

ROGERS, Rev. GEORGE THOMAS, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 18th Nov., 1899).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bengal; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1935.

ROGERS, KENNETH DAVID, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 22nd Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Roundhay Sch., Leeds, and Leeds Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.
