SIMPSON, ALEXANDER ALLEN, M.A., late Indian Educ. Service (United Provs.) (b. 2nd Feb., 1881).—Joined the service, 10th March, 1915; head master, govt. high school, Cawnpore, Jan., 1916; prinpl., training coll., Allahabad, Oct., 1925; retd., Dec., 1932.

SIMPSON, EDWARD SKINNER, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 31st Oct., 1897).—Joined the service, 3rd Sept., 1923; arrived in India, 2nd Nov., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1928; confid., Nov., 1932; regr., high ct., Calcutta, appellate side, Mar., 1935.

SIMPSON, FRANK DOUGLAS, M.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 13th Oct., 1870).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Dec., 1901; addl. judge, Nov., 1908; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1911; offg. addl. judl. commr., April, 1922; dist. judge, June, 1927; retd., Dec., 1929.

SIMPSON, JOHN ALEXANDER, M.A., India Office (jt. secy., mil. dept.) (b. 25th Nov., 1892).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and Aberdeen University; apptd. junr. clerk, 15th Sept., 1919, and attid. to pol. dept.; resdt. clerk, April, 1920, to March, 1921; served

with 14th Bn. Co. of London Regt. (London Scottish), Sept., 1915, to Feb., 1917, 4th Bn. Gordon Hlrs., March to Aug., 1917, and 124th Baluchistan Infy., Sept., 1917, to Aug., 1919; principal, April, 1921; dep. clerk of council, June, 1931; actg. joint sec., mil. dept., Jan.-July, 1932; and again, Sept., 1934; confid., Apr., 1937.

SIMPSON, SIR JOHN HOPE, K.B.E., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at foreign schs. and at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 30th Oct., 1889; and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1895; asst. commr., July, 1898; dep. commr., Dec., 1901; joint sec., board of revenue, Jan., 1903; regr., co-operative credit societies, Nov., 1904, to June, 1907; C.I.E., Jan., 1913; offg. supt., Port Blair, and chief commr. of the Andamans and Nicobar Islands, April to Oct., 1914, and from May, 1916; retd., Dec., 1916; M.P. for Taunton Divn., Nov., 1922, to Oct., 1924; chairman, Colonies Cttee., 1924; Kt., June, 1925; K.B.E., Feb., 1937.

SIMPSON, SIR MAURICE GEORGE, KT., C.S.I., M.I.E.E., late India Office and India Tel. Dept. (b. 3rd Nov., 1866).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1887; supt., Feb., 1901; electrical engr.-in-chief, April, 1910; retd. from India Tel. Dept., Dec., 1914; apptd. asst. to dir.-in-chief, Indo-European telegraphs, Jan., 1915; dir.-in-chief, ditto, July, 1923, to Mar., 1931; C.S.I., June, 1928; Kt., Jan., 1931.

SIMPSON, PATRICK, late Survey of India (b. 12th Feb., 1881).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1901; extra asst. supt., Oct., 1916; supt., Sept., 1932; retd., Feb., 1936.

SIMPSON, REGINALD HUGH, Indian Police (b. 14th Aug., 1912).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt. of police, Bombay, Nov., 1933.

SIMPSON, ROBERT ROWELL, C.I.E., M.Sc. (Durham), late Dept. of Mines in India (b. 27th Aug., 1876).—Joined the service, 13th Nov., 1901; inspr. of mines, May, 1906; offg. chief inspr., May, 1920; confid., Jan., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1930; retd., Oct., 1932.

SIMPSON, SYDNEY, Indian State Rlys. (ch. elec. engr., N.W. rly.) (b. 3rd May, 1885).—Joined the service, 24th March, 1927; offg. senr. elec. engr., N.W. rly., Mar., 1931; dep. ch. mechl. engr. (elec.), E.B. rly., Nov., 1931; offg. ch. elec. engr., N.W. rly., May, 1935; confid., Nov., 1936.

SIMPSON, WILFRID JAMES, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 4th Feb., 1885).—First comm., dated 1st Feb., 1908; agency surg., Gilgit, Oct., 1919; ditto, Hazara, March, 1923; ditto, Bhopal, March, 1925; again, Sept., 1929; resdy. surg. and C.M.O. in C.I., June, 1929; agency surg., Bhopal, Sept., 1929; resy. surg., Mewar; offg. resdt., Mewar, in addn., Aug., 1934; retd., May, 1935.

SIMS, ARTHUR MITFORD, B.Sc., Indian State Rys. (dep. ch. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 31st Mar., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1911; on mil. duty from 30th March, 1916, to 8th Nov., 1918; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. dep. ch. engr. (bridges), July, 1934; offg. dep. agent, N.W. rly., Apr., 1936; dep. chief engr. (prov. perm.), Apr., 1938.

SIMS, EDWARD ALFRED, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. rly. (b. 3rd March, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 3rd Nov., 1914; dist. traffic supt., Dec., 1922; member and secty., rly. rates advisory ctee., May, 1933; offg. traffic manager, Burma rlys., June, 1937.

SIMS, FREDERICK, Public Works Dept., Bihar (b. 4th Feb., 1894).—Joined the service as electrical engr., 17th Oct., 1927.

SINCLAIR, MALCOLM CECIL, O.B.E., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 25th Sept., 1899).—First commn., dated 1st Oct., 1918; in milty. employ to 7th Sept., 1920; asst. resident, Aden, Oct., 1924; 2nd asst. to resident in Kashmir, April, 1927; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, Mar., 1930; asst. commr., Manshera, Apl., 1931, dep. commr., Hazara, Apl., 1932; dep. commr., Bannu, Sept., 1932; civil secty. to ch. commr., Aden, Sept., 1935; civ. secty., dist. mag. and p'port. offcr., Aden, Apl., 1937, again, Oct., 1937; O.B.E., June, 1938.

SINCLAIR, SAMUEL CHRISTINA, O.B.E., B.A., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 2nd Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as supt., 1st May, 1907; in milty. employ from 20th Oct., 1914, to 13th June, 1922; O.B.E., Jan., 1923; asst. dir. genl., post office, Mar., 1929; dep. P.M.G., Madras, Mar., 1932; ditto, Bengal and Assam, July, 1932; p.m.g., Aug., 1936; retd., Dec., 1937.

SINCLAIR, WILLIAM, late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 31st May, 1874).—Joined the service, 4th April, 1895; in mil. employ from 25th Sept., 1918, to 21st March, 1919; asst. engr., March, 1919; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; offg. exec. engr., Dec., 1925; retd., May, 1929.

SINGH, AJIT (see Ajit Singh).

SINGH, BADAN (see Badan S.).

SINGH, BAKSHI (see Bakshi S.).

SINGH, BALWANT SHIHN (see Balwant Singh Shihn.).

SINGH, BANAPHAR JANAKIRAM (see Janakiram Singh, B.).

SINGH, BAWA JEWAN (see Bawa Jewan Singh).

SINGH, BAWA KARTAR (see Bawa Kartar Singh).

SINGH, BAWA RAMNICK (see Bawa Ramnick Singh).

SINGH, BHAI LEHNA (see Lehna Singh Bhai).

SINGH, BRAJ BHUSHAN (see Braj Bruhan, S.).

SINGH, BUDH BAWA (see Budh S. B.).

SINGH, BUDHI (see Budhi, S.).

SINGH, DALIP (see Dalip Singh).

SINGH, DALJIT (see Daljit Singh).

SINGH, DARSHAN (see Darshan Singh).

SINGH, DEVI (see Devi Singh).

SINGH, GURBACHAN (see Gurbachan, Singh).

SINGH, GURBAKHSH (see Gurbakhsh, S.).

SINGH, GURDIAL SUNDER (see Gurdial, S. Singh).

SINGH, GURMUKH (see Gurmukh Singh).

SINGH, HARI (see Hari Singh).

SINGH, JAGDEO, GAREWAL S. (see Jagdeo, S. G. S.).

SINGH, JHUNNA (see Jhunna Singh).

SINGH, JIWAN AMRIK (see Jiwan, A. S.).

SINGH, KAPUR (see Kapur, S.).

SINGH, KARAN (see Karan Singh).

SINGH, KARTAR (see Kartar Singh).

SINGH, KHARAK (see Kharak Singh).

SINGH, KUNWAR BALBIR (see Balbir Singh, Kunwar).

SINGH, KUNWAR SHAMSHER (see Shamsher Singh, Kunwar).

SINGH, LAIQ (see Laiq Singh).

SINGH, LAL RAM SHARAN (see Sharan Singh, Lal Ram).

SINGH, LALLAN PRASAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 1st July, 1912).—Educ. at Queen's Intermediate College, Benares, Allahabad University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

SINGH, MAHARAJ KUNWAR (see Mahara Kunwar Singh).

SINGH, MALIK ANUP, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bengal (insp. genl.) (b. 22nd Sept., 1891).—First commn., 1st Nov., 1920; supt., central jail, Midnapore, Oct., 1929; ditto, presdy. jail, Jan., 1931; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Bengal, Apl., 1934; and again, Nov., 1936; confd., May, 1937.

SINGH, MAN (see Man Singh).

SINGH, MOHAN (see Mohan Singh).

SINGH, NATHA (see Natha Singh).

SINGH, NAWA (see Nawab Singh).

SINGH, NEHPAL (see Nehpal Singh).

SINGH, PARTAB (see Partab Singh).

SINGH, PRABH (see Prabh Singh).

SINGH, PRITAM, BRAR (see Pritam Singh, B.).

SINGH, RACHPAL (see Rachpal S.).

SINGH, RAGHBIR (see Raghbir Singh).

SINGH, RAJ NARAYAN (see Raj Narayan S.).

SINGH, RAM (see Ram Singh).

SINGH, RIPDAMAN (see Ripdaman, S.).

SINGH, SANT PRAKASH (see Sant Prakash, S.).

SINGH, SANT (see Sant, Singh).

SINGH, SARDAR (see Sardar Singh).

SINGH, SAR P (see Sarup Singh).

SINGH, SEWA RAM (see Sewa Ram S.).

SINGH, SHAM (see Sham Singh).

SINGH, SHEO ADHAR (see Sheo Adhar Singh).

SINGH SITAL (see Sital Singh).

SINGH, SUCHA, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 3rd April, 1903).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr.; jt. mag., Mar., 1930; exec. officer, improvement trust, Cawnpore, May, 1936.

SINGH, SUNDAR (see Sundar Singh).

SINGH, TARA (see Tara Singh).

SINGH, TARLOK (see Tarlok Singh).

SINGH, TEJA (see Teja, Singh).

SINGH, TRIBHUBAN PRASAD (see Tribhuban Prasad Singh).

SINGHA, JAI RAJ (see Jai Raj Singha).

SINGHAM, JOSEPH ROBERT THURAI, A.K.C., Madras P.W. Dept. (b. 5th Mar., 1877).—Joined the service, 13th Aug., 1907; dep. sanitary engr., 5th March, 1922; offg. sany. engr., Mar., 1929.

SINGLE, HERBERT STANLEY, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 6th Mar., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 22nd Dec., 1921; lecturer in forestry, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, May, 1924; dep. consvr., Oct., 1925; retd., May, 1933.

SINGLETON, PATRICK WILLIAM, Impl. Customs Service (collr.) (b. 1st Feb., 1884).—Joined the service, 5th April, 1905, as asst. opium agent (proby.); asst. opium agent, Nov., 1907; asst. collr. of customs, July, 1912; offg. collr., Rangoon, Jan., 1935; confd., July, 1937.

SINHA, ALAKH KUMAR, O.B.E., M.A., Indian Police (suppt., Bihar) (b. 29th June, 1884).—Joined the service, 21st Feb., 1908; asst. suppt., Jan., 1920; suppt., Apl., 1924; O.B.E., June, 1926; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1936; awarded King's police medal, Feb., 1937.

SINHA, AVADH KISHOR PRASHAD, Rai Bahadur, Indian Police (suppt., Bihar) (b. 16th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service, 2nd Jan., 1921; promoted to I.P. as suppt., Feb., 1935.

SINHA, BABU NIRSU NARAYAN, M.A., B.L. (member, govr.'s exec. council, Bihar).—Apptd. presdt., Bihar and Orissa legisl. council, 21st Jan., 1931; member, govr.'s exec. council, Bihar and Orissa, Jan., 1934; actg. vice-presdt., ditto, Oct., 1934.

SINHA, HARNANDAN PRASAD, Indian Serv. of Engrs. (b. 3rd Jan., 1906).—Apptd. as asst. exec. engr., United Provs., Jan., 1929; transfed. to the central public works dept., 1932.

SINHA, KALI PRASHAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 1st March, 1897).—Educ. at Calcutta University and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Feb., 1924; arrived, 1st Feb., 1924, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1930; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1933; magte. and collr. (confd.), 1935.

SINHA, MADAN MOHAN (see Madan M. S.).

SINHA, MITHILESH KUMAR, B.A., Indian Police (b. 30th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. suppt., Bihar and Orissa, 23rd April, 1928.

SINHA, RAMA AKHANDALESHVAR PRASHAD, Indian Police (b. 29th Jan., 1913).—Apptd. asst. suppt., Bihar, on probn., 27th Jan., 1936.

SINHA, RAMANI MOHAN, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 19th Jan., 1886).—Joined the service, 25th Jan., 1910, and held non-gazetted appt. prior to 1st Feb., 1920; asst. engr., Feb., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1928; exec. engr., Jan., 1933.

SINHA, ROMANI MOHAN, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr. (signals)) (b. 22nd Mar., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. signal engr., 1st Nov., 1919; dist. signal engr., E.I. Rly., Mar., 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr. (signals), E.I. Rly., Nov., 1934; confd., Feb., 1935.

SINHA, SUDHIR KUMAR, Indian Police (b. Jan., 1910).—Apptd. proby. asst. suppt., Bengal, 1st Mar., 1932, confd.; Dec., 1935.

SINHA, Honble. SUSHIL KUMAR, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 9th June, 1895).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1919; arrived, 19th Jan., 1920, and posted to Bengal; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1924; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1927; confd., June, 1931; offg. ch. pres. mag., Calcutta, June, 1931; confd., Sept., 1933; retd., Apl., 1937.

SINHA, Hon. TARU, B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 9th Feb., 1899).—Joined the service. as officer in cl. II, Oct., 1922.

SINTON, John ALEXANDER, V.C., O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (dir., malaria survey of India, Kasauli) (b. 2nd Dec., 1884).—First commn., 29th July, 1911; in milty. employ until 8th Feb., 1921, when apprd. bacteriological officer, Pasteur inst., Kasauli; dir. malaria survey of India, Apl., 1932.

SIRAJUDDIN AHMAD, B.Sc., I.L.B., Indian Police (b. 1st Nov., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. suppt., United Provs., 29th June, 1925; offg. suppt., May, 1929, and again, Mar., 1933.

SIRAJ-UD-DIN, KHAN BAHADUR, Shaikh late Punjab Civil Service (b. 9th Sept., 1874).—Joined the service as extra asst. commr., 21st Jan., 1900; dep. commr., April, 1926; retd., Sept., 1929.

SIRCAR, Sir NRIPENDRA NATH, K.C.S.I., Barr., Bengal Judicial Dept. (member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council).—Apptd. advocate-genl., Bengal, Dec., 1928; Kt., June, 1931; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, May, 1934; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1936.

SITAL PRASAD, B.Sc., Inc. tax Dept. (asst. commr.) (b. 7th May, 1892).—Apptd. asst. commr., United Provs., June, 1934.

SITAL SINGH, M.A., Sardar Bahadur, Indian Finance Dept. (b. 10th Mar., 1886).—Joined the service, 21st Nov., 1910, and held non-gazetted appt.; offg. suppt., Jan., 1918; revereted to non-gazetted appt. from 2nd Mar., 1918; offg. suppt., Jan., 1920; confd., Mar., 1922; asst. sec., Oct., 1922; offg. under secty., Apl., 1929; dep. acct.-genl., central revs., Dec., 1932; ditto, Punjab (works), Apl., 1936.

SITZLER, EDWARD ALBERT—Name changed by Deed Poll to SEATON, G.V.

SITZLER, HENRY FERDINAND, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 4th March, 1883).—Educ. at Malvern, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., May, 1918; offg. excise commr., June, 1925; offg. secty. to govt., judl. dept., Dec., 1928; retd., June, 1930; ditto Punjab, (Works) April, 1936.

SIVARAMA KRISHNAN, RAMANATH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 19th Oct., 1899).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1924; arrived 8th Dec., 1924, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. magte., July, 1927; sub-collr., Jan., 1928; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1937; confd., Nov., 1937.

SIVARAMAN, BALARAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and acp. collr., Orissa) (b. 10th Jan., 1912).—Educ. at Christian College School, Madras, University of Madras and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; posted to Orissa, Aug., 1937.

SIVA RAMAN, MANAKKAL SUNDARALINGAM Iyer, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 9th May, 1907).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and London Sch. of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in

India, Nov., 1930, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Sept., 1932; spec. settlement officr., Apl., 1937; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Aug., 1937.

SIVASANKAR, TUMKAR, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 14th Oct., 1902).—Educ. at University of Mysore, Victoria Univ., Manchester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. magte., July, 1929; offg. dep. commr. of excise, Madras, Apl., 1934; secty., cent. road traffic bd., Apl., 1938.

SIVASANKARA MENON, KUMAR PADMA, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 18th Oct., 1898).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to resdt., Hyderabad, Feb., 1925; under-sec. to C.C. and A.G.G. in N.W.F.P., May, 1927; dist. judge, Peshawar, Apl., 1929; agent to the govt. of India in Ceylon, Oct., 1929; dist. and sess. judge, Peshawar, Aug., 1934; offg. addl. dep. secty. to the govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, June, 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Sept., 1935; addl. dep. secty. to the govt. of India for. and pol. dept., Apl., 1936; ditto, external affairs dept., Apl., 1937; relief commr. for Baluchistan in addn., Apl., 1936; pol. agent, Zhob, Nov., 1937.

SIVASWAMI AIYAR, Sir PAZHAMANHERI SUNDARAM AIYAR, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., B.L.—Vakil, High Court of Judicature, Madras, 9th Oct., 1907; advocate-gen., Madras, Jan., 1908; C.I.E., Jan., 1908; member of exec. council, Madras, Feb., 1912, to Feb., 1917; C.S.I., June, 1912; K.C.S.I., June, 1915; vice-chancellor of Madras Univ., 1916-18; vice-chancellor of Hindu Univ., Benares, 1918-19; elected member of Indian legisl. assembly, 1920.

SKEMP, FRANK WHITTINGHAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Lahore) (b. 13th Dec., 1880).—Educ. at Owens Coll., Manchester; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 10th Dec., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1923; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, March, 1927; actg. ditto, April, 1927; addl. ditto, from Nov., 1928; confd., Oct., 1933.

SKINNER, ARTHUR FREDERICK, Office of High Commr. for India (higher executive officer, store dept.) (b. 28th May, 1887).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 22nd Sept., 1906; on mil. service from Dec., 1916, to Feb., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; actg. higher exec. officer, June, 1929; confd., Nov., 1932.

SKINNER, JAMES MACGREGOR, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret. (b. 14th Apl., 1880).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1906; surg., 1st Dist., Madras, and supt., Rayapuram medl. school, Feb., 1927; offg. 2nd physician, gen. hosp., Madras, Feb., 1926; confd., June, 1929; ret., Apl., 1935.

SKINNER, PHILIP ERNEST FREEMAN, late Indian C.S. (b. 6th Mar., 1904).—Educ. at Charterhouse and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1927; arrived,

12th Dec., 1927, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under secty. to govt., C.P., Oct., 1931; offg. dep. commr., May, 1933; ret., Mar., 1936.

SKRINE, CLARMONT PERCIVAL, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Feb., 1888).—Educ. at Winchester, New College, Oxford, and University College, London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 25th Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; temp. asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Sibi, Oct., 1915; H.B.M.'s vice-consul at Kerman, Oct., 1916; H.B.M.'s consul for Kerman and Persian Baluchistan, Dec., 1917; prob. pol. dept., Jan., 1918; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Jan., 1920; pol. agent, Quetta, April, 1921; H.M.'s consul-general, Kashgar, July, 1922; sec. to agent to govr.-gen., Punjab States, July, 1926; H.B.M.'s Consul for Sistan and Kain, April, 1927; pol. agent and dep. commr., Sibi, Mar., 1929; pol. agent, Kalat, Mar., 1932; and again, Dec., 1934; rev. and judl. commr. in Baluchistan, Aug., 1934; O.B.E., Nov., 1935; offg. resdt., Madras States, Nov., 1936.

SLADE, MEAD, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr. of customs) (b. 25th Jan., 1894).—Educ. at Ilminster Sch., and University Coll., London; on mil. service from Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1923; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; and asst. to commr. of settlmnt. and land records, May, 1925; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1927; confd., July, 1932; asst. collr., customs, Calcutta, April, 1928; offg. collr., ditto, May, 1930; ditto, Rangoon, Nov., 1932; ditto, Bombay, Feb., 1934; offg. jt. secty. to the govt. of India, dept. of commerce, June, 1936; temp. jt. secty., ditto, Mar., 1937; member, council of state, Mar., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938.

SLADEN, JOSEPH MAURICE, Indian C.S. (secty., home dept., Bombay) (b. 22nd Feb., 1896).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coli., Oxford; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1920; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1920, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. asst. pte. sec. to govr., May to Nov., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. magte., and pol. agent, March, 1925; asst. commr. in Sind and Sindhi translator to govt., March, 1928; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; minister, Khairpur state, May, 1932; collr., Mar., 1936; secty., home dept., Bombay, Mar., 1938.

SLADEN, REGINALD JOHN LAMBART, F.R.C.S., D.P.H., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 24th July, 1877).—Joined the service as dist. surg., G.I.P. Rly., 2nd May, 1902; offg. principal medl. and health officer, May, 1923, and again, Aug., 1925; services transferred to E.B. Rly., March, 1926; offg. ch. medl. officer, March, 1926; offg. prinpl. medl. and health officer, G.I.P. Rly., March, 1928; confd., July, 1929; ret., Mar., 1934.

SLATER, ALFRED, M.A., Mil. Accts. Dept. (b. 25th June, 1887).—Joined the service as mil. acct., 2nd cl., Dec., 1909; D.M.A.G. (junr.), Mar., 1923; ditto (senr.), Feb., 1924; and again, Mar., 1929; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Feb., 1925; secty., army retrenchment cte., Dec., 1931; confd., as cl. I officr., Dec., 1929; confd. as cmd. contr., Nov., 1934.

SLATER, ALFRED FRANK MURRAY, Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Indian Police Service (Burma) (b. 26th Sept., 1882).—First commn. dated 4th May, 1901; in mil. employ prior to Aug., 1909; asst. supt., Aug., 1909; dist. supt., Nov., 1911; on mil. service from 20th Dec., 1914, to 15th March, 1916, and from 12th Nov., 1918, to 12th April, 1919; dep. inspr.-gen., Jan., 1924; ret'd., June, 1931.

SLATER, OWEN, M.C., Lt.-Col. R.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 19th June, 1890).—First commn. dated 23rd July, 1910; asst. supt. (proby), 20th Apl., 1920; confd., Oct., 1922; supt., May, 1928.

SLATER, SAMUEL HENRY, C.M.G., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Jan., 1880).—Educ. at King Edward VII Gr. Sch., Louth, and Oxford Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to govt., local and munpl. dept., May, 1910; ditto, pol. dept., May, 1910; under-sec. to govt. of India, dept. of comm. and ind., April, 1913; on special duty to S. Africa under comm. and ind. dept., Dec., 1913, to April, 1914; temp. employed in rev. and stat. dept., India Office, from May to Oct., 1916; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1916; temp. dep. sec., comm. and ind. dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1916; spl. duty, comm. and industry dept., Dec., 1916, to June, 1917; on mil. duty from Feb., 1918, to March, 1919; finl. sec., Mesopotamian administration, March, 1919, to May, 1922; C.I.E., June, 1919; on foreign service under Iraq civil administration, Sept., 1922, to Feb., 1925; C.M.G., June, 1923; collr. and dist. mag., March, 1925; offg. sec. to govt., pub. wks. dept., May, 1926; commr. of labour, July, 1927; collr. and dist. magte., Apl., 1930; secty. to govt., Madras, dev. dept., Feb., 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

SLATTERY, JOHN, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 26th June, 1886).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1906; asst. supt., Nov., 1907; supt., Sept., 1914; asst. inspr.-gen., rly. police, Jan., 1924; O.B.E., June, 1927; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Mar., 1928; offcr. on spl. duty, central bd. of rev., May, 1929; dep. inspr.-genl., Punjab (prov. subs.), July, 1932; confd., June, 1933; offg. inspr.-genl., N.W.F.P., June, 1933; ret'd., May, 1937; chairman, pub. serv. commn., Punjab and N.W.F. Prov., May, 1937.

SLEEM, M. Mr., Barr. (advocate-general, Punjab) (b. 14th Jan., 1892).—Apptd. advocate-general, 11th Feb., 1938.

SLESSER, WILLIAM DUNCAN VIVIAN, M.A., Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 28th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 18th Dec., 1908; asst. supt., Oct., 1909; on mil. duty from 24th Aug., 1916, to 24th March, 1919; supt., March, 1919.

SLOAN, TENNANT, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (b. 9th Nov., 1884).—Educ. at Glasgow Academy and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 25th Nov., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; under-sec. to govt. Jan., 1915; asst. supt., Dehra Dun. April., 1917; city mag., Lucknow, July, 1917; temp. addl. under-sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Sept., 1917; spl. duty, home dept., Nov., 1917; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, home dept., May, 1918; on deputn.

to England, May to Oct., 1919; dep. sec. to govt., United Provs., March, 1921; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., March, 1924; jt. sec., ditto, April, 1925; secty. to govt., rev. and public wks. dept. (B. and R.), U.P., March, 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, Home dept., May, 1932; jt. secty. to the Govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1933; offg. secty., ditto, June, 1935; C.S.I., Jan., 1936.

SLOCOMBE, CHARLES, late India Office (b. 16th Sept., 1872).—Clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-genl.'s dept., 22nd July, 1892; transfd. to Indo-European Telegraph Dept., June, 1900; staff clerk, May, 1917; clerk for special duties, March, 1920; re-transferred to acct.-genl.'s dept., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

SLOSS, DUNCAN JOHN, C.B.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 20th June, 1881).—Joined the service, 10th June, 1920; prof., Oct., 1920; princpl., University Coll., Rangoon, June, 1925; C.B.E., June, 1925; ret'd., Dec., 1936.

SMALL, GERALD AGNEW, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instrn., Assam) (b. 31st Aug., 1886).—Joined the service, 21st April, 1911; inspr. of schools, July, 1911; on mil. service from April, 1916, to May, 1920; offg. dir. public instrn., June, 1928; confd., Oct., 1931.

SMART, COLIN N. B., Indian State Railways (exec. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 18th Feb., 1885).—Joined the service, 7th Jan., 1921; resdt. engr., G.I.P. rly., Jan., 1926; statistical officer, Mar., 1932.

SMART, ROBERT BROUGHTON, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 9th Apl., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 17th Jan., 1930; confd., Jan., 1932.

SMART, WILFRED WILMOT, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Jan., 1876).—Educ. at Dulwich Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd after exam. of 1899; arrived, 16th Nov., 1900, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. offr., settlmt. offr., Baluchistan, Oct., 1905, to April, 1907; collr. and dist. mag., Feb., 1920; actg. commr. in Sind and controller of prices in Sind, Sept., 1920; supt. of stamps, Karachi, Feb., 1921; offg. commr., April, 1928; confd., Jan., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1935; ret'd., Nov., 1935.

SMELLIE, BENJAMIN ALEXANDER, B.A., LL.B., Central Provs. C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 26th Mar., 1890).—Joined the service as extra asst. commr. on probn., 18th May, 1914; confd., July, 1915; ditto and under-sec. to Govt., May, 1928; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1930; confd., Feb., 1933.

SMELLIE, JOHN HUGH, D.S.O., O.B.E., late Indian State Railways (N.W. Rly.) (b. 15th Feb., 1874).—Joined the service, 27th Sept., 1907, as dist. loco. supt., after having previously served on the Kalka Simla Railway; on mil. duty with Indian expedy force 'B' from Oct. to Dec., 1914, and from Nov., 1915, to Sept., 1918; lcco. supt. (offg.), Nov., 1919; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; chief mechanical engr., Sept., 1925; ret'd., Feb., 1929.

SMITH, ARCHIBALD CLIFFORD, Indian Police (princpl., p.t. school, Burma) (b. 9th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 7th Feb., 1921; dist. supt., June, 1924; awarded King's police medal, Feb., 1937 princpl., p.t. school, Dec., 1936.

SMITH, BERTRAM GILBERT, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 4th Apl., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 18th May, 1908; dist. traffic supt., Sept., 1917; on mil. service from 28th Feb., 1916, to 9th Oct., 1919; O.B.E., 3rd June, 1918; officer on sp. duty, Rly. Board, July, 1925; temp. dep. agent, E.I. Rly., Feb., 1926; offg. divl. supt., Mar., 1928; confd., Apl., 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1938.

SMITH, CECIL CURTIS HAROLD, Major, I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 22nd Aug., 1898).—First commn. dated 26th Nov., 1915; in milty. employ up to 5th April, 1925; asst. pol. agent, Chilas, July, 1926; asst. commr., Thal, Sept., 1930; asst. commr., Thal sub-divn., Apl., 1931; dep. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1932; pol. agent, Kurrum, June, 1935; dep. commr., Peshawar, Mar., 1938.

SMITH, CHARLES HAROLD, O.B.E., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 19th June, 1883).—First commn., 31st July, 1909; O.B.E., June, 1919; offg. agency surg. on deputation to Rewa State as supt. medl. dept., from 18th Apl., 1922; agency surg., Bundelkhand, Jan., 1928; legation surg., Nepal, Apl., 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1938.

SMITH, DANIEL RICHARD, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 18th Jan., 1897).—First commn. dated 4th June, 1915; in milty. employ prior to May, 1923; asst. pol. agent, Bahrein, June, 1924; vice-consul, Duzdap, Nov., 1924; asst. for Mekran to pol. agent in Kalat, Feb., 1927; pol. agent, Chagai, May, 1929; British trade agent at Gyanthe and asst. to pol. offr. in Sikkim, Sept., 1929; under-secy. to agent to govr. genl., Rajputana, March, 1930; officer on special duty, Mirpir, June, 1932; pte. secty. to Maharajah of Rewa, Oct., 1932; pol. agent, Harasti and Tonk, Oct., 1933; asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Mar., 1934; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha Agency, Oct., 1937; pol. agent and dep. commr., Sibi, Nov., 1938.

SMITH, EDWIN CHARLES ALLAN, L.R.F.P.S., L.R.C.P. & S., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Bombay) (b. 16th Nov., 1887).—First commn., 1st July, 1921; offg. asst. dir. of public health, Rangoon, Aug., 1926; offg. supt., central mental hosp., Yeravda, Nov., 1928; confd., Aug., 1929.

SMITH, EDWIN DENSHAM, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 25th Nov., 1906; supt., June, 1920; offg. asst. inspr. genl., rly. police, June, 1931.

SMITH, EGERTON, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 7th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 25th Jan., 1910; prof., Dacca Coll., Jan., 1910; prin., Krishnagar Coll. Oct., 1923; ret'd., July, 1930.

SMITH, ERIC CONRAN, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 3rd Dec., 1890).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Corpus Christi, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1915, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; temp. empd. in judl. and public dept., India Office, from April, to June, 1919; pte. sec. to govr. of Madras, April, 1921; C.I.E., June, 1924; on foreign service as commr. for Corp'n. of Madras, Oct., 1928; secty., local self-govt. dept., Madras, Oct., 1931; collr. (prov'l. subs.), Feb., 1933; addl. jt. secty.

to govt. of India in the reforms office, Oct., 1934; collr., Dec., 1934; offg. pte. secty. to Viceroi, June, 1935; member, council of state, Apl., to May, 1936; jt. secty., govt. of India, reforms office, Mar., 1937.

SMITH, FRANCIS HERBERT, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 31st July, 1876).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 25th March, 1910; junr. chaplain, March, 1913, senr. chaplain, March, 1923; ret'd., June, 1930.

SMITH, FRANCIS WILLIAM HEAD, C.I.E., India Office (sec., services and general dept.) (b. 18th Aug., 1886).—Educ. at Tonbridge, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. junior clerk, India Office, 2nd Nov., 1909, and attached to fin. dept.; resdt. clerk, Dec., 1911, to Dec., 1912; asst. sec. to Rl. Commn. on Indian fin. and currency, May, 1913, to Feb., 1914; reading clerk to council, July, 1913, to April, 1914; transf. to public works dept., April, 1914; senr. clerk, May, 1917; services temporarily lent to military dept., Dec., 1914; actg. asst. sec., public works dept., Jan., 1920; confd., March, 1921; actg. joint sec., military dept., May, 1923; dep. clerk of the Council, April, 1924; sec. to Rl. Commn. on Agric. in India, 1926-28; actg. secty., services and general dept., 1st Dec., 1928; confd., June, 1929; C.I.E., March, 1929.

SMITH, FRANK LOVELL, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Perse School, Cambridge, and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. supt. of census operns., U.P., Mar., 1931; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935.

SMITH, FREDERICK WILLIAM JAMES, Office of High Commr. for India (executive officer, store dept.) (b. 25th Dec., 1893).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Store Depot, 6th Aug., 1914; on mil. service from Jan., 1916, to Feb., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922.

SMITH, G. CORLEY, M.B.E., Commerce Dept., Govt. of India (asst. secty.) (b. 12th June, 1895).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1913; supt., commerce dept., Nov., 1928; offg. asst. secty., commerce dept., Sept., 1930; confd., June, 1932; offg. admin. officer, central p.w. d., Apl., 1933; and again, Apl., 1935; asst. secty., comm. dept., Nov., 1935.

SMITH, GEOFFREY WILLIAM JOHN, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 30th March, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Assam Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; asst. to dep. commr., Naga Hills, Sept., 1937.

SMITH, Ven. GEORGE CECIL AUGUSTUS, M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estab. (b. 15th Dec., 1876).—Joined the service as chaplain, 26th Dec., 1905; senr. chaplain, 26th Dec., 1918; archdeacon, Feb., 1928; ret'd., July, 1931.

SMITH, GEORGE McIVER CAMPBELL, C.M.G., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 13th May, 1869).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1892; apptd. med. adviser, Patiala State, June, 1903; offg. civil surg'n., Punjab, Feb., 1904; confd., June, 1909; temp. reverted to mil. duty, Jan., 1916; C.M.G., June, 1919; ret'd., Sept., 1921; addl. member, medl. bd., India Office, Dec., 1928; member, ditto, Jan., 1931 to May, 1934.

SMITH, HARRY PERCIVAL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Assam) (b. 26th May, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 22nd March, 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1923; on deputation to Andamans from Jan., 1926, to May, 1929; p.a. to consvr. of forests, Assam, Oct., 1935.

SMITH, Sir HENRY MONCRIEFF, Kt., C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd Dec., 1873).—Educ. at Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., April, 1907; addl. sess. judge, July, 1908; sess. and sub-judge, June, 1913; offg. sec. to govt., April, 1914; attaché, legislative dept., govt. of India, from Sept., 1915; dep. sec. to govt. of India, legislative dept., Oct., 1915; dist. and sess. judge, and ditto, Jan., 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; joint sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Jan., 1920; sec. to ditto, Jan., 1921; Kt., Jan., 1923; president, council of state, Dec., 1924, to Dec., 1932; retd., Dec., 1932.

SMITH, Sir HENRY SCOTT, Kt., Indian C.S. (rettd.).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Stratford-on-Avon, and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 4th Dec., 1886, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dist. judge, June, 1894; divl. judge, March, 1902; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1913; temp. addl. judge, chief ct., Punjab, Jan., 1914; judge, do., April, 1915; judge, high ct., Punjab, April, 1919; Kt., June, 1924; actg. chief justice, high ct., Punjab, June, 1924; retd., May, 1925.

SMITH, HENRY WRAY SANDES, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Burma) (b. 13th Apl., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Sept., 1926; confd., July, 1929.

SMITH, HERBERT CECIL, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 27th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 21st Dec., 1915; dep. consvr., Jan., 1922; offg. consvr., Feb., 1936.

SMITH, HERBERT ST. CLAIR, M.B.E., late Bombay C.S. (b. 9th June, 1880).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 5th Sept., 1898, to 22nd Aug., 1908; dep. collr., Aug., 1908; offg. dep. sec. to govt., dev. dept., etc., Jan., 1922; offg. dep. sec. to govt., dev. dept., May, 1925; retd., June, 1935; M.B.E., Jan., 1936.

SMITH, IAN WILLIAM KNIGHTON, B.A., B.E., Indian State Rlys. (ch. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 13th Oct., 1882).—Joined the service as junr. engr., 4th Sept., 1909; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; resdit. engr., Dec., 1923; offg. supt., way and works, May, 1925; offg. dep. ch. engr., March, 1927; dep. ch. engr. (provl.), July, 1929; confd., Nov., 1931; offg. ch. engr., June, 1935; confd., August., 1935.

SMITH, Sir JAMES COWLISHAW, Kt., C.I.E., B.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 14th April, 1873).—Educ. at Derby, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896; and served in the N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. commr., asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settmt. officer; joint mag., Nov., 1905; dep. commr., Dec., 1913; mag. and collr., Nov., 1914; C.I.E., June, 1920; ffg. commr., June, 1922; confd.,

Dec., 1924; offg. member, bd. of rev., Dec., 1928; confd. July, 1930; temp. member, govr's exec. council, United Provs., Oct., 1930, and again, July, 1931; retd., Dec., 1931; Kt., Jan., 1932.

SMITH, JOHN EDWARD, Indian Police (b. 14th Oct., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 14th Dec., 1927; offg. supt., May, 1934.

SMITH, Sir JOSEPH BENJAMIN GEORGE, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 14th Feb., 1878).—Joined the service, 18th Aug., 1898; asst. engr. (provl. service), Aug., 1899; exec. engr., July, 1910; suptg. engr., Nov., 1920; chief engr. and sec. to govt., P.W.D., irrigation branch, March, 1926; C.I.E., June, 1927; Kt., June, 1932; retd., Feb., 1933.

SMITH, LESLIE EVAN, late Indian Forest Service (b. 28th July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Dec., 1929; retd., Feb., 1936.

SMITH, NORMAN PERCIVAL ARTHUR, Indian Police (dep. commr., Bombay) (b. 30th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service, Nov., 1912; supt. of police, June, 1924; offg. dep. dir., intel. bureau, June, 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1935; cent. intell. offcr., Bombay Presdy., Mar., 1936; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1938; dep. commr. police, Bombay, Aug., 1938.

SMITH, RALPH JEANRENAUD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 7th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1923, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.

SMITH, RICHARD HUGH, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 22nd July, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; retd., Jan., 1931.

SMITH, Rev. RICHARD MARMADUKE LANGDALE, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 29th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1915; junr. chaplain, Madras, 1st Apl., 1918; senr. chaplain, 1st Apl., 1928; retd., Sept., 1938.

SMITH, ROBERT GEORGE, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th July, 1907).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., United Provs., 8th Feb., 1931; confd., Feb., 1933.

SMITH, SAMUEL McLEOD, late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 16th Jan., 1879).—Joined the service, 5th Feb., 1903; dep. mag. and coll., Apl., 1907; diwan, Gangpur State, Apl., 1921; ditto, Mayurbhanj State, Jan., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1929; magte. and collr., Mar., 1931; retd. Jan., 1934.

SMITH, THOMAS WILLIAM, late Office of High Commr. for India.—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store depot, 23rd May, 1888; supervisr., June, 1914; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; prinpl. exec. officer, March, 1926; retd., Apl., 1929.

SMITH, VICTOR WILLIAM, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 6th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service, Nov., 1911; asst. supt., May, 1913; O.B.E., Jan., 1921; supt., Nov., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924; on spl. duty, Home Dept., from Nov., 1925; pers. asst. to genl., inspr.-Punjab, Nov., 1926; retd. Aug., 1933.

SMITH, WALTER ROBERT GEORGE, C.I.E., Indian Police (commr. of police, Bombay) (b. 5th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1908, as asst. supt.; supt. of police, Mar., 1921; dep. commr. of police, Bombay, Feb., 1932; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1932; offg. commr. of police, Bombay, July, 1933; confd., Apl., 1935; awarded King's Police medal, Jan., 1933; C.I.E., May, 1937.

SMITH, WILFRED ARTHUR, O.B.E., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 30th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 11th May, 1905; confd., Jan., 1907; on field postal service on N.W. frontier, May, 1919, to Aug., 1920; offg. dep. P.M.G., July, 1928; confd., Feb., 1929; dep. P.M.G., Madras, Apl., 1933; ret'd., July, 1937.

SMITH, WILLIAM ERLAM, M.A. (Oxon), Indian Educl. Service (profir. of chemistry, presdy. coll., Madras) (b. 16th July, 1881).—Apptd. acting prof. of natural sciences, Rangoon Coll., 3rd Sept., 1906; prof. of chemistry, presdy. coll., Madras, Oct., 1907; on mil. service, from Sept., 1915, to March, 1920; offg. dir. of public instrn., Madras, Jan., 1930, and again, Nov., 1932.

SMITH, WILLIAM FREDERICK, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 5th Aug., 1880).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 19th Jan., 1903; asst. engr., Jan., 1923; apptd. to the I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1927; exec. engr., Jan., 1932; ret'd., May, 1934.

SMITH, WILLIAM ROSE, B.Sc., Indian State Railways (exec. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 19th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 17th Sept., 1909; resdt. engr., G.I.P. rly., Mar., 1923; exec. engr., May, 1932.

SMITH-PEARSE, THOMAS LAWRENCE HOWARTH, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (principal, Rajkumar Coll., Raipur) (b. 15th July, 1893).—Educ. at Marlborough and St. John's Coll., Oxford; vice-principal. Rajkumar Coll., Raipur, Feb., 1921; prinpl. ditto July, 1931.

SMITHERMAN, FRANK, Indian Police (asst. supt., Burma) (b. 13th Oct., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Burma, Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

SMYTH, ARCHIBALD ADAMS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 12th June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Madras, Sept., 1925; offg. exec. engr., Aug., 1931; confd., Sept., 1934; awarded K.-i.-H. medal, cl. II, Jan., 1936.

SMYTH, DAVID ALEXANDER, C.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Central Provs.) (b. 24th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, Dec., 1907; asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1909; dist. supt., Feb., 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1925; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., June, 1926; confd., June, 1928; offg. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1933; and May, 1935; C.B.E., June, 1935.

SMYTH, JOHN WILLIAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A. Indian C.S. (commr., Poona) (b. 13th May, 1880).—Educ. at Hymer's Coll. Hull, and Clare Coll. Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived 25th Nov., 1904 and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; dep. commr., Upper Sind frontier July, 1911; asst. commr. Sind, and Sindh translator to govt., Sept., 1912; dep. commr.,

Upper Sind Frontier, July, 1916; offg. collr. and district mag., Oct., 1921; ditto and pol. agent, Feb., 1922; collr. and dist. mag., March, 1926; sec. to govt., rev. ept., Oct., 1926; member, legis. council, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ch. commr., Andaman & Nicobar Islands, Dec., 1931; commr., Poona, June, 1936; C.S.I., May, 1937.

SMYTH, SIR SAMUEL ANDREW, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 18th March, 1877).—Educ. at London-derry Sch., and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 10th Dec., 1901, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Nov., 1905, to Nov., 1908; dep. commr., Feb., 1915; on spl. duty in sectt.; offg. sec. to govt., Sept., 1917; offg. chief sec. to govt., Nov. to Dec., 1917; offg. commr., May, 1919; C.S.I., Jan., 1924; commr. (s.p.t.), Aug., 1923; confd., May, 1924; offg. finl. commr., March, 1925; confd., April, 1926; member, exec. council, Burma, May, 1928; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1931; ret'd., March, 1931; delegate to Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931.

SMYTH, SAMUEL, Major, I.M.S. (b. 10th June, 1899).—Date of first commn., 4th Aug., 1927; apptd. civil surgeon (offg.), Apl., 1934; O.S.D., Mayo Hosp., Lahore, Mar., 1937.

SMYTHIES, EVELYN ARTHUR, B.A., Indian Forest Service (chief consvr., United Provs.) (b. 19th Mar., 1885).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1908, as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Jan., 1914; offg. consr., April, 1926; confd., April, 1929; chief consvr., Dec., 1937.

SNADDEN, WILLIAM GEORGE, late Indian Police (b. 23rd Apl., 1889).—Apptd. dist. supt., Burma, 1st Dec., 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; ret'd., June, 1936.

SNELL, OF PLUMSTEAD, Baron, HENRY SNELL, C.B.E. Parly. under-secty. of State for India, Mar.-Aug., 1931; delegate to Indian round table conf., 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931.

SNELSON, EDWARD ALEC ABBOTT, Indian C.S. (asst. commr. Central Provinces) (b. 31st Oct., 1904).—Educ. at St. Olave's Grammar School, Caius Coll., Cambridge, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, 9th Nov., 1929, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; sub-judge, Dec., 1931.

SNOW, CHARLES ALFRED, M.A. (Oxon), late Indian Educational Service (Burma) (b. 31st July, 1879).—Arrived in India, 1st Feb., 1905; on mil. service, April, 1915, to May, 1919; asst. dir. of public instruction, May, 1921; dir. of public instrn., Aug., 1923; reid., July, 1931.

SOAMES, GEOFFREY EWART, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Jan., 1881).—Educ. at Eastbourne Coll., and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1905, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., fin. and munpl. dept., Nov., 1909; under-sec. to govt. of India, home dept., May to Nov., 1911; under-sec. to chief commr., Assam, April, 1912; asst. commr., Assam, Aug., 1915; dep. commr., Jan., 1921; second sec. to govt. of Assam, April, 1923; offg. chief sec. to govt. of Assam, Feb., 1925; confd., Oct., 1925; C.I.E., June, 1927; ch. secty. to govt. of Assam, Oct., 1931; ret'd., June, 1933.

SOARES, ALEXANDER DUARANTE, M.B., B.S., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Rlys. (dist. medl. officer, G.I.P. rly.) (b. 25th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service, 8th June, 1923; dist. medl. officer, G.I.P. rly., April, 1929.

SOBHAN, KONDKAR FAZLE (see Khondkar Fazle Sobhan).

SODHI, HARBHAJAN SINGH, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 24th May, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

SODHI, NARENDRA SINGH, M.C., L.R.C.P., D.T.M., Col., I.M.S. (inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Burma) (b. 9th Aug., 1882).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1905; in mil. employ till June, 1910, when he was apptd. asst. plague med. offr., Punjab; dist. plague med. offr., July, 1910; plague med. offr., June, 1911; on mil. duty, Sept., 1914, to July, 1918; civil surgeon, Punjab, 1921; dep. inspr. of civil hosp., Nov., 1925; offg. inspr.-genl., Punjab, Nov., 1933; ditto, Burma, April, 1935; confd., Aug., 1935.

SOE, MAUNG AUNG, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 27th Mar., 1906).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty., fin. dept., Burma, Apr., 1936.

SOHONI, VISHRANATH VISHNU, B.A., M.Sc. (Bombay), Meteorological Dept. (meteorologist) (b. 6th Sept., 1898).—Joined the service as meteorologist, 12th Mar., 1922.

SOKHEY, SAHIBSINGH, M.A., B.Sc., M.D. (Edin.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 15th Dec., 1887).—Joined, 26th July, 1913; in milty. employ to 7th Aug., 1923; apptd. supernumerary offr., Haffkine Inst., Bombay, Aug., 1925; offg. asst. dir., ditto, Feb., 1926; offg. dir., ditto, March, 1928; and again, Oct., 1932.

SOLOMAN, SAMUEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 20th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Clifton College, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and coll.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1933; offg. publicity officer, Bihar and Orissa, Mar., 1935.

SOLOMON, WILLIAM EWART GLADSTONE, late Indian Educl. Service (b. 24th Mar., 1880).—Apptd. princpl. and decorative artist and archtl. sculptor, Sir J. J. School of Art and Industries, Bombay, 25th Nov., 1919; designation changed to dir., ditto, Apr., 1929; 1st class K-i-H. medal, Jan., 1924; ret'd., Mar., 1937.

SOM NATH, KAPUR LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 10th Oct., 1908).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 17th July, 1931; apptd. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., July, 1932.

SONAM WANGFEL LADEN LA, Sardar Bahadur (see La len La. S. W.).

SONDHI, GURU DATT, B.A. (Cantab), M.A. (Pb.), Barr., Indian Educl. Service (b. 10th Dec., 1890).—Apptd. prof. of economics, govt. coll., Lahore, 17th Sept., 1917.

SONDHI, RAM LALL (see Ram Lall, S.).

SONDHI, SUNDAR DAS, M.C., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Punjab (supt.) (b. 25th Apr., 1890).—First commn. dated 9th Nov., 1917; in milty. employ prior to 25th March, 1927; supt., April, 1927.

SOOD, MANOHAR LAL (see Manohar Lal Sood).

SOOFI, MAHMOOD ALI, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 20th Mar., 1901).—Educ. at Punjab Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1925; arrived, 7th Dec., 1925, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., a idl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, variously from May, 1932; confd., Nov., 1936.

SORBY, WILLIAM JOHN WOODCRAFT, Indian State Rlys. (divl. mech. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 3rd Apr., 1893).—Asst. loco. supt., junr. scale, 2nd Oct., 1920; divl. mech. engr., senr. scale, Oct., 1936.

SORLEY, HERBERT TOWER, M.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., genl. and educn. depis., Bombay) (b. 12th April, 1892).—Educ. at Grammar School, Aberdeen, Aberdeen University, Christ's College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 9th Dec., 1915, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. dep. commr. of salt and excise, June, 1923; criminal tribes settlt. officer, Bombay Presy. (offg.), March, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1926; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; offg. collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Feb., 1929; offg. prov. supt. of census, Bombay presidy.; collr., Apr., 1933; secty. to govt., genl. and educul. depts., Bombay, Mar., 1936.

SOTHERS, DONALD BEVAN, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Bombay) (b. 11th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1912, as asst. consvr.; on duty as asst. censor from 2nd Dec., 1914, to 11th Jan., 1919; I.A.R.O., from 23rd April, 1915; asst. contr., famine fodder, Jan. to April, 1919; dep. consvr., April, 1919; offg. consvr., May, 1928; and again, March, 1932; confd., Aug., 1933; offg. ch. consvr., Bombay, June, 1935.

SOUSA, AUGUSTUS, F.R.C.S., F.S.Sc. D.P.H., late Public Health dept., United Provs. (b. 3rd Oct., 1875).—Joined the service, 1st March, 1914; dep. santy. commr., Lucknow, Feb., 1917; dir. of epidemiology, Oct., 1921; asst. dir. of public health, Aug., 1922; Kaiser-i-Hind Medal, 1st Class, Jan., 1924; ret'd., Apr., 1931.

SOUTER, Sir CHARLES ALEXANDER, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th June, 1877).—Educ. at Arbroadh High Sch., Edinburgh Univ., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 25th Dec., 1901, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. sec. to govt., May, 1906; under-sec., rev. dept., March, 1909; sub-collr. and jt. mag., May, 1910; offg. commr., Coorg, June to Dec., 1916; commr. of Coorg, Dec., 1918, to Dec., 1923; collr. and dist. mag., Jan., 1924; offg. secty. to govt., public wks. dept., March, 1928; 3rd member, bd. of rev. (provl. perm.), Mar., 1930; offg. 2nd ditto, May, 1930; confd., Aug., 1933; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; 1st member, bd. of rev. (provl. subs.), Oct., 1933; member gov'r.s. exec. council, Madras, Jan., 1935; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1936; ret'd., Apr., 1937.

SOUTHERN, Rev. GERALD HOLTE BRACE-BRIDGE, M.A., late Indian Eccles Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 1st Nov., 1889).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 11th May, 1922; junr. chaplain, May, 1924; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1932; retd., Feb., 1938.

SOUTHORN, ANDREW HERBERT, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 2nd Feb., 1909).—Educated at St. Edwards School, Oxford, and Hertford College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Sept., 1934.

SPACKMAN, WILLIAM COLLIS, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Bombay) (b. 23rd Sept., 1889).—First commn. 26th July, 1913; offg. civil surg., March, 1924; confd., Dec., 1927; offg. prof., G.M. Coll., and supt., Bai Motilbai and Sir D. M. Petit hospitals, Bombay, July, 1927, Mar., 1930, and again, Nov., 1932.

SPALDING, HERBERT HENRY, V.D., late Indian State Railways (Eastern Bengal) (b. 6th Jan., 1874).—Joined the service, 20th Sept., 1897, as asst. loco. supt.; dist. traffic supt., April, 1907; dep. loco. and carr. supt., Jan., 1914; loco. and carr. supt., May, 1922; retd., Jan., 1929.

SPARGO, FREDERICK WILSON, B.A., B.Sc., Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sessions judge, Burma) (b. 30th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at William Ellis Sch., Gospel Oak, London, London Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 10th Dec., 1912, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service, Nov., 1917, to Oct., 1919; offg. dist. and sessions judge, March, 1923; confd., Dec., 1923; offg. judge, high ct. Rangoon, Mar. 1936; and again, Nov., 1936.

SPARKE, HARRY CHARLES, late Indian State Railways (b. 6th Sept., 1877).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1897; asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1899; dist. supt., April, 1907; on special duty with the rly. board, Aug., 1914, to Jan., 1915; offg. dep. agent, N.W. rly., April, 1916; on milty. duty with I.A.R.O. from Feb., 1917, to March, 1918 (temp. lt.-col., Feb., 1917); dep. traffic manager, N.W. Rly., Aug., 1917; temp. junr. dep. agent, ditto, April, 1918; offg. traffic manager, E. Ben. Ry., March, 1919; confd., Nov., 1924; offg. dir. traffic, rly. board, April, 1926; member, rly. rates advisory commee., May, 1928; retd., Feb., 1933.

SPARKE, WALTER, V.D., late Indian State Rylys. (b. 29th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 14th Sept., 1899; asst. engr., Jan., 1901; dist. engr., May, 1911; retd., Nov., 1934.

SPEECHLY, ALBERT GEORGE, late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 10th Jan., 1881).—Held non-gazetted posts from 1st Feb., 1898; on depn. with E. Persian Cordon F.F., Sept., 1918, to May, 1920; ch. supt., Calcutta tel. office, Oct., 1932; retd., Jan., 1936.

SPEECHLEY, EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rylys. (b. 26th July, 1880).—Joined the service as junr. engr., 16th April, 1910; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; resdt. engr., Feb., 1920; exec. engr., March, 1926; retd., Apl., 1936.

SPENCE, GEORGE HEMMING, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (sec. govt. of India, legis. dept.) (b. 6th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Oxford, apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1917; addl. dist. mag., Delhi, Jan. to Nov., 1918; temp. under-sec. to govt., May, 1919; spl. duty under for. and pol. dept., govt. of India, from June, 1919; under-sec. to govt. of India, reforms office, Dec., 1919; addl. dep. sec. to govt. of India, legislative dept., Dec., 1920; offg. dep. sec., ditto, April, 1923; confd., Nov., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; secty., council of state, Nov., 1933; and Jan., 1935; offg. secty. to the govt. of India, legis. dept., June, 1934, and again, Nov., 1935; confd., Apl., 1936; secty. to the Govr.-genl.'s exec. council, in addn., Aug., 1936; C.S.I., Feb., 1937.

SPENCER, ARDESHER BARJORJI, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 17th Nov., 1879).—Joined the service, 30th April, 1903; asst. engr., April, 1904; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. supt. engr., Aug., 1926, and July, 1927; supt. engr. (provl. perm.), Dec., 1931; confd., Nov., 1932; retd., Nov., 1934.

SPENCER, FRANK, M.B.E. (revision offcr., govt. of India, def. dept.) (b. 26th Sept., 1888).—Served with reg. army from 18th Nov., 1908, to 11th Mar., 1924; held minis. appts. at A.H.Q. up to Dec., 1930; offcr. supervisor confd., Mar., 1931; M.B.E., Jan., 1932; apptd. revision offcr., def. dept., Apl., 1938.

SPENCER, JOHN EDWARD, late Indian Police (b. 5th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., 4th Dec., 1911; confd., May, 1914; supt., Sept., 1923; offg. asst. to inspr.-genl., March, 1927; confd., Nov., 1927; retd., Aug., 1934.

SPILLER, THOMAS REUBEN, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (b. 9th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1920; prinpl., New Coll., Patna, Oct., 1923; headmaster, Zila Sch., June, 1927; offg. prinpl., Patna training coll., variously from May, 1933; inspr. of sch., Bihar and Orissa, variously from Apl., 1933; headmaster, Ranchi Zila Sch., again, Oct., 1933.

SPILSBURY, LIONEL BRUCE, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Sept., 1881).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1902, as asst. engr.; United Provs.; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; offg. supt. engr., Nov., 1921; confd., Aug., 1923; retd., July, 1933.

SPITTELER, ARTHUR ERNEST, Indian Police (b. 29th Oct., 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., Madras, on probn., 24th Jan., 1936.

SPRATT, NOEL NEWTON, India Office (higher clerical officer, mil. dept.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1891).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 9th March, 1914; on mil. service from Nov., 1916, to July, 1919; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

SPRAWSON, Sir CUTHBERT ALLEN, Kt., C.I.E., M.D., M.R.C.P., Maj.-Genl., I.M.S., retd., (b. 1st Mar., 1877).—First commn. dated 28th June, 1900; profrr. of physiology, King George's Medl. Coll., Lucknow, July, 1911; profrr. of medicine, ditto, July, 1913; on mil. duty from June, 1917; C.I.E., March, 1919; principal, King George's Medl. Coll., Lucknow, Nov., 1921; prof., ditto, Oct., 1925; inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Mar., 1929; surg.-genl. with govt. of

Madras, Apl., 1930; apptd. hon. physician to the King, Feb., 1933; offg. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Oct., 1933; confd., Nov., 1933; presdt., medl. council of India, Feb., 1934; member, council of state, Apl., 1936; served on N.W. Frontier of India, Waziristan, 1901-02, medal and clasp; knighted, June, 1936; retd., Mar., 1937.

SPRAY, WILLIAM ALFRED, Indian State Ry. (colliery manager, E.I. Ry.) (b. 27th Feb., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. colliery manager, 13th Aug., 1919; offg. variously as colliery manager from Oct., 1919; confd., Sept., 1926.

SPRINGFIELD, ERNEST, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 3rd Nov., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 7th Dec., 1922; offg. supt., May, 1929; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934; supt., July, 1934.

SPURGEON, CHRISTOPHER EDWARD, late Indian State Railways (b. 24th May, 1879).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1904, as asst. loco. supt., state ry. s.; dist. loco. supt., Apl., 1911; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1924; offg. dep. chief mechanical engr., Feb., 1926; offg., supt., mechanical workshop, N.W. Ry., Oct., 1926; offg. ch. mechanical engr., Nov., 1927; confd., Feb., 1929; retd., May, 1934.

SQUIRE, GILES FREDERICK, B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 12th Oct., 1894).—Educ. at Marlborough and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Aug., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, on probation as first asst. to resdt. of Baroda, April, 1924; confd., March, 1925; sec. to agent to gov.-genl. in Central India, April, 1926; under-sec. to agent to gov.-genl. in Central India, Nov., 1926; secty. to agent to gov.-genl., Punjab States, Dec., 1927; ditto, W.I. States, Oct., 1929; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat, Nov., 1933; pol. agent, Kalat, Apl., 1934; ditto, Sibi, Dec., 1935; offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Mar., 1936; consul genl. for Khorasan, Nov., 1936.

SQUIRES, HOWARD JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 3rd Sept., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 21st Oct., 1928; exec. engr., Oct., 1937.

SREEKANTESWARAN, KOLAR VENKATSWAMIER, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 11th Mar., 1903).—Joined the service, 8th Mar., 1926; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Apl., 1929; curr. offcr., Bombay, Dec., 1933; dep. acct.-genl., Punjab, June, 1935; accts. offcr., E.I. rly., Apl., 1936.

SRI HARI RAO NAYUDU, BANDI VENKATA, Diwan Bahadur, B.A., B.C., Registration Dept., Madras (inspr.-genl.) (b. 19th Feb., 1887).—Held non-gazetted appt., offg. dep. collr., Feb., 1920; confd., May, 1923; offg. 3rd presdy. magte., Apl., 1928; offg. 4th ditto, July, 1928; offg. asst. secty., bd. of rev., May, 1929; 3rd presdy. magte., Mar., 1930; 2nd ditto, Oct., 1930; inspr.-genl. of registration, Feb., 1931.

SRI NARAYAN, RAI BAHADUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 3rd Mar., 1894).—Joined the service, 28th Sept., 1916; asst. engr., Sept., 1917; personal asst. to ch. engr. and under-sec. to govt., Aug., 1925; exec. engr., Jan., 1926; services placed

at disposal of inspr.-gen. of police, from Apl., 1926, to July, 1929; on depn. to Improvement Trust Cawnpore, as ch. engr., Mar., 1932; Rai Bahadur, Jan., 1934.

SRINIVASA RAO, CADAPANATHAM, RAO BAHADUR, M.A., late India Posts and Tels. (Madras) (b. 1875).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 1st Aug., 1898; supt. Feb., 1904; dep.-postmaster-genl., Aug., 1920; retd., July, 1930.

SRINIVASA RAO, CHIKBALLAPUR VENKATA, Indian Audit Dept. (b. 12th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service, 17th March, 1914; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Nov., 1915; asst. compt., Assam, Dec., 1915; asst. compt., India treasuries, Nov., 1916; currency officer, Rangoon, May, 1919; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, July, 1919; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Madras, Jan., 1924; on spl. duty in fin. dept., March, 1925; offg. dep. contr. of rly. accts., Mar., 1933; addl. dep. dir. (fin.) rly. bd., Jan., 1935; offg. finl. adviser, posts and tels., May, 1935; offg. dep. ch. accts. officer, Burma ry. s., Mar., 1936; offg. ch. ditto, Apl., 1936; on for. service as acct.-genl., Burma, Apl., 1937.

SRINAVASAN, KALYANA CHENNAKESAVA, M.A., F.C.S., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 26th Apl., 1896).—Joined the service, 24th March, 1921; Asst. Auditor, E.B. Ry., Aug., 1922; on depn. with Govt. of India in connection with accounts for dairy farms, Feb., 1923; Secty., rly. rates advisory ctee., May, 1923; divl. audit officer, Karachi, June, 1928; Govt. exr. of accts., B.N. Ry., Mar., 1929; dy. ch. auditor, G.I.P. Ry., May, 1930; dy. govt. exr., B.B. and C.I. Ry., Apl., 1931; on spl. duty under contr. of rly. accts., 1936.

SRINIVASAN, KUPPUSWAMI, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 1st May, 1907).—Educ. at University Coll., London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Jan., 1934.

SRINAVASAN, MANDAYAM MADUMBAL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 15th Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 10th Nov., 1931.

SRIRAMULU NAYUDU, BUNDY, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 4th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 2nd Jan., 1907; promoted to I.P. as dist. supt., Mar., 1932.

SRIVASTAV, JASODA NAND, Rai Sahib, P.M.S., Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. Dec., 1884).—Civil surg., Apl., 1928.

SRIVASTAVA, RAM CHANDRA, O.B.E., B.Sc., Industries Dept., United Provs. (dep. dir.) (b. 10th Sept., 1891).—Apptd. dep. dir. of industries, United Provs., 1st Sept., 1928; services placed at disposal of Impl. Council of Agricultural Research, for employment as sugar technologist, July, 1930; dir., Impl. Inst. of sugar technology, Cawnpore, Oct., 1936; confd., Oct., 1937; O.B.E., Jan., 1938.

SRIVASTAVA, SHYAM MOHAN, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (l. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 13th July, 1910).—Educ. at K.P. Intermediate College, Allahabad, University of Allahabad and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1934; arrived in India Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr., jt. magte., Nov., 1936.

STACE, RALPH EDWARD, Lt.-Col., R.E. (ret'd.) (b. 9th Dec., 1881).—Apptd. dep. mint master, April, 1919; offg. mint master, Calcutta, April to Nov., 1920, and Nov., 1921, to April, 1922; junior mint master, Bombay, May, 1923; mint master, ditto, July, 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

STAFFORD, PAUL, Indian Police (b. 12th Dec., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., Nov., 1938.

STAGG, MONTAGU, Col., R.E., O.B.E., (mint master, Calcutta) (b. 4th Nov., 1885).—Apptd. dep. mint master, Bombay, 11th May 1920; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; dep. mint master, Calcutta, March, 1921; offg. mint master, Calcutta, from April, 1921; confd., Feb., 1926.

STAIG, BERTIE MUNRO, C.S.I., Indian C.S., att'd. Indian Finance Dept. (finl. commr. rlys.) (b. 14th Aug., 1892).—Educ. at St. Andrews, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1916; arrived, 18th Dec., 1917, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., May, 1918, to January, 1919; offg. under-sec., govt. of Bengal, pol. and appts. dept., March, 1921; ditto, fin. dept., Nov., 1921; transf'd. to Indian audit and accts. list, Nov., 1922; dep. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, May, 1924; offg. acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1924; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1925; finl. advr., P.W.D., and jt. sec. to govt. of Punjab, fin. dept., March, 1926; secty., hydro-electric branch enquiry commee., Oct., 1929; secty. to govt., P.W.D. (electricity), Punjab, Apl., 1933; addl. finl. adviser, milty. finance, July, 1934; finl. adviser, milty. finance, Mar., 1935; also relief commr., Baluchistan and jt. secty., govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., June, 1935; C.S.I., June, 1936; on spl. duty in for. and pol. dept., Sept., 1936; member, frontier watch and ward cttee., Nov., 1936; finl. commr., rlys., Apl., 1937.

STAINTON, VERNON BRUCE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty., home dept., govt. of Punjab) (b. 31st Mar., 1904).—Educ. at Harrow, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1932, and again, Jan., 1933; confd., Nov., 1936; dep. secty. to govt., home dept., Nov., 1937.

STALEY, HENRY SAMBLE, M.A., Indian Educ. Service (inspr. of schools, Central Provs.) (b. 20th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 27th March, 1915; inspr. of schools, April, 1916; offg. dep. dir. of public instrn., April, 1926; offg. prinpl., Spence training coll., Jubbulpore, July, 1934.

STALLARD, PHILIP LECHMERE, M.A., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 20th May, 1880).—Joined the service as dist. surg., 14th May, 1909; offg. principal medl. and health officer, March, 1927; dist. medl. officer, April, 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

STALLARD, ROBERT HUMPHRY, O.B.E., Major, R.E. (ret'd.), late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., July, 1910; on milty. duty from 22nd Oct., 1914, to 29th Nov., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; exec. engr., Dec., 1919; offg. dep. agent, E.B. Rly., Oct., 1926; on foreign service with M. & S. M. rly. as dep. agent, April, 1929; ret'd., June, 1930.

STALLARD, WILLIAM LEONARD O'BRIAN, Indian Police (asst. supt., Orissa) (b. 29th Aug., 1908).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 28th Dec., 1928; confd. Apl., 1930; A.D.C. to govt., Orissa, Apl., 1936.

STAMPE, Sir WILLIAM LEONARD, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 31st Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; profir., civil engrg. coll., Roor-kee, Nov., 1920, to Oct., 1924; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1925; confd., Apl., 1926; K-i-H., medal, 1st Class, Jan., 1926; offg. ch. engr. and jt. sec. to govt., P.W. dept., I.B., Apl., 1927; and again, Mar., 1931; confd., Nov., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; Knighted, Jan., 1935; ret'd. Oct. 1937.

STAMPER, THOMAS HENRY GILBORN, C.I.E., F.S.I., M.C. (b. 27th Oct., 1884).—Apptd. asst. consultg. surveyor to govt., Bombay, Apl., 1920; offg. consulting surveyor, Feb., 1926; confd., June, 1930; member, legisl. council, Oct., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1938.

STANDEN, Sir BERTRAM PRIOR, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Uppingham, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 5th Dec., 1888, and served in Burma and the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; registrar, jud. commr.'s court, Nagpur, March, 1891, to March, 1892; settlt. officer, Dec., 1894; dep. commr., Aug., 1897; C.I.E., Jan., 1901; commr. of settlmt. and agric., Sept., 1904; dir. of agric. and regr., co-operative credit societies, April, 1908; chief sec. to chief commr., Nov., 1908, to Jan., 1911; commr., April, 1918; C.S.I., June, 1920; member of exec. council of govt. of Central Provs., Dec., 1920, to Dec., 1925; K.C.I.E., June, 1925; ret'd., June, 1926.

STANFORD, JOHN KEITH, O.B.E., M.A., M.C., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th Apl., 1892).—Educ. at Rugby, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Nov., 1914; apptd., 24th Oct., 1919; arrived, 24th Dec., 1919, and served in Burma; dep. dir. of comml. intellge., Calcutta, Jan., 1922; services replaced at disposal of govt. of Burma, June, 1923; under-sec., home and pol. dept., Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., June, 1924; regr., high ct., Rangoon, June, 1925; offg. dep. commr., June, 1927; confd., Sept., 1930; O.B.E., June, 1932; secty., rev. dept., Burma, June, 1932; ret'd., Mar., 1938.

STANGER-LEATHES, HUGH ELLIS, Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 1st Nov., 1878).—First commn. dated 30th Jan., 1904; in milty. employ up to 28th Feb., 1922; health officer, Delhi, March, 1922; dep. dir., public health, New Delhi, April, 1926; offg. dep. dir.-genl., I.M.S., March, 1928; confd., July, 1929; hon. surg. to the King, Sept., 1932; A.D.M.S., Madras Dist., Aug., 1933; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

STANIFORTH, WALTER HARLAND, India Office (suptg. executive officer. acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 21st June, 1886).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 15th Aug., 1907; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Feb., 1919; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; actg. staff clerk, April, 1921; acting higher exec. officer, April, 1922; confd., Oct., 1925; also assessor of income tax; suptg. exec. officer Oct., 1936.

STANLEY, Lord, EDWARD MONTAGU CAVEN-
DISH STANLEY, P.C., M.C., M.P. (b. 9th July,
1894).—Parl. under-secty. of state for India
and for Burma, May, 1937, to May, 1938.

STANSFIELD, Rev. HERBERT RIGG, Indian
Eccles. Estabt. (b. 22nd Sept., 1897).—Apptd.
junior chaplain, 25th Dec., 1931; name
changed by deed poll to RIGG-STANSFIELD
(q.v.).

STANTIAL, WILLIAM, C.I.E., late India
Office (b. 13th June, 1865).—Educ. at the City
of London Sch.; clerk, 2nd class, 25th Sept.,
1882; junior clerk, July, 1898; senior clerk,
Oct., 1903; sec., cttee. on Indian railway
finance and administration, 1907-08; asst. sec.
P.W.D., May, 1911; sec., ditto, Nov., 1919;
C.I.E., Jan., 1923; rly. adviser and govt. dir.
Indian rly. cos., Oct., 1926; ret'd., June, 1931.

STANTON, WILLIAM THOMAS, Indian State
Rlys. (colliery manager) (b. 16th May, 1887).—
Joined the service as asst. colliery manager,
15th Aug., 1919; colliery manager, Sept., 1931.

STAPLES, FRANCIS HAMMOND, M.A. (Oxon),
Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st July, 1884).—Educ.
at Downside Coll., Bath, and St. John's Coll.,
Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived,
25th Nov., 1907, and served in the Central
Provs. as asst. commr.; sub-judge, July, 1914;
dist. judge, March, 1916; addl. dist. and sess.
judge, Dec., 1916; dist. and sess. judge, June,
1919; addl. judl. commr., Dec., 1928; ret'd.,
July, 1936

STAPLETON, HENRY ERNEST, D.Litt., B.Sc.
(Oxon), late Indian Educatl. Service (b. 3rd
May, 1878).—Joined the service, 16th Nov.,
1900, as prof., presdy. coll., Calcutta; inspr. of
schools, Oct., 1905; on mil. duty from May,
1915, to Dec., 1919; principal, Presy.
Coll., Calcutta, July, 1924; offg. dir. of public
instrn., Bengal, June, 1926; confd., July, 1930;
ret'd., May, 1933.

STAPYLTON, JOHN MILES, late Indian
C.S. (b. 13th Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Lancing
Coll.; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1923;
arrived, 16th Nov., 1923, and served in Central
Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr.,
Feb., 1927; dep. commr., etc., Port Blair,
Aprl., 1932; dep. commr. (subst.), Aug., 1936;
ret'd., Sept., 1938.

STARTE, HORACE WILDERSPIN, B.A., Indian
Forest Service (consvr., Bombay) (b. 13th Mar.,
1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 1st
Dec., 1911; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; offg.
consvr., Feb., 1929, and again, Sept., 1932;
confd., May, 1935.

STARTE, OLIVER HAROLD BAPTIST, C.B.E.,
B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd Jan., 1882).—
Educ. at Perse Grammar School, Cambridge,
and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after
exam. of 1905; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1906,
and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.;
on spl. duty in connection with settl. of
criminal tribes in the Bijapur dist., from Nov.,
1909; K.-i.-H. medal, 2nd class Dec., 1911;
member, jail commn., 1919-1920; criminal
tribes settlement officer, June, 1920; collr.,
Aug., 1926; C.B.E., June, 1932; ret'd., Aprl., 1936.

STATHAM, RANDULPH MEVEREL, C.I.E.,
B.A. (Hons.), Indian Educl. Service (dir. pub.
instrn., Madras) (b. 4th Sept., 1890).—Joined
the service, 13th July, 1913; dep. dir. of public
instrn., Madras, May, 1923; C.I.E., June, 1930;
dist. educ. officer, Sept., 1930; offg. princpl.,
teachers' coll., Saidapet, Sept., 1930; princpl.,
govt. training coll., Rajahmundry, Aprl., 1932;
princpl., govt. Muhammadan coll., Madras, Oct.,
1933; offg. educ. commr. with the Govt. of
India, May, 1935; dir. of public instrn., Aprl
1936.

STEAD, Sir CHARLES, Kt., C.B.E., M.V.O.,
late Indian Police Service (b. 30th Aug.,
1877).—Joined the service, 17th Nov.,
1898; supt. of police, July, 1906; M.V.O.,
Jan., 1912; awarded King's Police medal, Jan.,
1915; personal asst. to inspr.-gen., Sept., 1915;
O.B.E., Jan., 1921; asst. inspr.-gen., govt. rly.
police, Punjab, March, 1922; C.B.E., June,
1922; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Aprl., 1924;
confd., Nov., 1925; offg. inspr.-genl. of police,
N.W.F.P., May, 1927; ditto, Punjab, Feb.,
1928; confd., May, 1930; Kt., Jan., 1931;
ret'd., Aug., 1932.

STEADMAN, JOHN WILLIAM, Office of High
Commr. for India (asst. chief acctg. officr.)
(b. 3rd July, 1885).—Clerk to Rl. commn.
on Indian decentralisation, June, 1908, to
March, 1909; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India
Office, 28th March, 1909, and attached to
acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; clerk to royal commn. on
public services, May, 1913, to Sept., 1915;
asst. sec. to Jails Commee., from July, 1919,
to Sept., 1920; minor staff clerk, Nov., 1920;
services lent to high commr. for India from
Aprl., 1921; higher exec. officer, Aprl., 1922;
suptg. exec. officr., June, 1927; asst. chief
acctg. officr., Nov., 1937.

STEAVENSON, FREDERICK HENRY, late
Indian C.S. (b. 19th Dec., 1885).—Educ.
at Malvern, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford;
apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 18th Dec.,
1909, and served in Burma as asst. commr.;
asst. sec., rev. sec.'s dept., Aprl., 1913, to Dec.,
1916; jt. regr., co-opt. societies, Nov., 1920;
dep. commr., and ditto, March, 1925; offg.
secty. to govt., forest dept., May, 1928; offg.
dep. secty., home and pol. dept., Dec., 1928;
ditto, edn. dept., Mar., 1929; offg. commr.,
July, 1933; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

STEEL, RICHARD FRANCIS, F.R.C.S., M.B.,
B.Ch., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 28th Aprl.,
1878).—First commn., 31st Aug., 1903;
apptd. civil surgn., Bombay, June, 1909;
obstet. phys., J. J. Hospl., prof. of midwifry.,
G. M. Coll., Aprl., 1923; supt., B. J. Med.
School, Poona, Oct., 1925; ret'd., Aprl., 1933.

STEELE, GEOFFREY LANYON, late Salt
Dept., Bombay (b. 23rd Dec., 1880).—Held
non-gazetted appts., salt and customs depts.,
Bombay, from 15th Feb., 1900; asst. collr.
of salt rev., Bombay, May, 1910; supt. of salt
rev., Mar., 1924; asst. collr., Sept., 1926;
ret'd., Oct., 1934.

STEEN, HUGH BARKLEY, M.D., Lt.-Col.,
I.M.S. (ret'd.) (addl. member, medl. bd., India
Office) (b. 23rd Nov., 1875).—Joined the
service, 20th Jan., 1902; arrived, 19th Aprl.,
1902; in mil. employ to Jan., 1906, when

employed as civil surg., Bengal; on mil. service, Sept., 1914, to Dec., 1915; prof., medical coll., Calcutta, Dec., 1916; retd., Nov., 1930; apptd. examr. of surgical instruments, India Store Dept., and addl. member, medl. bd., India Office, May, 1934.

STEIGER-HAYLEY, THOMAS THEODORE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 4th Oct., 1913).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 13th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Nov., 1938, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

STEIN, ARCHIBALD HYNDMAN, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 11th Jan., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 21st Feb., 1924; dep. consvr., Feb., 1928.

STEIN, Sir (MARC) AUREL, K.C.I.E., Ph.D., D.Litt., D.Sc., F.B.A., late Indian Archaeological Survey (b. 26th Nov., 1872).—Educ. at Budapest and Univs. of Vienna and Tübingen; principal, Oriental Coll., Lahore, and regr. of the Punjab Univ. from 1883 to May, 1899, when he was apptd. principal of the Calcutta Madrasah; proceeded on archl. tour with Buner Field Force, 1898; in 1900-01 deputed on special duty for archl. exploration in E. Turkestan; inspr. of schools, Punjab, Nov., 1901; inspr.-genl. of educn. and archl. surveyor, N.W. Frontier Prov. and Baluchistan, Jan., 1904; proceeded on a second archaeological and geographical expdn., Turkestan and West China, April, 1906; supt., archaeological survey, Eastern Circle, Oct., 1909; transferred to Frontier Circle, May, 1910; proceeded on spl. duty for explorations in E. Turkestan, W. China, Pamirs and Persia, Aug., 1913, to Feb., 1916; C.I.E., June, 1910; K.C.I.E., June, 1912; retd. June, 1929; author of 'Kalhanas Chronicle of Kashmir,' ed. and translated (1892-1901); 'Sand-buried Ruins of Khotan' (1903); 'Ancient Khotan' (1907); 'Ruins of Desert Cathay' (1912); 'Serindia' (1922); various reports on his other explorations, &c.

STEIN, GEORGE LESLIE HAMILTON, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 4th March, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Nov., 1909; supt., Oct., 1920.

STEIN, JOHN ALAN, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 31st Oct., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1931; and again, Apl., 1933; codfd., Mar., 1934.

STENT, PERCY JOHN HODSOLI, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Central Provs. and Berar) (b. 24th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at King's Coll. School, Wimbledon, and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 28th Nov., 1913, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., May, 1916; serving under govt. of India, army dept., from Sept., 1916, to Aug., 1919; under-sec. to govt., Cent. Provs., Dec., 1920; offg. dep. commr. and dep. regr., co-operative societies, March, 1922; dep. commr., Dec., 1925; rev. secty. to govt., United Provs., Oct., 1933; offg. commr., Dec., 1933, confd., Mar., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

STEPHEN, Sir HARRY LUSHINGTON, Bt., Barr. (1885).—Apptd. judge, high court, Calcutta, 18th Nov., 1901; knighted June, 1913; retd., Nov., 1914.

STEPHENS, CHARLES VIVIAN, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 28th Apl., 1887).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 25th June, 1910; perm. in provl. service, 1st Feb., 1920; asst. exec. engr. in I.S.E., 1st Oct., 1925; offg. exec. engr., May, 1926; confd., Nov., 1931.

STEPHENS, IAN MELVILLE, C.I.E., Dep. dir. of public information, govt. of India, Mar., 1930; dir., ditto, Aug., 1932 to Aug., 1937.

STEPHENS, JAMES, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 6th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 30th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.
