Sept., 1926; offg. contr. of the currency, Calcutta, April, 1927; confd., Oct., 1929; offg. sec., fin. dept., govt. of India, June, 1932, and again, July, 1933; temp. addl. secty., ditto, Sept., 1933; and again, Nov., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; retd., Feb., 1935; dep. govr., Reserve Bank of India, Feb., 1935; knighted, June, 1935; govr., Reserve Bank of India, July, 1937.

TAYLOR, JOHN, C.I.E., D.S.O., V.H.S., M.D., D.P.H., Col. I.M.S. (dir., Central research inst., Kasauli) (b. 14th Feb., 1884).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1906; arrived in India, 26th Feb., 1907; in milty. employ from 26th Feb., 1907, to 31st Aug., 1909; in civil employ. in Bombay from 1st Sept., 1909, to 15th Oct., 1914; reverted to milty. duty from 16th Oct., 1914, to 5th Aug., 1920; offg. sanitary commr., Assam, Aug., 1920; offg. asst. dir., Pasteur Inst., Rangoon, March, 1923; offg. dir., ditto, March, 1925; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. dir., Haffkine inst., Feb., 1932; dir., central research inst., Kasauli, Oct., 1932; C.I.E., May, 1937.

TAYLOR, JOHN FREDERICK, A.T.C.L., Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 5th Jan., 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 8th Aug., 1904; minor staff clerk, May, 1920; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922.

TAYLOR, LESLIE FERNANDES, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Burma) (b. 21st Aug., 1886).—Joined the service as prinpl., govt. high school, Bassein, 18th April, 1915; offg. as research lecturer in Indo-Chinese ethnology and linguistics, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, May, 1926; retd., Nov., 1930.

TAYLOR, ROBERT WILLIAM, Indian State Ryls. (dep. ch. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 16th Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. ch. engr., Mar., 1932; and again, Sept., 1935; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1936; dep. ch. engr. (prov. perm.), Oct., 1935; confd., Nov., 1937.

TAYLOR, SAMUEL, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 23rd Feb., 1883).—Joined the service, 27th Dec., 1913; works manager, June, 1919; dist. loco. supt., Apl., 1927; offg. dep. ch. mechnl. engr., E.B. rly., June, 1935; retd., Feb., 1938.

TAYLOR, STANLEY GRISEWOOD, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 19th May, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 2nd Dec., 1913; addl. supt., March, 1919; offg. supt., Nov., 1923; confd., Aug., 1925; awarded King's Police medal, Jan., 1934; prin., police training coll., Sardah, Jan., 1936.

TAYLOR, WALTER, India Office (hr. executive officer, finl. dept.) (b. 29th Oct., 1895).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 5th Aug., 1914, and attached to acct.-gen.'s dept.; on mil. service from July, 1915, to Jan., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922; jt. editor, India Office List, Nov., 1934; actg. hr. exec. officer, Sept., 1935; confd., Oct., 1936; attd. finl. dept., Dec., 1935; editor, India Office and Burma Office List, Nov., 1937.

TAYLOR, WALTER, D.V.H., M.R.C.V.S., Indian Veterinary Service (prinpl. and prof. of medicine, vety. coll., Punjab) (b. 25th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as prinpl. and prof. of medicine, vety. coll., Punjab, 21st Mar., 1913; offg. dir., civil vety. dept., Punjab, Mar., 1929; offg. dir., vety. services, Punjab, Apl., 1932; offg. dir., impl. inst. of vety. research, Nov., 1933.

TAYLOR, WILFRED REGINALD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., late Indian State Ryls. (b. 18th Mar., 1881).—Joined the service as dist. medl. officer, 10th Mar., 1910; offg. C.M.O., Burma rlys., Jan., 1918; confd., Sept., 1931; retd., Mar., 1938.

TEAGUE, LIONEL ELLES STANLEY, late Indian Forest Service (Bengal) (b. 12th Feb., 1882).—Arrived in India, 10th Nov., 1905, and served as asst. consvr.; I.A.R.O., Nov., 1917, to April, 1919; dep. consr., Dec., 1919; retd., July, 1930.

TEDMAN, CYRIL, M.C., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Ryls. (b. 20th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 6th March, 1902; resdt. engr., March, 1905; senr. ditto, April, 1915; in milty. employ from 15th June, 1915, to 10th April, 1919; dist. engr., Nov., 1919; dep. chief engr., May, 1922; retd., Nov., 1933.

TEGART, SIR CHARLES AUGUSTUS, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.V.O., late Indian Police Service (Bengal) (b. 5th Oct., 1881).—Joined the service, Nov., 1901, and served in Bengal as asst. supt.; supt., Jan., 1908; awarded King's police medal, 1911; M.V.O., Jan., 1912; dep. commr. on special duty in addition, Feb., 1913; C.I.E., Jan., 1917; supt. and dep. inspr.-gen. in charge intell. divn. (temp.) April, 1918; offg. commr. of police, Calcutta,

March, 1923; confd., June, 1925; Kt. Jan., 1926; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; retd., Dec., 1931; member of Council of India, Dec., 1931, to Dec., 1936; K.C.I.E., May, 1937.

TEJ NARAYAN MULLA, M.A., LL.B. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. July, 1887).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1921, as addl. dist. and sess. judge; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1923; offg. judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1935; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1936.

TEJA SINGH, SARDAR Bahadur, Sardar, B.A., LL.B., Punjab C.S., Judcl. Bch. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 1st Jan., 1889).—Joined the service, 8th July, 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934.

TEJASWI PRASAD BHALLA, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (sup't, United Provs.) (b. 11th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. sup't., United Provs., 24th May, 1923; offg. sup't., July, 1926; awarded King's Police medal, Jan., 1928; sup't., June, 1935; asst. to D.I.G. C.I.D. (Gen.), Apl., 1936; O.S.D. at govt. hdqtrs., Nov., 1937.

TEK CHAND, BAKSHI, M.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., Punjab (judge, high ct., Lahore).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Lahore, 27th Jan., 1927.

TELFER, GEORGE HENRY, Indian State Railways (signal engr., Burma rlys.) (b. 11th May, 1883).—Joined the service, 15th June, 1904; asst. engr. (signals), Burma rlys., Oct., 1923; signal engr., Mar., 1931.

TEMPLE, JOHN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th May, 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 22nd Sept., 1905; resdt. engr., June, 1910; dist. engr., Mar., 1924; divl. engr., G.I.P. rly., June, 1926; retd., Mar., 1938.

TEN-BROEKE, BERTRAM ST. LEGER, M.C., Indian Police (sup't, Bihar and Orissa) (b. 8th Sept., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. sup't., 20th Mar., 1921; sup't., July, 1925; awarded Indian police medal, Feb., 1937.

TENNANT, WILLIAM ROBERT, M.A., Indian C.S. (b. 26th Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ.; arrived, 30th Nov., 1919, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mgr. and collr.; dep. sup't. of census, March, 1921; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, fin. dept., Oct., 1923; depy. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1924; ditto, U.P., Nov., 1925; addl. dep. sec., govt. of India, finance dept., Nov., 1927; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Dec., 1929; confd., Mar., 1930; offg. financial adviser, milty. finance, Apl., 1933; acct.-genl., Bombay, Oct., 1934; dir. of audit, defence services, Oct., 1937.

TERRY, DOUGLAS EDGAR, India Office (hr. clerical officer) (b. 15th May, 1899).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st June, 1920, after previous service from 2nd May, 1917; hr. clerical officer, Nov., 1935.

TEULON, OLIVER HENRY, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 29th June, 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. sup'tg. engr., Sept., 1923; confd., Mar., 1927; offg. dep. ch. engr., June, 1928; offg. ch. engr., June, 1929; confd., July, 1933; retd., June, 1938.

TEW, GILBERT CHARLES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th June, 1884).—Educ. at Warwick Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec., rev. dept., July, 1916; pte. sec. to the lieut.-govr., July, 1919, to March, 1920; dep. commr. (s.p.t.), May, 1923; confd., July, 1924; custodian of enemy property, Burma, from Oct., 1924; offg. excise commr., June, 1927; confd., June, 1932; retd., Sept., 1934.

THA, MAUNG CHAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 14th Feb., 1910).—Educ. at Government High School, Minbu, Rangoon University and New College, Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

THACKER, PITAMBERDAS LAVJI, B.A., LL.B., Bombay late Judicial Dept. (b. 31st July, 1881).—Joined the dept., 29th Mar., 1925; 6th presdy. mag., Mar., 1925; retd., July, 1936.

THACKWELL, ALBERT HENRY, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 19th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. carr. and wagon sup't., 19th June, 1920; asst. works manager, July, 1920; works manager, June, 1925; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., July, 1932, May, 1933, and again, Apl., 1935; confd., Jan., 1937.

THADANI, NENUMAL POHOOMAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 10th Sept., 1891).—Educ. in India and at Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. to I.C.S. by nomination; arrived, 4th Jan., 1917, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. offr.; jt. mag., July, 1922; addl. dep. commr., June, 1925; magte. and collr. (provl. sub.), Dec., 1931; confd., Oct., 1933.

THAKOR, KRISHNALAL KIRPARAM, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Civil Service (b. 4th Feb., 1879).—Joined the service as offg. sub-judge, 2nd June, 1909; confd., Apl., 1911; asst. judge, Jan., 1927; judge and sess. judge, Mar., 1930; retd., Feb., 1934.

THAKUR, KESHAR SADASHIV, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Bengal) (b. 2nd Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 31st July, 1909; in milty. employ up to 22nd Feb., 1921; offg. civilsurgeon, Bengal, Sept., 1921; ditto 24th Parganas and protector of emigrants, Calcutta, Feb., 1934.

THAMBAN, VENGANAD KOVILAGAM RAJAGOPALA (see Rajagopala Thamban, V. K.).

THAN, U BA, late Judl. Dept., Burma (b. 26th Mar., 1880).—Joined the service, 22nd July, 1908; asst. judge, July, 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1929; confd., Oct., 1932; retd., Mar., 1935.

THAPAR, PREM NATH, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 13th April, 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of the Punjab and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1933; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), July, 1935.

THARLE-HUGHES, REGINALD, O.B.E. (b. 17th Dec., 1873).—Served with 4th D. Gds. from July, 1893, to Nov., 1900; M.B.E., Dec., 1917; held ministerial appts. at A.H.Q. and with govt. of India up to 1920; asst. sec., April, 1920; estab. officer, 1921; offg. dep. sec. from May to Aug., 1924, and April, 1926; on Punjab frontier, 1897–98, and with Tirah expeditionary force, 1897–98; on special duty in Mesopotamia and Persia, Oct. to Dec., 1918; O.B.E., Mar., 1929; ret'd., June, 1929.

THAVARDAS DAYARAM, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 8th Jan., 1875).—Joined the dept., 1st Dec., 1903; sub-judge, March, 1904; offg. asst. judge and asst. sessions judge, April, 1925; ret'd., Jan., 1930.

THAW, ALFRED BA, M.B., Major, I.M.S., Burma Medl. dept. (civil surg.) (b. 13th March, 1892).—First commn. dated 15th Jan., 1918; in milty. employ prior to 29th May, 1924; asst. medl. supt., Rangoon genl. hosp., June, 1924; civil surg., March, 1926.

THAW, U PO, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th July, 1875).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., 11th Feb., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1928; ret'd., July, 1930.

THEIN, MAUNG KYAW, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. customs) (b. 10th June, 1910).—Educ. at Government High School, Akyab, Rangoon University and St. John's College, Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct. 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. collr. customs, Nov., 1937.

THEIN, U BA HLA, Burma Judl. Service (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 24th Nov., 1885).—Joined the service, 11th Jan., 1909; asst. judge, July, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1934; confd., Nov., 1937.

THI, U BA, B.A., Income Tax Dept., Burma (asst. commr.) (b. 22nd Sept., 1886).—Apptd. to dept., 9th July, 1923; inc. tax officer, Dec., 1923; offg. asst. commr., Aug., 1928; confd., Dec., 1930.

THICKETT, JOSEPH HEDLEY, M.A. (Cantab.) late Indian Educational Service (b. 3rd July, 1880).—Joined the service, 15th March, 1907, as inspr. of schools, Patna divn.; principal, training coll., Patna, Oct., 1908; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

THIN, U BA (Ar), B.A., late Burma Civil Service (b. 6th Apl., 1876).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., May, 1910; dep. commr., April, 1926; ret'd., July, 1931.

THIRUMAL, YENGAR, MANDAYAM ANNADHORAI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 6th Nov., 1907).—Educ. at Madras University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1934.

THOM, HERBERT JAMES, M.C., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 24th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service, 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., May, 1923; confd., May, 1929; services lent to H.H. the Maharaja Bahadur of Benares from Feb., 1927; supt., Dec., 1931; princpl., police trng. sch., Apl., 1936.

THOM, SIR JOHN GIBB, D.S.O., M.C., M.A., LL.B., Judicial Dept., United Provs. (ch. justice, high ct., Allahabad).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Allahabad, 17th Mar., 1931; offg. ch. justice, ditto, Mar., 1936; confd., Oct., 1937; knighted, May, 1937.

THOMAS, ALAN ROBERT, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Assam) (b. 26th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 22nd Dec., 1911; in mil. employ from 24th Sept., 1915, to 11th Oct., 1919; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; offg. consvr., Jan., 1933.

THOMAS, ANTHONY RYLANDS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 8th Dec., 1905).—Joined the service as prob. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1929.

THOMAS, ARNOLD NEWALL, D.S.O., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lon.), M.B., B.S. (Lon.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 7th Aug., 1880).—Joined the service, 27th July, 1907, and served in the mil. dept. prior to 15th Jan., 1920; personal asst. to surg.-gen., Jan., 1920; offg. supt. of Mahabaleshwar, Oct., 1924; confd., May, 1926; presy. surg., Oct., 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

THOMAS, BERNARD GORDON PROTHERO, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 25th Nov., 1906; supt., Apl., 1920; asst. inspr.-genl. rly. police, Mar., 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1933; O.B.E., Jan., 1934; dep. inspr.-genl. Oct., 1936.

THOMAS, DAVID REES, O.B.E., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 12th April, 1889).—Apptd. chemical examiner to govt., Punjab, 1st March, 1924; O.B.E., June, 1932.

THOMAS, EDWARD FRANCIS, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th June, 1880).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; served in police dept., Feb., 1907, to April, 1908; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Sept., 1912; ditto, and dep. commr., salt, abkari, etc., Nov., 1912; sec. to commr., salt, abkari and sep. rev., Oct., 1915; on milty. duty from May, 1917, to Jan., 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; offg. director of industries, Madras, Nov., 1922; collr. and dist. mag., March, 1925; collr. of Madras, Dec., 1926; collr. and dist. magte., June, 1928; offg. 3rd member, bd. of rev., Madras, July, 1931; ditto (provl. sub.), Sept., 1932; offg. 2nd member, bd. of rev., Madras, Mar., 1933; offg. 1st member, ditto, July, 1933; confd. as 3rd member, ditto, and 2nd member (provl. subs.), Aug., 1933; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Madras, May, 1934; 1st member, bd. of rev. (provl.), Jan., 1935; subst., Feb., 1936; C.S.I., June, 1935; ret'd., Feb., 1938.

THOMAS, FREDERICK WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A., Hon. Ph.D. (Munich), late India Office.—Educ. at Birmingham, and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; elected Fellow of Trinity, 1892; apptd. asst. librarian, 1898; librarian, Dec., 1902; has (with Prfr. Cowell) translated from the Sanskrit 'The Harsa-Carita of Bana' (Oriental Translation Fund, N.S.); 'India Office Library Publications' 'Catalogue of the Royal Society

Collection' (in conjunction with Mr. Tawney), 1903; 'The Aufrecht Collection, 1908'; 'Catalogue of the Library of the India Office,' Vol. I; Supplement 2, 1895-1909 (joint-author); other publications; retired, Sept., 1927 on appt. to Boden professorship of Sanskrit in Univ. of Oxford; C.I.E., Jan., 1928.

THOMAS, GEOFFREY DAVID IVOR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 21st Aug., 1905).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

THOMAS, GEORGE ARTHUR, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th May, 1877).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 30th Nov., 1900, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge; asst. collr. of customs, Bombay (Imperial customs service), May, 1906; ditto, Madras, Jan., 1910; asst. collr., Bombay, Nov., 1911; temp. dep. sec. to govt., rev. and finl. depts., Bombay, Nov., 1915; sec. to govt., genl. educt. marine and eccl. depts., Oct., 1916; also sec. to indigenous industries committee; collr. and mag., Feb., 1920; secty. to govt., rev. dept., April, 1922, and Dec., 1924; ditto, gen. educt., etc., depts., Dec., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1925; offg. chief sec. to govt., rev. dept., Bombay, Jan., 1926; confd., April, 1926; collr. of Bombay and supt. of stamps, Jan., 1927; offg. commr. in Sind, April, 1928; confd., Sept., 1929; member, govr.'s exec. council, Bombay, Apl., 1931; chairman, re-organisation comtee.; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

THOMAS, GEORGE HECTOR, Barr., Judicial Dept., United Provs. (chief judge, ch. ct., Oudh) (b. 23rd June, 1884).—Apptd. govt. advocate, ch. court, Lucknow, Nov., 1925; judge, ch. ct., Oudh, May, 1934; chief judge, ditto, July, 1938.

THOMAS, HERBERT PERCIVAL, C.I.E., B.Sc., M.A.I.E.E., Public Works Dept., Punjab (ch. engr., elec.) (b. 12th Dec., 1879).—Apptd. suptg. engr., engrg. circle, Lahore, 16th Oct., 1926; offg. ch. engr., hydro-elec., Apl., 1929; ch. engr., elec. bch., Apl., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1933.

THOMAS, JOHN HERBERT, F.I.A., Govt. of India, Commerce Dept. (supt. of insur.) (b. 21st Feb., 1895).—Apptd. to home civil service, 1914; services loaned to govt. of India, dept. of commerce as supt. of insurance, June, 1938.

THOMAS, REGINALD, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th Nov., 1874).—Joined the service, 20th April, 1909; registrar, rly. board, Jan., 1917; asst. secty., ditto, April, 1924; personnel officer, N.W. Rly., Nov., 1925; ret'd., Nov., 1929.

THOMAS, ROBERT HENRY, C.S.I., D.S.O., Brigadier, R.E., ret'd., late Survey of India (b. 23rd April, 1877).—First commn. dated 21st June, 1896; apptd. to survey of India dept. as asst. supt., 17th July, 1901; dep. supt., March, 1910; temp. reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to June, 1919; dir., Jan., 1926; surveyor-genl., Dec., 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1933; C.S.I., Jan. 1934.

THOMAS, VINCENT EUSTACE, A.M.I.Struct.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 16th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service as temp. engr., G.I.P. rly., 17th Aug., 1916; resdt. engr., May, 1922; exec. engr., G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1935.

THOMASSON, JAMES, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (resdt. elec. engr.).—Apptd. resdt. elec. engr. (temp.) 21st Sept., 1926; ditto (permt.), Dec., 1929.

THOMPSON, Miss AMY, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (inspectress of schools, Central Provs.) (b. 5th Nov., 1883).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1915, as head mistress, govt. girls' high school, Amraoti; inspectress of schools, Jan., 1923.

THOMPSON, BRIAN BELL, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 16th June, 1898).—Joined the service, 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., March, 1925; supt. of police, Central India Agency, April, 1927; supt., Nov., 1932; awarded Indian Police medal, July, 1936.

THOMPSON, CLARENCE EDWARD, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service, 20th April, 1904; asst. engr., Oct., 1907; resdt. engr., Nov., 1911; dist. engr., June, 1924; divl. engr., Nov., 1926; offg. dep. ch. engr. (maintenance), G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1933; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

THOMPSON, CLAUDE MATTHEW, Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Survey of India (b. 21st Sept., 1883).—First commn. dated 21st Jan., 1903; apptd. asst. supt., trigl. survey, 1st Nov., 1907; 2nd asst. dir. of surveys, Bengal and Assam, Nov., 1913; temp. reverted to mil. duty from Jan., 1915, to Mar., 1919; dep. supt., Sept., 1922; asst. surveyor-genl., Jan., 1926; supt., Apl., 1928; offg. dir., eastern circle, May, 1932; offg. dir., variously from May, 1934; confd., Sept., 1937; ret'd., Sept., 1938.

THOMPSON, COLIN HUSTWICK, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 20th Mar., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

THOMPSON, EDWARD HENRY, M.C., Office of High Commr. for India (hr. executive officer, store dept.) (b. 30th Oct., 1889).—Apptd. second class clerk, 30th June, 1908; on mil. service from Jan., 1916; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; attached Indian del., Imp. econ. conf., July to Sept., 1932; hr. exec. officer, Dec., 1935.

THOMPSON, ERIC BERTRAM, M.B.E., Indian Posts and Tels. (dep. p.m.g.) (b. 21st July, 1887).—Joined as proby. supt., 25th Apl., 1908; confd., Dec., 1909; on depts. to I.E.F., Aug., 1914, to Apl., 1919; deputy p.m.g., July, 1935.

THOMPSON, JOSEPH HERBERT, M.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 9th Mar., 1898).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1923; arrived, 12th Dec., 1923, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. m.g., Nov., 1924; offg. asst. commr., D.I. Khan, July, 1926; city magte., Peshawar, Dec., 1926; dist. magte., ditto, Jan., 1928; asst. commr., Nowshera, Sept., 1929; divl. and sess. judge, Peshawar, Aug., 1930; dist. and sess. judge, Peshawar and Hazara, Aug., 1931; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Oct., 1932; secty. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Oct., 1934; resdt., Jaipur, Mar., 1938; pol. agent, E. Rajputana States, Nov., 1938.

THOMPSON, MALCOLM STUART HAUGHTON, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (princl., govt. coll., Madras) (b. 20th Mar., 1885).—Joined the service, 6th Jan., 1910; asst. inspr. of schools, Dec., 1911; inspr., Sept., 1922; dist. edul. offcr., Feb., 1923; offg. princpl., govt. training coll., Rajahmundry, Nov., 1927; offg. inspr. schools, Coorg and Bangalore, Feb., 1936; princpl., govt. Muhammadan Coll., Madras, Feb., 1937.

THOMPSON, SAMUEL PERCY, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 16th Dec., 1902).—Educ. at St. Olave's and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and magte.; sub. collr. and jt. magte., Nov., 1928; asst. secty., Indian franchise comee., Jan., 1932.

THOMPSON, WILLIAM PERCY, M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suplg. engr., Punjab) (b. 7th Jan., 1884).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, Oct., 1905; asst. engr., Nov., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. supt. engr., May, 1932; ditto (provl. perm.), Jan., 1935; confd., July, 1935.

THOMSON, ALEXANDER WELSH, O.B.E., India Office (ch. exec. offcr., mil. dept.) (b. 26th Dec., 1878).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 12th Nov., 1900; temply attached to mil. dept. from Dec., 1915; minor staff clerk, April, 1918; staff clerk (actg.), Nov., 1919; dep. reading clerk to council, Feb., 1920; to Feb., 1923; higher exec. officer, June, 1922; suptg. exec. officer, June, 1924; asst. acct.-genl., Dec., 1934; assessor of inc. tax, from Dec., 1934, to Dec., 1937; actg. chief exec. offcr., mil. dept., Nov., 1937; confd., Jan., 1938; O.B.E., June, 1938.

THOMSON, DAVID, M.A., B.Sc., Ph.D., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 29th Mar., 1879).—Joined the service, 28th Feb., 1911, as prof., Cotton Coll., Gauhati; inspr. of schools, Oct., 1922; princpl., Murarichand Coll., Sylhet, Nov., 1923; offg. dir. of public instrn., Assam, Feb., 1925; princpl. Cotton Coll., Gauhati, Dec., 1926; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

THOMSON, ERIC SCOTT, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 11th Dec., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 25th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., April, 1926; confd., July, 1933.

THOMSON, GEORGE, late Indian State Ryis. (b. 3rd July, 1882).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 28th Feb., 1907; dist. loco. supt., Sept., 1913; offg. dep. loco. supt., Nov., 1923; confd., Feb., 1930; loco. and carr. and wagon supt., July, 1930; on spl. duty with rly. bd., Oct., 1930-Feb., 1931; dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. rly., Jan., 1932; offg. ch. mechl. engr., May, 1934; ret'd., Jan., 1938.

THOMSON, JAMES, India Office (hr. exec. offcr., mil. dept.) (b. 1st Apl., 1895).—Apptd. exec. offcr., 16th Aug., 1927; actg. hr. exec. offcr., Mar., 1935; confd., Feb., 1936; actg. asst. information offcr., Oct., 1936, and again, Dec., 1937 to Feb., 1938; actg. inform. offcr., Oct., 1937, to Dec., 1937; sec. admin. side, mil. dept., Feb., to Oct., 1938.

THOMSON, JOHN STUART, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th Aug., 1888).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 18th Nov., 1912, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. asst. to commr., Lahore, March, 1914; on mil. duty from March, 1915; att'd. to civil admin., Mesopotamia, from June, 1917, to Feb., 1922; asst. pol. offr., Hillah, Feb., 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), April, 1925; development commr., N.W.F.P., May, 1931; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Sept., 1931; secty. to govt. (rev.), N.W.F.P., Apl., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1935; ret'd., July, 1937.

THOMSON, RONALD ORD CAMPBELL, A.M.I.C.E., M.C., Indian State Railways (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 11th Dec., 1892).—Asst. exec. engr., 27th Nov., 1919; exec. engr., Apl., 1936.

THOMSON, The Rev. THOMAS ALBERT, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain Bengal) (b. 6th July, 1884).—Joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1913; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1915; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1925.

THOMSON-GLOVER, Lt.-Col. J. W. (see Glover, Lt.-Col. J. W. T.).

THORNE, HAROLD LIONEL, Indian State Ryis. (divl. transpn. offcr., N.W. rly.) (b. 23rd Nov., 1886).—Joined the service Sept., 1905; asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1919; divl. transpn. offcr., May, 1935.

THORNE, JOHN ANDERSON, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (secty. to govr.-genl., public dept.) (b. 17th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; sec. to board of revenue, land revenue, March, 1920; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Dec., 1922; ditto, May, 1924; confd., Aug., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1931; secty. to govt., rev. dept., Madras, Nov., 1931; offg. jt. secty. to govt. of India, home dept., June, 1935; addl. ditto, Oct., 1935; jt. secty., ditto, Apl., 1936; again, Nov., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938; secty. to govr.-genl. (public) Apl., 1938.

THORNTON, FRANCIS RUTHVEN, M.C., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Bombay) (b. 5th Aug., 1884).—First commn., 25th Feb., 1921; civil surg., Bombay, Feb., 1925; ditto, Dharwar Nov., 1937; ditto, Poona, June, 1938.

THORNTON, HUGH AYLMER, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 10th May, 1872).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 30th Nov., 1896, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; supt., Northern Shan States, Sept., 1909; dep. commr., and ditto, Nov., 1911; C.I.E., June, 1916; supt., and pol. officer, Southern Shan States, April, 1920; commr., Jan., 1922; C.S.I., Jan., 1930; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

THORNTON, NORMAN, M.I.E.E., Public Works Dept., Punjab (exec. engr.) (b. 5th Mar., 1894).—Joined the service, 22nd Feb., 1934; exec. engr., Mar., 1934; offg. dep. ch. engr., Mar., 1935.

THORPE, ARTHUR FANSHAWE, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 13th Nov., 1878).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1902; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; under-sec. to govt., P.W. dept., Bombay, April, 1912, to Feb., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., Aug., 1924; confd., June, 1926; retd., July, 1930.

THORPE, WILLIAM ALFRED CHARLES, O.B.E., late Indian State Railways (N.W. Rly.).—Joined the service, 30th Oct., 1903, as asst. loco. supt., state rlys.; dist. loco. supt., April, 1915; on mil. duty from March, 1916, to Feb., 1919 (mentd. in despatches); offg. dep. chief mechanical engr., July, 1925; offg. chief ditto, Feb., 1926; retd., April, 1929.

THOULESS, KENNETH JAMES, M.A. (Mech. Sc. Hons., Cantab.), Indian Posts and Tels. (director, S. and B. circle) (b. 13th May, 1900).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1923, as asst. div. engr., Tels.; div. engr., Oct., 1932; dir., S. and B. circle, Aug., 1933.

THRUPP, WILFRED GRAHAM, late Indian Police (b. 26th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 28th Nov., 1912; confd., Oct., 1915; supt., Jan., 1926; prin., police training coll., Sardah, Feb., 1934; retd., Nov., 1937.

THUBRON, JOHN BROWN SYDNEY, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 26th Aug., 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1901, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1909; chairman of the Aden Port Trust and of exec. cttee. of the Aden settlt. from May, 1912; C.I.E., Sept., 1918; chairman, Karachi Port Trust, Nov., 1919; actg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1921; confd. Sept., 1925; offg. chief engr. Dec., 1926; confd., Nov., 1928; retd., Jan., 1934.

THUILLIER, LESLIE CARDEW, Lt.-Col., I.A. (retd.), late Survey of India Dept. (b. 7th Oct., 1877).—First commn. dated 20th Jan., 1897; apptd. to survey of India, Sept., 1901; dep. supt., April, 1910; on mil. service in Mesopotamia, Nov., 1917 to March, 1919; offg. supt., northern circle, March 1924; war service, North West Frontier, 1897-98, Tirah Expedy. Force, medal, two clasps; supt. dir., Oct., 1925; dir. (temp.), Jan., 1926; retd., Oct., 1932.

THURSFIELD, Rev. GERALD ARTHUR RICHARD, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt., Burma (b. 13th April, 1886).—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, 18th Jan., 1917; on mil. service from 17th July, 1917, to 17th Oct., 1919; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1927; offg. archdeacon, June, 1935; retd. Feb., 1938.

THURSTON, EDWARD THOMAS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 4th June, 1889).—Educ. at St. Paul's, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Oct., 1917, to Jan., 1919; city mag., April, 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1920; offg. sess. and sub. judge, July, 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1923; sess. and sub-judge, Nov., 1926; retd., Oct., 1929.

TIDY, DOUGLAS RAMSAY, late Indian Police (b. 13th Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 16th Nov., 1927; retd., Oct., 1933.

TIKA RAM MISRA, RAI BAHADUR, PANDIT, M.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., United Provs. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 1st Feb., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1906; sub-judge, Oct., 1925; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1934.

TILNEY-BASSETT, Rev. HUGH FRANCIS EMRA, M.A. (Oxon), Indian Eccles. Estabt (junr. chaplain) (b. 16th Jan., 1901).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, Bengal, 12th Aug., 1929; confd., Nov., 1931.

TIMMS, ARCHIBALD BARTON, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th June, 1883).—Joined as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; retd., Aug., 1934.

TIMS, BASIL DUNCAN, India Office (staff officer for currency duties, fin. dept.) (b. 2nd Aug., 1898).—Apptd. clerical officr., 19th Sept., 1921; higher clerical officr., Apl., 1930; staff officr. for currency duties, finl. dept., Dec., 1937.

TIN, MAUNG (A), Indian C.S. (dep. secty., agric. and for. dept., Burma) (b. 5th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ. and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., Burma, educn. dept., Dec., 1934; dep. secty., agric. and for. dept., Nov., 1937.

TIN, MAUNG SEIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 15th June, 1904).—Educ. at University of Rangoon and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived Nov., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

TIN TOON, MAUNG A. (see Toon, Maung Tin).

TIN TUT, MAUNG (see Tut, Maung Tin).

TIN, U PE MAUNG, M.A., B.Litt., Indian Educl. Service (b. 24th April, 1888).—Apptd. to the I.E.S. as lecturer in Oriental Studies, Univ. coll., Rangoon, from 1st Sept., 1924.

TIN, U. PO (O) late Burma Civil Service (b. 13th Oct., 1879).—Served with co-op. societies dept., from 1st April, 1899; dy. regr., co-op. societies, Dec., 1927; offg. jt. regr., ditto, July, 1930; confd., Mar., 1932; regr., ditto, Aug., 1933; retd., Aug., 1935.

TINKER, HAROLD, B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 8th Dec., 1878).—Joined the service as prof., training coll., Allahabad, 12th Oct., 1915; princpl., training coll., Agra, July, 1920; offg. inspr. of schools, Feb., 1929; princpl. training coll., Allahabad, Sept., 1930; retd., Mar., 1934.

TINKLER, FREDERICK CHARLES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 3rd Apl., 1915).—Educ. at March Grammar Sch. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn. 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 7th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

TINSLEY, WILLIAM STEWART, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, Oct., 1914, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1923.

TINT, MAUNG BA, B.A., Indian C.S. (under-secty., fin. dept., Burma) (b. 6th Nov., 1908).—Educ. at St. Peter's High Sch. and Kelly High Sch., Mandalay, Rangoon Univ., and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty., fin. dept., Nov., 1937.

TINT, SOE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 5th Nov., 1910).—Educated at Govt. High School, Rangoon, Rangoon University and Wadham College, Oxford; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

TIPPER, GEORGE HOWLETT, M.A. (Cantab), F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 26th Feb., 1881).—Apptd. asst. supt., geological survey, 6th Dec., 1903; curator, geological museum, Calcutta, Aug., 1910; apptd. to I.A.R.O., May, 1915, to Dec., 1919; supt., Jan., 1920; retd., Sept., 1929; mineral adviser to H.C. for India, Nov., 1928.

TIPSON, KENNETH GEORGE, Indian Police (b. 29th May, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 15th Nov., 1925; offg. supt., Mar., 1933; services placed at disposal of Benares State, Apl., 1937.

TIRUMALAI AYYANGAR, MALUR SRINIVASA AYYANGAR, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 1st Dec., 1897).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 1st June, 1920; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1921; exec. engr., Jan., 1930.

TIRUVENKATA ACHARIYAR, CUMBAKONAM RANGASWAMI, Diwan Bahadur, late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. Nov., 1868).—Apptd. Madras City civil court judge, 18th Feb., 1912; dist. and sessions judge, Jan., 1920; chief judge, court of small causes, Madras, July, 1921; acting judge, high ct., July, 1927; addl. judge, ditto, Feb., 1928; retd., Jan., 1929.

TODD, HERBERT JOHN, Indian Political Service (b. 15th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Burma, 4th Dec., 1913; confd., Nov., 1914; in milty. employ from 1st Mar., 1917, to 8th Jan., 1919; under civil administration, Bagdad, from Jan., 1919, to July, 1921; asst. pol. agent, Sibi, Oct., 1921; ditto, Kalat, 5th July, 1922; pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1927; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, Oct., 1932; pol. agent, E. Rajputana states, Oct., 1935; presdt., Council of State, Bharatpur (in addn.), from May, 1936, to Aug., 1936.

TODD, JOHN, B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 3rd May, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th Jan., 1921; dep. consvr., May, 1925; retd., Apl., 1937.

TODD, NORMAN ALBERT, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys (N.W. Rly.) (b. 6th Feb., 1877).—Joined the service, 24th June, 1899; officiated variously between July, 1909, and Dec., 1914, as asst. traffic supt.; confd. as ditto, Dec., 1914; O.B.E., Sept., 1921; asst. dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; transportation officer, July, 1927; senr. asst. comml., Mar., 1931; retd., Feb., 1932.

TODHUNTER, SIR CHARLES GEORGE, K.C.S.I., F.R.Hist.S., F.S.S., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Aldenham Gr. Sch. and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888;

arrived, 26th Nov., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. and head asst. collr., asst. sec. to govt., dep. registrar, and registrar, high court; dep. commr., salt, &c., rev., offg., Sept., 1896; confd., Nov., 1897; sec. to commr., salt, &c., rev., offg., Jan., 1898, confd., Oct., 1900; sub-collr. and joint mag., June, 1903; presdt., Central Prov. excise committee, Dec., 1903, to Nov., 1904; on special duty to advise on excise arrangements in the Central India states and the Malwa opium agency, Jan., 1905; sec., excise commn., Oct., 1905; collr. of customs, Madras (impl. customs service), Oct., 1906; employed in fin. dept., govt. of India, as inspr.-gen., excise and salt, Dec., 1909, to April, 1912; collr. and dist. mag., Apr., 1912; prest., life-saving appliances cttee., Oct., 1913, to Feb., 1914; protector of emigrants, June, 1915; sec. to govt., judl. dept., July, 1915; ditto, local and municipal, &c., depts., Jan., 1916; acting member, bd. of rev., Nov., 1916; confd., July, 1917; chief sec. to govt., Feb., 1919; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, April, 1919; member, ditto, Aug., 1919, to April, 1924; C.S.I., Jan., 1920; K.C.S.I., June, 1921; president, taxation enquiry cttee., Oct., 1924; retd., May, 1925.

TOLLEMACHE, HAROLD VINCENT, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 14th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Feb., 1927.

TOLLER, GEORGE GORDON TAYLOR, O.B.E., V.D., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., Burma Rlys.) (b. 23rd Feb., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 6th Jan., 1910; ditto (perm.), July, 1911; on milty. duty from 18th Dec., 1914, to 18th Dec., 1919; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; dist. engr., April, 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1927; ditto, Irrawaddy bridge constrn. Nov., 1932; exec. engr., ditto, July, 1933; offg. dep. ch. engr., Mar., 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1936; presdt., rly. bd., Burma, Mar., 1938.

TOLLINTON, HUGH PHILLIPS, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 19th Aug., 1904).—Educ. at Radley, Balliol Coll., Oxford, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1932, and again, Apl., 1933; asst. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1934; ditto, Tank, Feb., 1935; ditto, Charsadda, Nov., 1936; home secty., govt., N.W.F.P., June, 1938; secty., ditto devel. depts., July, 1938; confd., Sept., 1938.

TOLSON, KENNETH, Indian Police (assi. supt.) (b. 11th Nov., 1910).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 3rd Dec., 1931; awarded Indian police medal, 1935; confd., Jan., 1934; a.d.c. to govr. of Bengal, May, 1938.

TOMKINS, THOMAS LESLIE, India Audit Office (exec. officer) (b. 4th Dec., 1889).—Apptd. clerical officer, 29th Mar., 1922; exec. officer, Oct., 1926.

TOMKINS, WILLIAM DOUGLAS, O.B.E., India Office (principal, economic and overseas dept.) (b. 29th July, 1882).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept.,

24th Jan., 1902; transf. to rev. and stat. dept., Mar., 1906; minor staff clerk, May, 1914; staff clerk, June, 1919; sec., Indian delegations at Intl. Labour Conference Genoa, 1920, and Intl. Conference on Transit and Communications, Barcelona, 1921; administrative officer, April, 1922; pte. sec. to delegate for India at Intl. Economic Conference, Genoa, 1922, and Hague Conference, 1922; delegate for India at Intl. Conference on Customs Formalities, Geneva, Oct., 1923; pte. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, Aug., 1924; actg. principal, Dec., 1927; confd., July, 1929; secty. to Indian delegations to Assembly of League of Nations, 1934 to 1936; O.B.E., May, 1937.

TOMS, FRAZER WOODLAND, late Indian Police, Punjab (b. 22nd Mar., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1907; asst. supt., Oct., 1908; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1913; supt., Sept., 1915; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1928; confd. (prov. subst.), Sept., 1930; confd., July, 1931; retd., July, 1938.

TOOGOOD, GEORGE WATTS, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt.) (b. 20th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 18th Dec., 1920 as asst. loco. supt.; dist. loco. supt., E.B. rly. (prov.), Aug., 1934.

TOOKE, COLIN, Indian Police (asst. supt., Burma) (b. 30th Sept., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Burma, Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

TOON, J. CHAN (see Chan Toon, J.).

TOON, MAUNG TIN, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 26th Sept., 1896).—Educ. at Norris Coll., Rangoon, and Cambridge Univ.; joined the service, 1st July, 1922; arrived, 7th Sept., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1925; confd., May, 1932.

TOPLIS, ARTHUR ROBERT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th Nov., 1885).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; on settlt. duty from Oct., 1915; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1917; mag. and collr., July, 1924; dep. commr., Oct., 1925; offg. commr., June, 1930; and again, May, 1932; retd., Jan., 1936.

TORICK, AMEER ALI, Judl. dept., Bengal (judge, high ct., Calcutta).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Calcutta, 28th Nov., 1931.

TOSTEVIN, FRED PERCY, O.B.E., M.A. (Oxon), Indian Educl. Service (principal, King Edward Coll., Amraoti) (b. 29th Oct., 1886).—Joined the service, 22nd Jan., 1912; prof. of English, Morris Coll., Nagpur, Feb., 1912; dep. dir. of public instrn., Central Provs., July, 1921; principal, Morris Coll., Nagpur, April, 1922; principal, King Edward Coll., Amraoti, April, 1923; O.B.E., June, 1935.

TOTTENHAM, SIR ALEXANDER ROBERT LOFTUS, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st July, 1873).—Educ. at Clifton and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in

Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sec. to commrs. of land rev., July, 1906, to April, 1911; sub-collr. and jt. mag., April, 1911; collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1916; temp. addl. sec. to govt., rev. dept., April, 1919; commr. of income tax, Nov., 1922; senior member, central board of revenue, Nov., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; Kt., Jan., 1931; retd., June, 1933.

TOTTENHAM, CLIVE GORDON, Barr., late Indian Police Service (Madras) (b. 6th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service, 10th Dec., 1911; asst. supt., 2nd Nov., 1912; awarded King's police medal, Dec., 1921; offg. dist. supt., May, 1925; confd., July, 1926; retd., Jan., 1930.

TOTTENHAM, SIR GEORGE RICHARD FREDERICK, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (b. 18th Nov., 1890).—Educ. at Harrow and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1914, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. to resdt., Travancore and Cochin, Sept., 1917; sub. collr. and jt. mag., June, 1919; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1921; offg. dep. sec. to govt., March, 1924; offg. dep. sec., govt. of India, army dept., 1924; addl. ditto, Aug., 1926; offg. ditto, Sept., 1926; dep. sec. ditto, Nov., 1926; C.I.E., June, 1930; addl. secty. to govt., finance dept., Madras; secty. to govt. of India, Army dept., Apl., 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1936; member, council of state, Oct., 1936; knighted, May, 1937; presdt. of council, Bharatpur, Mar., 1938.

TOWER, JOHN ALEXANDER, Indian State Rlys. (ch. transportation supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 10th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service, 22nd Dec., 1911; asst. traffic supt., Dec., 1914; dist. ditto, Feb., 1919; divl. traffic manager, Nov., 1922; offg. dep. agent, Sept., 1927; offg. dir. of fin. rly. board, March, 1929; dep. agent, July, 1929; ch. transportation supt. (prov.), July, 1932.

TOWLE, JOHN HOWARD, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 2nd Aug., 1878).—Joined the service, 12th March, 1919; dir. of public instrn., Feb., 1922; retd., Aug., 1933.

TOWNEND, HERBERT PATRICK VICTOR, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal) (b. March, 1887).—Educ. at King's Sch., Canterbury, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., finl. dept., Aug., 1917; jt. mag. and dep. collr. and dir. of civil supplies, Oct., 1918; collr. dir.-gen. of comml. intellge., Jan., 1920; dep. sec. to govt. of Bengal, Finance, Commerce and Marine Depts., April, 1923; sec., Indian coal cttee., Oct., 1924, to April, 1925; mag. and collr., May, 1925; dep. commr., April, 1926; offg. secty., agri and indus. and pub. wks. depts., Bengal, Nov., 1930; offg. sec. local self-govt. dept., Apl., 1932; confd., Sept., 1932; offg. rural dev. commr., Feb., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; spec. offcr., home dept., govt. of Bengal, Oct., 1937; confd. as commr. divn., Mar., 1938.

TOWNSEND, CREWE ARMAND HAMILTON, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 5th June, 1874).—Educ. at Shrewsbury and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 7th Dec., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

and settlt. officer; offg. dir. of land records, May, 1911; dep. commr., Nov., 1914; dir. of agric. and industries, Nov., 1914; was also controller of munitions, Punjab circle; dir. of industries, May, 1918; ditto, and dir. of civil supplies, June, 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; offg. commr., May, 1923; offg. finl. commr. and secty. to govt. rev. dept., March, 1927; pres., commee. on co-operation, Madras, Aug., 1927; commr., Mar., 1928; finl. commr. (revenue), May, 1928; retd., Nov., 1931.

TOWNSEND, FREDERICK OWEN, D.S.O., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 1st Mar., 1878).—Joined the service, 5th Sept 1905; asst. engr., Oct., 1910; on mil. duty from 25th March, 1915, to 4th May, 1919; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; retd., Sept., 1931.

TOWNSEND, REGINALD STEPHEN, M.C., M.D., Col., I.M.S. (inspr.-genl., civil hospils. N.W.F.P.) (b. 10th Nov., 1882).—First commn. dated 1st Feb., 1908; in mil. employ to 31st May, 1911, and again from 1st Oct., 1914, to 23rd Mar., 1921; civil surg., Apl., 1922; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., United Provs., Dec., 1934; inspr.-genl., civil hosp. and dir. p.h., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1937.

TOWNSHEND, EDMUND GEORGE, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E.E., Indian State Rlys. dist. loco. supt., Burma Rlys. (b. 5th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., N.W. Rly., 24th Jan., 1914; asst. rolling stock officer, Nov., 1926; divl. ditto, Sept., 1927; transferred to Burma Rlys., 29th Jan., 1930; dist. loco supt., Feb., 1930.

TOYNE, WILLIAM, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 26th July, 1882).—Joined the service, 14th Dec., 1905, as asst. loco. supt.; asst. rolling stock officer., Oct., 1924; divl. mechl. engr., N.W. rly. (prov.), 'Aug., 1934; perm., July, 1937; retd., July, 1937.

TREASURE, EDWARD SHEPPARD, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 4th July, 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Madras, 22nd Nov., 1934; confd., Jan., 1937.

TREASURE, PATRICK MOORE, Indian Police (b. 21st Aug., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on probn., Dec., 1936.

TREGENZA, EDWARD LESLIE, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 15th May, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 11th March, 1922; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., March, 1928.

TRENCH, Rev. ALBERT CHARLES, M.C., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 25th Jan., 1884).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Madras, 6th Oct., 1920; junr. chaplain, 6th Oct., 1922; senr. chaplain, 6th Oct., 1922; senr. chaplain, 29th Dec., 1928; retd., Oct., 1938.

TRENCH, WILLIAM LAUNCELOT CROSBIE, C.I.E., B.A.I., M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 22nd July, 1881).—Joined, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; princpl. coll. of civil engineering, Poona, Nov., 1922; dep. secty. to govt. P.W.D. (irrgn. and rlys.), Bombay, Oct., 1928; offg. supt. engr., Mar., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; ch. engr., June, 1935; chief engr. and secty. to govt., p.w.d., Sind, Apl., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1936; retd., July, 1936.

TRENCH, CHENEVIX (see Chenevix-Trench).

TRESHAM, CECIL HENRY, V.D., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 3rd Apl., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1903; extra asst. supt., June, 1911; supt., Oct., 1932.

TRESIDDER, ALFRED GEDDES, C.I.E., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 15th Dec., 1881).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1908; offg. surg. to govt., Bombay, March, 1924; reverted to milty. employ from 17th Dec., 1924, to 31st March, 1925; surg. to govt. of Bombay, April, 1925; C.I.E., June, 1927; offg. civil surg., May, 1929; retd., Aug., 1931.

TRESTON, MAURICE LAWRENCE, F.R.G.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Burma Medl. Dept. (civil surg.) (b. 9th Feb., 1891).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1914; civil surg., June, 1923; asst. medl. supt. and police surg., Rangoon gen. hosp., March, 1926; supt., Dufferin hosp., Rangoon, Feb., 1928; gynaecologist, genl. hosp., Rangoon, June, 1934.

TREVASKIS, HUGH KENNEDY, O.B.E., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 14th June, 1882).—Educ. at Marlborough Sch., and King's Coll., Cambridge (honours degree in math., 1st class); apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 29th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. luty, from July, 1917, to Dec., 1918; O.B.E., June, 1919; dir. of land records, Punjab, April, 1923; retd., Oct., 1930.

TREVELYAN, HUMPHREY, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 27th Nov., 1905).—Educ. at Lancing Coll., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, October, 1929; arrived, November, 1929, and served as asst. coll. and mag., Madras; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Mar., 1931; under-secty. to A.G.G. in Central India, Nov., 1932; offg. dep. opium agent in Malwa, Apl., 1933; under-secty., pol. dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1934; offg. dep. secty. ditto, Apl., 1935; guardian to Maharajah of Gwalior, Nov., 1935; secty. to Resident and C.C., Coorg, Mar., 1937.

TREVOR, Sir CHARLES GERALD, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 28th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 24th Nov., 1903; dep. consvr., Nov., 1911; offg.; asst. inspr.-gen. of forests, Nov., 1917, to March, 1918; consvr., March, 1924; vice-pres. and profr. of forestry, Dehra Dun, Oct., 1926; on depn. with internl. congress of forestry, Stockholm, July, 1929; chief consvr., Feb., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; offg. inspr.-genl., and presdt., Forest Research inst., Dehra Dun, Nov., 1933; confd., July, 1934; on depn. with fourth Brit. Empire forestry conf. S. Africa, Aug., 1935; knighted, Feb., 1937; retd., Apl., 1938.

TREVOR, DIGBY BRUCE, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th Aug., 1881).—Apptd. asst. traffic supt., Feb., 1906; dist. traffic supt., N.W. Rly., April, 1912; dep. traffic mgr., ditto, April, 1918; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; divl. supt. (prov.), Oct., 1924; confd., Oct., 1927; offg. ch. comml. manager, April, 1928; and again, April, 1929; retd., May, 1933.

TRIBHUMAN PRASAD, SINGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 13th May, 1913).—Educ. at the Banka H.E. Sch., Bhagalpur, Patna Univ., and London School of Economics; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

TRIGG, CHARLES THOMAS, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 15th Mar., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; on depn. to Forest research institute, Dehra Dun, from 14th July, 1922; dep. consvr., Jan., 1932.

TRIMMING, GEORGE AUGUSTUS RODERICK, Indian State Ry's. (ch. mechl. engr. E.I. Ry.) (b. 24th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 8th Oct., 1913; works manager, April, 1923; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., Sept., 1928; confd., Mar., 1934; offg. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. rly., Mar., 1935; confd., July, 1935.

TRIMURTI, NARAYANASWAMI, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 15th July, 1900).—Arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1925; transferred from Central Provs., 27th June, 1926; asst. consvr., Madras, July, 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1929.

TRIPATHI, SHYAMA CHANDRA, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (princl., Ravenshaw Coll.,) (b. 1st Mar., 1893).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1920; prof. of physics, Feb., 1921; offg. dep. dir. of public instn., Oct., 1930; confd., Aug., 1933; offg. princpl., Ravenshaw Coll., Feb., 1936; confd., Aug., 1936.

TRIVEDI, CHANDULAL MADHAVLAL, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 2nd July, 1893).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and at St. John's Coll. Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1916; arrived, Dec., 1917, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1924; offg. regr. co-op. societies and dir. of industries, Central Provs., June, 1925; confd., Nov., 1926; dep. commr., March, 1927; O.B.E., Jan., 1931; dep. sec., home dept., govt. of India, May, 1932; offg. jt. secty., ditto, Apl., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1935; offg. chief secty. to govt., C.P., Oct., 1937.

TRIVEDI, NARAYAN VISHWANATH, Rao Bahadur, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 5th Sept., 1886).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 18th Jan., 1909, to 26th Aug., 1914; dep. supt., Aug., 1914; employed under dir., intell. bureau, from Oct., 1923, to Ja., 1929; promoted to I.P.D. as supt. from 24th Jan., 1929.

TRIVEDI, RAM NARAYAN, M.A., late Judl. Dept., Central Provs. (b. 9th Dec., 1878).—Joined the service as temp. extra asst. commr., July, 1901; confd., March, 1903; sub-judge, Jan., 1908; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1933; retd., Dec., 1933.

TRIVEDI, RAMANLAL SHANKERLAL, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 20th Nov., 1905).—Educ. at Bombay University and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1932.

TRIVEDI, RATILAL VITHAL DAS, M.A., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 13th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service, 11th Apl., 1921;

asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, Aug., 1922; asst. dir. of audit, United Provs., Sept., 1928; currency officer, Cawnpore, Dec., 1928; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Sept., 1931, and again June, 1937; examr., outside audit dept. Bombay, Mar., 1935; examr., local audit dept., Bombay, Feb., 1936; dep. acct.-genl., Central Rev., Oct., 1937.

TROTMAN EWART WASHINGTON, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 10th July, 1895).—Educ. at the Rich Sch., Gloucester; joined the service 1st Nov., 1923; arrived, 28th Oct., 1923, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. dep. secty. to govt., home and eccl. depts., June, 1927; offg. under secty. to ditto, Aug., 1927; confd., Oct., 1928; offg. secty. to ditto, June, 1929; offg. collr. and mag., April, 1931; confd., Nov., 1935.

TROTMAN, Rev. LIONEL WILLIAM, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 12th Aug., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, Bengal, 19th June, 1930; junr. chaplain, June, 1932.

TROTTER, HUGH, Indian Forest Service (asst. forest economist, forest research institute and coll., Dehra Dun) (b. 16th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, 30th Dec., 1913; on mil. service from 28th Nov., 1914, to 9th March, 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1918; asst. forest economist, Sept. 1921.

TROUSDELL, HENRY FRANK, Printing and Stationery dept. (manager, govt. of India press) (b. 14th July, 1891).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st Nov., 1916; asst. manager, govt. of India press, Jan., 1926; offg. dep. contr., forms, Nov., 1927; confd., Dec., 1930; offg. manager, govt. of India press, Nov., 1931; confd., Oct., 1932.

TRUSCOTT, ROBERT RODGER BATE, Indian Police (b. 12th Nov., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Madras, on probn., 14th Nov., 1935.

TRUIWEIN, PERCY WALLACE, B.A., late Burma Commission (b. 30th Jan., 1883).—Apptd. offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922; confd., Aug., 1923; retd., Jan., 1938.

TUBBS, Right Rev. NORMAN HENRY, D.D., M.A., late Indian Eccl. Establ. (b. 5th July, 1879).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge (hon. classical and theolog. triposes), curate, Whitechapel parish church, 1902-5; sub-warden, Oxford and Cambridge hostels, Allahabad, 1905-8; princpl., St. John's Sch., Agra, 1908-17; princpl., Bishops Coll., Calcutta, 1917-23; bishop in Tinnevelly, Madura and Rannad, 1923-1928; bishop of Rangoon, August, 1928, to Nov., 1934.

TUCK, HOWELL ARTHUR, Indian State Ry's. (dist. signal engr., N.W. Ry.) (b. 26th Feb., 1888).—Asst. signal engr., 1st Nov., 1917; dist. ditto, 1st July, 1937.

TUCKER, Rev. GORDON ELIJAH, B.Sc., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 8th Oct., 1866).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, 22nd Feb., 1929; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1931.

TUCKER, OWEN RUFUS, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Ry's. (transpn. supt.) (b. 27th Mar., 1897).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1923; asst. supt., E.I. rly., July, 1925, transpn. supt. (power), June, 1935.

TUCKER, WILLIAM ALFRED, late Central Provs. C.S. (b. 1st July, 1880).—Joined the service as extra asst. commr., 5th May, 1903; in milty. employ from 13th April, 1915, to 15th June, 1916; on foreign service as Diwan of Bastar state, May, 1923; dep. commr., March, 1927; ret'd., May, 1931.

TUCKETT, ROBERT HENRY, India Office (actg. hr. exec. officer, acctnt.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 17th Jan., 1891).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 1st July, 1913; on mil. service from Dec., 1916, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, Aprl., 1922; actg. hr. ditto, Sept., 1938.

TUCKEY, ARTHUR DAVYS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Oct., 1886).—Educ. at Shrewsbury Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1913; jt. mag. and dep. collr., and ditto, Oct., 1917; settl. offr., Chota Nagpur, Dec., 1920; offg. dir. of land records and survey, March, 1922, and again, Feb., 1925; mag. and collr., Oct., 1923; offg. commr., April, 1930, Aprl., 1933, and Oct., 1933; ret'd., Mar., 1936.

TUCKWELL, CHARLES ADOLPHUS, O.B.E., India posts and tels. (b. 20th Jan., 1884).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 26th May, 1909; asst. supt., workshops, Oct., 1928; supt., ditto, Dec., 1930; O.B.E., Jan., 1938.

TUDBALL, SIR WILLIAM, Kt., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Bed. Modern Sch. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 12th Nov., 1887, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., March, 1892; asst. commr., Feb., 1896; dep. commr., April, 1897; mag. and collr., March, 1900; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1901; second addl. commr., Oudh, May, 1908; judge, high court, Allahabad, April, 1909; knighted, June, 1921; ret'd., Oct., 1922.

TUDOR-OWEN, WILLIAM COURTENAY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 20th Feb., 1877).—Educ. at Shrewsbury Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 29th Nov., 1901; and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; administr., Palitana State, Aug., 1905; tutor to Nawab of Junagad, Jan., 1912; on depts. to England with minor chiefs of Junagad and Palitana, March, 1913, to June, 1914; pol. agent, Jhalavad Prant, Jan., 1915; ditto, Gohilwad Prant, and administrator, Palitana State, June, 1916; asst. to agent to govt., Kathiawar, Oct., 1918; administrator, Bhavnagar State, Aug., 1919, to Nov., 1920; collr., Oct., 1921; collr. and dist. mag. and pol. agent., Nov., 1922; offg. commr. of excise, April, 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1931.

TUFFIELD, GEORGE WILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt. [senr. scale]), G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 19th Mar., 1889).—Asst. transpn. supt., (junr. scale), July, 1926; ditto, (enr. scale), June, 1937.

TUFNELL-BARRETT, HUGH, Indian C.S., (jt. magt. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 13th Jan., 1900).—Educ. at St. John's Sch., Leatherhead, and Wellington Coll., India; joined the service, 10th Sept., 1923; arrived, 16th Nov., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under secty., pol. and

appt. dept., March, 1927; confd., July, 1927; 2nd addl. dep. secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. dept., June, 1930; offg. mag., and collr., Nov., 1931; offg. addl. secty., pol. dept., Bengal, Nov., 1933; K.I.H. medal, June, 1938.

TULLIS, J., B.Sc., Dept. of Industries, Bihar (b. 20th May, 1888).—Joined the service as prof., Bihar coll. of engrg., 4th April, 1924; offg. princpl., April, 1925.

TUN, MAUNG SEIN NYO, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 24th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Rangoon University and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

TURNBULL, FRANCIS FEARON, B.A., India Office (princpl., milty. dept.) (b. 30th Aprl., 1905).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge (Scholar); asst. clerk, House of Commons, 1928-30; apptd. asst. princpl., 12th Aug., 1930; asst. pte. secty. to secty. of state, Jan., 1932; pte. secty. to perm. under-secty. of state, Sept., 1934; actg. princpl., July, 1935; confd., Aprl., 1937.

TURNER, ALEXANDER BERTRAM, late Indian State Rlys., A.M.I.M.E. (b. 15th May, 1882).—Joined the service as dist. C. and W. supt., 6th May, 1921; supt., Feb., 1925; offg. divl. supt., E.I. rly., Aug., 1935; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Jan., 1936; confd., Jan., 1937; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

TURNER, ALFRED CHARLES, M.B.E., M.A., Indian Civil Service (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 12th March, 1892).—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; arrived, 11th May, 1920, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag.; M.B.E., June, 1919; jt. mag., Sept., 1921; offg. mag. and collr. from Feb. to Oct., 1924, and from March, 1925; confd., Feb., 1932; provl. supt. of census opers., United Provs., Aprl., 1930; offg. dep. secty., United Provs., fin. dept., Nov., 1933; offg. secty. to govt., U.P., rev. and p.w. depts., Feb., 1935; ditto, fin. dept., Aprl., 1936.

TURNER, ALFRED ELLIS, late Indian Police (b. 6th Aug., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., C.P., Nov., 1914; dist. supt., Aprl., 1925; ret'd., July, 1938.

TURNER, ERNEST JAMES, C.B., C.B.E., late India Office (b. 26th Feb., 1877).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. junior clerk, mil. dept., India Office, 10th Oct., 1900; resdt. clerk, April, 1901; private sec. to party. under-sec. of state, Nov., 1902; senr. clerk, mil. dept., April, 1904; asst. sec., judl. and public dept., Oct., 1911; ditto, rev. and stat. dept., Oct., 1912; C.B.E., June, 1918; sec., commerce and rev. dept., April, 1921; sec., economic and overseas dept., April, 1924; seconded for duty with Imp. Econ. Conf., July to Sept., 1932; adviser, Ind. del., monetary and economic conf., June, 1933; C.B., Feb., 1937; ret'd., Oct., 1937.

TURNER, FREDERICK CHARLES, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 22nd Aug., 1872).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 19th Dec., 1895, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., July, 1908; commr., Dec., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; ret'd., May, 1931.

TURNER, JOHN TRENCH, M.A. (Cantab.) (b. 24th Dec., 1879).—Joined the service, 6th Sept., 1905, as head-master, High School, Poona; vice-principal, Rajkumar Coll., Rajkot, Aug., 1906; acting principal, ditto, Nov., 1912, to Nov., 1913; acting principal, Elphinstone High School, Bombay, Dec., 1914; on mil. service, April, 1915, to Jan., 1919; principal, Rajkumar Coll., Rajkot, Jan., 1923.

TURNER, JOSHUA EDGAR CARRINGTON, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1883).—Joined the Imperial forest service 1st March, 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1923.

TURNER, LEONARD GEORGE HENRY, Indian State Rlys. (elec. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 15th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service as power stn. supt., G.I.P. rly., 28th Dec., 1928; electrical engr., June, 1932.

TURNER, SIDNEY, C.B.E., B.A., F.I.A., India Office (acct.-gen.) (b. 18th Jan., 1882).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge (10th wrangler); apptd. junr. clerk, acct.-gen.'s dept., 6th Oct., 1905; sec. to Lord Welby's commn. on Indian Mil. Service Family Pension Regns., 1913; senr. clerk, July, 1915; acct.-gen., Dec., 1921; also controller of pension funds; C.B.E., June, 1927; commr. for inc. tax, Nov., 1930; apptd. chairman, applicn. ctee. for the Indo-Burma finl. award, Jan., 1936; apptd. commr. for the I.C.S. Family Pension Fund, Indian Military Service Family Pension Fund, Indian Military Widows' and Orphans' Fund, Superior Services (India) Family Pension Fund from Oct., 1937.

TURNER, WILLIAM HENRY, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 2nd Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., May, 1920; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1920; offg. exec. engr., April, 1925; confd., Oct., 1927.

TURNER, WILLIAM HOVELL, M.A., M.C., India Office (asst. to auditor) (b. 10th Sept., 1891).—Educ. at Perse School and Christ's Coll., Camb.; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to Mar., 1919; apptd. junr. clerk, public wks. dept., 29th Aug., 1919; pte. sec. to parly. under sec. of state, Oct., 1920; actg. principal, acct.-genl.'s dept., Mar., 1922; transfed. to finl. dept., June, 1923; confd. as principal, Jan., 1924; posted to econ. and overseas dept., Apl., 1932; acting dy. clk. of council, Jan., July, 1932; secty., Indian del. to Assembly of League of Nations, 1932 and 1933; asst. to auditor, Mar., 1934.

TUT, MAUNG TIN, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (secty., finance dept., Burma) (b. 1st Feb., 1895).—Educ. at Dulwich, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 16th Jan., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt. agric. dep., July, 1924; jt. regr., co-op. societies, June, 1927; under-secty., govt. of India dept. of industries and labour, Apl., 1928; dep.-secty., ditto, June, 1929; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1933; confd., Dec., 1935; dep. secty., fin. dept., govt. of Burma, May, 1935; secty., forest dept., Burma, Dec., 1935; ditto,

fin. dept., June, 1938; author of essays on 'The Kingdom of Pagan in Legend and History,' 'Burma's part in the War,' 'Tales of Burma Labour Corps,' etc.

TWELLS, Miss HAZEL VIOLET, B.A. (Lond.) Indian Educl. Service (b. 7th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as inspectress of girls' schools in Bombay 1st Oct., 1922.

TWINBERROW, JOHN DENIS, M.O., Indian Police (asst. supt., Punjab) (b. 14th Feb., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on probn., N.W.F.P., 22nd Feb., 1924; confd., Apl., 1927.

TIWISS, CLIFFORD CHARLES HORACE, Lt.-Col., D.S.O., B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 22nd Jan., 1879).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1905, as asst. master, Mayo Coll., Ajmer; offg. vice-principal, ditto, July, 1911, and from Oct., 1913, to Oct., 1914; on mil. service, Oct., 1915, to May, 1919; on foreign service in Jaipur State, July, 1927; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

TIWYNAM, HENRY JOSEPH, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A. (Man.), Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal) (b. 24th April, 1887).—Educ. at Pensionat de la Salle, Rouen, Manchester, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. collr. and mag.; transfed. to Bengal, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1916; I.A.R.O., June, 1915, to Jan., 1919; sec., Bengal employment and labour board, Jan., 1919; vice-presd't, Cooch Behar state, Feb., 1920, to April, 1921; mag. and collr., June, 1924; secty. to govt. of Bengal, rev. and irrign. depts., March, 1929; offg. ch. sec. to govt. of Bengal, July, 1932; offg. commr., Oct., 1932; confd., Dec., 1933; temp'y. adml. member, bd. of rev., Bengal, June, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bengal, variously from Apl., 1936; C.S.I., May, 1937.

TYABJI, BADRUDDIN FAIZ HASAN BADRUDDIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Nov., 1907).—Educated at Bombay Univ. and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, November, 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. under-secty., home and pol. depts., Oct., 1936; secty., Punjab and N.W.F. Prov., jt. pub. serv. commn., May, 1937.

TYABJI, SALMAN BADR-U-DIN, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th Nov., 1883).—Trained at R.I.E., Coll.; joined the service, 1st Jan., 1907, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; in foreign service as offg. chairman of Aden Port Trust, etc., March, 1928; to July, 1928; suptg. engr., Bombay, June, 1935; ret'd., Nov., 1938.

TYAGARAJA, AYYAR, P.C., B.A., B.L., Judl. Dept., Madras (dist. and sess. judge) (b. Jan., 1884).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1908; sub-judge, 1933; dist. and sess. judge Nov., 1936.

TYDD, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 12th Nov., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 3rd Dec., 1929; confd., Dec., 1931; offg. supt., Dec., 1935.

TYLER, Sir HENRY HEWEY FRANCIS MACDONALD, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras (b. 13th Feb., 1877).—Educ. at Rossall Sch. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; manager, Vizianagaram Zamindari, Oct., 1909; sub. collr. and jt. mag., April, 1911; dep. commr., salt and abkari, July, 1911; sec. to commr., salt, abkari and sep. rev., Nov., 1912; on milty. duty from Oct., 1915; attd. to civil admin., Mesopotamia, from May, 1918; pol. offr., Hillah divn., Nov., 1918; C.I.E., June, 1919; collr. and dist. mag., May, 1922; offg. commr. of labour, Nov., 1926; secty., central commee. of the Indian Statutory Commn., Oct., 1928; on depn. as dep. secty. of ditto, April, 1929; Kt., June, 1930; retd., May, 1931.

TYMMS, FREDERICK, C.I.E., M.C., Dept. of Industries and Labour, Govt. of India (b. 4th Aug., 1889).—Apptd. director of civil aviation in India, 23rd Feb., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1935.

TYNDALE, ARTHUR CARRINGTON, O.B.E., M.A., Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (b. 20th Nov., 1885).—Joined the service, 2nd Dec., 1909, and employed in milty. accts. dept.; offg. dep. finl. adviser, milty. finance, Mar., 1927; confd., May, 1927; confd. as Cl. I. offcr., Aug., 1929; confd. as Cmd. controller, Feb., 1934; O.B.E. June, 1938.

TYNDALE-BISCOE, HAROLD L'ESTRANGE, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 21st Oct., 1892).—Joined the army in the summer of 1915, and reported his arrival at Rangoon on 26th June, 1919; asst. consvr., Feb., 1916; dep. consvr., April, 1921; retd., Sept., 1933.

TYRRELL, JASPER ROBERT JOLY, C.I.E., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.) (b. 14th June, 1876).—First commn., 29th Jan., 1902; in mil. employ till Dec., 1905; offg. agency surg., Nov., 1906; awarded K.-i.-H. gold medal, 1911; agency surg., Bunkelkhand, April, 1913; on mil. duty, Sept., 1914, to Dec., 1918; admn. medl. offr. in C.I. and residency surg., May, 1925; resdy. surg. in States of W.I., Sept., 1926; ch. medl. officer and resdy. surg., Indore, April, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; retd., June, 1931.

TYSON, JOHN DAWSON, C.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Aldenham Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Aug., 1914; joined the service, 14th Feb., 1920; arrived, 11th May, 1920, and posted to Bengal; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, fin. and commerce dept., Nov., 1922, to March, 1924; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1924; offg. chief presdy. mag., June, 1926; secty. to agent of govt. of India in South Africa, May, 1927; pte. secty. to govr. of Bengal, June, 1930; C.B.E., Jan., 1933; mag. and collr., Sept., 1935; adviser to H.H. the Maharajah of Couch Behar, Mar., 1936.

TYSON, THOMAS (b. 14th Sept., 1879).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 14th Feb., 1907, to 1st Mar., 1914; supt., govt., printing, Mar., 1914; retd., Sept., 1934.

U (see also Maung).

U BA U, M.A., Barr., Burma Commn. (judge, high ct., Rangoon).—Apptd. govt. prosecutor, April, 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922; confd., Jan., 1928; offg. judge, high ct., Rangoon, Feb., 1930; confd., Apl., 1932.

UTHA TUN KHINE, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 22nd Apl., 1913).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Feb., 1937.

U TIN MRA U, A. T. M., Burma Police (Class I.) (supt.) (b. 8th June, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1914; promoted to Burma Police (class I.), June, 1937.

ULLAH, CHAUDHRI NIAMAT (see Niamat Ullah, Chaudhri).

ULLAH, H. M. SALAMAT (see Salamat Ullah, H. M.).

ULLAH, MUHAMMAD SANA ULLAH (see Muhammad, S.U.).

UMAR HAYAT KHAN, Hon. Maj.-Gen. NAWAB MALIK, Sir, Tiwana, of Kalra, G.B.E., K.C.I.E., M.V.O. (b. 1874).—Educ. at Aitchison Chief's Coll., Lahore; served in Somaliland; joined Tibet expedition; was attached to late Amir of Afghanistan; M.V.O., Dec., 1911; served in France and Mesopotamia in Great War; K.C.I.E., June, 1916; represented Punjab, Delhi War Conference, 1918; served in 3rd Kabul war; C.B.E., June, 1919; member, Esher committee, 1920; member, council of State, 1926; member of Council of India, June, 1929 to June, 1934; Hon. A.D.C. to King, Nov., 1930; G.B.E., June, 1934; hon.-col., 19th (K.G.O.) Lancers, Oct., 1935.

UNDERWOOD, FREDERICK LEWIS, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 16th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 10th April, 1923; offg. dist. supt., Sept., 1931; confd., Feb., 1933; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1934; cent. intell. offcr., Madras presdy., Feb., 1936, to May, 1938.

UNGER, OSCAR RAMSEY, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bihar (inspr.-genl. of prisons) (b. 12th Sept., 1889).—First commn. dated 16th July, 1915; in milty. employ to 13th April, 1924; supt., central jail, Gaya, Jan., 1925; ditto, Hazaribagh, Feb., 1925; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933.

UNITT, ALFRED, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 22nd June, 1902).—Joined the service, 2nd Dec., 1921; comdt., Baluch. levy, May, 1924; offg. supt., March, 1928; confd., Aug., 1932.

UNNITHAN, KRISHNAN MADHAVAN, Indian C.S. (Sub. collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 13th Oct., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 17th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. magte. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. magte., Aug., 1930.

UNWIN, RICHARD, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Burma) (b. 6th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 16th Dec., 1911; dep. consvr., Dec., 1914; lecturer in forestry, Univ. coll., Rangoon, from 1st March, 1923, to 10th Sept., 1924, and again from 26th Oct., 1925; offg. consvr., Apl., 1934; confd., Oct., 1936.

UPENDRA PAI, AMMEMBALA, late Madras Civil Service (b. 17th Dec., 1877).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., 6th April, 1899; confd., Oct., 1903; offg. collr., Mar., 1920; confd., Nov., 1928; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

UPTON, SIR THOMAS EVERARD TIGHBORNE, Kt., late Legislative dept., govt. of India.—Solicitor to the govt. of India, 26th March, 1927, to Apl., 1932; Kt., June, 1931.

U'REN, CECIL WILLIAM EDWARD, Indian Police (b. 9th Nov., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 4th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., Feby., 1932, Jan. and Sept., 1933; offg. dep. commr., Bombay, Feb., 1936.

URQUHART, DONALD WALLACE JOHNSTONE, Barr., late Burma Commn. (b. 5th Oct., 1879).—Apptd. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

USMAN, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Usman).

USMAN, SAIYID MISBAHUL (see Misbahul Usman, S.).

U THA ZAN U, B.A., B.L., late Burma Commn. (b. 17th May, 1881).—Joined the service as myook, 29th June, 1908; jud. extra asst. commr., May, 1913; asst. judge, July, 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1925; confd., July, 1926; ret'd., May, 1936.

UZIELLI, HERBERT REX, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (secty., home dept., Madras) (b. 31st Aug., 1890).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 12th Dec., 1914, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Feb., 1918; tempy. under-sec. to govt., rev. spl. dept., Nov., 1919; dep. sec. to govt., Dec., 1925; offg. sec. to govt., local self-govt. dept., April, 1926; dep. sec. to ditto, May, 1926; offg. collr. and dist. magte., June, 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; collr. (prov. subs.), Aug., 1932; confd., Aug., 1933; secty. to govt., Madras, rev. dept., Nov., 1934, to May, 1937; ditto, home dept., Aug., 1938.

VACHHA, JAMSHEDJI BEJANJI, Khan Bahadur, C.I.E., B.A., B.Sc., Bombay C.S. (commr., income tax, Bombay Presdy.) (b. 26th May, 1879).—Joined the service, 17th April, 1902; dep. collr. of income tax, Dec., 1909; 1st income tax collr., Bombay, 1919; asst. commr. of income tax, Bombay, April, 1922; commr., ditto, Bombay Presdy., Oct., 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; offg. member, central bd. of rev., variously from Dec., 1932.

VAHID, SYED ABDUL (see Syed Abdul Vahid).

VAIDYA, JAGANNATH BALKRISHNA, M.R.C.S. Major, I.M.S., Medical Dept., United Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 14th Jan., 1888).—First commn., Jan., 1914; civil surg., United Provs., Apl., 1930.

VAIDYANATHAN, PERUVEMBA PARASURAMAIYER, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 1st Jan., 1915).—Educ. at Madras Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Sept., 1938, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

VAISH, RADHA KRISHNA, M.A., Imperial Customs Service (b. 28th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service, Apl., 1924; asst. coll. of customs, Apl., 1926.

VAKIL, KRISHNALAL SURAJRAM, B.A., M.Ed. (Leeds), late Indian Educl. Service) (b. 13th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service as edul. inspr., Bombay, 7th Sept., 1923; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

VAN WYCK, WILLIAM, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 12th Mar., 1913).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ.; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1938; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1938, and served as asst. commr.

VAN DER BEEK, AUBREY GUILDFORD, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 22nd Apl., 1890).—Joined the service, 4th Mar., 1914, as asst. engr. (tempy.); ditto (perm.), Mar., 1915; exec. engr., Burma rlys., Sept., 1932.

VALLURI KAMESWARA, RAO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 15th July, 1914).—Educ. at the Board High Sch., Razole, Madras Univ., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 9th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

VAN SOMERAN, LEONARD WILLIAM, V.D., Indian State Railways (traffic manager, E.B. Rly.) (b. 16th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 28th Jan., 1903; asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1907; dist. ditto, May, 1915; offg. dep. traffic manager, May, 1927; confd., Mar., 1931; offg. traffic manager, variously from May, 1931; confd., Jan., 1937.

VANCE, ROBERT LANCELOT, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (legationsurg.).—First commn., 30th May, 1916; in milty. employ to 29th Apl., 1928; offg. C.M.O., W.I. States Agency and resdy. surg., Rajkot, May, 1928, and again, Mar., 1933; offg. civil surg., Quetta, Aug., 1928; confd., Apl., 1931; resdy. surg., Kashmir, May, 1935; legation surg., Nepal, Mar., 1937.

VARADACHARI, SRINAVASA, RAO BAHADUR, Judl. Dept. Madras (Judge high ct., Madras).—Apptd. Judge, high ct., Madras, 3rd March, 1934.

VARGHESE, THOPIL ANTHONY, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 17th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Madras University, University College, London, and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, November, 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Apl., 1932.

VARMA, BRIJ BHUSHAN SARAN (see Brij, B. S. V.).

VARMA, B. P. (see Bhagwati Prasad Varma).

VARMA, FATEH BAHADUR (see Fateh, B. V.).

VARMA, RAGHUNANDAN PRASAD, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 5th Sept., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921.

VARMA, RUF LALL, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 18th Dec., 1904).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Assam, 2nd Jan., 1929.

VARMA, SARADA PRASHAD, Babu, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 15th Jan., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 2nd Jan., 1931; confd., Dec., 1931.

VARMA, S. P. (see Sidheshwari Prasad).

VARMA, SUKHDEV PRASAD, Barr., Judl.
