Dept., Bihar (judge, high ct., Patna).—Appptd.
judge, high ct., Patna, 23rd Jan., 1934.

VAS, EDWIN THOMAS CHRISTIAN, M.A., B.Sc.,
Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay)
(b. 25th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service, 20th
July, 1921; asst. consvr., Aug., 1921; dep.,
consvr., Feb., 1927.

VASUDEEV, KESHAVRAO BALKRISHNA, B.A.,
LL.B., Bombay C.S. (judge, high ct., Bombay)
(b. 14th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service,
Oct., 1907; dep. collr., Aug., 1909; actg. asst.
judge and sess. judge, April, 1913; asst. judge
and jt. judge and addl. sess. judge, Oct., 1921;
judge and sess. judge, Nov., 1924; offg. judge,
high ct., Bombay, June, 1936; confd., Nov.,
1937.

VASUDEVA, BHANDARI, Lala, Indian Ser-
vice of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 3rd August, 1902).
Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab,
8th Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Sept., 1933.

VASVANI TAHILRAM DHARAMDAS,
RAI BAHADUR, Indian Police (supt., Sind)
(b. 11th Dec., 1897).—Joined the service, 2nd
July, 1906; King's police medal, 1923;
promoted to I.P. as supt., Bombay, Sept., 1935.

VAZIFDAR, SOHRAB SHAPOORJI, L.M. & S.
(Bom.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.),
Lt.-Col. (ret'd.), late I.M.S. (b. 1st Aug., 1883).—
Joined the service 1st Aug., 1908, and served in
mil. dept. prior to 24th Jan., 1923, offg. as prof.
of medicine &c., G.M. Coll., and physician and
supt., J. J. Hosp., Bombay, June, 1926; confd.,
Oct., 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1938.

VEALE, CHARLES JOHN, F.R.G.S., F.R.A.S.
(b. 8th Mar., 1877).—Joined the service, 25th
Nov., 1896; prof. of surveying and drawing,
Oct., 1908; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

VELLODI, MULLAT VIKKARA, Rao Bahadur
Madras C.S. (sub. collr.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1884).—
Joined the service, 12th Apl., 1910; dep. collr.,
Dec., 1928; sub-collr., Apl., 1936.

VELLODI, MULLATH KADINGI, B.A., Indian
C.S. (secty., govt. of Orissa, educ., etc., depts.) (b.
14th Jan., 1896).—Educ. at Presidency Coll.,
Madras; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; ar-
rived, 2nd Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as
asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and mag., Jan.,
1924; spl. asst. settlt. offr., Jan., 1926; spl.
settl. offr., Sept., 1927; offg. collr. and dist.
magte., Mar., 1931; offg. dir. of land records,
Madras, Apl., 1933; jt. secty., Bd. of rev.,
Madras, Nov., 1933; served temp. with govt.
of Orissa from Mar., 1937; secty., govt. of Orissa,
educ., etc., depts., Dec., 1937.

VENABLES, ARTHUR VINCENT, M.C., V.D.,
late Indian State Rlys. (b. 16th Oct.,
1880).—Joined the service, 30th Jan.,
1903; junr. engr., Apl., 1903; on milty. duty
from 23rd Nov., 1914, to 9th May, 1919; resdnt.
engr., June, 1917; divl. engr., April, 1923;
divl. supt., Jan., 1926; offg. senr. dep. agent,
June, 1929; offg. ch. engr., E.I. Rly., Dec., 1929;
confd., July, 1932; offg. agent, E.I. rly., Apl.,
1934; confd., July, 1934; ret'd., July, 1937.

VENGANAD KOVILAGAM RAJAGOPALA
THAMBAN (see Rajagopala Thamban, V. K.).

VENKATA ACHARYA, ATUR RANGASWAMI,
Rao Sahib, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs.
(dep. chief engr. for irrign.) (b. 21st June, 1895).
—Joined the service as appr. engr., Madras,
30th June, 1917; asst., engr., Mar. 1919;
asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1920; offg. exec. engr.,
Apl., 1926; confd., July, 1928; dep. chief
engr. for irrign., Madras, Sept., 1936.

VENKATA RAO, GHANTA (sany. engr.,
Madras) (b. 18th Feb., 1886).—Appptd. dep.
sany. engr., 3rd Mar., 1923; offg. sany. engr.,
Sept., 1931; confd., Feb., 1935.

VENKATA REDDI NAYUDU, RAI
BAHADUR, Sir Kurma, K.C.I.E.—Appptd. member
of govr.'s exec. council, Madras, 26th Apl.,
1934; actg. govr. of Madras, June, 1936;
K.C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

VENKATA, SUBRAMANYAN MINAKHISUN-
DARAM (see Subramanyan Minakhisundaram, V.).

VENKATACHALA AYYAR, KUNNISSERI
SUBRAHMANYA, B.A., B.L., late Judl. dept.,
Madras (b. 1st June, 1878).—Joined the
service, 3rd July, 1905, as offg. dist. munsif;
confd., Jan., 1906; sub-judge, Aug., 1923;
offg. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1932; ret'd.,
June, 1933.

VENKATACHAR, CADAMBI SHESHACHAR,
B.A., Indian C.S. (agent to Govt. of India
British Malaya) (b. 11th July, 1899).—
Educ. at Madras Univ., Univ. Coll.,
London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined
the service, 31st Oct., 1922; arrived, 15th Jan.,
1923, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag.
and collr.; jt. mag., 1925; offg. magte. and
collr., March, 1927; under secty. to the agent
to the govr.-genl. in States of W.I. Oct., 1927;
first asst. to resdnt., Baroda, Nov., 1927; under-
secty. to A.G.G. in States of W.I., Dec., 1928;
census supt. C.I., Apl., 1930; offg. dep. commr.,
United Provs., Apl. 1933; agent to Govt. of
India in British Malaya, Sept., 1937.

VENKATAKRISHNA AYYAR, LAKSH-
MINARAYANA AYYAR, Rao Bahadur, B.A., B.E.,
Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Orissa) (b.
7th May, 1888).—Joined the service as appr.
engr., 18th July, 1912; asst. engr., July, 1913;
exec. engr. July, 1922.

VENKATANARAYANA NAYUDU, JANNY-
AVULA, Diwan Bahadur, B.A., B.L., late Madras
Civil Service (b. 9th Nov., 1875).—Joined
the service, 4th May, 1899; offg. dir. of land
records, Aug., 1919; offg. collr., Sept., 1921;
inspr.-gen. of regn., Jan., 1922; commr.,
corporation of Madras, July, 1925; offg. secty.
to govt., law and educn. dept., Oct., 1928; ret'd.,
Nov., 1930.

VENKATAPPIAH, BURRA, M.A., Indian C.S.
(asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 4th October, 1907).—
Educated at Madras University and School of
Oriental Studies, London; joined the service,
12th October, 1932; arrived in India, Nov.,
1932, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

VENKATARAMA AYYAR, K.C., B.A.,
B.L., Income Tax Dept., Madras (asst. commr.
of inc. tax) (b. 1st Apl., 1882).—Joined the
service, 1st Apl., 1922; asst. commr., Jan., 1931.

VENKATARAMA AYYAR, KUMBACONAM, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 17th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. examr., 13th Aug., 1904; dep. examr., Jan., 1908; asst. acct.-genl., Sept., 1910; dep. ditto, May, 1914; asst. auditor, N.W. Rly., June, 1918; dep. auditor, ditto, Feb., 1920; offg. asst. auditor-genl., April, 1926; offg. dep. auditor, E.I. Rly., Jan., 1927; dep. ch. auditor, E.I. Rly., Apl., 1929; offg. ch. auditor, rlwy. clearing accts., Apl., 1930; retd., May, 1933.

VENKATARAMA AYYAR, THIRUVADAMARUDUR SUBRAHMANYA, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 10th Sept., 1898).—Joined the service as appr. engr. June, 1921; asst. exec. engr., June, 1922; offg. profr., coll. of engrg., Guindy, Aug., 1927; exec. engr., Nov., 1931.

VENKATARAMA AYYAR, TIRUKKARUGAVUR NANA AYYAR, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 19th July, 1883).—Held non-gaz. appt. as temp. sub-engr., from 9th Aug., 1914; asst. engr., Feb., 1920; apptd. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., Madras, 1st Jan., 1931; offg. exec. engr., Mar., 1933; confd., Jan., 1936; retd., July, 1938.

VENKATARAMA RAMACHANDRA AYYAR (see Ramachandra Ayyar, V.).

VENKATARAMAN, KOMARATCHI SRINIVASA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 25th Jan., 1913).—Educ. at Pachayappa's Coll., Madras, Madras Univ., University College, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. magte., Nov., 1937.

VENKATARAMAN, SWAMINATHA ADUTHURAI, M.A., Ind. C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 22nd June, 1899).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived 13th Dec., 1922; and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1924; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Apl., 1931; dep. secty., fin. dept., Apl., 1932.

VENKATARAMAN, ANAIKUPPAM RAMASWAMI, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th Nov., 1906).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Madras, 1st May, 1933; confd., Sept., 1933.

VENKATARAMAN, TIRUVADI SAMBASIVAIYER, C.I.E., B.A., Rao Bahadur, Indian Agricultural Service (dep. dir. of Agric., Madras) (b. 1884).—Apptd. asst. sugar-cane expert, Nov., 1918; sugar-cane expert, June, 1921; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

VENKATARAMANA AYYAR, KODUMUDI RAMASWAMI Rao Bahadur, late Indian Forest Service (b. Jan., 1880).—Joined the service 23rd Oct., 1888; dist. forest officer, Apl., 1911; dep. consvr., May, 1926; actg. consvr., May, 1926; retd., Jan., 1935.

VENKATARAMANA RAO NAYUDU, PAKALA, Rao Bahadur (judge, high ct., Madras).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1935.

VENKATARAMANA RAJU, PATTAYYA RAJU, B.E., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 14th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 14th June, 1918; asst. engr., June, 1919; asst. exec. engr., Feb., 1922; offg. exec. engr., June, 1925; confd., June, 1928.

VENKATARAMANI, P., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 28th March, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th Nov., 1931; dep. consvr., Nov., 1936.

VENKATASUBBA RAO, SIR MUTTA, Kt., B.A., B.L., late Madras Judicial Dept. (b. 18th July, 1878).—Apptd. judge high ct., Madras, 17th Nov., 1921; offg. ch. justice, July, 1935, and again, July, 1936; member, Ind. delimitn. ctce., Aug., 1935; Kt. Jan., 1936; retd., July, 1938.

VENKATASUBRAMANIAN, VAITHINATHIER (see Subramanian, V. V.).

VENKATESWARA AYYAR, Gopala, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 19th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Madras University, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., May, 1932.

VENKATESWARAN, SUNDARAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 2nd Jan., 1901).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 30th Nov., 1925, and served as asst. collr. and mag. in Madras; sub. collr., Aug., 1927.

VENUGOPAL, GEORGE, late Indian Forest Service (b. 14th July, 1882).—Joined the service, 22nd Jan., 1905; dep. consvr., for., Madras, July, 1922; retd., July, 1937.

VERDON, PHILIP, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (dist. medl. officer, Madras) (b. 22nd Feb., 1886).—First commn. dated 8th Aug., 1917; in milty. employ until 30th March, 1925; dist. medl. officer, Feb., 1925; offg. surg., 4th dist., Nov., 1928; confd., June, 1929.

VERGHESE, GEORGE, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (supt., cent. jail, Orissa) (b. 9th June, 1888). First commn., 27th Aug., 1917; in milty. employ to Mar., 1933; supt., central jail, Orissa, July, 1933.

VERMA, CHHOTELAI, I.S.O., Rai Bahadur, late Central Provs. C.S. (b. 6th Jan., 1883).—Held ministerial and non-gaz. appts. from 27th Mar., 1906, to 21st Feb., 1909; extra asst. commr., June 1917; offg. dep. commr. and settlement offcr., Oct., 1927; confd., Dec., 1928; I.S.O., May, 1937; retd., Jan., 1938.

VERMA, DHANI RAM (see Dhani Ram V.).

VERMA, HIRALAL, Rai Sahib, M.B.E., B.A., late Central Provs. C.S. (b. 25th Apl., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1909; extra-asst. commr., Apl., 1916; under-secty. to govt. Central Provs., and publicity officer in addn., Sept., 1931; M.B.E., June, 1935; retd., Jan., 1937.

VERMA, KAMALAKANTA, (judge, high ct., Allahabad).—Apptd. Nov., 1937.

VERMA, KRISHNA PRASAD, Rai Sahib, B.A., late Income Tax Deptt., Central Provs. (b. 1st June, 1882).—Joined the service as extra asst. commr., Aug., 1906; asst. commr. of income tax, May, 1925; commr., July, 1936; retd., June, 1938.

VERNEDE, RAYMOND VEVEYSAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 9th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Bradford and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928; and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and coll.; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1935.

VERNIEUX, ASHLEY CYRIL, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 3rd July, 1875).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 23rd June, 1893; supt., May, 1904; supt., post offices, Mar., 1925; dep. P.M.G., Lahore, Apl., 1927; ditto, R.M.S., eastern circle, Mar., 1928; ditto, Bengal and Assam, Sept., 1928; retd., July, 1930.

VERNON, HAROLD ANSELM BELLAMY, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Sept., 1874).—Educ. at Clifton and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 8th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sec. to commr. of salt, &c., rev., March, 1906; sub. collr. and joint mag., Nov., 1912; collr. and dist. mag., April, 1918; offg. agent to govr.-gen., Madras States, May, 1926; offg. third member, bd. of revenue, Nov., 1926; offg. first ditto, April, 1928; C.I.E., March, 1929; 1st member, bd. of rev. (provl. perm.), Jan., 1930; C.S.I., June, 1930; addl. charge of commr. of labour, Mar.-July, 1931; retd., Sept., 1932.

VERNON, REGINALD GEORGE, late Indian Fin. Deptt. (b. 31st Jan., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 30th Nov., 1905, as asst. examr.; dep. examr., Oct., 1908; asst. auditor, June, 1918; offg. dep. dir., rly. clearing accts., Dec., 1926; with finl. commr., rlys., Apl., 1929; offg. ch. auditor, N.W. Rly., Apl., 1931; retd., Apl., 1934.

VESUGAR, JAMSHED BURJORJI, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 26th May, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 22nd Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1926; under-secty., p.w.d., b. and r. branch, Apl., 1937.

VIBART, FRANK, Indian State Rlys. (supt., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 17th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service, 31st Mar., 1911; asst. traffic supt., Apl., 1915; supt., Sept., 1929.

VICK, HENRY JOHN HOWARD, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 28th Sept., 1912).—Joined the service as probationary asst. supt., United Provs., Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934.

VICKERS, CHARLES ALFRED, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 31st Oct., 1899).—Educ. at King Edward's High Sch., Birmingham, Quetta Cadet Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1924, and served in Burma as an asst. commr.; sub. divl. officer, June, 1925; offg. under sec., home and pol. dep., Nov., 1926; judge, sub-dnl. courts, Aug., 1931; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1934.

VICKERS, HAROLD JAMES, C.B.E., Indian Police (dep. dir., intell. bureau) (b. 1st June, 1895).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1914; on milty. duty from 16th Sept., 1916, to 17th Feb., 1920; asst. supt., May, 1920; asst. dist. officer, frontier constabulary, June, 1920; dist. officer, ditto, Dec., 1922; supt., Oct., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1926; dep. dir., intelligence bureau, Nov., 1933; C.B.E., June, 1936.

VICKERY, PHILIP CRAWFORD, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 23rd Feb., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1909; asst. supt., April, 1911; on mil. duty, 1st Nov., 1915, to 24th March, 1921; supt., Aug., 1921; O.B.E., June, 1923; on depn. outside India from Oct., 1926.

VIDYANT, H. P. (see Hari Prasad Vidyant). VIEYRA, HERBERT CLARENCE, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th Dec., 1879).—Joined the service, 9th Feb., 1906, and served in Bengal as asst. engr.; exec. engr., May, 1915; offg. suptg. engr., July, 1924; retd., Aug. 1933.

VIJAYARAGHAVA ACHARYA, Sir Tiruvalyangudi, K.B.E., M.A., Diwan Bahadur, late Madras Civil Service (b. 27th Aug., 1875).—Joined the service, 30th April, 1898, as dep. collr.; rev. offr., corpn. of Madras, March, 1912; sec. to commr. of rev. settlt., May, 1917; actg. presdt., Madras corporation, Aug., 1917; diwan of Cochin, March, 1919; commr. for India, British Empire Exhibition, from June, 1922, to March, 1925; K.B.E., Jan., 1926, offg. dir. of industries, April, 1926; member, public service commn., Nov., 1926; vice-chairman, impl. council of agricl. research, Delhi, Sept., 1929; retd., Oct., 1935.

VIJAYARAGHAVAN, CHERUVARY KOTTIETH, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 2nd Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Madras Christian Coll. and Law Coll.; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 2nd Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1923; temp. dep. commr. of excise, May, 1930; offg. collr., Sept., 1934; offg. dep. secty., finance dept., Madras, Apl., 1935.

VIJAYARAGHAVAN, NARASINHA (see Raghavan, N. V.).

VILLAR, ARTHUR REGINALD, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Burma) (b. 28th June, 1888).—Arrived 28th Dec., 1910, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Jan., 1916; offg. consvr., Aug., 1928; and again, Apl., 1931; confd., Feb., 1935.

VINAYANAND PATHAK, PANDIT, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 17th Feb., 1889).—Joined the service as dep. supt., 1st Nov., 1912; asst. supt., Feb., 1920; offg. supt., Nov., 1921; confd., Feb., 1927; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Aug., 1933; asst. I.G. rly. police, Aug., 1938.

VINCENT, FRANK ARTHUR MONEY HAMPE, C.I.E., C.B.E., M.V.O., late India Police Deptt. —Joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1895; asst. supt. of police, July, 1897; supt. and asst. inspr.-gen., Jan., 1904; prinpl. pol. training sch., Bhamburda, June, 1906; dep. commr. of pol.,

Bombay, June, 1909; M.V.O., Jan., 1912; dep. dir., cr. intell., March, 1913; commr. of police, Bombay, April, 1916; C.I.E., June, 1919; retd., March, 1920; C.B.E., Jan., 1926; jt. social secty., Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932.

VINCENT, Sir WILLIAM HENRY HOARE, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., Kt., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st Apl., 1866).—Educ. at Christ Coll., Brecon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived 10th Dec., 1887, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1894; mag. and collr., June, 1898; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1900; ditto, and jud. commr., Dec., 1906; ag. judge, Calcutta High Court, March, 1909; sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., from Oct., 1911; transf. to Bihar, April, 1912; knighted, Jan., 1913; member, exec. council, Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1915; member of the Viceroy's Council in charge of home dept., from April, 1917; K.C.S.I., June, 1918; G.C.I.E., Nov., 1922; retd., April, 1923; member of the council of India, April, 1923, to July, 1931; nominated sheriff of Anglesey, Nov., 1929.

VINING, ARTHUR CYRIL, Indian State Rlys. (divl. transp. supt., N.W. Rly.) (b. 22nd Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 22nd Nov., 1909; on milty. duty, 1st April, 1916; dist. traffic supt., Aug., 1922; divl. transp. supt., Oct., 1924.

VINING, LEONARD EDWARD, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. operg. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 29th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., Nov., 1908; dist. ditto, May, 1917; on spl. duty with Midland Rly., June, 1921; supt., July, 1925; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., June, 1929, and again, Dec., 1932; offg. divl. supt., May, 1932; dep. ch. operating supt., E.I. rly., (provl.), June, 1934; confd., Jan., 1936.

VINCENT, N., O.B.E., D.F.C., Dept. of Industries and Labour, Govt. of India (b. 3rd Oct., 1902).—Dept. dir. of civil aviation in India, 4th Dec., 1931 to July, 1932; O.B.E., June, 1938.

VIPAN, ALFRED, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (secty. to Govt., p.w.d., Orissa) (b. 9th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; ch. engr. to Travancore durbar, Apl., 1927; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1933; confd., Oct., 1935; secty. to govt., p.w.d., Orissa, Apl., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

VIRA, DHARMA, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 20th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad University, Lucknow University, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

VISHNU PARASHURAM RAVERKAR (see Raverkar, V. P.).

VISHNU SAHAY (see Sahay, V.).

VISVANATHA AYYAR, PATHIYAD SESHACHALA, M.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 10th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Madras University and School of Oriental Studies, London. Joined the service, 12th

Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., May, 1933; served temp. with govt. of Orissa from Apl., 1936.

VISVANATHA RAO, VELLIYUR NOTT, Diwan Bahadur, M.A., B.L., Madras C.S. (sub. collr.) (b. 16th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service, 8th Feb., 1913; dep. collr., Mar., 1916; offg. collr., Mar., 1930; offg. secty. to govt., Madras, law and educn. dept., Sept., 1931; sub. collr.; June, 1931; actg. secty. to govt., Madras, law and educn. dept., Sept., 1931.

VISVESVARAIYA, Sir MOKSHAGUN-DAM, K.C.I.E., B.A., L.C.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1884, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1897; K.-i.H. medal, 2nd class, Jan., 1906; retd., Oct., 1909; joined the service of Mysore govt. as chief engr. and sec. to govt., P.W. and Rlys., Nov., 1909; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; Dewar of Mysore, Nov., 1912, to 1918; K.C.I.E., June, 1915; chairman, Indian Economic Enquiry Committee, 1925.

VISWANATHAN, VENKATA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 25th Jan., 1909).—Educ. at University College, London; and Balliol College, Oxford. Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1936; asst. settlement offcr., Oct., 1936; settlement offcr., Oct., 1937.

VIVIAN, GRAHAM LINSSELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 1st Aug., 1887).—Educ. at Epsom Coll., and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; ditto, and asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1916; jt. mag., June, 1917; on mil. duty, Sept., 1917, to Jan., 1919; special duty at Lucknow, Oct., 1922, to Feb., 1923; settl. offr., Oct., 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; confd., July, 1928; offg. presdt., ct. of wards, Feb., 1935, to Nov., 1935.

VOMBATHKERE PANDURANG RAO (see Pandurang Rao, V.).

VON-BOCK, FRANCIS JOSEPH, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 11th Oct., 1883).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll., joined as asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Nov., 1917; retd., July, 1932.

VORLEY, JOHN STUART, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 19th Dec., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 23rd Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1924.

VYALL, FREDERICK STANLEY, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Burma) (b. 8th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; on mil. service from 22nd Aug., 1916, to 25th Nov., 1919; exec. engr., Aug., 1922; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1930; confd., Dec., 1935.

VYAS, BHANUSHANKAR SUNDARJI, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 19th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; in Bhavnagar State as state engr. from May, 1924, to Jan., 1930; exec. engr., Oct., 1926.

VYAS, DEVSHANKAR VAJESHANKAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 14th Aug., 1898).—Educ. at Bombay, and Jesus Coll., Camb., apptd. after exam. in 1923; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1923, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; offg. judge and sess. judge, Mar., 1931; and again, Feb., 1933; confd., Nov., 1934.

WACE, ERNEST WILLIAM CORNISH, Indian Police (supnt., Punjab) (b. 11th May, 1894).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1914; on mil. duty from 5th March, 1915, to 17th Dec., 1917; asst. supnt., April, 1918; supnt., Sept., 1924; asst. to dep. inspr.-genl., C.I.D., Dec., 1926.

WACE, FERDINAND BLYTH, B.A., Indian C.S. (commissioner, Punjab) (b. 21st Feb., 1891).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 14th Dec., 1915, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from Sept., 1916, to Jan., 1919; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1923; colonization officer, Nili Bar, Nov., 1924; dep. commr. (provl.), Oct., 1926; confd., Oct., 1928; regr.; co-op. societies, Punjab, Apl., 1933, and again, Jan., 1937; addl. secty. to govt., Punjab, July, 1935; offg. commr., Oct., 1936; commr., June, 1938.

WADDELL, GILBERT, Indian Police (supnt., United Provs.) (b. 24th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supnt., 5th Dec., 1914; offg. supnt., April, 1920; confd., Feb., 1926.

WADDELL, PETER HATELY, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th Dec., 1881).—Educ. at Kelvinside Acad. and the Univ., Glasgow, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1905, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settl. officer; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1912; mag. and collr., March, 1918, and again, Dec., 1927; dep. commr., Nov., 1924; offg. commr., Feb., 1930; retd., Oct., 1932.

WADDY, Rev. RICHARD PATTESON STACY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 31st May, 1904).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Mar., 1935; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1936.

WADE, HAROLD, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 7th Aug., 1893).—Educ. at Preston Gr. Sch.; served as second class clerk, India Store Dept., from 27th Jan., 1913; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 28th Feb., 1913, and attd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to April, 1919; services lent to high commr. for India from April, 1921; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, April, 1925.

WADHAMS, JOHN OSWALD, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 15th Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Aug., 1929.

WADIA, BOMANJI JAMSHEDJI, Barr., Judicial dept., Bombay (judge, high ct., Bombay).—Apptd. offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, 17th June, 1930; confd., June, 1931.

WADIA, DARASHAW NOSHERWAN, M.A., B.Sc., F.G.S., F.R.G.S., Geological Survey of India (geologist) (b. 23rd Oct., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. supnt., 16th Apl., 1921; offg. supnt., June, 1930.

WADIA, HIRJIBHAY HORMUSJI, M.A., Barr., late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 24th Mar., 1875).—Acted as prof. of maths., Deccan Coll., Poona, 15th June to Sept., 1900; as prof. of maths., Coll. of Science, Poona, Jan. to Aug., 1901; held non-gazetted appts. in Bombay high ct. from April, 1905; addl. dep. regr., Bombay high ct., Nov., 1907; senr. judge's clerk and commr. for taking affidavits, ditto, April, 1908; 2nd dep. regr. and commr. for taking affidavits, ditto, July, 1910; master and regr. in equity, ditto, April, 1916; offg. court receiver and liquidator, Bombay, Apl., 1929; confd., Mar., 1932; retd., Mar., 1935.

WADIA, MANECK DHUNJISHAW, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col. (retd.), late I.M.S. (b. 5th July, 1883).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1908; on mil. duty to 25th Sept., 1922; civil surg., Punjab, Feb., 1924; retd., July, 1938.

WADIA, NAVROJI JEHANGIR, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Bombay) (b. 27th Aug., 1884).—Educ. at St. Vincent's High Sch., Poona; Bombay Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 19th Nov., 1909, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge; actg. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1923; dep. sec., govt. of Bombay, legal dept., and sec. to the leg. council (in addn.), Aug., 1925; reforms secy. (tempy), Dec., 1927; judge and sess. judge, Nov., 1928; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, Jan., and again June, 1933; confd., Dec., 1933; chairman, Bombay provl. delimitn. ct., June, 1935.

WADLEY, KEITH LLOYD HAMILTON, B.A., Indian Service of Engineers (b. 5th Sept., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 20th Oct., 1930; transfed. to Central P.W.D., Oct., 1935.

WADSWORTH, SIDNEY, Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. 21st Dec., 1888).—Educ. at Loughborough Grammar School, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 8th Dec., 1913; and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. censor, Madras, Oct., 1914; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; temp. under sec. to govt., rev. (spl.) dept., June, 1918; sec. board of revenue, land revenue, Aug., 1922; actg. registrar, high ct., July, 1925; confd., Sept., 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1926; confd., July, 1927; judge, high ct., Madras, June, 1935.

WADSWORTH, SYDNEY HERBERT, Office of High Commr. for India (suptg. exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 25th Dec., 1881).—Apptd. and class clerk, 26th June, 1905; minor staff clerk, Sept., 1920; services lent to high commr. for India from Oct., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; suptg. exec. officer, Oct., 1930.

WAGLE, NITYANAND MANGESH, M.A., M.Sc. (Econ.), Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 16th Aug., 1914).—Educ. at Theosophical National Sch., Benares, Benares and Bombay Univ. and London Sch. of Economics; apptd.

on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 20th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

WAGSTAFF, HENRY WYNTEs, M.C., Lt.-Col. R.E., Indian State Rlys. (conciliation offcr. [rlys.]) (b. 19th July, 1890).—First commn. dated 23rd Dec., 1910; asst. exec. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., June, 1922; offg. supervisor of rly. lab., tly. bd., Apl., 1931; officer on spl. duty under for. and pol. dept., Aug., 1936; conciliation offcr (rlys.), Dec., 1937.

WAGSTAFFE, KEITH DESMOND, Indian Police (asst. supt., Punjab) (b. 15th June, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., 20th Nov., 1927; confd., Nov., 1929; offg. supt., Jan., 1932.

WAH, LAO HTIN (see Lao Htin Wah).

WAHID, A. (see Abdul Wahid).

WAHID, A N. M. (see Abu Nazr Muhammad Wahid).

WAIGHT, HARRY GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 16th Mar., 1892).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1928.

WAITE, HERBERT WILLIAM, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., N.W.F.P.) (b. 30th June, 1887).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1907; asst. supt., Apl., 1909; supt., Sept., 1917; prinpl. police training sch., Phillour, Feb., 1926; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1928; and again, June, 1933; confd., Nov., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1938.

WAJAHAT HUSAIN, M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (secty., p.w.d., United Provs.) (b. 23rd Aug., 1894).—Educ. at presidency coll., Calcutta, and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1919; arrived, 9th Jan., 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1925; confd., June, 1932; del. to Indian round table conf., London, 3rd session, Nov., 1932; minister, Kashmir Darbar, July, 1932; secty., p.w.d., b. and r., Jan., 1938.

WAKEFIELD, EDWARD BIRKBECK, Indian C.S. Indian Political Service (b. 24th July, 1903).—Educ. at Haileybury and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec., self-govt. dep. Punjab, Sept., 1930; asst. commr., Ajmer Merwara, Dec., 1930; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, Mar., 1933; on foreign service in Kalat state, Oct., 1933; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, Feb., 1937; secty. to resdt. for Punjab States, Oct. 1937.

WAKELY, Sir LEONARD DAY, K.C.I.E., C.B., B.A., B.Sc., India Office (dep. under-secy., of state) (b. 29th Mar., 1880).—Educ. at St. Olave's Grammar School, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. junior clerk, judl. and public dept., 15th Oct., 1902; resdt. clerk, Nov., 1902; private sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, April, 1904; private sec. to permanent under-

sec. of state, March, 1906, to Dec., 1909; senior clerk, rev. and stat. dept., Oct., 1909; asst. sec., political dept., March, 1917, but temply. retained in rev. and stat. dept., until April, 1919; acting sec., pol. dept., Feb., 1921; confd. as sec., pol. dept., Jan., 1924; C.B., June, 1924; actg. asst. under-secy. of state, Jan., 1929; confd., July, 1920; commr. of inc. tax, Nov., 1930; actg. dep. under-secy. of state, Sept., 1934; confd., Dec., 1934; K.C.I.E., June, 1935; compiled decennial report on moral and material progress of India, 1913.

WAKELY, LEONARD JOHN DEAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (under-sec., pol. dept., govt. of Punjab) (b. 18th June, 1909).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., Christ Church, Oxford, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; appointed after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; under-secy. to govt., pol. dept., Nov., 1937.

WALI MUHAMMAD, B.A., Khan Bahadur, late Income Tax Dept., Central Provs. (b. Oct., 1876).—Joined the service, 29th June, 1898; asst. commr., May, 1922; commr., Nov., 1924; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

WALI MUHAMMAD, M.A., Ph.D., Indian Educl. Service (prof., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad) (b. 18th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service, 26th Sept., 1919; asst. sec. to the govt. of India, dept. of educn., June, 1920; with Lucknow University as prof. of physics from 31st Aug., 1921.

WALI MUHAMMAD, MAULAVI, B.L., late Bihar and Orissa C.S. (Judl. branch) (b. 6th Nov., 1874).—Joined the service, 13th July, 1903; munslf, Feb., 1904; sub. judge, Feb., 1918; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1929.

WALKER, FREDERIC PERCY, late Indian Police (b. 17th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1903; on spl. duty at Calcutta in connection with visit of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales, Dec., 1905, to Jan., 1906; supt., April, 1912; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., May, 1924. and again, Feb., 1927; confd., Aug., 1929; ret'd., July, 1937.

WALKER, G. E. D., Indian Police (b. 9th Sept., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Assam, on probn., 27th Nov., 1934; asst. to pol. officer, Sadiya, May, 1937.

WALKER, GAVIN, Indian Police (b. 29th Nov., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Bengal, Nov., 1933; confd., June, 1936.

WALKER, GEORGE DAVID, M.B.E., M.A. B.Sc., Indian C.S. (commr., Assam) (b. 23rd Sept., 1884).—Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edinb., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 4th Dec., 1908, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1912; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Oct., 1916, to April, 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; dep. commr., Nov., 1924; offg. commr., Nov., 1932; commr., Oct., 1935; Assam repres. to Indian legis. assembly (nom.), Jan., 1938.

WALKER, Sir GILBERT THOMAS, Kt., C.S.I., M.A., D.Sc., Ph.D., F.R.S., late Meteorological Dept.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge (late Fellow); senior wrangler, 1889; apptd. special scientific asst. to the meteorological reporter to the govt. of India, 15th Dec., 1902; meteorological reporter and dir.-genl. of Indian observatories, Jan., 1904; dir.-gen. of observatories, Sept., 1906; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; Kt., June, 1924; prof. of meteorology, Impl. Coll. of Science and Technology since 1924.

WALKER, HAROLD MACKENZIE, Indian State Ry's. (divl. rolling stock officer, N.W. Ry.) (b. 26th July, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. carr. and wagon supt., 17th Nov., 1915; divl. rolling stock officer, Nov., 1928; offg. divl. supt. (junr.), N.W. Ry., Jan., 1936; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Sept., 1936 to Sept., 1937.

WALKER, JAMES, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 4th Dec., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 24th Nov. 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1925.

WALKER, JOHN NORMAN, C.I.E., M.R.C.S., M.R.C.P., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 30th Mar., 1872).—First commn., 28th Jan., 1899; offg. civil surgn., United Provs., May, 1903; confd., Aug., 1907; supt., dist. jail (in addn.), Oct., 1908; temp. reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to March, 1917; civil surgn. and prof. of midwifery, King George's Med. Coll., Lucknow, June, 1919; ditto, and chief medl. officer, Delhi, Oct., 1921; surg. to H.E. the Viceroy, April, 1924; inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Central Provs., Sept., 1927; ret'd., Mar., 1929; dir. medl. dept., Nizam's govt., C.I.E., Jan., 1933; name changed by Deed Poll to John Norman Norman-Walker.

WALKER, RONALD LESLIE, Indian C.S. (dep. secty. fin. comm. and marine depts., Bengal) (b. 9th Apr., 1896).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1920; arrived 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, fin. dept., March, 1924; under-sec., finl., commerce and marine depts., Nov., 1924; offg. dep. sec. to ditto, March, 1926; offg. secty., Indian Tariff Board, April, 1928; confd., Feb., 1929; offg. dir.-genl., comml. intell., Nov., 1928; offg. magte. and collr., July, 1930; confd., Oct., 1932; offg. dep. secty., fin., commerce and marine depts., Bengal, Mar., 1934; confd., Sept., 1934; offg. secty., ditto, Oct., 1934.

WALKER, SAMUEL, C.I.E., B.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (N.W.F.P.) (b. 16th Oct., 1875).—Joined the service, 5th Jan., 1901, as temp. engr., Punjab; asst. engr., July, 1906; exec. engr., Jan., 1910; offg. supt. engr., March, 1922; confd., Sept., 1925; chief engr. (temp.), Nov., 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; ret'd., Apr., 1931.

WALKER, THOMAS JACK, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 4th July, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 15th Dec., 1923; offg. addl. supt., Mar., 1932; supt., July, 1936.

WALKER, ULRIC WILLIAM FERRIER, M.C., M.R.C.V.S., Indian Veterinary Service (prof. of surgery, vety. coll., Punjab) (b. 3rd Dec., 1891).

—Joined the service, Aug., 1920; prof. of surgery, Punjab vety. coll., Oct., 1920; offg. princpl., ditto, Mar., 1929, Sept., 1930, Apr., 1932 and Nov., 1933; offg. dir. vety. service, Punjab, Apr., 1935.

WALKER, WILLIAM FRANK, M.C., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 11th July, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Dec., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1924.

WALL, WILLIAM GEORGE POYNER, M.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (princl., trng. coll., Allahabad) (b. 7th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service as prof., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, 30th Nov., 1916; vice-princl., training coll., Allahabad, Aug., 1923; offg. dir., technological laboratory, Bombay, April, 1926; princpl., govt. inter. coll., Allahabad, Dec., 1927; on spl. duty, office of dir. of public instrn., Apr., 1929; offg. inspr. of schools, Oct., 1930; confd., Nov., 1931; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., United Provs., Apr., 1932; princpl. trng. coll., Allahabad, and inspr. of schools, Oct., 1933.

WALL, WILLIAM RICHARD, Income Tax Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 9th Nov., 1893).—Apptd. asst. commr. of income tax, B. & O., 1st Dec., 1923; offg. commr. of inc. tax, Apr., 1930, variously to Mar., 1937.

WALLACE, CHARLES LINDSAY, M.C., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 22nd Nov., 1887).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., London, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Mar., 1917; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Mar., 1918 to Apr., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1920; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1926; dep. commr. (prov. sub.), July, 1927; confd., Jan., 1928; mag. and collr., Nov., 1930; ret'd., July, 1938.

WALLACE, Sir EDWARD HAMILTON, Kt., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th May, 1873).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 16th Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Oct., 1906; sub. collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; judge, chief court, Mysore, July, 1912, to July, 1914; dist. and sess. judge, Madras, July, 1914; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1923; apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, June, 1924; Kt., June, 1931; ret'd., May, 1933.

WALLACE, HILL CECIL, B.A., B.E., V.D., late Indian State Ry's. (b. 18th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. C. & W. supt., 15th Oct., 1908; dist. ditto, July, 1917; supt., Feb., 1925; on deputation to rly. board from 7th June, 1925; princpl., rly. sch. of transpn., Nov., 1925; pers. asst. to dep. ch. mechl. engr., Aug., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Nov., 1920; offg. ch. mechl. engr., May, 1933; ret'd., Jan., 1937.

WALLACE, SAMUEL THOMAS DICKSON, B.Sc., V.C., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 7th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric., 29th Nov., 1919; dep. dir. of agric., Nov., 1924; ret'd., June, 1932.

WALLACE, WALTER IA MES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 18th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Bedford Modern and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambr. joined the service, 13th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1933.

WALLER, Sir DAVID GRIERSON, Kt., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th July, 1872).—Educ. at Bath Coll., and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 30th Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; transf. to Bombay, April, 1901; re-transf. to Madras, Dec., 1901; head asst., Oct., 1906; sub. collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1914; offg. judge, high court, Sept., 1923; apptd. judge, high court, Madras, Nov., 1924; Kt., June, 1932; retd., July, 1932.

WALLER, Right Rev. EDWARD HARRY MANSFIELD, D.D., M.A. (Cantab.), Madras Ecclesi. Dept. (Bishop of Madras) (b. 8th Dec., 1871).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; Bishop in Tinnevelly and Madura, Nov., 1915, to Dec., 1922; Bishop of Madras from 1st Jan., 1923.

WALLER, FREDRICK JOHN, C.I.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Jan., 1884).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., Nov., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1926; confd., Oct., 1930; offg. ch. engr., and secty., confd., Oct., 1920; ch. engr., and secty. to govt., p.w.d., Punjab, May, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1935.

WALLEY, ARTHUR FRANKS, Indian State Rlys. (supt. of tels., Burma Rlys.) (b. 14th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. tel. supt., 16th Nov., 1914; tel. supt., Nov., 1925.

WALLEY, DAVID, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 15th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Grammar School, Swansea, and Jesus College, Oxford. Apptd. after examination of 1930; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.

WALLIS, Sir JOHN EDWARD POWER, Kt., M.A.—Called to the bar (Middle Temple), 1886; apptd. advocate-general at Madras, 22nd Mar., 1900; addl. member of legisl. council of Madras, April, 1904; judge of the high court, March, 1907; knighted, June, 1912; acting chief justice, July, 1914; confirmed, Dec., 1914; resigned, July, 1921; member of judl. ctee. of Privy Council since 1926; has edited several volumes of the State Trials.

WALLIS, LEONARD GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 12th Mar., 1900).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1924; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1924, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. and appts. depts., May, 1929; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Jan., 1930; under-secty. to C.C. and Agent to G.G., N.W.F.P. Aug., 1930; secty. to A.G.G., Eastern states, Apl., 1933; vice-presdnt., State Council, Cooch-Bihar, Nov., 1936.

WALLIS, WILLIAM CLIFFORD, India Office (hr., exec. officer, accnt. gen.'s dept.) (b. 21st Apl., 1900).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 11th Aug., 1919, after previous service from 19th Oct., 1917; second class clerk, 29th April, 1920; exec. officer, Apl. 1, 1922; actg. higher ditto, Sept., 1936; confd., Jan., 1938.

WALMSLEY, Sir HUGH, Kt., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 28th Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1902; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; mag. and collr., Jan., 1906; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1906; re-transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; acg. judge, Calcutta high ct., April, 1913; temp. addl. judge, ditto, Jan., 1915; judge, ditto, Nov., 1915; Kt., June, 1923; retd., Nov., 1926.

WALMSLEY, JAMES ANDREW, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 4th Oct., 1912).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

WALSH, Sir CECIL HENRY, Kt., K.C., M.A. (Oxon), Barr., late Judl. dept., United Provs.—Apptd. offg. judge of the high ct. of jud., Allahabad, 23rd Nov., 1915; judge, ditto, Aug., 1916; knighted, July, 1926; actg. chief justice, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1926; retd., Nov., 1928.

WALSH, EDMUND WILLIAM PATRICK, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Nov., 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1901; exec. engr., Oct., 1909; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1925; confd., Feb., 1929; offg. ch. engr., June, 1931; retd., Nov., 1934.

WALSH, ERIC, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 11th June, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 20th Nov., 1923; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. supt., May, 1927; confd., May, 1937.

WALSH, WILFRED CULLEN DE CLANCY, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 5th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1922.

WALSH-ATKINS, LEONARD BRIAN, B.A., India Office (asst. princpl., econ. and overseas dept.) (b. 15th Mar., 1915).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. princpl., 11th Oct., 1937.

WALTON, Sir CUSACK, Kt., D.S.O., Col. R.E., retd., late Indian State Railways (b. 26th Feb., 1878).—First commn., dated 25th July, 1897; apptd. asst. engr., state rlys., May, 1901; exec. engr., 1911; on mil. duty from Oct., 1914, to Nov., 1919 (dep. asst. dir. of rly. transport, May, 1915; D.S.O., 1916); offg. agent, North Western rly., March, 1924; suptg. engr., July, 1924; agent, N.W. Rly., Apl., 1927; Kt., Jan., 1933; retd., Feb., 1933.

WALTON, HENRY GEORGE, C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Aug., 1876).—Educ. at Newcastle Gr. Sch. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Dec., 1899, and served in the N.W.

Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Dec., 1910; dep. commr., June, 1918; jt. sec., bd. of rev., Oct., 1918; mag. and collr., Oct., 1922; offg. commr., March, 1925, and again, April, 1928; dep. commr., Nov., 1927; commr. (prov. sub.), Dec., 1928; confd., Apl., 1929; C.S.I., Jan., 1932; offg. membr., bd. of rev., Dec., 1931; confd., May, 1932; retd., Dec. 1934.

WALTON, JOHN CHARLES, C.B., M.C., M.A., India Office (asst. under secty. of state) (b. 14th March, 1885).—Educ. at Ton-Bridge Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (Arnold Prize, 1909); secty.'s dept., Admiralty, Dec., 1908; junior clerk, India Office (mil. dept.), 29th Oct., 1909; resdt. clerk, Sept. 1910; transfld. to judl. and public dept., Dec., 1911; reading clerk to council, Dec., 1911; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, July, 1913; on mil. service from May, 1916, to Dec., 1918; senr. clerk, March, 1917; asst. sec., March, 1921; sec. to Indian delegations to Assembly of League of Nations, 1920 and 1923; actg. sec., industries and overseas dept., Jan. to March, 1924; a delegate for India, intl. opium conference, 1924-25; adviser intl. labour conference, 1927; adviser and substitute delegate, ditto, 1928; actg. secty., pol. dept., Jan., 1929; C.B., Jan., 1930; secty., pol. (external) dept., Apl., 1930; asst. under secty. of state, July, 1936.

WALTON, JOHN HUGH, M.A., M.Sc., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 12th Mar., 1890).—Joined the service as supy. agricl. bacteriologist, 25th Oct., 1912; confd., Oct., 1915; I.A.R.O., June, 1915; asst. agricl. bacteriologist, Feb., 1920; agricl., ditto, Apl., 1926; retd., Feb., 1934.

WANCHOO, KAILAS NATH, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 25th Feb., 1903).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Wadhamb Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, 1st Dec., 1926, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. sess. and sub-judge, Oct., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1933; confd., Sept., 1936.

WANCHOO, NIRANJAN NATH, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 1st May, 1910).—Educ. at Rangmahal Mission High School, Lahore, University of the Punjab and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under secty., govt. of Orissa, h. and r. depts., Aug., 1938.

WARD, ALFRED WILLIAM, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 28th Feb., 1874).—Clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-genl.'s dept., 2nd Aug., 1892; princpl. exec. officer, Apl., 1922; retd., Aug., 1932.

WARD, HUMPHREY PLOWDEN, B.A. (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 20th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1925.

WARD, LANCELOT BANGOR, B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 14th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service, 31st June, 1905; asst. acctn.-gen., United Prov., Nov., 1906; ditto in charge paper currency, Dec., 1908; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, July, 1909; ditto and examr. of local fund accts., E. Bengal

and Assam, March, 1911; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1911; ditto, Punjab, Nov., 1914; dep. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, Oct., 1918; ditto, Central Provs., Aug., 1919; dep. comptr., Assam, Feb., 1920; dep. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, Dec., 1923; offg. dep. auditor-gen., mil. test audit, April, 1924; dep. dir. army audit, Eastern Comd., March, 1925 offg. acctnt.-gen., Central Provs., April, 1926; comptr., Assam, Nov., 1926; offg. acct.-genl., Bengal, Dec., 1927; ditto, C.P., Nov., 1928; confd., July, 1929; acct. genl., Madras, Mar., 1931; retd., Dec., 1935.

WARD, RICHARD PERCYVALE, M.C., D.F.C., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Orissa) (b. 28th Aug., 1894).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 7th March, 1921; arrived 1st May, 1921, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. magte. and collr., Apl., 1927; confd., Dec., 1935.

WARD, Sir THOMAS ROBERT JOHN, Kt., C.I.E., M.V.O., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., April, 1896; on depn. with Seistan boundary commn. from Nov., 1902; C.I.E., Jan., 1906; supg. engr., Oct., 1909; M.V.O., Dec., 1911; special duty, Delhi, Oct., 1912; attd. to P.W. secretariat govt. of India, June, 1913; on depn. to service under Siam govt., from July, 1913, to Feb., 1915; chief engr., and sec. to govt., irrigation branch, P.W. dept., Punjab, March, 1915; inspr.-gen. of irrign., India, March, 1917; knighted, June, 1920; retd., June, 1921.

WARD, WILLIAM JOSEPH, B.A., Imperial Customs Service (collr., Madras) (b. 11th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service as probnr., 6th Oct., 1911; asst. collr., Oct., 1913; offg. collr., Oct., 1932; confd., July, 1936.

WARE, FRANK, C.I.E., F.R.C.V.S., Indian Vety. Service (dir. of impl. instit. of vety. research) (b. 22nd Feb., 1886).—Apptd. supt., civil vety. dept., Madras, Nov., 1912; ch. supt., civil vety. dept., Madras, Oct., 1925; offg. dir., Imperial Inst., Vety. Research, Muktesar, Mar., 1926, and again, June, 1929; prinpl., vety. coll., Madras, Nov. 1926; offg. vety. adviser to govt. of Madras, Nov., 1927; confd., May, 1928; dir. of vety. services, Madras, Sept., 1928; offg. dir., impl. instit. of vety. research, June, 1929; confd.; Sept., 1930; offg. animal husbandry expert, Impl. council of agric. research, Aug., 1933; again Mar., 1937 and May, 1938; C.I.E., May, 1937.

WARES, DONALD HORNE, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 3rd April, 1882).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll. and the Univ., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 2nd Dec. 1905, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1912; transfld. to Bengal, July, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1912; K.-i-H. medal, 2nd class, Jan., 1914; s.p.t. sec. bd. of rev., Oct., 1917; mag. and collr. Nov., 1917; and pol. agent, Tippera, June, 1921; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1926; retd., Mar., 1931.

WARIS AMEER ALI (see AMEER ALI, W.).

WARMINGTON, Rev. GUY WILSON, M.A., Eccles. Dept. (senr. chaplain) (b. 22nd May, 1886).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 3rd Feb., 1921; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1923; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1931; offg. archdeacon, March, 1933.

WARNER, HERBERT WALTER, Indian Police (asst. commr.) (b. 21st Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 19th May, 1920; offg. dist. supt., June, 1926; confd., Sept., 1928; asst. commr., Rangoon, Jan., 1936.

WARREN, AUBREY GRANVILLE, Lt.-Col. (elec. inspr.) (b. 17th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. elec. inspr., United Provs., 1st Oct., 1914; offg. ditto, Sept., 1918; confd., Nov., 1934.

WARREN, DOUGLAS DAINTRY, M.C., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 17th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub.-collr. and jt. mag., June, 1922; offg. under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1926; pte. secty. to govt., June, 1929; dir. of land records, Madras, Apl., 1930; on foreign service as commr., corpn. of Madras, June, 1931; offg collr., Nov., 1934.

WARREN, WILLIAM DOUGLAS MARDEN, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (asst. consvr., Bihar) (b. 26th July, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bihar and Orissa, 19th Dec., 1922.

WARTH, FREDERICK JOHN, B.Sc., M.Sc., (Bir.), late Indian Agrl. Service (b. 23rd Mar., 1879).—Apptd. to the service and arrived in India, Oct., 1906, and served as agric. chemist, Burma; physiological chemist, research institute, Pusa, July, 1921; retd., Nov., 1935.

WARWICK, FRANK PHILLIPS, D.S.O., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.) (b. 19th Apl., 1881), First commn. dated 1st Feb., 1906; in mil. employ to 18th March, 1912; civil surg., May, 1920; retd., July, 1931.

WARWICK, GEORGE DUNGATE, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 25th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 17th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Jan., 1927; dir. forest school, Dec., 1936.

WASSOODREW, K. B. (see Vasudev, K. B.).

WATAL, ANAND PRAKASH (see Anand Prakash, W.).

WATCHMAKER, BEHRAMJI KAWASJI, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (b. 7th Jan., 1884).—Apptd. prof., Robertson coll., Jubbulpore, 17th Sept., 1906; offg. princl., King Edward Coll., Amraoti, July, 1932.

WATERBURY, Rev. FRANK GARFIELD, Indian Eccl. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 8th Nov., 1885).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 25th Jan., 1922; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1924; senr. chaplain Jan., 1934.

WATERFALL, CHARLES FRANCIS, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (chief commr., A. & N. Islands) (b. 24th Feb., 1888).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1911, and served in the Central

Provs. as asst. commr., dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1921; confd., Oct., 1924; dir. of land records, Cent. Provs., March, 1930; secty. in settls. and land records, etc., depts., Mar., 1930; offg. financ. secty. to govt., Centr. Provs., Mar., 1933; offg. commr., July, 1933; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Central Provs., June, 1934; C.I.E., June, 1935; ch. secty. to govt., Central Provs., Oct., 1936; C.S.I., May, 1937; chief commr., Andamans, Feb., 1938.

WATERFIELD, THOMAS EDWARD, Indian C.S. (asst. coll., Bombay) (b. 8th Oct., 1905).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., Magdalen Coll., Oxford, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 12th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Bombay as asst. coll.

WATERHOUSE, PERCIVAL, India Office (higher clerical officer, accts. drpt.) (b. 5th June, 1886).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 24th April, 1906 and attached to acctnt.-gen.'s dept., transfd. to pol. dept., April, 1909; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to March, 1919; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

WATERSTON, STEWART, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 7th Feb., 1881).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 28th Nov., 1905, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; assist. to commr. of settls., and dir. of land records, Nov., 1909, to May, 1912; dep. commr., Sept., 1919; rev. sec. to govt. of Central Provs., May, 1925; excise commr., and supt. of stamps, 1926; offg. commr., April, 1927; retd., Mar., 1931.

WATERWORTH, CHARLES HUGHILL, late Indian Police (b. 3rd July, 1885).—Joined the service, 2nd Dec., 1905; supt., Oct., 1913; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., July, 1924, April, 1927, April, 1928, and again, Aug., 1929; confd., Sept., 1930; retd., July, 1934.

WATKINS, CHARLES ROWLATT, C.I.E., B.A., Impl. Customs Service (collr. of salt rev., Madras) (b. 17th Sept., 1882).—Joined the service, 27th May, 1906; asst. collr. of customs, April, 1907; asst. wheat commr., April, 1915; on duty in Mesopotamia, Aug., 1915, to Jan., 1921; C.I.E., Aug., 1917; offg. collr. of customs, Madras, Nov., 1923; ditto, Bombay, Oct., 1926; asst. collr. of customs, Bombay, Nov., 1926; offg. collr. ditto, June, 1927; offg. coll., Madras, May, 1928; confd., Oct., 1930; collr. of salt rev., Madras, Mar., 1932.

WATKINS, OSCAR WILLIE, late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1876).—Joined the service, 8th May, 1903; asst. loco. supt., April, 1917; works manager, April, 1921; retd., Dec., 1931.

WATLING, ROBERT GEORGE, late Indian Police Service (b. 23rd Dec., 1878).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 17th Jan., 1901; asst. supt., July, 1903; supt. (provl.), Jan., 1908; confd., Aug., 1908; retd., Aug., 1932.

WATSON, ANDREW HUGH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 24th July, 1911).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 4th Dec., 1930; confd., Dec., 1932.

WATSON, CHARLES SCOTT MONCRIEFF CHALMERS, D.S.O., O.B.E., Lt.-Col., R.E., late Indian State Railways (b. 25th Feb., 1881).—First commn. dated 2nd May, 1900; joined the railway branch as asst. engr., May, 1905; asst. traffic supt., Feb., 1907; dist. traffic supt., April, 1910; on mil. duty from Sept., 1914, to April, 1920; O.B.E., June, 1919; dep. traffic mgr., March, 1923; divl. supt., Oct., 1924; offg. chief operating supt., Apl., 1926; confd., May, 1928; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

WATSON, CHARLES MARCUS GUY, Indian Police (b. 5th Nov., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1938.

WATSON, DENNIS GEORGE, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 23rd Apl., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Feb., 1912; dist. supt., April, 1924; inspr.-genl., police, Indore, Oct., 1929, and again, May, 1937; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1936; awarded Indian police medal, Feb., 1937; on for service as I.G. of police, Indore State, Mar., 1937.

WATSON, EDWIN, R.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Nov., 1877).—Joined the service, 15th Sept., 1905; asst. engr., Sept., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; junr. dep. agent, Nov., 1925; offg. senr. ditto, Oct., 1930; ret'd., Nov., 1932.

WATSON, ERSKINE PHILLIP, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 1st Oct., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1927; ditto, (provl. perm.), Sept., 1935; confd., Feb., 1936; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

WATSON, GEORGE LEO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provinces) (b. 13th Dec., 1900).—Educ. at the County School, Whitby, and Trinity College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.

WATSON, HAROLD ARGYLL, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 6th Dec., 1884).—Educ. at Nottingham High School and Queen's Coll., Camb., apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived 24th Nov., 1908, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. settlt. offr.; under-sec. to govt., May, 1913, to Sept., 1914; spl. settlt. offr., Oct., 1914; on mil. duty from May, 1918; sub-collr. and Jt. mag., March, 1919; dir. of land records, Jan., 1920; dep. sec. to govt., finance dept., Nov., 1922; manager, Sivaganga Estate, Aug., 1923; collr. and dist. mag., Dec., 1927; secty. to govt., finance dept., Madras, Dec., 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ret'd., May, 1934.

WATSON, Sir HUGH WESLEY ALLEN, Kt., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 12th Sept., 1875).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1897, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., June, 1906; consvr., April, 1921; chief consvr. Feb., 1925; Kt., June, 1930; ret'd., Sept., 1930; timber adviser to H.C. for India, Sept., 1932.

WATSON, JOHN DOUGLAS, Indian State Rlys. (bridge mgr.) (b. 17th Mar., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. exec. bridge engr., 26th Mar., 1925; exec. engr., bridges, N.W. rly., Mar., 1933.

WATSON, JOHN DYKES, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 22nd July, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 4th Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933; services perm. transf. to M.S. and M. Rly., Oct., 1937.

WATSON, JOHN WILLIAM, C.I.E., C.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 8 h Nov., 1874).—In mil. employ from 27th July, 1898; apptd. medl. offr. with H.B.M.'s Consul, Turbat-i-Haidari, 12th Oct., 1903; civil surgn., Bikanir, Aug., 1910; agency surgn., Alwar, April, 1911; ditto, E. Rajputana States, April, 1912; ditto, Bhopal, Nov., 1913; ditto, Bundelkhand, Dec., 1918; ditto, E. Rajputana Sta es, Jan., 1919; C.I.E., June, 1919; civil surgn. Ajmer. and chief medl. office., Rajputana, Nov., 1926; C.B.E., June, 1929; etd., Nov., 1929.

WATSON, ROBERT, N.D.A., Indian Agric. Service (b. 9th June, 1894).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, 28th Dec., 1921.

WATT, WILLIAM HUTCHEON, R.N.R. (ret'd.) Lt.-Comdr., Civil Aviation Directorate (chief aerodrome offcr.) (b. 8th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service as chief aerodrome offcr., on 1st Mar., 1935.

WATTAL, PYARE KISHAN, M.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 10th May, 1890).—Joined the service, 9th May, 1912; asst. acctnt.-gen., Nov., 1912; dep. ditto, April, 1924; examr., ou side audit, Bombay, Nov., 1924; services placed at disposal of Kashmir durbar, 11th June, 1926; dep. acctnt.-genl. (civil), Punjab, Nov., 1932; ditto (works), Oct. 1933; offg. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Apl., 1935; ditto, Madras, Mar., 1936.

WATTERS, WILLIAM JAMES, Indian State Rlys. (dist. electrical engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 26th Sept., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. electl. engr., 17th May, 1920; resdt. ditto, April, 1923; offg. dist. electrical engr., Feb., 1925; confd., Feb., 1930.

WATTS, HAROLD CHRISTOPHER, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. co-svr., Central Provs.) (b. 3rd Sept., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. 16th Dec., 1921; dept. consvr., Nov., 1924; sylviculturist, June, 1933.

WATTS, RALPH PONSONBY, Major., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 24th Sept., 1892).—First commn., dated 23rd Jan., 1914; in mil. employ prior to 1st April, 1925; asst. mag. and collr., Allahabad, April, 1925; first asst. to resident, Baroda March, 1926; secty. to pol. resident in Persian Gulf, Dec., 1927; vice-consul, Ahwaz, May, 1928; under-sec. to resdt., Hyderabad, April, 1930; vice-consul, Kashgar, Nov., 1930; under secty. to pol. resident in Persian Gulf, Dec., 1933; secty. to ditto, Feb., 1934, and again, Oct., 1934; pol. agent, Kuwait, June, 1934; ditto Muscat, June, 1935; and again, July, 1938.

WAUCHOPE, ROBERT STUART, O.B.E., Major I.A., ret'd., late Survey of India (b. 20th Mar., 1887).—First commn. dated 29th Aug., 1906; in mil. employ to 3rd. July, 1910; asst. supt., July, 1910; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; offg. dep. supt., April, 1921; confd., July, 1925; supt., Jan., 1926; ret'd., Mar., 1936.

WAUGH, ARTHUR ALLEN, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., rev. and p.w.d. (b. and r.), United Provs.) (b. 25th July, 1891).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll. Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1914, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., April, 1918; offg. excise commr., March, 1924; mag. and collr. (provl. sub.) July, 1930; confd., Apl., 1931; secty. to govt. U.P., rev. and p.w.d. (b. and r.), Apl., 1934, and again, Sept., 1935; C.I.E., May, 1937.

WAZIR CHAND CHOPRA, B.A., M.I.E. (Ind.), RAI BAHADUR LALA, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th May, 1877).—Joined the service, 29th June, 1899; asst. engr., June, 1900; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; offg. supt. engr., Oct., 1920; confd., Dec., 1921; retd., May, 1932.

WAZIR CHAND, L. MEHRA, Indian Police (b. 11th Sept., 1907).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Punjab, 6th Jan., 1930; confd., Feb., 1932.

WAZIR HASAN, Sir, SAIYID, Kt., B.A., LL.B. (b. 14th May, 1874).—Joined the service as offg. second addl. judl. commr., 20th May, 1920; judge, chief court, Qudh, Nov., 1925; chief judge, Feb., 1930; Kt., Jan., 1932; retd., May, 1934.

WEAKFORD, CHARLES FREDERICK, M.B.E., Printing and Stationery Dept. (contr.) (b. 29th Sept., 1885).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st June, 1915; on deputation to pol. resdy. in Persian Gulf, March, 1916; M.B.E., Dec., 1919; manager, form printing press, Calcutta, March, 1924; overseer, G.I. Press, Calcutta, June, 1924; offg. dep. contr., Jan., 1925; confd., Oct., 1926; offg. contr., May, 1932; and again, Jan., 1935; confd., Mar., 1935.

WEALE, CYRIL, Indian Police (asst. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 12th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 29th Nov., 1921; addl. supt., Mar., 1926; supt., Mar., 1932; prin., police training coll., Sardah, June, 1933; and again, Jan., 1935; asst. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1936; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1938.

WEBB Sir CHARLES MORGAN, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (retd.).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 30th Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr., asst. settlmt. officer, and settlmt. offr.; dep. commr., Feb., 1910; offg. sec. to govt. from July, 1914; confd., Sept., 1915; member of lieut.-govr.'s council, Nov., 1915; offg. chief sec., Sept. to Dec., 1917; chief sec., April, 1919, to April, 1920; member of Burma legisl. council Nov., 1919; commr. Feb., 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1921; chairman, Development Trust, Feb., 1921; Kt., Jan., 1924; retd., Feb., 1926.

WEBB, HORACE OSCAR, Engr.-Comdr., R.I.M., Marine Dept., Bombay (b. 17th Nov., 1881).—Apptd. 2nd engr. and ship surveyor, Mar., 1927; prinpl. engr. and ship surveyor, Bombay, Feb., 1929; engr. and ship surveyor, Karachi, Aug., 1931.

WEBB, JOHN ROBERT DOUGLAS, O.B.E., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. retd. (b. 4th Jan., 1888).—First commn. dated 25th Jan., 1913; O.B.E., June, 1919; in mill employ prior to 17th July, 1923; medl. officer of health, Simla, July, 1923; asst. dir., public health, Delhi, March, 1928; offg. ditto, Madras, Aug., 1931; retd., July, 1936.

WEBB, ALICE, C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon), late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 23rd April, 1880).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay and Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; manager, Sind encumbd. estates, Sept., 1912, to June, 1913; dep. sec. to govt., rev. dept., Sept., 1920; offg. sec. to govt., rev. dept., Feb., 1921; settl. officer (temp.), Nov., 1921; offg. collr. and mag., Nov., 1922; ditto, May, 1924; offg. pte. sec. to H.E. the govr. of Bombay, March, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag. and pol. agent, Sept., 1925; temp. dep. sec. to govt., pol. dept., Nov., 1925; offg. sec. to govt., pol. dept., Jan., 1926; collr., dist. mag., and pol. agent, June, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; retd., June, 1930.

WEBB, Miss M. V., L.R.C.P. & S.E. (b. 15th Sept., 1878).—Prinpl. women's medl. school, Agra, 9th Nov., 1921, to Feb., 1932.

WEBB, WILFRED FRANCIS, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Feb., 1897).—Joined the dept. as asst. commr., Ajmer Merwara, 6th May, 1921; on duty under High Commr., Bagdad, from Dec. 1918; on foreign service in Tonk State from Oct., 1924; under secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1929; secty. to ditto, Mar., 1930; on foreign service in Rewa; diwan, Bundi state, Feb., 1932; pol. agent, Orissa States, Sambalpur, Oct., 1937; ditto, Chhaltesgarh, Raipur, May, 1938.

WEBB-JOHNSON, STANLEY, O.B.E., Indian Legive. Dept. (2nd solicitor) (b. 1st Mar., 1888).—Apptd. asst. solicitor to govt. of India, Feb., 1920; 2nd solicitor, Nov., 1927; O.B.E., Dec., 1930; offg. solicitor, variously from Apl., 1933.

WEBSTER, GEOFFREY ALAN, Indian Police (b. 3rd Mar., 1919).—Apptd. asst. supt., Assam, on probn., Nov., 1938.

WEBSTER, GEORGE EVELYN EDEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 21st April, 1905).—Educ. at Calabar High School, Kingston, Jamaica, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1929; arrived Dec., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., fin. dept., Sept., 1932, July, 1934, and again, Sept., 1935; ditto home and pol. dept., Feb., 1935.

WEIGHTMAN, HUGH, B.A. (Cantab), Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 29th Nov., 1898).—Educ. at Hymer's Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Jan., 1922; arrived, 17th Feb., 1922, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; presdt., Manipur State Durbar, June, 1925, to Feb., 1926; offg. under-secty. to govt., Assam, Oct., 1927; confd., March, 1928; under secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Apl., 1929; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr. in Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1930; secty. to A.G.G. and ch. commr., Baluchistan, Apl., 1932; pol. agent, Kalat, June, 1935; ditto, Bahrain, Oct., 1937; again, May and Oct., 1938.

WEIR, JAMES LESLIE ROSE, C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Indian Pol. Service (b. 29th Jan., 1883).—In mil. employ from 21st Dec., 1900; famine offcr. under pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Jan., 1908; asst. to resdt. Dwalior, Feb., 1908; supy. asst. commt., Punjab, Dec., 1908; pol. asst., Nov., 1909; British trade agent, Gyantse, Dec., 1909; 2nd asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, Mar., 1912; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Sibi, Dec., 1912; temply. reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914; on spl. duty, Persian Gulf, Aprl., 1918; sec. to agent to govr.-genl. and chief commr. in Baluchistan, Nov., 1923; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta, Aprl., 1925; 1st asst. to resident in Kashmir, Oct., 1926; offg. resident in Kashmir, Jan., 1927; pol. officer in Sikkim, Oct., 1928; resdt., Baroda, Jan., 1933; agent to the govr.-genl., Gujerat states, Aprl., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1933; resident, Gujerat States and Baroda, Aprl., 1937; ret'd., Mar., 1938.

WEIR, RALPH SOMERVILLE, M.A., B.Sc. Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instrn. United Provs.) (b. 10th Sept., 1884).—Joined the service as head master, govt. high school, Allahabad, 2nd March, 1917; vice-prinpl., training coll., Allahabad, June, 1920; prinpl. govt. inter. coll., Etawah, Aprl., 1926; inspr. of schools, Aug., 1928; asst. dir. of public instrn., Nov., 1929; offg. dep. dir. of public instrn., U.P., and under-secty. to govt., educn. dept., Aprl., 1932; confd., Feb., 1935; offg. dir., Oct., 1936; confd., Oct., 1937.

WELLBORNE, CYRIL DE MONTFORT, C.I.E., O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Police (inspr.-gen., Burma) (b. 2nd July, 1884).—First commt. 21st Jan., 1903; apptd. asst. supt. of police, Burma, Aug., 1909; dist. supt., Aprl., 1913; on mil. servic., Dec., 1915, to Aprl., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; dep. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1922; offg. inspr.-gen., March, 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928; offg. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1931; confd., Aug., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1936.

WELLS, ARTHUR GEOFFREY, B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 10th Aprl., 1903).—Educ. at King's School, Canterbury, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived Dec., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. magte. and collr.; asst. judge and sess. judge, Feb., 1933; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1935; dist. and sess. judge, Aden, Oct., 1935.

WELLS, DOUGLAS THURBURN, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 12th Oct. 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1926; confd., Mar., 1928; offg. dep. ch. engr., June, 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

WELLS, HARRY LESLIE, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 25th July, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

WELLS, ROBERT TAIT, M.A., M.B., Ch.B., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 24th Aprl., 1878).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1904; in mil. employ till July, 1908; asst. to dir., research institute, Kasauli, Oct., 1908;

plague medl. offr., Punjab, Oct., 1911; on mil. duty, Aug., 1914, to May, 1920; civil surgn., Feb., 1921; ret'd., Aprl., 1933.

WELLS, WILLIAM GEORGE, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 24th Aprl., 1875).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Mar., 1891; dep. supt., Aprl., 1914; on field service from 22nd Aprl., 1917, to 22nd Nov., 1918; asst. to dep. dir.-genl., traffic, Feb., 1920; supt., tele. traffic, Mar., 1921; supt., Agra tele. office, May, 1927; ret'd., Aprl., 1930.

WELSH, JOHN ALBERT VICTOR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 24th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as forest engr., Burma, 30th Nov., 1921; trans. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1931.

WEMYSS, FREDERICK FLEMING, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 7th Dec., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 6th Sept., 1921; asst. commr. of police, Rangoon, Feb., 1927; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1930; confd., Dec., 1930.

WENDT, REGINALD ERNEST GEOFFREY, B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 15th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service, 24th Dec., 1913; asst. acct.-gen., Mar., 1916; ditto, posts and tels., May, 1918; dep. contr., Assam, Mar., 1925; examr., outside audit, Bengal, Oct., 1926; asst. acctnt.-gen., Central revenues, Nov., 1927; dep. ditto, July, 1928; offg. dir. rly. audit, Aprl., 1930; ret'd., July, 1934.

WERNHAM, JOHN WILLIAM KENCH, M.C., B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 8th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Madras, 11th Dec., 1921; ret'd., Jan., 1931.

WESSON, R. A. Major, I.M.S. (civ. surg.) (b. 7th Feb., 1895).—Date of first commn., 2nd Feb., 1932; civil surg., United Provs., Dec., 1934.

WEST, EDWARD JOHN WRIGHT, Indian Posts and Tels. (divl. engr.) (b. 4th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service, 18th Mar., 1904; supt., engrg. branch, 2nd div., Mar., 1919; asst. engr., Aprl., 1921; divl. engr., May, 1935.

WEST, MICHAEL PHILIP, M.A. (Oxon), D.Ph. (Oxon), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 26th Sept., 1888).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1912, as prof., David Hare training coll., Calcutta; vice-prinpl., Dacca training coll., Sept., 1913; prinpl., ditto, Dec., 1920; ret'd., July, 1933.

WEST, STEWART ELLES LAWRENCE, O.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., (junr.), N.W. rly.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 1st March, 1909; on milty. duty, 1st May, 1916; O.B.E., Feb., 1919; dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; offg. dep. ch. comml. manager, Nov., 1925; transpn. supt., Nov., 1930; offg. dep. ch. comml. mgr., Feb., 1932; and again, Jan., 1934; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., N.W. rly., May, 1933; offg. divl. supt., junior, N.W. rly., Mar., 1934; confd., Sept., 1934; ditto (senior), May, 1937.

WEST, WILLIAM FREDERICK, Office of High Commr. for India (controller, store dept.) (b. 13th June, 1882).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class store dept., 22nd Nov., 1900; junior clerk, Aprl., 1909; senr. clerk, Nov., 1915; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; contr., Aprl., 1922.

WESTCOTT, The Most Rev. Foss, M.A., D.D., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (Bishop of Calcutta and Metropolitan of India and Ceylon) (b. 23rd Oct., 1863).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Peterhouse, Cambridge; Bishop in Chota Nagpore, 1905-1919; Bishop of Calcutta, Aug., 1919.

WESTERDALE, JOSEPH STANLEY, B.Sc., M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. eng., Madras) (b. 10th Apr., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Ap il, 1921; offg. sanitary engr., April, 1922; confd., Oct., 1925; on foreign service under Travancore durbar, Aug., 1930, to Aug., 1933; suptg. engr., May, 1938.

WESTLAKE, ALAN ROBERT CECIL, Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 18th July, 1894).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., Frogmal, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 7th Aug., 1921; arrived, 7th Oct., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; subcollr. and jt. mag., July, 1923; offg. collr., Feb., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1931; ditto (provrl. subs.), Sept., 1935; secty., bd. of rev., Madras, Aug., 1933; collr., and dist. mag. (subst.), Sept., 1936.

WESTOBY EDWARD HAROLD, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer) (b. 22nd Apr., 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, 6th Feb., 1920; actg. exec. offr., June, 1927; confd., Nov., 1929.

WESTON, A. T., M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Dept. of Industries, Bengal (dir. of industries, Bengal) (b. 10th May, 1882).—Joined the service 4th March, 1909; temp. works manager, Co sipore Dec., 1915; dep. dir. of industries, Bengal, Nov., 1920; offg. dir. ditto, April, 1925; confd., May, 1927.

WESTON, DONALD, late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 21st Feb., 1872).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Stratford-on-Avon, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 26th Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Dec., 1902; chief presy. mag., Calcutta, Oct., 1903, to Aug., 1904; mag. and collr., April, 1907; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; on special duty in the Bengal sectt., Oct., 1911, to Sept., 1912; commr. of excise and salt, inspr.-gen. of registrn., &c., Bihar and Orissa, from July, 1918; commr., Oct., 1922; offg. member, bd. of revenue, April, 1928; confd., Dec., 1928; retd., Dec., 1929.

WESTON, ERIC, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, ct. of judl. commr., Sind) (b. 8th Dec., 1892).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1916, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; asst. judge, Sept., 1921; asst. judge and asst. sess. judge, Jan., 1924; judge and sess. judge, April, 1929; judl. asst., and addl. sess. judge, Aden; dist. and sess. judge, Aden, Oct., 1933; judl. commr., states of W.I., Sept., 1934; judl. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, June, 1937; judge, ct. of judl. commr., Sind, July, 1938.

WETHERILL, HENRY BUSWELL, C.I.E., late Indian Educl. Service (United Provs.) (b. 21st Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1913; head master, Allahabad, Jan., 1914; inspr. of schools, Jan., 1917; C.I.E., June, 1931; retd., Nov., 1931.

WEYMOUTH, JAMES CHARLES, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., United Provs.) (b. 5th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 30th Dec., 1907; supt., Aug., 1921; asst. inspr.-genl., rly. police, June, 1933; dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1937.

WHARTON, JOHN, late Indian State Rlys. (Burma Rlys.) (b. 6th May, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. loco. carr. supt., 9th Apr., 1908; on milty. duty from 29th May, 1917, to 2nd Jan., 1920; dist. loco. supt., Nov., 1921; retd., June, 1930.

WHAYMAN, EDWARD, late Marine dept., Bengal (b. 10th May, 1874).—Joined the service as offg. asst. shipping master, 4th Dec., 1919; confd. Feb., 1921; dep. shipping master, Apr., 1927; retd., May, 1931.

WHEATLEY, WILLIAM GEOFFREY, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 6th June, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 26th Sept., 1924; exec. engr., Apr., 1934.

WHEELER, Rev. CHARLES ERNEST RUAPERU, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Madras) (b. 13th Oct., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 20th Dec., 1920; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1924; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1930.

WHEELER, EDWARD OLIVER, M.C., Lt.-Col. R.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 18th Apr., 1890).—Joined the service, 23rd June, 1910; in milty. employ to 8th Dec., 1919; asst. supt., 9th Dec., 1919; offg. dy. upt., Oct., 1920 on depn. with expedition for ascent of Mount Everest from 15th April to 20th Oct., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1926; confd., Oct., 1927.

WHEELER, Sir HENRY, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S.
