(b. 2nd June, 1870).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 14th Dec., 1891; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1898; under-sec. to govt., various depts., July, 1898, to May, 1899; junr. sec., board of rev., April, 1902; member of salt committee, 1903-04, and drainage commn., 1906-07; mag. and collr., Sept., 1906; sec. to Royal Commn. on Ind an Decen rallisat on, Nov., 1907, to April, 1909; sec. to govt., finl. and munipl. depts., July, 1909; C.I.E., Jan., 1910; sec. to govt. of Ind. a Home Dept., Feb., 1912 to 1916; addl. member, govr.-gen.'s council; C.S.I., Jan., 1914; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1917; member, Bengal exec. counci, April, 1917; K.C.S.I., June, 1921; govr. of Bihar and Orissa, 12th April, 1922; retd., Oct., 1927; member of council of India, Oct., 1927; vice-presdt., ditto, Aug., 1932; delegate to disarmament conf., Geneva, Feb., 1932.

WHEELER, THE VEN. HUGH TREVOR, M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (Punjab) (b. 27th Sept., 1874).—Apptd. proby. chaplain, 23rd Oct., 1902; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1904; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1914; archdeacon of Lahore, Jan., 1919; retd., Apr., 1930.

WHELAN, ERNEST CARNEGIE, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr. N.W. Rly.) (b. 2nd Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; govt. inspr. of rlys., Burma, Apr., 1932.

WHELAN, JOHN PRATT, Indian Police (b. 18th Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 22nd Nov., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Nov., 1927.

WHISH, ERIC VIPAN, O.B.E., Capt., R.I.M., rettd. (b. 31st Aug., 1878).—Apptd. principal officer, Bombay Dist., 1st Feb., 1930; rettd., July, 1931; nautical adviser to the govt. of India until May, 1935.

WHITAKER, CECIL CHARLES, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 6th Nov., 1911).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934.

WHITAMORE, VERNON NORTHWOOD, O.B.E., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. rettd. (b. 7th June, 1879).—Firs. commn., 1st Sept., 1905; O.B.E., June, 1919; apptd. civil surgn., Punjab, May, 1923; rettd., June, 1934.

WHITBY, ROBERT BERTRAM HINDHAUGH, Indian State Rlys. (supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 21st Aug., 1897).—Joined the service, Nov., 1919; asst. traffic supt., Dec., 1920; supt., senr. scale, Apr., 1936.

WHITCOMBE, CHARLES EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 30th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service, 6th Oct., 1911; asst. engr., Oct., 1912; on mil. servic. from 6th Oct., 1917, to 5th Oct., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; rettd., Jan., 1935.

WHITE, ARTHUR DENHAM, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., rettd. (b. 26th Feb., 1879).—Joined the service 1st Feb., 1905; arrived 18th Dec., 1905; in mil. employ. to March, 1910; services placed at the disposal of chief commr., Assam, April to Oct., 1914; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1914, to Sept., 1917; offg. p.o.r. med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, Oct., 1917; offg. civil surgn., April, 1919; ditto (permanent), Jan., 1921; offg. protector of emigrants, Calcutta, Oct., 1924; rettd., Feb., 1934.

WHITE, ARTHUR JOHN STANLEY, O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Aug., 1896).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1922; arrived, 26th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., Nov. 1924; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1928; confd., Dec., 1934; O.B.E., June, 1932; secty. to finl. commr., Burma, Nov., 1933; secty. to govt., rev. dept., Burma, Dec., 1934; secty. educn. dept., Burma, Mar., 1935; and again, Dec., 1936; rettd., Mar., 1938.

WHITE, CHARLES FITZWILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. works manager, 7th Jan., 1925; works manager, Feb., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr. G.I.P. Rly., Nov., 1933; provl. confd., Sept., 1937.

WHITE, GERALD SEBASTIAN, O.B.E., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 12th Feb., 1878).—Apptd. asst. regr., April, 1911; dep. regr., Feb., 1915; offg. admin.-gen. and offl. trustee, April, 1926; offg. regr., high ct., July, 1927; offg. master, ditto, Sept., 1927; confd., Sept., 1929; offg. registrar, Nov., 1929; confd., July, 1930; O.B.E., Jan., 1938; rettd., Mar., 1938.

WHITE, REV. JACK, Indian Ecdl. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 12th Mar., 1903).—Apptd. 1st Apl., 1930; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1932.

WHITE, JOHN GROVE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 30th Aug., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. dist. loco. supt., 23rd Dec., 1911; dist. loco. supt., June, 1917; pers. asst. to dep. mech. engr., July, 1925; works manager, Sept., 1928; rettd., Sept., 1935.

WHITE, LESLIE STUART, Indian C.S. (judl. secty. to govt., United Provs.) (b. 30th March, 1888).—Educ. at Marlborough, and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 27th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; asst. supt. and judge, small cause ct., Dehra Dun, April, 1917, to May, 1918; jt. mag., April, 1917; on mil. duty, Aug. to Dec., 1918; dep. secty. to govt., and dep. leg. remembr., Oct., 1921; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1923; offg. ditto, Jan., 1924; confd., Nov., 1926; judl. secty. to govt., United Provs., July, 1934.

WHITE, M. H. GLEESON, late Indian Forest Service (b. 13th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Madras, 20th Oct., 1930; rettd., Dec., 1931.

WHITE, NICHOLAS, C.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 7th May, 1875).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service 1st Oct., 1897, as asst. examr., P.W. accts.; employed in Sibpur Eng. Coll., March to Aug., 1898; asst. engr., Nov., 1898; exec. engr., March, 1908; suptg. engr. (temp.), July, 1916; confirmed, Jan., 1919; chief engr. and sec. to govt., P.W.D. irrigation branch, March, 1925; C.B.E., June, 1929; rettd., May, 1930.

WHITE, SYDNEY WILFRED, Indian State Rlys. (bridge engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 22nd June, 1884).—Joined the service, 7th Nov., 1921; resdt. engr., Feb., 1924; exec. engr. (junr.), E.I. rly., July, 1927.

WHITE, WILLIAM PURCELL, late Indian Police Service (b. 31st July, 1877).—Joined the service, 4th Nov., 1898, and posted to Central Prov.; dist. supt., Jan., 1907; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Oct., 1920; dep. inspr.-gen., Jan., 1923; rettd., Jan., 1929.

WHITEHEAD, GEORGE SYDNEY, B.Sc. (Econ.), India Office (exec. offcr., milty. dept.) (b. 15th Nov., 1915).—Apptd. exec. offcr., 12th Dec., 1934.

WHITEHEAD, GEORGE UNSWORTH, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 5th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at Bury Grammar Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., Jan., 1932; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apr., 1933; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1936.

WHITEHEAD, THOMAS ALEC, C.I.E., Indian Forest Service (chief consvr., forests, Madras) (b. 8th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 30th Oct., 1907, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1912; offg. consvr., June, 1923; confd., Feb., 1926; chief consvr., June, 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938.

WHITEHOUSE, GEORGE EDWARD, Indian Police (asst. supt., United Provs.) (b. 22nd July, 1911).—Joined the service, 26th Nov., 1930; asst. supt., Nov., 1932; offg. A.D.C. to govr., U.P., Dec., 1933; offg. supt., June, 1936.

WHITEHOUSE, RICHARD HENRY, M.Sc., D.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service.—Joined the service, 16th Sept., 1917; vice-prinpl., C.T. Coll., Lahore, Sept., 1922; offg. prinpl., Sept., 1924; retd., May, 1929.

WHITEHOUSE, VICTOR, Indian Police (b. 31st May, 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs. on probn., 1st Nov., 1935.

WHITEHURST, SQUIRE, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 21st Sept., 1878).—Joined the service, 31st Oct., 1913; asst. works manager, Feb., 1920; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., May, 1925; works manager, Dec., 1927; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., Feb., 1929; retd., Nov., 1931.

WHITLEY, ROGER, B.A., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 21st June, 1879).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 16th Feb., 1906; resdt. engr., Sept., 1912; dist. engr., Feb., 1925; retd., Mar., 1934.

WHITLOCK, PERCY ODDIE, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 15th Mar., 1884).—Joined the service as prof., 23rd Dec., 1914; inspr. of schools, Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1925; prof. of English, Ravenshaw Coll., Cuttack, Feb., 1926; dep. dir. public instrn., Dec., 1929; offg. prinpl., Patna Coll., Feb., 1932; prof. of English, ditto, Aug., 1933; retd., Apl., 1935.

WHITMORE, FRANK BELWORTHY, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 6th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as head master Patna collegiate school, 7th Dec., 1915; prinpl., training coll., Cuttack, June, 1923; retd., Mar., 1933.

WHITNEY, ALBERT, Indian State Railways (dist. signal engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 9th Mar., 1886).—Joined the service, 16th Jan., 1915; dist. signal engr., G.I.P. rly., Mar., 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr., Apl., 1936.

WHITNEY, WILLIAM WELCH, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 11th May, 1888).—Joined the service, 25th Feb., 1921, as asst. dist. loco. supt.; asst. supt., E.I. rly., Feb., 1925; transpn. supt., E.I. rly., May, 1934.

WHITTAKER, ARNOLD, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 27th July, 1900).—Educ. at London Sch. of Economics, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1924; arrived, 9th Dec., 1924, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty. to govt. appt. and pol. depts., Dec., 1927; ditto home dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1930; served in Bengal from Feb., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1938; magte. and collr. (confid.), 1938.

WHITTAKER, HARRY, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., A.M.I.E.E. (b. 23rd Feb., 1879).—Prinpl. of technical and mechanical engrg. coll., Lahore, 2nd April, 1923, to Oct., 1934.

WHITTAKER, HERBERT, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bihar) (b. 26th June, 1903).—Educ. at Colne Grammar School, School of Economics, London, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. judl. commr., Chota Nagpur, Mar., 1934; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1936; regr. high ct., Patna, 1938.

WHITTLE, GEORGE WILLIAM MURDOCH, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 7th April, 1907).—Educ. at Secondary School, Washington Village, Durham, Durham University, and King's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, December, 1930, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.

WHITTY, Sir JOHN TARLTON, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st Feb., 1876).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., New Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 29th Dec., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dept. collr., Dec., 1908; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; mag. and collr., April, 1912; C.I.E., Jan., 1916; manager of the Bettiah Wards estate, Apr., 1916; offg. commr., April, 1925; confid., Nov., 1928; temp. member of council, B. & O., April, 1929; confid., Dec., 1931; C.S.I., Jan., 1932; acting govr., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1934; retd., Feb., 1935; knighted, June, 1935.

WHITWORTH, CHARLES STANLEY, C.I.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th June, 1880).—Joined the service, 24th Oct., 1909, as asst. mining manager; senr. asst. coal supt., Dec., 1911; chief mining engr., April, 1922; C.I.E., June, 1927; retd., June, 1935.

WHITWORTH, HUGH HOPE ASTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 21st May, 1914).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

WHURR, LEONARD ERNEST, Indian Police (b. 1st Mar., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 7th Dec., 1924; offg. dist. supt., Mar., 1928.

WHYTE, Sir ALEXANDER FREDERICK, K.C.S.I., M.A.—Lecteur adjoint at the Sorbonne, 1905-6; special commr. on industrial insurance, Vienna and Budapest, 1908; Lieut., R.N.V.R., on special duty, 1914-17; M.P. for Perth City, 1910-18; president, Indian legve. assembly, 1920 to 1925; Kt., Jan., 1922; K.C.S.I., June, 1925; author of the Old Quadrangle (part), 1907, The Practice of Diplomacy (from the French of François de Callières).

WHYTE, ALEXANDER HORNEY, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd Dec., 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1901, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1924; suptg. engr. (provl. perm.) Nov., 1928; confid., July, 1930; retd., Dec., 1934.

WHYTE, QUINTIN ALEXANDER, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th Mar., 1909).—Educated at Morgan Academy, Dundee, St. Andrew's University and Downing College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933; and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collector; retd., Oct., 1935.

WICKENDEN, THOMAS DOUGLAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Central Provs.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1903).—Educ. at St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1925; arrived, 5th Dec., 1925, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., June, 1929; sub-judge, Oct., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1932, May, 1933, and again, May, 1934; confd. Jan., 1936.

WICKHAM, EDWARD THOMAS RUSCOMBE, M.V.O., Lt.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Dept. (b. 4th May, 1890).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1910; in mil. employ to 31st Dec., 1918; on special duty in Europe with H.I.M. the Shah of Persia, Aug.-Dec., 1919; asst. to consul, Ahwaz, Dec., 1921; sec., Kabul Legation, Feb., 1922; offg. H.B.M.'s consul and agent of govt. of India in Khorasan, March, 1925; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta Pishin, March, 1926; sec. to agent to gov. genl. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1926; counselor, Kabul legation, March, 1927; M.V.O., March, 1928; pol. agent, Chagai, Nov., 1928; on spl. duty under A.G.G. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, May, 1929; sec. to agent to gov.-genl., Baluchistan, Nov., 1929; pol. agent and dep. commr. in Quetta-Pishin, Oct., 1930; offg. dep. secty. to the govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., May, 1932; retd., Jan., 1935.

WICKHAM, HARRY TEMPLE, late India Police Dept.—Joined the service, 10th Nov., 1904; supt. of police, Dec., 1912; dist. offr., frontier constabulary, Dec., 1913, to Oct., 1917; comdt. mily. police, N.W.F. Prov., Sept., 1919; retd., June, 1925; apptd. commr. for the Superior Services (India) Family Pension Fund, Oct., 1937.

WICKRAMASINGHE, PERCIVAL HERBERT, B.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th Dec., 1909).—Educated at Royal College, Colombo, University of Ceylon and Trinity College, Cambridge; joined the service 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; retd., Dec., 1934.

WIGHTWICK, HERBERT MILNER, Lt.-Col. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 17th Apl., 1885).—First commn., 16th Aug., 1905; apptd. asst. pol. agent and asst., Aden, Sept., 1909; controller, hostile trading concerns, 1917; 1st asst. to resdt., Baroda, Oct., 1925; pol. agent, Banas Kuntha, March, 1926; 1st asst. resdt., Aden, Mar., 1931; resdt., Kolhapur and pol. agent, S. Maratha country states, Oct., 1932; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha, May, 1933; offg. ch. commr. and C. in C., Aden, Dec., 1933; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, Feb., 1934; resdt., W. Rajputana states, Aug., 1934; offg. resdt., Jaipur, in addn., June, 1936; offg. resdt., Jaipur, in addn., Sept., 1936, to Feb., 1937; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar agency, Nov., 1938.

WILBERFORCE-BELL, Sir HAROLD, K.C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 17th Nov., 1885).—First commn., 29th Nov., 1905; asst. pol. agent, Kolhapur, Jan., 1910; on special duty in connection with the Delhi Durbar, 1911; pol. agent, Sorath (Kathiawar) Nov., 1912; tutor and guardian to H.H. Minor Nawab of Junagadh, Apl. to Dec., 1914; on mil. duty, 26th Dec., 1914, to 9th Dec., 1919; asst. mil. sec. to H.E. the C.-in-C. in India, May, 1918, to March, 1919; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, Feb., 1920; dep. sec., pol. dept., Bombay, 1920; asst. resdt., Aden, March, 1922; pol. agent, Kathiawar, Jan., 1924; adviser to H.H. the Raja of Chamba, 1925; sec. to agent to gov.-genl. in C.I., Nov., 1926; pol. agent in Bhopal, May, 1927; ditto in Southern States of C.I. and in Malwa, July, 1927; offg. dep. secty. govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1928; confd., May, 1929; offg. pol. secty., ditto, July, 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; pol. agent, Southern Maratha country states, Apl., 1932; on special duty under for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1932; A.G.G. Deccan states and resdt., Kolhapur, Apl., 1933; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Punjab states, Oct., 1934; resdt., 1st class, confd., Mar., 1937; resdt. for Punjab States, Apl., 1937; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1938; author of "The History of Kathiawar," and other works.

WILBY, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Indian Finance Dept. (b. 22nd May, 1882).—Joined the service, 1st March, 1909; asst. auditor, O. & R. Rly., June, 1918; auditor, J.B. Rly., March, 1921; govt. examr. of accts., B. & N.W. Rly., June, 1922; dep. auditor, O. & R. Rly., March, 1922; dep. acctnt.-gen., rlys., Sept., 1925; asst. auditor, N.W. Rly., Dec., 1926; services placed at disposal of Jodhpur Durbar, Jan., 1927, to Feb., 1930; with finl. commr. for rlys., Nov., 1930; with Burma Rlys., Feb., 1931; offg. ch. accts. officer, Burma rlys., June, 1933; addl. dep. dir., rly. clearing accts., (temp.), Oct., 1934; offg. ch. auditor, N.W. rly., June, 1935.

WILCOCK, JOHN STEWART, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (secty. to govr., Orissa) (b. 6th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Manchester Grammar Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, 30th Nov., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under secty. to govt., pol. and appts. dept., Mar., 1932; offg. secty. to A.G.G., Eastern States, Mar., 1935; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1935; pte. secty. to govr., Orissa, Apl., 1936; secty., ditto, Apl., 1937.

WILCOX, NEVILLE CECIL, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 2nd Feb., 1876).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1896; joint opium officer Malwa, March, 1924; retd., Feb., 1931.

WILD, ARTHUR CLEMENT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th June, 1875).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 24th Nov., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge; asst. rembr. of legal affairs, and sec. to council of govr., March, 1908, to Feb., 1909;

dist. and sess. judge, April, 1914; judge and rembr. of legal affairs and secy. to govt., legal dept., Oct., 1919; agent for Sardars in the Deccan, Oct., 1923; addl. judl. commr. of Sind, Jan., 1929; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1929; judl. commr. of Sind, Nov., 1930; retd., Sept., 1933.

WILKS, Sir GILBERT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt. (pol. and serv. dept., Bombay)) (b. 25th Mar., 1880).—Educ. at Perse Gr. Sch., and Sr. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; supt. of land records and regn., Nov., 1910; supt. of land records, Aug., 1915, to May, 1916; temp. dep. dir. of civil supplies, June, 1918; dep. sec. to govt., home dept., Nov., 1921; asst. collr. and sec. to govt., finance dept., Dec., 1923; collr. and ditto, Oct., 1925; member legisl. council, 1924, 1927, 1930, 1931, and 1932; also finl. adviser to govt., dev. and P.W. depts., April, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; member, tariff bd., Sept., 1933; presdt., ditto, Oct., 1934; chairman, Bombay Port Trust, June, 1935; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1938; ch. secty. to govt. (pol. and serv. dept.), Bombay, Jan., 1938.

WILKS, JOHN SEBASTIAN, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 31st Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1908; asst. supt., May, 1910; supt., Nov., 1922; dep. commr., Apl., 1929; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1934; confd., June, 1936; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1938.

WILKIE, HUGH GRAHAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., govt. of Burma lands and rev. dept.) (b. 13th Mar., 1893).—Educ. at Uppingham and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to March, 1919; apptd., 7th Oct., 1919; arrived, 9th Dec., 1919, and served in Burma; colonization officer, Jan., 1922; headqrs. asst., May, 1923; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1924; confd., May, 1931; administrator of govt. estates, Nov., 1926; land officer, Rangoon dev. trust, Mar., 1928; offg. secty. to govt., educn. dept., Burma, Oct., 1930; secty., lands and rev. dept., May, 1937.

WILKIE, RALPH SCUDAMORE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 13th March, 1896).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 20th Dec., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., June, 1924; asst. settl. officer, Nov., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1928; confd., Oct., 1934; offg. settlement officer, Mandalay, Nov., 1930.

WILKINS, PAUL BURGMAN, M.C., D.F.C., Indian Police (supdt., Bombay) (b. 11th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 11th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., May, 1929; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1933; supt., Jan., 1936.

WILKINSON, Rev. ERNEST ROLAND, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 24th July, 1882).—Joined the service, 5th Nov., 1913; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1916; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1926; retd., Mar., 1936.

WILKINSON, HECTOR RUSSELL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 11th March, 1888).—Educ. at Clifton and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, Nov., 1917; pte. sec. to H.E. the govr. of Burma in addn., Feb., 1918; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, judl., pol. and appt. depts., April, 1918; pte. sec. to the govr. of Bengal, Nov., 1918, to Dec., 1919, and from March, 1922, to April, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; mag. and collr., Feb., 1927; offg. secty. to govt. of Bengal, dept. of educn., Oct., 1931; confd., Apl., 1932.

WILKINSON, WALTER HUGH JOHN, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st May, 1874).—Educ. at Winchester, and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 29th Nov., 1897, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. collr. of customs, &c., and chief inspr. of factories, March, 1902; asst. to resdt., Hyderabad, March, 1903; tutor to Maharaja of Kishengarh, May, 1904; offg. asst. sec. and subsequently offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., June to Dec., 1906; pol. asst., Ajmer, Nov., 1907, and offg. principal, Mayo College; 1st asst. to agent to govt.-gen., Rajputana, Jan., 1909; pol. agent and ditto, Oct., 1909; tutor and guardian to second surviving son of H.H. the Gaekwar of Baroda, March, 1910, to May, 1911; pol. offr. with H.H. the Maharaja of Patiala for the coronation, May to July, 1911; 1st asst. to agent to govt.-gen., Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Sept., 1911, to May, 1912; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., May to Sept., 1912; 1st asst. to agent to govt.-gen., Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Sept., 1912, to April, 1913; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., April to Dec., 1913; served under the War Office in France from Sept., 1914, to Dec., 1918 (four times mentioned in despatches); chevalier of the Order of Leopold I of Belgium, June, 1917; chevalier of the Légion d'Honneur, July, 1917; C.I.E., June, 1919; junr. collr. (seconded), Aug., 1915; 1st asst. to the resdt. in Mysore, and sec. to the chief commr., Coorg, March, 1919; offg. resdt., Baroda, May, 1919; resdt. in Mewar and pol. agent, Kotah and Jhalawar, June, 1920; British Envoy at Court of Nepal, April, 1924; C.S.I., Jan., 1930; retd., Mar., 1932; name changed by Deed Poll to Wilkinson-Guillelard, Feb., 1934.

WILKINSON-GUILLELARD, WALTER HUGH JOHN (see Wilkinson, W.H.J.).

WILL, William Miller, M.B., Ch.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S.) (b. 18th Feb., 1888).—First comm. dated 29th Jan., 1918; in milty. employ prior to 21st Apl., 1925, when he joined medl. store dept., asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Oct., 1935.

WILCOCKS, HAROLD, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., central P.W.D.) (b. 11th May, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1921; exec. engr., Mar., 1927; offg. supt. engr., variously from Apl., 1932.

WILLFORD, GEORGE MARK, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 20th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service as extra asst. consvr., July, 1915; dep. consvr., July, 1930.

WILLIAMS, ALEXANDER THOMAS, India Office (prinr., finl. dept.), (b. 27th Feb., 1886).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 22nd Mar., 1907; asst. editor, 'India Office List,' Oct., 1918; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1919; asst. principal, Apl., 1921; accts. dept., June, 1921; finl. dept., Feb., 1926; joint editor, 'India Office List,' Apl., 1926, to Aug., 1927; reading clerk to Council, Aug., 1927; pte. secty. to perm. under-secty. of state, Sept., 1930, seconded to finl. dept., Jan. to May, 1932; actg. princpl., finl. dept., May, 1933; secty. Ind. staty. rly. bd. ctee., June, 1933; secty. Ind. Res. Bank ctee., July, 1933; princpl., Mar., 1936; apptd. to undertake duties of asst. dir. of Indian rly. companies, Jan., 1936.

WILLIAMS, ALFRED ARTHUR, B.A., Indian Civil Service (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 16th May, 1910).—Educated at Merchant Taylor's School, Crosby, and Selwyn College, Cambridge; apptd. after the exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub divl. officer., Oct., 1937.

WILLIAMS, ALFRED EDWARD, O.B.E., V.D., M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Mar., 1878).—Joined the service, 3rd May, 1907; asst. works manager, Nov., 1912; works manager, Nov., 1918; dist. loco. supt., April, 1919; dist. mech. engr., Nov., 1922; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., April, 1923; confd., May, 1925; offg. ch. mech. engr., Oct., 1926; and again, Dec., 1931; confd. (provrl.), Mar., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1927.

WILLIAMS, ARTHUR DE COETLOGAN, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (addl. secty. and draftsman to the Govt. of India, legisl. dept., and secty., exec. cl.) (b. 27th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 7th May, 1915, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; cont. mech. transport stores depot, Rawal Pindi, Dec., 1916, to March, 1919; on mil. duty, May to Sept., 1919; services lent temporarily to govt. of Assam as offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1925; offg. sec., govt. of Bengal, legl. dept., and sec., legis. council, Feb., 1926; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1926; addl. dep. secty., legis. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1927; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Apl., 1929; on spl. duty, home dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1931; sec. to govt., Bengal, legl. dept., Feb., 1934; supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, July, 1934; offg. jt. secty. to Govt. of India, legl. dept., Mar., 1935; addl. dep. secty., ditto, Oct., 1935; secty., council of state, Dec., 1935; dep. secty. to govr. of India, legis. dept., Apl., 1936; addl. jt. secty. ditto, Nov., 1936; offg. secty. to the govr.-genl.'s exec. council in addn., Nov., 1936; confd., Mar., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1938.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES FREDERICK VICTOR, Indian C.S. (collr. and dep. magte.) (b. 21st May, 1898).—Educ. at West Ham and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1923; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1923 and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-

collr. and jt. magte., Feb., 1928; under secty. to govt., ch. secretariat, Madras, July, 1929; jt. secty., Indian round-table conf., London, Nov.-Dec., 1932; under secty. to govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1933; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Apl., 1934; secty. to agent of the govt. of India in South Africa, Sept., 1934; collr. and dep. magte., Aug., 1937; collr. civil and mil. station, Bangalore, Aug., 1937.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES ROBERT FARMER, M.C., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 19th June, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. con vr., 21st March, 1921; dep. consvr., March, 1922; prinpl., Madras forest coll., Coimbatore, July, 1924, to July, 1931.

WILLIAMS, CUTHBERT ARTHUR, late Indian Stores Dept. (b. 20th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. signal engr., Indian State Rlys., 9th Oct., 1910; with Indian Munitions Board from 13th Oct., 1917, to 24th Feb., 1920; contr. of sales, Calcutta, Oct., 1920; offg. contr. of stores, O. & R. Rly., May, 1921; dep. dir. of purchase, June, 1923; offg. dir. of purchase, Mar., 1931; ret'd., July, 1935.

WILLIAMS, DAVID, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (transportation supt., G.I.P., Rly.) (b. 12th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as dist. loco. supt., 25th March, 1920; asst. trans. supt., 1st Nov., 1922; supt., May, 1926; offg. divl. trans. supt., variously from Aug., 1926.

WILLIAMS, EDWIN OWEN, Indian Forest Engrg. Service (b. 22nd Jan., 1894).—Joined the service as forest engr., Punjab, 11th Nov., 1921; lecturer, forest coll., Delra Dun, Apl., 1938.

WILLIAMS, EMILY MATILDA, India Office (higher clerical officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 21st Feb., 1894).—Appointed clerical officer, 5th Sept., 1921; higher clerical officer, Sept., 1932.

WILLIAMS, FRANK ROBERT, India Office (higher clerical officer, fin. dept.) b. 26th Nov., 1887).—Apptd. clerical officer, 15th Aug., 1921; actg. higher clerical officer, 1st Nov., 1926; confd., June, 1930.

WILLIAMS, GEOFFREY BAYNTON, M.C., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 19th Aug., 1896).—First commn. dated 13th Oct., 1914; in milty. employ prior to 27th July, 1923; asst. collr. and magte., Bareilly, July, 1923; under-secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in C.I., March, 1926; ditto, in States of W.I., Dec., 1926; guardian and tutor to heir-apparent of Nawab of Junagadh, Dec., 1928; secty. to A.G.G., in the states of W.I., Nov., 1932; under-secty. ditto, Oct., 1933; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha, Dec., 1933; secty. to A.G.G. Eastern states, Dec., 1935; pol. agent, Bhopal, Mar., 1936.

WILLIAMS, HAROLD VINITH, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 9th June, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 13th Oct., 1931; exec. engr., Oct., 1930; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

WILLIAMS, HARRY EDWIN, Indian State Rlys. (E.B. Rly) (b. 15th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 23rd July, 1909; dist. ditto, Aug., 1922; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

WILLIAMS, Rev. HENRY FRANK FULFORD, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 11th Nov., 1885).—Joined the service, 20th Feb., 1913; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1916; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1926; retd., Dec., 1936.

WILLIAMS, HERBERT ARMSTRONG, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., D.S.O., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.), (b. 11th Feb., 1875).—First commn. dated 27th Jan., 1900; in mil. emp. to June, 1903, when he was apptd. dep. sany. commr., Burma; resdt. medl. offr., Rangoon genl. hosp., Jan., 1905; plague medl. offr., Burma, Sept., 1906; supt., Rangoon lunatic asylum, Jan., 1907; resdt. medl. offr., Rangoon genl. hosp., Dec., 1907; D.S.O., Nov., 1900; resdt. medl. offr., Mandalay, Nov., 1912; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, April, 1915, to Jan., 1920; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Adl., 1929; retd., Jan., 1930.

WILLIAMS, HUBERT WATSON, B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Sept., 1884).—Educ. at Richmond (Yorks) Gr. Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived 27th Nov., 1909, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfid. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1916; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1924; confd. Jan., 1927; registrar, high ct., Patna, March, 1926; retd., Sept., 1929.

WILLIAMS, Rev. JAMES ERNEST HARRIS, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt.—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 16th March, 1906; junr. chaplain, March, 1909; senr. chaplain, March, 1919; retd., Mar., 1929.

WILLIAMS, JAMES HAROLD, late Survey of India (b. 14th Oct., 1879).—Joined the service as proby. sub-assst. supt., 20th Nov., 1899; confd., Nov., 1900; extra asst. supt., Feb., 1908; on milty. duty from 1st Oct., 1915, to 12th Aug., 1921; supt., Oct., 1929; retd., Apl., 1932.

WILLIAMS, JOHN HOWARD FRY, Indian Police (b. 6th Oct., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Assam, on probn., 10th Nov., 1935; asst. to pol. offcr., Sadiya, May, 1937.

WILLIAMS, RICHARD AELWYN ELLIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 5th Dec., 1901).—Educ. at Univ. of Wales and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1925; arrived, 7th Dec., 1925, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt. pol. dept., March, 1930; offg. asst. pol. agent, Orissa feudatory states, Mar., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1933.

WILLIAMS, RUSHBROOK—see RUSHBROOK WILLIAMS.

WILLIAMS, SEVERN, O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 14th Feb., 1884).—First commn., 27th Jan., 1904; in mil. employ. to July, 1910; probr., pol. dept. and asst. pol. agent, Mekram, July, 1910; pol. agent, Tochi, Nov., 1920; city mag., Peshawar, May, 1921; under-sec. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Oct., 1923; pol. agent and dep. commr., Sibi, March, 1925; pol. agent, Zhob, Dec., 1927; ditto, Chagai and dep. commr., Shorarud, Nov., 1929; pol. agent, Loralai, Apl., 1931; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, Apl., 1935;

O.B.E., Nov., 1935; asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Apl., 1936; and again, Oct., 1938; resdt., ditto, July, 1938.

WILLIAMS, THOMAS TALIESIN, M.A., B.Sc., F.S.S., late Indian Educ. Service (Bengal) (b. 30th Aug., 1876).—Joined the service, 30th Dec., 1910; on mil. duty, Aug., 1918, to May, 1919; on special duty in office of collr. of income tax, Calcutta, May, 1919; collr. of income tax, Calcutta, Aug., 1919; asst. commr. of income tax, April, 1922; prinpl., Rajshahi Coll., Nov., 1926; retd., Aug., 1931.

WILLIAMS, Rev. WILLIAM PERCIVAL, B.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, 10th Mar., 1929; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1931; offg., archdeacon, Feb., 1936.

WILLIAMSON, ALEC, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 7th Nov., 1886).—Educ. at Kirkcudbright Academy, Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1910, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settlt. officer; on mil. service, Jan., 1918, to April, 1919; dep. chairman, bd. of trustees for development of Rangoon, March, 1925; dep. commr. (provl. sub.), Feb., 1926; offg. land officer, Rangoon Development Trust, March, 1927; officer i/c., Mandalay municipal commee., Oct., 1929; offg. chairman, Rangoon dev. trust, July, 1933; excise commr., Mar., 1934; commr. of settlements and land records, Burma, Mar., 1936.

WILLIAMSON, HAROLD, O.B.E., M.D., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg.) (b. 26th Feb., 1891).—First commn., 3rd Dec., 1921; offg. agency surg., Quetta, Apl., 1931; confd., Feb., 1933; resdy. surg., Hyderabad, Apl., 1935; civil surg., Sibi and Loralai, Oct., 1935; offg. resdy. surg., Mysore, Mar., 1937.

WILLIAMSON, HERBERT STANSFIELD, M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Leeds Grammar Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 10th Dec., 1901, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. coln. offr., Chenab Canal Colony, July, 1906, to Dec., 1907; junior sec. to finl. commrs., Punjab, April, 1909; on sol. duty in office of finl. commr., Oct., 1910; junr. sec. to finl. commrs., Feb. to April, 1912; offg. dep. commr., April, 1912; settlt. offr., Oct., 1912; employed in finl. dept., India Office, from Jan. to Aug., 1917; on mil. duty from May to Aug., 1918; dep. commr., Feb., 1918; senior sec. to fin. commrs., Punjab, March 1920; retd., Oct., 1922; apptd. sec., Univ. Delegacy for Oriental Students, April, 1923.

WILLIAMSON, Sir HORACE, Kt., C.I.E., M.B.E., late Indian Police (adviser to sec. of state for India) (b. 16th July, 1880).—Joined the service, 8th Dec., 1900, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt., April, 1906; supt., March, 1913; asst. to inspr.-gen. of police, Mar., 1917; M.B.E., Jan., 1919; temply. employed in judl. and public dept., India Office, from Dec., 1920, to April, 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1923; confd., July, 1925; offg. inspr.-genl., May, 1928; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; dir.,

intell. bureau, home dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1931; Knighted, Jan., 1934; ret'd., Sept., 1936; member, Council of India, Dec., 1936; adviser to sec. of state for India, Apl., 1937.

WILLIAMSON, RICHARD HARCOURT, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st Dec., 1879).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Sept., 1913; mag. and collr., March, 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. commr., Sept., 1931; confd., Oct., 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1938.

WILLINGDON, Most Hon. MARQUESS OF, FREEMAN FREEMAN-THOMAS, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., G.B.E., LL.D.—Govr. of Bombay, Apl., 1913, to Dec., 1918; Govr. of Madras, Apl., 1919, to Apl., 1924; Viceroy and govr.-genl. of India, Apl., 1931, to Apl., 1936.

WILLIS, ALFRED CHARLES, M.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 15th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service, 17th Oct., 1908; asst. engr., Oct., 1909; on mil. service from 16th Aug., 1917, to 2nd March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1930; M.B.E., Jan., 1931; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

WILLIS, Sir GEORGE HENRY, Kt., C.I.E., M.V.O., M.I.Mech.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), Col., R.E., ret'd. (b. 21st Oct., 1875).—First commn. dated 3rd Aug., 1895; apptd. to mint dept., June, 1907; dep. mint master, Calcutta, Oct., 1909; M.V.O., Dec., 1911; dep. mint master, Bombay, July, 1912; offg. mint master, Calcutta, Jan., 1913, to Feb., 1915; junr. mint master, Bombay, Oct., 1915; C.I.E., June, 1918; mint master, Bombay, March 1921; senior mint master, May, 1923; master, security printing, India, Feb., 1926; Kt., June, 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

WILLIS, HAROLD MITCHELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 26th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at St. Paul's School, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived 9th Dec., 1915, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. asst. collr. of customs, Oct., 1919; asst. collr. and personal asst. to collr., Nov., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. magte. and Talukdari settlt. officer; offg. dist. magte. and collr., May, 1926, and again, Apl., 1933; confd., Apl., 1935.

WILLMORE, WILLIAM SOUTHWICK, M.D., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 7th Aug., 1872).—First commn., 28th Jan., 1899; apptd. temply. to civil employ, United Provs., May, 1903; civil surgn., ditto, March, 1907; ditto and supt., dist. jail, April, 1909; princpl., med. sch., Agra, May to Oct., 1914; reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to Jan., 1920; prof., King George's Med. Coll., Lucknow, Oct., 1922, to Jan., 1923; supt., dist. jail, Cawnpore, July, 1923; inspr.-genl. of civil hospitals, May, 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1929.

WILLMOTT, HENRY CLAUDE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 18th May, 1914).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

WILLMOTT, HERBERT, India Audit Office (higher exec. officer) (b. 9th June, 1887).—Apptd. clerical officer, 9th Nov., 1921; exec. officer, April 1922; hr. exec. officer, Mar. 1936.

WILLOUGHBY, VERE WILLIAM DIGBY, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 18th Aug., 1901).—First commn., 23rd Dec., 1921; in milty. employ to 20th June, Dec., 1921; in milty. employ to 20th June, 1927; vice-consul, Ahwaz, Dec., 1928; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan Nov., 1931; under secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Mar., 1932; secty. to ditto (temp.), July, 1934; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Aug., 1935; ditto, Zhob, Oct., 1935; vice-consul, Zabul, June, 1938.

WILLS, CECIL UPTON, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.).—Educ. at Tiverton Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 16th Nov., 1901, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr., and regr., judl. commr.'s court; settlmt. offr., Jan., 1906; regr., co-op. credit soc., Dec., 1910; under-sec. to chief commr., May, 1911, to Feb., 1912; commr. of settlts. and dir. of land records, July, 1912; dir. of agric. and industries, Feb., 1913; dep. commr., April, 1915; spl. duty as member of the village Panchayats commee., Jan., 1918; 2nd sec. to chief commr., March, 1918; chief sec. to chief commr., Aug., 1918; spl. duty in sectt., Oct., 1918; dep. dir. of civil supplies, Jan. to April, 1919; excise commr., Cent. Provs., Dec., 1920; supt. of stamps (in addn.), Feb., 1922; offg. commr., April, 1925; confd., Aug., 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; ret'd., July, 1929.

WILSDON, BERNARD HOWELL, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (Punjab) (b. 11th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as prof. of chemistry, govt. coll., Lahore, 17th March, 1913; agric. chemist, March, 1917; prof., Amritsar, April, 1922; inspr. of schools, Jan., 1923; scientific research officer, Aug., 1924; ret'd., Aug., 1929.

WILSON, ANDREW, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. agric., Oolacamund) (b. 27th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. economic botanist, United Provs., 27th Jan., 1912; dep. dir. of agric., I.A.S., on probn., June, 1916; confd., Mar., 1920; spec. offcr., Impl. Cl. of Agric. Research, Oct., 1937.

WILSON, Sir ARNOLD TALBOT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.M.G., D.S.O., M.P., Bt. Lieut.-Col. I.A. (ret'd.).—First commn. dated 19th Aug., 1903; was in milty. employ until Dec., 1907, when apptd. to special duty in S. Persia; H.B.M.'s consul for Arabistan, July, 1909; on special duty under pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Jan., 1911; C.M.G., June, 1912; 2nd asst. to ditto, July, 1912; addl. asst. to ditto for special duty in Khuristan, March, 1913; on special duty as dep. British commr., and later as British commr., Turko-Persian Frontier Boundary Commn., from Aug., 1913; on spl. duty, Persian Gulf, Jan., 1915; dep. chief pol. offr., Basrah, from Jan., 1916; dep. civil commr., Baghdad, from Oct., 1917; offg. civil commr., Baghdad, from March, 1918; C.S.I., June, 1919; K.C.I.E., Dec., 1919; pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Oct., 1920; ret'd., Aug., 1921; M.P., Hitchin, June, 1933.

WILSON, ARTHUR HENRY, B.A., Mil. Accts. Dept., (b. 12th July, 1893).—Joined the service as mil. acct., 2nd Cl., July, 1920; D.M.A.G. (junr.), Dec., 1931; ditto (senr.), Nov., 1932; dep. finl. advr., mil. fin., Mar., 1934.

WILSON, ARTHUR NORMAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 4th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 25th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1935; vice-princl., govt. school of engrg., Rasul, Sept., 1936, to Nov., 1937.

WILSON, CECIL CLAUDE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consr., Madras) (b. 11th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1908, as asst. consvr., dep. consvr., 1914; on milty. service, Oct., 1916, to Mar., 1919; actg. consr., Sept., 1924; confd., March, 1926; forest economist, Dehra Dun, Nov., 1925.

WILSON, CECIL HENRY ERSKINE, late Salt Dept., Bombay (b. 7th Dec., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 15th Aug., 1907, to 17th Aug., 1915; asst. collr. of salt rev., Aug., 1915; on milty. duty, 22nd Nov., 1914, to 20th Sept., 1919; supt. of salt and excise, Sept., 1920; asst. collr. of salt rev. (designation changed), Sept., 1926; on depn. to Jamnagar State, April, 1928 to Apl., 1931; retd., June, 1933.

WILSON, CHARLES WATERHOUSE M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Vety. Service (b. 5th Dec., 1874).—Joined the service, 23rd April, 1906; supt., vety. dept., Aug., 1916; vety. adviser to govt., Central Provs., May, 1921; retd., Dec., 1929.

WILSON, DAVID HUTCHISON, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Railways (N.W. Rly.) (b. 15th Apl., 1875).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 24th Feb., 1905; exec. engr., March, 1912; retd., Mar., 1929.

WILSON, DENIS GREY, Lt.-Col., I.A., retd. (b. 6th Jan., 1880).—First commn. dated 12th Aug., 1899; in mil. employ until July, 1906, when he was apptd. asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.; asst. to resdt., Hyderabad. Aug., 1907; asst. pol. agent, N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1907; asst. commr., Oct., 1908; ditto, and commdt., B.M. Police, May, 1909; city mag., Peshawar, Oct., 1912; asst. pol. agent, Dir and Swat, July, 1913; ditto Chitral, Aug., 1913; temply. to mil. duty, Oct., 1914; asst. commr., Peshawar, Oct., 1915; ditto, Thal sub-divn., Nov., 1915; dep. commr., Hazara, March, 1919; asst. commr., Thal sub-divn., May, 1916; dist. judge, Peshawar, Nov., 1917; first asst. resdt. at Hyderabad, Dec., 1920; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, June, 1923; pol. agent in Bhopal, May, 1928; resdt. Baroda, Nov., 1929; offg. resdt., Gwalior, Apl., 1931; offg., resdt., and A.G.G. in C.I., Apl., 1932; retd., Jan., 1935.

WILSON, FRANCIS ARCHIBALD, late Excise and Salt Dept., Bengal (b. 17th May, 1875).—Held non-gazetted appt. prior to 1st April, 1915; supt., April, 1915; offg. dep. commr., April, 1924, July, 1925, and again, April, 1926; retd., May, 1930.

WILSON, FREDERICK ERNEST, M.B., Col., I.M.S., retd., late Medl. Dept., Cent. Provs. (b. 21st Apl., 1879).—First commn. dated 26th Jan., 1902; in milty. employ to 5th Dec., 1907; offg. civil surg., Miranshah, Dec., 1907; ditto, Peshawar, Oct., 1909; offg. agency surg., Meshed, Dec., 1910; ditto, Sibi, April, 1914; offg. medl. offr., Mewar Bhil Corps, Sept., 1914; dir., medl. dept., Bikaner, March, 1915; in milty. employ from Aug. 1917, to Aug., 1921; resdy. surg. and C.M.O., in Baluchistan, Aug., 1921; civil surg., Quetta, Oct., 1923; resdy. surg. and C.M.O., Baluchistan, Jan., 1928; ditto, W.I. States, Mar., 1930; inspr.-genl., civil hospitals, Cent. Provs., Feb., 1931; retd., Nov., 1933.

WILSON, REV. GEORGE ALBERT, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 24th Apl., 1892).—Appointed chaplain on probation, Madras, 29th March, 1926; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1928.

WILSON, GEORGE, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th Sept., 1877).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 6th Jan., 1896, to 16th July, 1913, and again from 14th Nov., 1913, to 30th June, 1914; offg. dist. loco. supt., July, 1913; confd., July, 1914; dist. loco. supt., June, 1923; supt., Feb., 1925; retd., Mar., 1934.

WILSON, GERALD SIDNEY, C.S.I., late Indian Police (b. 29th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1901; supt. of police, Sept., 1911; ditto, and dep. commr. of police for Port of Bombay, June, 1915; awarded King's Police Medal, Jan., 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Oct., 1921; confd., April, 1929; offg. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1928, and again, May, 1932; offg. commr. of police, Oct., 1930; C.S.I., June, 1931; inspr.-genl. of police, July, 1933; retd., Jan., 1934.

WILSON, Rt. Hon. Sir GUY DOUGLAS ARTHUR FLEETWOOD, P.C., G.C.I.E., K.C.B., K.C.M.G.—Member of govr.-genl.'s council, fin. dept., 9th Nov., 1908, to June, 1913; discharged functions on behalf of the govr.-genl. after the attempt on the latter's life in Dec., 1912; K.C.M.G., 1909; G.C.I.E., Dec., 1911; P.C., 1914.

WILSON, JAMES LEITCH, M.A., Edin., Punjab Educl. Service (supt. of educ., Baluchistan) (b. 31st Mar., 1892).—Joined the service as offg. inspr. of schools, 27th Mar., 1925; confd., Mar., 1930; vice-princl., Central Training Coll., Lahore, Nov., 1935; supt. of educ., Baluchistan, May, 1937.

WILSON, JOHN ANDREW MUIR, B.Sc., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 15th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1915; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1917; examr., local fund accts., ditto, Nov., 1923; divl. audit officer, E.I. Ely., Aug., 1927; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Apl., 1929; ditto, Madras, Jan., 1931; retd., Apl., 1933.

WILSON, JOHN CYRIL, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 24th May, 1902).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1924; confd., Nov., 1930; asst. to inspr.-genl. dep. commr., police, Bombay, Apl., 1938.

WILSON, JOSEPH ARNOLD, B.A., Indian Forest Services (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 12th Dec., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926.

WILSON, LEONARD, B.E., Indian State Ry.s. (agent, G.I.P. Ry.) (b. 12th Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (tempy.), 24th April, 1911; confd., June, 1915; resdt. engr., Sept., 1919; offg. divl. trans. supt., Sept., 1925; confd., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. trans. supt., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. agent, Dec., 1929; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1930; confd., Mar., 1933; offg. agent, G.I.P. rly., May, 1934; confd., Feb., 1935.

WILSON, Rt. Hon. Sir LESLIE ORME, P.C., G.C.S.I. G.C.I.E., C.M.G., D.S.O., Lt.-Col., R.M.L.I. (ret'd.).—Educ. at St. Michaels, Westgate, and St. Paul's Sch.; 2nd lieut., R.M.L.I., 1895; capt., 1901; A.D.C. to Govt. of New South Wales, 1903 to 1909; C.M.G., 1916; parly. asst. sec. to the War Cabinet, 1918; chairman National Maritime Board, 1919; parly. sec. to Ministry of Shipping, 1919; jt. parly. sec. to Treasury and Chief Unionist Whip, 1921; P.C., 1922; govr. of Bombay from 10th Dec., 1923, to 8th Dec., 1928; G.C.I.E., Oct., 1923; G.C.S.I., March, 1929; served in S. Africa, 1899-1901 (mentd. in despatches, D.S.O.); served through operations in Gallipoli, 1914-15 (mentd. in despatches, C.M.G.), and in France, 1915-16; govr. of Queensland, Mar., 1932.

WILSON, LESLIE TALBOT, Indian Police (asst. inspr.-genl., Burma) (b. 11th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 5th Apl., 1921; offg. dist. supt. and asst. commr. of police, Rangoon, Mar., 1926; confd., Mar., 1932; asst. inspr.-genl., Jan., 1936.

WILSON, NORMAN METHUEN, O.B.E., Col., I.M.S., (surg.-genl. with govt. of Madras) (b. 23rd Feb., 1881).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1905; in mil. employ till June, 1912; plague medl. offr., Punjab, Nov., 1912; on mil. service, Aug., 1914, to Sept., 1921; O.B.E., Aug., 1920; civil surgn., Punjab, Sept., 1921; war services, Waziristan operations, 1919-21 (mentioned in despatches); civil surg. and C.M.O., Delhi, Apl., 1932; inspr.-genl. of civil hospes.; Central Provs., Jan., 1934; offg. dir. of public health, Central Provs., Mar., 1936; surg.-genl. with govt. of Madras, Sept., 1937.

WILSON, PERCY, A.R.I.B.A., late Indian State Ry.s. (b. 4th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. architect, 22nd July, 1921; architect, G.I.P. Ry., Sept., 1930; ret'd., Sept., 1938.

WILSON, RALPH ALEXANDER, M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at High Sch., Dundee, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 17th Nov., 1902, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr. and regr., judl. commr.'s court; dep. commr., Dec., 1915; offg. chief sec. to govt., Nov., 1925; commr., Dec., 1927; ret'd., July, 1929.

WILSON, Sir ROGER COCHRANE, K.C.B., D.S.O., M.C., Lieut.-General., I.A. (b. 26th Dec., 1882).—First commn., 8th May, 1901; Indian Army, 1904 (114th Mahrattas); Brig. Major, India, Dec., 1914-April, 1915; D.A.G., Feb., 1916-May, 1916; A.A.G., Mesopotamian E.F. and Iraq E.F., Sept., 1917-Nov., 1921; Asst.

Director, Auxiliary and Territorial Forces, India, Dec., 1921-Feb., 1922; G.S.O. I, India, Feb., 1922-Feb., 1926; Brig. Commander, India, Feb., 1926-Feb., 1930; C.B., June, 1930; Comdt., Staff College, Quetta, Sept., 1931-Oct., 1934; Commander, Rawalpindi District, Oct., 1934-May, 1936; Milty. Secty., India Office, May, 1936; K.C.B., May, 1937; Adj.-Genl., India, Oct., 1937; Colonel, 5th Royal Battalion, 5th Mahratta Light Infantry; War Services, European War, 1914-18, brevet of Lieut.-Col.; D.S.O., M.C.; six times mentioned in despatches.

WILSON, RONALD PATERSON, late Indian Police (b. 31st Oct., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Feb., 1920; offg. supt., Nov., 1923; confd., Apl., 1931; ret'd., Apl., 1935.

WILTON DAVIES, PERCY WILLIAM, V.D., Indian State Railways (exec. engr., N.W. rly.) (b. 14th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1912; asst. engr., Dec., 1913; exec. engr., Mar., 1931.

WIMBERLEY, Capt. EDMUND ARABIN, R.E. (dep. mint master) (b. 16th Sept., 1906).—Under mil. dept. up to 14th Apl., 1937; deputy mint master (on probn.), Apl., 1937.

WIMBUSH, ANTHONY, late Indian Forest Service (b. 19th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 30th Oct., 1907, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1912; acting consvr., Aug., 1922; confd., July, 1925; offg. ch. consvr., Aug., 1928; confd., Nov., 1932; ret'd., June, 1937.

WIN, U. ZAW, Burma Police (class I) (b. ).—Appdt. asst. supt. of police, on probn., Dec., 1937.

WINDLE, OLIVER ETHELBERT, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., Madras) (b. 20th July, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 17th Dec., 1905; supt., April, 1915; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1920; O.B.E., Jan., 1931; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Aug., 1930; confd., Oct., 1930; commr. of police Madras, Apl., 1933; dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1937.

WINGATE, RONALD EVELYN LESLIE, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 30th Sept., 1889).—Educ. at Bradfield, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 8th Dec., 1913, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., transf. to Delhi as city mag., April to Sept., 1916; spl. duty on staff of lieut.-govr., Punjab, from April, 1917; spl. duty under civil commr. of occupied territories, Mesopotamia, from Oct., 1917; pol. agent and H.M.'s consul at Maskat, Oct., 1919; special asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Nov., 1921; pol. agent and H.M.'s Consul, Maskat, March, 1923; sec. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Sept., 1924; ditto, Baluchistan, March, 1927; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta-Peshin, March, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; pol. agent. Sibi, Oct., 1931; dep. secty. to the govt. of India, foreign and pol. dept., Mar., 1932; offg. secty., ditto, Oct., 1932, and May, 1934; jt. secty., ditto, June, 1933; temp. addl. ditto, Mar., 1935; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Dec., 1935; offg. A.G.G., and ch. commr., Baluchistan, Nov., 1936; rev. and judl. commr., ditto, Dec., 1936; offg. A.G.G., Resdt. C.C., ditto, June, 1937.

WINGFIELD, DESMOND ERNEST, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 17th Nov., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 26th Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932; offg. supt., Nov., 1935.

WINTERTON, EARL, RIGHT HON. EDWARD TURNOUR, P.C., M.P., J.P., D.L.—Parliamentary under-sec. of state for India, Mar., 1922, to Jan., 1924, and Nov., 1924, to June, 1929; delegate to Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931; delegate to Indian round table conf., Ldn., Nov.-Dec., 1932.

WISE, JOHN HUMPHREY, Indian C.S. (b. 11th Mar., 1890) (dep. commr. Burma).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Oxford (Univ.); apptd. after exam. of 1913; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1914, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from April, 1915, to July, 1919; under-sec. to govt., Feb., 1921, to Aug., 1923; offg. sec. to finl. commr., Aug., 1923; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, health dept., from Nov., 1923, to Feb., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1924; secy., public service commn., Oct., 1926; dep. commr., Feb., 1930; secty. rev. dept., Burma, Nov., 1932, and again, Apr., 1934; secty., commerce and indus. dep., Mar., 1937.

WITHERS, FREDERICK WALTER, late Indian Forest Service (b. 16th June, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 12th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Aug., 1923; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

WITHINSHAW, LESLIE, late Indian Police (b. 27th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1906; asst. supt., July, 1907; supt., Oct., 1915; awarded King's police medal, 1916; on milty. duty from 12th Aug., 1918, to 28th Feb., 1919; dep. commr., March, 1919; on duty in central intell. office, Simla, Jan.-July, 1920; offg. dep. dir. central intelligence, Madras, Nov., 1921; asst. inspr.-gen., Jan., 1923; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, rly. bd., for appt. as supt., watch and ward dept., E.I. Rly., from 4th April, 1924; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

WITT, DAVID OTTO, late Indian Forest Service (Central Provs.) (b. 29th Sept., 1874).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1896, as asst. consr. of forests; dep. consr., Dec., 1903; consr. (provl.), Sept., 1920; confd., Dec., 1920; chief consvr., Oct., 1926; ret'd., Apr., 1929.

WOLFENDEN, RALPH, M.B.E., M.Sc., M.I.M.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), Barr., Indian Educl. Service (princl., Bengal Engrg. Coll.) (b. 18th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service as prof., civil engrg. coll., 16th Feb., 1921; M.B.E., Jan., 1918; asst. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, Nov., 1928; inspr. of European sch., Dec. 1934; princpl., Bengal Engrg. Coll., Nov., 1936.

WOOD, ANTHONY ST. JOHN, Indian Police, (asst. supt.) (b. 19th Jan., 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, 24th Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

WOOD, ARTHUR EDWARD FRANCIS, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 18th June, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Dec., 1910; supt., Nov., 1925.

WOOD, ARTHUR WESTMORELAND, Barr., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 1st Aug., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. examr., Oct., 1908; in naval service from 11th Aug., 1914, to 22nd

April, 1919; field examr. of mil. works accts. Aug., 1919; dep. finl. adviser, May, 1920; dir. of army audit., Western Command, May, 1923; offg. ditto, N.C., March, 1926; ditto, E.C., May, 1927; with finl. commr., rlys., Apr., 1929; on foreign service under Jodhpur State from Feb., 1930; ch. auditor, E.B. rly. and Govt. exr. of accts., Assam Bengal rly., Oct., 1933; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

WOOD, BERNARD READER, Indian Forest Service (b. 20th Feb., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 30th Nov., 1914; dep. consvr., Oct., 1924; transferred to for. service as consvr. of forests in British Guiana, from 10th Oct., 1925.

WOOD, CHRISTOPHER FRANCIS, India C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 26th Aug., 1884).—Educ. at Grocers' Company's Sch., London, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1908; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and asst. commr.; on mil. duty, I.A.R.O., Sept., 1916, to Feb., 1919; jt. mag., March, 1918; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1920; jt. sec. to board of rev., Feb., 1921; sec., ditto, April, 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1924; confd., July, 1925; offg. commr., Dec., 1925.

WOOD, ERIC RAWLINSON, M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., govt. of Orissa educ. dept.) (b. 24th Mar., 1893).—Educ. at Denstone Coll., and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1919; arrived, 1st Dec., 1919, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1931, and again, Jan., 1933; ditto (provl. subs.), Mar., 1935; collr., Mar., 1936; served temp. with govt. of Orissa from Apr., 1936; secty. to govt. of Orissa, Educ. Dept., Mar., 1937.

WOOD, ERNEST CLEMENT, Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 12th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Cheltenham and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 10th Dec., 1915, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on milty. duty from Oct., 1916, to May, 1919; asst. to resdt., Travancore and Cochin, June, 1919; sub-collr., March, 1922; offg. commr. of Coorg, May, 1926; offg. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Sept., 1926; confd., March, 1927; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Oct., 1930; collr. (provl. subs.), May, 1934; confd., Jan., 1935.

WOOD, GEORGE HERBERT ANDERSON, Indian Finance Dept. (b. 31st Aug., 1894).—Joined the service, 14th June, 1919; asst. acctnt.-gen., Nov., 1920; currency officer, Madras, Oct., 1922; ditto, Lahore, May, 1925; ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1926; dep. ch. auditor, E.B. Rly., June, 1932; services placed at disposal of finl. commr. of rlys., Feb., 1933; offg. dep. dir., rly. clearing accts., June, 1935; dep. ch. accts. officer, E.B. rly., May, 1936; on for. service under Jodhpur State, Jan., 1937.

WOOD, JOHN LUTHER, Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 25th Sept., 1908).—Educ. at Burnley Grammar School and Exeter College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931 and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Mar., 1934.

WOOD, LESLIE DERING, Indian Police (b. 5th Jan., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 24th Nov., 1928; offg. supt., April, 1935.

WOOD, PERCY, Indian Railway Accts. Service (chief accts. offcr.) (b. 17th April, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. auditor and acctnt., G.I.P. rly., 7th Nov., 1913; confd. as asst. auditor, G.I.P. rly., Dec., 1920; on depts. to Agency Dept. as supt. of statistics, G.I.P. rly., Feb., 1924, to Mar., 1929; served under finl. commr., rlys., Oct., 1929, and again Dec., 1935; dep. chief accts. offcr., G.I.P. rly., Oct., 1929; served under agent, G.I.P. rly., July, 1932; confd. as deputy agent, Jan., 1933; dep. chief accts. offcr., Nov., 1936; chief accts. offcr., G.I.P. rly., Feb., 1938.

WOOD, WILLIAM GEORGE, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 27th July, 1897).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st Apl., 1927, after previous service from Apl., 1921; exec. officer, 1st Nov., 1932.

WOOD, WILLIAM OUTERSON, O.B.E., Indian Police (b. 11th Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., 20th Nov., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Mar., 1929; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; O.B.E., May, 1936.

WOODHEAD, SIR JOHN ACKROYD, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (member, exec. council govr. of Bengal) (b. 19th June, 1881).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfd. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1911; re-transfd. to Bengal, April, 1912; jt. mag. and collr., Jan., 1913; dist. and sess. judge, s.p.f., Jan., 1917; confd., March, 1917; 1st land acquisition offr., June, 1918; sec. to govt. of Bengal, fin., commerce and marine depts., Nov., 1924; jt. sec., dept. of commerce, govt. of India, Nov., 1927; sec., ditto, Mar., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s council, May, 1931; delegate to Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931; member, exec. council govr. of Bengal, Apl., 1932; actg. govr. of Bengal, Aug., 1934 to Dec., 1934; K.C.S.I., Aug., 1934.

WOODHOUSE, ALBERT CYRIL, C.I.E., B.A., Madras Civil Service (sub. collr.) (b. 5th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as dep. collr., 30th Nov., 1912; offg. collr. and magte., July, 1926; sub. collr. (provl. perm.), Feb., 1927; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

WOODHOUSE, ARTHUR ALDERSON, late Indian Police Service (United Provs.) (b. 23rd Oct., 1874).—Joined the service, 16th April, 1898, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt. of police, April, 1906; supt., June, 1912; retd., Jan., 1930; author of 'New Revolver Manual of Police and Infantry Forces.'

WOODHOUSE, HAROLD LISTER, M.C., Col., R.E., Indian State Rlys. (chief engr.) (b. 30th Jan., 1887).—First commn. dated 25th July, 1906; asst. engr., 23rd March, 1911; on mil. service from 20th Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Jan., 1917; divl. supt., Sept., 1928; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1933; confd., May, 1935; offg. dir., rly. bd., July, 1934; confd., June, 1935; chief engr., Nov., 1937.

WOODHOUSE, SIDNEY, late Survey of India (b. 9th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 9th June, 1905 and held non-gaz. appts. to 25th Oct., 1919; officer in charge mathematical instrument office, Feb., 1923; supt., ditto, Mar., 1926; retd., Aug., 1937.

WOODS, DOUGLAS LESLIE, Indian Police (b. 12th Oct., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, 22nd Nov., 1929; confd., Feb., 1932; transfd. to N.W.F.P., Jan., 1933; asst. dist. offcr., frontier constab., Mar., 1937.

WOODS, GEORGE WILLIAM MURRAY, O.B.E., B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 3rd June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Burma, Apl., 1928; O.B.E., June, 1932; retd., Sept., 1935.

WOODS, PHILLIP DION, late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 19th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. storekeeper, 21st Oct., 1908; services placed at disposal of Indian Munitions Board, 6th Oct., 1917; dist. contr. of stores, Aug., 1919; retd., Nov., 1931.

WOODS-BALLARD, BASIL, M.B.E., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Sept., 1900).—First commn. dated 29th Jan., 1920; in milty. employ prior to 1st Jan., 1925; probnr. in pol. dept., May, 1925; asst. commr., D.I. Khan, Dec., 1926; city magte., Peshawar, Jan., 1928; supy. asst. commr., Hazara, June, 1929; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, July, 1919; undersecty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, Apl., 1931; asst. pol. agent, Quetta, Pishin, Oct., 1934; M.B.E., Nov., 1935; pol. agent, Loralai, Feb., 1936.

WOODTHORPE, MRS. MARIA VAUGHAN, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 19th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service as inspectress of girls' schools, 20th Nov., 1915; retd., Apl., 1934.

WOODWARD, FRANK JAMES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Sept., 1884).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 27th Nov., 1909, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1914; sub-judge (provl.), June, 1919; confd., Dec., 1919; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1923; retd., June, 1934.

WOODYARD, CHRISTOPHER RICHARD, India Office (exec. officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 10th Apl., 1901).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st Jan., 1920, after previous service from Oct., 1918; actg. exec. officer, Sept., 1935; confd., Feb., 1936.

WOOLDRIDGE, LOVELL WILLIAM, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 9th Dec., 1901).—First commn., 23rd Dec., 1921; in milty. employ to May, 1927; asst. pol. agent at Chilas, Nov., 1930; under secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Dec., 1934; asst. commr., Tank, Feb., 1937; dep. commr., Dera Ismail, Khan, Nov., 1937; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, Oct., 1938.

WOOLLER, ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and sub div. offcr., Bengal) (b. 23rd May, 1912).—Educ. at Bradford Grammar Sch., and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and sub divl. offcr., Apl., 1937.

WORGAN, TREVOR HARTLAND, late Indian Finance Dept.—Joined the fin. dept., 19th Oct., 1901; asst. acct.-gen., United Provs., Nov., 1903; asst. compt.-gen., forests, Jan., 1904; asst. acct.-gen., Bengal, April, 1905; asst. compt.-gen. in charge paper currency, Calcutta, Nov., 1905; asst. acct.-gen., United Provs., Nov., 1907; ditto, Bombay, Dec., 1908; examr., local fund accs., Bombay, May, 1912; examr., outside audits, Calcutta, Sept., 1916; offg. compt., Assam, May, 1920; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1920; ditto, Madras, Oct., 1922; dep. auditor-gen., India inspection (civil), Apl., 1924; on spec. duty, Feb., 1926; retd., Aug., 1928.

WORMALD, Rev. ROBERT LEONARD, M.B.E., M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estab. (b. 17th April, 1882).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 30th Oct., 1914; junr. chaplain Oct. 1917; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; retd., Sept., 1935.

WORT, ALFRED WILLIAM EWART, Barr., Judl. dept., Bihar (b. 17th Mar., 1883).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, Feb., 1927; offg. ch. justice, July, 1933; Apl., 1936; and July, 1938.

WORTH, MEREDITH, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Repton, Grenoble, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; Brit. trade agent, Gyantse, and asst. to pol. officer in Sikkim, Apl., 1933; offg. Brit. trade agent, Yatung, Apl., 1933; asst. pol. agent, Bahrain, (temp.), May, 1934; asst. pol. agent, Sibi, Oct., 1935; offg. pol. agent in addn., Mar., 1936; senior sub judge, Hazara, Dec., 1936; asst. commr., Hazara, Jan., 1937; ditto, in addn., Manshera, Apl., 1937; confd., Apl., 1937; Counsellor, Brit. Legation, Kabul, July, 1937; asst. commr., Hazara, Dec., 1937; secty. to resdt. for Baroda and Gujarat States, Mar., 1938.

WORTHY, GEORGE TOYNBEE, India Office (exec. officer, military dept.) (b. 9th Apl., 1882).—Educ. at Royal Gr. Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne; apptd. 2nd div. clerk, War Office, 14th March, 1901; apptd. 2nd class clerk, acctnt.-genl.'s dept., 13th Nov., 1905; transfed. to registry and record dept., April, 1906, and to military dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service, July, 1918, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922; actg. higher clerical officer, April, 1922, to Sept., 1923.

WRENCH, JOHN CECIL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 13th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Madras, 31st Jan., 1922; dep. consvr., Jan., 1925; offg. princpl., Madras forest coll., Jan., 1930, to Apl., 1934.

WRENCH, JOHN MERVYN DALLAS, C.I.E., Indian State Railways (ch. contr. of standardisation, Rly. Bd.) (b. 15th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 13th Aug., 1906; dist. loco. supt., April, 1913; supt. of state workshops, Gwalior, Dec., 1914, to April, 1915; spl. duty with controller of stores, mil. rys., from Nov., 1916 (metd. in despatch); chief mechanical engrg., G.I.P. rly.; offg. dir. mechl. engrg., rly. dept., March, 1925; on spl. duty, Rly.

Board, Oct., 1926; ch. mech. engr., G.I.P. Rly., June, 1928; C.I.E., March, 1929; on spl. duty with Rly. Bd., Feb., 1929; offg. ch. contr. of standards, Rly. Bd., March, 1930; dir. mechl. engrg. in addn., Nov. 1932; ch. contr. of standardisation, Apl., 1935.

WRENICK, BERNARD CHARLES, late India Posts and Tels. (Madras) (b. 24th Nov., 1875).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1897; supt., April, 1910; div. engr., tels., Oct., 1920; dir., S.C., Nov., 1920; dep. dir.-genl., Nov., 1926; dir., telegraph engrg., Eastern circle, Nov., 1927; ditto, Northern circle, Dec., 1928; P.M.G., Madras, Jan., 1930; retd., Aug., 1930.

WRIGHT, ALBERT ERNEST, B.A. (Oxon.), Imperial Customs Service (b. 30th June, 1902).—Joined the service, Nov., 1926; asst. collr. of customs, Nov., 1926; dep. commr., cent. excises and salt dept., N. India, Feb., 1938.

WRIGHT, FRANCIS GERALD GRIEBLE, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 2nd June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 4th Oct., 1921; offg. dist. supt., March, 1925; confd., Jan., 1929.

WRIGHT, Miss GERTRUDE MARIAN, late Indian Educl. Service.—Apptd. princpl., Bethune coll., Calcutta, 5th Feb., 1919; retd., Feb., 1929.

WRIGHT, HERBERT LAWRENCE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Punjab) (b. 24th Jan., 1887) joined the service, 24th Dec., 1909; served in the Punjab as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1914; on deputation to Kashmir as consvr. of forests from Jan., 1923; chief consvr., Kashmir, Aug., 1924; consvr., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1932; apptd. offcr. on special duty in Gilgit Agency, Aug. to Oct., 1937.

WRIGHT, JOHN MONCRIEFF, C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 31st July, 1884).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Jesus Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; supt., Chin Hills, Aug., 1913; C.B.E., July, 1920; dep. commr., June, 1922; offg. chief commr., Andaman and Nicobar Islands, June, 1924, and again, Nov., 1927; spl. duty, Rangoon, as dep. commr., Oct., 1924; ch. commr., Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Nov., 1927; retd., Mar., 1931.

WRIGHT, PHILIP VICTOR, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bengal) (b. 23rd Sept., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 16th Dec., 1934; confd., Jan., 1937.

WRIGHT, RICHARD GANE, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Punjab) (b. 1st Jan., 1877).—Apptd. asst. princpl., Aitchison Coll., Lahore, 15th Sept., 1915; offg. princpl., ditto, April, 1926; retd., Jan., 1932.

WRIGHT, ROBERT ERNEST, C.I.E., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 2nd Oct., 1884).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1907; in mil. employ to Nov., 1911; offg. asst. dir., Pasteur institute, Nov., 1911; offg. as prof., medl. coll., Madras, Apl., 1914; reverted to mil. duty from 20th Aug., 1914, to 9th Feb., 1920; offg. prof., medl. coll., Madras, May, 1920; confd., Jan., 1922; C.I.E., Mar., 1929; offg. surg.-genl. with Govt. of Madras, Oct., 1933; retd.

WRIGHT, ROBERT MARTIN, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 8th May, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Nov., 1908; supt., March, 1917; awarded King's police medal, 1918; prinpl. police training coll., Bengal, Feb., 1928; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1929; confd., Feb., 1932.

WRIGHT, ROBERT WALLACE, M.B.E., India Office (asst. supt. of records) (b. 20th Sept., 1892).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 2nd July, 1912, and attd. to acctnt.-genl.'s dept.; transf. to registry and record dept., July, 1912, and to store dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service from Nov., 1915; retransferred to registry and record dept., June, 1919; transf. to comm. and rev. dept., Feb., 1922; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; resident clerk, March, 1923; parly. clerk, April, 1926; asst. sec. Indian delegation to 7th and 8th assemblies of League of Nations, 1926 and 1927; M.B.E., June, 1932; asst. supt. of records, Nov., 1937.

WRIGHT, SYDNEY WILLIAM WALTER, Indian Police (b. 29th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 20th Nov., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Sept., 1931; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1933.

WRIGHT, WALTER HENRY, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 13th Sept., 1891).—Joined 22nd Nov., 1911; asst. supt., Nov., 1914; offg. supt., July, 1919, May, 1921, and Oct., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1918; supt. (provl. perm.), Jan., 1927; confd., Dec., 1928.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM ALAN, A.F.C., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 27th Nov., 1895).—Educ. at Oundle, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1921; arrived, 30th Nov., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1924; confd., Mar., 1929.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM DUNDAS, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 9th Nov., 1880).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1905; in mil. employ to 23rd Dec., 1907, and again from 8th May to 14th Sept., 1908; on plague duty, Sept., 1908; civil surg., United Provs., March, 1921; ret'd., Dec., 1931.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM MCKENDREE, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 17th June, 1912).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., United Provinces, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934.

WRIGHT, WILFRID THOMAS MERMOND, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Dec., 1882).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; appd. after exam. of 1905, arrived, 28th Nov., 1906, and served in United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. A.G., United Provs., July, 1911; D.A.G. ditto Mar., 1912; jt. mag., May, 1915; dep. sec. govt. of I., legisl. dept., Nov., 1918; jt. sec., ditto, May, 1920; dep. sec., ditto, July, 1920; temp. jt. sec., ditto, Jan., 1921; confd., Nov. 1921; C.I.E., June, 1924; ret'd., Jan., 1932.

WRINCH, ROBERT CORNICK, India Posts and Tels. (dir. tels.) (b. 26th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. divl. engr., Mar., 1921; divl. engr., tels., Aug., 1928; dir., posts and tels., Sind and Baluchistan, Mar., 1934; offg. dir., tels., Apl., 1936; confd., Dec., 1936.

WUNNAM, ESWARA VENKATA SITAPATI RAO, LL.B. (London Univ.), Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, accts. dept.) (b. 20th June, 1893).—Apptd. to India Office, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 19th April, 1920; transferred to office of High Commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; clerical officer, Oct., 1921; exec. officer, 1st April, 1923; higher clerical officer (actg.), April, 1927; confd., June, 1927.

WYLIE, SIR FRANCIS VERNER, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Pol. Service (govr., Central Provs. and Berar) (b. 9th Aug., 1891).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Dungannon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 4th Dec., 1915, and served in the Pun ab as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from July, 1916, to March, 1919; temp. offg. as asst. commr., Charsadda, from May, 1919; pol. agent, Tochi, May, 1921; settlt. officer, Peshawar, Sept., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1929; offg. dep. sec. for and pol. dept., Dec., 1929 to June, 1930; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1930; dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1930; prime minister, Alwar, Mar., 1933; dep. agent to Govr.-Genl. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1935; jt. secty. to the govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Apl., 1936; resdt., Jaipur, Aug., 1936; on spl. duty in for. and pol. dept., Sept., 1936; resdt., Jaipur, Feb. to Dec., 1937; govr., Central Provs., and Berar, May, 1938; K.C.S.I., May, 1938.

WYNNE, MARTIN GOYMOUR, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 26th May, 1910).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Central Provs., 29th Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; asst. commr., Port Blair, Nov., 1934, to Feb., 1938.

WYNNE, SIR TREVREDYN RASHLEIGH, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., V.D., M.I.C.E., Col., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 18th Nov., 1874; transf. to Bombay, 1877 to 1882; exec. engr., May, 1883; resigned, April, 1887, and became agent and chief engr., Bengal Nagpur rly. co.; C.I.E., Jan., 1903; apptd. member of rly. board, Calcutta, Feb., 1905; presdt., May, 1908; K.C.I.E., 1st Jan., 1909; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1911; ret'd., May, 1914; apptd. govt. dir., Indian rly. cos., India Office, May, 1914; ret'd. from India Office, May, 1915.

YA (A), U TUN, late Burma Commission (b. 27th Oct., 1877).—Previous service from April, 1897; dep. commr., May, 1924; secty. to govt., judl. dept., Mar., 1930; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

YADAVA MOHAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr. United Provs.) (b. 10th Jan., 1900).—Joined the service as engr. student, 10th Oct., 1928; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1929.

YAHYA ALI SAHIB, KHAN BAHADUR, M.A., B.L., Judl. Dept., Madras (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 23rd Aug., 1893).—Joined the service June, 1936; apptd. dist. and sess. judge, on probn., June, 1936; confd., June, 1937.

YAKUB SHAH, Indian Audit Dept. (b. 2nd Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Jan., 1929; asst. contr., rly. accts., Mar., 1930; offg. dep. ch. accts. officer, N.W. rly., Sept., 1932; dep. acct.-genl., Nov., 1933; exr., local fund accts.,

Punjab, May, 1935; offg. asst. secty., finance dept., Govt. of India, Nov., 1935; dep. dir., rly. bd. (temp.), May, 1936; offg. dep. dir., finance, rly. bd., Oct., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937.

YARNOLD, FREDERICK HERBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (under-secty., home dept., Burma) (b. 18th Nov., 1911).—Educ. at the King's Sch., Worcester, and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty., home dept., Nov., 1937.
