YATE, Sir CHARLES EDWARD, Bart., C.S.I., C.M.G., D.L., Col., Indian Army (ret'd.), late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 9th Nov., 1867; arrived, 27th Feb., 1868; from Aug., 1871, served under the foreign dept.; on special duty, Sind border, 1872 and 1873; boundary settlt. officer, Mewar-Marwar border, Sept., 1874; and Mewar-Tonk border, Dec., 1875; offd. for various periods as pol. asst., asst. to supt. for suppression of thagi and dakaity, &c.; supt. of operations for control of Moghias, Sept., 1879; served in Afghanistan, 1880-81 (medal and clasp and bronze star); subsequently served in the pol. dept. in Bhopal, Central India, and Bhopawar; attaché with Afghan boundary commn., July, 1884; on duty at Herat in connection with the fortifications, 1885; employed on various special services in connection with the Afghan boundary commn.; pol. agent, Thal Chotiali, March, 1890; offg. rev. commr., Baluchistan, Oct., 1892; pol. agent, Zhor, March, 1893; on special duty on Russo-Afghan frontier, April, 1893; acting govr.-gen.'s agent and consul-gen., Khorassan and Seistan, Sept., 1893; confd. 1897; rest., Mewar, June, 1898; offg. rest., and agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, Aug. to Oct., 1898; offg. resdt., Western States, Rajputana, April, 1899; agent to govr.-gen. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1900; ret'd., Oct., 1904; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; C.M.G., June, 1888; M.P. for Melton Dn., Leicestershire, from Dec., 1910, to Oct., 1924; Baronet, Jan., 1921; knight of justice of St. John of Jerusalem; member of Afghan Order of Hurmat; author of gazetteers of Mewar, Banswara and Partabgarh, 'Northern Afghanistan,' 'Khurasan and Seistan,' &c.

YATES, CLEMENT MERVYN SCOTT, Indian Police (b. 7th June, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 7th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., June, 1932, and again Feb., 1933; A.D.C. to govt., Bombay, Feb., 1936, to Sept., 1937.

YATES, JAMES ALFRED, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 17th Apl., 1874).—Joined the service, 26th March, 1906, and apptd. inspr. of schools, Madras; principal, govt. coll., Kumbakonam, Aug., 1912, to Oct., 1916; ret'd., Apl., 1929.

YEAMAN, WILLIAM GEORGE, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 9th Mar., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1925, June, 1926, and again, Feb., 1932; ret'd., May, 1934.

YEATTS, WILLIAM WALTER MURRAY, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty. to the Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands) (b. 30th Sept., 1894).

—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 28th Dec., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1922; under-sec. to govt., chief sectt., April, 1926; census supt., Madras, Apl., 1930; dep. secty., reforms office, June, 1932; addl. dep. secty., to Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Apl., 1935; offg. dep. secty. ditto, June, 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; offg. jt. secty., ditto, variously from Sept., 1935; and again, Apl., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

YELD, PERCIVAL HARLEY, Indian State Ryis. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Ry.) (b. 14th Sept., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 29th Oct., 1909; dist. ditto, April, 1918; on milty. duty from Sept., 1915, to Feb., 1919; offg. dep. traffic manager, May, 1928; ditto (comm'l.), E.B. rly., Oct., 1932; offg. traffic manager, Mar., 1933; offg. dep. ch. manager, transpn., E.B. rly., Sept., 1935.

YENNEMADI, DATTATRAYA VENKATRAO, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Civil Service. Judicial Branch (b. 23rd Apl., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Apl., 1908, to 31st Mar., 1914; sub-judge, Apl., 1914; offg. asst. judge, May 1927; confd., March, 1930; offg. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1929; confd., Dec., 1934; ret'd., May, 1938.

YIN, KHIN MAUNG, B.Sc., Barr., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 19th Feb., 1897).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Dec., and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., educn. dept., May, 1927; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1931.

YO, U SEIN, late Burma Civil Service (b. 11th Feb., 1873).—Held various ministerial appts. from 1st Oct., 1893; extra asst. commr., May, 1910; dep. commr., Dec., 1926; ret'd., Apl., 1929.

YORKE, ROBERT LANGDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, Oudh Chief Court) (b. 2nd Feb., 1887).—Educ. at Haileybury, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1917; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., Oct., 1917; reverted to civil duty, Jan., 1919; actg. dist. and sess. judge from July, 1919; offg. dep. sec. to govt., judl. branch, March, 1926; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1926; judl. sec. to govt., May, 1927; apptd. judge, Oudh chief ct., Jan., 1938.

YOUNG, Rev. ERNEST JOSEPH, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (archdeacon of Calcutta) (b. 26th Oct., 1888).—Joined the service, 6th April, 1918; junr. chaplain, April, 1918; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1928; offg. archdeacon of Calcutta, Mar., 1935; confd., Sept., 1936.

YOUNG, FRANCIS WILLIAM WALLACE (asst. collr. of customs) (b. 26th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1906; held non-gaz. posts June, 1906, to Jan., 1926; pay and accts. officer, Jan., 1926, to July, 1931; ch. accts. officer, Apl., 1931; asst. collr. of customs, Mar., 1938.

YOUNG, FREDERICK, C.I.E., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 16th May, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 16th Dec., 1909; supt., June, 1923; C.I.E., June, 1924; inspr.-genl. of police, Jaipur State, May, 1931.

YOUNG, GEORGE EDGAR, late India Office (b. 26th Aug., 1868).—Educ. at Warminster Gr. Sch., and King's Coll. Sch., London; apptd. 2nd class clerk, registry and record dept., 22nd May, 1888; transf. to finl. dept., 14th May, 1901; resdt. clerk, Oct., 1907, to March, 1914; staff clerk, March, 1914; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; transferred to acct.-genl.'s dept., May, 1922; suptg. exec. officer, July, 1922; ret'd., Jan., 1932.

YOUNG, GERARD MACKWORTH, C.I.E., M.A., F.R.G.S., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th April, 1884).—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 4th Dec., 1908, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., April, 1913; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., July, 1916; to March, 1919; dep. sec., ditto, Feb., 1919; employed in mil. dept., India Office, from July, 1919, to Sept., 1920; dep. commr., Jan., 1921, to May, 1924; dep. sec. to govt. of India, army dept., Nov., 1924; offg. jt. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., April, 1926; jt. sec. army dept., Sept., 1926; sec. to govt. of India, army dept., Nov., 1926; C.I.E., Mar., 1929; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

YOUNG, HAROLD NORMAN, Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. offcr., N.W. rly.) (b. 1st Sept., 1890).—Asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1919; divl. transpn. offcr., May, 1935.

YOUNG, JAMES, Major, M.B.E., Indian Stores Dept. (dep. dir. of purchase) (b. 4th July, 1885).—In milty. employ from 20th May, 1915, to 15th September, 1924; M.B.E., Jan., 1920; asst. dir. of purchase, Sept., 1924; offg. dep. dir., ditto, May, 1925; confd., Feb., 1926.

YOUNG, JAMES WOLSTON, O.B.E., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 18th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1909; asst. acctnt.-gen., Sept., 1909; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; employed by Jodhpur Durbar from 10th March, 1923; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931.

YOUNG, SIR JOHN DOUGLAS, Kt., Barr., Judl. dept., United Provs. (ch. justice, high ct., Lahore) (b. 7th Apl., 1883).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Allahabad, 15th Jan., 1929; ch. justice, high ct., Lahore, May, 1934; knighted, Jan., 1935.

YOUNG, REV. PHILIP NORTON FRUSHARD, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain). Apptd. chaplain on probation, Punjab, 2nd Nov., 1930; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1932.

YOUNG, THOMAS CHARLES McCOMBIE, M.D., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.).—First commn. dated 1st September, 1902; in mil. employ to Oct., 1909; dep. sanitary commr., Eastern Bengal and Assam, Nov., 1909; ditto, Assam, April, 1912; sanitary commr., Assam, July, 1915; offg. health officer, Port of Bombay, April, 1926; on foreign service under Indian Research Fund Assocn., Nov., 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

YOUNG, WILLIAM HOARE HATCHELL, M.C., Indian State Rlys. (ch. operating supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 13th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service 28th April, 1904; asst. traffic supt., N.W. Rly., Jan., 1909; I.A.R.O., March, 1915; dist. traffic supt., Aug., 1917; offg. dep. chief operating supt., July, 1925; offg. divl. supt., Nov., 1926; confd., Jan., 1929; offg. ch. operating supt., E.I. rly., Aug., 1935; ch. comml. manager, E.I. rly., Nov., 1935; ch. operating supt., E.I. rly., Dec., 1937.

YOUNGHUSBAND, SIR FRANCIS EDWARD, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., LL.D. (Hon.), D.Sc. (Hon.) Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (ret'd.), late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 10th May, 1882; apptd. to the foreign dept., July, 1889; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, April, 1892; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Oct., 1892; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Rajputana, Nov., 1897; pol. agent in Haraot and Tonk. Sept., 1898; K.-i.-H. medal, Jan., 1901; offg. resdt., Indore, Feb., 1902; on depn. with Tibet Frontier commn. from June, 1903, to Nov., 1904; K.C.I.E., Dec., 1904; resdt. in Kashmir, June, 1906; ret'd., May, 1910; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1917; presdt., R. Geographical Society, 1919; author of 'The Relief of Chitral,' 'The Heart of a Continent,' and 'S. Africa of To-Day'; holds gold medal of R. Geographical Soc., and R. Scottish ditto, and silver medal of the R. Soc. of Arts.

YOUNGMAN, WILLIAM, B.Sc., Ph.D., late Indian Agric. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 6th Aug., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. economic botanist, 5th Aug., 1918; economic botanist, May, 1920; ret'd., Feb., 1930; dir. of agric., Ceylon, Mar., 1930; ret'd., Nov., 1935.

YOUNIE, JOHN, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 5th Apl., 1893).—Educ. at Elgin Acad. and Edinburgh University; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived in India, Dec., 1916, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., Nov., 1918, to Jan., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1923; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1925; offg. ditto, April, 1926; confd., Mar., 1927.

YOUSUF ALI SAYEED, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 20th May, 1911).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Madras, 23rd Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1935.

YULE, HARRY HOWARD, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 30th Nov., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 30th Jan., 1901; dist. supt., March, 1914; supt., Feb., 1915; divl. supt., Jan., 1927; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., Nov., 1927; confd., Mar., 1932; offg. divl. supt., Mar., 1932; confd., June, 1934; offg. ch. comml. manager, E.I. rly., Feb., 1935; ret'd., July, 1937.

YUSAF, SALAHUDDIN AHMED (see Salahuddin A. Y.).

YUSUF ALI, A. (see Abdullah Ibn Yusuf Ali).

YUSUF ALI KHAN, AGHA, Indian Agricl. Service (b. Jan., 1886).—Joined the service, 15th Apl., 1907, and held non-gazetted appts. to 31st Mar., 1919; extra asst. dir. of agric., Apl., 1919; offg. dep. dir. of agric., Mar., 1928.

YUSUF, SHEIKH MOHAMMAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 13th Nov., 1913).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 19th Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Oct., 1938, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

ZACHARIAH, KURUVILA, M.A. (Oxon), Indian Educl. Service (princl. Hooghly Coll., Calcutta) (b. 24th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as prof., presdy. coll., 10th Jan., 1916; princpl. Hooghly coll., July, 1930.

ZAFAR, MUHAMMAD ABU, Indian Police.—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., 11th Feb., 1935; confd., Feb., 1937.

ZAFAR ALI, SIR, KHAN BAHADUR, MIRZA, Kt., B.A., late Punjab Judl. Dept. (b. 20th Feb., 1870).—Joined the service, 4th Feb., 1893; extra asst. commr., Nov., 1899; extra judl. asst. commr., Sept., 1907; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1918; offg. addl. judge, Punjab high ct., Oct., 1922, to March, 1923, and from Oct., 1923; judge, ditto, Oct., 1926; ret'd., 1930; Kt., Jan., 1931.

ZAFAR HASAN, KHAN BAHADUR, MAULVI, B.A., Archaeological Survey Dept. (supt. northern circle) (b. 17th July, 1886).—Joined the service, 17th June, 1913; supt., Feb., 1925; offg. dep. dir.-genl., Feb., 1931; confd., Apl., 1932; supt., northern circle, Oct., 1935.

ZAFAR OMAR, B.A., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 27th Apl., 1883).—Joined the service as dep. supt., 7th Jan., 1908; supt., Sept., 1924; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1938.

ZAFRULLAH KHAN, CHAUDHRI, SIR MUHAMMAD, K.C.S.I. (member govr.-genl.'s exec. council).—Apptd. temp. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Apl., 1935; confd., May, 1935; Knighted, June, 1935; chairman, central advisory council of rlys., July, 1935, to Nov., 1937; K.C.S.I., May, 1937; on spec. duty, Apl., to Aug., 1938.

ZAHEER, SAIVID HASAN (see Hasan Zaheer Saiyid).

ZAHHADUR, RAHIM ZAHID SUHRAWARDY (see Suhrawardy, Sir Z. R. Z.).

ZAHID HASAN KHAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 9th Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as appr. engr., Nov. 19.6; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1927.

ZAHID HUSAIN, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 6th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service, 14th Mar., 1918; asst. auditor, N.W. rly., Apl., 1921; ditto, E.B. rly., Nov., 1923, ditto, E.I. rly., Dec., 1925; dir. of army audit, westn. circle, Feb., 1929; dep. contr., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1931; asst. auditor-genl., Apl., 1932; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts. office, Mar., 1933; dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels. (finance), Jan., 1934; orig. comptr., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1935; fin. advr. to C.C., Delhi, Feb., 1937.

ZAHURUDDIN AHMAD FARUQI, MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.,) (b. 9th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1913; asst. engr., Sept., 1914; asst. exec. engr., Dec.,

1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; transfid. to central p.w.d., Feb., 1933; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1934, and again, Apl., 1935.

ZAKIR HUSAIN, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 2nd Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 26th Sept., 1923; offg. supt., Sept., 1930; confd., Aug., 1936.

ZAMAN MEHDI KHAN, KHAN BAHADUR, Malik, B.A., late Punjab Civil Service (b. 1879).—Joined the service, 9th Feb., 1901; extra asst. commr., May, 1904; dep. commr., July, 1929; ret'd., June, 1934.

ZAW PE, U. (see Pe, U Zaw).

ZETLAND (MARQUESS OF), MOST. HON. LAWRENCE JOHN LUMLEY DUNDAS, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E. (sec. of state for India and for Burma).—Educ. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; A.D.C., Viceroy's staff, 1900; late captain 1st Bn. N. Riding of Yorks. Vol. Art., and late major, 4th Bn. Prince of Wales' Yorks. Regt.; M.P. for Hornsey divn. of Middlesex, 1907-16; member of the Royal Commn. on the public services in India, 1912-15; G.C.I.E., Feb., 1917; govr. of Bengal from 26th March, 1917, to 20th March, 1922; P.C., 1922; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; sec. of state for India, June, 1935; sec. of state for Burma, Apl., 1937; author of 'Sport and Politics under an Eastern Sky,' 'On the Outskirts of Empire in Asia,' 'A Wandering Student in the Far East,' 'An Eastern Miscellany.'

ZIA-UD-DIN AFZAL, LESLIE, Indian Police—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 16th July, 1928; offg. supt., May, 1934.

ZIA-UD-DIN AHMAD (see Ahmad, Z.).

ZIA-UL HASAN, KHAN BAHADUR, MUNSHI, B.A., Judl. Dept., United Provs. (judge, ch. ct., Oudh) (b. Dec., 1880).—Joined the service, 26th June, 1907; sub. judge, Jan., 1922; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1932; judge, ch. ct., Oudh, July, 1934.

ZIAUL HAQ, MIAN MUHAMMAD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 10th Sept., 1892).—Joined the service, 20th July, 1915; extra asst. consvr., July, 1917; promoted to I.F.S., United Provs., Apl., 1931; dep. consvr., Oct., 1931.

ZIAULLAH KHAN, MUHAMMAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 4th Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Lucknow Univ. and Magdalene College, Cambridge; joined the Service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.

ZINKIN, MAURICE, LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 4th May, 1915).—Educ. at Haberdashers' Aske's Hampstead Sch., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. on probn., 29th Sept., 1937; confd., 21st Sept., 1938; arrived in India, Dec., 1938, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

ZUBERI, MASARRAT HUSAIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 25th June, 1911).—Educated at St. Catherine's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

ZUTSHI, MANOHAR LAL (see Manohar Lal Z.).

CASUALTIES.
